column1: Quest for Glory II
column2: 英雄傳奇 II:烈火神兵

column1: According to an ancient prophecy, when Raseir fell into darkness, a hero from the north would come to restore light to the city.
column2: 古老的預言有云:當拉希爾墮入黑暗,北方終將有一位英雄前來,為這座城市重燃光明。

column1: Ad Avis is intent on raising Iblis.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯一心要喚醒伊布利斯。

column1: Ad Avis stands, glaring sternly, his black robes blowing in the wind.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯佇立著,目光森冷,一襲黑袍在風中獵獵翻飛。

column1: Although few see the Sultan, he sees all. The Sultan knows everyone and everything in the city of Shapeir.
column2: 蘇丹深居簡出,卻洞燭一切。夏皮爾城裡的每一個人、每一樁事,都瞞不過他的眼睛。

column1: A Raseirian guard patrols this section of the city.
column2: 一名拉希爾衛兵在這一帶巡邏。

column1: As you listen through the keyhole, you hear the regular goings on of Shapeirian life.
column2: 你貼著鑰匙孔聽,夏皮爾市井的尋常喧鬧一一傳入耳中。

column1: A business owner's sign hangs overhead.
column2: 一面店家招牌懸在頭頂上。

column1: A caravan is many travelers journeying together. It is much less dangerous that way.
column2: 所謂商隊,就是一大群旅人結伴上路——這樣一來,危險可就少多了。

column1: A barrel full of animal entrails. Lovely.
column2: 一整桶的動物內臟。真是「賞心悅目」。

column1: A strong aura of magic radiates from the Water Elemental. You suspect the creature isn't nearly as innocent as its appearance suggests.
column2: 水元素精靈渾身散發著濃烈的魔法氣息。你直覺這傢伙絕不像外表那般人畜無害。

column1: An Air Elemental grows in power the more it blows.
column2: 風元素精靈颳得越狂,力量就越驚人。

column1: A Water Elemental in the fountain of town would be the greatest of misfortunes.
column2: 要是讓水元素精靈鑽進城裡的噴泉,那可就是天大的災難了。

column1: As you have discovered, incense is quite a lure for Fire Elementals.
column2: 你已經發現:薰香對火元素精靈很有吸引力。

column1: A powerful aura of dark magic radiates from the Air Elemental.
column2: 風元素精靈散發出強大的黑暗魔法氣息。

column1: All you have to do is to throw it at the Earth Elemental. It makes such a lovely flame when it explodes.
column2: 你只管把它往土元素精靈身上一扔就成。它炸開時,那火花可漂亮得很。

column1: It is Tashtari, the lamp merchant.
column2: 這位是塔什塔里,賣燈的商人。

column1: It is Ugarte, the water smuggler.
column2: 這位是烏加特,走私水的販子。

column1: It is Saba, the basket merchant.
column2: 這位是薩巴,賣籃子的商人。

column1: It is Lasham, the plant merchant.
column2: 這位是拉珊,賣花草的商人。

column1: It is Khaveen, captain of the Raseirian Guard.
column2: 這位是哈維因,拉希爾衛隊的隊長。

column1: It is my honor to aid you, Effendi.
column2: 能為您效勞,是小的的榮幸,閣下。

column1: It is I who owe you my humble thanks, Effendi.
column2: 該道謝的是小的才對,閣下。

column1: It is always a pleasure to see you, great Hero.
column2: 能見到您,永遠是一件樂事,偉大的英雄。

column1: It is an ordinary, slightly rusty nail.
column2: 一根普通的釘子,還有點生鏽。

column1: It is a small clay pot, filled with dirt.
column2: 一只小陶罐,裡頭裝滿了泥土。

column1: It is dark beyond the open doorway.
column2: 敞開的門後一片漆黑。

column1: It is the reflection of your lamp light.
column2: 那是你燈火的倒影。

column1: It is hard to be a hero in the bottom of a dungeon.
column2: 困在地牢底下,英雄也難當啊。

column1: It is beyond repair.
column2: 已經修不好了。

column1: It is something you will never forget.
column2: 這是你一輩子都忘不了的經歷。

column1: I am sorry, but Uhura is the Guildmaster and combat instructor here. I generally do not spar with visitors.
column2: 抱歉,烏胡拉是這兒的公會會長兼格鬥教官。我一般是不跟訪客過招的。

column1: Maybe you do not hear so good. I be thinking you give me back Rakeesh's sword, or maybe I be getting plenty mad.
column2: 你耳朵不大靈光吧?我看哪,你最好把拉基什的劍還來,不然我可要發大火了。

column1: If you be willing to know more about swordsmanship, talk to Rakeesh. He already be wielding sword when we were babies.
column2: 你要是想多學點劍術,就去找拉基什。我們還是娃娃的時候,他就已經會耍劍了。

column1: Don't stand over Uhura when she flips back to her feet. She uses surprise attacks to turn the tables on you.
column2: 烏胡拉翻身站起時,別站在她正上方——她會冷不防反擊,把局面整個翻過來。

column1: Farewell, young adventurer. We shall speak again soon, no doubt.
column2: 再會了,年輕的冒險者。我們很快會再聊的,這點毋庸置疑。

column1: Good day. I hope you are well, young adventurer.
column2: 你好。願你一切安好,年輕的冒險者。

column1: Obviously a graduate of the Famous Adventurers' Correspondence School.
column2: 一看就是「著名冒險家函授學校」的高材生。

column1: Nothing you can say will mend the broken pot.
column2: 破罐子難補,你再怎麼說好話也沒用。

column1: Perhaps the carpet ride into the city was a bit much for you, Hero?
column2: 乘魔毯進城,對你來說是不是有點吃不消啊,英雄?

column1: Perhaps you should dismount first.
column2: 或許你該先下坐騎。

column1: Perhaps you should learn more about who sent you before you seek me.
column2: 在來找我之前,你或許該先弄清楚究竟是誰派你來的。

column1: Perhaps there is more a brain about you than I suspected.
column2: 看來你腦袋比我想的還要靈光些。

column1: That doesn't make you look very sexy.
column2: 這可不怎麼讓你顯得有魅力。

column1: That doesn't seem to affect the living flames of the Elemental.
column2: 這對元素精靈那活生生的火焰,似乎起不了作用。

column1: That file already exists. Please pick another name.
column2: 這個檔名已經有了,請另取一個。

column1: That is a most valuable item you have there.
column2: 你手上那玩意兒,可是件稀世珍寶啊。

column1: That is most kind of you. We love treats like these.
column2: 您真是太客氣了。我們最愛這種好東西了。

column1: That is indeed a sad tale. Let me show you of that which we speak.
column2: 這的確是個令人傷感的故事。讓我把我們所說的指給你看吧。

column1: That person isn't interested in the item you offer.
column2: 那人對你拿出來的東西不感興趣。

column1: That Saurus doesn't belong to you.
column2: 那頭索魯斯可不是你的。

column1: A potion to break enchantments? I know of one in which the victim drinks the potion and the spell ends.
column2: 破除魔咒的藥水?我聽過一種——只要中咒的人喝下去,法術便會解除。

column1: Aziza's pawn isn't a valid target for that spell.
column2: 阿姿莎的棋子不能當這個法術的目標。

column1: Casting spells when guards are around is not the wisest course of action.
column2: 衛兵就在旁邊還施法,可不是什麼高明的舉動。

column1: Don't fight them tonight!
column2: 今晚別跟他們動手!

column1: For some reason, your mind can't seem to focus enough to cast that spell.
column2: 不知怎地,你心神無法集中,施展不出那個法術。

column1: Furthermore, an overhead slash can be used to knock an enemy to the ground and avoid a quick counterattack.
column2: 此外,由上而下的劈砍能把敵人擊倒在地,還能避開對方迅捷的反擊。

column1: Get the hell out of dodge? Not you!
column2: 腳底抹油、溜之大吉?那可不是你的作風!

column1: Good sword. Good fight. There be many proud of you.
column2: 好劍法。好身手。會有不少人以你為榮的。

column1: Maybe you find work for hero here, too.
column2: 說不定這兒也有適合英雄幹的活兒。

column1: You would try the patience of a dervish! Do something!
column2: 你連苦行僧的耐性都要磨光了!倒是做點什麼啊!

column1: A beautifully ornate window adorns this area of the wall.
column2: 牆上這一處鑲著一扇華美雕飾的窗。

column1: A decorative basket adorns the alley.
column2: 一只裝飾用的籃子點綴著巷子。

column1: A delicately crafted jar of copper and bronze.
column2: 一只以紅銅與青銅精工打造的罐子。

column1: A derelict window hangs unevenly from its battered frame.
column2: 一扇破窗歪斜地掛在殘破的窗框上。

column1: A discarded barrel rests nearby. The wood shows heavy signs of rot.
column2: 附近擱著一只廢棄的木桶,木頭已嚴重腐朽。

column1: A eunuch patrols the parapet.
column2: 一名宦官在城垛上巡邏。

column1: A feeling of peace and tranquility permeates the area.
column2: 一股祥和寧靜的氣息瀰漫此地。

column1: A ghastly moan is all you hear from the other side of the door.
column2: 門的另一側,你只聽見一聲淒厲的呻吟。

column1: A handshake wouldn't do much to change this guy's bad attitude.
column2: 光握個手,可改變不了這傢伙的臭脾氣。

column1: A large iron grate blocks further passage in this direction.
column2: 一道巨大的鐵柵欄擋住了這個方向的去路。

column1: A most interesting tale.
column2: 真是個有意思的故事。

column1: A most prudent decision upon your part.
column2: 你這決定下得真是明智。

column1: An Acme Lock Pick, suitable for most standard locks and a few squirrely ones.
column2: 一把「頂尖牌」開鎖器,應付大多數標準鎖綽綽有餘,連幾種刁鑽的也難不倒它。

column1: A pile of heavy wooden crates has been stacked here.
column2: 這裡堆著一疊沉重的木箱。

column1: A pity we couldn't make an exchange. Perhaps some other time, then.
column2: 真可惜,這筆買賣沒談成。那就改日再說吧。

column1: A quick glance at the skyline tells you how much in decline the city is.
column2: 只消瞄一眼天際線,你便知道這座城市已沒落到何種地步。

column1: A simple yes or no will suffice.
column2: 答個「要」或「不要」就行了。

column1: A square, flat box lies discarded among the trash.
column2: 一只方扁的盒子被丟棄在垃圾堆裡。

column1: A strange sort of person, even for a human.
column2: 真是個怪人——即便以人類的標準來看也一樣。

column1: A topiary expert, you are not.
column2: 修剪花木的高手,你肯定不是。

column1: A wise choice. I dislike a man who doesn't drink.
column2: 明智之選。我可不喜歡滴酒不沾的男人。

column1: Greetings, Effendi. Could you spare some alms for one who is not so fortunate?
column2: 您好,閣下。能否施捨些錢財,給我這不幸之人?

column1: Greetings, friend of the Katta. Perhaps we should look for a way out of here!
column2: 你好,卡塔族的朋友。或許我們該找條路離開這兒!

column1: Greetings, Hero. Do you wish to engage in another wager with me?
column2: 你好,英雄。想再跟我賭一把嗎?

column1: Greetings, Hero. I am glad you have come to my aid.
column2: 你好,英雄。你能來相助,我很欣慰。

column1: Greetings, nimble one. The crowd seems very pleased with your achievement.
column2: 你好,身手敏捷的傢伙。群眾似乎對你的本事很是滿意。

column1: He doesn't appreciate being looked down upon. Why don't you dismount first?
column2: 他可不喜歡被人居高臨下地看著。你何不先下坐騎?

column1: He doesn't seem to have any useful items left in stock.
column2: 他似乎沒剩什麼有用的貨了。

column1: He is one of the local guards.
column2: 他是本地的衛兵之一。

column1: Hello! Bone jar! Hi, guy! Set a spell, take your shoes off! What may I do to you?
column2: 哈囉!笨豬有!嗨呀老兄!坐下歇會兒,把鞋脫了吧!有啥能「效勞」你的?

column1: At nightfall tonight, go the door at the end of 'Askeri Darb,' off 'Saif Darb.' Obey, or you'll regret it.
column2: 今晚入夜後,去『Saif Darb』岔出的『Askeri Darb』盡頭那扇門。乖乖照辦,否則你會後悔。

column1: Alas, my heart is broken.
column2: 唉,我的心都碎了。

column1: Ask for a sign another time.
column2: 改天再來求籤吧。

column1: By golly, it is! There's a silver lining on the underside of Keapon's cloud.
column2: 我的天,還真是!姬朋這朵烏雲底下,總算透出一線光明。

column1: Don't expect any cutting replies.
column2: 別指望聽到什麼犀利的回嘴。

column1: For some reason the item seems stuck in your pocket. It appears you have only your magic skills to rely upon here.
column2: 不知怎地,那件東西像黏在口袋裡似的。看來在這兒,你只能靠魔法了。

column1: Good. I hate to part with it unless there is a very good cause.
column2: 很好。要不是有天大的理由,我可捨不得割愛。

column1: Hello, Master. You still have one wish left.
column2: 您好,主人。您還剩一個願望。

column1: I already gave you the bellows, remember?
column2: 我風箱已經給你啦,記得吧?

column1: I believe, O Hero, that for you I have a pin.
column2: 英雄啊,我想我這兒有根別針正合你用。

column1: I know only that it is something to be greatly feared.
column2: 我只知道,那是個極其可怕的東西。

column1: I will need a Griffin's feather. You must add the hair of the victim yourself at the last moment. It must be fresh.
column2: 我需要一根獅鷲的羽毛。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻親手加進去,而且必須是新鮮的。

column1: If you be tired, we go back to Main Hall. Much more comfortable there.
column2: 你要是累了,咱們就回大廳去。那兒舒服多了。

column1: Issur's bellows really blow up a storm with the Air Elemental inside.
column2: 把風元素精靈關進去,伊蘇的風箱當真能颳起一場風暴。

column1: It is my honor, Effendi.
column2: 這是小的的榮幸,閣下。

column1: It will flame for the battle only, for it draws its strength from mine.
column2: 它只在戰鬥時燃燒,因為它的力量取自於我。

column1: It's an exotic potted plant.
column2: 一盆奇特的盆栽。

column1: It's the city of Shapeir inside a glass dome. When the dome is shaken, a sand storm envelops the city.
column2: 玻璃罩裡是夏皮爾城。一搖那罩子,沙暴便籠罩全城。

column1: Khaveen is anxiously pacing back and forth across the hall.
column2: 哈維因正焦躁地在大廳裡來回踱步。

column1: May thee be granted a happy evening, Effendi.
column2: 願您有個愉快的夜晚,閣下。

column1: My name is Larisha.
column2: 我叫拉莉莎。

column1: Nevertheless, shall we make a deal at forty dinars?
column2: 不過呢,四十第納爾,這筆買賣成交如何?

column1: Now, please excuse me. I really must get back to my work.
column2: 好了,失陪了。我真的得回去幹活兒了。

column1: Our fellow Katta promised they would send us support and aid whenever we were in need of it.
column2: 我們卡塔族的同胞答應過,只要我們有需要,必定伸出援手。

column1: Please leave the sword with Uhura in the Guild Hall when you are done with it.
column2: 用完那把劍,請交還給公會大廳的烏胡拉。

column1: Rakeesh's sword arm and reflexes be very quick. Don't be surprised if your attacks miss their target most of the time.
column2: 拉基什的劍臂和反應都快得很。你的攻擊要是常常落空,可別意外。

column1: Shema just gave you these clothes. It'd be rude to discard them right in front of her.
column2: 雪瑪才剛把這身衣裳送你,當著她的面丟掉可太失禮了。

column1: Sorry, Chief. With your spell collection this incomplete, you'll only meet with quick defeat.
column2: 抱歉啦,老大。你這法術學得七零八落,上去也只是速速吃敗仗。

column1: Thank you for the kindness you have shown Julanar. You are a true hero for helping her.
column2: 謝謝你對茱拉娜爾的善意。肯幫她,你是個真正的英雄。

column1: That would have no effect on the Air Elemental.
column2: 這對風元素精靈起不了作用。

column1: The Earth Elemental crushes everything in its path.
column2: 土元素精靈會輾碎一切擋路之物。

column1: The Liontaur is carefully looking you over, almost as if he's analyzing you.
column2: 那獅人仔細地打量著你,簡直像在分析你似的。

column1: The bag feels pretty heavy and is obviously filled with plenty of coins.
column2: 這袋子沉甸甸的,顯然裝滿了錢幣。

column1: The crash of boulder upon boulder echoes through the cavern.
column2: 巨石撞擊巨石的轟響在洞穴中迴盪。

column1: The grate, while damaged and rusty, is still solid enough to withstand your attempts at opening it.
column2: 那鐵柵雖然殘破生鏽,卻仍夠堅固,任你怎麼弄都打不開。

column1: The magic rope you purchased from the Magic Shop looks like an ordinary rope, although it coils tighter and weighs less.
column2: 你從魔法店買來的魔繩,看著跟普通繩子沒兩樣,只是盤得更緊、也更輕。

column1: The pill makes you thirsty, so you take out your waterskin.
column2: 藥丸讓你口渴,於是你取出了水袋。

column1: The shop has closed for the night.
column2: 店家已經打烊了。

column1: The thick layer of dust in this merchant stall suggests it hasn't been used in a long time.
column2: 這攤位上積了厚厚一層灰,看來已經很久沒人用了。

column1: The wooden door's latch seems to be stuck. It doesn't respond.
column2: 木門的門閂似乎卡住了,毫無反應。

column1: There is nothing left in the box.
column2: 盒子裡什麼也不剩了。

column1: There's nobody around to talk to.
column2: 附近沒有人可以攀談。

column1: This constellation is said to resemble a Saurus.
column2: 據說這個星座的形狀像一頭索魯斯。

column1: Those guards are well-paid to ignore my friends.
column2: 那些衛兵收了好處,專門對我的朋友睜一隻眼閉一隻眼。

column1: Utilize the skull-splitter on opponents who use low attacks to quickly end the battle in your favor.
column2: 對付慣使下盤攻擊的對手,用「劈顱式」能速戰速決、占得上風。

column1: Well, young Jackal, may fortune always favor you.
column2: 好啦,小豺狼,願好運永遠眷顧你。

column1: Who do you call upon to be your sponsor and mentor in these hallowed halls of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 在這巫師技術學院的神聖殿堂裡,你要請誰當你的引薦人與導師?

column1: You are suddenly, forcefully reminded that "It isn't the fall that hurts; it's when you hit the ground."
column2: 你猛地、狠狠地體會到一句話:「摔下來不疼,著地那一下才要命。」

column1: You can now pick (on) locks your own size!
column2: 現在你可以開(捉弄)跟你差不多大的鎖了!

column1: You can't see a thing. It's too dark.
column2: 你什麼也看不見,太暗了。

column1: You describe to Zayishah how the Emir was transformed into a Saurus, and that Aziza is currently taking care of him.
column2: 你向札伊莎描述埃米爾如何被變成了一頭索魯斯,如今正由阿姿莎照料著。

column1: You don't have a use for any of the pots on display.
column2: 陳列的這些罐子,你都用不上。

column1: You drop the basket into the storage chest.
column2: 你把籃子放進儲物箱。

column1: You have not answered my question. Who are you that knocks upon my door?
column2: 你還沒回答我的問題。敲我門的,究竟是何人?

column1: You look at the dangerous-looking guard and think better of it.
column2: 你看了看那一臉兇相的衛兵,還是打消了念頭。

column1: You really ought to prepare for the departing caravan, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你真該為即將啟程的商隊做準備了。

column1: You return the reward to Rakeesh and tell him that the city being safe again is enough of a reward already.
column2: 你把酬勞退還給拉基什,告訴他城市重歸安寧,這份回報就已足夠。

column1: You should sit down first if you wish to speak with Abdulla.
column2: 你若想跟阿布杜拉說話,得先坐下。

column1: You still don't have the skill, Phil, so I'll spare you the bill.
column2: 你火候還不到啊老兄,這筆帳我就先不跟你算清。

column1: You take a careful sip from your waterskin. It tastes great.
column2: 你小心地就著水袋啜了一口,味道好極了。

column1: You tell the Katta about your friendship with his cousin Shema.
column2: 你向這位卡塔族人說起你和他表親雪瑪的交情。

column1: You'd just get fingerprints all over it!
column2: 你只會在上頭按得到處都是指紋!

column1: Alas, you will need to visit the Moneychanger before you can buy anything here.
column2: 唉,你得先去找錢莊兌換,才買得起這兒的東西。

column1: Aziza does not want anything from you.
column2: 阿姿莎不需要你任何東西。

column1: Certainly you do not wish for more to eat!
column2: 你肯定不想再吃了吧!

column1: Don't try to interrupt Uhura's chain of attacks by dodging the first blow and attacking in between.
column2: 別想著閃過烏胡拉的第一擊、再趁隙還手來打斷她的連段——那行不通。

column1: For you, it will take a mere 18 dinars to loosen my lips.
column2: 看在你的份上,區區十八第納爾,我這嘴就鬆了。

column1: Greetings, Hero. Do you feel brave and lucky today? Nobody else around here seems willing to take my challenge.
column2: 你好,英雄。今天覺得自己又勇又走運嗎?這附近可沒人敢接我的挑戰。

column1: Hello, Master. You still have two wishes left.
column2: 您好,主人。您還剩兩個願望。

column1: I am always glad to be of assistance.
column2: 能幫上忙,我向來樂意。

column1: I confess I am a bit disappointed in you, sir. It seems I expected too much.
column2: 老實說,我對你有點失望,先生。看來是我期望太高了。

column1: I love a man who likes to keep in shape.
column2: 我就喜歡懂得鍛鍊身材的男人。

column1: I wish I had as much time to waste as you do.
column2: 真希望我也有你這麼多時間可以揮霍。

column1: If you know not whom you seek, you will surely not find that person here.
column2: 你若連自己要找誰都不知道,在這兒是絕對找不著那人的。

column1: It doesn't look like it would make a very suitable mount.
column2: 看起來不太適合拿來當坐騎。

column1: It is the finest suit of mail in all the realm. Its value is far beyond you.
column2: 這是全國最上乘的一副鎖子甲,它的價值遠非你所能企及。

column1: It would be impolite to disturb the Katta musician while he's playing.
column2: 卡塔族樂師正在演奏,打擾他可不禮貌。

column1: It's empty. This is a good thing as eating leftover scraps would be a very bad idea.
column2: 空的。這是好事,畢竟吃別人的剩菜殘羹可不是什麼好主意。

column1: It's too dark to see what you're dropping. You'd better not drop anything until you can see properly.
column2: 太暗了,看不清你要丟什麼。等看得清楚再丟吧。

column1: Lamb falafels and soured cream, along with a rabbit curry, are part of the meal for tonight. May it do you good.
column2: 今晚的菜色有羊肉炸豆丸佐酸奶,還有一道兔肉咖哩。願它讓你吃得舒坦。

column1: Maybe today you and I get in some practice at fighting. It will be good to feel the spear in my hand again.
column2: 或許今天你我可以切磋切磋。能再次握握長矛的手感,挺不錯的。

column1: My name is Sashari.
column2: 我叫薩沙莉。

column1: No response. The residence is either abandoned or the occupant is unwilling to answer you.
column2: 沒有回應。這宅子若不是早已荒廢,就是裡頭的人不願搭理你。

column1: Oh, it is so good to know my uncle is still alive. He is such a kind and friendly man.
column2: 啊,得知我叔叔還活著,真是太好了。他是個多麼和善親切的人。

column1: Please, you must sit down and relax before we shall speak of what you seek.
column2: 來,你得先坐下放鬆,我們再來談你所求之事。

column1: Scoree is the local food merchant.
column2: 斯科里是本地的食品商。

column1: Since it's forbidden to be out at night, the torches in this city don't seem to have a practical use anymore.
column2: 既然夜裡禁止外出,這城裡的火把似乎也派不上用場了。

column1: Success! You now have an open nose.
column2: 成功!你的鼻子現在「暢通無阻」了。

column1: That's one way of getting more fiber in your diet.
column2: 這倒是替三餐多添點膳食纖維的法子。

column1: The Griffin looks like a cross between a lion and an eagle. It seems to be sleeping.
column2: 那獅鷲長得像獅子和老鷹的混血,似乎正在熟睡。

column1: The Saurus seems curious and excited.
column2: 那頭索魯斯顯得既好奇又興奮。

column1: The bellows are a sign of my trade. What about them?
column2: 風箱是我這行當的招牌。問它做什麼?

column1: The door is locked and barred from within. Your Open spell has no effect.
column2: 門從裡頭上了鎖、閂得死死的。你的「開啟」術毫無作用。

column1: The money you find there should be more than enough to compensate the price of this little bit of information.
column2: 你在那兒找到的錢,拿來付這一小條消息的費用,綽綽有餘。

column1: The rings I sell are perfect when you wish to propose to your significant other.
column2: 我賣的這些戒指,拿來向心上人求婚再合適不過了。

column1: The still, small voice within this ring must be that of a Djinni -- a very weak one.
column2: 這戒指裡那細微的低語,想必是個鎮尼——而且是非常孱弱的一個。

column1: The western section of the city is visible beyond the roofs of the nearby buildings.
column2: 越過附近屋頂,可以望見城市的西區。

column1: There is a strong sense of magic beyond the Palace walls.
column2: 宮牆之外,有一股濃烈的魔法氣息。

column1: There's no point in returning Soulforge before the Earth Elemental has been dispatched permanently.
column2: 在徹底了結土元素精靈之前,歸還鑄魂劍是沒有意義的。

column1: This is not a good place to practice your Levitate spell.
column2: 這裡可不是練「飄浮」術的好地方。

column1: This sign has deteriorated to a point where it is no longer even legible.
column2: 這招牌已殘破到連字都認不出來了。

column1: Ugarte and I are old friends.
column2: 烏加特和我是老交情了。

column1: When mounting an offense, an inverse swing can be used to follow up a swing for additional damage.
column2: 進攻時,可以接著一記反向揮砍,追加傷害。

column1: Without water, you would not survive even an hour in the desert.
column2: 沒有水,你在沙漠裡連一個鐘頭都撐不過。

column1: You are getting really thirsty.
column2: 你開始覺得非常口渴。

column1: You bet your life you've seen this guy before.
column2: 你敢拿性命打賭,你以前見過這傢伙。

column1: You can't get a good shot at Ad Avis from where you are.
column2: 從你現在的位置,瞄不準阿德·阿維斯。

column1: You could never catch the magical mass of swirling wind using just your hands.
column2: 光憑一雙手,你絕對抓不住那團旋轉的魔法旋風。

column1: You dispose of the incense.
column2: 你把薰香處理掉了。

column1: You drop a healing pill.
column2: 你掉了一顆治療藥丸。

column1: You drop the poisonous claws on the ground and grind them to powder with your heel.
column2: 你把毒爪丟在地上,用腳跟碾成了粉末。

column1: You get a small glass of tepid, stale-tasting water.
column2: 你拿到一小杯溫吞、走味的水。

column1: You have done all you can to weaken the spell on the tree.
column2: 削弱那棵樹上法術的事,你已盡力而為了。

column1: You hear the sound of silence from behind the door.
column2: 門後傳來的,是一片寂靜。

column1: You never need be hungry again!
column2: 你再也不必挨餓了!

column1: You retrieve the EOF badge from your chest.
column2: 你從箱子裡取出永恆戰士團的徽章。

column1: You seem to have hit a communication barrier here.
column2: 你在這兒似乎碰上了溝通的障礙。

column1: You spend an hour checking out the interior of the Apothecary and watching Harik work.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭打量藥舖內部,看著哈里克幹活。

column1: You store the incense.
column2: 你把薰香收了起來。

column1: You tell Harik about the caged beast.
column2: 你把籠中那頭野獸的事告訴了哈里克。

column1: You will need the magic of a flaming sword to defeat the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你需要一把燃燒之劍的魔力,才能擊敗土元素精靈。

column1: You're not THAT desperate for a light.
column2: 你還沒淪落到「那麼」需要一點火光的地步。

column1: Abdulla glances at you and looks a bit uncomfortable. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 阿布杜拉瞥了你一眼,神色有些不自在。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: A finely crafted jar of brass and copper. The surface is so shiny and smooth it almost acts like a mirror.
column2: 一只黃銅與紅銅精製的罐子,表面光亮平滑,幾乎能當鏡子照。

column1: After all we've been through to come to an agreement, and you have the nerve not to have enough money.
column2: 費了這麼大勁才談攏,你倒好,竟厚著臉皮說錢不夠。

column1: After an hour's rest, you feel better.
column2: 歇了一個鐘頭,你覺得好多了。

column1: After carefully squeezing the last drop of poison out of the tail, you drop it on the ground.
column2: 你小心地把尾巴裡最後一滴毒液擠乾,隨手扔在地上。

column1: After the hour of rest, you don't really feel better. In fact, your thirst is worse than ever.
column2: 歇了一個鐘頭,你並沒覺得好些。事實上,你比先前更渴了。

column1: After you, my friend. I will humbly follow.
column2: 您先請,朋友。我恭敬地跟在後頭。

column1: Ah, good day. I am busy as always, making up a batch of pills. If you need something, just let me know.
column2: 啊,你好。我一如往常地忙,正在調一批藥丸。有什麼需要,儘管說。

column1: Ah, I did not notice you come in.
column2: 啊,我沒注意到你進來。

column1: Ah, men... always coming and going. Always going and coming, and always too soon.
column2: 唉,男人哪……總是來來去去,去去來來,而且總是走得太早。

column1: Ah, then you are indeed a friend in a place where we both need friends. We need to escape from this prison.
column2: 啊,那麼在這你我都需要朋友的地方,你果真是個朋友。我們得逃出這座牢獄。

column1: Ah, true love at first sight. And what a sight for Saurus eyes.
column2: 啊,一見鍾情的真愛。這在索魯斯眼裡,可真是養眼哪。

column1: Alas, you are out of water.
column2: 唉,你的水喝光了。

column1: Ali Fakir said after you left that he had sold his last Saurus and must be going.
column2: 你走後,阿里·法基爾說他最後一頭索魯斯也賣掉了,該動身了。

column1: All you hear is a ringing in your own ears.
column2: 你只聽見自己耳中的嗡嗡聲。

column1: Always happy to be of service, Handsome.
column2: 隨時樂意效勞,帥哥。

column1: A mere week after Abdulla's arrival, another new face appeared in Spielburg.
column2: 阿布杜拉抵達不過一週,史畢柏格又出現了一張新面孔。

column1: Announcing that you are a thief would not be a good idea right now.
column2: 現在嚷嚷自己是個盜賊,可不是什麼好主意。

column1: Antidote pills to magically cure venomous stings and scratches.
column2: 解毒藥丸,能以魔法治癒毒螫與抓傷。

column1: Anything he says, ain't gonna be much.
column2: 他就算開口,也說不出什麼名堂。

column1: Are you saying you wish to help me weave baskets, Hero of the North?
column2: 你是說你想幫我編籃子嗎,北方來的英雄?

column1: Are you sure you want to quit?
column2: 你確定要離開嗎?

column1: As a favor for getting yourself busted, Jabbar first beat you senseless before cutting off your hand.
column2: 為「答謝」你害自己被逮,賈巴爾先把你打個半死,才剁了你的手。

column1: As I have said before, there is no room for amateurs in this city.
column2: 我說過了,這座城市容不下外行人。

column1: As it's getting late, you decide to return to the inn for the night.
column2: 天色已晚,你決定回客棧過夜。

column1: As you blow into the barrel, a white cloud of mold escapes into the air.
column2: 你往桶裡一吹,一團白色的黴菌粉塵散入空中。

column1: As you call out, you realize how silent the streets around you are.
column2: 你一出聲,才驚覺四周街道是何等寂靜。

column1: As you expected, the door is locked and bolted.
column2: 不出你所料,門上了鎖、還閂著。

column1: As you press your ear to the door, you hear the sound of solid wood.
column2: 你把耳朵貼上門,聽見的只是一片厚實木頭的悶響。

column1: As you search through your pouch, you discover that you have no more coins from Spielburg to exchange.
column2: 你翻遍錢囊,發現再也沒有史畢柏格的錢幣可換了。

column1: At any rate, I'm here to sell you a Saurus, and you're ready to buy it, right?
column2: 總之,我是來賣你一頭索魯斯的,你也準備好要買了,對吧?

column1: A. You don't. You get down off a duck.
column2: 答:你不「下」大象——鴨絨才是從鴨子身上拔的。

column1: Aziza would most likely be very offended if you tried to mount the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你要是想騎那位中了魔法的埃米爾,阿姿莎多半會大為光火。

column1: Bad idea. The sound of the coins being shifted into your own purse would surely alert the guards.
column2: 餿主意。錢幣落進你錢袋的聲響,準會驚動衛兵。

column1: Behind the locked door, you hear a family quarrel. Better not get involved.
column2: 上了鎖的門後傳來一陣家庭口角。還是別淌這渾水。

column1: Be it ever so humble, there's no face like Gnome's.
column2: 金窩銀窩,都不如地精的臉窩。

column1: Best of all, they can always find their way back to where they are stabled, so you need never get lost.
column2: 最妙的是,牠們總能自己找回棚廄,你再也不必擔心迷路。

column1: Better not. His bite is worse than his bark.
column2: 還是別了。這傢伙咬起人來可比叫得兇。

column1: Beyond the buildings to the south lies the great Shapeirian Desert.
column2: 南面建築之外,便是浩瀚的夏皮爾大沙漠。

column1: Beyond the door, all is deathly silent.
column2: 門後是一片死寂。

column1: Boy, did you look silly!
column2: 老天,你那模樣可真蠢!

column1: Bring it here, quickly. Give it to me!
column2: 快拿過來,給我!

column1: But sir, we have not yet done business. Surely what I have to tell you is worth five paltry dinars?
column2: 可是先生,咱們生意還沒談成呢。我要告訴你的,難道不值區區五枚第納爾?

column1: But, wait, perhaps Lasham, the plant merchant, might be able to assist you.
column2: 不過,等等——或許賣花草的商人拉珊幫得上你。

column1: CAUTION: Do not try this at home.
column2: 警告:請勿在家嘗試。

column1: Come over here and face me when you talk to me, wimp.
column2: 跟我說話就過來當面說,膽小鬼。

column1: Darkness will triumph unless you stop the last light.
column2: 除非你阻止最後一道光熄滅,否則黑暗終將得勝。

column1: Death sting: fatal without antidote
column2: 致命螫刺:無解藥即致死

column1: Deflection is your new middle name!
column2: 「格擋」就是你的新外號!

column1: Discretion is your Friend!
column2: 謹慎是你的好朋友!

column1: And not a drop to drink
column2: 卻沒有一滴水可喝。

column1: Carelessly tossing this gift aside wouldn't be very compassionate of you.
column2: 把這份禮物隨手一丟,可顯不出你的慈悲心腸。

column1: Better keep it. You're supposed to give it back, remember?
column2: 還是留著吧。你可是得歸還的,記得嗎?

column1: Beyond the buildings to the north lies the desert you recently crossed to get here.
column2: 北面建築之外,是你不久前橫越而來的沙漠。

column1: And I want to thank you for all the enjoyment you've taken out of it.
column2: 我還要謝謝你從中得到的那些樂子。

column1: As I said, I really could not give you the little bit that I have.
column2: 我說過了,我手頭這一點點,實在沒法給你。

column1: As you look the plants over, you realize that you don't have the time or place to keep a potted plant.
column2: 你把這些花草打量一番,意識到自己既沒時間、也沒地方養盆栽。

column1: As you search through your purse, you realize that you have no more coins to exchange.
column2: 你翻遍錢袋,發現再也沒有錢幣可兌換了。

column1: At this point, it is merely a sign of deterioration.
column2: 事到如今,這不過是衰敗的徵兆罷了。

column1: Back to the matter at hand. As a matter of fact, I was just selling you a Saurus, wasn't I?
column2: 言歸正傳。說真的,我剛才不正在賣你一頭索魯斯嗎?

column1: Bag with magic earth
column2: 裝著魔法泥土的袋子

column1: Behind this door, you hear someone cursing about the series finale of The Sopranos.
column2: 這扇門後,有人正在咒罵《黑道家族》的大結局。

column1: Bellows with air element
column2: 封著風元素的風箱

column1: Better hold on to your compass for a while.
column2: 這羅盤你還是先留著吧。

column1: Better not. You might not be able to get another one so easily.
column2: 還是別了。你未必能再輕易弄到一個。

column1: Booger like Truth. Man seeks it out, but knows not what to do with it when he gets it.
column2: 鼻屎好比真理。人人挖空心思去尋,真到手了卻又不知如何是好。

column1: Buon giorno! Good and tag! Buenas dias, Senor Caws. I haven't seen you in so long.
column2: 日安!勾的摸您!喔啦,烏鴉先生。好久不見啦。

column1: Debug: All locations added to map.
column2: 除錯:已將所有地點加入地圖。

column1: Does this mean you do not wish to buy my wares, Effendi?
column2: 閣下,這是說您不想買我的貨了?

column1: Do not give up. The creature may be durable, but it is not indestructible. Not yet.
column2: 別放棄。這生物或許耐打,卻並非不死之身——還沒到那地步。

column1: Do not trouble yourself with such actions.
column2: 您不必費這個事。

column1: Don't be such a Chatty Cathy. Get moving.
column2: 別這麼囉哩囉嗦的。動起來。

column1: Don't bother blowing away the dust. There are other ways to clean up this place.
column2: 別費勁去吹灰了。要清理這地方,另有別的法子。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to Merv the Griffin.
column2: 別費勁跟葛里芬獸默夫搭話了。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the funny-lookin' dude.
column2: 別費勁跟那長相滑稽的傢伙搭話了。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to this townsperson.
column2: 別費勁跟這位市民搭話了。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to your Saurus.
column2: 別費勁跟你的索魯斯搭話了。

column1: Don't expect any slick replies.
column2: 別指望聽到什麼油嘴滑舌的回話。

column1: Don't waste any lines on it.
column2: 別在它身上浪費口舌了。

column1: Do you have a belly ache?
column2: 你肚子疼嗎?

column1: Do you mind if I'm smoke?
column2: 你介意我「化作」一縷煙嗎?

column1: Do you need one to capture the Fire Elemental? I can let you have this one.
column2: 你需要一個來捕捉火元素精靈嗎?這個可以讓給你。

column1: Do your clothes itch? A hero such as yourself should always carry a spare outfit, Effendi.
column2: 衣裳穿著發癢嗎?像您這樣的英雄,身上總該備一套換洗的,閣下。

column1: Do you suffer an allergic reaction to pollen, Effendi?
column2: 您對花粉過敏嗎,閣下?

column1: Drain: claws drain hp
column2: 吸取:利爪吸取生命力

column1: Elsa could not have wished for a truer friend.
column2: 艾莎再也求不到比這更真心的朋友了。

column1: Enjoy the sights of Shapeir, O Hero.
column2: 英雄啊,好好欣賞夏皮爾的風光吧。

column1: Enough. You do not want to make me angry!
column2: 夠了。你可不會想惹我發火。

column1: Every day, the Astrologer thanks his lucky stars that he has lucky stars.
column2: 占星師每天都感謝他的幸運星,慶幸自己有幸運星可感謝。

column1: Every hero needs a break now and then.
column2: 再了不起的英雄,偶爾也得歇口氣。

column1: Everyone here keeps their doors tightly locked and bolted.
column2: 這兒家家戶戶都把門鎖得緊緊、閂得死死的。

column1: Falafel is pita bread stuffed with spicy roasted lamb. A hearty meal.
column2: 炸豆丸是夾了香烤辣羊肉的口袋餅,是頓紮實的好料。

column1: Farewell, Effendi. May you have a well-rounded day.
column2: 再會,閣下。願您有圓圓滿滿的一天。

column1: Farewell, Hero from the North.
column2: 再會,北方來的英雄。

column1: Farewell, Hero of my people.
column2: 再會,我族的英雄。

column1: Farewell. I never forget a face, but in your case I'll make an exception.
column2: 再會。我從不忘記任何一張臉——不過你這張,我願破例。

column1: Farewell is like your footwear: well-worn but to the point.
column2: 「再會」就像你那雙鞋:穿得舊了,倒也直截了當。

column1: Fenrus, when is a rat not a rat?
column2: 芬魯斯,老鼠什麼時候不是老鼠?

column1: File could not be created. Disk may be full.
column2: 無法建立檔案,磁碟可能已滿。

column1: Flowers are sweet, but I prefer diamonds to daisies any day.
column2: 花兒雖甜美,但要我選,我寧取鑽石,不要雛菊。

column1: Flowers seem to wilt quickly in the desert heat.
column2: 在沙漠的酷熱下,花兒似乎謝得特別快。

column1: Focus: fast mp recharge
column2: 凝神:法力快速回復

column1: Forgive me, but I am not entirely familiar with the customs of humans.
column2: 請見諒,我對人類的習俗不甚熟悉。

column1: Forgive me, but I have no time for such a thing.
column2: 請見諒,我沒空理會這種事。

column1: Forgive me, Effendi. I am not worthy of your kindness.
column2: 請原諒我,閣下。我當不起您的善意。

column1: Expect no cheap replies.
column2: 別指望聽到什麼廉價的回話。

column1: Expect no fancy replies.
column2: 別指望聽到什麼花俏的回話。

column1: From among the Griffin's feathers, you choose a nice-looking one and pluck it.
column2: 你從獅鷲的羽毛裡挑了一根好看的,把它拔了下來。

column1: From behind the door, you hear "Zzzzzz..."
column2: 門後傳來「呼……呼……」的鼾聲。

column1: From the branches of Julanar, you have obtained the Fruit of Compassion.
column2: 你從茱拉娜爾的枝椏間,取得了慈悲之果。

column1: Game Logging is turned off.
column2: 遊戲記錄已關閉。

column1: Game Logging is turned on.
column2: 遊戲記錄已開啟。

column1: Get back on your feet first, Hero.
column2: 先站起來再說,英雄。

column1: Ghoul claws are horrible things to look at.
column2: 食屍鬼的爪子看著就讓人發毛。

column1: Give yourself a little room, Hero.
column2: 給自己留點空間,英雄。

column1: Goodbye? Of course a Saurus is a good buy! Don't leave. You haven't purchased one yet.
column2: 再見?那頭索魯斯當然「再划算不過」啦!別走嘛,你還沒買呢。

column1: Good day. A pleasant one for those such as myself who like the heat, although most humans might find it hot.
column2: 你好。對我這種愛熱的傢伙來說是個好天氣,雖說多數人類大概會嫌太熱。

column1: Good day, Hero of Spielburg. I hope you slept well.
column2: 你好,史畢柏格的英雄。希望你睡得安穩。

column1: Good luck, sir, and I do hope you are as skilled as I suspect.
column2: 祝你好運,先生。我真心希望你和我猜想的一樣有本事。

column1: Go to the east and then go south when you can. Follow the road and you be finding her.
column2: 往東走,能往南時就往南。沿著路走,你就會找著她。

column1: Griffins tend to nest near the mountains.
column2: 獅鷲多半在山邊築巢。

column1: Guards hate knife throwers
column2: 衛兵痛恨擲刀的人

column1: Guards hate rock throwers
column2: 衛兵痛恨擲石的人

column1: Habari, young adventurer. It is good to have your kind here.
column2: 你好(Habari),年輕的冒險者。有你這樣的人來,真好。

column1: Hagatha only wishes she knew about this cheat!
column2: 哈嘎莎要是知道有這個作弊碼,不知會多眼紅!

column1: Hail, fellow, well met! What da ya know, Joe?
column2: 幸會幸會,老兄!最近如何呀,老喬?

column1: Harik doesn't need the bellows. Besides, you may still need them.
column2: 哈里克用不著這風箱。再說,你說不定還用得上。

column1: Have a nice nap.
column2: 好好睡一覺吧。

column1: Ha! You think to win this campaign by slinging mud?
column2: 哈!你以為靠潑髒水就能贏下這一仗?

column1: Heavy iron bars cover the window. It makes the residence feel like a prison.
column2: 厚重的鐵條封住了窗,讓這宅子像座牢房。

column1: He just loves it when you talk dirt to him.
column2: 你跟他說泥巴的事,他可愛聽了。

column1: Hello, handsome. I love a man who knows how to properly address a woman.
column2: 你好,帥哥。我就喜歡懂得如何得體稱呼女士的男人。

column1: Hello, I must be going. An exit line if ever I heard one.
column2: 你好,我得走了。要說退場台詞,這可真是一句。

column1: He looks like a charming fellow.
column2: 他看起來是個討喜的傢伙。

column1: Here is lemon chicken with garlic and lamb stewed with herbs. Eat it in good health.
column2: 這是蒜香檸檬雞,還有香草燉羊肉。祝您吃得健健康康。

column1: Here is some of our wonderful tea.
column2: 這是我們上好的茶,請用。

column1: Here is your morning meal of warm bread and honeyed butter, fresh fruit, and tea, just as you prefer.
column2: 這是您的早餐:溫熱的麵包配蜜奶油、新鮮水果,還有茶,正合您的口味。

column1: Here you are. I just love intimate exchanges like this.
column2: 給你。我就愛這種親密的交流。

column1: Her house is the first door on the west, at the place where the road is blocked off.
column2: 她家是西邊第一扇門,就在路被封住的地方。

column1: Hero, please take care of yourself. I worry for you.
column2: 英雄,請保重自己。我為你擔心。

column1: Hero types don't do that sort of thing more than once.
column2: 英雄這種人,同樣的蠢事是不會做第二次的。

column1: Her shop is at the south end of Dinar Tarik.
column2: 她的店在 Dinar Tarik 的南端。

column1: He seems like such a nice man. You would really like to be able to help him.
column2: 他看起來是個大好人。你真希望自己能幫上他。

column1: He seems to have a sore throat. You get no reply.
column2: 他似乎喉嚨痛,沒有回應你。

column1: He smiles at you and you are reminded of a weasel. He speaks, and you think of snakes.
column2: 他朝你一笑,讓你想起黃鼠狼;他一開口,你又想到了蛇。

column1: He's participating in the Persian Golf tournament.
column2: 他正在參加波斯高爾夫球賽。

column1: He's probably carrying enough junk of his own already.
column2: 他自己身上大概已經背了夠多破爛了。

column1: He's too busy flexing to talk.
column2: 他忙著秀肌肉,沒空說話。

column1: Hey! What you think this is, a charity? You ain't gotta the money.
column2: 喂!你當這是在做慈善哪?你錢都不夠。

column1: Hey you! Come over here if you wanta buy something!
column2: 喂,你!想買東西就過來這兒!

column1: Highway to the danger zone.
column2: 通往危險地帶的快車道。

column1: His poetry is very good. I listen to him when he reads in the plaza.
column2: 他的詩寫得很好。他在廣場上吟詩時,我都會去聽。

column1: His rat friend seemed to get nervous whenever we got close to him.
column2: 每次我們一靠近他,他那隻老鼠朋友就顯得很緊張。

column1: Hmm... most interesting. The stars say nothing about you having a sudden illness. I'll have to look into that.
column2: 嗯……真有意思。星象裡可沒提到你會突然得病。我得好好查查。

column1: How do you do, adieu. Good night and don't let the bed bugs bite!
column2: 您好,再會。晚安,別讓臭蟲咬了!

column1: However, any other guard you meet will not be so discreet.
column2: 不過,你遇上的其他衛兵可就沒這麼通融了。

column1: How much would you like to drop?
column2: 你想丟下多少?

column1: How much would you like to store?
column2: 你想存放多少?

column1: I am always out of Ghoul claws for my experiments. I will pay 15 dinars for the claws of a Ghoul.
column2: 我做實驗總缺食屍鬼的爪子。一對食屍鬼爪,我出十五第納爾。

column1: I am always out of Scorpion venom to make poison cure pills. I will pay 20 dinars for the tail of a Scorpion.
column2: 我做解毒藥丸總缺蠍毒。一條蠍尾,我出二十第納爾。

column1: I am an admirer of birds, you see.
column2: 你瞧,我是個愛鳥之人。

column1: I am called Gharib Ajib.
column2: 我叫加里布·阿吉布。

column1: I am called Sadik Isfahani
column2: 我叫薩迪克·伊斯法罕尼。

column1: From Harik you have obtained a quantity of the powder of burning.
column2: 你從哈里克那兒取得了一些燃燒之粉。

column1: General Room Info was dumped to 'alley.txt' file.
column2: 房間概況已匯出至 'alley.txt' 檔。

column1: General Room Info was dumped to 'desert.txt' file.
column2: 房間概況已匯出至 'desert.txt' 檔。

column1: General Room Info was dumped to 'general.txt' file.
column2: 房間概況已匯出至 'general.txt' 檔。

column1: Get back to your feet first, Hero.
column2: 先站穩了再說,英雄。

column1: Give yourself some room, Hero.
column2: 給自己留點餘地,英雄。

column1: Harik would not be pleased if you simply discarded it. You decide to keep the powder of burning.
column2: 你要是隨手把它丟了,哈里克可不會高興。你決定留著這燃燒之粉。

column1: Here is the meal you ordered. I hope you will enjoy it.
column2: 這是您點的餐,希望您吃得盡興。

column1: Hey! You don't have the money for this. What you think this is, a charity? Get some money or get lost.
column2: 喂!你這點錢可買不起。當這是做慈善哪?有錢再來,沒錢就走人。

column1: His shop is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 他的店在「群星之街」(Tarik of Stars)的盡頭。

column1: I am happy you were able to grant her request in time and escape with your life.
column2: 我很高興你及時達成了她的心願,還保住了性命。

column1: I am having a special sale on my boots. All boots come with a free bottle of wax to polish them.
column2: 我的靴子正在特價,每雙都附贈一瓶擦靴蠟。

column1: I am here to serve you.
column2: 我在此為您效勞。

column1: I am honored to aid you.
column2: 能助您一臂之力,是我的榮幸。

column1: I am known as Jabbar bin Ma'amar.
column2: 人稱我賈巴爾·賓·馬阿瑪爾。

column1: I am known as Kuzay bin Bishr.
column2: 人稱我庫宰·賓·比什爾。

column1: I am sorry, but I do not practice much anymore.
column2: 抱歉,我已經很少練了。

column1: I am sorry, Hero, but I cannot afford to give away all of my wares, lest my family starve.
column2: 抱歉,英雄,我可不能把貨全送光,否則一家老小就要挨餓了。

column1: I am sorry, I can spare but the one waterskin, Hero.
column2: 抱歉,英雄,我只能勻出這一個水袋。

column1: I am sorry to hear that you could not help the creature in time. At least it is no longer suffering.
column2: 聽說你沒能及時救那生物,我很遺憾。至少牠不必再受苦了。

column1: I beg you to be careful when you leave the inn today. The thing of fire grows ever more dangerous.
column2: 求你今天出客棧時務必小心。那團火焰之物越來越危險了。

column1: I believe I asked you a question, young man.
column2: 我記得我問了你一個問題,年輕人。

column1: I be needing no more Ghoul heads. If I be putting more on the wall, Simba get plenty frightened.
column2: 我不需要更多食屍鬼的頭了。再往牆上掛,辛巴會嚇得不輕。

column1: I be seeing you later.
column2: 回頭見啦。

column1: I cannot make the potion without all the ingredients.
column2: 材料不齊,我這藥水就調不成。

column1: I do not see strangers who come unbidden.
column2: 不請自來的生人,我概不接見。

column1: I don't have bellows anymore. Someone must have figured they needed them badly enough to steal them.
column2: 我沒風箱了。準是有人覺得非要不可,把它偷走了。

column1: I don't much like nosy little twerps.
column2: 我可不太喜歡愛管閒事的小屁孩。

column1: I fear, Hero, that if I gave away all my dirt then I would have none left with which to pot my plants.
column2: 英雄啊,我怕要是把泥土全送了,就沒剩半點來種我的花草了。

column1: If I may aid you by giving you some earth, I shall be more than happy to do so. Here, take this.
column2: 若能贈你些泥土聊表心意,我求之不得。來,拿去吧。

column1: If it is your wish, I will return it to Omar for you with your regards.
column2: 你若願意,我便替你把它連同問候一併還給奧瑪爾。

column1: If it's time to split...
column2: 若是該「開溜」(分裂)的時候了……

column1: If it were larger, you could sit in the shade.
column2: 它若再大些,你還能坐在陰涼處。

column1: If the time is right, you're in a plight, you can get it across if you give it a toss. Burma Shave.
column2: 時機若對,你陷困境,輕輕一擲,便能解圍。——Burma Shave

column1: If this bag of cloth will but aid you, it shall be yours!
column2: 這只布袋若幫得上你,就歸你了!

column1: If weapons don't harm it, there's very little chance your fists will.
column2: 連武器都傷不了它,你那兩個拳頭就更別指望了。

column1: If you are caught, I will know nothing of your actions, of course.
column2: 你要是被逮,我對你的所作所為自然一概不知。

column1: If you can but walk from one end to the other, I will give you five dinars.
column2: 你只要能從這頭走到那頭,我就給你五第納爾。

column1: If you did that, you would be lost in the dark.
column2: 你要那麼做,就會迷失在黑暗裡。

column1: If you have no further need of me, sir, there are thirsty customers in need of my wares.
column2: 先生,您若沒別的吩咐,還有些口渴的客人等著買我的貨呢。

column1: If you keep doing that, I'm gonna stick something else up there.
column2: 你再這樣下去,我可要往那兒塞點別的東西了。

column1: If you need to know more about the future, visit me again when it arrives.
column2: 你若想多知道些未來的事,等未來到了,再來找我吧。

column1: If you return this to me, I will make you my right-hand man.
column2: 你若把這個還給我,我就讓你當我的左右手。

column1: If you want to clean up this town, start with your heroic task list first!
column2: 你想清理這座城?先從你那份英雄任務清單做起吧!

column1: If you want your spell to reach that spot, you should stand on the other side of the wall, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你若想讓法術打到那個位置,該站到牆的另一邊去。

column1: If you wish to know your fortune, I will need to know your guiding signs.
column2: 你若想知道自己的命運,我得先知道你的主宰星象。

column1: If you wish to talk, you should have the courtesy to sit down.
column2: 你若想談,好歹也該有點禮貌坐下來。

column1: I get my lamps from the brass seller at the bazaar.
column2: 我的燈是跟市集裡賣黃銅的進的。

column1: I grow impatient with your nonsense. Either find a way to make the moon obey you, or you will face my wrath!
column2: 我對你這些胡鬧已經不耐煩了。要嘛想辦法讓月亮聽你的,要嘛承受我的雷霆之怒!

column1: I guess I bleu it. I'd cheddar be gouda for awhile, right?
column2: 看來我「起司」(搞砸)了。我還是「乾酪」(乖乖)躲一陣子比較好,對吧?

column1: I have already given you a lamp, Hero. I fear that I cannot spare another.
column2: 英雄,我已經給過你一盞燈了。恐怕沒法再勻出一盞。

column1: I have been by her house on Sitt Tarik. It has an eye painted upon the door.
column2: 我去過她在 Sitt Tarik 的家,門上畫著一隻眼睛。

column1: I have been named Abd al-Malik.
column2: 我名叫阿卜杜勒-馬利克。

column1: I have been named al-Hajjaj.
column2: 我名叫哈賈吉。

column1: I have been named Badr Basim.
column2: 我名叫巴德爾·巴西姆。

column1: If the vine wasn't withering, that is.
column2: 前提是那藤蔓沒在枯萎啦。

column1: If you wanted one, you'd probably buy a new one.
column2: 你要是想要一個,大概會去買個新的。

column1: I have heard that even now my cousin Sharaf works with the Underground to free Raseir.
column2: 我聽說,我表親沙拉夫至今仍與地下組織合作,要解放拉希爾。

column1: I have heard that it was you that vanquished the Fire Elemental. How wondrously brave you must be.
column2: 我聽說是你擊敗了火元素精靈。你想必勇敢得驚人。

column1: I have made up three potions from what you gave me.
column2: 我用你給的東西調出了三瓶藥水。

column1: I have many pots for sale that are as practical as they are beautiful.
column2: 我有許多陶罐出售,既實用又美觀。

column1: I have no use for an empty purse.
column2: 空錢袋對我沒用。

column1: I haven't got all day. Make it quick.
column2: 我可沒一整天時間,快點。

column1: I have seen a Griffin flying out over the desert. It must have a nest somewhere near here.
column2: 我見過一頭獅鷲飛越沙漠。牠的巢一定就在這附近某處。

column1: I have wonderful clothing and robes fit for an emperor.
column2: 我有上好的衣裳與長袍,連帝王穿了都相稱。

column1: I know this because it was stolen from my very own home the other night!
column2: 我之所以知道,是因為那晚它就是從我自己家裡被偷走的!

column1: I'll take what's behind door number 3.
column2: 我選三號門後面那個。

column1: I'm afraid you do not have enough money for the purchase.
column2: 恐怕你的錢不夠買這個。

column1: I'm a frayed knot.
column2: 我是個磨破的繩結哪(I'm afraid not 的諧音)。

column1: Imagine! Such a warrior's tail used as a playtoy for a baby!
column2: 你想想!這麼一條戰士的尾巴,竟拿來給嬰兒當玩具!

column1: I make the finest swords in the city.
column2: 我打的劍是全城最好的。

column1: I'm not sure either, but I know a way to find out. Care to volunteer?
column2: 我也不確定,不過我有法子查清楚。想自願試試嗎?

column1: I'm sorry, but I can only accept the coins of Shapeir. You will need to first see the Moneychanger.
column2: 抱歉,我只收夏皮爾的錢幣。你得先去找錢莊兌換。

column1: I'm terribly sorry. That doesn't seem to be a proper Quest for Glory I save file.
column2: 非常抱歉,這似乎不是一個有效的《英雄傳奇 I》存檔。

column1: I must know who you are before I can be of service to you. What is your name that I might call you?
column2: 我得先知道你是誰,才能為你效勞。你叫什麼名字,好讓我稱呼你?

column1: In its formerly legible state, it could have informed YOU what's going on.
column2: 它要是還認得出字,本來能告訴「你」這是怎麼回事。

column1: In some games damaged bricks may indicate a secret door, but that's not the case here.
column2: 在有些遊戲裡,破損的磚塊可能暗示著密門,不過這裡並非如此。

column1: In Spielburg, these things weren't so friendly.
column2: 在史畢柏格,這些東西可沒這麼友善。

column1: Instead of dropping the purse, you might try returning it to where you found it.
column2: 與其把錢袋丟了,你不如試著把它放回原處。

column1: In this land, water is life. You'd be wise to hold on to your last waterskin.
column2: 在這片土地上,水就是命。留著你最後一個水袋才是明智之舉。

column1: In this place, you must rely on only your magic.
column2: 在這個地方,你只能仰賴你的魔法。

column1: In your pack is a whirl of the desert Dervish's beard.
column2: 你的包裡有一撮沙漠苦行僧的鬍鬚。

column1: I overheard that lion guy talking about paladins. How can any respectable fighter be a goody two-shoes?
column2: 我聽見那個獅子傢伙在談聖騎士。一個像樣的戰士,怎麼能當個假道學的乖寶寶?

column1: I regret it very much, but I can not help you with that.
column2: 我深感抱歉,但這件事我幫不了你。

column1: I sell bottles of sunscreen for tourists who are not prepared for the desert heat.
column2: 我賣防曬乳,專給沒備好應付沙漠酷熱的遊客。

column1: I sell fabulous second-hand weaponry that is almost as good as new.
column2: 我賣的二手兵器棒極了,幾乎跟新的一樣。

column1: I sell giant purses that can hold up to 500 pieces of currency.
column2: 我賣超大錢袋,最多能裝五百枚錢幣。

column1: I sell oils and ointments that revitalize the skin and make one feel ten years younger.
column2: 我賣油膏,能活化肌膚,讓人感覺年輕十歲。

column1: I sell pottery renowned around all of Shapeir for its sturdiness.
column2: 我賣的陶器以堅固聞名全夏皮爾。

column1: I shall find myself missing Ugarte.
column2: 我會想念烏加特的。

column1: Is it really a basket? Won't know till you ask it.
column2: 這真是個籃子嗎?不問問它,怎會知道呢?

column1: Is that a refusal, Effendi?
column2: 這是拒絕的意思嗎,閣下?

column1: I still need the feather of the Griffin. You need to add the victim's hair at the last moment.
column2: 我還需要那根獅鷲羽毛。受害者的頭髮得由你在最後一刻加進去。

column1: I stock high-quality oil, made from desert snakes. The snakes are especially bred for this purpose.
column2: 我備有上等的油,用沙漠蛇煉成。那些蛇就是專為此目的培育的。

column1: It ain't much, but the snake calls it home.
column2: 雖然簡陋,但那蛇管它叫家。

column1: It appears that not only will he not fight with you, your attacks do him no harm.
column2: 看來他不僅不願跟你動手,你的攻擊對他也毫髮無傷。

column1: It doesn't appear he has time for a quick chat.
column2: 看來他沒空閒聊幾句。

column1: It doesn't appear she has time for a quick chat.
column2: 看來她沒空閒聊幾句。

column1: It doesn't need your support.
column2: 它不需要你的支持。

column1: It drains out your words.
column2: 它把你的話語都吸了進去。

column1: I tell ya, Mel, my Calm spell would help quell your fell itch well. Try it, it's swell!
column2: 我跟你說啊老梅,我這「鎮定」術能好好平息你那要命的癢,試試嘛,棒得很!

column1: It grieves me to the last degree that my knowledge of that subject could never be of any use to you.
column2: 我懂的那點東西對你毫無用處,這讓我難過到了極點。

column1: I thank thee once again. Farewell, friend.
column2: 我再次謝過你。再會了,朋友。

column1: I thank you on behalf of the Guard for saving Shapeir.
column2: 我代表衛隊感謝你拯救了夏皮爾。

column1: It has a vague aura of magic.
column2: 它隱隱透出一絲魔法的氣息。

column1: It has been a pleasure conversing with you. Perhaps we shall share tea again sometime.
column2: 與你交談十分愉快。或許改日我們能再一同品茶。

column1: It has been a pleasure. I'm sorry we didn't converse at greater length.
column2: 很高興認識你。可惜沒能多聊一會兒。

column1: I think I have some pills in stock to cure you of that habit.
column2: 我想我這兒有些藥丸,能治治你那毛病。

column1: It is amazing that anything can survive in the furnace-like desert.
column2: 在這座熔爐似的沙漠裡居然還有東西能活下來,真是驚人。

column1: It is an experience found nowhere but in Shapeir.
column2: 這是唯有在夏皮爾才能有的體驗。

column1: It is a pleasure to see you, great Hero.
column2: 見到您真高興,偉大的英雄。

column1: It is a variety of red Saurus that you never saw in Spielburg.
column2: 這是一種紅色的索魯斯,你在史畢柏格從沒見過。

column1: It is best that you speak to Signor Ferrari about such matters.
column2: 這類事情,你最好去找費拉里先生談。

column1: It is cheap at any price.
column2: 無論開價多少,它都算便宜。

column1: It is foolish to ask that of me.
column2: 拿這種事問我,未免太傻了。

column1: It is good for Simba to watch us practice. He will need to learn soon enough.
column2: 讓辛巴看著我們練習是好事。他很快也得學了。

column1: It is good to see one with manners in Raseir. Thank you for aiding my mistress.
column2: 在拉希爾能見到懂禮數的人,真難得。多謝你相助我家女主人。

column1: It is good to see you, young Hero. A nice day to be adventuring.
column2: 見到你真好,年輕的英雄。今天是個適合冒險的好日子。

column1: It is hoped that my gift will aid you in saving the city, Hero.
column2: 但願我這份禮物能助你拯救這座城市,英雄。

column1: It is most polite to eat restaurant meals while seated at a table.
column2: 在餐館用餐,坐到桌邊吃才最合禮數。

column1: It is my duty to protect this city against monsters that approach it.
column2: 保護這座城市、抵禦來犯的怪物,是我的職責。

column1: It is my pleasure and my purpose to serve the ways of knowledge.
column2: 侍奉知識之道,是我的樂事,也是我的本分。

column1: It is rumored that you wish to journey to Raseir. If you do so, please try to find my cousin Sharaf.
column2: 聽說你想前往拉希爾。你若真去了,請設法找到我表親沙拉夫。

column1: It is said that in Raseir, it is death to break the smallest of laws.
column2: 據說在拉希爾,哪怕違犯最小的律法,也是死罪。

column1: It is said that the duty of the guards of Raseir is to make certain all people obey the many laws.
column2: 據說拉希爾衛兵的職責,就是確保所有人都遵守那一條條律法。

column1: It is very dishonorable of you to try that in my home. It would be wise for you to leave at this time.
column2: 你在我家裡耍這種手段,實在有失體面。你最好現在就離開。

column1: It is very well guarded, and only those whom the Sultan trusts and respects may enter.
column2: 那裡守衛森嚴,只有蘇丹所信任敬重之人才得入內。

column1: It is your good friend, Abdulla Doo.
column2: 是你的好朋友,阿布杜拉·杜。

column1: It is your good friend Shameen.
column2: 是你的好朋友沙明。

column1: It is your good friend Shema.
column2: 是你的好朋友雪瑪。

column1: It'll take hands more skilled than your own to restore this window to its former state.
column2: 要讓這扇窗恢復原貌,得靠比你更巧的手才行。

column1: It'll take more than a calm spell to extinguish these flames.
column2: 光靠一個「鎮定」術,可澆不熄這片火焰。

column1: It looks like the poor man couldn't find an oasis in time.
column2: 看來這可憐人沒能及時找到綠洲。

column1: It may be hip to be square, but you won't look very suave conversing with these.
column2: 方方正正或許挺時髦,但你對著這些東西說話,可顯不出什麼風度。

column1: It may have wings and feathers, but the Griffin is not what you'd normally call a bird!
column2: 牠雖有翅有羽,但獅鷲可不是你一般所說的那種「鳥」!

column1: It remains the property of the Gibson and Phoenix Signs company.
column2: 它仍屬於「吉布森與鳳凰招牌公司」所有。

column1: It reveals a distinct lack of trust. And you can trust me, my good sir.
column2: 這分明是信不過人哪。可你大可信我,我的好先生。

column1: I try to be of help.
column2: 我盡量幫忙。

column1: It's a dead guard.
column2: 是個死掉的衛兵。

column1: It's a door. No doubt about it.
column2: 是扇門。這點毫無疑問。

column1: It's a local townsperson.
column2: 是個本地市民。

column1: It's already on the verge of withering. Uprooting it would kill it.
column2: 它已經快枯萎了,連根拔起只會要了它的命。

column1: It's always a pleasure. I wish I could see more of you.
column2: 每次都很愉快。真希望能多見見你。

column1: It's a magical mass of fire.
column2: 是一團帶著魔法的火焰。

column1: It's a member of the local Katta population.
column2: 是當地卡塔族的一員。

column1: It's an open and shut case: the window won't talk.
column2: 這案子明擺著沒戲:窗戶不肯開口。

column1: It's an original Trial by Fire game box. And it's signed by Sloree and Scoree!
column2: 是一個《烈火神兵》的原版遊戲盒,上頭還有斯洛里和斯科里的簽名!

column1: It's apparent nobody hangs their clothes out in the open anymore.
column2: 顯然再也沒人把衣服晾在外頭了。

column1: It's a rare blue-skinned desert dwelling frog.
column2: 是一種罕見的藍皮膚沙漠青蛙。

column1: It's a simple oil lamp that you nabbed from the Blue Parrot Inn.
column2: 是一盞普通的油燈,你從「藍鸚鵡客棧」順手摸來的。

column1: It's a sublime sign finely designed with care and time.
column2: 這是塊精雕細琢、用心又費時的絕妙招牌。

column1: It's a simple oil lamp you took from the Blue Parrot Inn.
column2: 是一盞普通的油燈,你從「藍鸚鵡客棧」拿來的。

column1: It's a writhing funnel of air. What a windbag!
column2: 是一道扭動翻騰的氣漩。真是個吹牛皮的風袋!

column1: It's best if you just walk carefully.
column2: 你還是小心走路為妙。

column1: It's beyond your carrying capacity.
column2: 這超出你能背負的重量了。

column1: It seems to have no effect on the wall.
column2: 對那面牆似乎毫無作用。

column1: It's impolite to shout. Get closer if you wish to talk to someone.
column2: 大吼大叫不禮貌。想跟人說話就走近些。

column1: It's just a mirage.
column2: 不過是海市蜃樓罷了。

column1: It's just as it appears.
column2: 它就跟看上去的一樣。

column1: It's just some damaged brickwork. Not some secret passage.
column2: 只是些破損的磚砌,不是什麼密道。

column1: It's just your average, everyday magic lamp containing a Fire Elemental.
column2: 就是一盞再平常不過、封著火元素精靈的魔法神燈罷了。

column1: It's like talking to a brick wall.
column2: 簡直像在對一堵磚牆說話。

column1: It's not a chatterbox.
column2: 它又不是個多嘴的傢伙。

column1: It's not a talkasaurus.
column2: 牠又不是「多嘴龍」。

column1: It's not going to help you against the Earth Elemental from inside a chest.
column2: 把它鎖在箱子裡,可幫不了你對付土元素精靈。

column1: It's not intended for expectorating into.
column2: 這東西可不是拿來吐口水的。

column1: It's not much to look at.
column2: 它看起來沒什麼看頭。

column1: It's of no use to you.
column2: 它對你沒有用處。

column1: It's old news, walked on by old shoes. You can do without it.
column2: 陳年舊聞,早被踩爛了。沒它你照樣過得去。

column1: It's one of the Raseirian guards.
column2: 是拉希爾衛兵中的一個。

column1: It sounds as if there's a stream trickling on the other side of the purple door.
column2: 聽起來,紫色門的另一側有一道淙淙流淌的溪水。

column1: It sounds like a family of rats have taken up residence behind this locked door.
column2: 聽起來,有一窩老鼠在這上鎖的門後安了家。

column1: It sounds like a very nice place to live.
column2: 聽起來是個很適合居住的好地方。

column1: It sounds like someone is using the other side of this door as a dartboard.
column2: 聽起來,有人把這門的另一面當成飛鏢靶了。

column1: It's probably government propaganda. You can do without it.
column2: 八成是官方的宣傳。沒它你照樣過得去。

column1: It's really hard to get Khaveen to open up.
column2: 要讓哈維因敞開心扉,可真不容易。

column1: It's Sashanan, the jewelry merchant.
column2: 是賣珠寶的商人薩沙南。

column1: It's the Djinni of the ring.
column2: 是戒指裡的鎮尼。

column1: It's the Poet Omar, and his companion and translator, Ja'Afar.
column2: 是詩人奧瑪爾,還有他的同伴兼翻譯賈法爾。

column1: It's the stall of a Katta merchant who conducts business in the alleyways.
column2: 是一個在巷弄裡做買賣的卡塔族商人的攤子。

column1: It's too bulky to move.
column2: 它太笨重,搬不動。

column1: It's too dangerous around here to search the body right now. More guards could arrive at any moment.
column2: 這附近太危險,現在不宜搜身。隨時可能有更多衛兵趕到。

column1: It's too frail to even put in your pack without damaging it.
column2: 它太脆弱了,連放進包裡都會弄壞。

column1: It's too heavy to carry around.
column2: 它太重了,帶不走。

column1: It's too large to put in your backpack.
column2: 它太大了,塞不進你的背包。

column1: It's too slippery for your Fetch spell.
column2: 它太滑了,你的「取物」術抓不住。

column1: It swings both ways (or at least it used to in its day.)
column2: 它兩邊都能擺動(至少當年是這樣)。

column1: It's your Raseirian visa. Don't leave town without it.
column2: 是你的拉希爾通行證。沒它別出城。

column1: It's your trusty riding Saurus.
column2: 是你那頭可靠的坐騎索魯斯。

column1: It took some effort to win Sharaf's trust. You don't want to make him suspicious of you again.
column2: 好不容易才贏得沙拉夫的信任,你可不想再讓他起疑。

column1: It was cozy enough, but we were saddened to see such a place so dormant and lifeless.
column2: 這地方倒也舒適,只是見它如此沉寂無生氣,著實令人傷感。

column1: It will flame for you because I wish it to.
column2: 它會為你燃燒,因為這是我的意願。

column1: It would be a waste to strip this desert of the few plants that still grow here.
column2: 把這沙漠裡僅存的幾株植物拔光,未免太可惜了。

column1: It would be best to do that under the cover of complete darkness.
column2: 這事最好趁著一片漆黑時動手。

column1: It wouldn't be wise to disturb a snake-charmer in the middle of his song.
column2: 在弄蛇人吹奏到一半時打擾他,可不是明智之舉。

column1: It wouldn't really be wise announcing to the local guard that you are of the profession.
column2: 向本地衛兵宣告你是幹這行的,實在算不上聰明。

column1: It wouldn't survive a trip in your backpack.
column2: 它經不起在你背包裡顛簸。

column1: It would please me if you would come to my dance this evening.
column2: 你今晚若肯來看我跳舞,我會很高興。

column1: I've had better luck trying to buy a glow-in-the-dark turban. I think I'll quit while I'm behind.
column2: 我連買夜光頭巾的運氣都比這好。我看還是趁著沒輸更慘收手吧。

column1: I will ask the questions around here. Learn your place!
column2: 這兒由我來發問。認清你的身分!

column1: I will be honored to put your Saurus back into the stable for you, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,我很榮幸替您把索魯斯牽回棚廄。

column1: I will return the purse to Omar for you, although I do not think he will be happy to see it is now empty.
column2: 我替你把錢袋還給奧瑪爾,不過他見它空了,大概不會高興。

column1: I will return with a splendid meal for you.
column2: 我這就去替您備一頓豐盛的餐點。

column1: I will return with some tea for you.
column2: 我這就去替您沏些茶來。

column1: I wish you to bring me a small ornament to prove your skills.
column2: 我要你帶一件小飾品來,證明你的本事。

column1: I would follow you gladly. But first we must escape this cell.
column2: 我樂意跟隨你。但我們得先逃出這間牢房。

column1: I wouldn't be doing that. You don't know where that lock pick's been.
column2: 我可不會那麼做。誰知道那把開鎖器去過什麼地方。

column1: Ja'Afar does not want your money.
column2: 賈法爾不要你的錢。

column1: Jackalmen are humanoid canines who prefer to travel in packs.
column2: 豺狼人是人形的犬類,慣於成群行動。

column1: Jackalmen travel in packs and prefer attacking things previously wounded.
column2: 豺狼人成群結隊,專挑已經受傷的目標下手。

column1: Judging from those crazy colors, this frog just may be toxic. You decide not to suck on it.
column2: 看那一身瘋狂的顏色,這青蛙八成有毒。你決定還是別舔它。

column1: Just a minute and I'll introduce your Saurus to you.
column2: 稍等一下,我把你的索魯斯介紹給你。

column1: Just before your eyes close for the very last time, you reconsider the wisdom of abstaining from sleep..
column2: 就在你的雙眼最後一次闔上之前,你重新斟酌起「不睡覺」這主意到底明不明智……

column1: Keapon doesn't need the bellows. Besides, you may still need them.
column2: 姬朋用不著這風箱。再說,你說不定還用得上。

column1: Keapon isn't a valid target for spells.
column2: 姬朋不能當法術的目標。

column1: Keapon Laffin sold you one pair of Ali Fakir's Genuine X-Ray Glasses.
column2: 姬朋·拉芬賣給你一副阿里·法基爾的正牌透視眼鏡。

column1: Keapon likes to engage in magical games with visitors every so often. You should ask him about it.
column2: 姬朋偶爾喜歡跟訪客玩玩魔法遊戲。你該問問他這事。

column1: Keep it to yourself.
column2: 這事你自己知道就好。

column1: Keep it with you for now. It may come in handy.
column2: 暫且留在身邊吧,說不定派得上用場。

column1: Khaveen can't hear you from here.
column2: 從這兒哈維因聽不見你。

column1: Kiram is the local clothing merchant.
column2: 基拉姆是本地的服裝商。

column1: Knocking won't help. The door has been nailed shut.
column2: 敲也沒用,這門已經被釘死了。

column1: Konnichiwa! Come and tell me who! Hola! How now, brown cow?
column2: 空你機哇!來告訴我是誰!喔啦!最近如何呀,花斑牛?

column1: Kwa heri, Hero. Come back if you need more information or combat practice.
column2: 再會(Kwa heri),英雄。需要更多消息或想練練身手,再回來。

column1: Lasham fills your empty pot with dirt from one of her flower pots.
column2: 拉珊從她的一個花盆裡舀了些土,把你的空罐填滿。

column1: Leave him a loan, shark.
column2: 放他一馬吧,放高利貸的。

column1: Leave that job to the termites.
column2: 那活兒就交給白蟻去幹吧。

column1: Leave the window where it is. The occupant would appreciate it.
column2: 讓那扇窗留在原處吧,屋主會感激你的。

column1: Let him out of the pen first.
column2: 先把牠放出柵欄。

column1: Let's get this story straight.
column2: 咱們把話說清楚。

column1: Like a lit brass lamp, may you always find your way. Farewell, Effendi.
column2: 願你如一盞點亮的銅燈,永遠找得到方向。再會,閣下。

column1: Looking back at you with joy, Abdulla makes a gesture of approval towards you with his tea cup.
column2: 阿布杜拉欣喜地回望你,舉起茶杯朝你比了個讚許的手勢。

column1: Looks like you need to get some more practice today. You be getting plenty good.
column2: 看來你今天還得多練練。你已經很有長進了。

column1: Magic users are known for their powerful spells and subtle, indirect solutions to problems.
column2: 法師以強大的法術,以及巧妙迂迴的解題之道聞名。

column1: Man who is always polite finds many people to help him.
column2: 待人時時有禮者,自有眾人相助。

column1: Man who keep one hand on weapon have difficulty walking in public.
column2: 手不離兵刃者,難以坦然行於人前。

column1: Man who wrongfully attack peaceful dervish will soon find himself up to his earlobes in wrongful attacks.
column2: 無故攻擊和平苦行僧者,不久便會發覺自己深陷無故的攻擊裡,直沒到耳根。

column1: Master, please hurry! We don't have much time left!
column2: 主人,快點!我們時間不多了!

column1: Master, we don't have time for purposeless spells.
column2: 主人,我們沒空施那些沒用的法術。

column1: Master, we must hurry onward.
column2: 主人,我們得快點趕路。

column1: Maybe these cases will once again decorate the environment. Better let them where they are.
column2: 或許這些箱子有天會再次點綴此地。還是讓它們留在原處吧。

column1: May diamonds light your day, Hero-friend.
column2: 願鑽石照亮你的日子,英雄朋友。

column1: May good fortune favor you, Effendi. Farewell.
column2: 願好運眷顧您,閣下。再會。

column1: May my lamp serve you well.
column2: 願我這盞燈好好為你效力。

column1: May you have a good day today, Effendi. Farewell.
column2: 願您今日順心如意,閣下。再會。

column1: May your day be filled with the scent of sweet flowers, Effendi.
column2: 願您的一天充滿芬芳花香,閣下。

column1: May you stay safe.
column2: 願你平安。

column1: Mayzun pays no attention to you.
column2: 梅尊沒理會你。

column1: Mirak is the local leather merchant.
column2: 米拉克是本地的皮革商。

column1: Most strange. If you have an upset stomach, perhaps you should buy a healing pill.
column2: 真奇怪。你若是腸胃不適,或許該買顆治療藥丸。

column1: Move out from behind the cage where there is more room.
column2: 從籠子後面移到空間大些的地方。

column1: Move out from under the parapet first.
column2: 先從城垛底下移開。

column1: Move out in front of the balcony first.
column2: 先移到陽台前方。

column1: Move up to a pillar first.
column2: 先移到柱子旁。

column1: My collection of perfumes contains fragrances for every occasion you could imagine.
column2: 我收藏的香水,你想得到的任何場合都有對應的香氣。

column1: My dirt should help you defeat the thing of air.
column2: 我的泥土應該能助你擊敗那團風之物。

column1: My name is Abd al-Malik.
column2: 我叫阿卜杜勒-馬利克。

column1: My name is Kadash.
column2: 我叫卡達什。

column1: My name is Kaleema.
column2: 我叫卡莉瑪。

column1: My name is Kareem.
column2: 我叫卡里姆。

column1: My name is Kasheem.
column2: 我叫卡希姆。

column1: My name is Laram.
column2: 我叫拉拉姆。

column1: My name is Laree.
column2: 我叫拉里。

column1: My name is Liram.
column2: 我叫里拉姆。

column1: My name is Mareek.
column2: 我叫馬里克。

column1: My name is Matak.
column2: 我叫馬塔克。

column1: My name is Neko.
column2: 我叫內可。

column1: My name is Sareena.
column2: 我叫薩莉娜。

column1: My name is Sayf al-Maluk.
column2: 我叫賽夫·馬魯克。

column1: My name is Shareem.
column2: 我叫沙里姆。

column1: My name is Sillah.
column2: 我叫希拉。

column1: My name is Sira.
column2: 我叫希拉。

column1: My name is Sonee.
column2: 我叫索尼。

column1: My name is Tarah.
column2: 我叫塔拉。

column1: My name is Tarim.
column2: 我叫塔里姆。

column1: My name is Tirak.
column2: 我叫提拉克。

column1: My name is Tracheema.
column2: 我叫特拉奇瑪。

column1: My pleasure it is to serve you, Effendi.
column2: 能為您效勞是我的榮幸,閣下。

column1: My pots can be used for anything, from carrying water to housing plants.
column2: 我的陶罐什麼都能用,從盛水到種花無所不可。

column1: My spear arm is getting tired of just holding Simba. Good thing for you, too, if we practice today.
column2: 我這持矛的手光抱著辛巴都快累了。今天練一練,對你也是好事。

column1: My sword is called 'Soulforge.' It is a flaming sword.
column2: 我這把劍名叫「鑄魂」。是一把燃燒之劍。

column1: My thanks, but I have no need for gifts.
column2: 謝謝你,但我不需要禮物。

column1: My, you are quite the eloquent and articulate one!
column2: 哎呀,你還真是個能言善道、口齒伶俐的人哪!

column1: Mzuri sana. I accept your challenge. Let's fight.
column2: 很好(Mzuri sana)。我接受你的挑戰。來打吧。

column1: Name contains invalid characters.
column2: 名稱含有無效字元。

column1: Nice try, but you'll have to find a more traditional method of opening her heart.
column2: 想得美,你得用更老派的法子才能打開她的心扉。

column1: Nobody responds to your call.
column2: 沒有人回應你的呼喚。

column1: Nobody seems to be listening.
column2: 似乎沒有人在聽。

column1: No fan game would be complete without one of these.
column2: 少了這個,任何同人遊戲都稱不上完整。

column1: No, Master. You'd only get yourself caught.
column2: 不,主人。你那麼做只會害自己被逮。

column1: No, Master. You don't need to do that now. Let's go.
column2: 不,主人。你現在不必那麼做。我們走吧。

column1: No, Master! You need to keep your feet on the ground.
column2: 不,主人!你得腳踏實地。

column1: No need to blow them dry. The sun can manage quite well on its own.
column2: 不必把它們吹乾,太陽自己就辦得很好。

column1: No need to buy a new purse. It'd be exactly like your old purse, except with less money in it.
column2: 不必買新錢袋。那只會跟你舊的一模一樣,只是裡頭錢更少。

column1: No one hears you knocking.
column2: 沒有人聽見你敲門。

column1: No rest for the weary
column2: 累歸累,可沒得歇。

column1: My name is Sharim.
column2: 我叫沙里姆。

column1: No rest for the weary! Something's after you.
column2: 累歸累,可沒得歇!有東西在追你。

column1: Not now. Get to the pillar.
column2: 現在不行。先到柱子那兒去。

column1: Not right now. Somebody might get hurt.
column2: 現在不行,可能會有人受傷。

column1: Not while you're riding your Saurus.
column2: 你騎著索魯斯時可不行。

column1: No way -- not with your backpack this heavy!
column2: 門兒都沒有——你背包這麼重哪行!

column1: Now, do we have a deal, or is this a deal?
column2: 怎麼樣,這筆買賣成不成?

column1: No, when it's e'rat'icated. What did the rat say as he fell 12,000 feet straight down?
column2: 不,是牠被「鼠」除的時候。那隻老鼠直直墜下一萬兩千呎時,說了什麼?

column1: Now I must get back to my work. If you see something that interests you, let me know.
column2: 我得回去幹活了。你要是看上了什麼,就告訴我。

column1: Now is not a good time to use your map.
column2: 現在不適合看地圖。

column1: Now seems like an excellent time for such a venture.
column2: 現在似乎正是幹這檔事的大好時機。

column1: Now that you've made friends and told tales, it's time to think about escaping.
column2: 如今你已交了朋友、敘過往事,該想想怎麼脫身了。

column1: Now would not be a good time for that.
column2: 現在做那件事不是時候。

column1: Occasional bumps and scratches can be heard from behind this door.
column2: 這門後不時傳來碰撞與抓搔聲。

column1: Of course, such places are hardly suitable for adventurers who wish to be heroes.
column2: 當然,這種地方可不適合一心想當英雄的冒險者。

column1: Oh, a wise guy, eh?
column2: 喔,自作聰明是吧?

column1: Oh, is that a pizza? I have never seen such a perfect one before. Did you make it yourself?
column2: 喔,那是披薩嗎?我從沒見過這麼完美的一個。你親手做的?

column1: Oh, is your pouch deflated? I'm so sorry. Well, I hope you can come again soon.
column2: 喔,你的錢囊癟了?真遺憾。好吧,希望你很快能再來。

column1: Oh, leaving are you? Farewell, then. Be sure to return if you need any of my pills.
column2: 喔,要走了?那就再會吧。需要我的藥丸,記得再來。

column1: Okay. What do you want to know?
column2: 好。你想知道什麼?

column1: Okay. You drop the ceramic container.
column2: 好。你放下了那只陶罐。

column1: Omar does not need that.
column2: 奧瑪爾不需要那個。

column1: Omar frowns at your gesture.
column2: 奧瑪爾對你的舉動皺起了眉。

column1: One of the few living things in the desert.
column2: 是沙漠裡少數的活物之一。

column1: One thing at a time, Hero.
column2: 一次一件事,英雄。

column1: Only a Fresno Raisin could love the blazing orange sun of Shapeir.
column2: 也只有弗雷斯諾的葡萄乾,才會愛夏皮爾那輪炙熱的橘色太陽。

column1: Only a Shapeirian sunset beats my jewelry as far as beauty and splendor are concerned.
column2: 論美麗與華彩,唯有夏皮爾的落日能勝過我的珠寶。

column1: On the opposite side, you hear deep and heavy shuffling and dragging sounds.
column2: 另一側傳來沉重的拖曳與挪動聲。

column1: On the other hand, where did you pick these up again?
column2: 話說回來,你這些又是打哪兒撿來的?

column1: On the other side of the door, heavy breathing and crying are audible.
column2: 門的另一側,聽得見沉重的喘息與哭泣。

column1: On this shelf are various examples of stuff, things, and megusalems.
column2: 這架子上擺著各式各樣的玩意兒、東西,還有梅古撒冷。

column1: Opening up nose like opening out heart. Keep a tissue handy for both.
column2: 敞開鼻孔猶如敞開心扉。兩者都記得備條手帕。

column1: Other than its shape, there's nothing special about this rock.
column2: 除了形狀,這塊石頭沒什麼特別的。

column1: Ouch! Why do you treat yourself so inappropriately?
column2: 哎喲!你幹嘛這樣折騰自己?

column1: Our lizard jerky rations are very cheap.
column2: 我們的蜥蜴肉乾乾糧很便宜。

column1: Our special blend of preservatives lets the rich, full-bodied lizard flavor come through.
column2: 我們特調的防腐配方,能把那濃郁醇厚的蜥蜴風味完整保留下來。

column1: People will think you've gone fruity!
column2: 人家會以為你發瘋了!

column1: Perhaps it would be best, Effendi, if you returned it to Omar himself.
column2: 閣下,或許最好還是由您親自把它還給奧瑪爾。

column1: Perhaps it would be wise to just hide under a rock for a few days or so.
column2: 或許躲到石頭底下避個幾天,才是明智之舉。

column1: Perhaps you should return when you have learned your name and some manners.
column2: 等你弄清自己的名字、學會點規矩,再回來吧。

column1: Permit me to loan you my sword, 'Soulforge.'
column2: 請容我把我的劍「鑄魂」借給你。

column1: Picking nose like picking fight. Both leave hands dirty.
column2: 挖鼻孔猶如挑釁打架,兩者都弄髒了手。

column1: Picking your nose like picking your underwear. Better without audience.
column2: 挖鼻孔猶如挑內褲,沒有觀眾時做比較好。

column1: Pills to magically restore your health.
column2: 以魔法恢復生命力的藥丸。

column1: Pills to magically restore your spell-casting abilities.
column2: 以魔法恢復施法能力的藥丸。

column1: Pills to magically restore your stamina.
column2: 以魔法恢復耐力的藥丸。

column1: Pinned to the Guild wall was a poster requesting a hero for the realm of Spielburg to the west.
column2: 公會牆上釘著一張海報,為西邊的史畢柏格之地徵求一名英雄。

column1: Pity. Ah, well, there will be others. Good day, sir.
column2: 可惜。唉,算了,還會有別人的。再會,先生。

column1: Plants thrive beneath this residential window. A decorative awning provides shade from the harsh sun.
column2: 這扇民宅窗下花草繁茂,一張裝飾用的遮篷擋去了烈日。

column1: Plaza of the Fighters East
column2: 戰士廣場 東

column1: Plaza of the Fighters West
column2: 戰士廣場 西

column1: Plaza of the Fountain North
column2: 噴泉廣場 北

column1: Plaza of the Fountain South
column2: 噴泉廣場 南

column1: Plaza of the Palace
column2: 王宮廣場

column1: Please don't leave. I need your help.
column2: 請別走。我需要你的幫助。

column1: Please, Effendi. You must come inside quickly before the guards notice us.
column2: 拜託,閣下。趁衛兵還沒注意到我們,您得趕快進來。

column1: Please keep it with you for as long as you are our guest.
column2: 只要你還是我們的客人,就請隨身帶著它。

column1: Please post all queries and comments on our forums at www.AGDInteractive.com.
column2: 一切疑問與意見,請發表於我們的論壇 www.AGDInteractive.com。

column1: Please select a game to delete.
column2: 請選擇要刪除的存檔。

column1: Please stand up first to avoid issues when returning to the current room after battle is over.
column2: 請先站起來,以免戰鬥結束、回到當前房間時出狀況。

column1: Please tell me what happens when you use it. I'm sorry I can't be there, but I have much work to do.
column2: 請告訴我你用它時發生了什麼。抱歉我不能在場,實在有太多活兒要忙。

column1: Please type a longer description.
column2: 請輸入較長的描述。

column1: Please type a longer name.
column2: 請輸入較長的名稱。

column1: Press the key you wish to assign to this action.
column2: 請按下你想指派給此動作的按鍵。

column1: Pretty jewelry and amulets hang on the stand.
column2: 架上掛著精美的珠寶與護身符。

column1: Q. How do you get down off a Saurus?
column2: 問:你要怎麼從索魯斯身上「下」來?

column1: Quiet. Someone might hear you.
column2: 安靜。可能會被人聽見。

column1: No one seems to be listening.
column2: 似乎沒有人在聽。

column1: Quit blubbering and DO something.
column2: 別再哭哭啼啼了,倒是做點什麼啊。

column1: Rakeesh bears a grim expression, and there's a hint of shame on his face.
column2: 拉基什神情嚴峻,臉上隱隱透著一絲羞愧。

column1: Rakeesh' condition seems to have improved since yesterday, but his mood seems unchanged.
column2: 拉基什的傷勢看來比昨天好些了,心情卻似乎沒什麼變化。

column1: Rakeesh frowns and you realize that sparring will be difficult if you don't return his sword first.
column2: 拉基什皺起眉,你意識到不先把劍還他,這架是切磋不成的。

column1: Rakeesh has trouble keeping body balanced when getting hit. If you create opening, exploit it.
column2: 拉基什挨打時不易穩住身形。你若製造出破綻,就好好把握。

column1: Rakeesh just frowns at you. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 拉基什只是朝你皺眉。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: Rakeesh looks completely recovered from his battle a few days back and seems to be in quite high spirits.
column2: 拉基什看來已從幾天前那場戰鬥中完全康復,精神還相當不錯。

column1: Rakeesh relaxes, but his tail stays ever alert.
column2: 拉基什放鬆了下來,那條尾巴卻始終保持警戒。

column1: Rakeesh sits calmly nearby. The Liontaur seems well-accustomed to this room.
column2: 拉基什在一旁安然端坐。這獅人似乎對這房間很是熟悉。

column1: Rakeesh wouldn't have loaned it to you if he knew you'd be careless with it.
column2: 拉基什要是早知道你會這麼粗心,根本不會把它借給你。

column1: Raseir needs your urgent attention. You don't have time to sleep your valuable days away in Shapeir.
column2: 拉希爾急需你關注。你可沒空在夏皮爾睡掉這些寶貴的日子。

column1: Rather than taking full responsibility for a possible burglary, you decide to hold on to the key.
column2: 與其為一樁可能的竊案扛下全責,你決定還是把鑰匙留著。

column1: Remember to have the victim drink the potion in order to break the spell.
column2: 記得讓受害者喝下藥水,才能解除咒語。

column1: Right. At any rate, Wizard-Hero, good luck and farewell!
column2: 好。總之,巫師英雄,祝你好運,再會了!

column1: Roget the Saurus, is at a loss for words.
column2: 「羅傑詞」索魯斯,一時語塞,說不出話來。

column1: Run a local script here.
column2: 在此執行本地腳本。

column1: Run around the balcony? If you were down there, you might try running.
column2: 繞著陽台跑?你要是在下頭,倒可以試著跑跑。

column1: Salt already has everything he needs in his own workshop.
column2: 索特在自己的工坊裡,要的東西一應俱全。

column1: Scoree sometimes gets belly aches when he eats too much Saurus jerky.
column2: 斯科里有時蜥蜴肉乾吃太多會鬧肚子。

column1: Scorpion tails look as venomous as they are.
column2: 蠍尾看起來有多毒,實際就有多毒。

column1: Select a game to restore:
column2: 選擇要讀取的存檔:

column1: Shall we shake hands on forty dinars for the Saurus?
column2: 這頭索魯斯,四十第納爾,咱們握手成交如何?

column1: Shameen gave you the key to the front door of the Katta's Tail Inn.
column2: 沙明把「卡塔之尾客棧」前門的鑰匙交給了你。

column1: Shameen looks at you and just scratches his head. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 沙明看著你,只是搔了搔頭。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: Shameen spends some time making you familiar with the words that make up the names of Shapeir's streets.
column2: 沙明花了些時間,讓你熟悉組成夏皮爾街名的那些字詞。

column1: Shapeir is safe for the time being. Concentrate your efforts on Raseir instead.
column2: 夏皮爾暫時安全了。把心力轉到拉希爾去吧。

column1: Shema has given you a spare suit of clothes for overnight trips, sales meetings, etc.
column2: 雪瑪給了你一套備用衣裳,供你過夜出行、洽談生意等場合穿。

column1: Shema spends some time explaining the names of the various streets to you.
column2: 雪瑪花了些時間,向你解釋各條街道的名稱。

column1: Shema will be with you shortly with one of her splendid meals.
column2: 雪瑪很快就會端著她那豐盛的餐點來招呼你。

column1: Shema will be with you shortly with some of our wonderful tea.
column2: 雪瑪很快就會端著我們上好的茶來招呼你。

column1: She too will forever have our gratitude for allowing us to return home.
column2: 她讓我們得以重返家園,我們同樣永遠感念。

column1: Show me something worth bargaining for.
column2: 拿點值得我討價還價的東西出來瞧瞧。

column1: Sign here! No, sign there! On second thought, don't sign at all.
column2: 在這兒簽!不,在那兒簽!再想想——還是別簽了。

column1: Silence, infidel. Listen and pay attention or we will use force.
column2: 住口,異教徒。給我聽好,否則別怪我們動粗。

column1: Silly you! They're right there on the end of your nose!
column2: 你這傻瓜!它們不就在你鼻尖上嗎!

column1: Simba here is more like a lion. He is having a wonderful time.
column2: 這位辛巴倒更像頭獅子。他正玩得開心呢。

column1: Since you haven't received any service from Salt, there's no need to pay him for anything.
column2: 你還沒接受索特任何服務,沒必要付他半文錢。

column1: Since your need is great, this waterskin shall be a gift.
column2: 既然你急需,這個水袋就送你了。

column1: Sloree is the local meat merchant.
column2: 斯洛里是本地的肉販。

column1: Sneak around the balcony? If you were down there, you might try sneaking.
column2: 繞著陽台潛行?你要是在下頭,倒可以試著潛行。

column1: So, Hero of Spielburg, you think you want to go practice some? I could use workout.
column2: 怎麼樣,史畢柏格的英雄,想去練練嗎?我也正好需要活動活動。

column1: Someone behind this door is repeatedly saying 'Ma fhimt.' You don't understand.
column2: 這門後有人反覆說著「Ma fhimt」。你聽不懂。

column1: Someone is jiggling the handle on the other side of this locked door.
column2: 有人在這上鎖的門另一側,不停搖晃著門把。

column1: Someone is whistling a familiar tune in an odd key behind this door.
column2: 這門後,有人用古怪的調子吹著一首耳熟的曲子。

column1: Someone (or something) behind this door is thumping something as heavy as a pumpkin.
column2: 這門後有人(或某種東西)正在敲打一件南瓜般沉重的物事。

column1: Someone probably lives behind this door.
column2: 這門後大概住著人。

column1: Someone with your skills can do without my powder of burning.
column2: 憑你的本事,沒我這燃燒之粉也行。

column1: Something in the back of your mind tells you that you may need this magical earth at a later date.
column2: 你腦海深處有個聲音告訴你,日後或許用得上這魔法泥土。

column1: Sorry, boss, all I got is a buncha burnt wood.
column2: 抱歉啦,老大,我這兒只有一堆燒焦的木頭。

column1: Sorry, Master, but we must get Iblis first.
column2: 抱歉,主人,但我們得先解決伊布利斯。

column1: Sorry mister, don't have any spare parts for that model lying around. It'll take some time to order them.
column2: 抱歉先生,那型號的備件我這兒沒現貨,得花些時間訂貨。

column1: Sorry, my little Hero, but you just don't have the cash. You'll have to pay up to play with me.
column2: 抱歉啦,我的小英雄,你錢就是不夠。想跟我玩,得先付錢。

column1: Sorry, not right now.
column2: 抱歉,現在不行。

column1: Sounds like there's a whole lot of nothing going on behind this door.
column2: 聽起來這門後啥事也沒有。

column1: Sounds like work going on behind this door.
column2: 聽起來這門後有人在幹活。

column1: So what do you say, shall we shake hands and call it a deal?
column2: 怎麼樣,咱們握個手,這買賣就算成交了?

column1: Speak now the name of FLOWER!
column2: 現在,說出「花」的名字!

column1: Speak the name of power:
column2: 說出力量之名:

column1: Stick to dealing with the kind of trash you're used to. Monsters, murderers, and madmen.
column2: 還是去對付你慣常那些渣滓吧——怪物、殺人犯,還有瘋子。

column1: Strong boots with soles reinforced with Mirak's toughest leather.
column2: 結實的靴子,鞋底用米拉克最堅韌的皮革加固過。

column1: Such a shame that we could not make a deal. The pin was truly perfect for you.
column2: 這筆買賣沒成,真是可惜。那根別針真是再適合你不過了。

column1: Such a thing is not nearly so grand as the Fire Elemental.
column2: 這種東西可遠遠及不上火元素精靈那般了得。

column1: Such a thing was done with pleasure, Hero, and needs no mention.
column2: 舉手之勞,英雄,何足掛齒。

column1: Suddenly, you feel much weaker than before.
column2: 突然間,你覺得比先前虛弱了許多。

column1: Sure, buddy! What do you want to know?
column2: 當然啦,老兄!你想知道什麼?

column1: Surely an adventurer gets hungry at times.
column2: 冒險者總有餓肚子的時候吧。

column1: Surely but a small amount for such a treat.
column2: 這等美味,這點錢算什麼。

column1: Swinging from them might be fun... if you were in a jungle.
column2: 抓著它們盪來盪去或許挺有趣……前提是你身在叢林裡。

column1: Take a drink from the fountain, Hero-friend. It will do no end of good.
column2: 從噴泉喝口水吧,英雄朋友。好處說不完。

column1: Take one pill with water and get plenty of rest.
column2: 配水服一顆藥丸,再好好休息。

column1: Taking quick stock of your condition, you find the reason -- you're dead. Better aim elsewhere next time.
column2: 你迅速盤點了一下自己的狀況,找出了原因——你死了。下次最好瞄準別處。

column1: Tarik of Rafir[(Street of the Guard)
column2: 拉菲爾之街[(衛兵之街)

column1: Tell me about your past and the things you have done.
column2: 跟我說說你的過往,還有你做過的事。

column1: Thank you, a thousand thank-yous. It is with your support that I may continue my vital work
column2: 謝謝你,千恩萬謝。全靠你的支持,我才能繼續這要緊的工作。

column1: Thank you, but I have no need for such things.
column2: 謝謝你,但我用不著這些東西。

column1: Thank you, I shall put them somewhere suitable.
column2: 謝謝你,我會把它們妥善安置。

column1: Thank you. Perhaps someday you'll earn the right to wield such a sword permanently.
column2: 謝謝你。或許有朝一日,你能掙得永久持有這把劍的資格。

column1: Thank you, thank you, Effendi. You shame me with your kindness.
column2: 謝謝您,謝謝您,閣下。您的善意讓我受之有愧。

column1: Thank you. They are most beautiful.
column2: 謝謝你,它們美極了。

column1: Thank you very much. That is most thoughtful of you.
column2: 非常感謝。你真是體貼。

column1: That feels good; just what you needed to relieve some of the aches and pains of everyday adventuring.
column2: 這感覺真好,正好替你舒緩了日常冒險帶來的痠痛。
