column1: You put the map inside the chest. Nothing beats the experience of getting through a maze of alleyways using nothing but your intuition, after all.
column2: 你把地圖放進箱子。畢竟,單憑直覺穿越迷宮般的巷弄,那種體驗無可比擬。

column1: Your character has been imported as a magic user. If you do not feel this is the correct profession, please indicate your preference below...
column2: 你的角色已匯入為法師。你若覺得這職業不對,請於下方指明你的偏好……

column1: You realize that you don't have 50 centimes to pay the stable and board fee. You tell the guard you'll pay him as soon as you get some loose change.
column2: 你發覺自己連付馬廄草料費的五十生丁都拿不出來。你告訴衛兵,一有零錢就馬上付他。

column1: Your leather armor is made of leather boiled in wax, a process which preserves and strengthens the material. As a side effect, there is a certain amount of olfactory discomfort in warm climates.
column2: 你的皮甲是用蠟煮過的皮革製成,這道工序能保養並強化材質。副作用是:在溫暖的氣候下,會有那麼點氣味上的不適。

column1: Your Open spell parts the drapes, revealing your presence. The occupants aren't happy to discover a thief in their house and opt to demonstrate the extent of their unhappiness to you physically.
column2: 你的「開啟」術撥開了帷幔,暴露了你的行蹤。屋裡的人發現家中有賊,很不高興,並決定用拳腳讓你見識他們有多不高興。

column1: Your Thieves' Toolkit contains a variety of slim, sharp instruments. In the right hands, these implements will open any locked (but unbarred) door.
column2: 你的盜賊工具裡有各式纖細鋒利的器具。在行家手中,這些傢伙能打開任何上鎖(但沒閂)的門。

column1: Your waterskin is getting much easier to carry. Unfortunately, that probably means you don't have much water left.
column2: 你的水袋越來越好提了。可惜,這多半表示你的水所剩無幾。

column1: You see a Katta musician playing some sort of musical instrument and charming a dangerous-looking snake. There are some brass coins thrown into his bowl.
column2: 你看見一個卡塔族樂師奏著某種樂器,逗弄著一條看來危險的蛇。他的缽裡丟著幾枚銅幣。

column1: You see Tiram rubbing his eyes and then continuing to glance at you. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 你看見提拉姆揉了揉眼,接著又不時瞄你。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: You sense a feeling of purity and honesty from the enchantress which makes you feel she would not associate with, nor approve of, thieves.
column2: 你從那女術士身上感受到一股純淨與誠實的氣息,讓你覺得她不會與盜賊往來,也不會認同他們。

column1: You should hold onto that. You'll need it to prove ownership and registration of your Saurus.
column2: 那個你該留著。你需要它來證明索魯斯的所有權與登記。

column1: You should now return to Shapeir. They really need a real wizard-hero like you there. And, remember, 'Those who can, do.'
column2: 你現在該回夏皮爾了。那兒真的很需要像你這樣一位真正的巫師英雄。還有,記住:「能者,行之。」

column1: You should probably get some rest while Shameen and I make some final preparations for tomorrow. We will be sure to wake you up in the morning.
column2: 你大概該歇一歇,沙明和我來為明天做最後的準備。早上我們一定會叫醒你。

column1: You show Harik a flask of undead unguent you purchased in Spielburg and ask him if he'd like to analyse it for his experiments.
column2: 你拿出一瓶在史畢柏格買的驅亡靈藥膏給哈里克看,問他想不想拿去做實驗分析。

column1: You spend an hour "browsing" the shop. You feel a lot better after the hour's rest, but Issur seems crankier than ever.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭「逛」店。歇了這一個鐘頭你舒坦多了,伊蘇卻似乎比平常更不耐煩了。

column1: You spend an hour listening to stories about constellations you didn't even know to exist.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,聽人說起你連存在都不知道的那些星座的故事。

column1: You spend a short time making small talk with your friends while sharing Shema's delicious herbal tea.
column2: 你花了點時間和朋友閒話家常,一邊分享著雪瑪美味的花草茶。

column1: You stare at the awning and find yourself yawning -- maybe you woke up too early this morning.
column2: 你盯著那遮篷,卻打起了哈欠——也許是今早醒得太早了。

column1: You take off the weighty chainmail armor and store it in the chest. Whew, that thing sure was heavy!
column2: 你脫下那身沉重的鎖子甲,收進箱子。呼,那玩意兒可真重!

column1: You take the pill, but it doesn't seem to have any effect. Next time, follow the prescription.
column2: 你服下藥丸,卻似乎毫無作用。下次,照處方來。

column1: You tear open the game box. What's this? You find a complex map depicting Shapeir's alley system inside.
column2: 你拆開遊戲盒。這是什麼?你在裡頭發現一張描繪夏皮爾巷弄系統的複雜地圖。

column1: You tell about the enchanted meadow and the spell of protection surrounding the town of Spielburg.
column2: 你說起那片受魔法庇佑的草原,以及環繞史畢柏格鎮的那道防護咒。

column1: You tell about your arrival in Spielburg and how you were welcomed by Sheriff Meistersson.
column2: 你說起自己抵達史畢柏格,以及邁斯特森郡長如何歡迎你。

column1: You tell about your encounter with Baba Yaga and how she forced you to bring her a mandrake root.
column2: 你說起自己與芭芭雅嘎的遭遇,以及她如何逼你為她取來一根曼德拉草根。

column1: You tell Harik that you vanquished the Earth Elemental and that he may have the remaining powder back.
column2: 你告訴哈里克,你已擊敗土元素精靈,剩下的粉末他可以收回去。

column1: You tell Shameen and Shema about how you turned the Brigand leader back into Elsa von Spielburg.
column2: 你向沙明和雪瑪說起,你如何把強盜頭子變回艾莎·馮·史畢柏格。

column1: You tell Shameen and Shema about the Famous Adventurer's Correspondence School and how you used the information from those books to develop the skills of an adventurer.
column2: 你向沙明和雪瑪說起「著名冒險家函授學校」,以及你如何靠那些書裡的知識,練就一身冒險者的本領。

column1: You tell Shameen and Shema about the graveyard near Spielburg where you picked the root of the mandrake plant and how it was haunted by spirits at night.
column2: 你向沙明和雪瑪說起史畢柏格附近那座墓地,你在那兒採了曼德拉草根,而那地方一到夜裡就鬧鬼。

column1: You tell Shema she probably wouldn't believe you if you told her where you got it from, but that you will offer a slice to her and Shameen.
column2: 你告訴雪瑪,你要是說出這東西的來歷,她大概不會信,但你還是願意分一片給她和沙明。

column1: You tell your friends about the many fearsome monsters you encountered in the forest of Spielburg.
column2: 你向朋友們說起你在史畢柏格森林裡遇上的眾多可怕怪物。

column1: You tell your Katta friends about the Brigand warlock, who turned out to be the Baron's jester, Yorick.
column2: 你向你的卡塔族朋友說起那個強盜術士,他到頭來竟是男爵的弄臣約里克。

column1: You think about it, and then think better about it. You don't really want to get the guy upset. You'd probably wind up with ants in your pants, egg on your face, and pie in your eye.
column2: 你考慮了一下,還是打消了念頭。你並不真想惹惱那傢伙。否則你大概會落得個坐立難安、顏面盡失、自討沒趣的下場。

column1: You think about shaking hands with him, but then you become aware of the faint smell of tabasco sauce, gun powder, and mule excrement on his palms. You don't know where this guy has been and you don't think you want to know either.
column2: 你想跟他握手,卻聞到他掌心那股淡淡的辣椒醬、火藥和騾糞味。你不知道這傢伙都去過哪些地方,而且你想你也不想知道。

column1: You think maybe you go visit the Enchantress, huh? Well, go north from the Plaza of the Fountain. When you come to a place where you can go south, go thatta way.
column2: 你大概是想去拜訪那位女術士吧?這樣啊,從噴泉廣場往北走。走到能往南的地方,就往那邊去。

column1: You think twice about eating more than one meal at a sitting. Bicarbonate of soda hasn't been invented yet.
column2: 一頓吃兩餐?你還是再想想吧。小蘇打可還沒發明呢。

column1: You touch the palm of your right hand to your left shoulder, extend the third digit of your left hand until the pad of the finger touches the underside of your right eye, touch your left toe to your left wrist, and twiddle your thumbs furiously.
column2: 你以右手掌心貼住左肩,伸出左手中指,直到指腹觸及右眼下方,再讓左腳趾碰著左手腕,然後拚命轉動兩根拇指。

column1: You uncurled me some whirl from a Dervish? Oh, frabjous day, hoorah, hooray! Here's the dinars that I must pay.
column2: 你替我從苦行僧身上捲來了一陣旋風?喔,歡欣之日,呼啦,萬歲!這是我該付的第納爾。

column1: You've never been comfortable fighting with a shield and dagger, so you store your shield as well.
column2: 你向來不慣盾與匕首並用,於是把盾牌也一併收了起來。

column1: You've never been comfortable fighting with a shield and dagger, so you take your sword as well.
column2: 你向來不慣盾與匕首並用,於是把劍也一併取了出來。

column1: You walk the hallowed halls of the ivory towers of WIT and devote the next twenty years of your life to learning. You become one of the greatest scholars of all time upon the subject of 'Scrying Scrolls.'
column2: 你漫步於巫師技術學院象牙塔的神聖殿堂,將此後二十年的人生奉獻給學問。你成了「占卜卷軸」這門學科有史以來最偉大的學者之一。

column1: You will find things of interest there, but know that there are those who sleep in the next room. Be wary and, if you are very good, we will both share your profits.
column2: 你會在那兒找到有意思的東西,但要知道,隔壁房裡有人睡著。多加小心;你若夠俐落,所得我倆便平分。

column1: You will need to remember to add a freshly collected sample of the victim's hair before the victim drinks it.
column2: 你得記住,在受害者喝下之前,加入一撮新採集的受害者頭髮。

column1: You wouldn't stand a chance against him with your bare hands. Try getting your hands on a weapon.
column2: 赤手空拳,你根本不是他的對手。設法弄把兵器吧。

column1: ZAP: Places a magical charge on your weapon which will inflict some damage in combat.[[CALM: Pacifies your opponent for a short time.[[DAZZLE: Blinds your opponent for a short time.[[DETECT MAGIC: Tells you whether there is any magic present in your current surroundings.[[FETCH: Used to fetch small items from a distance.[[FLAME DART: An offensive fireball used in combat.
column2: 電擊:在你的兵器上施加魔法電荷,於戰鬥中造成些許傷害。[[鎮定:短時間內安撫你的對手。[[炫目:短時間內使你的對手目盲。[[偵測魔法:告訴你當前四周是否有魔法存在。[[取物:用來從遠處取來小型物品。[[火鏢:戰鬥中使用的攻擊性火球。

column1: Zayishah seems slightly bothered by your gesture. Perhaps this isn't the best time for that.
column2: 札伊莎似乎被你的舉動弄得有點不快。也許現在不是做那事的最佳時機。

column1: You should dismount first.
column2: 你該先下坐騎。

column1: You should exchange your money to Shapeirian currency first.
column2: 你該先把錢換成夏皮爾的貨幣。

column1: You should first finish that which is on your table, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你該先把桌上的東西吃完。

column1: You should get rid of your pursuer first.
column2: 你該先甩掉追你的人。

column1: You should get up first.
column2: 你該先起身。

column1: You should give that to Omar.
column2: 你該把那個給奧瑪爾。

column1: You should not be doing that in front of Simba. Bad example.
column2: 你不該在辛巴面前那麼做,壞榜樣。

column1: You should practice your lock-picking on a different door.
column2: 你該換一扇門練開鎖。

column1: You should stand up first.
column2: 你該先站起來。

column1: You should try that on a different door.
column2: 你該換一扇門試試。

column1: You should wait until the guard is closer.
column2: 你該等衛兵再靠近些。

column1: You should wait until there are no guards around.
column2: 你該等附近沒衛兵時再動手。

column1: You should wait until your Saurus has stopped walking.
column2: 你該等你的索魯斯停下腳步。

column1: You show great honor. May your deeds continue to shine.
column2: 你展現了崇高的榮譽。願你的事蹟持續閃耀。

column1: You take an oil-filled lamp.
column2: 你拿了一盞盛滿油的燈。

column1: You take off the glasses and put them away.
column2: 你摘下眼鏡收了起來。

column1: You take the dagger.
column2: 你拿走了那把匕首。

column1: You take the money and put it away.
column2: 你拿了錢收了起來。

column1: You take the three potion bottles and put them away.
column2: 你拿了那三瓶藥水收了起來。

column1: You take your leather jerkin off and store it.
column2: 你脫下皮背心收了起來。

column1: You think twice about going out completely unarmed.
column2: 完全不帶武器就出門?你還是再想想吧。

column1: You trash the trashcan.
column2: 你把那垃圾桶扔了。

column1: You've already got a drink.
column2: 你已經有酒喝了。

column1: You've just poured some water on yourself. You're still wet.
column2: 你才剛往自己身上澆了水。你還濕著呢。

column1: You've mopped the floor with Sweeping Sir James.
column2: 你把「橫掃詹姆斯爵士」打得落花流水。

column1: You want fries with that?
column2: 要不要再來份薯條?

column1: You will find it most filling.
column2: 你會覺得它很管飽。

column1: You will need to buy a map first.
column2: 你得先買張地圖。

column1: You will only succeed in attracting more guards.
column2: 你只會招來更多衛兵。

column1: Your weapon is now magically charged.
column2: 你的兵器現在帶著魔法電荷了。

column1: Your waterskin can't squirt that far.
column2: 你的水袋噴不了那麼遠。

column1: You see nothing of interest there.
column2: 那兒沒什麼有意思的東西。

column1: You see nothing that would be useful to you.
column2: 你沒看見什麼對你有用的東西。

column1: You see nothing that you wish to bargain for.
column2: 你沒看見什麼想討價還價的東西。

column1: You sense a great deal of magic coming from the tree.
column2: 你感覺到那棵樹散發出大量的魔法。

column1: You see a charming young Katta with a smile for you.
column2: 你看見一個迷人的年輕卡塔族人,對你綻出笑容。

column1: You spend an hour flirting with Dinarzad.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭跟迪娜扎德調情。

column1: You spend an hour discussing Shapeirian politics with Aziza.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭跟阿姿莎討論夏皮爾的政局。

column1: You spend an hour waiting in the shadows.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭在暗處等候。

column1: You spend an hour watching the Saurii run around.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭看那些索魯斯四處奔跑。

column1: You spend an hour being the butt of Keapon's jokes.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭當姬朋笑話裡的笑柄。

column1: You should be so lucky. Lucky, lucky, lucky...
column2: 你哪有那麼好命。好命,好命,真好命……

column1: Zap: boost attack once
column2: 電擊:單次強化攻擊

column1: Magic, magic everywhere. Find it at Aziza's lair. Magic, magic, wait a bit. Find it in the soul of WIT. Magic, magic in the stars. The Astrologer can see that far.
column2: 魔法魔法,無處不在。阿姿莎的居所裡尋它。魔法魔法,且慢些。巫師技術學院的魂魄裡尋它。魔法魔法,藏於星辰。占星師看得那般遠。

column1: The soles of your boots show signs of heavy deterioration already.
column2: 你的靴底已顯出嚴重磨損的跡象。

column1: The window doesn't open up to you.
column2: 那扇窗不為你而開。

column1: This local reed basket is simple but elegant.
column2: 這個當地的蘆葦籃簡樸卻雅致。

column1: This 'painting' shows the city of Shapeir under a starry sky.
column2: 這幅「畫」描繪著星空下的夏皮爾城。

column1: Who do you call upon to be your sponsor?
column2: 你要請誰當你的引薦人?

column1: With fiery emotion, you explain to Harik that the Earth Elemental is attacking the city.
column2: 你情緒激昂地向哈里克說明,土元素精靈正在攻擊這座城市。

column1: You already took a feather, so don't be greedy.
column2: 你已經拿走一根羽毛了,別貪心。

column1: You are carrying a large cloth bag.
column2: 你帶著一個大布袋。

column1: You are most welcome.
column2: 不客氣。

column1: You awaken at dawn.
column2: 你在破曉時醒來。

column1: You awaken at nightfall.
column2: 你在入夜時醒來。

column1: You drop the blue frog.
column2: 你放下了那隻藍青蛙。

column1: You drop the lamp.
column2: 你放下了那盞燈。

column1: You hear a faint giggle and notice the flower merchant quickly looks away when you glance in her direction. Maybe you shouldn't be picking your nose in public.
column2: 你聽見一聲輕笑,留意到你朝賣花女望去時,她連忙別開了臉。也許你不該在大庭廣眾下挖鼻孔。

column1: You pick up the loose dagger.
column2: 你撿起那把散落的匕首。

column1: You're more likely to make it out alive if you don't interfere with the guard.
column2: 你要是不去招惹那衛兵,活著脫身的機會還大些。

column1: You retrieve the basket.
column2: 你取回那個籃子。

column1: You retrieve the note.
column2: 你取回那張字條。

column1: You retrieve the waterskin.
column2: 你取回那個水袋。

column1: Your waterskins are getting very light -- better find some water soon.
column2: 你的水袋變得很輕了——最好快點找些水。

column1: Your words are distressing. Ad Avis is a most dangerous wizard.
column2: 你的話令人憂心。阿德·阿維斯是個極其危險的巫師。

column1: You search the body of the fallen Griffin but find nothing of value.
column2: 你搜了搜倒下的獅鷲的屍身,卻沒找到什麼值錢的東西。

column1: You see a Katta merchant peddling his wares.
column2: 你看見一個卡塔族商人在叫賣他的貨。

column1: You see a little astromech droid traveling down the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 你看見一個小型星際機械人,沿著「群星之街」前行。

column1: You see a Sharkee traveling casually down Sharki Darb.
column2: 你看見一個沙基,悠閒地沿著 Sharki Darb 走著。

column1: You should go back inside the cell if you wish to speak to the Katta.
column2: 你若想跟那卡塔族人說話,該回到牢房裡去。

column1: You should have had one for the road
column2: 你真該帶一個上路的。

column1: You should let the Elemental get farther into the alley first.
column2: 你該先讓那元素精靈走進巷子深處些。

column1: You should talk to the Enchantress Aziza, for she would know more about this than I.
column2: 你該去找女術士阿姿莎談談,這事她比我清楚。

column1: You should walk over in front of the Dervish if you wish to speak to him.
column2: 你若想跟那苦行僧說話,該走到他面前去。

column1: You spend an hour listening to Rakeesh' stories about his travels.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,聽拉基什講他遊歷的故事。

column1: You spend an hour listening to the Dervish's one-liners.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,聽那苦行僧抖他的短笑話。

column1: You spend an hour listening to Uhura's stories about her homeland.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,聽烏胡拉講她家鄉的故事。

column1: You spend an hour sitting in the shade of the mountains, watching the strange tree.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭坐在山蔭下,看著那棵奇怪的樹。

column1: You spend an hour trying to avoid eye contact with the rest of the room's occupants.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,設法避開房裡其他人的目光。

column1: You spend an hour watching the acrobat perform flips and twirls on the rope.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭,看那雜耍藝人在繩上翻騰旋轉。

column1: You spend an hour watching the shop and catching glares from Salt.
column2: 你花了一個鐘頭看店,還不時迎上索特的瞪視。

column2: 你存放了你那把可靠的闊劍。

column1: You straighten out your jacket and re-buckle your shoes. Appearances count.
column2: 你把外套理了理,重新扣好鞋子。儀表是要緊的。

column1: You suddenly feel very nimble!
column2: 你突然覺得身手敏捷了起來!

column1: You suspect he has gained quite a few pounds since his return.
column2: 你猜他自回來後胖了好幾磅。

column1: You take 6 dinars and 25 centimes from the purse and add it to your own.
column2: 你從錢袋裡取出六第納爾又二十五生丁,添進自己的錢裡。

column1: You tell about how you came to Spielburg.
column2: 你說起自己是怎麼來到史畢柏格的。

column1: You tell about the destruction that the Earth Elemental is inflicting upon the city.
column2: 你說起土元素精靈正對這座城市造成的破壞。

column1: You tell about the fairies dancing around the mushroom ring at night in Spielburg forest.
column2: 你說起史畢柏格森林裡,夜晚繞著蘑菇圈起舞的仙子。

column1: You tell about the Guild Hall in Spielburg.
column2: 你說起史畢柏格的公會大廳。

column1: You tell about your visit to 'Enry the 'Ermit in his cave near the Flying Falls.
column2: 你說起自己造訪飛瀑附近洞穴裡的「隱士恩利」。

column1: You tell about your visit to the house of the wizard Erasmus and his companion Fenrus.
column2: 你說起自己造訪巫師伊拉斯謨與他的同伴芬魯斯的家。

column1: You tell Aziza about the caged beast.
column2: 你向阿姿莎說起那頭籠中野獸。

column1: You tell Aziza everything you know about the caged beast.
column2: 你把你所知關於那頭籠中野獸的一切,全告訴了阿姿莎。

column1: You tell Rakeesh you were honored to wield his sword in battle.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,能在戰鬥中使用他的劍是你的榮幸。

column1: You tell Shameen and Shema about your hometown of Willowsby.
column2: 你向沙明和雪瑪說起你的家鄉柳鎮。

column1: You tell your friends about how you dispelled the baronet Barnard von Spielburg.
column2: 你向朋友們說起,你如何解除了小男爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格身上的魔咒。

column1: You've already asked them about that, Hero.
column2: 這事你已經問過他們了,英雄。

column1: You've already told them that story, Hero.
column2: 那故事你已經跟他們講過了,英雄。

column1: You've really made an impact
column2: 你著實留下了深刻的印象。

column1: You want to buy a Saurus, and I am trying to sell you one. Does that make sense to you?
column2: 你想買頭索魯斯,我正想賣你一頭。這道理你聽得懂吧?

column1: You want to find the Moneychanger's, right? No problem, boss.
column2: 你想找錢莊吧?沒問題,老大。

column1: You will need to retrieve your compass from the chest in your room first.
column2: 你得先從房裡的箱子取回你的羅盤。

column1: You will need to retrieve your map from the chest in your room first.
column2: 你得先從房裡的箱子取回你的地圖。

column1: You would do best to avoid unnecessary confrontation in Raseir.
column2: 在拉希爾,你最好避免不必要的衝突。

column1: You wouldn't happen to be HARD OF HEARING, WOULD YOU? Shall we agree upon THIRTY DINARS?
column2: 你該不會是「重聽」吧?咱們就講定「三十第納爾」如何?

column1: You wouldn't think of harming such a friend.
column2: 你不會想去傷害這樣一位朋友的。

column1: Yuck! Whoever taught you such disgusting habits? Better not try that again...
column2: 噁!誰教你這麼噁心的習慣的?還是別再試了……

column1: Is it not amazing how quickly we have set up this inn? The Katta's Tail is already the center of Shapeir's entertainment. Where else can you watch Shema dance?
column2: 我們這客棧張羅得這般神速,豈不教人嘖嘖稱奇?「卡塔之尾」如今已是夏皮爾的玩樂中心啦。除了這兒,你還上哪兒看舍瑪曼妙起舞?

column1: You can't reach it.
column2: 你搆不著它。

column1: They're the buffoon's balloons, ya Goon.
column2: 那是小丑的氣球啦,你這呆瓜。

column1: No, you're wrong. It's the USS Exitprise.
column2: 不對,你錯了。那是「出口號」星艦。

column1: Sorry, not now.
column2: 抱歉,現在不行。

column1: THE OPTIONS MENU[[COMBAT OPTIONS: Allows you to select custom keys for use in battles and calibrates the combat copilot.[[DIALOGUE SYSTEM: Selects which conversation method to use. The choices are Point & Click (a tree with clickable topics), Text Parser (all communication topics must be typed manually), or Combo Mode (a combination of Point & Click and Text Parser modes).[[SILLY CLOWNS: Enabling the Silly Clowns option allows certain Easter eggs to appear in the game.
column2: 選項選單[[戰鬥選項：讓你自訂戰鬥時使用的按鍵,並校正戰鬥輔助系統。[[對話系統：選擇要用哪種對話方式。可選「點選式」（一棵可點擊主題的選單樹）、「文字輸入式」（所有交談主題都得自己手動打字）,或「混合模式」（點選式與文字輸入式並用）。[[搞笑小丑：開啟「搞笑小丑」選項後,遊戲中會冒出一些彩蛋。

column1: The Swing/Counter Slider: When this slider favors swing, the copilot will focus on assisting you with follow-up attacks after a normal swing. The maximum amount of attacks performed is determined by the xX slash button near the bottom. When this slider favors counter, the copilot will attempt to retaliate after a blocked/ducked attack. Only the fighter class will learn counterattacks over the course of the game.
column2: 揮擊/反擊滑桿:當滑桿偏向揮擊時,輔助會專注於協助你在普通揮擊後接續攻擊,連擊次數上限由底部的 xX 斬擊按鈕決定。當滑桿偏向反擊時,輔助會在敵人的攻擊被你格擋或閃避後試圖反打。只有戰士職業能在遊戲過程中學會反擊。

column1: The Defense Slider: The further this slider is set to the right, the further the copilot will tend to concentrate on defense-related assistance.[[The Parry/Dodge Slider: When this slider favors parry, the copilot will focus on blocking/parrying enemy attacks. When this slider favors dodge, the copilot will react to enemy attacks by attempting to perform an evasive action, determined further by the Sidestep/Misc slider beneath it.
column2: 防禦滑桿:此滑桿越往右,輔助越傾向專注於防禦相關的協助。[[格擋/閃避滑桿:當滑桿偏向格擋時,輔助會專注於擋下或招架敵人的攻擊。當滑桿偏向閃避時,輔助會以閃身動作來應對敵人的攻擊,細節再由下方的側移/雜項滑桿決定。

column1: The bags I have are woven so fine they can carry the sand and plaster used to repair the city walls, yet strong enough that they will not break under so much weight. They cost but %d centimes.

column1: The waterskin will hold enough water for a day's journey in the desert. It can be refilled in the fountain. It is often the only thing between a traveler and certain death when one explores around the city. It will cost you but %d centimes.
column2: 這水袋裝的水夠你在沙漠中走上一整天。可以到噴泉重新裝滿。當你在城外探索時,它往往是旅人與必死之間唯一的依靠。只要 %d 生丁。

column1: My baskets are made of reeds brought from the east, hand-dyed and woven by myself and my family. They cost but %d centimes each.
column2: 我的籃子是用從東方運來的蘆葦,由我和家人親手染色編織而成。每個只要 %d 生丁。

column1: My oil lamps will light the way through the darkest night. Surely an adventurer such as yourself would have need for such a thing. For you, Effendi, I will charge but %d dinars.
column2: 我的油燈能在最漆黑的夜裡為你照亮前路。像您這樣的冒險者,想必正需要這麼一件東西。給您的話,閣下,我只收 %d 第納爾。

column1: My pots are all made from clay which is shaped and painted by my hand. I would be most pleased if you were to buy one. There is a very pretty one here for only %d centimes.
column2: 我的陶罐都是用黏土親手塑形、親手彩繪的。您若肯買一個,我會非常高興。這裡有個很漂亮的,只要 %d 生丁。

column1: It is only %d centimes.

column1: For two handfuls, you need pay only %d centimes.

column1: You can get five meals for just %d centimes.

column1: We have a special on magic ropes this year. It usually costs 12500 centimes. For a limited time only, though, it's only %d dinars. So do you wanta scope a rope, Miss Anthrope?
column2: 今年我們的魔法繩索有特價喔。平常要賣 12500 生丁。不過限時優惠,現在只要 %d 第納爾。怎樣,想瞧瞧這條繩子嗎,孤僻小姐?

column1: If you have a need for nudging or a bent for bouncing, this spell is swell. It can punch, it can push, it can even beat around the bush. All this for a mere %d dinars.

column1: I sell oil for filling lamps and for quieting squeaky hinges. A small container of oil costs %d centimes.

column1: My sword's blade is sharper and more durable than any other. It will only cost you %d dinars when you trade in your own sword.

column1: You couldn't convince anyone of anything. You stutter as you start to speak and can't think of anything to say.
column2: 你說服不了任何人任何事。一開口就結結巴巴,腦袋一片空白,什麼都想不出來。

column2: 才剛開口,你就發覺自己只是想到什麼說什麼。

column1: You need to stop and think for a moment about what you are going to say and how to say it.
column2: 你得先停下來,好好想想要說什麼、又該怎麼說。

column1: Then you might start again. Forget it! Come back when you've overcome your speech impediment.
column2: 然後再重新開始吧。算了!等你不再口拙時再回來。

column1: You kick a few clods of dirt in the direction of the tree's roots.
column2: 你朝樹根的方向踢了幾塊土塊過去。

column1: The tree remains immutable.
column2: 那棵樹依舊紋風不動。

column1: You remember Aziza's story, and you know that the gifts you have given to Julanar are what needed to be done for her. Her shape is by her own choice, and cannot be dispelled.
column2: 你想起阿姿莎說過的故事,明白你送給茱拉娜爾的那些禮物,正是她所需要的。她的形貌是她自己的選擇,無法被驅散。

column1: You sense that your kind words and deeds are worth more than magic to the tree. Its shape cannot be dispelled.
column2: 你感覺得到,對這棵樹而言,你的善言善行比魔法更有價值。她的形貌無法被驅散。

column1: How unecological. Not to mention rude!
column2: 真不環保。更別提有多沒禮貌了!

column1: The tree doesn't react to the item in any way.
column2: 那棵樹對這件物品毫無反應。

column1: A purple fruit has been created on one branch of the tree. The "Fruit of Compassion" drops into your hands, Julanar's gift to you for restoring her spirit.
column2: 樹的一根枝椏上結出了一顆紫色果實。這顆「慈悲之果」落進你手中,是茱拉娜爾為了感謝你喚回她的心靈,送給你的禮物。

column1: You carefully put the fruit away.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把果實收了起來。

column1: Off you go to the computer store for a new monitor. By the way, real heroes don't throw things at ladies (even rather stiff ones).

column1: You broke the monitor screen!
column2: 你把螢幕砸破了!

column1: Off you go to the computer store for a new monitor. By the way, real heroes don't cast spells at ladies (even rather stiff ones).

column1: The act of hugging the tree is fruitless.
column2: 抱樹這個舉動毫無結果。

column1: No matter how hard you try, you seem unable to communicate with the tree about your heroics.
column2: 無論你怎麼努力,似乎都沒辦法跟這棵樹聊你的英雄事蹟。

column1: The tree shivers slightly as you talk, but there is no other response.
column2: 你說話時,樹微微顫了一下,但沒有別的回應。

column1: Not again! We've heard that before.
column2: 別又來了!這段我們聽過了。

column1: Please! Not that story again!
column2: 拜託!別再講那個故事了!

column1: The tree shows no sign of recognition.
column2: 那棵樹沒有任何認出你的跡象。

column1: Julanar's leaves rustle lightly at the sound of her name.
column2: 聽見自己的名字,茱拉娜爾的葉子輕輕沙沙作響。

column1: You have come to a sandy alcove in the mountains. A strange, human-shaped plant seems to have grown out of the sand here.
column2: 你來到山間一處鋪滿細沙的凹地。這裡的沙中,似乎長出了一株奇異的、形似人形的植物。

column1: The plant almost looks like a woman.
column2: 這株植物幾乎像個女子。

column1: You admire the way she rotates her crops.
column2: 你欣賞著她輪作的姿態。

column1: The plant surely was once a woman.
column2: 這株植物想必曾經是個女子。

column1: You think she likes you.
column2: 你覺得她喜歡你。

column1: You thank Julanar for her gift and promise her it will be put to good use.
column2: 你向茱拉娜爾道謝,並答應她一定會好好善用這份禮物。

column1: The plant nods as if in a gentle breeze.
column2: 那株植物點了點頭,彷彿微風拂過。

column1: You have thanked her already. Don't get carried away.
column2: 你已經謝過她了。別得寸進尺。

column1: The tree has no idea why you are thanking her, but she nods as in a gentle breeze.
column2: 那棵樹不曉得你為什麼要謝她,但仍像被微風拂過似地點了點頭。

column1: The tree doesn't respond.
column2: 那棵樹沒有回應。

column2: 你抓起一把石塊。

column2: 你舀了些沙子進布袋裡。

column1: That didn't do any good.

column1: And they ask, 'What is a hero?',[   though the answer's very clear,[He's the one who faces danger[   when the Darkness hovers near.
column2: 他們問道:「何謂英雄?」,[   答案其實再清楚不過,[當黑暗在身旁盤桓逼近,[   英雄便是直面凶險的那一個。

column1: He will face the fiercest foe[   when another needs his aid,[He will dare to defy death[   even though he is afraid.[He works not just for glory[   and he does it not for gain,[But because he knows that others[   will be spared a greater pain.
column2: 當有人需要援手,[   他便迎向最凶猛的強敵,[縱使心中恐懼,[   他仍敢於藐視死亡。[他不只為了榮耀而戰,[   也不為私利而為,[只因他深知,如此一來,[   旁人便能免去更深的苦痛。

column1: He won't always follow orders,[   for he dares to answer, 'Why?'[And unless he likes the reason,[   he refuses to comply.[He will brave the battle boldly[   even though he may not win;[He will face his fate unflinching,[   for he is a paladin.
column2: 他不會事事服從命令,[   因為他敢於反問:「為何?」[除非那理由能說服他,[   否則他絕不順從。[他將無畏地奮勇赴戰,[   縱使未必能勝;[他將從容直面命運,[   因為他是一名聖騎士。

column1: And they ask 'What is a hero?',[   though the answer's evident;[He's the one who faces death[   knowing that his life's well spent.
column2: 他們問道:「何謂英雄?」,[   答案其實顯而易見;[英雄便是那直面死亡的人,[   深知自己這一生,沒有白活。

column1: You are blessed with a tongue of fortune. You need pay me but %d centimes.
column2: 你天生一張福氣嘴。你只需付我 %d 個小錢便成。

column2: 這只陶罐值 %d 個小錢。再少我可不能收。

column1: I would make no living, were I to sell my wares at such a price. It is still a bargain at %d dinars.
column2: 若照這價錢賣我的貨,我可要喝西北風了。就算 %d 第納爾,也仍是撿便宜呢。

column1: Hero-friend, this is my final offer. I cannot accept any less than %d dinars.

column1: Alas, you will need to visit the Moneychanger before you can buy something here.

column2: 這是個小巧卻討喜的陶罐。

column1: Here is a sample of my finest work.

column2: 既然您說得這麼動聽,那就只算您 %d 生丁吧。

column2: 不不,這麼一番好話只值這點錢可不對勁,該收 %d 生丁才是。

column1: Very well. For you, we shall make a special price. It will cost you %d centimes.
column2: 好吧,看在您的份上,我們給個特別價,算您 %d 生丁。

column1: The prices we charge are but a pittance of what our food is worth.

column1: The finest food in all the bazaar is also the cheapest.
column2: 整個市集最棒的吃食,同時也是最便宜的。

column1: The prices we charge are but a pittance of what our articles are worth.

column1: The finest goods in all the bazaar are also the cheapest.
column2: 整個市集最棒的貨色,同時也是最便宜的。

column1: Our final offer is %d centimes. Any lower and we will be losing too much money, Hero-friend.
column2: 我們最後的價碼是 %d 生丁,再低我們可要虧大了,英雄朋友。

column1: I am sorry, Effendi, but we can go no lower.
column2: 抱歉啊,閣下,我們實在不能再低了。

column1: You do not have enough money.
column2: 你的錢不夠。

column1: May you be much the better for it.
column2: 願它讓您過得更好。

column1: May it please you well.
column2: 願它叫您稱心如意。

column1: May it bring you entertainment and enjoyment.
column2: 願它帶給您歡樂與享受。

column1: Surely a signed and sealed original-release game box is of much greater value to you, O Hero?
column2: 一個有簽名又封膠的初版遊戲盒,對您來說想必價值連城吧,英雄啊?

column1: You shall find no less than nine 3.5" floppy disks tucked within the package.
column2: 您會在包裝裡找到足足九片 3.5 吋磁碟片喔。

column1: Not to mention five 5.25" floppies.
column2: 更別提還有五片 5.25 吋的磁片呢。

column1: It comes complete with cardboard slip-cover.
column2: 它還附上完整的紙板外殼。

column1: And Famous Adventurer's Correspondence School primer.
column2: 外加一本「知名冒險家函授學校」入門手冊。

column1: No hero should ever be without one.
column2: 哪個英雄能少得了這麼一件寶貝。

column1: This rarity shall be yours, all for the paltry sum of %d dinars.
column2: 這稀世珍品就歸您了,只要區區 %d 第納爾。

column1: The game box is a collector's item and is worth every bit of the asking price.
column2: 這遊戲盒可是收藏品,開這個價一點都不冤。

column1: Let it be known that you shall never find a better deal on all of eBay, Effendi.
column2: 告訴您,整個 eBay 上您都找不到比這更划算的買賣了,閣下。

column1: You can attest to the enjoyment of the gameplay, for you live the experience as we speak.
column2: 遊玩的樂趣您大可作證,因為此時此刻您正親身體驗著呢。

column1: Our final offer is %d dinars.
column2: 我們最後的價碼是 %d 第納爾。

column1: Surely such authentic merchandising memorabilia is of much greater value to you, O Hero?
column2: 這麼正版的周邊紀念品,對您來說想必價值連城吧,英雄啊?

column1: You shall find the sticker to be very adhesive.
column2: 您會發現這貼紙黏性十足。

column1: It bonds to anything.
column2: 什麼東西都黏得牢。

column1: Stick it on the back of your Saurus to gain instant respect and credibility.
column2: 把它貼在您的索魯斯背上,立刻就能贏得敬重與名望。

column1: Fellow members of your riding caravan shall provide you with a generous buffer zone.
column2: 同行商隊的夥伴們可會替您空出一大圈寬敞的距離呢。

column1: The sticker is a collector's item and is worth every bit of the asking price.
column2: 這貼紙可是收藏品,開這個價一點都不冤。

column1: Let it be known that supplies are limited.
column2: 告訴您,數量可是有限的。

column1: Very well.
column2: 好吧。

column1: For you, we shall make a special price. It will cost you %d centimes, instead of the normal price.
column2: 看在您的份上,我們給個特別價,不照原價算,只收您 %d 生丁。

column1: Humble apologies, Effendi, but %d centimes is my final offer to you.
column2: 小的萬分抱歉,閣下,%d 生丁是我給您的最後價碼了。

column1: Perhaps you should reconsider our last offer, Hero of the North.
column2: 也許您該重新考慮一下我們剛才的開價,北方來的英雄。

column1: The dates taste very good.
column2: 這椰棗嚐起來真不錯。

column1: The bread is delicious.
column2: 這麵包很美味。

column1: The falafels are quite tasty.
column2: 這炸豆丸子相當可口。

column1: The Saurus-On-A-Stick tastes wonderfully.
column2: 這串烤索魯斯肉串味道好極了。

column1: The door is barred, and your magic has no effect.
column2: 門閂著,你的魔法起不了作用。

column1: The door's lock is too strong for your magic.
column2: 這道門鎖對你的魔法來說太堅固了。

column1: You Fetch the purse and open it. It has six dinars and 25 centimes. It also has a small scrap of paper with the words "Quelling Chaos From Shapeir, Driving Darkness from Raseir."
column2: 你取來錢袋打開一看,裡頭有六枚第納爾和二十五生丁。還有一小片紙條,上面寫著「平息夏皮爾的混亂,驅散拉希爾的黑暗」。

column1: lIPracticing magic here might get you in trouble with the local authorities.
column2: 在這兒施展魔法,小心招來當地官府的麻煩。

column1: May it please you, my name is Toshur.
column2: 若您不嫌棄,小的名叫托舒爾。

column1: For that you must talk to Sloree and Scoree, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事您得去問廣場另一頭的斯洛里和斯科里。

column1: Sloree and Scoree are across the plaza.
column2: 斯洛里和斯科里在廣場的另一頭。

column1: The Apothecary Shop is right here in this plaza.
column2: 藥舖就在這座廣場裡。

column1: Clay is a special type of earth which is used to make pots.
column2: 黏土是一種特別的泥土,專門用來捏陶罐。

column1: This is a most friendly and comfortable place to reside.
column2: 這是個再友善舒適不過的居處了。

column1: There were once many Katta in Raseir. When the new Emir took over, he drove our people away.
column2: 拉希爾從前住著許多卡塔族。新埃米爾上台後,把我族人都趕走了。

column1: It can be said that all Katta are related to one another. We are all family.
column2: 可以說,所有卡塔族彼此都是親戚。我們全是一家人。

column1: Such a destructive thing in the plazas means a terrible loss of business and lives. When water is thrown at it, the thing of fire merely leaps away!
column2: 這種破壞力十足的東西闖進廣場,生意和人命都損失慘重。潑水上去,那團火竟然一下就竄開躲掉了!

column1: Wind of such force can easily blow our stands away.
column2: 這麼大的風輕輕鬆鬆就能把我們的攤子吹跑。

column1: It is a thing of such force that little can stand in its way.
column2: 它力量大到幾乎沒什麼能擋得住它。

column1: There is little the Katta can do with such a thing except suffer.
column2: 面對這種東西,卡塔族除了受罪之外幾乎無能為力。

column1: We know little of such things but that we will suffer when Elementals are around.
column2: 這類東西我們所知不多,只曉得元素精靈一出現,我們就要遭殃。

column1: The Katta know but little magics, practical magics. Perhaps you might ask at the Shop of Magic.
column2: 卡塔族只懂一點點魔法,都是些實用的小法術。也許您可以去魔法舖問問。

column1: The Katta respect the Sultan very much.
column2: 卡塔族非常敬重蘇丹。

column1: The Emir Arus of Raseir was a friendly man who chatted daily with the Katta merchants in the plazas. We mourn the loss of such a one.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾阿魯斯是位和善的人,天天在廣場上和卡塔商人閒話家常。失去這樣一位人物,我們深感哀痛。

column1: The Palace is where the Sultan Harun al-Rashid resides.
column2: 宮殿是蘇丹哈倫·拉希德的居所。

column1: Our people have always lived in the deserts around here.
column2: 我族人世世代代都住在這一帶的沙漠裡。

column1: The only coins we can accept are the dinars and centimes of Shapeir.
column2: 我們只收夏皮爾的第納爾和生丁這兩種錢幣。

column1: The Guild of Adventurers is on the west side of the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在戰士廣場的西邊。

column1: It is good to have Shameen and Shema back to run the inn.
column2: 沙明和舍瑪回來打理客棧,真是太好了。

column1: The weather is always wonderful this time of year.
column2: 每年這個時節,天氣總是好得很。

column1: The fountain is very beautiful, but it is also very wet.
column2: 那座噴泉很美,不過也很濕就是了。

column1: It is told that a hero from the north will come to restore the Katta to their home in Raseir. So did Shema and Shameen travel to find such a one.
column2: 傳說有位北方英雄會前來,讓卡塔族重返拉希爾的家園。於是舍瑪和沙明便動身去尋訪這樣一位英雄。

column1: The Moneychanger will give you the dinars and centimes with which you need to purchase things in this land. She is on Dinar Tarik.
column2: 換錢的婦人會給您第納爾和生丁,在這片土地上買東西全靠它。她在第納爾塔里克街上。

column1: She is most wise and powerful and one to be respected.
column2: 她睿智又有法力,是位值得敬重的人物。

column1: The poetry of the Poet is most illuminating.
column2: 那位詩人的詩篇最是發人深省。

column1: We Katta enjoy the chance to sit in the sun and chat with our neighbors as a way of living.
column2: 我們卡塔族就愛這樣過日子——曬曬太陽,和左鄰右舍閒話家常。

column1: We are a quiet people who endure like the sands of the desert.
column2: 我們是個安靜的民族,像沙漠裡的黃沙一樣堅忍。

column1: He is a very brave and daring Katta.
column2: 他是位非常勇敢、敢作敢為的卡塔族。

column1: Shema is most wise and clever.
column2: 舍瑪最是睿智聰慧。

column1: It is feared that he has been imprisoned in Raseir.
column2: 只怕他已經被囚禁在拉希爾了。

column1: We merchants set up the bazaar in all the plazas.
column2: 我們這些商人在每一座廣場都擺起了市集。

column2: 拉珊在噴泉廣場賣耐養的植物。

column2: 薩夏南在宮殿廣場賣精緻的珠寶。

column2: 米拉克在戰士廣場賣皮件。

column2: 薩巴在噴泉廣場賣結實的籃子。

column2: 托舒爾在噴泉廣場賣精緻的陶器。

column2: 斯科里和斯洛里在噴泉廣場賣可口的食物。

column2: 莉莎在城門廣場賣討喜的鮮花。

column2: 基拉姆在戰士廣場賣耐穿的衣物。

column2: 武器舖在戰士廣場。

column1: The Magic Shop is on the other side of the plaza.
column2: 魔法舖在廣場的另一頭。

column1: It is a name for one who is highly admired by my people.
column2: 這是我族人對極為敬仰之人的尊稱。

column1: It is most regrettable that I am so ignorant of such a thing that I could tell you nothing which would add to your own knowledge.
column2: 很遺憾,小的對這件事一無所知,實在說不出半點能增進您見識的話。

column1: A thousand pardons, Effendi. I could add little to your knowledge of that subject.
column2: 萬分抱歉,閣下,那個話題小的實在沒什麼能補充給您的。

column1: We are most fortunate you have come to Shapeir, Hero of the North.
column2: 您能來到夏皮爾,是我們天大的福氣,北方來的英雄。

column1: For that you must talk to Toshur, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事您得去問廣場另一頭的托舒爾。

column1: She is across the plaza.
column2: 她在廣場的另一頭。

column1: We have delicious things for you to eat.
column2: 我們有好吃的東西供您享用。

column1: Mouth-tempting falafels of fresh-roasted lamb.
column2: 教人垂涎的炸豆丸子,配上現烤的羊肉。

column1: Dates stuffed with honeyed almonds to tease your tongue.
column2: 椰棗裡塞滿蜜漬杏仁,挑逗您的味蕾。

column1: It is a certainty that you will enjoy what we sell.
column2: 我們賣的東西,您準會喜歡,這是一定的。

column1: This is my home, Effendi.
column2: 這兒就是小的的家,閣下。

column1: Today I traveled the long route to get here, Effendi, for an Elemental of Water occupies the fountain.
column2: 今天小的繞了好大一圈才走到這兒,閣下,因為有個水元素佔住了噴泉。

column1: Such a thing is a peril to us all, for it prevents anyone from approaching and quenching their thirst. Without water, nobody can survive even a day in a desert land such as this.
column2: 那玩意兒是咱們所有人的大禍患,誰都沒辦法靠近喝水解渴。在這種沙漠地方,沒了水,連一天都撐不下去。

column1: You are a most worthy hero, Effendi, for without your bravery this city would have perished.
column2: 您真是位了不起的英雄,閣下,要不是您的勇氣,這座城早就毀了。

column1: Such magical flames will keep burning until this city stands no more.
column2: 那些魔火會一直燒下去,直到這座城不復存在為止。

column1: It grieves me to the last degree, but I have no answer for you.
column2: 小的萬分慚愧,實在答不出您要問的。

column1: The wind that now blows in the Plaza of the Palace is a great hazard to anyone who approaches.
column2: 如今刮在王宮廣場上的那陣風,誰靠近誰倒大楣。

column1: I have seen it with my own eyes, Effendi. Magical stone and rock of the strongest type, in the very semblance of man!

column1: The poet is a source of information and entertainment for the land.
column2: 這位詩人,是這片土地上消息與娛樂的來源。

column1: For all who journey from[    the lands afar,[ The center of the city,[    the bazaar,[ Is filled with merchants' magic,[    and shall be,[ A wonder to behold,[    a joy to see.
column2: 對所有從[    遠方而來的旅人,[ 那城中央的[    市集,[ 滿是商人的奇貨妙術,[    更是,[ 一處嘆為觀止、[    賞心悅目的景緻。

column1: The bazaar of the merchants is a lure for tourists.
column2: 商人雲集的市集,是吸引遊客的一大去處。

column1: The Dervish of the desert[    sits upon the sands beside the pool,[ Of the oasis as men pass him[    and they laugh in ridicule at such a fool,
column2: 那沙漠中的苦行僧[    坐在綠洲水潭旁的沙地上,[ 旁人經過他身邊,[    都譏笑他是個傻瓜,

column1: But he watches and he listens[    and he thinks upon the things which pass him by,[ And he smiles upon his mockers[    when they question all his answers in reply.
column2: 可他只是靜觀、細聽,[    思索著從他眼前掠過的種種,[ 當嘲笑他的人[    對他的回答百般質疑時,他只是報以微笑。

column1: Many people go to the Dervish for knowledge, but few understand him.
column2: 許多人去向苦行僧求教問道,可真正聽懂他的人沒幾個。

column1: A poem is but words[    used in a different way,[ In which the meaning is[    far more than words can say.
column2: 詩,不過是文字[    換了個用法,[ 那其中的意涵,[    遠非字面所能道盡。

column1: A poem goes beyond the mere meaning of its words to express the deeper truths within them.
column2: 詩超越了字面的意思,道出其中更深一層的真諦。

column1: Upon that question[    I would further ponder,[ for guidance I suggest[    that thee seek yonder.
column2: 這個問題嘛,[    容我再細細思量,[ 至於指點,我建議[    你還是另尋他處吧。

column1: The Poet Omar (May His Words Be Ever Heard!) has no answer for you.
column2: 詩人奧瑪爾(願他的話語永被聆聽!)答不出你要問的。

column1: Upon some things[    I can but little say,[ that others yet might[    further guide your way.
column2: 有些事情,[    我能說的不多,[ 還是讓旁人[    進一步替你指路吧。

column1: Perhaps it would be wiser for you to ask someone who knows more about the subject.
column2: 或許,你去問問更懂這方面的人,會更明智些。

column1: If you want to become a hero here, you will be better off keeping any stories about your illegal activities to yourself.
column2: 你要是想在這裡當英雄,最好把你那些違法勾當的事爛在肚子裡別說出去。

column1: Please, Effendi. A thousand pardons, but I do not understand.
column2: 閣下,千萬別見怪,可是小的實在聽不明白。

column1: We are happy to be of assistance.
column2: 能幫上忙,我們很開心。

column1: Such a thing sounds dangerous beyond measure.
column2: 這種事聽起來危險得沒邊了。

column1: Please take care, friend of the Katta.
column2: 多保重,卡塔族的朋友。

column1: That is amazing.
column2: 太了不起了。

column1: You are beyond doubt a hero!
column2: 您毫無疑問是位英雄!

column1: I am happy to help, Effendi.
column2: 能幫上忙是小的的榮幸,閣下。

column1: A wonderful tale, Effendi. Thank you for sharing.
column2: 好精彩的故事,閣下。多謝您願意說給我們聽。

column1: A tale from a hero is always good to hear,[  For such tales are welcome here in Shapeir.
column2: 英雄說的故事,聽起來總是津津有味,[  這樣的故事在夏皮爾人人愛聽。

column1: The poet thanks you for your words.
column2: 詩人感謝您的這番話。

column1: It will take a most brave hero to deal with the thing of wind.
column2: 要對付那個風造出來的東西,得要一位最英勇的英雄才行。

column1: My pots are shaking with every step the Earth Elemental takes.
column2: 那土元素每踏一步,我的陶罐就跟著抖一下。

column1: What good is a pot which cannot be filled? We need your aid!
column2: 裝不了東西的罐子有什麼用?我們需要您的幫助啊!

column1: There are no thanks great enough for all you have done and will do.
column2: 您所做的、以及將要做的一切,再多的感謝都不足以回報。

column1: Pots for sale, earthenware pots in brightly colored glazes. I have the pot you have long sought!
column2: 賣陶罐囉,上了鮮豔釉彩的陶罐。您找了好久的那只罐子,我這兒就有!

column1: I will be happy to sell you the pot which best suits your needs. I have pots in every size and many colors.
column2: 我很樂意賣您一只最合用的罐子。各種尺寸、各種顏色,應有盡有。

column1: The Plaza of the Palace has been abandoned by merchants today.
column2: 今天王宮廣場的商人全跑光了。

column1: There is an Elemental of Air which blew them from the plaza.
column2: 有個風元素把他們從廣場上吹跑了。

column1: The Katta are afraid to walk the streets.
column2: 卡塔族都不敢上街了。

column1: An Elemental of Earth is moving there.
column2: 有個土元素正在那邊到處遊蕩。

column1: Friend of the Katta, the Water Elemental will have us all dying of thirst!
column2: 卡塔族的朋友,那個水元素會讓我們全都渴死啊!

column1: There must be something you can do to help us, Hero of Shapeir!
column2: 您一定有辦法幫幫我們的,夏皮爾的英雄!

column1: Rumor has said that you are journeying to Raseir.
column2: 外頭傳說您要動身前往拉希爾了。

column1: It has been said that you seek to restore the old Emir!
column2: 大家都說您打算讓老埃米爾復位!

column1: You could not forget your friends the Katta.
column2: 您不會忘記卡塔族這些老朋友的。

column1: The homeland of our people shall be restored through your actions!
column2: 靠著您的行動,我們族人的家園終將重建!

column1: Would you care today to try some fried bread and honey?
column2: 今天要不要嚐嚐炸餅淋蜂蜜?

column1: Perhaps you would like hot-spiced Saurus-On-A-Stick?
column2: 或者來一串香辣索魯斯肉串如何?

column1: What about some nice lizard jerky?
column2: 不然來點上好的蜥蜴肉乾?

column1: Mmmmm--mmm! I could go for some lizard jerky, myself!
column2: 嗯——嗯!我自己都想來點蜥蜴肉乾了!

column1: The pots don't look like they're worth enough to justify the loss of a hand when you'd get caught.
column2: 這些罐子看起來不值幾個錢,被逮到剁一隻手可划不來。

column1: The door is locked. The Apothecary is closed for the night.
column2: 門鎖上了,藥鋪今晚打烊了。

column1: You wouldn't be able to get close enough to the fountain without provoking the Elemental to attack you.
column2: 你還沒走近噴泉,就會先惹得元素撲上來打你。

column1: The fountain water is cool and refreshing.
column2: 噴泉的水又涼又爽口。

column1: For your information, "go with the flow" is just an expression. Wave bye bye, everyone...
column2: 提醒你一下,「隨波逐流」只是個說法而已。各位,揮揮手說再見吧……

column1: eeYou feel drained.
column2: 你感到精疲力竭。

column1: You pick up the purse and open it. It has six dinars and 25 centimes. It also has a small scrap of paper with the words "Quelling Chaos From Shapeir, Driving Darkness from Raseir."
column2: 你撿起錢袋打開來看,裡面有六枚第納爾和二十五分錢,還有一張小紙條,上頭寫著:「平息夏皮爾之亂,驅逐拉希爾之暗。」

column1: urYou see a small leather purse on the plaza floor.
column2: 你看到廣場地上有個小皮錢袋。

column1: Any adventurer who would try to get water from an Elemental-inhabited fountain must be wet behind the ears. That's no way to make a splash.
column2: 敢去元素盤據的噴泉打水的冒險者,八成是個嫩到滴水的菜鳥。這可不是闖出名堂的法子。

column1: It's a shame you don't have a waterskin to fill.
column2: 可惜你沒帶水袋可以裝水。

column1: Your waterskin is full of water.
column2: 你的水袋裝滿了水。

column1: Your waterskins are full of water.
column2: 你的幾個水袋都裝滿了水。

column1: You fill your waterskin with cool, clear water from the fountain.
column2: 你用噴泉裡清涼的淨水把水袋裝滿。

column1: You fill your waterskins with cool, clear water from the fountain.
column2: 你用噴泉裡清涼的淨水把幾個水袋都裝滿。

column1: You're a hero, remember? You're supposed to SAVE the city, not aid in its destruction.
column2: 別忘了你是英雄欸?你的任務是「拯救」這座城,不是幫忙毀掉它。

column1: It would be best to do that under the cover of darkness.
column2: 這種事最好趁夜色掩護來幹。

column1: The door appears to be barred from the inside. Picking the lock will not help you enter.
column2: 門似乎是從裡頭閂上的,撬鎖也進不去。

column1: You will have to practice some more to improve your lock-picking skill.
column2: 你得再多練練,才能提升你的開鎖技巧。

column1: You've already dropped a waterskin on the other side of the fountain.
column2: 你已經在噴泉另一邊放了一個水袋了。

column1: You've already put a waterskin on the ground.
column2: 你已經把一個水袋放在地上了。

column1: The trap is set. Now it's time to go after the prey.
column2: 陷阱布好了,該去獵物上鉤了。

column1: The water splashes into the fountain in a most inviting way.
column2: 水嘩啦啦地濺進噴泉裡,看起來誘人極了。

column1: Though it is far below the price I must pay for such an object, nevertheless I am out-bargained and you shall have it for a mere %d dinars.
column2: 這價錢遠低於我進貨的成本,不過我被您殺價殺得沒輒,就區區 %d 枚第納爾賣給您吧。

column1: It grieves me very much, but what can I do? It is impossible for me to charge any less than %d dinars for such a beautiful and valuable object.
column2: 教我心痛萬分,可我又能怎麼辦?這麼漂亮又貴重的東西,少於 %d 枚第納爾我實在賣不了。

column1: Surely a great hero such as yourself can afford a trifle amount like %d dinars? This will have to be my final offer, Effendi.
column2: 像您這樣的大英雄,區區 %d 枚第納爾總出得起吧?這是小的最後的價碼了,閣下。

column1: I see no further progress in our negotiations. I am sorry, Effendi.
column2: 看來我們這筆買賣談不下去了。很抱歉,閣下。

column1: May this lamp light your darkness.
column2: 願這盞燈照亮您的黑暗。

column1: I will have it delivered to the inn for you, Effendi.
column2: 小的會幫您把它送到客棧去,閣下。

column1: May this suit your needs, Effendi.
column2: 願這件東西合您的用,閣下。

column1: The item is worth much more than that. I believe you will find %d centimes to be a fair price.
column2: 這東西可值錢得多了。我想 %d 分錢才算公道價。

column1: At that price, I would be giving it away. %d centimes is my last offer to you.
column2: 照那個價我等於白送了。%d 分錢是小的給您的最後價碼。

column1: The item is worth much more than that. I believe you will find %d dinars to be a fair price.
column2: 這東西可值錢得多了。我想 %d 枚第納爾才算公道價。

column1: At that price, I would be giving it away. %d dinars is my last offer to you.
column2: 照那個價我等於白送了。%d 枚第納爾是小的給您的最後價碼。

column1: Such a shame that we could not come to an agreement, my friend.
column2: 真可惜我們談不攏,我的朋友。

column1: Thank you, Effendi. May you be happy with my work.
column2: 多謝您,閣下。願我的手藝能讓您滿意。

column1: I could never turn a profit at such a price. May I suggest %d dinars?
column2: 這價錢我可賺不到半毛錢。容我建議 %d 枚第納爾如何?

column1: I must stand firm in my price, Hero-friend. I will not accept less than %d dinars.
column2: 我這價得守住了,英雄朋友。少於 %d 枚第納爾我不能答應。

column1: My last offer still stands, Effendi.
column2: 小的最後的價碼仍然有效,閣下。

column1: Practicing magic here might get you in trouble with the local authorities.
column2: 在這裡施展魔法,可能會惹上當地官府的麻煩。

column1: I am Saba, and my husband Tasman and my three daughters help weave the baskets.
column2: 我叫薩巴,我丈夫塔斯曼和三個女兒一起幫忙編籃子。

column1: For that you must talk to Lasham, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事您得去找拉珊,就在廣場對面。

column1: He is across the plaza.
column2: 他在廣場對面。

column1: For that you must talk to Tashtari, right here next to me.
column2: 那件事您得去找塔什塔里,就在我旁邊這兒。

column1: He is next to me.
column2: 他就在我旁邊。

column1: The Magic Shop is right here in this plaza. As a matter of fact, it is looking at you right now.
column2: 魔法店就在這座廣場上。說真的,它這會兒正盯著您看呢。

column1: The Apothecary is on the other side of this plaza.
column2: 藥鋪在這座廣場的另一邊。

column1: Lasham I am called; sharing the living beauty of plants is my joy.
column2: 小女子名叫拉珊;能與人分享花草那鮮活的美,是我的樂趣。

column1: For that you must talk to Saba, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事您得去找薩巴,就在廣場對面。

column1: For that you must talk to Tashtari, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事您得去找塔什塔里,就在廣場對面。

column1: I have flowering plants and green plants, planted in specially prepared earth and placed in pots from the potter across the way. All will brighten a room and freshen the air.
column2: 我這兒有開花的盆栽,也有觀葉的綠植,種在特製的土裡,擺在對面陶匠做的罐子中。每一盆都能讓房間生色、空氣清新。

column1: The earth the plant is in is specially suited to the type of plant. If you wish, I will gladly give you some.
column2: 盆裡的土是特別配給這種植物用的。您若想要,我很樂意分您一些。

column1: As you look the plants over, you realize you won't have a use for a potted plant right now.
column2: 你打量了一番這些盆栽,發覺自己眼下用不上盆栽植物。

column1: The Apothecary is on the other side of the plaza.
column2: 藥鋪在廣場的另一邊。

column1: I am called Tashtari, which means 'Sunset Glow.'
column2: 小的名叫塔什塔里,意思是「夕陽餘暉」。

column1: For that you must talk to Saba, right here next to me.
column2: 那件事您得去找薩巴,就在我旁邊這兒。

column1: She is next to me.
column2: 她就在我旁邊。

column1: Brass is the essence of all I sell: lanterns and lamps, braziers and bottles, tea servers and cups. I have the goods of the finest workers of metal in the city.
column2: 黃銅是我所賣一切東西的精髓:燈籠與油燈、火盆與瓶罐、茶具與杯盞。我這兒擺的全是城裡頂尖金工師傅的手藝。

column1: It is a mixture of copper and zinc, minerals most plentiful in this region, and is beautiful and long lasting. It makes very fine lamps, braziers, and tea servers.
column2: 它是銅和鋅的合金,這兩種礦在本地最是豐富,既漂亮又耐久。拿來做油燈、火盆和茶具最是上乘。

column1: Braziers are mostly used for cooking or for apothecary work. You most likely have no need for them.
column2: 火盆多半是用來煮東西、或是給藥鋪煉藥用的。您八成用不上。

column1: My tea servers are exquisitely wrought, objects of elegance whenever you serve tea.
column2: 我的茶具做工精緻,每回奉茶都是一件雅事。

column1: Alas, I just sold my last bottle last week to a stranger named Hadji. He said something about a dream of Djinni.
column2: 唉呀,上禮拜我才把最後一只瓶子賣給一個叫哈吉的外鄉人。他嘴裡還唸著什麼夢見精靈的事。

column1: The Apothecary sells oil, if you wish to buy some.
column2: 您若想買油,藥鋪有賣。

column1: The price of the brass lamp is %d dinars.
column2: 這盞黃銅油燈賣 %d 枚第納爾。

column1: The price of the brazier is 20 dinars.
column2: 這只火盆賣二十枚第納爾。

column1: The price of the tea server is 12 dinars.
column2: 這套茶具賣十二枚第納爾。

column1: You thank Lasham for giving you a quantity of dirt for use against the Elemental of Air and vow to use it well.
column2: 你感謝拉珊給了你一些泥土,好用來對付風元素,並暗暗發誓要好好善用它。

column1: May it serve you well, Effendi. Good luck!
column2: 願它能助您一臂之力,閣下。祝您好運!

column1: You thank Lasham and tell her about your triumph over the Air Elemental.
column2: 你向拉珊道謝,並把你擊敗風元素的事告訴她。

column1: Thank you for sharing that story with me, Effendi. I am honored I was able to play a small part in the defeat of the creature!
column2: 謝謝您把這故事說給我聽,閣下。能在擊敗那怪物一事上盡一份小小的力,小女子深感榮幸!

column1: You thank Tashtari for his kindness in giving you a lamp free of charge and vow to use it well.
column2: 你感謝塔什塔里的好意,免費送了你一盞油燈,並暗暗發誓要好好善用它。

column1: That goes without saying. Who would need oil lamps when the city is burning?
column2: 那還用說。城都快燒起來了,誰還需要油燈呢?

column1: You thank Tashtari for his kindness in giving you a lamp free of charge.
column2: 你感謝塔什塔里的好意,免費送了你一盞油燈。

column1: How exciting. One of my lamps aided in the survival of the city!
column2: 真叫人振奮。我的油燈竟然幫了這座城一把!

column1: I shall inform potential customers of this anecdote in the future.
column2: 往後我可要把這段軼事說給上門的客人聽聽。

column1: It is said that an Elemental of Air blows in the city.
column2: 聽說城裡颳起了一個風元素。

column1: As I left where I live this morning, I heard something very large behind me.
column2: 今早我出門的時候,聽見身後有個好大的東西。

column1: All the fine brass-work that I sell is worth nothing to a city without water. Surely you can do something, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 我賣的這些精緻黃銅器,對一座沒水的城來說一文不值。您一定有辦法的,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: It has been spoken on the streets that you journey to Raseir. Surely our homeland will be returned.
column2: 街頭巷尾都在說您要前往拉希爾了。我們的家園想必能夠收復。

column1: Brass is essence of all that I sell: lanterns and lamps, braziers and bottles, tea servers and cups. I have the goods of the finest workers of metal in the city.
column2: 黃銅是我所賣一切東西的精髓:燈籠與油燈、火盆與瓶罐、茶具與杯盞。我這兒擺的全是城裡頂尖金工師傅的手藝。

column1: Surely a great adventurer as yourself will need a lamp on your travels. Allow me to sell to you the finest oil lamp in the land.
column2: 像您這樣的大冒險家,旅途上想必少不了一盞燈。容我把全國最上等的油燈賣給您。

column1: The merchants in the Plaza of the Palace could not set their stands there because of a strange wind.
column2: 王宮廣場的商人因為一陣怪風,沒法子在那兒擺攤了。

column1: There is a strange thing upon the streets. It is formed of the stone of the city.
column2: 街上出現了個古怪的東西,是由這座城的石頭凝聚而成的。

column1: Only a true hero such as yourself can deal with such a wicked thing of water magic! There is no doubt that your kindness and bravery are great.
column2: 唯有像您這樣真正的英雄,才對付得了那邪惡的水魔之物!您的仁慈與勇氣之偉大,毋庸置疑。

column1: There is no doubt that you are the hero all the Katta have waited for! You are truly wonderful to travel to Raseir to restore our home.
column2: 毫無疑問,您就是全體卡塔族期盼已久的英雄!您願意遠赴拉希爾重建我們的家園,實在令人敬佩。

column1: I have baskets of all colors, sizes, and shapes to contain whatever you wish. I will be most honored if the Hero of the North were to purchase one of my baskets.
column2: 我這兒有各色、各種尺寸與形狀的籃子,您想裝什麼都成。北方來的英雄若肯買下我一只籃子,小的將深感榮幸。

column1: O Hero, would you but honor this humble Katta by spending a trifle of your great riches for one of the baskets I have spent many months of making just for you.
column2: 噢,英雄啊,這些籃子是小的花了好幾個月專為您編的,您可願賞臉,從您的萬貫家財中撥出區區一點,買下其中一只,讓小小的卡塔族沾沾光?

column1: It is feared that the wind that now blows is an ill one.
column2: 人人都怕,如今颳起的這陣風是不祥之兆。

column1: My plants are trembling as is the very city. You can feel it shake beneath your feet.
column2: 我的花草都在發抖,整座城也跟著抖。您腳下都能感覺到它在晃。

column1: My plants wither already because I cannot water them. The grant of this single favor is worth to me a thousand others. Please rid this land of the Elemental of Water.
column2: 我的花草已經因為澆不了水而枯萎了。您若肯成全這一件事,勝過為我做一千件別的。求求您把這片土地上的水元素除掉吧。

column1: May good fortune watch over you always.
column2: 願好運永遠眷顧您。

column1: Living plants to bring the outdoors inside. Flowers alive to grow and thrive.
column2: 把戶外的綠意帶進屋裡。活生生的花朵,自會生長茁壯。

column1: Enjoy the beauty of the living plant. Its very breath lightens the air and fills a room with gladness.
column2: 好好欣賞這活生生植物的美吧。它的吐息讓空氣清新,為滿室帶來歡愉。

column1: The door is locked. The Magic Shop is closed at nighttime.
column2: 門鎖著。魔法店一到夜裡就打烊了。

column1: QUIT STARING AT ME!!
column2: 別再盯著我看了!!

column1: You feel drained.
column2: 你感到精疲力竭。

column1: As the door closes, you hear the bar fall into place behind you. The door is now barred from the inside.
column2: 門一關上,你聽見門閂在身後落定。這扇門現在從裡頭閂死了。

column1: The punishment for thievery in this city is to lose a hand. Do not let me catch you trying to steal again!
column2: 在這座城裡,偷竊的下場是剁掉一隻手。別再讓我逮到你動手腳!

column1: Do you not know that practicing magic is best done outside of the city? Do not let me see you using magic around here again unless it is needed!
column2: 難道你不曉得施法最好到城外去嗎?除非萬不得已,別再讓我看到你在這附近用魔法!

column1: By picking a lock you were choosing your fate.
column2: 你撬開那把鎖的那一刻,就等於選好了自己的下場。

column1: Blatant Thievery!
column2: 明目張膽的偷竊!

column1: After arresting you, the guards make sure you'll never ever abuse your magic power again... or do anything else that involves the use of your hands.
column2: 逮捕你之後,衛兵確保你這輩子再也別想濫用魔法……或者再用這雙手做任何事。

column1: The guard says, "I warned you once!" as he hauls you off to the local pokey. Now play it again, Sam.
column2: 衛兵一邊把你拖往大牢,一邊說:「我可警告過你一次了!」好啦,重新來過吧,老兄。

column1: Nothing Magical about This
column2: 這可一點都不神奇

column1: What are you doing there?
column2: 你在那兒幹什麼?

column1: My nephew, Shameen the Innkeeper, said you were smooth of tongue! Very well, you may have the bag for only %d centimes.
column2: 我那開客棧的姪子沙明說你很會說話!好吧,這個袋子算你便宜,只要 %d 生丁。

column1: With a family as large as mine, I must make what little profit I can. The bag sells for %d centimes, no less.
column2: 我這一大家子要養,能賺一點是一點。這袋子賣 %d 生丁,一毛都不能少。

column1: There seems to be little point in haggling further, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,再殺價下去恐怕也沒什麼意思了。

column1: Thank you, Effendi. May it please you well.
column2: 謝謝您,閣下。願它合您的心意。

column1: Since what you say is said so well, it must be that I will sell it for only %d centimes.
column2: 您這話說得這麼漂亮,那我就只賣 %d 生丁了。

column1: Alas, I can sell you a waterskin for no less than %d centimes.
column2: 唉,這水袋我最低只能賣 %d 生丁了。

column1: That is not an offer I can take without incurring a loss. How does %d centimes please you?
column2: 這個價我接了可是要賠本的。%d 生丁您看如何?

column1: We have reached a stalemate, Effendi, unless you can accept my final offer of %d centimes.
column2: 閣下,我們僵住了,除非您肯接受我最後開的 %d 生丁。

column1: Obviously, we can not agree on the price my wares are worth paying for.
column2: 看來我們對這貨色該賣多少,是談不攏了。

column1: Here you go, Effendi. Come again.
column2: 給您,閣下。歡迎再來。

column1: Thank you very much, Effendi. I hope to have them ready within two days.
column2: 太感謝您了,閣下。我希望兩天內就能幫您弄好。

column1: Forgive me, but it would be a waste of leather to mend your boots while they are still in relatively good condition, Effendi.
column2: 恕我直言,閣下,您這靴子還算完好,現在補它根本是浪費皮料。

column1: By the sands! These boots are in terrible condition.
column2: 我的老天!這雙靴子簡直慘不忍睹。

column1: You have not the coins to cover the repair costs. It fills me with regret, Effendi, but I cannot repair your boots for nothing.
column2: 您的錢不夠付修理費。閣下,我很遺憾,但我可沒辦法白白幫您補靴子。

column1: I shall do my best to mend your boots. The task should take me but a few hours.
column2: 我會盡力把您的靴子補好。這活兒大概只要幾個鐘頭。

column1: You take the bellows and put them away.
column2: 你拿起風箱,把它收了起來。

column1: The punishment for thievery in this city is to lose a hand. Do you have anything to say in your defense?
column2: 在這座城裡,偷竊的下場是剁掉一隻手。你有什麼話要為自己辯解嗎?

column1: Very nicely done, Hero. You've just destroyed the only bellows in the city. Too bad there's no bellows doctor here, because these need major healing.
column2: 幹得真漂亮啊,英雄。你剛把全城唯一的風箱給毀了。可惜這兒沒有風箱大夫,不然這玩意兒可得好好搶救一番。

column1: You tell the guard that the bellows are the only item capable of containing the Air Elemental blowing around in the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 你告訴衛兵,這風箱是唯一能收服在皇宮廣場上四處亂吹的風元素的東西。

column1: Is that what you are doing here? It is certainly not right to steal, but if you are just borrowing those bellows to save the city, I certainly will not stop you. I will talk to Issur in the morning and explain matters.
column2: 原來你是為了這個?偷東西當然不對,但你若只是借這風箱來救城,我自然不會攔你。我明早會去跟伊蘇爾說清楚原委。

column1: You make up a quick excuse.
column2: 你隨口編了個藉口。

column1: The guard, however, does not seem to believe you.
column2: 可是那衛兵似乎不太相信你。

column1: You already did that.
column2: 這你已經做過了。

column1: For that you must talk to Mirak, who is across the plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去找米拉克,他就在廣場對面。

column1: Mirak is across the plaza.
column2: 米拉克在廣場對面。

column1: The Weapon Shop of Issur is right here in this plaza.
column2: 伊蘇爾的武器店就在這座廣場上。

column1: I am Kiram.
column2: 我是基拉姆。

column1: I have clothes of all sizes and styles, cloth of the finest dyes! If it is made of cloth, then I carry it in my stand. Perhaps I could sell you a cloth bag to contain your extra equipment?
column2: 我這兒有各種尺寸、各種款式的衣裳,還有染得最漂亮的布料!只要是布做的,我這攤子上通通有。要不要我賣您一個布袋,裝您多出來的裝備?

column1: The bags I have are woven so fine they can carry the sand and plaster used to repair the city walls, yet strong enough that they will not break under so much weight. They cost but 50 centimes.
column2: 我這些袋子織得細密,連修城牆用的砂石灰泥都裝得住,又結實到扛這麼重也撐得住。只要 50 生丁。

column1: The price of the cloth bag is %d centimes.
column2: 這布袋賣 %d 生丁。

column1: The price of the clothes is 10 dinars.
column2: 這些衣裳賣 10 第納爾。

column1: The Apothecary is in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 藥舖在噴泉廣場。

column1: The Magic Shop is in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 魔法店在噴泉廣場。

column1: I am Mirak.
column2: 我是米拉克。

column1: For that you must talk to Kiram, who is across the plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去找基拉姆,他就在廣場對面。

column1: Kiram is across the plaza.
column2: 基拉姆在廣場對面。

column1: The belts and boots I sell are made from dyed Saurus skin. The very ones you wear came from my stand. I sell many other items made from leather, but the one thing that I sell which every adventurer needs most in this land is a waterskin.
column2: 我賣的腰帶和靴子都是染過的索魯斯皮做的。您身上穿的那雙就是出自我這攤子。我還賣許多其他皮件,不過在這片土地上,每個冒險者最少不了的,就是一只水袋。

column1: The belts and boots are cut and shaped for the customer and take a few days to create.
column2: 腰帶和靴子都是量身裁剪、依客人腳型製作,得花上幾天才做得好。

column1: The price of the boots is %d centimes.
column2: 這雙靴子賣 %d 生丁。

column1: The price of the belt is 300 centimes.
column2: 這條腰帶賣 300 生丁。

column1: The Ghoul is a creature of the night. It spits acidic bile, capable of searing human flesh to the bone. The thick Saurus hide that I craft my wares from is somewhat resistant to Ghoul bile.
column2: 食屍鬼是夜行的生物。牠會噴出酸液膽汁,能把人肉灼燒見骨。我做貨用的這種厚實索魯斯皮,對食屍鬼的膽汁多少有點抵抗力。

column1: The price of the waterskin is %d centimes.
column2: 這只水袋賣 %d 生丁。

column1: May my waterskin serve you well, Hero.
column2: 願我這水袋好好為你效力,英雄。

column1: You thank Mirak for his kindness in giving you a waterskin free of charge and swear to restore the water supply of the city.
column2: 你感謝米拉克好心送你一只水袋,並發誓要為這座城恢復供水。

column1: I hope you will be successful, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,我祝您馬到成功。

column1: You thank Mirak for his kindness in giving you a waterskin free of charge.
column2: 你感謝米拉克好心免費送你一只水袋。

column1: It was my pleasure, Effendi. After all, what good is selling waterskins if there is no water to get?
column2: 這是我的榮幸,閣下。說到底,要是連水都打不到,賣水袋又有什麼用呢?

column1: We live in interesting times.
column2: 我們可活在多事之秋啊。

column1: You promise Kiram you will destroy the Earth Elemental and thank him for his generosity.
column2: 你向基拉姆保證會摧毀土元素,並感謝他的慷慨。

column1: I know you will prevail, Hero.
column2: 我知道你一定會贏的,英雄。

column1: You tell Kiram the city is safe once again thanks to his generosity.
column2: 你告訴基拉姆,多虧他的慷慨,這座城又恢復了平安。

column1: I am glad I could be of help, Effendi. The money would not be much good to me with the city in ruins, after all.
column2: 我很高興能幫上忙,閣下。說到底,城要是毀了,那些錢對我也沒什麼用處。

column1: You should become a storyteller like my sister Sava.
column2: 你該去當個說書人,就像我妹妹薩瓦那樣。

column1: The talk of the bazaar is that something strange stalks the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 市集上盛傳,有個怪東西在皇宮廣場上潛行作祟。

column1: It is said that the alleyways are not safe to travel today, for the very earth is starting to move.
column2: 聽說今天那些巷弄走不得,因為連大地本身都開始動起來了。

column1: My waterskins can not be filled today, for the very fountain is beset with an Elemental of Water and no one can get even a drink.
column2: 我的水袋今天裝不了水,因為噴泉本身被一隻水元素佔住了,連喝口水都辦不到。

column1: All Katta rejoice that you now go to Raseir, for we know you will restore our homeland.
column2: 卡塔族人都為你即將前往拉希爾而歡欣,因為我們知道,你會讓我們的家園重獲新生。

column1: The finest in leatherwork will I sell to you, Hero from a distant land. My leather is noted the world over for its beauty and durability.
column2: 來自遠方的英雄,我要賣給你的是最上乘的皮件。我的皮革以美觀耐用聞名天下。

column1: Every hero has a need for belts and boots and waterskins. May it be that I sell you something, and I shall be most pleased to serve you.
column2: 每位英雄都少不了腰帶、靴子和水袋。願我能賣你點什麼,我會非常樂意為你效勞。

column1: I am still working on your boots, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你的靴子我還在趕工呢。

column1: Marhaba, Effendi. I have finished your boots. May they serve you well.
column2: 您好,閣下。您的靴子我做好了。願它們好好為您效力。

column1: Remember, if you wear them out, I will be happy to repair them for you for a mere dinar.
column2: 記著,要是穿壞了,只要一第納爾,我很樂意幫您修補。

column1: My second cousin on my mother's side, Tiram, was unable to set up his carpet stand today, for the wind was blowing far too fiercely!
column2: 我母親那邊的遠房表親提拉姆,今天沒法擺他的地毯攤,因為風刮得實在太猛了!

column1: My brother by marriage, Tashtari the brass merchant, had something huge chase him in the streets as he was going to his work this morning!
column2: 我那當連襟的塔什塔里,就是賣銅器的那個,今早去開工時,在街上被一個巨大的東西追著跑!

column1: My aunt, Toshur the potter, was nearly caught by the Elemental which now inhabits the fountain!
column2: 我那做陶的姑姑托舒爾,差點就被現在賴在噴泉裡的那隻元素給逮住了!

column1: My cousin, Sashanan the jeweler, has something from the Katta for you! Seek his stand in the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 我那做珠寶的表親薩沙南,有樣卡塔族交付的東西要給你!到皇宮廣場找他的攤子吧。

column1: Cloth of the finest of weaves and the loveliest of colors, clothes to entice the touch, veils to reveal and conceal, all this have I and more! You can attest to how comfortable and conforming my wares are, for you wear them well!
column2: 最細密的織法、最動人的色彩,摸了讓人愛不釋手的衣裳,半遮半掩的面紗,這些我這兒通通有,還不止呢!我這貨色有多舒適、多合身,您自己最清楚,穿在您身上多好看!

column1: See how fine my clothes look upon you! Make sure all who ask about what you wear know that I, Kiram the cloth merchant, made them for you!
column2: 瞧瞧我這衣裳穿在您身上多好看!誰要是問起您身上這身,可得讓他們知道,是我布商基拉姆親手為您做的!

column1: The door is locked. The Weapon Shop is only open during the day.
column2: 門鎖著。武器店只在白天營業。

column1: Are you crazy? The guard is still watching you.
column2: 你瘋了嗎?那衛兵還盯著你看呢。

column1: You can't reach them from here.
column2: 你從這裡搆不著它們。

column1: The sword is the finest of weapons. It requires both skill and strength in order to wield correctly.
column2: 劍是最上乘的兵器。要正確使劍,技巧和力量缺一不可。

column1: My brother, Rajah sah Tarna, is the current ruler of the Kingdom of Tarna.
column2: 我的兄弟,拉賈·薩·塔納,是塔納王國當今的統治者。

column1: Your physical strength determines the damage dealt by your blows, but power only makes a difference if you are at least as good a swordsman as your opponent.
column2: 你的體力決定每一擊的傷害,但力量要派得上用場,前提是你的劍術至少不輸對手。

column1: Skill is what really matters in combat. A talented swordsman can adapt his style the second the situation requires it. If someone only depends on his physical power in combat, he may be better off wielding a club.
column2: 真正決定勝負的是技巧。高明的劍客能隨機應變,情勢一變就立刻調整套路。要是有人打鬥時只靠一身蠻力,那他還不如去抡根棍子。

column1: Training is vital to a warrior's prowess. You can afford to make mistakes and learn from them. In real combat, making too many mistakes will leave you gravely injured or even dead.
column2: 訓練對戰士的本領至關重要。練功時你犯了錯還能從中學乖。可在真刀真槍的戰鬥裡,錯得太多只會讓你身受重傷,甚至送命。

column1: The nature of the Earth Elemental is to endure. It draws its strength from the very stones that make up this city.
column2: 土元素的本性就是堅忍不拔。它的力量正是來自構成這座城的一塊塊石頭。

column1: It cannot be defeated by a single blow, but must be worn away through your actions and its vulnerability to magical flame, such as a flaming sword.
column2: 它沒辦法一擊就打倒,得靠你一步步把它消磨掉,並且利用它對魔法火焰的弱點,比方說一把燃燒的劍。

column1: Fire disrupts the essence of earth and allows the Elemental to be damaged. Without flame, the Elemental simply cannot be defeated.
column2: 火能擾亂大地的本質,讓元素受到傷害。沒有火焰,這元素根本就打不倒。

column1: I come from a long line of proud warriors. Fighting is our strength, and battle is our glory.
column2: 我出身於一支驕傲的戰士世家。戰鬥是我們的本領,廝殺是我們的榮耀。

column1: My leg was damaged a long time ago when I tried to fight a Demon that was destroying my people. The Demon struck me with its poisoned tail.
column2: 很久以前,我為了對抗一隻正在屠戮我族人的惡魔,腿就傷在那時候。那惡魔用牠帶毒的尾巴擊中了我。

column1: The bone was shattered and, because of the poison, the leg did not heal correctly, even with magic.
column2: 骨頭碎了,又因為中了毒,就算用上魔法,這條腿也沒能好好癒合。

column1: Petalumeir is another citadel in this southern land. It lies to the north of here and is the last place where the caravans stop before returning to Shapeir.
column2: 佩塔魯梅爾是這片南方土地上的另一座要塞城。它在這兒的北方,是商隊返回夏皮爾前最後一個歇腳的地方。

column1: I know little about the valley of Mordavia, other than the fact that it would take many months for one to travel there. It is rumored that the pass leading into the valley has flooded and is now completely inaccessible.
column2: 關於莫達維亞谷地,我知道的不多,只曉得要走到那兒得花上好幾個月。據說通往谷地的那條山道已被洪水淹沒,如今根本進不去了。

column1: Silmaria is a kingdom surrounded by the sea, and is a marvelous place to visit. The King of Marete is a personal friend of mine.
column2: 希爾瑪利亞是個四面環海的王國,是個值得一遊的好地方。瑪雷特的國王是我的私交好友。

column1: The Demon was sent by a wizard who sought to overthrow my kingdom.
column2: 那惡魔是一個妄圖顛覆我王國的巫師派來的。

column1: I defeated the Demon and eventually the wizard, but I had to give up the rule of my kingdom to my brother.
column2: 我擊敗了那惡魔,最終也擊敗了那巫師,但我不得不把王國的統治權交給我的兄弟。

column1: I ruled the Kingdom of Tarna, until I had to choose between ruling my people or preventing the wizard from doing greater evil.
column2: 我原本治理著塔納王國,直到我必須在「繼續統治我的子民」和「阻止那巫師作下更大的惡」之間做出抉擇。

column1: I do not regret the choice I made.
column2: 我不後悔自己當初的抉擇。

column1: The wizard turned out to be a Demon who sought through magical means to break the barriers between his world and ours.
column2: 那巫師原來竟是一隻惡魔,想藉魔法的手段打破他的世界與我們世界之間的屏障。

column1: It took many years, but I at last managed to send the Demon-wizard back to his own world.
column2: 這花了許多年,但我終究還是把那惡魔巫師送回了他自己的世界。

column1: My brother became a most wise ruler of Tarna, and I learned what it is to be a paladin.
column2: 我的兄弟成了塔納最賢明的統治者,而我則領悟了身為聖騎士的真諦。

column1: The Kingdom of Tarna lies to the southwest many, many miles from here. I have not seen it for a very long while.
column2: 塔納王國在這兒的西南方,離此地有許許多多哩遠。我已經很久很久沒見過它了。

column1: The power of a warrior comes not from his strength, but rather from his will, his inner nature.
column2: 戰士的力量不是來自他的蠻力,而是來自他的意志,他內在的本性。

column1: I find I am becoming less a warrior and more a philosopher as I get older.
column2: 我發現自己年紀越大,越不像個戰士,反倒越像個哲人了。

column1: The will is the very essence of your soul.
column2: 意志,正是你靈魂的本質所在。

column1: I was given the sword 'Soulforge' when I first became a paladin. Only a paladin has the will to make it flame.
column2: 我初成聖騎士時,獲贈了「鑄魂」這把劍。唯有聖騎士才有那股意志,能讓它燃起火焰。

column1: The way of the paladin is to seek to know that which truly is.
column2: 聖騎士之道,在於探求事物真正的本相。

column1: The paladin strives to learn his own inner nature and that of others.
column2: 聖騎士努力認清自己內在的本性,也認清他人的本性。

column1: The paladin does whatever needs to be done to bring light to the world.
column2: 凡是能為世界帶來光明的事,聖騎士都會去做。

column1: Not for glory, not for gain, the paladin becomes a paladin because it is his will.
column2: 不為榮耀,不為私利,聖騎士之所以成為聖騎士,只因這是他的意志。

column1: She is my friend. She is a good fighter and teacher, and you would do well to practice with her.
column2: 她是我的朋友。她既是好戰士,也是好老師,你能跟她過幾招對你大有好處。

column1: She can teach you much about the warrior skills.
column2: 她能教你許多戰士技藝。

column1: In order to become a warrior in Uhura's tribe, one must be speedy, accurate and agile. This is easily recognized in her fighting style.
column2: 想在烏胡拉的部族裡當上戰士,人就得迅捷、精準又靈巧。從她的戰鬥架式上,這一點一望即知。

column1: Uhura mostly depends on her agility and mobility to get the better of her opponents.
column2: 烏胡拉主要靠她的靈巧和身法來壓制對手。

column1: Properly evading or blocking Uhura's attacks should be the first thing on your mind at all times. Attack when you see an opening, but be prepared for counterattacks.
column2: 無論何時,把烏胡拉的攻勢閃好、擋好,都該是你腦子裡擺第一位的事。看到破綻就出手,但也要提防她反擊。

column1: He reminds me of a lion cub.
column2: 他讓我想起一頭小獅子。

column1: When I was younger, I did much adventuring and exploring. That was, of course, before my leg was injured. I do not travel much now. I am here at the Sultan's request.
column2: 我年輕時,曾四處冒險探索。當然,那都是我這條腿受傷以前的事了。如今我不太遠行了。我來這兒,是應蘇丹之請。

column1: My name is Rasha Rakeesh SahTarna. I am called Rakeesh. I am of the Liontaur people.
column2: 我的名字是拉夏·拉基什·薩塔納。人們喚我拉基什。我是獅人族的一員。

column1: The Sultan summoned me to Shapeir to aid against the dark magic which even now hovers near this city. I fear, though, that I am not the hero I was once. Perhaps you will become the hero they need.
column2: 蘇丹召我來夏皮爾,助這座城抵禦此刻仍在城外盤桓的黑魔法。只是我恐怕已不是當年那個英雄了。或許,你會成為他們所需要的英雄。

column1: A true hero knows what must be done when there is need, and he must be able to do it.
column2: 真正的英雄,在危難當頭時知道該做什麼,而且必須有本事去做到。

column1: A Ghoul is a very dangerous thing to fight. Its very touch draws your life from you. You must be very quick to avoid its touch.
column2: 跟食屍鬼交手是非常危險的事。光是被牠碰一下,就會把你的性命抽走。你動作得快到能躲開牠的觸碰才行。

column1: The Terrorsaurus is very swift, and its powerful hindquarters can easily disembowel a man.
column2: 恐怖龍速度極快,牠那強而有力的後腿,輕易就能把人開膛破肚。

column1: The Griffin is a tough fighter, but it seldom attacks humans unless it is provoked.
column2: 獅鷲是個難纏的對手,但除非被激怒,牠很少主動攻擊人類。

column1: Brigands search the desert for unwary travelers. They will kill gladly and steal whatever they can.
column2: 強盜在沙漠裡四處尋找疏於防備的旅人。他們殺人毫不手軟,能搶的東西絕不放過。

column1: When a pack of Jackalmen attack, it is the weakest which strikes first.  Keep your blade trained upon one at a time until that foe is defeated, or you will find yourself overwhelmed by their numbers.
column2: 一群豺狼人來襲時,打頭陣的往往是最弱的那個。把你的劍鋒一次只對準一個,直到把那敵手解決掉,否則你會被牠們的數量給淹沒。

column1: It is unwise to get into a fight with a Scorpion unless you have taken a poison cure pill and are very skilled at dodging and parrying. Few escape from combat with a Scorpion.
column2: 除非你已吞下解毒丸,而且閃躲、招架的功夫了得,否則跟蠍子開打可不是明智之舉。能從跟蠍子的搏鬥中全身而退的人沒幾個。

column1: The Simbani are a roaming tribe from East Fricana. My friend, Uhura, is a Simbani warrior.
column2: 辛巴尼是來自東弗里卡納的游牧部族。我的朋友烏胡拉,就是辛巴尼戰士。

column1: You can find pills at the local Apothecary. You must take the poison cure pill before you are struck by the Scorpion's venomous tail.
column2: 你可以在本地的藥舖找到藥丸。你必須趕在被蠍子的毒尾刺中之前,先吞下解毒丸。

column1: This is a fine kingdom, and its ruler is very wise, but there are signs of dark magic seeking to overwhelm Shapeir.
column2: 這是個美好的王國,它的統治者也十分賢明,但已有跡象顯示,黑魔法正企圖吞噬夏皮爾。

column1: The rumors from Raseir are that laws have become more important than men, and that it has become a crime to think. Few go to that city now, and even fewer return.
column2: 拉希爾傳來的消息說,那裡的法律比人命還重要,連動腦思考都成了罪。如今沒幾個人敢去那座城,去了能回來的更是寥寥無幾。

column1: A Djinn is a creature of magic. Some are good and some bad. They are all dangerous because they have such power.
column2: 精靈是魔法生靈,有善有惡。但牠們個個都危險,因為手裡握著那等駭人的力量。

column1: To understand the nature of the Fire Elemental, speak to someone who understands the essence of flame.
column2: 想看透火元素的本質,就去找一個懂得火焰精髓的人聊聊吧。

column1: To understand the nature of the Water Elemental, seek the person to whom water is inner nature.
column2: 想看透水元素的本質,就去尋訪那位以水為本性的人。

column1: There is someone else in this city whose knowledge of the nature of air far surpasses my own. Ask the one who has his head in the clouds.
column2: 這城裡有個人,對風的本質懂得遠比我多。去問問那個成天把頭埋在雲端裡的人吧。

column1: The Earth Elemental is the magical essence of stone and rock. It is extremely dangerous and difficult to defeat, unless you understand its nature.
column2: 土元素是石頭與岩塊的魔法精魄。除非你摸透牠的本質,否則牠凶險難纏,極難擊敗。

column1: An Elemental is formed by magic which gives being to the essence of an element. It is chaotic and extremely destructive.
column2: 元素生靈是由魔法將某種元素的精髓賦予了生命而成。牠混亂無序,破壞力驚人。

column1: There are many monsters out on the desert. They are all easy to avoid if you simply do not go out in the desert to look for them.
column2: 沙漠裡頭怪物多得是。不過要躲也容易——只要你別自己跑進沙漠去找牠們就行了。

column1: Since a dagger is unable to deliver much damage, a magical charge is often a great help. The increased chance of knocking your opponent back is especially useful in order to keep an enemy at bay.
column2: 匕首造不成多大傷害,這時附上一道魔法蓄能往往幫助極大。把對手震退的機會提高了,要把敵人擋在身外尤其管用。

column1: A Zap spell can be the only way out of a corner at times. Casting leaves you vulnerable, however.
column2: 被逼到絕境時,蓄能咒有時是你唯一的活路。不過施法的當下,你也會門戶大開。

column1: Fireballs are powerful projectiles and cost-effective. They do not pack a physical punch though. Like Force Bolts, they cannot be used in close quarters.
column2: 火球是威力強又划算的遠程攻擊,只是欠缺物理衝擊力。跟力箭一樣,近身肉搏時派不上用場。

column1: Force Bolts do not do as much damage as Flame Darts, but they pack a powerful physical punch and are useful to keep the opponent out of melee range. Like Flame Darts, they cannot be cast in melee range.
column2: 力箭的傷害不如火鏢,但物理衝擊力十足,很適合把對手擋在近戰距離之外。跟火鏢一樣,貼身肉搏時施不出來。

column1: Dazzle can blind an opponent, but causes no actual damage. An opponent can easily avoid the effects by shielding his eyes in time.
column2: 炫目咒能讓對手暫時失明,卻造不成實際傷害。對方只要及時遮住眼睛,就能輕鬆躲過。

column1: Make good use of each successful cast. It may be wise to save it for emergencies, like when you need to get out of a corner, but do not have time to cast a Zap spell.
column2: 每一次成功施法都要好好把握。明智的做法是把它留到緊要關頭——譬如你被逼到死角,卻又來不及施蓄能咒的時候。

column1: A dagger is incapable of doing large amounts of damage, but it can still be deadly in the hands of an experienced wielder. They can double as projectiles.
column2: 匕首造不成大量傷害,可一旦落到老手手裡,照樣能要人命。它還能拿來當飛刀擲出。

column1: Daggers are best thrown when the target is just outside melee range. I personally do not prefer their use.
column2: 匕首最適合在目標剛好在近戰範圍外時擲出。不過我個人是不太愛用它。

column1: There are several techniques that can aid you in battle. Proper use of each of them is often the key to victory.
column2: 有好幾種招式能在戰鬥裡幫上你的忙。把每一招用對地方,往往就是致勝的關鍵。

column1: Magic surrounds all of us in this world and is a part of us also. Aziza has many more answers than I.
column2: 在這世上,魔法環繞著我們每一個人,也是我們的一部分。這方面阿姿莎懂得比我多得多。

column1: The use of magic is often considered unfair by warriors unless the opponent knows magic as well. Of course, a dagger against a sword can be deemed unfair too, so if your life depends on the outcome of the battle, you would be foolish not to rely on spells.
column2: 戰士們常覺得用魔法不夠光明正大,除非對手也會魔法。當然啦,拿匕首對上長劍同樣可算不公平,所以要是這一戰攸關你的性命,不靠咒語才是真的傻。

column1: During training or competition, the use of magic will merely result in disapproval and disrespect. Spectators will not appreciate your use of the Dazzle spell either.
column2: 在訓練或比武場上動用魔法,只會招來白眼與不屑。觀眾也絕不會欣賞你施炫目咒這一手。

column1: The inverse swing is a quick and easy follow-up whenever your initial swing connects. Repeated double swings can wear down enemies quickly. Unless your opponent has sharp reflexes, your second swing will always connect.
column2: 只要第一刀砍中,反手揮砍就是又快又順手的接續招。連續的雙重揮砍能迅速耗垮敵人。除非對手反應極快,否則你的第二刀總能砍中。

column1: The plunge is a quick and easy follow-up whenever your swing connects. Repeated plunges can make just the difference in damage you need.
column2: 只要一刀砍中,突刺就是又快又順的接續招。反覆突刺,正好能補足你需要的那點傷害差距。

column1: Chaining your inverse swing into an overhead slash takes practice, but once mastered it will make you lethal at close range. The only disadvantage is that your opponent may be knocked out of range or to the ground by the preceding swings.
column2: 把反手揮砍接成頭頂劈斬得多練,可一旦練成,近身就能要命。唯一的缺點是,前頭那幾刀說不定會把對手震出範圍外、或直接撂倒在地。

column1: An overhead slash is strong enough to knock back the fiercest foes, and thus it is a good way to gain terrain.
column2: 頭頂劈斬力道夠猛,連最凶悍的敵人都能震退,所以是個搶佔地利的好辦法。

column1: The rising slash is a perfect counter against high attacks. The opponent's attacking momentum will often prevent him from dodging.
column2: 上撩斬是化解高位攻擊的完美反擊。對手出手的衝勢,往往讓他來不及閃避。

column1: Counterattacks are what separate an experienced swordsman from one who is still learning the basics. An opponent whose weapon is blocked will be off-balance for a split second.
column2: 懂不懂反擊,正是老練劍客與還在練基本功的人之間的分水嶺。武器被擋下的對手,會有那麼一瞬間重心不穩。

column1: Quick retaliation will turn your adversary's aggression against him, bringing quick defeat to those who rely solely on relentless attacks. Though swift, counterattacks can still be blocked or dodged by your opponent. You must always keep your guard up.
column2: 迅速反擊能把對手的攻勢反過來對付他自己,讓那些只會一味猛攻的人速速落敗。反擊雖快,照樣可能被對方擋下或閃過。你的防備可絕不能鬆懈。

column1: The lunge is useful as a surprise attack, but only someone who has mastered his weapon will be able to use it at will. The lunge depends more on instinct than anything else.
column2: 刺擊很適合拿來偷襲,可只有真正把武器練到爐火純青的人,才能隨心所欲地使出來。這一招靠的多半是直覺,而非別的什麼。

column1: The leaping lunge cannot be employed directly, but will be randomly activated when jumping back.
column2: 躍刺沒辦法直接使出,而是在你向後跳開時隨機觸發。

column1: The skull-splitter is one of the deadliest attacks one can perform, but requires high dodging skill and experience with the blade.
column2: 劈顱斬是人所能使出的最致命招式之一,不過需要高超的閃避身手與用劍的歷練。

column1: Once mastered, the swordsman can jump into the air, evading low attacks, and let gravity and his own strength join forces to deliver an extremely lethal vertical slash.
column2: 一旦練成,劍客便能躍上半空、避開低位攻擊,讓重力與自身力道合而為一,劈下一記殺氣騰騰的垂直斬。

column1: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid watches over his people and strives to make sure they are happy and content.
