column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德守護著他的子民,費盡心思要讓大家過得快樂安樂。

column1: It sorrows him greatly to think of Raseir. It angers him greatly to think of the one behind Raseir.
column2: 一想到拉希爾,他便深感悲痛;一想到拉希爾背後那個人,他更是怒火中燒。

column1: The Emir Arus of Raseir was well-loved, but not wise. The current Emir Hasan of Raseir is neither loved nor wise.
column2: 拉希爾的前埃米爾阿魯斯深受愛戴,卻不夠睿智。如今這位埃米爾哈山,既不得人心,也談不上明智。

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is a very beautiful place. I believe you will like it.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿是個極美的地方,我想你會喜歡的。

column1: The caravan to Raseir leaves in a few weeks. You should consult with the guards at the gate to the city.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊再過幾週就要出發。你該去城門口找衛兵打聽打聽。

column1: Someone without much money may be far richer than someone with much money.
column2: 一個沒什麼錢的人,說不定遠比一個腰纏萬貫的人富有。

column1: That is a very dangerous subject about which to inquire.
column2: 那可是個打聽起來非常危險的話題。

column1: This place is very much a home for me.
column2: 這地方對我來說,很有家的味道。

column1: Your Katta friends have a most excellent inn, from all I hear.
column2: 聽大家都說,你那兩位卡塔朋友開的客棧好得不得了。

column1: I find the warm weather here most pleasant on my old bones.
column2: 我這把老骨頭,覺得這裡暖洋洋的天氣舒服極了。

column1: The fountain which forms this city is indeed magical. It gives life to all that live around it.
column2: 孕育出這座城的噴泉,確實帶著魔力。它賜予了周遭一切生靈生命。

column1: The stories told about Raseir lead one to suspect that it is not a very pleasant place to be.
column2: 關於拉希爾的種種傳聞,叫人不禁懷疑那地方住起來大概不怎麼舒坦。

column1: Her place is in the middle of the web of alleys to the southeast.
column2: 她的住處就在東南方那片像蛛網似的巷弄正中央。

column1: He seeks knowledge in the stars. I find knowledge everywhere.
column2: 他在星辰之中尋求學問,而我則是處處都能尋得學問。

column1: Aziza is a very powerful enchantress, wise in the ways of the elements. You might visit her house near the Shmali Tarik, if you can win her respect.
column2: 阿姿莎是位法力高強的女術士,深諳元素之道。要是你能贏得她的敬重,不妨去施瑪利塔里克附近的她家拜訪拜訪。

column1: The Dervish speaks. All listen, but few understand.
column2: 苦行僧開口了。眾人都在聽,真正聽懂的卻沒幾個。

column1: It is easy to underestimate the Katta. They are shrewd, and see much.
column2: 人很容易小看卡塔族。他們精明得很,看得也透。

column1: The merchants will gladly sell you anything they have.
column2: 商人們可樂意把手上的任何東西賣給你。

column1: There is much truth in what he speaks.
column2: 他說的話,有很多都是實情。

column1: The adventurer's logbook contains the stories of many great fighters' adventures.
column2: 冒險者日誌裡記載著許多偉大鬥士的冒險故事。

column1: We are a proud race of strong warriors, although we do tend to be a bit vain and perhaps a bit boastful.
column2: 我們是個驕傲的強悍戰士民族,不過嘛,我們也確實有點愛慕虛榮,或許還有點愛吹噓。

column1: It is getting far easier to sit and talk about how to solve problems than to do something about them, I am afraid.
column2: 恐怕如今坐著空談該怎麼解決問題,已經遠比真正去做些什麼來得容易了。

column1: I have personally never encountered one.
column2: 我自己倒是從來沒碰上過一個。

column1: The desert is very dangerous to the unwary. Even a powerful warrior equipped with plenty of water can perish if he travels during the heat of the day.
column2: 沙漠對粗心大意的人極其凶險。哪怕是帶足了水的強悍戰士,要是在大白天最熱的時候趕路,照樣可能命喪當場。

column1: Perhaps there is someone else who would know more about that than I, for I know very little about it.
column2: 這事我所知甚少,或許另有他人比我懂得更多。

column1: I fear that I can add nothing further to your knowledge.
column2: 恐怕我沒辦法再為你補充些什麼了。

column1: Surely you have not already forgotten your noble deed.
column2: 你該不會這麼快就把自己那樁義舉給忘了吧。

column1: This city is a perfect place to improve your skills at climbing and balancing.
column2: 這座城是磨練攀爬與平衡身手的絕佳去處。

column1: I have not been back there since the Emir Arus was overthrown. Entertainers and wagering have been forbidden there.
column2: 自從埃米爾阿魯斯被推翻,我就再也沒回去過那裡。賣藝跟賭博在那兒都被禁了。

column1: I know only that it makes the challenge all the greater, for it is hard to cross with such wind.
column2: 我只知道這會讓挑戰難上加難,畢竟刮著這麼大的風,要走過去可不容易。

column1: Ah, so you are quite the hero! It is no wonder you passed my challenge with such ease.
column2: 啊,原來你是個了不得的英雄!難怪你能這麼輕鬆過了我這一關。

column1: Ah, so you are quite the hero! Surely, then, crossing a simple rope would be no match for your skills. Care to try?
column2: 啊,原來你是個了不得的英雄!那麼,走過區區一條繩子,想必難不倒你的身手吧。要不要試試看?

column1: I have not seen the Fire Elemental for myself, but according to those who have, it is quite a fearsome sight to behold.
column2: 火元素我倒是沒親眼見過,不過據看過的人說,那場面可駭人得很。

column1: The Sultan (May He Rule Forever!) must be a wise man, for he allows those like myself to make a living without interference.
column2: 蘇丹(願他萬世統治!)想必是位明君,因為他讓我這種人能不受干擾地討口飯吃。

column1: The Emir Arus used to watch as I performed in Raseir, and throw me coins. He was always laughing. It is a shame that he is gone now.
column2: 從前埃米爾阿魯斯總愛看我在拉希爾表演,還賞我錢幣。他老是笑呵呵的。如今他不在了,真叫人惋惜。

column1: I hope someday for a command performance there.
column2: 我盼著有朝一日能在那兒來場御前獻藝。

column1: The caravan to Raseir takes a week to cross the desert, but it is never boring.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊得花上一週橫越沙漠,不過這趟路可一點都不無聊。

column1: That is a place best avoided.
column2: 那地方,最好還是別去。

column1: I do not know about such things, but I have heard rumors that the Moneychanger has some connections.
column2: 這種事我是不懂,不過我聽到風聲,說那位錢莊兌幣商有點門路。

column1: The weather is seldom windy this time of year.
column2: 一年裡這個時節,難得颳風。

column1: I make a living by challenging others.
column2: 我靠著向旁人下戰帖討生活。

column1: The Guild Hall is a good place for me to be working, for many pass by.
column2: 公會大廳是個讓我擺攤的好地方,因為來往的人多。

column1: There are too many people and things in that plaza for me to set myself up there.
column2: 那座廣場上人多物雜,我可沒法在那兒擺攤。

column1: There have been rumors about ill omens around the city.
column2: 城裡近來流傳著一些不祥之兆的傳言。

column1: I have heard that she is not above working with thieves.
column2: 我聽說,她可不介意跟盜賊打交道。

column1: The many merchants around town help attract my customers.
column2: 城裡這麼多商人,倒幫我招攬了不少客人。

column1: I never challenge Kattas. They are too good at this.
column2: 卡塔族我可從來不下戰帖。這玩意兒他們太在行了。

column1: I am called Agi the Agile.
column2: 人家都叫我「靈巧阿吉」。

column1: Do you wish to wager, Effendi?
column2: 閣下,您可想下注?

column1: You have but to give me a dinar, and you will gain ten, if you walk the rope.
column2: 您只消給我一枚第納爾,要是走得過這繩子,就能贏得十枚,閣下。

column1: You have but to give me a dinar, and you will gain five, if you walk the rope.
column2: 您只消給我一枚第納爾,要是走得過這繩子,就能贏得五枚,閣下。

column1: Well, then, Effendi, where is your dinar?
column2: 那麼,閣下,您的第納爾呢?

column1: Yes, yes. Climb up and win the ten dinars.
column2: 好,好。爬上去,把那十枚第納爾贏回家吧。

column1: Yes, yes. Climb up and win the five dinars.
column2: 好,好。爬上去,把那五枚第納爾贏回家吧。

column1: Effendi, you must not turn away now that we have wagered. Climb the rope!
column2: 閣下,賭都賭下了,您可不能這會兒掉頭就走。爬上繩子去!

column1: Is there no one of courage among you? Will no one take my challenge?
column2: 你們裡頭就沒一個有膽量的嗎?難道沒人敢接我的戰帖?

column1: Upon that, I can say but little, for I only know a little about it.
column2: 這事我只能說上一點點,因為我懂得也就那麼一點點。

column1: That subject has little to do with the challenge.
column2: 那話題跟這場挑戰,可沒什麼干係。

column1: Goodbye, agile one. May you someday meet a worthier challenge than the one I could offer you.
column2: 再會了,靈巧的朋友。願你有朝一日能遇上比我所能提供的更值得一試的挑戰。

column1: I look forward to challenging you again in the future. Until we meet again, goodbye.
column2: 我盼著日後能再向你下一回戰帖。後會有期,再會。

column1: Are you sure you don't wish to take up my challenge before you leave, friend?
column2: 朋友,你當真不想在走之前接下我的挑戰嗎?

column1: I can't accept a challenge for any less, Effendi.
column2: 再少我可就接不了這戰帖了,閣下。

column1: I would gladly listen to your stories, Hero, if I wasn't busy making a living right now.
column2: 英雄,要不是我這會兒忙著討生活,我可樂意聽聽你的故事。

column1: Farewell. We shall speak again soon, no doubt.
column2: 告辭了。咱們不久後想必還會再聊。

column1: Hero types don't do that sort of thing.
column2: 像英雄這種人,可不幹這種事。

column1: You thank Rakeesh for the honor of getting to wield his sword in battle.
column2: 你向拉基什道謝,感謝他賜予你在戰鬥中揮舞他寶劍的榮耀。

column1: It is I who should be thanking you for allowing me to contribute to the defeat of the Earth Elemental, young Hero.
column2: 年輕的英雄,該道謝的是我才對——多虧你,我才有份參與擊敗土元素。

column1: You tell a few things about your adventures in Spielburg.
column2: 你說起了一些自己在史畢柏格的冒險經歷。

column1: You tell about your encounters with Baba Yaga in Spielburg.
column2: 你說起了自己在史畢柏格與芭芭雅嘎交手的經過。

column1: I am sorry to hear that you could not invoke the counter-curse. However, take heart, as the baron's own daughter sounds more than capable of dealing with that particular threat.
column2: 聽說你沒能施出那道解咒,我深感遺憾。不過你寬心吧,聽起來男爵的千金本人,綽綽有餘能應付那樁威脅。

column1: You were most brave in dealing with her. Very few adventurers can claim to have entered the lair of an ogress and lived to tell the tale.
column2: 你對付她時當真勇氣可嘉。能闖進女食人魔的巢穴、還活著回來說嘴的冒險者,可沒幾個。

column1: You tell about WIT and how you decided not to take up a full-time study as wizard.
column2: 你說起了巫師技術學院,以及自己為何決定不投入全職的巫師修習。

column1: You are better off keeping that information to yourself. You can already tell beforehand that Rakeesh would not at all approve of your actions.
column2: 那件事,你還是爛在肚子裡比較好。你早就料得到,拉基什對你的所作所為絕不會苟同。

column1: You tell about EOF and how you ascended to one of the highest ranks of the organization with a few quick swats of the blade.
column2: 你說起了永恆戰士團,以及自己如何揮上幾記俐落的劍,就爬到了那組織數一數二的高位。

column1: Congratulations on winning their respect. Still, I ask you to keep in mind that you will often earn more respect through actions of peace than through those of battle.
column2: 恭喜你贏得了他們的敬重。不過我仍要請你記著一件事——以和為貴的作為,往往比戰鬥更能為你贏來敬重。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about the EOF and how you ended up with a beginner's rank because you couldn't bring yourself to kill a defenseless adversary. Rakeesh nods understandingly.
column2: 你向拉基什說起永恆戰士團,以及自己如何因為下不了手殺一個毫無還手之力的對手,結果只落得個新手的位階。拉基什會意地點了點頭。

column1: True honor is often misjudged by people. It is one of the things that can make a paladin's life very difficult sometimes. Yet, your own instincts are often better indicators than the opinions of others.
column2: 真正的榮譽,常常被人誤解。這正是有時讓聖騎士活得格外艱難的緣由之一。然而,你自己的直覺,往往比旁人的看法更值得依循。

column1: You made the right decision. Killing a defenseless opponent is murder, plain and simple, no matter what others tell you.
column2: 你的決定沒有錯。殺一個毫無還手之力的對手就是謀殺,再簡單不過,不管旁人怎麼說。

column1: You relate the tale of how you extinguished the Fire Elemental.
column2: 你娓娓道來自己是如何撲滅火元素的。

column1: Yes, it has been the talk of the city for the last few days. I have heard tell that a Fire Elemental can no longer be controlled by its conjurer after being created. Containing such a thing is a greater feat than you may have considered it to be.
column2: 是啊,這幾天這事可是全城的話題。我聽人說過,火元素一旦被召喚出來,連召喚牠的人都再也駕馭不了。能制伏這麼個東西,可比你想像中還要了不起得多。

column1: You relate the tale of how you imprisoned the Air Elemental.
column2: 你娓娓道來自己是如何將風元素囚禁起來的。

column1: People believed the creature to be invulnerable, simply because it could not be touched. It is a good thing you have proven them wrong, my friend.
column2: 人們以為那生靈刀槍不入,只因為碰都碰不著牠。我的朋友,幸虧你證明了他們是錯的。

column1: You relate the tale of how you blew the Water Elemental away.
column2: 你娓娓道來自己是如何把水元素吹散的。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about how Aziza revealed the true identity of your riding Saurus.
column2: 你向拉基什說起阿姿莎是如何揭穿你那頭坐騎索魯斯的真實身分。

column1: So, the missing Emir has been among us all along. There is no need to worry about him any further. He shall be safe here and I am certain that he will return to his former self in time.
column2: 這麼說,那位失蹤的埃米爾,原來一直就在我們之中。再不必為他擔心了。他在這兒會很安全,我也確信他遲早會恢復成原來的模樣。

column1: I am more concerned about the one who enchanted the Emir in the first place.
column2: 我更掛心的,是當初對埃米爾施下魔咒的那個人。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about how you turned the beast back into an ungrateful little twerp.
column2: 你向拉基什說起自己是如何把那頭野獸變回了一個忘恩負義的小傢伙。

column1: After all the praise you have received in Shapeir, you must have been disappointed by the man's reaction. Heroes do not always get the credit they deserve. This is particularly true for paladins. Never let another's ungratefulness discourage you, my friend.
column2: 在夏皮爾受了那麼多讚譽,那人的反應想必讓你大失所望吧。英雄並不總能得到應有的肯定,聖騎士尤其如此。我的朋友,千萬別讓別人的忘恩負義叫你灰心。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about the beast you found in the desert.
column2: 你向拉基什說起你在沙漠裡發現的那頭野獸。

column1: I have never heard of such a creature before. It is possible the beast is under an enchantment of some kind. Perhaps someone else can help you break the spell.
column2: 這樣的生靈,我可從來沒聽說過。那野獸很可能是中了某種魔咒。或許另有人能幫你解開這道咒。

column1: You tell Rakeesh the name Al Scurva mentioned to you.
column2: 你把阿爾·斯庫瓦對你提過的那個名字告訴了拉基什。

column1: Ad Avis... the name sounds very familiar. I believe he was connected to the court of the former Emir.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯……這名字聽著好生耳熟。我記得他跟前任埃米爾的宮廷有些瓜葛。

column1: You tell about your meeting with Aziza.
column2: 你說起了自己與阿姿莎會面的經過。

column1: So you have met Aziza? She is a very wise person whose magic and knowledge have benefited the sultan on many occasions. She greatly values etiquette, so if you want her to share her knowledge with you, a formal and polite approach is advised.
column2: 這麼說你見過阿姿莎了?她是位極有智慧的人,她的魔法與學識曾多次造福蘇丹。她非常看重禮數,所以你若想讓她與你分享所學,最好以正式而有禮的態度相待。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about Omar's performances at the inn.
column2: 你向拉基什說起奧瑪爾在客棧裡的表演。

column1: Poetry is one of the aspects of Shapeirian culture that many foreigners, including myself, have grown to admire. In my own kingdom, people generally think war tales are more exciting to listen to than poems.
column2: 詩歌是夏皮爾文化中,許多外邦人——包括我在內——日漸傾慕的一面。在我自己的國度,人們大多覺得戰爭故事聽起來比詩來得帶勁。

column1: You tell about how you helped out Julanar in the desert.
column2: 你說起自己在沙漠裡如何幫了茱拉娜爾一把。

column1: That was an act of great kindness. Perhaps someday, when her spell is completely broken, she will cross paths with you again.
column2: 那真是莫大的善舉。也許有朝一日,當她身上的咒語徹底解除,她會再與你相遇。

column1: You tell about how the Astrologer told you your fortune.
column2: 你說起占星師為你卜算命運的事。

column1: I have heard many prophecies myself. The dilemma with predictions is that they always have multiple interpretations. In a sense, this is also a good thing. For knowing one's exact future before it unravels brings either laziness or desperation.
column2: 我自己也聽過不少預言。預言為難之處,在於它們總有好幾種解法。從某個角度看,這倒也是好事。因為若一個人在命運揭曉前就把未來看得一清二楚,等著他的不是怠惰,就是絕望。

column1: You tell about your last training session with Uhura.
column2: 你說起上一回和烏胡拉的訓練。

column1: Do not lose heart if you are defeated at first. Training is not about winning -- it is about learning.
column2: 就算一開始輸了也別灰心。訓練的重點不在贏,而在學。

column1: Do not lose heart if you are defeated at first. Remain focused during battle and exploit any opening you detect.
column2: 就算一開始輸了也別灰心。戰鬥時保持專注,一抓到破綻就攻進去。

column1: You are making quick progress. I do not think Uhura will be able to keep beating you much longer.
column2: 你進步神速。我看烏胡拉再也壓不了你多久了。

column1: You are making quick progress. Your previous match seemed like a very close call. Perhaps the next session will result in a new victor.
column2: 你進步神速。上一場打得相當險,說不定下一回合就要換人贏了。

column1: Congratulations on winning, although it seemed like a close call. I suggest that you continue training until you can win without sustaining major injuries in combat.
column2: 恭喜你贏了,雖然贏得有點驚險。我建議你繼續練,練到能在交手中全身而退、不掛大彩為止。

column1: Congratulations on winning. Your combat skills are definitely improving.
column2: 恭喜你獲勝。你的戰鬥本領確實在精進。

column1: You have talent. Uhura is considered a very capable combatant by her people.
column2: 你很有天分。在族人眼中,烏胡拉可是個相當厲害的鬥士。

column1: You have talent. If this continues, you shall be teaching Uhura instead of the other way around.
column2: 你很有天分。再這麼下去,該是你來教烏胡拉,而不是反過來了。

column1: You tell Rakeesh you'd like to test your skills against Uhura.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,你想找烏胡拉切磋身手。

column1: Simply enter the training room inside the Guild Hall. Uhura will be happy to train with you.
column2: 走進公會大廳裡的訓練室就成。烏胡拉很樂意陪你練。

column1: You tell Rakeesh you were honored to train with him.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,能與他切磋是你的榮幸。

column1: Thank you, my friend. I hope you enjoyed the experience despite the outcome.
column2: 謝謝你,我的朋友。不論勝負,但願你享受了這場交手。

column1: Thank you, my friend. You put up a good fight.
column2: 謝謝你,我的朋友。你打得很漂亮。

column1: You were a very worthy opponent. I greatly enjoyed the challenge.
column2: 你是個極為難得的對手。這場較量我打得痛快。

column1: You were a very worthy opponent. I can not even recall the last match where I had to try so hard to win.
column2: 你是個極為難得的對手。我都記不得上一回得這麼拚命才贏是什麼時候了。

column1: The honor was all mine. It seems I have met my match in you.
column2: 榮幸的是我才對。看來我在你身上遇到旗鼓相當的對手了。

column1: The honor was all mine. You more than deserved your victory.
column2: 榮幸的是我才對。這場勝利,你當之無愧。

column1: I should be the one to feel honored, young Hero. You will have a difficult time finding an adversary whose skills match your own.
column2: 該感到榮幸的是我才對,年輕的英雄。往後你恐怕很難再找到本事與你相當的對手了。

column1: I should be the one to feel honored, young Hero. You certainly are the most competent swordsman I have ever met.
column2: 該感到榮幸的是我才對,年輕的英雄。你絕對是我見過最高明的劍士。

column1: You tell Rakeesh you'd like to test your skills against his.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,你想跟他較量一番身手。

column1: I am honored by your challenge. Just enter the training room behind me and tell me when you are ready.
column2: 你的挑戰是我的榮幸。走進我身後的訓練室,準備好了就告訴我。

column1: I am sorry, young Hero, but since you are not a swordsman, you would receive little benefit from a training session with me.
column2: 抱歉了,年輕的英雄,你並非劍士,跟我練上一場對你益處不大。

column1: You tell Rakeesh about how you met Shameen and Shema in Spielburg and how they convinced you to accompany them to Shapeir after the ceremony at Spielburg castle.
column2: 你向拉基什說起自己在史畢柏格遇見沙明和舍瑪,以及他們如何在史畢柏格城堡的典禮過後,說動你一同前往夏皮爾。

column1: I have heard that your friends left Shapeir in search for a hero. It explains their presence in Spielburg. The Katta generally avoid regions with colder climates unless absolutely essential.
column2: 我聽說你那兩位朋友離開夏皮爾,是去尋訪英雄的。這就難怪他們會出現在史畢柏格了。卡塔族通常會避開氣候較寒冷的地方,除非萬不得已。

column1: You tell about how you returned Abdulla's carpet after the Brigands stole it and how he used it to transport the four of you to Shapeir.
column2: 你說起自己在強盜偷走阿布杜拉的飛毯後又物歸原主,而他便用那毯子載著你們四人飛回夏皮爾。

column1: It must be quite the experience to fly through the air in such an exhilarating way. The view of Shapeir from the sky must have been magnificent.
column2: 能那樣痛快地翱翔天際,想必是難得的體驗。從空中俯瞰夏皮爾,那景致一定壯麗極了。

column1: You tell about your battle with the Sweeping Sir James.
column2: 你說起自己與「掃街騎士詹姆斯」的那場交手。

column1: I will never understand why a man would choose to traverse the road to infamy of his own free will.
column2: 我永遠也想不通,一個人怎會心甘情願地踏上這條惡名昭彰的歧路。

column1: You tell about the acrobat's challenge in front of the Guild Hall.
column2: 你說起公會大廳前那名雜耍藝人的挑戰。

column1: There have been occasions in the past where I have envied not having a human torso and hindquarters. Climbing, rope-walking, and swimming are near-impossible for Liontaurs. Still, with my injured limb, I am thankful for having three extra legs to spare instead of just one.
column2: 過去有些時候,我著實羨慕人類有那樣的軀幹與後身。攀爬、走繩、游泳,這些對獅人來說幾乎是不可能的。不過呢,拖著這條傷腿,我倒慶幸自己多了三條腿可用,而不是只剩一條。

column1: Your stories are very exciting, young Hero.
column2: 你的故事真精彩,年輕的英雄。

column1: You get the feeling Uhura was really serious when she asked you to return Rakeesh's flaming sword.
column2: 你感覺得出來,烏胡拉要你歸還拉基什那把烈焰之劍時,是認真的。

column1: The beast has been running through the city searching for you! There was no stopping him. You must take this thing back to the stables where it belongs.
column2: 這頭畜生滿城亂竄找你呢!根本攔不住牠。你得把這玩意兒送回牠該待的馬廄去。

column1: I challenge you all to a wager of skill versus skill.
column2: 我向各位下戰帖,來場本事對本事的賭局!

column1: Who will dare to follow where I lead across this narrow path of rope?
column2: 誰敢跟著我,走過這條窄窄的繩索?

column1: Who will risk a dinar and his very life that he may gain ten dinars in return?
column2: 誰肯押上一枚第納爾與身家性命,換回十枚第納爾?

column1: Who will risk a dinar and his very life that he may gain five dinars in return?
column2: 誰肯押上一枚第納爾與身家性命,換回五枚第納爾?

column1: Ah, the brave one returns! Will you take my challenge?
column2: 啊,勇者又回來啦!可願接下我的挑戰?

column1: Golden Hair, are you willing to risk a dinar and take the challenge?
column2: 金髮的朋友,可願押上一枚第納爾,接下這場挑戰?

column1: Shall we challenge one another, Hero of the Katta?
column2: 咱們來較量較量如何,卡塔族的英雄?

column1: This day of all days is a special day for those of us who dare walk the rope. It is a day when the wind adds spice to the challenge.
column2: 今天對我們這些敢走繩索的人來說,是個特別的日子。風,替這場挑戰添了幾分滋味。

column1: Shall anyone take my wager? Then I will give ten dinars to the one who can brave both wind and height to walk the rope!
column2: 可有人要接我的賭局?誰能頂著風、踩著高,把這繩索走完,我就賞他十枚第納爾!

column1: My noble friend, will you challenge the very element of air as well as the rope by trying to cross now? You will gain ten dinars if you can do it.
column2: 我高貴的朋友,可願趁此刻一闖,既鬥這繩索、也鬥這風之元素?走得過去,十枚第納爾就是你的。

column1: Hero, will you pit your courage and skill against mine?
column2: 英雄,可願拿你的膽識與身手,跟我一較高下?

column1: The day is bright and the land is free of Elementals because of your courage, Hero. Will you not improve your skills by taking my challenge?
column2: 天朗氣清,大地也因你的勇氣而擺脫了元素精靈的威脅,英雄。何不接下我的挑戰,順道練練本事?

column1: You have more than proved your skills in saving our city. Yet you do not have the skill at rope that I have, and you never know what skills may come in handy during one's travels. Take the challenge, and you cannot but improve.
column2: 你救了我們的城,本事早已不在話下。可走繩這門功夫你還比不上我,而出門在外,誰知道哪一手本領哪天就派上用場了。接下挑戰吧,你只會更上一層樓。

column1: Practice is the only way to success. Defeater of the Elementals, will you try the challenge?
column2: 唯有勤練方能成事。擊退元素精靈的勇者啊,可願試試這場挑戰?

column1: Ah, you of great skill, do you think you can rest on your laurels?
column2: 啊,身手不凡的人哪,你以為就能高枕無憂、坐享其成了嗎?

column1: I challenge you again to face the rope and see if it was skill or mere luck that you crossed it before!
column2: 我再向你下一回戰帖,來會會這繩索,瞧瞧你上回走過去靠的是本事,還是純粹運氣!

column1: You collect your ten dinars.
column2: 你收下了十枚第納爾。

column1: You can not get off so easily! Let us test the rope again, that I may regain my lost dinars.
column2: 想這麼輕易脫身可不行!咱們再走一回繩,讓我把輸掉的第納爾贏回來。

column1: You have the luck of the Katta, but you cannot win on luck alone. Come, we shall again try our skills!
column2: 你有卡塔族的好運氣,可光靠運氣是贏不到底的。來,咱們再比一場本事!

column1: We seem to have an expert among us!
column2: 看來咱們當中出了位高手啊!

column1: You collect your five dinars.
column2: 你收下了五枚第納爾。

column1: Hero, you are good, but you are not as good as I! Let us test your skills and dinars against mine!
column2: 英雄,你是不賴,可還沒我厲害!來,拿你的本事和第納爾,跟我的賭一賭!

column1: I hope you are not injured. You need to practice at landing better.
column2: 但願你沒摔傷。落地這門功夫,你還得多練練。

column1: It is said that the best thing to do after failing is to try again.
column2: 人家說,失敗過後最該做的事,就是再來一次。

column1: Do not be discouraged.
column2: 別氣餒。

column1: Such things take practice. That was much better.
column2: 這種事得多練。這回好多了。

column1: You are starting to learn balance.
column2: 你開始抓到平衡的訣竅了。

column1: Ah, you are learning.
column2: 啊,你上道了。

column1: You will soon be making a profit off me.
column2: 用不了多久,你就要從我這兒賺錢囉。

column1: You are getting too good for me.
column2: 你越來越行,我都快招架不住了。

column1: Surely I will be forced to pay you next time.
column2: 下回我準得乖乖把錢賠給你了。

column1: So close! I told you it only took practice.
column2: 差一點點!我就說嘛,多練練就成了。

column1: You almost made it. It was mere bad luck that you did not cross this time.
column2: 你就快成功了。這回沒走過去,純粹是運氣不好。

column1: The dinars were nearly yours. If you can but walk from one end to the other, I will give you ten dinars.
column2: 那些第納爾差點就歸你了。你只要從這頭走到那頭,我就給你十枚第納爾。

column1: The dinars were nearly yours. If you can but walk from one end to the other, I will give you five dinars.
column2: 那些第納爾差點就歸你了。你只要從這頭走到那頭,我就給你五枚第納爾。

column1: There's nobody up there and even if there was, you're not in the mood for a serenade.
column2: 上頭根本沒人,就算有,你這會兒也沒心情唱小夜曲。

column1: Why don't you just buy a souvenir like everyone else?
column2: 你怎麼不像大家一樣,買個紀念品就好了呢?

column1: This is not what people mean when they tell you to take the street to the south.
column2: 人家叫你「往南邊那條街走」,可不是這個意思。

column1: This is not what people mean when they tell you to take the street to the north.
column2: 人家叫你「往北邊那條街走」,可不是這個意思。

column1: Goo goo. Ma fhimt.
column2: 咕咕。Ma fhimt(聽不懂啦)。

column1: Is there no one of courage and daring among you? Will any take my challenge?
column2: 難道你們當中就沒一個有膽有種的?可有人要接我的挑戰?

column1: You, stranger from a distant land! Are you brave and skilled enough to cross this rope?
column2: 喂,你這位遠方來的客人!可有膽量、有本事走過這條繩索?

column1: Will you wager with me?
column2: 可願跟我賭一把?

column1: It is such a simple thing, to walk this rope. Surely you have the skill.
column2: 走這繩索可是樁再簡單不過的事。你準有這本事。

column1: You will gain ten dinars because of it. Will you wager with me?
column2: 走過去,你就能賺到十枚第納爾。可願跟我賭一把?

column1: You will gain five dinars because of it. Will you wager with me?
column2: 走過去,你就能賺到五枚第納爾。可願跟我賭一把?

column1: It be time to close the Guild for the night. I still need to prepare meal for both of us.
column2: 該關公會的門過夜啦。我還得替咱倆張羅飯菜呢。

column1: I am looking forward to it.
column2: 我可期待著呢。

column1: Have a pleasant evening, young adventurer. May we speak again some other day.
column2: 祝你今晚愉快,年輕的冒險者。願咱們改日再聊。

column1: Kwa heri, and good night.
column2: Kwa heri(再會),晚安。

column1: It be bedtime for Simba and dinner time for us. I hope Simba be giving you no trouble today.
column2: 辛巴該睡覺啦,咱們也該吃晚飯了。但願辛巴今天沒給你添麻煩。

column1: Not at all. I remember my own children being a great deal more mischievous when they were cubs.
column2: 一點也沒有。我還記得自家孩子小時候,可比這調皮搗蛋多了。

column1: Have a pleasant evening, Hero of Spielburg. I will not be here for the next few days, but I do look forward to our next meeting.
column2: 祝你今晚愉快,史畢柏格的英雄。接下來幾天我不會在這兒,但我著實期待咱們的下次相會。

column1: Kwa heri, bwana.
column2: Kwa heri,bwana(再會了,老兄)。

column1: It be time to close the Guild for the night. You need some help getting up, Rakeesh?
column2: 該關公會的門過夜啦。要人扶你起來嗎,拉基什?

column1: I appreciate the offer, but I shall be fine on my own. The condition of my leg has improved significantly since yesterday.
column2: 謝謝你的好意,不過我自己能行。我這條腿從昨天起,已經好轉許多了。

column1: Have a pleasant evening, Hero of Shapeir. May fortune continue to smile upon you.
column2: 祝你今晚愉快,夏皮爾的英雄。願好運繼續眷顧你。

column1: Kwa heri, Hero. Take care.
column2: Kwa heri(再會),英雄。保重。

column1: Jambo, rafiki. You certainly be up bright and early.
column2: Jambo,rafiki(你好啊,朋友)。你今兒可起得真早。

column1: Good morning, my young friend. I hope today shall bring good fortune to you.
column2: 早安,我年輕的朋友。但願今日能為你帶來好運。

column1: You consider trying the challenge, then realize you wouldn't even make it up the platform with your possessions weighing you down like this.
column2: 你盤算著要不要接下挑戰,卻發覺自己這麼大包小包地壓著,連台子都爬不上去。

column1: But Effendi, I have your dinar. But climb up and cross the rope, and ten dinars are yours.
column2: 可是閣下,你那枚第納爾已經在我手上了。只要你爬上去走過繩索,十枚第納爾就是你的。

column1: But Effendi, I have your dinar. But climb up and cross the rope, and five dinars are yours.
column2: 可是閣下,你那枚第納爾已經在我手上了。只要你爬上去走過繩索,五枚第納爾就是你的。

column1: I only accept the coin of Shapeir, my friend.
column2: 我的朋友,我只收夏皮爾的錢幣。

column1: You are most brave. Now let's see if you are skilled as well.
column2: 你著實勇敢。這就來瞧瞧,你是不是也一樣有本事。

column1: I see it's getting dark already. Farewell, Effendi. May you never meet a challenge you cannot handle.
column2: 我看天都黑了。告辭了,閣下。願你永遠不會碰上應付不了的難關。

column1: I see it's getting dark already. No more wagering for today. May we meet again some other time.
column2: 我看天都黑了。今天就不再賭了。願咱們改日再會。

column1: The doors are locked; a sign on the door says "Back at 8 a.m."
column2: 門鎖著;門上的牌子寫著「早上八點回來」。

column1: You must first wager with me, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你得先跟我下注才行。

column1: Effendi, first your dinar.
column2: 閣下,先付你的第納爾。

column1: According to the sign above it, this is the entrance to the Adventurer's Guild.
column2: 照上頭的牌子看,這裡是冒險者公會的入口。

column1: Rakeesh would have your head off before you got ten feet. Besides, there is no baby market in Shapeir.
column2: 你還沒走出十呎,拉基什就先把你的腦袋給砍了。再說,夏皮爾可沒有買賣嬰兒的市集。

column1: Goo goo ga ga goo.
column2: 咕咕嘎嘎咕。

column1: Poltergeists make up the principal types of spontaneous material manifestations.
column2: 騷靈是自發性實體顯化裡最主要的一類。

column1: Don't bother. He only tries to show off.
column2: 別費事了。他只是想顯擺罷了。

column1: Please start on the other platform, Effendi.
column2: 請從另一頭的台子上起步,閣下。

column1: It is a high platform, part of a portable tightrope setup.
column2: 那是座高台,是這套可拆裝走繩裝置的一部分。

column1: It is a rope, stretched tight between two platforms.
column2: 那是條繩索,在兩座高台之間繃得緊緊的。

column1: It is a sturdy-looking rope, hanging from the platform.
column2: 那是條看起來挺結實的繩索,從高台上垂下來。

column1: The kid's hangin' loose.
column2: 這小傢伙倒是逍遙自在。

column1: The baby fearlessly plays with the Liontaur's tail.
column2: 那嬰兒毫不畏懼地玩弄著獅人的尾巴。

column1: A large crowd has gathered to watch the acrobat perform.
column2: 一大群人圍攏過來,看那雜耍藝人的表演。

column1: Don't try anything rash. There are several guards among the crowd.
column2: 別輕舉妄動。人群裡頭混著好幾個衛兵呢。

column1: They are busy watching the acrobat.
column2: 他們都忙著看那雜耍藝人。

column1: If you want their attention, you should accept the performer's challenge.
column2: 想引起他們注意,你該接下那表演者的挑戰。

column1: It's not a magical rope, so it won't obey your commands.
column2: 這又不是魔法繩,可不會聽你使喚。

column1: For that you must talk to Tiram, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去找廣場另一頭的提拉姆談。

column1: Tiram is across the plaza.
column2: 提拉姆在廣場的另一頭。

column1: I am called Sashanan, the 'Diamond of the Desert.'
column2: 人家都喚我薩珊南,「沙漠之鑽」是也。

column1: I have many types of necklaces and bracelets, but my specialty is to sell the jewelry best suited to the customer.
column2: 我這兒項鍊、手鐲樣樣都有,不過我的拿手好戲,是替客人挑出最相襯的那一件。

column1: May it someday be of value to you.
column2: 願它有朝一日能對你派上用場。

column1: This merchant's prices are too high.
column2: 這商人開的價碼太高了。

column1: There's no need to buy any more jewelry from the merchant.
column2: 沒必要再跟這商人買珠寶了。

column1: After trying to make a deal for some twenty minutes, you realize that you really don't need the pin anyway, and certainly can't afford it.
column2: 跟他殺價殺了約莫二十分鐘,你才醒悟自己根本用不著那枚別針,更何況也買不起。

column1: For that you must talk to Sashanan, across the plaza.
column2: 那件事你得去找廣場另一頭的薩珊南談。

column1: Sashanan is across the plaza.
column2: 薩珊南在廣場的另一頭。

column1: I am Tiram, and all my family makes the carpets.
column2: 我是提拉姆,我們一家子都做地毯。

column1: I sell the finest of rugs, hand-woven by my family.
column2: 我賣的是全城最上等的地毯,全由我家人親手織就。

column1: You thank Sashanan for his kindness in giving you such a valuable gift.
column2: 你向薩沙南道謝,感激他大方贈予如此珍貴的禮物。

column1: Its value is small compared to all you have done for our people, Katta-friend.
column2: 和你為我族所做的一切相比,這點價值微不足道,卡塔之友。

column1: Jewelry of the finest gems inlaid in rings of gold and silver, necklaces of beautiful stones, things of beauty which will last forever -- all of which I sell.
column2: 鑲嵌頂級寶石的金銀戒指、綴著美石的項鍊,件件都是能傳世永存的精品——這些我這兒全有。

column1: The finest of carpets in all of the land I have for your perusal, Hero of the North.
column2: 全境最上等的地毯都在此,任您細賞,北方的英雄。

column1: That is hardly enough to pay for the fabric. I ask that you consider %d dinars instead.
column2: 這價錢連布料都不夠付。還請您考慮 %d 第納爾如何?

column1: The time and effort alone are worth thrice your asking price. My final offer is %d dinars. You may take it or leave it, Effendi.
column2: 光是工時與心力就值您開價的三倍。我最後出價 %d 第納爾,要不要隨您,閣下。

column1: It is a true shame that we could not make a deal, Effendi.
column2: 沒能成交,實在可惜,閣下。

column1: You will need to go to the Changer of Money to trade your coins for those of Shapeir.
column2: 您得先去錢莊,把手上的錢幣換成夏皮爾通用的才行。

column1: Carrying around a rug would be cumbersome. I shall have it delivered to the inn for you.
column2: 帶著一張地毯到處跑太累贅了。我替您送到客棧去吧。

column1: The fear of fire is great to a seller of rugs. A thousand thanks for saving our city, brave one.
column2: 對賣地毯的人來說,火最是可怕。萬分感謝您拯救了我們的城市,勇者。

column1: Shameen and Shema have spoken truly of your courage. May fortune favor you, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 沙明與舍瑪所言不假,您的確勇氣過人。願好運眷顧您,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: My carpets nearly went flying by the force of the wind. But for your bravery, there would be little left here that was not blown away. Our people are in your debt.
column2: 那陣狂風差點把我的地毯全捲走。若不是您的英勇,這裡只怕沒幾樣東西能留下來。我族都欠您一份情。

column1: You have the gratitude of the Katta.
column2: 卡塔族對您感激不盡。

column1: It is good to know that such heroes as yourself are around when misfortune strikes.
column2: 災禍降臨時,有您這樣的英雄在身邊,真讓人安心。

column1: The Elemental of Earth would have crushed the city, but for you.
column2: 要不是有您,土元素早就把這座城市夷為平地了。

column1: We are again in your debt.
column2: 我們又欠了您一份情。

column1: Your name will be told in the tales of the Katta as long as there are Katta to tell them.
column2: 只要還有卡塔人能傳頌,您的名字就會永遠留在卡塔族的故事裡。

column1: We Katta tried to think of some way to say how much we were grateful. Please accept this gift of a sapphire pin.  May it do you good.
column2: 我們卡塔族絞盡腦汁,想表達心中的感激。請收下這枚藍寶石別針,願它能助您一臂之力。

column1: You take the sapphire pin and place it through your lapel.
column2: 你接過藍寶石別針,將它別在衣領上。

column1: The shape of the pin stirs an old memory. You recall how you used to practice with such simple pins when first learning how to pick locks. You decide not to mention this to the honest merchant.
column2: 別針的形狀勾起一段舊回憶。你想起當年初學開鎖時,就是拿這種簡單的別針練習的。你決定還是別跟這位老實的商人提這件事。

column1: You! Stranger! You justa in luck. I gotta everything you need right here.
column2: 喂!外地人!你運氣好得很哪。你要的東西,我這兒樣樣都有。

column1: You needa map? I gotta map!
column2: 你要地圖?我這兒有地圖!

column1: You needa compass? I gotta compass!
column2: 你要羅盤?我這兒有羅盤!

column1: You needa souvenirs of Shapeir for friends and relatives? I gotta best souvenirs in town.
column2: 想買夏皮爾的紀念品送親朋好友?全城最棒的紀念品都在我這兒。

column1: Hey you! I gotta justa thing! A keychain with real Shapeir brass centime on the end. Only costa you one dinar.
column2: 喂喂!我這兒有個好東西!鑰匙圈尾端掛著貨真價實的夏皮爾黃銅生丁幣,只要一第納爾。

column1: This you gotta see! The ashtray of Shapeir made from imported coconut shell, hand-carved by Katta natives into face of the Sultan! You notta finda this anywhere else!
column2: 這你非看不可!夏皮爾煙灰缸,進口椰殼製,卡塔原住民親手雕成蘇丹的臉!別處你絕對找不到!

column1: 'Guide to Sightseeing in Beautiful Raseir.' Only costa you one dinar since it only gotta one page.
column2: 《美麗拉希爾觀光導覽》。只要一第納爾,因為它就只有一頁。

column1: Since you my besta customer, I make you a deal. I sella you a duck.
column2: 看在你是我最好的顧客份上,我給你個優惠。我賣你一隻鴨。

column1: I have-a here what you need 'cause you so smart! Bookends! It gotta donkey head on one side anda ass on the other. Makesa great gift.
column2: 我這兒有你需要的東西,因為你聰明嘛!書檔!一邊是驢頭,另一邊是驢屁股。送禮一流。

column1: Genuine Saurus foot trash can for Mother's Day! Show your mother what you thinka her.
column2: 母親節限定!正宗索魯斯腳垃圾桶!讓你媽知道你心裡是怎麼想她的。

column1: Fire sale today![I sella you real wood burned by real Fire Elemental. Be the first on your block to owna this.
column2: 今天跳樓大拍賣![我賣你貨真價實、被真正火元素燒過的木頭。當你們街坊第一個擁有它的人吧。

column1: Hero of the North, a thousand thanks for defeating the thing of fire.  We were forced to flee for our lives when it set the stand of Alichica aflame.
column2: 北方的英雄,萬分感謝您擊敗了那團火怪。它把阿利奇卡的攤子點著時,我們只能拚命逃命。

column1: It was most brave and wise of you to defeat the thing of air, Hero of Shapeir. Again, the city is safe through your actions.
column2: 您擊敗那團風怪,既英勇又睿智,夏皮爾的英雄。城市再一次因您的作為而平安無事。

column1: The thing of earth was most fearsome. The very city trembled from it.  We are most fortunate that Shema and Shameen brought you here.
column2: 那團土怪最是駭人,整座城都被它震得發抖。所幸舍瑪與沙明把您帶來了這裡,真是我們的福氣。

column1: It has been said that you are going to journey to Raseir. The Katta all hope for your safety and wish for your success, Friend of the Katta.
column2: 聽說您即將動身前往拉希爾。卡塔族都祈願您一路平安、馬到成功,卡塔之友。

column1: Will you please take this Saurus back to the stables? I have been unable to move it back there. It seems to have been waiting for you.
column2: 能否請您把這頭索魯斯帶回馬廄?我怎麼也牽不動牠,牠似乎一直在等您。

column1: The Saurus is perfectly willing to follow you back to the stables.
column2: 這頭索魯斯非常樂意跟著你回馬廄。

column1: You are most skilled at bargaining. They shall be but %d centimes.
column2: 您真是討價還價的高手。那就只算您 %d 生丁吧。

column1: I hope you will find %d centimes acceptable? I regret to say that I can ask no less.
column2: 希望 %d 生丁您能接受?恕我直言,再低我就賣不下去了。

column1: The merchant silently shakes her head.
column2: 商人默默地搖了搖頭。

column1: You are most skilled at bargaining. It shall be but %d dinars.
column2: 您真是討價還價的高手。那就只算您 %d 第納爾吧。

column1: I hope you will find %d dinars acceptable? I regret to say that I can ask no less.
column2: 希望 %d 第納爾您能接受?恕我直言,再低我就賣不下去了。

column1: I think we shall have to agree to disagree, Effendi.
column2: 看來這筆生意我們只能各執己見了,閣下。

column1: I am sorry, but I really cannot afford to give you any more free flowers.
column2: 抱歉,我實在沒辦法再免費送您花了。

column1: I am sorry, but I really cannot afford to give you any more free flowers. Please visit the Moneychanger.
column2: 抱歉,我實在沒辦法再免費送您花了。請您去一趟錢莊吧。

column1: You will need to go to the Changer of Money to trade your coins for those of Shapeir. But since you are a hero to my people, you may have some of my flowers anyway.
column2: 您得先去錢莊,把手上的錢幣換成夏皮爾通用的才行。不過看在您是我族英雄的份上,這些花您還是儘管拿去。

column1: Since you are a friend of my people, then you may have some flowers, even though you have not the money.
column2: 既然您是我族之友,就算您沒帶錢,這些花也儘管拿去。

column1: I hope these flowers will bring you happiness.
column2: 願這些花能為您帶來歡喜。

column1: Creating art from dried flowers takes much time. I hope this tapestry brings you enjoyment.
column2: 用乾燥花做出藝術品很費工夫。願這幅花藝掛毯能讓您賞心悅目。

column1: All right, you drive a hard bargain. I give-a you this and you give-a me %d centimes.
column2: 好吧好吧,你殺價真狠。這個給你,你給我 %d 生丁。

column1: Whatta you think this is? Some kinda charity? You gotta give-a me %d centimes, or we gotta no deal.
column2: 你當這是啥?做善事?你得給我 %d 生丁,不然這買賣就吹了。

column1: Ali Chica fakes a heart attack.
column2: 阿利奇卡假裝心臟病發。

column1: You gotta be joking! At-a the price you ask, I make-a less money! How about-a %d dinars then, boss?
column2: 你開玩笑的吧!照你開的價,我還倒貼錢哪!那 %d 第納爾如何,老大?

column1: Whatta you think this is? Some kinda charity? You gotta give-a me %d dinars, or we gotta no deal.
column2: 你當這是啥?做善事?你得給我 %d 第納爾,不然這買賣就吹了。

column1: Sorry boss. We gotta no deal then.
column2: 抱歉啦老大,那這買賣就算了。

column1: Lemme see if I got one.
column2: 讓我瞧瞧還有沒有貨。

column1: There you go boss.
column2: 給您,老大。

column1: You no gonna regret this, boss. Guaranteed the best in town or you no getta your money back.
column2: 你不會後悔的,老大。保證全城最棒,不然錢一毛都不退你。

column1: The sign says that the door leads to the Katta's Tail Inn.
column2: 招牌上寫著,這扇門通往「卡塔之尾」客棧。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn sign has been burned beyond recognition.
column2: 「卡塔之尾」客棧的招牌已被燒得面目全非。

column1: This is a nice-a town, a nice-a land.
column2: 這是個好地方,好城好土哪。

column1: I understand the sightseeing business is notta too good there.
column2: 我聽說那兒的觀光生意不太好做哪。

column1: Thatsa what inherits the wind when-a Elemental makes a will.
column2: 元素立遺囑時,繼承到的就是這陣風囉。

column1: Thatsa what goes in the Elementary Canal.
column2: 那是流進「元素運河」裡的東西啦。

column1: Thatsa dirty word to me.
column2: 那詞兒對我來說可是髒話。

column1: The Fire Elemental, he burna down this place. I guess maybe he no like-a souvenirs.
column2: 那火元素啊,把這地方燒個精光。我猜大概是他不愛紀念品吧。

column1: You take-a something and you make it alive. Then you make-a the something dig a ditch. Thatsa Elementary Canal.
column2: 你拿個東西讓它活過來,然後叫這東西去挖溝渠。這就是「元素運河」啦。

column1: You needa souvenirs, I gotta them. How about a pair of glow-in-the-dark socks of Shapeir? This-a way, you no lose your feet at night.
column2: 你要紀念品,我這兒有的是。來雙夏皮爾夜光襪如何?這麼一來,晚上你就不會搞丟自己的腳啦。

column1: Why a duck? I tella you why a duck.
column2: 為啥是鴨子?我來告訴你為啥是鴨子。

column1: Say you on top of a mountain. You on top of this mountain and you wanna get down. Well, you no get down off a mountain, you get down off a duck. There you have it.
column2: 假設你在山頂上。你站在這山頂上想下來。可你下不了山,你只能從鴨子身上「下」絨毛呀。這不就對了。

column1: And this a very special duck. It will save your life. Say you getta lost in the desert. You getta lost and you no can find your way.
column2: 而且這可是隻很特別的鴨子,它能救你一命。假設你在沙漠裡迷路了,迷了路又找不到方向。

column1: You hungry and thirsty and you donna know what to do. Then you take out your duck and it will save you. You make-a duck soup.
column2: 你又餓又渴,不知如何是好。這時你掏出鴨子,它就能救你。你拿牠煮鴨湯嘛。

column1: My name, he is Alichica.
column2: 我的名字嘛,叫阿利奇卡。

column1: Thatsa good name for one who chase-a the chicks.
column2: 對一個整天追小妞兒的傢伙來說,這名字取得真好。

column1: I sella you what you need. What more can you want?
column2: 你要啥我賣啥,你還能要求啥?

column1: You gotta watch them. When you make-a deal with a Katta, it always seem fair. They musta be up to something.
column2: 你可得提防著他們。跟卡塔人做生意,表面上總是公道得很。準是在打什麼鬼主意。

column1: I heard that there was a stranger in town who asks a lotta questions.
column2: 我聽說城裡來了個外地人,問題多得不得了。

column1: The way to the fountain? Take-a the road Junub Tarik. Keep going until you get wet. Then you finda the fountain.
column2: 去噴泉的路?走「朱努布塔里克」那條路,一直走到你被淋濕為止,那就找到噴泉啦。

column1: I gotta carved statue of Shapeir that tells you what the weather is like. You leave it outa doors. Then you picka it up. If itsa wet, then the day is rainy. If itsa warm, then the day is hot.
column2: 我這兒有尊夏皮爾雕像,能告訴你天氣如何。你把它擺在門外,然後撿起來摸摸看。要是濕的,那今天就是下雨;要是熱的,那今天就是大熱天。

column1: You wanna know where the Palace is, justa look on my map. You finda the Palace right by the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 你想知道王宮在哪兒,瞧瞧我的地圖就行。王宮就在「王宮廣場」旁邊,一找就到。

column1: Sorry, I don't carry vans.
column2: 抱歉,我這兒不賣廂型車。

column1: You needa my map to find it.
column2: 你得靠我的地圖才找得到它。

column1: I gotta great coconut ashtray with his face on it. You putta your cigarette in his mouth and smoke rings come outa his ears.
column2: 我這兒有個超棒的椰殼煙灰缸,上頭刻著他的臉。你把香菸塞進他嘴裡,菸圈就會從他耳朵裡冒出來。

column1: I can no sell you something unless you gotta some dinars or centimes.
column2: 你得有點第納爾或生丁,我才能賣東西給你。

column1: Seeing you a stranger, you gotta strange money. You need to get the coins of Shapeir, the centime and dinar.
column2: 瞧你是個外地人,帶的也是外地的怪錢。你得去換夏皮爾的錢幣,也就是生丁跟第納爾。

column1: Eh, you wood knot want it.
column2: 欸,這木頭你「木」必想要吧。

column1: It's hot,[it's red,[it's something you cook with,[it burned my stand down.
column2: 它很燙,[它紅通通,[它是煮飯會用到的東西,[它把我的攤子燒了個精光。

column1: Isn't that where you take-a ice cream and putta whipped cream and fudge onna top?
column2: 那不就是拿冰淇淋,上頭再加鮮奶油跟巧克力醬的那玩意兒嗎?

column1: I can sell it to you for only thrice its real worth.
column2: 我只用它實際價值的三倍賣給你就好。

column1: I notta know where he lives.
column2: 我不曉得他住哪兒。

column1: Lasham sella her plants in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 拉珊在「噴泉廣場」賣她的花草。

column1: Sashanan sell-a some fine jewelry in the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 薩沙南在「王宮廣場」賣些精緻的珠寶。

column1: Mirak, he sell-a leatherwork in the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 米拉克啊,他在「鬥士廣場」賣皮件。

column1: Tashtari sella his brasswork in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 塔什塔里在「噴泉廣場」賣他的黃銅器。

column1: Saba sella some woven baskets in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 薩巴在「噴泉廣場」賣些手編籃子。

column1: Toshur sella her pottery
column2: 托舒爾賣她的陶器。

column1: Scoree and Sloree sella good snacks in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 斯柯利跟斯洛利在「噴泉廣場」賣好吃的點心。

column1: Lisha, she sell-a her flowers right beside-a you.
column2: 莉莎呀,她就在你旁邊賣花。

column1: Kiram, he sell-a some clothing in the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 基拉姆啊,他在「鬥士廣場」賣些衣裳。

column1: The Weapon Shop is in-a the Plaza of Fighters.
column2: 武器店在「鬥士廣場」裡頭。

column1: The Apothecary is in-a the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 藥鋪在「噴泉廣場」裡頭。

column1: The Magic Shop is in-a the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 魔法店在「噴泉廣場」裡頭。

column1: Sorry, boss -- I justa ran outa them. Maybe you come back tomorrow and I still notta have it.
column2: 抱歉啦老大——我剛好賣完了。也許你明天再來,我大概還是沒貨啦。

column1: You gonna ask me questions, or you gonna buy what I sell? I no make-a living off of answers.
column2: 你是要問我問題,還是要買我賣的東西?我可不是靠回答問題過活的。

column1: You needa answer to that, you aska someone else.
column2: 那問題你想要答案,去問別人吧。

column1: My name is Lisha, which means 'Quiet One' in the language of the Katta.
column2: 我叫莉莎,在卡塔語裡是「安靜者」的意思。

column1: His name is Sitar. He is shy, but a very good musician. He performs at the inn sometimes in the evening.
column2: 他叫西塔爾。他很害羞,卻是個很棒的樂師,晚上偶爾會在客棧裡表演。

column1: The name of the snake is Hissa. He is Sitar's companion and music critic.
column2: 那條蛇叫嘶撒,牠是西塔爾的夥伴兼樂評家。

column1: He makes his living by performing in the bazaar.
column2: 他靠在市集裡表演維生。

column1: You thank Lisha for her kindness in giving you some flowers free of charge.
column2: 你向莉莎道謝,感激她好心免費送你一些花。

column1: It was but a small favor for the one who brought Shameen and Shema back home, Effendi.
column2: 對於把沙明與舍瑪平安帶回家的人來說,這只是個小忙罷了,閣下。

column1: You tell Lisha the tale of the tree in the desert and how you did your part to restore her spirit.
column2: 你向莉莎講述了沙漠中那棵樹的故事,以及你如何盡了一份力,讓她的靈魂重獲生機。

column1: What a touching tale that is, Effendi. I heard about the tree from a passing Bedouin, but did not know why it suddenly blossomed again. Many thanks for sharing this with me.
column2: 多麼動人的故事啊,閣下。我曾從一位路過的貝都因人口中聽說過那棵樹,卻不明白它為何突然重新綻放。多謝您與我分享這一切。

column1: No problem, boss.
column2: 沒問題,老闆。

column1: Sorry, boss, all I got is a buncha burnt wood. Maybe you shoulda try the Apothecary for that. He might-a havea cream or something.
column2: 抱歉啦老闆,我這只有一堆燒焦的木頭。那種東西你該去找藥師問問,他搞不好有藥膏之類的。

column1: Thatsa good story, boss.
column2: 好故事,老闆。

column1: Flowers of beauty, flowers of fragrances most wonderful.  Flowers to bring cheer to your day.
column2: 美麗的花,芬芳到不可思議的花。能讓你一整天心花怒放的花。

column1: You haven't any money to give.
column2: 你身上沒錢可以給。

column1: It's the musician's donation bowl. There are a few dinars inside.
column2: 這是樂師的賞錢碗,裡頭擺著幾枚第納爾。

column1: Spitting in the bowl would not make you look very heroic.
column2: 往碗裡吐口水,可一點也不像個英雄該幹的事。

column1: You don't dare to take a dinar from the bowl with such a vicious snake so near.
column2: 旁邊盤著這麼一條兇神惡煞的蛇,你可不敢從碗裡拿走半枚第納爾。

column1: No one enters the gates of the Palace of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!) unless ordered to do so.
column2: 未奉旨意者,一律不得踏入蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願祂萬世為君!)的宮門。

column1: Please don't hamper us in our duties, Effendi.
column2: 請別妨礙我們執勤,閣下。

column1: The Palace doors are locked from the inside.
column2: 宮門是從裡頭上鎖的。

column1: Breaking into the Palace would be impossible with all those guards watching you.
column2: 那麼多衛兵盯著你,想硬闖進宮根本是癡人說夢。

column1: Good morning, my dear sir. Have you truly been waiting out here the entire night, just so you could be first in line to buy a Saurus? That really wasn't necessary.
column2: 早安啊,我的好先生。你該不會在外頭等了一整夜,就為了搶第一個買索魯斯吧?這實在大可不必呢。

column1: (There couldn't possibly be enough suckers like you around here to form a line to begin with.)
column2: (這附近哪湊得出夠多像你這樣的冤大頭,排得成一條隊伍嘛。)

column1: Anyway, I've been looking for you.
column2: 總之呢,我正到處找你。

column1: Ah, there you are! I've been looking for you.
column2: 啊,你在這兒啊!我正找你呢。

column1: (Although for the life of me, I can't imagine why.)
column2: (雖然我打死也想不通,自己幹嘛要找你。)

column1: I'm Ali Fakir. Honest Ali Fakir. Welcome to Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus Lot. 'If we don't have the Saurus you want, then you don't want one.'
column2: 我是阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·法基爾。歡迎光臨「老實人阿里·法基爾中古索魯斯場」。本店宗旨:「我們沒有的索魯斯,就是你不想要的索魯斯。」

column1: Of course, even if you don't want one, I'll be happy to sell one to you.
column2: 當然啦,就算你不想要,我也很樂意賣你一頭。

column1: You know, you remind me of someone I didn't sell a Saurus to. Nah, it couldn't be. Surely someone with your brains and looks needs a Saurus.
column2: 你曉得嗎,你讓我想起一個我沒賣成索魯斯的傢伙。不會吧,不可能。像你這種有頭腦又有相貌的人,肯定需要一頭索魯斯。

column1: (Either that, or some preschool and a plastic surgeon.)
column2: (不然就是需要去念個幼稚園,外加找個整形醫師。)

column1: So you want to buy a Saurus, eh? I'm Fakir, Honest Ali Fakir. Glad to meet you. You've come to the right place. Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus Lot -- If you don't buy one here, you don't buy one.
column2: 這麼說你想買索魯斯囉?我是法基爾,老實人阿里·法基爾,幸會幸會。你算是來對地方了。「老實人阿里·法基爾中古索魯斯場」——你不在這兒買,你就買不到。

column1: I've got a beauty of a Saurus for you. Only had one owner. A sweet little old lady who only took it out on Sundays to go drag racing. Or was that dragon racing?
column2: 我給你留了一頭漂亮極了的索魯斯。前任車主只有一位,是個和藹的小老太太,只在禮拜天牽出來飆車。還是說,是飆龍來著?

column1: So they call you a hero, eh? What kind of hero are you, walking around like a nobody when you could be riding your noble steed across the desert sands.
column2: 人家都喊你英雄,是吧?可你算哪門子英雄哪,明明能騎著高貴坐騎馳騁大漠,卻像個無名小卒似的走來走去。

column1: I can see it now, you in your flowing white robes, riding with the entire Bedouin nation at your side, your blue eyes blazing as you ride to glory!
column2: 我彷彿都看見了:你一身飄逸白袍,率領整支貝都因大軍並肩而行,湛藍的雙眼熊熊燃燒,策馬奔向榮耀!

column1: No, that was that Lawrence guy, wasn't it? Well, even YOU would look less reprehensible upon a Saurus.
column2: 不對,那好像是那個叫勞倫斯的傢伙吧?嗯,反正連「你」騎上索魯斯,看起來都沒那麼礙眼了。

column1: Hi, I'm Ali. Honest Ali Fakir. Welcome to Honest Ali Fakir's Used Saurus Lot -- where we always know a sucker when we see one. Pleased to meet you. Now about that deal we were going to make...
column2: 嗨,我是阿里,老實人阿里·法基爾。歡迎光臨「老實人阿里·法基爾中古索魯斯場」——本店看冤大頭,一看一個準。幸會幸會。話說回來,我們剛要談的那筆生意嘛……

column1: Let me guess. Even though I've tried to help you save the universe, you still won't even buy my lousy Saurus.
column2: 讓我猜猜。我都這麼賣力幫你拯救宇宙了,你卻連我這頭爛索魯斯都不肯買。

column1: That's it! I give up. I've tried to help you save the universe and you won't even buy my lousy Saurus.
column2: 夠了!我放棄。我都幫你拯救宇宙了,你卻連我這頭爛索魯斯都不肯買。

column1: After all I've gone through just so you could win this stupid game sequel, you wouldn't know a hint if it knocked you in the head.
column2: 我吃了這麼多苦頭,就為了讓你能破這款蠢透了的續作,結果提示就算砸到你頭上,你也認不出來。

column1: In fact, you wouldn't even know your own head!
column2: 說真的,你連自己的頭都認不出來!

column1: If you don't buy my Saurus this time, I'm going to pack up my lot and then see if you can win the game. We'll see who laughs last. Ha ha!
column2: 你這回再不買我的索魯斯,我就收攤打烊,看你還破不破得了這遊戲。咱們走著瞧,看誰笑到最後。哈哈!

column1: You just can't take a hint, can you? The Saurus you should have bought went to the wrong customer, and the entire free world will soon be enslaved as a result.
column2: 你就是聽不懂暗示,對吧?你本該買下的那頭索魯斯落到了不對的客人手裡,結果整個自由世界很快就要淪為奴隸了。

column1: Terminal Stubbornness
column2: 無可救藥的頑固

column1: You are not likely to live long if you go that way. One cannot travel in the desert unless one is better prepared or joins a caravan.
column2: 往那個方向走,你大概活不了多久。除非準備得更周全,或是加入商隊,否則沒人能橫越沙漠。

column1: It is good that you have returned safely from the desert.
column2: 你能從沙漠平安歸來,真是太好了。

column1: Thank heavens that thou hast survived the night desert. It can be truly dangerous when the sun has set.
column2: 謝天謝地,汝竟能在夜晚的沙漠中倖存。日落之後,沙漠當真兇險萬分。

column1: So, you are the stranger the merchants are talking about. The gate to Shapeir is always open, but never have I seen an entrance to the city like yours. To fly in on a carpet, it must be grand.
column2: 這麼說,你就是商人們口中那位陌生人。夏皮爾的城門向來敞開,可我從沒見過有人像你這樣進城的。乘著飛毯而來,那場面想必氣派非凡。

column1: If you are exploring the desert, you should visit the Dervish at the oasis. He is a very wise man.
column2: 你若要探索沙漠,該去綠洲拜訪那位苦行僧。他是位極有智慧的人。

column1: Good day, Hero.
column2: 日安,英雄。

column1: There is a caravan which will pass by us six days from now. If you wish to journey to Raseir, you will need to join it.
column2: 六天之後,會有一支商隊路過此地。你若想前往拉希爾,就得跟著它走。

column1: The Dervish, passing a message through one of the local Bedouin, has sent for you. The Dervish says, 'Man who looks for trouble comes to right place.' He also says, 'Good comes from evil when dark is enlightened.'
column2: 那位苦行僧託本地一名貝都因人捎話,差人來請你。苦行僧說:「找麻煩的人,來對地方了。」他還說:「黑暗一旦被照亮,惡中便生出善。」

column1: The caravan to Raseir will leave here at the light of dawn in two days' time.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊,將於兩天後的破曉時分由此啟程。

column1: Hero, the caravan to Raseir leaves here in two days time, granted that there are no Elementals in the city.
column2: 英雄,前往拉希爾的商隊兩天後從這裡出發,前提是城裡別冒出元素精靈。

column1: The caravan to Raseir will leave with the dawn of tomorrow's sun, Hero.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊,將隨明日的旭日啟程,英雄。

column1: May thee walk safely this evening, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願汝今夜行路平安,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: The caravan traveling to Raseir will depart the morning after this day, oh Hero.
column2: 前往拉希爾的商隊,將在後天清晨啟程,啊,英雄。

column1: May your journey tomorrow be a safe one, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 願你明日的旅途平安順遂,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: Pesky Saurus!
column2: 討厭的索魯斯!

column1: I see you left your Saurus out in the desert. Most Sauruses find their way back here to the stables.
column2: 看來你把索魯斯丟在沙漠裡了。大多數索魯斯都會自己摸回這馬廄來。

column1: All Sauruses must be stabled in the corral. Only if you dismount may you enter the city.
column2: 所有索魯斯都得拴進柵欄裡。你得下了坐騎,才能進城。

column1: Let's see, my asking price was 50, your calling price 10, I raised you two clubs, you doubled, I redoubled, the stock market crashed, I doubted your double, you called me (just what, I'll never repeat), and we came to a gentlemen's agreement of 40 dinars.
column2: 我想想啊:我開價五十,你喊十,我加注兩梅花,你加倍,我再加倍,股市崩盤,我質疑你的加倍,你嗆我(嗆什麼我可不重複),最後咱倆達成君子協議,四十第納爾成交。

column1: (How we got the gentlemen to agree is beyond me.)
column2: (至於我們是怎麼把那兩位君子湊到一塊兒同意的,我也想不明白。)

column1: Let's see, my initial asking price was 50, you parried with 10, I gracefully riposted with 55, you countered with 20, I recountered with 60, you accounted, I encountered, we recounted and came up a couple short.
column2: 我想想啊:我起初開價五十,你格擋還十,我優雅地回刺五十五,你反擊二十,我再反攻六十,你結帳,我遭遇,我們重數,結果還差了幾枚。

column1: So to make up the difference, we agreed upon whether the party of the first part is to pay the party of the second part part of the sums owing, but we tore up that contract and you are buying the Saurus for 50 dinars, COD.
column2: 為了補上差額,我們約定甲方是否應向乙方支付所欠款項之部分,可後來把那張合約撕了,於是你就用五十第納爾、貨到付款買下這頭索魯斯。

column1: (I knew there was something fishy about that last part, but let's not carp now that the deal's been made.)
column2: (我就知道最後那段有點不對勁,不過生意都成了,咱也別再雞蛋裡挑骨頭啦。)

column1: Shapeir is God's country, and he can have it.
column2: 夏皮爾是上帝的應許之地——這地方就送祂吧。

column1: I used to dream about a girl with light brown hair and that name.
column2: 我以前常夢見一個淺棕髮的姑娘,就叫那名字。

column1: There's a lot of big game around here. You know, pinochle, blackjack, soccer...
column2: 這附近大型賽事可多了。你曉得的,撲克、二十一點、足球……

column1: If you think that's insulting, you haven't heard anything yet.
column2: 你要是覺得這話很傷人,那你還沒見識到後頭呢。

column1: I've played in the Palace before the queen. Fortunately, the king wasn't around.
column2: 我可在宮裡當著王后的面玩過牌。所幸國王當時不在場。

column1: To travel the desert around here, you need a good Saurus. A good Saurus will never desert you. However, the Saurus I have for you is your just desserts.
column2: 要在這一帶橫越沙漠,你得有頭好索魯斯。好索魯斯絕不會「拋棄(desert)」你。不過嘛,我給你留的這頭索魯斯,正是你應得的「報應(desserts)」。

column1: Go for Harrass in the third race. It's a sure thing unless they scratch her.
column2: 第三場賽你壓「哈拉絲」準沒錯。除非她臨時退賽,否則十拿九穩。

column1: You call this hot? I've been in places where every suit was a sweatsuit. I've seen it so hot that matches struck themselves. I've been to Fresno! Hot? Ha!
column2: 這也叫熱?我去過的地方,隨便一套衣服都熱成運動排汗衫。我見過熱到火柴自己擦出火來的鬼天氣。我可是去過弗雷斯諾!熱?哼!

column1: You know, the stuff you need when you go to the diner to buy dinner around here. Or maybe you don't know. In that case, when you get some dinars, I'll be glad to trade some dandy centimes for them, at say, one for ten.
column2: 你曉得的,就是在這一帶上小館子買晚飯時用得著的玩意兒。也許你不曉得。那這樣吧,等你弄到幾枚第納爾,我很樂意拿幾枚漂亮的生丁跟你換,比方說,一換十。

column1: It makes cents to carry centimes here.
column2: 在這兒帶著生丁,可是「分分(cents)」精打細算的明智之舉。

column1: Ah, money! The sound of centimes, the din of dinars! Music to my ears!
column2: 啊,錢哪!生丁叮噹作響,第納爾喧鬧不休!簡直是我耳中的天籟!

column1: Speaking of money, would you happen to be interested in making an investment in the future? You could contribute to the 'Used Saurus Seller's Retirement Fund.'
column2: 說到錢,你會不會剛好有興趣為將來投資一筆?你可以捐一份給「中古索魯斯販子退休基金」哪。

column1: This is your chance. All you need to do is contribute, and I'll throw in a used Saurus. Better yet, buy the Saurus in the first place and then I'll retire.
column2: 機會就在眼前。你只要捐款,我就附送一頭中古索魯斯。更好的辦法是,你乾脆一開始就把索魯斯買了,然後我就能退休啦。

column1: Ah, fair Dinarzad! She'll change your money and steal your heart... or is it the other way around?
column2: 啊,美麗的迪娜薩!她會幫你換錢,順便偷走你的心……還是說,順序反過來才對?

column1: Steal one kiss from her and you'll probably be missing your eyeteeth.
column2: 你要是偷她一個吻,八成連犬齒都得賠進去。

column1: Actually, one kiss from her, and you won't notice that your eyeteeth are missing.
column2: 說真的,被她一吻,你連犬齒不見了都不會察覺。

column1: Now that's what I call a woman.
column2: 這才叫女人嘛。

column1: I've worked the fountain, but I'm no soda jerk. Anyway, I certainly seem to be a fountain of information for you.
column2: 我在噴泉邊討過生活,可我不是賣汽水的小弟。話說回來,對你而言,我倒真像座取之不盡的「情報泉源」。

column1: Personally, I'm a merchant. I sell Sauruses. I'm the local Saurus source. Would you be interested in buying one? I can make you a deal on a late model with all the options thrown in. Options like saddle, bridle, two feet...
column2: 我本人嘛,是個商人。我賣索魯斯。本地索魯斯,我是貨源頭。有興趣買一頭嗎?我可以給你算便宜點,新款的,配備全送你。配備像是馬鞍、韁繩、兩隻腳……

column1: I'm Ali Fakir. Honest Ali Fakir. Honest Ali T. Fakir. I bet you don't know what the T. stands for? Edgar. But you were close though. My name is Honest Ali Fakir. Pleased to meet you.
column2: 我是阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·法基爾,老實人阿里·T·法基爾。我賭你猜不出那個 T 代表什麼?答案是「艾德加」。不過你也猜得挺接近了。我叫老實人阿里·法基爾,幸會幸會。

column1: A case of mistaken identity I fear. Who are you going to believe, me or your own eyes?
column2: 恐怕是認錯人了。你是要信我,還是要信你自己的眼睛?

column1: I've seen an awful lot of Sauruses and I've seen a lot of awful Sauruses.
column2: 我看過「多得嚇人」的索魯斯,也看過「嚇人地爛」的索魯斯。

column1: The Saurus is the ship of the desert, and you can't ship it far enough to suit me.
column2: 索魯斯號稱沙漠之舟,可不管把這「舟」運到多遠,都遠不到讓我滿意。

column1: All a Saurus takes is a little grass (say, a bale), some water (around 20 gallons), and a little love, and it will follow you anywhere, which can get mighty embarrassing at times, believe me.
column2: 養一頭索魯斯只需要一點草(比方說,一綑啦)、一些水(大約二十加侖),再加一點愛,牠就會跟著你天涯海角,有時候黏到夠你尷尬的,相信我。

column1: Picture yourself, mounting your very own Saurus for the first time. You get up, you fall off, you mount up again and sit proud in the saddle, knowing you can ride with the best of them.
column2: 想像一下:你頭一回跨上自己的索魯斯。你爬上去,你摔下來,你再爬上去,神氣地端坐鞍上,曉得自己騎術已能與高手並駕齊驅。

column1: It's spring, and there is magic in the air! Well, actually, it's summer, and there is something else in the air, considering our proximity to the stable.
column2: 春意盎然,空氣裡都飄著魔法!嗯,其實是夏天,而且空氣裡飄的是別的東西——畢竟我們離馬廄這麼近嘛。

column1: A moose is an animal with horns on the front of his head and an Adventurer's Guild wall on the back of it.
column2: 駝鹿是一種頭前長角的動物,而牠的角後頭,通常就掛在冒險者公會的牆上。

column1: If the subject offends you, why did you bring it up?
column2: 這話題若惹你不快,那你幹嘛先提起它?

column1: Then, wait, you see your faithful Saurus running up to you. You are saved! You can eat Saurus steaks for a week. In fact, you can set up a concession stand right there and make a tidy sum off of Saurus steaks.
column2: 然後,且慢,你瞧見你那忠心的索魯斯朝你奔來。你得救了!你能吃上一整週的索魯斯排。事實上,你大可就地擺個小攤子,靠賣索魯斯排撈一筆。

column1: You know, that's a very good question. You'll have to ask me another sometime.
column2: 你曉得嗎,這問題問得真好。改天你得再問我一個。

column1: I've heard questions in my time, and that's certainly one of them.
column2: 我這輩子聽過不少問題,而這肯定算是其中一個。

column1: It costs but 50 centimes to stable a Saurus there. It shall be watered and fed regularly.
column2: 在那兒寄養一頭索魯斯,只要五十生丁,飲水餵食一律按時打理。

column1: It is against our laws to bring a Saurus into the city, since it can be fierce in defense of an owner.
column2: 把索魯斯帶進城裡是違反我們律法的,因為牠護主起來會變得十分兇猛。

column1: The Dervish lives in the oasis to the south. He knows everything that goes on in the desert.
column2: 那位苦行僧住在南方的綠洲。沙漠裡發生的一切,他無所不知。

column1: A skareen is the distance from here to over there.
column2: 一個「斯卡林」,就是從這裡到那邊的距離。

column1: The oasis is a place in the desert where there is life-giving water. It is five skareen lengths to the south and three skareen lengths to the east from the town overlook.
column2: 綠洲是沙漠中湧出救命之水的所在。從鎮上的瞭望點往南五個斯卡林,再往東三個斯卡林,便是綠洲。

column1: Bedouin are nomads whose home is the entire desert.
column2: 貝都因人是游牧民族,整片沙漠都是他們的家。

column1: Dunes are the waves of the desert.
column2: 沙丘,就是沙漠的波浪。

column1: One must never travel in the desert without a source of water. You will need to buy a waterskin from one of the merchants.
column2: 進沙漠絕不可沒有水源。你得向某位商人買個水袋才行。

column1: A compass is useful to keep from getting lost. Food supplies to keep away hunger are a good idea.
column2: 羅盤能幫你免於迷路,很管用。備些口糧以防飢餓,也是個好主意。

column1: The one thing that will aid you most to stay alive in the desert is a good riding Saurus.
column2: 在沙漠裡最能助你保命的,莫過於一頭好騎的索魯斯。

column1: May it please you, I am called Abdallah bin Tahir.
column2: 承蒙垂詢,小的名喚阿卜杜拉·賓·塔希爾。

column1: He guards the gate during the daytime. He will be here at the dawning of the next sun.
column2: 他白天守著城門。明日旭日東升時,他便會在這裡。

column1: In the middle of the day, the sun will burn one. At night, many foul monsters prowl in search of easy prey.
column2: 正午時分,烈日會把人烤焦。入夜後,許多兇惡的怪物會四處遊蕩,尋找好下手的獵物。

column1: The dunes are all alike, and it is easy to lose one's way. Only if one is properly prepared can one endure, or when one travels in a caravan.
column2: 沙丘座座相似,人很容易迷失方向。唯有準備周全,或是隨商隊同行,才撐得下來。

column1: The deadly black Scorpion, the Jackals who are as men, the terrible al-ghuls (what you would call Ghouls!) -- these creatures roam the desert at night.
column2: 致命的黑蠍、形貌如人的豺狼、可怖的「阿勒古勒」(也就是你們所謂的食屍鬼!)——這些東西夜裡都在沙漠中遊蕩。

column1: Only a man of great courage or foolhardiness would go off in the desert alone after the sun has set.
column2: 日落之後還敢獨自闖進沙漠的,不是膽識過人,就是有勇無謀。

column1: They are as durable as they are deadly. You should not attempt to fight one unless you are agile enough to avoid its claws and tail. Even then, Scorpions carry nothing of value and are best avoided.
column2: 牠們既致命又難纏。除非你身手敏捷,躲得開牠的螯與尾,否則別想跟牠交手。就算躲得開,黑蠍身上也沒半點值錢東西,還是避開為妙。

column1: They hunt in packs and can easily overwhelm a traveler. Take caution, Effendi.
column2: 牠們成群獵食,輕易就能把一名旅人吞沒。多加小心,閣下。

column1: Their claws are deadly and they control powerful magic. The al-ghul are best left avoided.
column2: 牠們的利爪足以致命,還能操控強大的魔法。阿勒古勒這種東西,最好敬而遠之。

column1: I know only that they are very dangerous. You should return when the day guard is here. Perhaps he can tell you more.
column2: 我只曉得牠們極其危險。你該等日班守衛在的時候再來。或許他能告訴你更多。

column1: My name is Uns al-Wujud, which means 'Delight in Existing Things.'
column2: 我名叫烏恩斯·阿勒-烏朱德,意思是「萬物存在之喜」。

column1: He guards the gate at night. He will be here after the sun sets.
column2: 他夜裡守著城門。日落之後,他便會在這裡。

column1: The Brigands of the desert and the swift Terrorsaurus make travel by day in the desert even more dangerous. The Brigands will happily kill you for whatever you carry. The Terrorsaurus will hunt you for your flesh.
column2: 沙漠裡的強盜,加上迅捷的「恐索魯斯」,讓白天的沙漠之旅更加凶險。強盜會為了你身上隨便一樣東西就樂呵呵地宰了你;恐索魯斯則會為了你身上的肉一路追獵你。

column1: Only the brave and foolish travel the desert alone in the daytime.
column2: 白天還敢獨自橫越沙漠的,不是勇者,就是傻子。

column1: The Brigands will happily kill you for whatever you carry.
column2: 強盜會為了你身上隨便一樣東西就樂呵呵地宰了你。

column1: The Terrorsaurus will hunt you for your flesh.
column2: 恐索魯斯則會為了你身上的肉一路追獵你。

column1: You should ask the night guard about them, for I seldom see them during the day.
column2: 這事你該去問夜班守衛,牠們白天我可難得見著。

column1: Really? That's so amazing I can't wait until you don't tell me again.
column2: 真的嗎?太神奇了,我都等不及你「下次別」再講給我聽了。

column1: I thank thee for your story, Effendi. I am happy to hear of your many deeds.
column2: 多謝汝的故事,閣下。能聽聞你諸多事蹟,小的深感欣喜。

column1: Your stories are most interesting, Effendi. I thank thee for sharing them with me.
column2: 你的故事著實精采,閣下。多謝汝願意說與小的聽。

column1: You look like a man looking for a Saurus. I have the finest saddle Saurus you can find. A magnificent saddle Saurus!
column2: 你看起來就像個在找索魯斯的人。我這兒有你能找到最頂級的乘騎索魯斯,一頭威風凜凜的乘騎索魯斯!

column1: (If you ride one, that's exactly what you get.)
column2: (你騎上去就曉得,你買到的正是這麼回事。)

column1: At any rate, I'm here to sell you a Saurus. I'll sell you a Saurus, and I won't take 'no' for an answer. I don't know the meaning of 'no.'
column2: 反正呢,我就是來賣你索魯斯的。我非賣你一頭不可,「不要」這個答案我可不接受。我根本不懂「不」是什麼意思。

column1: So you need to buy a Saurus to travel in the desert. You're desperate. Hello, I'm Honest Ali Fakir. Pleased to meet you, Desperate. Have I got a Saurus for you. I don't know, do you?
column2: 這麼說,你得買頭索魯斯才好橫越沙漠。你急著很哪。哈囉,我是老實人阿里·法基爾。幸會幸會,急先生。我這兒可有頭索魯斯正適合你。我是不知道啦,你說有沒有?

column1: The fact of the matter is that you need a Saurus and I've got a Saurus, so let's get together and play pinochle. On the other hand, let's not. The Sauruses would cheat.
column2: 事情的真相是:你需要索魯斯,而我有索魯斯,所以咱倆乾脆湊一塊兒打牌吧。話說回來,還是算了。那些索魯斯准會出老千。

column1: You DO want to buy a Saurus, don't you?
column2: 你「確實」想買頭索魯斯,對吧?

column1: You never know when a Saurus might come in handy. Why, just the other day, you were thinking to yourself, "Self," you thought, "if only I had someone to play pinochle with!" If you had a Saurus, you still couldn't play pinochle.
column2: 你永遠料不準索魯斯何時派得上用場。哎,就在前幾天,你還自個兒尋思著:「我啊,」你想,「要是有個人陪我打牌就好了!」可就算你真有頭索魯斯,你還是打不成牌。

column1: It just goes to show you. And speaking of shows, have I got the Saurus for you. The Saurus. That's where you look for synonyms. And speaking of sin, when it comes to Sauruses, well, let's just keep your personal preferences a secret, shall we?
column2: 這就證明給你看了嘛。說到「證明」,我這兒可有頭索魯斯正適合你。索魯斯——就是你查同義詞要翻的那本「字典」啦。說到「罪」,談到索魯斯這檔事,嗯,你個人那點偏好咱還是保密吧,好嗎?

column1: (They don't call it Saurus Rex for nothing.)
column2: (人家管牠叫「索魯斯·雷克斯」可不是叫假的。)

column1: Greetings, Abdallah. May the evening be peaceful and uneventful.
column2: 你好,阿卜杜拉。願今夜平靜無事。

column1: May thee be granted a happy evening, Uns.
column2: 願汝得享愉快的夜晚,烏恩斯。

column1: Greetings, Uns. May the day be peaceful and uneventful.
column2: 你好,烏恩斯。願今日平靜無事。

column1: May thee be granted a happy morning, Abdallah.
column2: 願汝得享愉快的清晨,阿卜杜拉。

column1: What a shame we couldn't make a deal today. Come back and buy a Saurus tomorrow if you're still interested.
column2: 真可惜今天沒能成交。你要是還有興趣,明天再回來買頭索魯斯吧。

column1: (Or better yet... just come back tomorrow and buy a Saurus. I'll charge interest!)
column2: (或者更妙的是……你明天回來把索魯斯買了就成。我可要收利息哦!)

column1: Unlike the town of Spielburg, Shapeir doesn't seem to have a town gate. Hence, what you're trying to do doesn't really work.
column2: 不像史畢柏格那座小鎮,夏皮爾似乎沒有城門這玩意兒。因此呢,你想做的那件事根本行不通。

column1: You can't open the gate while you're riding your Saurus.
column2: 你騎在索魯斯背上是開不了城門的。

column1: Let the guard do that for you.
column2: 讓守衛替你開吧。

column1: None of the Sauruses here belong to you. You left your own Saurus at Aziza's house.
column2: 這裡的索魯斯沒一隻是你的。你自己的索魯斯留在阿姿莎家了。

column1: You left your Saurus out in the desert. You would never find him if you went out looking. Better hope he wanders in like the guard said.
column2: 你把索魯斯丟在沙漠裡了。就算出去找也絕對找不著,只能盼牠像守衛說的那樣自己晃回來。

column1: Those Sauruses don't belong to you.
column2: 那些索魯斯不是你的。

column1: You pick up a handful of sand and watch mournfully as it trickles through your fingers and disappears.
column2: 你抓起一把沙,黯然望著它從指縫間流瀉而下,消失無蹤。

column1: Through this gate saunter some of the sorriest Sauruses ever.
column2: 這道門裡晃進晃出的,盡是些有史以來最寒酸的索魯斯。

column1: You wave in Shapeir's general direction. There is, of course, no reply.
column2: 你朝夏皮爾的大致方向揮了揮手。當然,沒人理你。

column1: You can't reach the Saurus pen from here.
column2: 你從這裡搆不到索魯斯欄。

column1: You call out in Shapeir's general direction. There is, of course, no reply.
column2: 你朝夏皮爾的大致方向喊了一聲。當然,沒人回應。

column1: Hostile intent is evident. You quickly move into battle position.
column2: 對方明顯來者不善。你迅速擺出戰鬥姿勢。

column1: You quickly move into battle position.
column2: 你迅速擺出戰鬥姿勢。

column1: Bull's-eye! Looks like those games of dag-nab-it paid off. Your dagger hits its target with deadly accuracy.
column2: 正中紅心!看來那些飛刀練習沒白費。你的匕首精準致命地命中目標。

column1: Persian Golfer
column2: 波斯高爾夫客

column1: You notice there's still venom dripping out of the insectoid's tail.
column2: 你注意到這蟲形怪物的尾巴還在滴著毒液。

column1: The Scorpion still looks threatening, despite the fact that its stinger is now missing.
column2: 蠍子的尾刺雖然沒了,看起來依舊不好惹。

column1: The Brigand's sword doesn't look like a weapon of high quality.
column2: 這名強盜的劍看起來不是什麼上等貨色。

column1: The Brigand just learned the consequences of picking on a victim tougher than himself.
column2: 這名強盜算是學到了:挑上比自己硬的對手,是要付出代價的。

column1: The Terrorsaurus appears to have met its match in you.
column2: 看來恐魯斯遇上你這個剋星了。

column1: The Ghoul finally lies still. Let's hope it never rises again.
column2: 食屍鬼終於不動了。但願牠永遠別再爬起來。

column1: You gave the Jackalman something to howl about.
column2: 你讓這頭豺狼人有得哀號了。

column1: All in a day's work.
column2: 不過是家常便飯罷了。

column1: The more the merrier.
column2: 人多熱鬧,多多益善。

column1: It's starting to get hard to count them.
column2: 數量多到開始數不過來了。

column1: Passersby might wonder if there's a rugby ball underneath.
column2: 路人說不定會以為那底下藏了顆橄欖球。

column1: You ease your nerves a bit by taunting the defeated Scorpion.
column2: 你嘲弄一番手下敗將的蠍子,稍稍紓解了緊繃的神經。

column1: It's a bit too late for that. He's no longer in any condition to reply.
column2: 現在說這個有點太遲了,牠早已沒能力回話。

column1: The dead Terrorsaurus is at a loss for words.
column2: 死透的恐魯斯啞口無言。

column1: Dead ghouls tell no tales.
column2: 死掉的食屍鬼是不會洩密的。

column1: You tell the Jackalman to play dead.
column2: 你叫這頭豺狼人裝死。

column1: You tell the Jackalmen to play dead.
column2: 你叫這群豺狼人裝死。

column1: You take the tail, carefully avoiding its poison.
column2: 你小心避開毒液,取下了那條尾巴。

column1: What a waste. No treasure.
column2: 真是白忙一場,半點財寶也沒有。

column1: Sorry, the monster's dead broke.
column2: 抱歉,這怪物身上窮得叮噹響。

column1: It is in such bad shape that it would be of no use to you.
column2: 這東西破損得太嚴重,對你毫無用處。

column1: You also find an additional dagger on the Brigand's corpse, which you take as well.
column2: 你還在強盜屍體上摸到一把多出來的匕首,順手也收下了。

column1: Sorry, the Brigand's dead broke.
column2: 抱歉,這強盜窮得身無分文。

column1: You take the Ghoul's severed head with you.
column2: 你帶走了食屍鬼被砍下的頭顱。

column1: With a feeling of disgust, you remove the Ghoul's claws and take them with you.
column2: 你強忍嫌惡,卸下食屍鬼的利爪一併帶走。

column1: Sorry, the monsters are dead broke.
column2: 抱歉,這些怪物個個窮得精光。

column1: It's a golf flag.
column2: 這是一面高爾夫球旗。

column1: The waves of desert dunes roll toward the mountains.
column2: 一波波沙丘如浪,朝著群山翻湧而去。

column1: A vast mountain range forms an impassable barrier to the north.
column2: 北面橫亙著一道遼闊的山脈,成了無法翻越的屏障。

column1: A vast mountain range forms an impassable barrier to the south.
column2: 南面橫亙著一道遼闊的山脈,成了無法翻越的屏障。

column1: The blazing stars take on new meanings in the deserts of Shapeir.
column2: 在夏皮爾的沙漠裡,那些燦爛的繁星彷彿有了全新的意義。

column1: You fetch the doughy disk from the desert floor, brush the sand off its bottom and eat it. What comes around goes around. Its taste is beyond anything you've ever tasted and you feel refreshed and invigorated.
column2: 你從沙地上撿起那塊麵餅般的圓盤,拍掉底下的沙子吃了下去。種什麼因得什麼果。那滋味勝過你這輩子嚐過的一切,令你頓覺神清氣爽、精神大振。

column1: Doughy Disk
column2: 麵團圓盤

column1: Eenough! No more! You ween! You ween!
column2: 夠惹!別打惹!你贏惹!你贏惹!

column1: Stop eet! Stop eet! I geeve up already!
column2: 住手!住手!偶投降惹啦!

column1: Strange Creature
column2: 奇異生物

column1: Mama mia, deez desert ees murderous.
column2: 媽呀,介片沙漠真要人命啊。

column1: Why ees dees ceety een dee meedle of nowhere?
column2: 為森麼介座城蓋在介種鳥不生蛋的地方啊?

column1: You! You exeest? Ever seenze I have been in zee desert, I have seen nothing but monsters and theengz that do not exeest. Where deed you come from?
column2: 你!你竟然栓在?偶自從進惹介片沙漠,看到的不是怪物就是根本不栓在的東西。你打哪兒冒出來的?

column1: You tell the creature that you are from the city of Shapeir.
column2: 你告訴這生物,你來自夏皮爾城。

column1: Seeriously? Is eet reelly posseeble that I have meessed eet?
column2: 真滴假滴?偶該不會真的把它給錯過惹吧?

column1: You ask the creature what kind of being it is and why it is looking for Shapeir.
column2: 你問這生物究竟是何方神聖,又為何要找夏皮爾。

column1: I am suppozed to viseet zee city and feeast upon eet. This ees what we elementals excel at.
column2: 偶本該去那座城裡大吃一頓的呀。介可是我們元素生物最拿手的本事呢。

column1: You realize that the creature, odd as it looks, nevertheless has malicious intentions. You wonder what you should do.
column2: 你發覺這生物模樣雖怪,心思卻不懷好意。你盤算著該如何是好。

column1: Mama mia, deez heat ees murderous.
column2: 媽呀,介種熱法真要人命啊。

column1: A little disk of dough is all that's left of the gargantuan creature.
column2: 那頭龐然巨物,如今只剩下一小塊麵餅般的圓盤。

column1: You pick up the doughy disk from the desert floor, brush the sand off its bottom and eat it. What comes around goes around. Its taste is beyond anything you've ever tasted and you feel refreshed and invigorated.
column2: 你從沙地上拾起那塊麵餅般的圓盤,拍掉底下的沙子吃了下去。種什麼因得什麼果。那滋味勝過你這輩子嚐過的一切,令你頓覺神清氣爽、精神大振。

column1: It can't reply, since it no longer has a mouth. You prefer it that way too.
column2: 它沒法回話,因為它連嘴都沒了。不過你倒寧可如此。

column1: You pick up the doughy disk from the desert floor, brush the sand off its bottom and put it in the container.
column2: 你從沙地上拾起那塊麵餅般的圓盤,拍掉底下的沙子,放進容器裡。

column1: That's really thinking outside the box!
column2: 這還真是別出心裁、跳出框框啊!

column1: You inform the creature that you are the Hero of Shapeir and if wishes to do harm to the city, it'll have to do so over your dead body.
column2: 你向這生物表明,你是夏皮爾的英雄,牠若想加害這座城,就得先跨過你的屍體。

column1: You weesh to fight? Is zees a joke? I have used beeger shrimps than you as toppeengs!
column2: 你想跟偶打?開森笑吧?比你大隻的蝦米,偶都拿來當配料撒過呢!

column1: You tell the creature to flee or fight.
column2: 你叫這生物要嘛逃,要嘛戰。

column1: I weell give you a fight, leetle one.
column2: 那偶就奉陪到底,小不點。

column1: You tell the creature that you got a little bit lost as well, but you remember Shapeir to be far to the west of here.
column2: 你告訴這生物,你自己也有點迷路了,不過你記得夏皮爾在這裡往西的老遠老遠處。

column1: I weell continue my journee then. Grazie and ciao to you.
column2: 那偶就繼續趕路啦。多謝惹,後會有期!

column1: It's a bit too late for that. He's no longer in the condition to reply.
column2: 現在說這個有點太遲了,牠早已沒能力回話。

column1: Land of Shapeir like rainbow; very colorful and always something new.
column2: 夏皮爾之地恰似彩虹,五光十色,總有新鮮事。

column1: When laws made without justice, men live without freedom.
column2: 律法若無公義,百姓便無自由。

column1: Djinn are like weather: always changeable, never predictable.
column2: 精靈如天氣,變化莫測,從不照牌理出牌。

column1: Fire Elemental like mule: cannot be driven, only be encouraged to follow.
column2: 火元素如騾子,趕不動,只能哄著牠跟。

column1: Quest for Glory is like same old story.
column2: 追尋榮耀,說來說去總是老調重彈。

column1: Wind blows fastest without dirt.
column2: 風無塵埃,吹得最疾。

column1: Perseverance can wear away rock.
column2: 滴水穿石,貴在恆心。

column1: The Water Elemental is to water as the lodestone is to metal.
column2: 水元素之於水,猶如磁石之於鐵。

column1: Elemental reflects its own nature.
column2: 元素生物,映照的正是自身的本性。

column1: Monster is as monster does.
column2: 怪物的本性,看牠所作所為便知。

column1: Magic is like water; without it, Shapeir is like sand.
column2: 魔法如水,沒了它,夏皮爾不過一盤散沙。

column1: A ruler who knows his people as his own hand.
column2: 好的統治者熟知子民,如熟知自己的手掌。

column1: A fool does not rule for long.
column2: 蠢人坐不久龍椅。

column1: Wizards sit and study problem while a hero solves it.
column2: 巫師坐著鑽研難題,英雄卻早已把它解決。

column1: Beggar in street happier than prisoner in palace.
column2: 街頭乞丐,也比宮中囚徒來得快活。

column1: A dervish sits from sun to sun, but a hero's work is never done.
column2: 苦行僧從日出枯坐到日落,英雄的活兒卻永遠做不完。

column1: Water is like wisdom; without it, man is just dust.
column2: 水如智慧,沒了它,人不過一抔塵土。

column1: Creature in need need hero indeed.
column2: 生靈有難,正需英雄相援。

column1: Time flies, desert dries, creature dies.
column2: 時光飛逝,沙漠乾涸,生靈隨之命喪。

column1: A wise man can survive where a fool will perish.
column2: 同一處絕境,智者能活,蠢人必亡。

column1: The mountains stand to the north and south, as they have for as long as I've been here.
column2: 群山屹立南北兩方,自我來到此地起便是如此。

column1: All that glitters is not gold; not all that is gold is for the taking.
column2: 閃閃發光的未必是金;就算是金,也未必拿得走。

column1: Man who steals is like nightfall -- sneaks everywhere and takes things from sight.
column2: 賊人如夜幕降臨,四處潛行,把東西悄悄從眼前偷走。

column1: Adventurer is one who has no home, but is never homeless; has no help, but is never helpless.
column2: 冒險者沒有家,卻從不無家可歸;沒有援手,卻從不束手無策。

column1: Man who ask about weather has stormy disposition.
column2: 老問天氣的人,脾氣多半像場風暴。

column1: Where all men share, no man thirsts.
column2: 人人懂得分享之處,沒人會口渴。

column1: Three men can keep secret if two are dead.
column2: 三人想守住祕密,除非其中兩人已死。

column1: A trader knows the value of a coin.
column2: 生意人最懂一枚錢幣的份量。

column1: Man who seeks future in stars finds business looking up.
column2: 往星空裡尋找未來的人,生意自然蒸蒸日上。

column1: Katta like Dervish -- content to sit in sun and think.
column2: 卡塔族像苦行僧,樂於曬著太陽靜靜沉思。

column1: Wise man learn much by saying little.
column2: 智者話說得少,學到的卻多。

column1: Man who speaks rhyme for reason.
column2: 出口成韻的人,自有他的一番道理。

column1: Man with no name like question with no answer.
column2: 無名之人,如同無解之問。

column1: Water is the liquid of life, and a mirror to the soul.
column2: 水是生命之液,也是靈魂的明鏡。

column1: One who watches world whirl by sees many things.
column2: 靜看世界轉動的人,見得到許多事物。

column1: Man who sells pill and potion created with skill and devotion.
column2: 賣藥丸與藥水的人,憑的是一手技藝與一片真心。

column1: Once you start, it's very hard to stop.
column2: 一旦開了頭,就很難收得了手。

column1: It does not give much shade, but I do get a date once in a while.
column2: 它遮不了多少蔭,不過偶爾倒能讓我摘顆椰棗嚐嚐。

column1: Freedom like temper -- only valued when lost.
column2: 自由如脾氣,失去了才懂得它的可貴。

column1: Your Saurus is like reflection in pool; both but seem to be real.
column2: 你的索魯斯像水池裡的倒影,看似真切,實則虛幻。

column1: Difference between beard and world is one whirls around tree, the other twirls around me.
column2: 鬍子與世界有何不同?一個繞著樹打轉,一個繞著我打轉。

column1: When he who seeks what is where finds out why.
column2: 當尋找「何物何處」的人,終於參透了「為何」。

column1: Oasis is like wise saying because both seem shallow, but are very deep.
column2: 綠洲就像至理名言,看似淺薄,實則深不可測。

column1: Griffin who nest on mountain ledge always edgy.
column2: 在山崖築巢的獅鷲,神經總是繃得緊緊的。

column1: Man who jokes with Scorpion gets it in the end.
column2: 拿蠍子開玩笑的人,最後總要在尾巴上吃虧。

column1: Man who steals purse gets trashed.
column2: 偷錢袋的人,終究會被痛扁一頓。

column1: Terrorsaurus is like man in desert: very fond of lesser Sauruses.
column2: 恐魯斯就像沙漠裡的漢子:對那些次等的索魯斯情有獨鍾。

column1: Jackalman is like sardines; always goes in packs.
column2: 豺狼人像沙丁魚,出沒總是成群結隊。

column1: Ghouls give fighter the cold shoulder.
column2: 食屍鬼總愛給戰士一個冷颼颼的下馬威。

column1: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man... Well, one out of three ain't bad.
column2: 早睡早起讓人……嗯,三樣裡中一樣也不算太差。

column1: It is said that man is like a caged beast. And a beast is like a rumor -- it is best to dispel it early. Harik knows the way, and I know the where.
column2: 人們說,人就像籠中之獸。而獸又像謠言,最好趁早化解。哈里克知道「如何」,而我知道「何處」。

column1: The Where is five skareen lengths to the west of Shapeir and but two skareen lengths north of here.
column2: 那「何處」在夏皮爾以西五個斯卡林之遙,離此地以北則僅兩個斯卡林。

column1: That is the heart of the puzzle. Harik may help you free the frozen heart from the body of the beast.
column2: 那正是這道謎題的核心。哈里克或許能助你將那顆冰封之心,從巨獸的軀體裡解放出來。

column1: If not soon, then never.
column2: 若不趁早,便再無機會。

column1: When you discover the Why, you will know more about the puzzle around the puzzle.
column2: 當你參透了「為何」,便會更懂得這道謎題之外的那道謎題。

column1: That is why you must solve the puzzle. Knowing Who will help you find out Why.
column2: 這正是你非解開謎題不可的緣由。知曉「何人」,將助你查明「為何」。

column1: Hows are for heroes. When you find the What, perhaps you will know How.
column2: 「如何」是留給英雄的。當你尋得了「何物」,或許你就會明白「如何」。

column1: Many Sauruses who do not wish to go ridden by men who do not wish to stay.
column2: 許多索魯斯不願被騎走,正如許多人不願久留。

column1: Why, a skareen length is about as far across as this area around my oasis.
column2: 唉呀,一個斯卡林大約就跟我這綠洲周遭一帶那麼寬罷了。

column1: If you want right answer, ask right person.
column2: 想要正確的答案,就得問對人。

column1: Man who ask same question twice get same answer twice.
column2: 同一個問題問兩遍的人,只會得到同樣的答案兩遍。

column1: Man who commits crimes and brags about it eventually becomes his own victim.
column2: 犯了罪還四處吹噓的人,終究會淪為自己的受害者。

column1: fight man
column2: 攻擊那人

column1: fight him
column2: 攻擊他

column1: attack man
column2: 攻擊那人

column1: attack him
column2: 攻擊他

column1: attack dervish
column2: 攻擊苦行僧

column1: It appears that not only will he not fight with you, your attacks to him cause no harm.
column2: 看樣子他不但不肯跟你過招,你的攻擊對他也毫髮無傷。

column1: Man who tells stories is wise. Man who listens to stories is even wiser.
column2: 說故事的人有智慧,聽故事的人更有智慧。

column1: Hero business like eating peanuts.
column2: 當英雄這檔事啊,就像嗑花生,一顆接一顆停不下來。

column1: Man who go about in desert sun soon find himself medium well.
column2: 在沙漠豔陽下亂晃的人,很快就會把自己曬成五分熟。

column1: He who turns to run away sometimes finds knife in back.
column2: 轉身逃跑的人,有時會發現背上插了一把刀。

column1: Freedom is like temper.
column2: 自由就像脾氣,一旦失去就難找回。

column1: If fire burns too bright, put damper on lamp.
column2: 火若燒得太旺,就把燈芯調暗些。

column1: Your Saurus is like reflection in pool.
column2: 你的索魯斯就像池中倒影。
