column1: He who seeks answers must first ask right questions.
column2: 尋求答案的人,得先學會問對問題。

column1: Early to bed and early to rise make Jack dull boy.
column2: 早睡早起,卻把人累成個悶葫蘆。

column1: Man who eat cookies in bed wake up with crumbs.
column2: 在床上吃餅乾的人,醒來滿身都是屑。

column1: He who knows what evil lurks in the hearts of men has Shadow of doubt.
column2: 看穿人心深處潛藏之惡的人,心中總有一抹疑影。

column1: Oasis like wise saying.
column2: 綠洲就如同至理名言,讓人在荒蕪中得到滋潤。

column1: Man who talk without thinking have foot-in-mouth disease.
column2: 講話不經大腦的人,難免一開口就出糗。

column1: Man who waits too late gets no response from man who waits even later.
column2: 等得太晚的人,別指望從等得更晚的人那兒得到回應。

column1: To the west of Shapeir and north of here stands a puzzle. Five W's.
column2: 夏皮爾以西、此地以北,佇立著一道謎題。五個 W。

column1: You take a sip of the warm water.
column2: 你啜了一口溫水。

column1: You refill your waterskins.
column2: 你把水袋重新裝滿。

column1: He who attacks without cause soon attracts without pause.
column2: 無故出手攻擊的人,很快就會招來沒完沒了的麻煩。

column1: Your attack startled the frog and scared it away.
column2: 你的攻擊嚇了青蛙一跳,把牠給驚跑了。

column1: Perhaps you should not attempt such a thing while riding a Saurus.
column2: 或許你不該騎在索魯斯背上做這種事。

column1: You can't do that on a Saurus.
column2: 騎在索魯斯背上可辦不到這事。

column1: You take a deep drink of the warm but refreshing water.
column2: 你大口暢飲這溫熱卻沁人心脾的水。

column1: You take another deep drink of the warm but refreshing water.
column2: 你又大口暢飲了一回這溫熱卻沁人心脾的水。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskin is full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現水袋已經滿了。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskins are full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現所有水袋都已裝滿。

column1: You then check and refill your waterskin.
column2: 接著你檢查並把水袋重新裝滿。

column1: You then check and refill all your waterskins.
column2: 接著你檢查並把所有水袋都重新裝滿。

column1: You should dismount first, Hero.
column2: 英雄,你該先下坐騎才行。

column1: You have some of his beard already.
column2: 你已經取到了一些他的鬍鬚。

column1: You can't unwrap the Dervish's beard. It's stuck to the tree.
column2: 你沒法解開苦行僧的鬍鬚,它牢牢黏在樹上。

column1: You take a small piece of the Dervish's beard and put it carefully away.
column2: 你取下一小撮苦行僧的鬍鬚,小心翼翼地收了起來。

column1: Beard must stay or Dervish drift away.
column2: 鬍鬚得留著,否則苦行僧就會飄走。

column1: Rocks of various size are scattered throughout the desert.
column2: 大大小小的石塊散落在整片沙漠中。

column1: These pebbles are too large to take with you.
column2: 這些石子太大了,沒法隨身帶走。

column1: Do that, and pretty soon you'll have no teeth left.
column2: 真這麼幹的話,你的牙很快就要掉光光了。

column1: Rocks form a natural alcove, protecting the oasis from the desert elements.
column2: 岩石圍成一處天然凹穴,為綠洲擋去沙漠的風吹日曬。

column1: These rocks are a little too big to carry in your pack.
column2: 這些石頭有點太大,塞不進你的背包。

column1: No. You'll chip your teeth.
column2: 別,你會把牙給崩了。

column1: They're very green.
column2: 它們綠得很。

column1: You release the frog back into the water. It dives under and swims away.
column2: 你把青蛙放回水裡,牠一頭鑽下去游走了。

column1: Draining the pool of water would be both unnecessary and cruel.
column2: 把池水放乾既沒必要,又太殘忍了。

column1: You don't want to get it wet.
column2: 你可不想把它弄濕。

column1: The Dervish spends most of his time reflecting.
column2: 苦行僧大半時間都在沉思冥想。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the Dervish's reflection.
column2: 別費勁去跟苦行僧的倒影說話了。

column1: Taking the Dervish's reflection might reflect badly on you.
column2: 拿走苦行僧的倒影,恐怕會讓你顏面盡失。

column1: The reflection of the Saurus proves that it is not a vampire.
column2: 索魯斯映得出倒影,證明牠不是吸血鬼。

column1: Try as you might, the Saurus' reflection just won't come out of the pool. It must be stuck to the surface of the water!
column2: 任你怎麼試,索魯斯的倒影就是撈不出池子。它八成黏死在水面上了!

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the Saurus' reflection.
column2: 別費勁去跟索魯斯的倒影說話了。

column1: Rings expand outward as your Saurus laps at the cool water.
column2: 你的索魯斯舔著清涼的水,一圈圈漣漪向外盪開。

column1: You can't possibly climb the tree carrying this much excess weight.
column2: 你帶著這麼多累贅,根本爬不上樹。

column1: You don't know how to climb.
column2: 你不會攀爬。

column1: You see dune upon dune, stretching in all directions as far as the eye can see.
column2: 你看見沙丘連著沙丘,向四面八方一路延展到天邊。

column1: Climbing the tree would require more skill than you currently possess.
column2: 爬這棵樹所需的本事,超出了你目前的能耐。

column1: This tree is not suitable for climbing.
column2: 這棵樹不適合攀爬。

column1: It's a lily pad.
column2: 那是一片睡蓮葉。

column1: A small, azure frog sits upon the lily pad.
column2: 一隻小巧的天藍色青蛙蹲在睡蓮葉上。

column1: Don't be silly, Lilly. Leave that pad, dad.
column2: 別傻了,小莉。離那片葉子遠點,老爹。

column1: You cannot get the frog. It floats on the lily pad, beyond your reach.
column2: 你抓不到那隻青蛙。牠浮在睡蓮葉上,在你搆不著的地方。

column1: Ribbit, ribbit! Croak. It seems like your frogspeak could use some practice.
column2: 呱、呱!呱呱。看樣子你的蛙語還得多練練。

column1: You can't use this here.
column2: 你沒法在這裡使用這個。

column1: Clever thinking! You suck the blue frog toward you using the bellows. You store the amphibian safely away in your pack.
column2: 好聰明!你用風箱把藍青蛙吸了過來,把這隻兩棲類穩穩收進背包。

column1: The bellows already contain the Air Elemental.
column2: 風箱裡已經裝了風之精靈。

column1: ;You've really made an impact
column2: 你這下可真是一鳴驚人。

column1: The Griffin, formerly peaceful and content, now unhappy and upset, leaves the scene.
column2: 那隻獅鷲原本安詳自得,如今卻悶悶不樂,氣呼呼地離開了現場。

column1: You've moved the rock about as far as it will budge.
column2: 這塊石頭能挪的,你都已經挪到底了。

column1: You're a big hero around here. You don't have to be tidy. You don't have to put all the nice rocks back where you found them.
column2: 你可是這一帶的大英雄,用不著愛乾淨,也不必把那些漂亮石頭一一放回原處。

column1: The rock was heavy, but after the sand was moved, you manage to get the rock off the feather.
column2: 石頭挺沉,不過把沙撥開後,你總算把石頭從羽毛上挪了開來。

column1: Even after moving the sand, you are still too weak to move the rock. Perhaps you should do some more exercise to build up your strength.
column2: 就算撥開了沙,你還是力氣不夠,搬不動這塊石頭。或許你該多鍛鍊鍛鍊,把力量練上去。

column1: The rock was heavy, but you manage to move it off the feather.
column2: 石頭挺沉,不過你總算把它從羽毛上挪了開來。

column1: The rock is firmly embedded in the sand.
column2: 這塊石頭牢牢嵌在沙裡。

column1: It's up there. You're down here.
column2: 它在上頭,你在下頭。

column1: You find a large feather in the nest and put it away.
column2: 你在巢裡找到一根大羽毛,把它收了起來。

column1: If you'd really wanted to climb, you wouldn't be doing what you're doing right now.
column2: 你要是真想攀爬,現在就不會在做手邊這檔事了。

column1: With your skill, you can tell that since it juts outward, it would be too dangerous to climb the cliff.
column2: 憑你的本事,你看得出這峭壁向外凸出,攀爬起來實在太危險。

column1: You can't possibly climb the cliffs carrying this much excess weight.
column2: 你帶著這麼多累贅,根本爬不上這些峭壁。

column1: It is hard to climb when the rock juts out toward you. You think that maybe climbing these cliffs is just too difficult.
column2: 岩石朝你這方向凸出來,實在難爬。你心想,要爬上這些峭壁恐怕難如登天。

column1: From this angle, the nest seems to be made up mostly of rocks.
column2: 從這個角度看,那巢似乎大半是用石頭堆成的。

column1: You can see that the nest is made up mostly of rocks, hair, pin-feathers, and the down from the Griffin's breast.
column2: 你看得出,那巢主要是由石塊、毛髮、初生的針羽,以及獅鷲胸前的絨毛堆成的。

column1: A Cliff Note:[[How high a cliff would make a miffed Griffin stiff, if the gifted Griffin could lift cliffs?
column2: 峭壁小註:[[假如那天賦異稟的獅鷲能舉起峭壁,那要多高的峭壁才能讓一隻氣呼呼的獅鷲僵在原地?

column1: You see various rocks lying on the ground around here.
column2: 你看見這附近的地上散落著大大小小的石塊。

column1: The sand and rock maintain their heat even during the chilly evening hours.
column2: 即便到了夜裡寒涼的時分,沙石依舊餘溫不散。

column1: The searing heat from the bright, burning sands is too much for your strained eyes.
column2: 炙熱滾燙的沙地反射出刺眼強光,你那疲憊的雙眼實在吃不消。

column1: Screech chirpy caw peep, squawka squawka!
column2: 唧——啾呱啾啾,嘎啦嘎啦!

column1: That sounded an awful lot like "I think there's a feather under that rock there," but of course we all know that Griffins can't talk!
column2: 那聽起來活像是「我想那邊那塊石頭底下有根羽毛」,不過大家當然都曉得,獅鷲是不會說話的!

column1: Hmm. Let's see now. "Chirpy" is the first person indicative...
column2: 嗯,我想想啊。「啾」是第一人稱直述語氣……

column1: No, wait. "Caw" is the noun form...
column2: 不對,等等。「呱」是名詞形態……

column1: Well, you think that might have been "I have a red pencil box," but you're not sure. Perhaps you should have paid more attention in "Griffin 101."
column2: 唔,你覺得那大概是「我有個紅色鉛筆盒」,但你也說不準。看來你該在「獅鷲語入門」課上多用點心才對。

column1: Chirp caw peepa-peepa, screechy screechy!
column2: 啾呱啾啾啾啾,唧唧唧唧!

column1: Hmm. That rather sounded like "Can you direct me to the public bath," but you're not sure. You should have kept your Griffin-English phrase book from Adventurer's School.
column2: 唔,那聽起來頗像是「能告訴我公共澡堂怎麼走嗎」,但你也說不準。你真該把冒險者學校發的那本獅鷲語會話手冊留著的。

column1: Freep squeaky pip-pip, awoooooo!
column2: 咈哩唧嘰嗶嗶,嗷——嗚——!

column1: You believe that was "I will not buy your rotten pears," but you're not sure. Of course, you can never be sure when talking with a Griffin.
column2: 你認為那是「我才不買你那些爛梨子」,但你也說不準。當然啦,跟獅鷲講話這事,你永遠也說不準。

column1: Screechy squawk, titwillow, titwillow!
column2: 唧唧嘎嘎,啼柳啼柳!

column1: If your translation is correct, that was "May a sleepy hippopotamus lie down on your house keys," but you're not sure. Unfortunately, your fluency in Griffin-speak is too low.
column2: 若你沒譯錯,那是「願一隻睏倦的河馬壓在你家鑰匙上」,但你也說不準。可惜啊,你的獅鷲語還太不流利。

column1: Cheep, cheep, tite, penurious!
column2: 啾、啾,嘀,摳搜搜!

column1: You think that roughly translates to "I can give you five dinars for the gourd," but you're not sure. Perhaps you should have paid more attention in "Griffin 101."
column2: 你覺得那大致可譯為「我願出五第納爾買那個葫蘆」,但你也說不準。看來你該在「獅鷲語入門」課上多用點心才對。

column1: FHScratch squawk screech do-be-do-be-doooo!
column2: 唰啦嘎嘎唧——嘟啵嘟啵嘟——!

column1: Well, you think that might have been "My sister has a Saurus for sale," but you're not sure. You should have kept your Griffin-English phrase book from Adventurer's School.
column2: 唔,你覺得那大概是「我姊姊有頭索魯斯要賣」,但你也說不準。你真該把冒險者學校發的那本獅鷲語會話手冊留著的。

column1: WMumble mumble... tourists... mumble mumble!
column2: 咕噥咕噥……觀光客……咕噥咕噥!

column1: Hmm. That rather sounded like "Excuse me, your camel has fallen on my mother," but you're not sure. Of course, you can never be sure when talking with a Griffin.
column2: 唔,那聽起來頗像是「不好意思,你的駱駝壓在我娘身上了」,但你也說不準。當然啦,跟獅鷲講話這事,你永遠也說不準。

column1: You believe that was "Your horse is foundered and I do not need that," but you're not sure. Unfortunately, your fluency in Griffin-speak is too low.
column2: 你認為那是「你的馬跑垮啦,我可不需要那玩意兒」,但你也說不準。可惜啊,你的獅鷲語還太不流利。

column1: If your translation is correct, that was "May I apply spices to your beard?", but you're not sure. Perhaps you should have paid more attention in "Griffin 101."
column2: 若你沒譯錯,那是「我能在你鬍子上撒點香料嗎?」,但你也說不準。看來你該在「獅鷲語入門」課上多用點心才對。

column1: You see sheer cliff walls where the mountains begin, stark against the brilliant blue of the sky. On a ledge jutting out from the cliff, a Griffin seems to have made a nest.
column2: 你看見群山起始之處豎著陡直的峭壁,在湛藍天空的襯托下格外鮮明。一隻獅鷲似乎在峭壁向外凸出的一處岩台上築了巢。

column1: Some debris has fallen from the nest.
column2: 一些殘屑從巢裡掉了下來。

column1: You can see part of a feather sticking out from under a rock near the cliff base.
column2: 你看見峭壁腳下一塊石頭底下,露出了一截羽毛。

column1: There is a feather lying near the rock.
column2: 石頭旁邊躺著一根羽毛。

column1: The rock is as you left it when you turned it over.
column2: 這塊石頭就維持在你翻過來後的模樣。

column1: It's a Griffin. It's a plane. It's... gone!
column2: 是獅鷲!是飛機!是……不見啦!

column1: You already took the only feather.
column2: 唯一的那根羽毛你已經拿走了。

column1: You pick up a large feather and put it away.
column2: 你撿起一根大羽毛,把它收了起來。

column1: You dig some sand away from around the base of the rock.
column2: 你把石頭底部周圍的沙撥開了一些。

column1: You can't quite free the feather, but you do manage to dig some sand from around the rock.
column2: 你還沒法把羽毛弄出來,不過總算把石頭周圍的沙撥開了一些。

column1: What fun! Just like your sandbox back home.
column2: 真好玩!就跟你老家的沙坑一個樣。

column1: The feather looks just like a Griffin feather.
column2: 這根羽毛看起來活脫脫就是獅鷲的羽毛。

column1: You hear a strangled cry of protest from the beast.
column2: 你聽見那頭怪獸發出一聲哽咽般的抗議。

column1: When you look back at it, it seems to say, "Water. I must have water."
column2: 你回頭望向牠時,牠彷彿在說:「水。我得喝水。」

column1: meThe beast looks a little weaker now, but still watches your every move with its cold stare.
column2: 那頭怪獸如今看來虛弱了些,卻仍以冷冷的眼神盯著你的一舉一動。

column1: He's wolfing that down pretty good, for a snake!
column2: 就一條蛇來說,牠這狼吞虎嚥的勁兒可真不賴!

column1: Get off your Saurus first.
column2: 先從索魯斯背上下來。

column1: You can water the beast until you're blue in the face, but it'll never grow again.
column2: 你就算給這頭怪獸餵水餵到臉發青,牠也再不會長大了。

column1: The beast greedily laps up the water while watching you with a hungry expression.
column2: 那頭怪獸貪婪地舔食著水,還用一副飢腸轆轆的神情盯著你。

column1: The beast greedily laps up the water.
column2: 那頭怪獸貪婪地舔食著水。

column1: By the dead-pan expression on the beast's face, you get the impression he doesn't want any, thank you all the same.
column2: 從那頭怪獸面無表情的模樣,你感覺得出牠並不想要,謝謝你的好意。

column1: The beast quickly swallows the dried meat and fruit, gagging a bit from lack of water.
column2: 那頭怪獸三兩下就把肉乾和果乾吞了下去,缺水嗆得牠有點作嘔。

column1: The beast quickly swallows the dried meat and fruit.
column2: 那頭怪獸三兩下就把肉乾和果乾吞了下去。

column1: The beast quickly swallows the pizza slice, gagging a bit from lack of water.
column2: 那頭怪獸三兩下就把那片披薩吞了下去,缺水嗆得牠有點作嘔。

column1: The beast quickly swallows the pizza slice.
column2: 那頭怪獸三兩下就把那片披薩吞了下去。

column1: Well, OK, but it probably won't help at this point.
column2: 唔,好吧,不過事到如今這大概也幫不上忙了。

column1: The beast laps greedily at the potion.
column2: 那頭怪獸貪婪地舔食著藥水。

column1: The potion doesn't seem to work, but the beast seems overjoyed with the liquid anyway.
column2: 那藥水看來沒起作用,不過那頭怪獸倒是對這液體欣喜若狂。

column1: Dismount from your Saurus first.
column2: 先從你的索魯斯背上下來。

column1: Whaddya want a bunch of dead fur fer?
column2: 你要一堆死毛幹啥?

column1: Whaddya want a bunch of fur fer?
column2: 你要一堆毛幹啥?

column1: You don't dare get any hair as long as the beast is staring at you.
column2: 只要那頭怪獸還盯著你,你就不敢去取牠的毛。

column1: You get some hair from the beast and mix it into the dispel potion.
column2: 你從那頭怪獸身上取了一些毛,摻進驅散藥水裡。

column1: It doesn't need it.
column2: 它不需要這個。

column1: I know not who you are, or why you saved me, but you are a fool if you expect to be rewarded for it.
column2: 我不知你是何人,也不知你為何救我,但你若指望因此得到報答,那你就是個傻瓜。

column1: I am Al Scurva, an apprentice sorcerer. My former master, Ad Avis, transformed me into that monster and left me to die.
column2: 我是阿爾·斯庫瓦,一名見習巫師。我從前的師父阿德·阿維斯把我變成了那頭怪物,丟下我等死。

column1: He will be most displeased that I am still alive.
column2: 他若知道我還活著,定會大為不悅。

column1: Do not think he will be unaware of your interference. He will be watching you from now on.
column2: 別以為他會察覺不到你的插手。從今往後,他都會盯著你。

column1: I will give you this warning: Do not go near Ad Avis unless you have some spell of protection or are very quick.
column2: 我給你這個警告:除非你身上備有某種防護咒法,或是身手夠快,否則別靠近阿德·阿維斯。

column1: If he manages to throw a spell at you, you will either be his willing servant or else be transformed into a creature most resembling your soul.
column2: 他一旦對你擲出咒法,你不是淪為他俯首聽命的奴僕,就是被變成一頭最肖似你靈魂的生物。

column1: There is some major ritual magic he has planned for when the moon arises between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth.
column2: 他正謀劃一場重大的儀式魔法,只待明月升起於天蠍之爪與巨龍之牙之間。

column1: If he succeeds in this it would be best not to be in this land. I certainly will not be.
column2: 他若得逞,你最好別待在這片土地上。我反正是絕不會留下的。

column1: Just remember, Ad Avis awaits you in Raseir.
column2: 只要記住一點:阿德·阿維斯正在拉希爾等著你。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the food and water.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的食物跟水。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the water.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的水。

column1: Oh, yeah, thanks for the food.
column2: 喔對,謝謝你的食物。

column1: The beast reacts in pain and fury.
column2: 那頭怪物又痛又怒,劇烈掙扎起來。

column1: The beast feels pain, no doubt, but its enchantment seems to prevent it from being killed by anything you are able to do.
column2: 那頭怪物確實會痛,沒錯,但牠身上的魔法似乎讓你用盡任何手段都殺不死牠。

column1: The cage door is stuck fast.
column2: 籠門卡得死死的。

column1: A starving monster dying of thirst in the desert is not the most honorable of gentlemen. Things aren't always as they seem, and there definitely seemed to be more to this thing.
column2: 一頭在沙漠裡又餓又渴、奄奄一息的怪物,可算不上什麼正人君子。事情往往不如表面那麼簡單,而這東西背後,顯然另有蹊蹺。

column1: Freeing a creature like this does not seem to have been a wise decision. Perhaps the fact that the beast is obviously starving and dying of thirst had something to do with its ill temper.
column2: 放出這種生物似乎不是什麼明智的決定。或許牠那副暴躁脾氣,跟牠明顯餓壞了、渴壞了脫不了關係。

column1: Trusting soul, weren't you?
column2: 還真是個容易相信別人的傻孩子,對吧?

column1: The magic on the cage door seems to be impervious to your spell from this angle. You might try casting the spell from in front of the cage (if you dare!).
column2: 從這個角度,籠門上的魔法似乎對你的法術毫無反應。你或許可以試著站到籠子正前方再施法(如果你有膽的話!)。

column1: Its death has freed it.
column2: 牠的死,反倒讓牠解脫了。

column1: Your Saurus isn't thrilled at the prospect of getting any closer to that cage.
column2: 你的索魯斯一點也不想再靠近那只籠子半步。

column1: You see an ex-beast. As you stare at it, you seem to hear a voice in your mind, saying, "I'm dead. Go away. Trust me, I'm dead. Aaaaahhhhhhh."
column2: 你看見一頭「前怪物」。當你盯著牠看時,腦中彷彿響起一個聲音:「我死了啦。走開。相信我,我真的死了。啊啊啊啊啊啊——」

column1: It stares at you with a cold, dead, gaze, much like your last blind date.
column2: 牠用一雙冰冷、毫無生氣的眼睛盯著你,活像你上回那場相親的對象。

column1: A closer look at the monster shows that it is fierce indeed, although obviously weakened by lack of food and water. It will not survive much longer.
column2: 湊近一看,這怪物確實兇猛,只是顯然因缺水缺糧而虛弱不堪。牠撐不了多久了。

column1: As you look into the eyes of the creature, you hear in your mind, "Come closer, much closer. Come closer and free me, release me. Open the cage and free me. Open the cage and trust me."
column2: 當你望進這生物的眼睛,腦中傳來低語:「靠近一點,再靠近一點。過來放我出去,放我自由。打開籠子,放了我。打開籠子,相信我。」

column1: FaYou see the carcass of the beast in its iron prison.
column2: 你看見那頭怪物的屍體,躺在牠的鐵牢之中。

column1: You see the carcass of the beast in its iron prison.
column2: 你看見那頭怪物的屍體,躺在牠的鐵牢之中。

column1: The desert sky is ablaze with stars.
column2: 沙漠的夜空被滿天繁星點得熾亮。

column1: The sun burns down.
column2: 烈日炙烤而下。

column1: The beast does not respond.
column2: 那頭怪物沒有反應。

column1: As the beast is dead, it does not respond.
column2: 那頭怪物已經死了,自然沒有反應。

column1: You should move closer to the front of the cage.
column2: 你應該再靠近籠子的正前方一點。

column1: You have no water to give the beast.
column2: 你沒有水可以給那頭怪物。

column1: The cage door is unlocked.
column2: 籠門沒有上鎖。

column1: The door to the cage is fastened shut, but there is no lock.
column2: 籠門被牢牢扣住,卻看不見任何鎖。

column1: It's dead enough already, thank you.
column2: 牠已經夠死的了,謝謝。

column1: You can't fight a caged beast. Your sword is hampered by the bars of the cage, and if you get too near, the beast could easily strike you with its tail.
column2: 你沒辦法跟籠中的怪物對打。鐵欄擋著你的劍施展不開,而且一旦靠得太近,牠的尾巴一甩就能輕鬆掃中你。

column1: You can't fight a caged beast. Your dagger is hampered by the bars of the cage, and if you get too near, the beast could easily strike you with its tail.
column2: 你沒辦法跟籠中的怪物對打。鐵欄擋著你的匕首施展不開,而且一旦靠得太近,牠的尾巴一甩就能輕鬆掃中你。

column1: Whew! Smooth talking, Dude. Ya got the gift for the gab, Tab.
column2: 唷!口才不錯嘛,老兄。你還真有張會說話的嘴,真是的。

column1: Tell ya what I'm going ta do. Seein' as you beein' a heero and all, I'll reduce the price to %d simoleons.
column2: 跟你說我打算怎麼做。看在你好歹是個大英雄的份上,我把價錢降到 %d 大洋。

column1: (Dinars, that is. Dough, Flo. Shapeir buck an ear.)
column2: (我是說第納爾啦。鈔票嘛,夏皮爾的銅板一串串。)

column1: Eenie, meenie, minie, moe. Here's the lowest I can go. %d dinars, yes or no? Eenie, meenie, minie, moe.
column2: 點兵點將,點到哪個是哪個。這是我能出的最低價了。%d 第納爾,要還是不要?點兵點將,點到你頭上囉。

column1: Naaaaaah, I guessed wrong. %d dinars it is, then.
column2: 唉呀——猜錯啦。那就 %d 第納爾,成交。

column1: If you want to do some dealing, then pick a better deck.
column2: 想做點買賣,就拿副像樣點的牌來。

column1: I laid my cards on the table, Grable, and if you're able, then ante up.
column2: 我已經把牌攤在桌上了,有本事你就掏錢下注吧。

column1: Pay or belay, Jay.
column2: 付錢,不然就閃邊去。

column1: So solly, Cholly. This money's funny.
column2: 真不好意思啊,這錢有問題喔。

column1: You need to get some dough from the Moneychanger, stranger.
column2: 你得先去找錢莊換點錢來才行,外鄉人。

column1: Now let me see where I last saw it. I'll have to look for it in the back room. I'll be back in a flash with the stash, Nash!
column2: 讓我想想上回放哪去了。我得進後頭那間屋子翻一翻。我一溜煙就把貨拿來,等著喔!

column1: Silly me. I had it here the whole time.
column2: 瞧我這記性。它一直都在這兒呢。

column1: Abracadabra and didgery-doo, make the thing appear for you!
column2: 阿布拉卡達布拉,嘰哩咕嚕,變個東西出來給你!

column1: Ah, I have that here, Dear.
column2: 啊,我這兒有,親愛的。

column1: Possible, probable, my blue hen, make it appear as I say when!
column2: 也許可能,八九不離十,我的小寶貝,聽我號令現身來!

column1: Eins, zwei, drei, vier,[make it snappy und bring it here!
column2: 一、二、三、四,[手腳俐落點,給我送過來!

column1: Nothing up my sleeve...
column2: 我袖子裡可什麼都沒藏喔……

column1: Presto, change-o,[Rearrange-o!
column2: 咻咻——變變變,[全給我換個位!

column1: Here you go, Joe. Hope you're happy, Pappy.
column2: 給你囉,老兄。希望你滿意啦,大爺。

column1: As you read the scroll, the knowledge is ingrained into your mind.
column2: 當你讀著卷軸,那份知識便深深烙進了你的腦海。

column1: As you read the scroll, you feel your understanding of the Force Bolt spell grow.
column2: 當你讀著卷軸,你感覺到自己對「力鏢」這道法術的理解又精進了一層。

column1: During combat, add some extra punch to your Force Bolts by holding down the spell's hotkey to charge them.
column2: 戰鬥時,按住法術熱鍵蓄力,就能讓你的力鏢威力更上一層。

column1: You put the glasses away.
column2: 你把眼鏡收了起來。

column1: You fill an empty pot with dirt from the jar.
column2: 你從罐子裡舀土,把空盆裝滿。

column1: You put the pot of dirt away.
column2: 你把那盆土收了起來。

column1: Oh, those old things? I could never get them to work correctly. But then again, I've never worn them.
column2: 喔,那些老古董啊?我從來就沒能讓它們好好運作過。不過話說回來,我也從沒真的戴過就是了。

column1: I hate to spoil the show, Joe, but me thinks it's time to go. Bon soir and au revoir.
column2: 真不想掃興啊,老兄,可我看時候差不多了。晚安,後會有期囉。

column1: Nharak sa'id! Aloha oe! Well come in! Gesundheit! Tip a canoe and how do you do?
column2: 祝你日安!阿囉哈!歡迎光臨!祝你健康!划划獨木舟,你好嗎你好嗎?

column1: Pleased to meet you and I mean to please you!
column2: 很高興認識你,我也存心要讓你滿意!

column1: Welcome to my little shop of wonders! Wonder what shop it is? It's magic, of course!
column2: 歡迎光臨我這間小小的奇妙店鋪!好奇這是什麼店?當然是賣魔法的囉!

column1: I am Keapon Laffin, proprietor. You must be Nobile Caws. Proud to know you, Mister Caws. Shapeir always hopes for heroes.
column2: 我是這店的老闆,奇朋·拉芬。你想必就是那位大名鼎鼎的考斯閣下吧。很榮幸認識你,考斯先生。夏皮爾向來盼著英雄到來。

column1: I see by your outfit that you know some magic.[You see by my outfit that I know some, too.[We can see by our outfits that we both know magic.[I guess that we two must eschew the hoodoo.
column2: 從你這身打扮,我看得出你也懂點魔法。[從我這身打扮,你也看得出我略懂一二。[從我倆這身打扮,我們都看得出彼此都會魔法。[看來咱們倆都得避開那些邪門歪道囉。

column1: Hello, sailor, new in town? So how ya doin', Bruin?
column2: 嗨,水手,剛到城裡來?最近過得怎樣啊,老兄?

column1: Had any WITty conversation? Been chanting with the Enchantress yet?
column2: 跟巫師技術學院(W.I.T.)的人聊過什麼有「智」之言沒?跟那位女術士一起唸過咒沒?

column1: Seen all the sights, Justin, or just citing all the scenes?
column2: 景點都逛遍了沒啊,還是光在那兒紙上談兵?

column1: Sell you a spell, Nell?
column2: 賣你道法術如何呀,小妞?

column1: It's sad, Dad, but if you need to climb to the sublime, you need a rope, Dope.
column2: 說來遺憾啊老兄,可你要是想往那高處攀,就少不了一條繩子,傻小子。

column1: I happen to have a magic rope for sale, direct from some Hindoo who did the best he kin do.
column2: 我這兒剛好有條魔法繩要賣,直接從某個印度老兄那兒進的貨,人家可是使出渾身解數才做出來的。

column1: Heard the one about the fire, sire? It's a torrid story.
column2: 聽過那則關於火的故事沒,大爺?那可是個火辣辣的故事。

column1: The Fire Elemental had a seething passion for a sultry old flame who burned him once.
column2: 火元素曾對一位風情萬種的老情人燃起熊熊烈愛,只可惜當年被人家狠狠燙傷過一回。

column1: Seems his spark was worse than his spite. There ardor be a law.
column2: 看來他那把火比他的怨氣還旺。這份熱情啊,真該立法管管。

column1: At least there will be a hot time in the old town tonight.
column2: 至少今晚這老城裡,鐵定要熱鬧上一場。

column1: Water it be today, matey?
column2: 今天「水」事如意嗎,夥計?

column1: How's the Chinook, Schnook?
column2: 那陣熱風吹得怎樣啊,傻小子?

column1: I heard the gusty Air Elemental has a tempestuous relationship with his own twister.
column2: 我聽說那陣風大的氣元素,跟他自己那道龍捲風鬧得正轟轟烈烈呢。

column1: They had a whirlwind affair, but it's winding down, if you catch my drift.
column2: 他倆談了場旋風般的戀愛,不過眼下風頭正漸漸消下去,你聽得懂我的弦外之音吧?

column1: They say an Earthy Elemental is aground, but he just wants to be left a loam.
column2: 人家說有個土元素正擱淺在這兒,可他只想被晾在一旁、安安靜靜當塊好土。

column1: What a clod. He's not very gneiss.
column2: 真是個土包子。對人一點也不「片麻」客氣。

column1: This town's sure going to rock tonight, but I suppose you prefer old Terra Firma (the more firmer, the less terror).
column2: 今晚這城肯定要被搖個天翻地覆,不過我猜你還是偏愛腳踏實地的老大地(地越穩,人越不慌)。

column1: Did you sea the swell Elemental that's made such a splash in the fountain?
column2: 你「瞧」見那位在噴泉裡掀起好大水花的水元素了沒?

column1: She's awfully soppy and tends to gush, but I tell you, Eddy, and I don't mean to deluge you, there's an undercurrent of still water running steep.
column2: 她濕答答的,還動不動就嘩嘩傾瀉,不過我跟你說啊,我可不是想拿話淹你,這底下可有一股深沉的暗流靜靜湧著呢。

column1: If I were you, I'd rain away.
column2: 要是我啊,我可就溜之大「雨」嘍。

column1: Looks like you ran out of luck, Chuck. Wanna snatch a rematch?
column2: 看來你手氣用光啦,老兄。想不想再來一局扳回一城?

column1: Two thumbs up, Chup! Here's a little reward to show I'm a good sport.
column2: 給你比兩個讚!這點小獎賞收下,證明我可是個輸得起的好對手。

column1: You feel your magical powers being completely restored.
column2: 你感覺自己的魔力完全恢復了。

column1: Clip, clop to the Magic Shop to see the owner flip his flop. I sell spells, can't you tell?
column2: 噠噠噠,快來魔法店,瞧瞧老闆耍寶翻跟斗。我賣法術,你看不出來嗎?

column1: Wonder of wonders and will wonders never cease? You'll wonder where the yellow went, and whether geese have fleece.
column2: 奇上加奇,奇事是不是沒完沒了?你會納悶那抹黃跑哪去了,還會納悶鵝身上到底長不長毛。

column1: magic shop owner
column2: 魔法店老闆

column1: shop owner
column2: 店老闆

column1: Keapon Laffin is my name. Ask me again and I'll tell you the same.
column2: 我叫奇朋·拉芬。再問我一遍,答案還是一樣。

column1: Do I know Yorick? He's my cousin, Laspo. Ah, Laspo Yorick, I knew him, Hero.
column2: 我認不認識約里克?他是我表親啦,叫拉斯波。啊,拉斯波·約里克,我認識他,英雄。

column1: I'll sell you a scroll of Force Bolt for only %d dinars.
column2: 「力鏢」卷軸賣你,只要 %d 第納爾。

column1: Detect will select what you suspect but can't inspect. Expect the effect to wreck the protection by creating a defect in its perfect reflection.
column2: 「偵測」能替你挑出那些你疑神疑鬼卻又查不出的東西。瞧好這效果如何破解防護——只消在那完美的倒影上戳出個破綻。

column1: I'll sell you a scroll of Detect Magic for only %d dinars.
column2: 「偵測魔法」卷軸賣你,只要 %d 第納爾。

column1: The Trigger spell is like a witch's switch with a hitch, Rich. It makes the last magic cast in the past happen fast. Not only that, Brat, but Trigger makes you hoarse.
column2: 「觸發」這法術啊,就像女巫手裡帶機關的開關,老兄。它能讓你方才施過的最後一道法術瞬間再現。不止如此呢,小鬼,「觸發」還會讓你嗓子發啞喔。

column1: I'll sell you a scroll of Trigger for only %d dinars.
column2: 「觸發」卷軸賣你,只要 %d 第納爾。

column1: It's got its ups and downs, but if you need a lift, this will get your feet up off the ground.
column2: 它有起有落啦,可你要是需要往上飄一飄,這玩意兒能讓你雙腳離地。

column1: I'll sell you a scroll of Levitate for only %d dinars.
column2: 「漂浮」卷軸賣你,只要 %d 第納爾。

column1: A shining example of a scintillating spell. It brings a tear to the eyes of the other guys who spy it.
column2: 這可是耀眼法術裡的閃亮典範。誰瞄上一眼,都要被閃得熱淚盈眶。

column1: I'll sell you a scroll of Dazzle for only %d dinars.
column2: 「炫目」卷軸賣你,只要 %d 第納爾。

column1: Whose bed am I in? What kind of personal question is that? Oh, Bedouin! He's a desert drifter who shifts with sifting sands.
column2: 我睡在誰的床上?這算哪門子私人問題啊?喔——你是說貝都因人(Bedouin)!那是隨著流沙四處飄蕩的沙漠遊子啦。

column1: Come here, oh stranger, and save us from danger. It's a dirty job, and I'm glad you're here to do it, Pruitt. People say you're pretty neat, Pete.
column2: 過來吧,陌生的外鄉人,救我們脫離險境。這是樁苦差事,真高興有你來扛,老兄。大夥兒都說你挺了不起的呢。

column1: You have a dearth of earth? That's worth my mirth. I do have some Fooler's Earth in stock, though.
column2: 你缺土啊?這倒值得我樂一樂。不過嘛,我店裡確實還有點「障眼土」的存貨。

column1: My information is muddy, Buddy.
column2: 我這消息渾得很喔,老兄。

column1: Thanks for the whirl, Shirl. I don't need more beard, Leard.
column2: 謝謝你陪我轉了一圈啦。我可不需要更多鬍子囉。

column1: It is der wish for the whirl of a Dervish. I will pay 1500 centimes (in dinars, of course) for such a thing. All you have to do is find it.
column2: 我心心念念想要苦行僧那旋轉的本事。為了這玩意兒,我願意付一千五百生丁(當然,折成第納爾算)。你只要去把它找來就成。

column1: Noticed my note, mister? It is der wish for the whirl of a Dervish. I will pay 1500 centimes (in dinars, of course) for such a thing. All you have to do is find it.
column2: 瞧見我那張告示了沒,先生?我心心念念想要苦行僧那旋轉的本事。為了這玩意兒,我願意付一千五百生丁(當然,折成第納爾算)。你只要去把它找來就成。

column1: If you're low on cash, Nash, check my notice at the Guild Hall, Paul.
column2: 要是手頭緊啊,老兄,去公會大廳瞧瞧我貼的那張告示吧。

column1: A challenge to the wits and mind. A game truly one-of-a-kind. If you're willing and in no hurry, let's have a game of Force Bolt Flurry.
column2: 一場考驗腦力與機智的較量,一個獨一無二的遊戲。要是你有意又不趕時間,咱們就來玩一局「力鏢連發」吧。

column1: Erasmus is a wizard I respect. He can out-pun almost anyone and he jests with the best. Between Erasmus and Fenrus, it's hard to get a joke in edgewise. Fenrus is an aristo-rat at that, too. Their humor is no rumor.
column2: 伊拉斯謨是位我敬重的巫師。論玩雙關他幾乎打遍天下無敵手,說笑也是箇中高手。在伊拉斯謨跟芬魯斯兩個之間,你連插句笑話的空檔都搶不到。芬魯斯那傢伙啊,還是隻貴族「鼠」呢。他倆的幽默可不是空穴來風。

column1: Hard to learn a spell unless you have a scroll. Once it's read it's in your head, Fred.
column2: 沒卷軸想學法術可難嘍。讀過一遍,它就進你腦袋裡啦,老兄。

column1: Behold the beast of burden: it travels in the herd, then it frequently is heard when a Saurus is absurd, men.
column2: 瞧瞧這馱獸:成群結隊地走,等到索魯斯耍起蠢來,那動靜可就常常傳得老遠囉,各位。

column1: Sorry, Charlie. I don't have a scroll for that wizard spell. It tends to disappear fast.
column2: 抱歉啦,老兄。那道巫師法術的卷軸我可沒有。那玩意兒一向消失得飛快。

column1: A magic user went to school so he could learn the magic rule. And when he learned what he had heard, he had become a great wizard. (He went to WIT, of course.)
column2: 有個法師上學去,就為了學會那魔法的門道。等他把聽來的本事都學到手,他就成了位了不起的巫師。(他上的當然是巫師技術學院(W.I.T.)囉。)

column1: But I see you already have some dirt, Kurt.
column2: 不過我看你身上已經有點土了呀,老兄。

column1: I fear you can't procure those here, Dear.
column2: 恐怕這些東西你在我這兒買不到喔,親愛的。

column1: Oh Cod, it makes me Eel when people Carp and Whale just for the Halibut, with no Porpoise. I'm Net that kind of Buoy, Sea? I'm Shore tired of Gills in School with their Tails of Roe.
column2: 老天「鱈」,人家一沒「鯨」由就在那兒又「鯉」怨又「鯢」嚎,真叫我渾身「鰻」不自在。我可「鮃」不是那種人,「魚」道嗎?我「鯊」是受夠了那些成天滿口「鱈」話的傢伙了。

column1: (Coral, Bob, and Brine made me say this. Tanks for rising to the bait and falling for it, hook, line, and sinker!)
column2: (都是珊瑚、鮑伯跟那一缸鹽水逼我說的啦。多「漁」你上鉤咬餌,還連魚鉤、魚線、鉛墜整套吞下肚!)

column1: (We're REALLY fishing here, folks.)
column2: (各位,我們這可是「真的」在釣魚喔。)

column1: Air, air, it's everywhere. Way down here, and way up there. In your bed and in my head and going anywhere it's led, Dunderhead.
column2: 空氣啊空氣,無所不在。低低在這兒,高高在那兒。在你的床上,在我的腦袋裡,被牽到哪兒就飄到哪兒,傻瓜。

column1: Shapeir is a city, small and quaint and pretty. Shapeir is a land, large and great and grand.
column2: 夏皮爾是座城,小巧、古樸又秀麗。夏皮爾是片地,遼闊、偉大又壯麗。

column1: Raseir, I fear, is very queer, and if you do not interfere, I think that it will disappear.
column2: 拉希爾啊,我怕得很,那地方詭異透頂,你要是再不出手干預,我看它遲早要憑空消失。

column1: Djinni, Genie, Jeannie, Djinn; the desert's where they all begin. Djinni, Ifreet, Marid, Djann; magic's where they all began.
column2: 神燈精靈、巨靈、燈神、鎮尼,沙漠便是他們的起點。鎮尼、伊夫利特、馬里德、賈安,魔法便是他們的源頭。

column1: The Fire Elemental has a weakness for water. He lives in the fast lane, so you'll have to get him restrained if you want to make him remain, so that you can get him contained. To get the flame to smother, don't ask me, go ask another.
column2: 火元素的弱點就是水。他過的是快節奏的日子,所以你想讓他乖乖待著,就得先把他制住,這樣才好把他關起來。想讓那火苗熄滅嘛,可別問我,去問別人吧。

column1: Ahoy, me boy, and shiver me timbers. Ya got what it takes to make a lady less limber. Pieces of eight, mate, and blow the maid down, sweep her off her feet and then clear out of town.
column2: 喂,小子,我的老天爺啊。你還真有本事讓一位姑娘渾身發軟。金幣一塊塊,夥計,把那姑娘哄得神魂顛倒,讓她拜倒在你腳下,然後你再溜出城去。

column1: If you're led to behead an airhead, don't let him lead the chase. Glue his shoes with goo, Blue, and throw mud in his face. It's fair to snare the air there, then put him in his place.
column2: 要是你被指派去砍掉某個風一般的呆頭,可別讓他帶著你滿場跑。拿黏糊糊的膠把他鞋子黏死,再朝他臉上甩泥巴。在那兒設陷阱困住那陣風,合情合理,然後好好教訓他一頓。

column1: There is no better way to arouse a rock's ire than to fight with a light and to smite him with fire.
column2: 要惹毛一塊石頭,再沒有比點起火光、用火痛擊他更管用的法子了。

column1: Matter monster, made by man. Chaos tossed across the land. Catch them, grab them if you can. Then they're there at your command.
column2: 物質怪物,人造之物。混亂被拋灑滿地。逮住牠們、抓住牠們,只要你抓得住。然後牠們就乖乖聽你號令了。

column1: Griffin, Jackal, Terrorsaurus, Ghoul; learn their ways in Adventurer's School. Brigand, Djinni, Scorpion, all; ask for info at the Guild Hall.
column2: 獅鷲、豺狼、恐懼龍、食屍鬼,牠們的門道都在冒險者學校裡學。強盜、鎮尼、蠍子,通通去公會大廳打聽消息。

column1: 'The Sultan sits upon this throne; I ponder if he's ever home? The Sultan sits inside his palace. Is he there? Well, go ask Alice.' (If you can find her -- last we met she had a chat with a cat and chatter with the Hatter. It's a wonder, man.)
column2: 「蘇丹端坐在這寶座上;我尋思他可曾回過家?蘇丹端坐在他的宮殿裡。他在不在裡頭?嗯,去問愛麗絲吧。」(前提是你找得到她——上回碰面,她正跟一隻貓閒聊,又跟那帽匠瞎扯。真是奇妙啊,老兄。)

column1: The Emir of Raseir did disappear a year ago.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾,一年前確實憑空消失了。

column1: If you want to be WITty, then search in the city. The nitty-gritty to this ditty: you need magic to find it, 'twas designed by committee.
column2: 你要是想沾上巫師技術學院(W.I.T.)的「智」氣,那就到城裡頭去找吧。這支小曲兒的關鍵就在:你得有魔法才找得到它,畢竟那是一群人合議設計出來的。

column1: You have to be a hero if you want to play the palace. The Sultan is selective about whom he will see.
column2: 你想進那宮殿可得是個英雄才行。蘇丹對見什麼人可是挑得很。

column1: It's sand, man. Hot, Scott. Dry, Guy.
column2: 就是沙啊,老兄。又燙,又乾,沒別的。

column1: The din of the bazaar is filled with the dinar. If you want to pay a cent, Dean, you have to have a centime.
column2: 市集裡那喧鬧聲,滿是第納爾的銅臭味。你要是想付個一分錢,老兄,那你手裡就得有生丁才行。

column1: The Plaza of the Fighters is west of here, Greer. There you'll find the Hall, Paul.
column2: 戰士廣場就在西邊呀朋友,公會大廳就在那頭。

column1: The Kattas trail into the Katta's Tail Inn, he says cattily, as he goes from the feline to the ridiculous.
column2: 卡塔族都拖著尾巴溜進「貓尾客棧」啦——他賊頭賊腦地說,從貓樣一路扯到沒個正經。

column1: In the summer, in the desert, it is hot 'til day is done. Only mad dogs and hero men go out in the noonday sun.
column2: 盛夏的沙漠,熱到日落都不歇。也只有瘋狗跟英雄,敢頂著正午的大太陽出門。

column1: The center of the city, Mitty. The water for the town, Clown.
column2: 城的正中央哪,小傻瓜。打水的地方哪,小笨蛋。

column1: Don't quote me, but Aziza has a waterbed and fiddles with fish.
column2: 別說是我講的喔——聽說阿姿莎睡水床,還愛擺弄她那些魚。

column1: There was a Moneychanger and her name was Dinarzad. It's understood that she was goood and very, very baad. You can find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, my lad.
column2: 從前有個換錢的,芳名叫蒂娜薩。人說她又好~~又壞~~壞得不像話。小子啊,她就在第納爾巷的盡頭那兒哪。

column1: Do not hurry. Do not wait. The Astrologer will tell your fate. He is not near. He is not far. He's at the end of Tarik of Stars.
column2: 別著急,別空等,占星師會說你的命。他不近,也不遠,就在星辰巷的最那端。

column1: Auntie Azizie lives off the east end of Shmali Tarik. Mind your p's and q's when you are there, or she might send you you-know-where.
column2: 阿姿莎阿姨住在什馬里巷的東頭外。去那兒可得規規矩矩,不然她一不高興,就把你送去你知道的那地方囉。

column1: He's not a bad poet, but don't let him know it. It might go from bad to verse.
column2: 他這詩人還不賴啦,可別讓他知道——不然只會從糟糕變成「更詩」糟糕。

column1: I roll scrolls and sell spells.
column2: 我捲卷軸,也賣法術。

column1: Purrfect purrsons.
column2: 喵~完美的客人哪。

column1: The Dervish of the Desert oversees his own oasis as he sits upon the sands by the shore of his small spring. It is somewhere south of Shapeir and in inches a bit east; if you're going to go and gab with him you'd better take a beast.
column2: 沙漠的苦行僧守著他自己的綠洲,就坐在小泉邊的沙地上。那地方在夏皮爾的南邊,再往東挪一丁點;你要去跟他攀談,最好騎頭坐騎才划算。

column1: If you want to halt a bad guy and make the dude relax, you've got to cast a Calm spell to stop him in his tracks.
column2: 想叫壞蛋停手、乖乖放鬆下來,你就得施個「鎮靜術」,叫他當場動彈不開。

column1: Too hot to handle, the Force Bolt's my candle.
column2: 那玩意兒燙手得很——「衝擊彈」才是我的小蠟燭。

column1: Fresh out of stock on that one, although I use it myself.
column2: 那個剛好缺貨啦,雖說我自己也常用呢。

column1: Zap's for saps. Force is the source. Besides, I don't carry that one.
column2: 電擊是給呆瓜玩的,衝擊才是正道。再說了,那個我也沒進貨。

column1: Turnabout's fair play, that's what I always say. But the last time I saw a Reversal was...[well, I don't give out my age and neither does anyone else.
column2: 禮尚往來嘛,我老這麼說。可上回我見著「逆轉術」是在……[嗯,我不說自己幾歲,別人也都不說。

column1: Go find a fellow who'll loan you his bellows. And if you can't play, just take them away, Jay.
column2: 去找個願意借你風箱的傢伙吧。要是借不來,那就直接拿走,小老弟。

column1: What are you trying to catch, Patch?
column2: 你到底想抓啥玩意兒呀,小補丁?

column1: You do? Do what? How do you do the hoodoo that you do so well?
column2: 是喔?做啥?你那一手把戲到底是怎麼變的呀,變得這麼順手?

column1: You have my last pair there, Claire, and I haven't got a spare.
column2: 那是我最後一副囉,親愛的,我可沒備用的了。

column1: Well, we can bargain all you like, Mike, but that won't change the fact that you already bought the only pair I had.
column2: 好啦,你愛怎麼殺價都行,可這也改變不了——我那唯一一副早被你買走啦。

column1: Say, Jay, what the hey? The rope you've got is one and the same.
column2: 欸,搞啥呀?你手上那條繩,跟這條根本是同一條啊。

column1: What do I look like, a department store? I fear you can't procure that here, dear.
column2: 你當我這兒是百貨公司啊?那玩意兒這裡可弄不到喔,親愛的。

column1: My knowledge is scanty, Auntie.
column2: 我懂的可不多哪,大姊。

column1: If you need to know the answer, then ask another, Prancer.
column2: 要想知道答案哪,你就去問問別人吧,小蹦跳。

column1: I told you before, Igor. Forget already, Freddy?
column2: 我早跟你說過啦。這就忘啦,健忘鬼?

column1: You thank Keapon for sharing his Fooler's Earth with you and promise to use it well.
column2: 你向奇朋道謝,謝他分你「迷障塵土」,並保證會好好用上它。

column1: Give Mister Twister a nice, long dirt nap by using my gift to set up the trap.
column2: 拿我送的這份大禮設個陷阱,好好讓那位「旋風先生」長眠在塵土裡吧。

column1: You thank Keapon and tell him about your victory over the Air Elemental.
column2: 你向奇朋道謝,跟他說了你擊敗風之元素的經過。

column1: There once was a hero whose worth,[Relied upon good Fooler's Earth.[Caught the Air Elemental but got sentimental,[When thanking a gnome filled with mirth.
column2: 從前有位英雄真有本事,[全靠那好用的迷障塵土。[捉了風之元素卻一時心軟,[對著樂呵呵的地精連聲道謝。

column1: So you left the ogress's mess for someone else to contest? Well, at least you tried your best, Celeste.
column2: 所以你把那食人母怪的爛攤子留給別人去收拾啦?嗯,至少你盡力了嘛,親愛的。

column1: Good to hear you drove the curser from the land, Stan.
column2: 聽說你把那下咒的傢伙趕出這片土地,真是好消息哪。

column1: You tell Keapon about WIT and your decision to reject their proposal.
column2: 你跟奇朋說了 W.I.T.的事,還有你決定回絕他們的邀約。

column1: WIT's wizards think WIT's it-and-a-bit, Kit. Turn them down and they'll think you're a clown, but you made the right choice, Joyce.
column2: W.I.T.那幫巫師自以為了不起得很哪。回絕他們,他們會當你是個小丑,可你這選擇做對了喔,親愛的。

column1: You tell about how you extinguished the Fire Elemental.
column2: 你說了你是怎麼撲滅火之元素的。

column1: I heard it was you who maimed the flames, James.
column2: 聽說啊,把那火苗整治得服服貼貼的就是你呀,小子。

column1: You tell about the Fire Elemental running rampant in the Gate Plaza.
column2: 你說了火之元素在城門廣場到處肆虐的事。

column1: A good firefighter can make his task lighter by using water to smite 'er.
column2: 好的滅火高手哪,懂得拿水去澆,活兒就輕鬆多啦。

column1: You don't say? And I sure won't.
column2: 真的假的?反正我是不會說出去的啦。

column1: You tell about how you sucked in the Air Elemental.
column2: 你說了你是怎麼把風之元素吸進去的。

column1: The Hero decrees that he seized the breeze with ease.
column2: 英雄宣告:這陣風,我手到擒來,輕輕鬆鬆。

column1: You tell about the Air Elemental blowing around near the Palace.
column2: 你說了風之元素在宮殿附近到處亂吹的事。

column1: Twisting, turning, running amok, to catch it will take more than luck.
column2: 又轉又繞,四處撒野——想逮住它,光靠運氣可不夠喔。

column1: You tell about how you made the Earth Elemental bite the dust.
column2: 你說了你是怎麼把土之元素打得趴在地上吃土的。

column1: Don't let your ego go sour and cocky after going the distance and beating old Rocky.
column2: 撐到最後、把那硬石頭給打趴了,可別就此自滿、囂張起來囉。

column1: You tell about the Earth Elemental laying waste to the city.
column2: 你說了土之元素把城裡夷為廢墟的事。

column1: You tell about how you blew the Water Elemental away.
column2: 你說了你是怎麼把水之元素一舉吹散的。

column1: I don't mean to sound soppy, Poppy, but you did swell.
column2: 我不是要肉麻啦,小寶貝——可你真是幹得漂亮哪。

column1: You tell about the Water Elemental occupying the fountain.
column2: 你說了水之元素霸佔噴泉的事。

column1: A tree-mendous triumph.
column2: 真是一場「樹」立大功的勝仗。

column1: You tell Keapon that the Saurus you used to ride around on is actually the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你跟奇朋說,你一路騎著的那頭索魯斯,其實是中了魔法的埃米爾。

column1: Restore Emir Arus to his rightful form by seeking the city where things aren't the norm.
column2: 想讓埃米爾阿魯斯恢復原形,就去找那座一切都不對勁的城吧。

column1: You tell about how you met the paladin Rakeesh.
column2: 你說了你是怎麼遇上聖騎士拉基什的。

column1: He came to Shapeir for the mane event, and I'm not lion.
column2: 他可是為了這場重頭戲才大老遠來夏皮爾的,我可沒在跟你「獅」混。

column1: Ah, Aziza, Aziza, Aziza. What does it take to please, her?
column2: 唉,阿姿莎、阿姿莎、阿姿莎呀。到底要怎樣才能討她歡心哪?

column1: You looks awfully familiar and familiarly awful.
column2: 你瞧著怪眼熟的,也眼熟得怪嚇人的。

column1: Haven't you been here before?
column2: 你以前是不是來過這兒呀?

column1: It sure LOOKED real.
column2: 它看起來可是真得很哪。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Ghost Writers in the Sky."
column2: 架上的書好像有好多種文字,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過倒是認得幾本老相識,比方說《天上的影子寫手》。

column2: 架上的書好像有好多種文字,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過倒是認得幾本老相識,比方說《是風皆有錯》。

column2: 架上的書好像有好多種文字,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過倒是認得幾本老相識,比方說《三人成雲》。

column1: idIt's a Flippy Flier Model XLR8 Frisbee.
column2: 那是一只「翻飛者 XLR8 型」飛盤。

column1: Don't watch this so much. You'll ruin your eyes!
column2: 別老盯著這個看啦,會把眼睛看壞的!

column1: You can't open the cupboards.
column2: 你打不開這些櫥櫃。

column1: You can't take the balloons.
column2: 你拿不走這些氣球。

column1: You can't get the pinwheels.
column2: 你拿不到那些紙風車。

column1: Don't touch them. You'll get fingerprints all over them.
column2: 別碰它們,你會弄得上頭全是指紋。

column1: You can't get the fan.
column2: 你拿不到那把扇子。

column1: You can't get the steins.
column2: 你拿不到那些啤酒杯。

column1: You can't get the waxen Icarus wings (maybe in a sequel).
column2: 你拿不到那對蠟做的伊卡洛斯翅膀(或許下一集吧)。

column1: You can't reach this shelf.
column2: 你搆不著這層架子。

column1: You can't get the books.
column2: 你拿不到那些書。

column1: You can't get the rubber chickens.
column2: 你拿不到那些橡皮雞。

column1: You can't get the toaster.
column2: 你拿不到那台烤麵包機。

column1: You can't get the USS Exitprise.
column2: 你拿不到那艘「出擊號」星艦。

column1: You can't get that.
column2: 那個你拿不到。

column1: You can't get the baby antwerps.
column2: 你拿不到那些小安特沃普寶寶。

column1: You can't get the Orat-On-A-Stick.
column2: 你拿不到那串「歐拉特肉串」。

column1: You can't get the octopus-like thing with wings.
column2: 你拿不到那個長翅膀、像章魚的玩意兒。

column1: You can't get the puppets.
column2: 你拿不到那些布偶。

column1: You can't get the orbs.
column2: 你拿不到那些寶珠。

column1: You can't get the Doughbury Pills Boy.
column2: 你拿不到那個「麵糰寶寶」。

column1: You can't get the green box.
column2: 你拿不到那個綠盒子。

column1: You can't get the top hats.
column2: 你拿不到那些高禮帽。

column1: You can't get the Commando Cody Flying Suit.
column2: 你拿不到那套「飛天突擊隊」飛行服。

column1: You can't get the Frisbee.
column2: 你拿不到那只飛盤。

column1: You can't get the whoopee cushions.
column2: 你拿不到那些放屁坐墊。

column1: You can't get the TV.
column2: 你拿不到那台電視。

column1: You can't get them.
column2: 那些你拿不到。

column1: You can't get that thing (whatever it is).
column2: 那玩意兒你拿不到(管它是啥)。

column1: SOME people cast shadows.
column2: 有些人哪,是會投下影子的喔。

column1: The tag says "Ali Fakir Genuine X-Ray Glasses -- %d Dinars." They look incredibly large, but the tag also says "One size fits all." It must be magic.
column2: 標籤上寫著「阿里·法奇正牌透視眼鏡——%d 第納爾」。看起來大得誇張,不過標籤上又寫著「一種尺寸,人人合戴」。這肯定是魔法做的。

column1: knowIf you need to know some knowledge about the magic way, then go and see Aziza who'll enchant you if you stay.
column2: 想多懂點魔法之道的學問哪,那就去找阿姿莎吧——你肯留下,她准把你迷得團團轉。

column1: Watch out, she's testy, though.
column2: 不過可得小心,她脾氣挺火爆的。

column1: So long, fair well, Oaf, we da same!
column2: 再會啦,珍重囉,大笨蛋,咱倆是一夥的!

column1: Goodbye! See you real soon, Effendi Caws I like you.
column2: 再見啦!很快再見囉,閣下——「鴉」為我喜歡你嘛。

column1: I don't mean to bluster, buster, but if you're in a hurry, Cain, you need to get down to earth before you blow up.
column2: 我不是要嚇唬你啦,老兄,可你要是趕時間哪,得先腳踏實地,才不會把自己給炸了。

column1: Then if you bellow, fellow, the breeze is sure to freeze.
column2: 那麼,只要你鼓動風箱,小老弟,那陣風保準給你凍住。

column1: The Dervish of the desert sent a Bedouin to my boudoir, and I hear he needs a hero to help him bait a beast.
column2: 沙漠的苦行僧差了個貝都因人到我閨房來,聽說他需要個英雄,幫他去誘捕一頭野獸哪。

column1: You have to watch the old codger -- he's being cagey, but Dervish is my command.
column2: 你可得提防那老頭兒——他賊精賊精的,不過苦行僧的事,包在我身上。

column1: Arrrrrhhh, me bucko, if you be thirstin' to sponge off the maritime maiden, you'll need to air out a few things first, or you'll be seein' the waters pout, arrrrrhhh!
column2: 呀啊啊——小子哎,你要是渴著想揩那「海上姑娘」的油哪,得先把幾樁事透透氣,不然你就等著瞧那水鬧脾氣吧,呀啊啊!

column1: Speaking of finals, Lionel, so you're graduating to Raseir.
column2: 說到收尾哪,老兄,看來你要「畢業」前往拉希爾囉。

column1: Beware, Larry, I hear it's hairy there, so watch your derriere.
column2: 當心哪,老兄,聽說那地方險得很,你可得看好你的小屁股。

column1: Happy trails to you, until we meet again.
column2: 一路順風囉,咱們後會有期。

column1: Buy those banditos! Good luck, Buck!
column2: 去收拾那些土匪吧!祝你好運囉,老弟!

column1: It's a cloud/table/tray/catch-all/watchamacallit.
column2: 那是朵雲/張桌子/個托盤/雜物盤/那啥玩意兒。

column1: There's no point in luring the Elemental into an even bigger open space, so you discard the incense.
column2: 把元素引到更大的空地上根本沒意義,於是你把線香扔了。

column1: Being fired really burns you up, doesn't it? Welcome to our exclusive, embers-only club.[[[
column2: 被「火」上身,真夠你受的吧?歡迎加入我們這個尊榮專屬、僅限灰燼成員的俱樂部。[[[

column1: All your joints feel inflamed...
column2: 你渾身關節都像著了火似的……

column1: Man, that's hot!
column2: 天哪,燙死了!

column1: Watch out!
column2: 當心!

column1: With the Fire Elemental on the loose, you don't want to keep the door open longer than necessary. Better get closer first.
column2: 火之元素還在外頭亂竄,這門可不能多開一刻。還是先靠近點再說吧。

column1: It would be prudent not to do that while you're overloaded.
column2: 你這會兒負重過頭,還是別這麼幹比較明智。

column1: You are currently leaving a trail of incense. I wouldn't stand in one spot if I were you.
column2: 你這會兒正一路撒著線香呢。換作是我,可不會傻站在原地不動。

column1: Since you don't want the Elemental to follow you inside, you discard the incense.
column2: 你可不想讓元素精靈跟著你進屋,於是把焚香丟了。

column1: You should get closer first.
column2: 你得先靠近一點。

column1: Not in broad daylight.
column2: 大白天可不行。

column1: It's a Fire Elemental dropping.
column2: 那是火元素精靈掉落的東西。

column1: Keep that up and you'll burn your hand.
column2: 再這樣下去,你的手會被燙傷。

column1: You're better off trying to douse the source.
column2: 你最好設法澆熄源頭。

column1: This isn't the flame you're after.
column2: 這不是你要找的那團火焰。

column1: The Katta's Tail Inn sign is aflame!
column2: 卡塔之尾客棧的招牌燒起來了!

column1: The well-kept plazas of Shapeir are octagonal in shape. Windows overlook the daily goings-on, and alleys lead outward toward other parts of the city.
column2: 夏皮爾的廣場整理得井然有序,呈八角形。窗戶俯瞰著每日的市井百態,巷弄向外延伸,通往城市的其他角落。

column1: As you pick up the lamp and rub it, a small flame appears. When you rub it again, the flame goes away. You now have a magic lamp. You put the lamp safely away in your backpack.
column2: 你撿起油燈擦了擦,一小簇火焰冒了出來。再擦一次,火焰便消失了。你得到了一盞神燈。你把油燈妥善地收進背包裡。

column1: Without any means of containment, the Fire Elemental makes a hasty retreat back into the plaza.
column2: 由於沒有任何能收容它的器具,火元素精靈急忙退回了廣場。

column1: Being fired really burns you up, doesn't it? Welcome to our exclusive, embers-only club.
column2: 被火烤可真讓你「火」大,對吧?歡迎加入我們這個只收灰燼成員的尊榮俱樂部。

column1: You barely manage to escape the Fire Elemental's searing heat.
column2: 你勉強逃過了火元素精靈灼人的高熱。

column1: You should lead the Elemental farther into the alley.
column2: 你應該把元素精靈再往巷子深處引。

column1: Gate Plaza
column2: 城門廣場

column1: Plaza of the Fighters
column2: 戰士廣場

column1: Fountain Plaza
column2: 噴泉廣場

column1: Jamal Darb
column2: 賈邁勒街

column1: You can't go back into the plaza now, Hero. You must deal with the Fire Elemental while you have it cornered.
column2: 英雄,你現在不能退回廣場。趁著把火元素精靈逼到角落,你得趕緊解決它。

column1: You have lured the Fire Elemental away from the plaza. Now you must try to weaken and capture it.
column2: 你已經把火元素精靈引離了廣場。現在你得設法削弱並捕獲它。

column1: You pick up the brass lamp from the ground.
column2: 你從地上撿起那盞黃銅油燈。

column1: The lamp is made of brass.
column2: 這盞油燈是黃銅製的。

column1: You got some useful lock-picking practice, but the door has been bolted from the inside. No amount of skill will allow you to open it.
column2: 你練了一手不錯的開鎖技巧,可惜這道門從裡頭閂死了。再高明的技術也別想打開它。

column1: You can't use that here.
column2: 你不能在這裡用那個。

column1: You won't need more than one lamp.
column2: 你用不著超過一盞油燈。

column1: You drop the lamp. It is very hurt and angry. "I have a new flame now," you say to the lamp, "and she's the light of my life. You couldn't hold a candle to her." But inside your heart burns with the loss of your bright young friend, the lamp.
column2: 你放下油燈。它又傷心又憤怒。「我現在有了新的火焰,」你對油燈說,「她是我生命中的光。你拍馬也比不上她。」但你心裡其實正為失去這位明亮的小夥伴——油燈——而隱隱作痛。

column1: You try to force yourself into the wind, but it is stronger than you, and you can't quite get to the center of the funnel.
column2: 你試著硬擠進風裡,可它比你還強,你怎麼也搆不到漏斗風的中心。

column1: You are just not strong enough to withstand the wind.
column2: 你的力氣就是不夠,擋不住這陣風。

column1: You manage to withstand the force of the wind.
column2: 你勉強頂住了風的衝力。

column1: The rock is easily tossed from the funnel.
column2: 那塊石頭輕易地被漏斗風甩了出去。

column1: The dagger is easily tossed from the funnel.
column2: 那把匕首輕易地被漏斗風甩了出去。

column1: You force some dirt into the center of the funnel.
column2: 你硬把一把泥土塞進漏斗風的中心。

column1: You drop some dirt into the funnel.
column2: 你把一些泥土撒進漏斗風裡。

column1: You managed to toss some dirt into the center of the funnel.
column2: 你成功地把一把泥土甩進了漏斗風的中心。

column1: You capture the Air Elemental in the bellows and proudly put them away.
column2: 你用風箱捕住了氣元素精靈,得意地把它收了起來。

column1: The swift Air Elemental easily avoids the bellows.
column2: 敏捷的氣元素精靈輕鬆地避開了風箱。

column1: The Palace gate is locked from the inside.
column2: 宮殿大門從裡頭鎖上了。

column1: The Palace gate appears to be securely barred from the inside. Picking the lock will not help you enter.
column2: 宮殿大門似乎從裡頭牢牢閂住了。開鎖也幫不了你進去。

column1: You take a short break while watching the merchants set up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著商人們擺開攤子。

column1: You take a short break as the local merchants start setting up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,這時當地的商人們開始擺開攤子。

column1: Unfortunately, the peace is quickly disrupted by the appearance of a being that seems to be made entirely out of water. After helping the merchants evacuate, you return to the plaza to take another look at the creature and see that it has taken residence in the city's fountain.
column2: 可惜,這份寧靜很快就被一個彷彿全由水構成的生物打破了。幫商人們撤離之後,你回到廣場再瞧那生物一眼,發現它已經在城裡的噴泉中住了下來。

column1: You take a short break while watching the merchants close up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著商人們收攤打烊。

column1: Unfortunately, the peace is quickly disrupted by the sudden appearance of a magical funnel of wind. After helping the merchants evacuate, you return to the plaza to take another look at the creature.
column2: 可惜,這份寧靜很快就被一陣突然出現的魔法漏斗風打破了。幫商人們撤離之後,你回到廣場再瞧那生物一眼。

column1: You take a short break while watching the merchant set up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著那位商人擺開攤子。

column1: Unfortunately, the peace is quickly disrupted as a magical being of pure fire appears in the plaza. As it rushes towards one of the stands, you quickly help the merchants salvage their belongings and retreat.
column2: 可惜,這份寧靜很快就被打破了——一個由純粹火焰構成的魔法生物出現在廣場上。它衝向其中一個攤位,你連忙幫商人們搶救財物並撤離。

column1: After helping the merchants evacuate their belongings, you return to the plaza only to find the creature gone.
column2: 幫商人們搶救完財物、撤離之後,你回到廣場,卻發現那生物已經不見了。

column1: You take a short break, expecting the merchants to show up.
column2: 你稍作休息,等著商人們現身。

column1: The only thing that appears on the plaza, however, is the living fire that burned up the souvenir stand the other day.
column2: 然而,廣場上出現的卻只有那團前幾天燒掉紀念品攤的活火焰。

column1: You watch as the musician picks up his rug and basket and heads for the inn.
column2: 你看著那位樂師收起地毯和籃子,朝客棧走去。

column1: You take a short break while watching Ali Chica pack up his ruined stand and leave.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著阿里·奇卡收拾他那毀掉的攤子離去。

column1: You take a short break while watching the merchant close up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著那位商人收攤打烊。

column1: You take a short break while watching the performer and his assistants deconstruct the tightrope structure.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著那位表演者和他的助手們拆掉走鋼索的架子。

column1: You take a short break while watching a performer and his assistants set up a tightrope structure.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著一位表演者和他的助手們搭起走鋼索的架子。

column1: The next morning...
column2: 隔天早晨……

column1: You can't remember what the party was like afterwards and you have no idea when or how you got to bed.
column2: 你完全想不起來後來派對是什麼樣子,更不曉得自己是何時、又是怎麼上床的。

column1: You awaken with a terrible headache and feel very sick. Somehow, this just isn't your idea of how a hero should be.
column2: 你醒來時頭痛欲裂,渾身不舒服。不知怎地,這實在不太符合你心目中英雄該有的樣子。

column1: The guard sullenly escorts you back to the Blue Parrot Inn.
column2: 衛兵臭著一張臉,把你押回藍鸚鵡客棧。

column1: You take a short break while watching a local merchant set up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著一位當地商人擺開攤子。

column1: You take a short break while watching the local merchant close up shop.
column2: 你稍作休息,一邊看著那位當地商人收攤打烊。

column1: You watch as the Katta musician enters the inn and starts his nightly performance. Along with him, two dignified-looking men walk in and take place at one of the tables.
column2: 你看著那位卡塔樂師走進客棧,開始他每晚的表演。隨他一起,兩名儀表堂堂的男子走了進來,在其中一張桌子旁坐下。

column1: You watch as the Katta musician enters the inn and starts his nightly performance. Along with him, the poet and his companion arrive and take place at one of the tables.
column2: 你看著那位卡塔樂師走進客棧,開始他每晚的表演。隨他一起,那位詩人和他的同伴也到了,在其中一張桌子旁坐下。

column1: You watch as the Katta musician enters the inn and starts his nightly performance.
column2: 你看著那位卡塔樂師走進客棧,開始他每晚的表演。

column1: You watch as Abdulla comes running in with a distressed look on his face.
column2: 你看著阿布杜拉一臉焦急地跑了進來。

column1: You watch as Abdulla comes walking into the inn and takes a seat at your designated table.
column2: 你看著阿布杜拉走進客棧,在你預留的那張桌子旁坐下。

column1: You take a short break as you watch your friends prepare the restaurant for the upcoming day.
column2: 你稍作休息,看著你的朋友們為即將到來的一天張羅餐館。

column1: Harik gives you a burning glare.
column2: 哈里克火辣辣地瞪了你一眼。

column1: Very well. If it will save the city, you may take the incense.
column2: 好吧。要是這能拯救這座城,焚香就拿去吧。

column1: Very well. You may take the incense.
column2: 好吧。焚香你拿去吧。

column1: I am trying to stay in business. The more I give, the more I lose. It shall be what I asked for, %d centimes.
column2: 我也是想做生意過活的。我讓得越多,虧得越多。就照我開的價,%d 生丁。

column1: That is not an offer I am prepared to accept. I request that you pay %d centimes.
column2: 這個價我可沒法答應。我請你付 %d 生丁。

column1: That is not an offer I am prepared to accept. I request that you pay %d dinars.
column2: 這個價我可沒法答應。我請你付 %d 第納爾。

column1: I am sorry, but I do not have time for this. If you need the item, you must pay my final asking price of %d centimes for it.
column2: 抱歉,我沒空跟你耗。你要這東西,就得照我最後開的價付 %d 生丁。

column1: I am sorry, but I do not have time for this. If you need the item, you must pay my final asking price of %d dinars for it.
column2: 抱歉,我沒空跟你耗。你要這東西,就得照我最後開的價付 %d 第納爾。

column1: My price is firm.
column2: 我這價可是說一不二。

column1: I'm sorry, but I can only accept the coins of Shapeir. You will need to see the Moneychanger.
column2: 抱歉,我只收夏皮爾的錢幣。你得去找錢莊兌換才行。

column1: Thank you for your patronage.
column2: 謝謝惠顧。

column1: There is a special money-back guarantee with every pill I sell. If you die because of a pill, I will gladly refund your money.
column2: 我賣的每一顆藥丸都附帶特別的退款保證。要是你因為某顆藥丸而送了命,我很樂意把錢退給你。

column1: I'm sorry, but it's starting to get dark and I'd like to call it a day. Please come back tomorrow.
column2: 抱歉,天色漸暗,我想收工了。請明天再來吧。

column1: Watch this.
column2: 看好了。

column1: See how the flame reacts to incense? Fire is attracted to the things it burns because it is very vain.
column2: 瞧瞧火焰是怎麼回應焚香的?火會被它所燃燒的東西吸引,因為它很愛慕虛榮。

column1: You know, incense would make quite a lure for the Fire Elemental.
column2: 話說回來,焚香拿來引誘火元素精靈倒是挺管用的。

column1: See how the earth is consumed by the flames! Pretty amazing, isn't it? I'm sure there's a lesson to be learned from this, although I don't quite know what that might be.
column2: 瞧瞧泥土是怎麼被烈焰吞噬的!很驚人吧?我敢說這裡頭一定有什麼道理可學,雖然我也說不上來究竟是什麼。

column1: You give the Fruit of Compassion and the Griffin feather to Harik.
column2: 你把慈悲之果和獅鷲羽毛交給哈里克。

column1: This city and land are the very heart of the world.
column2: 這座城、這片土地,正是世界的心臟所在。

column1: When the old Emir was overthrown, the Apothecary of Raseir was taken over by the new Emir. If you plan to go to Raseir, it might be wise to take some extra pills just in case such things are no longer available there.
column2: 老埃米爾被推翻後,拉希爾的藥鋪就被新埃米爾接管了。要是你打算去拉希爾,多帶些藥丸或許是明智之舉,以防那邊再也買不到這些東西。

column1: Such magical creatures are capable of actions both good and evil, much like mankind.
column2: 這類魔法生物善惡兩端的行徑都做得出來,跟人類差不了多少。

column1: Fire glowing in the very semblance of a man. A marvel to behold, and yet a deadly peril as well. To understand the Fire Elemental, you must know the flame.
column2: 火焰以人的形貌熊熊燃燒。看著固然是奇觀,卻也是要命的禍患。想了解火元素精靈,你就得先懂得火焰。

column1: It was a thing of beauty, to be sure. I envy you for the stories you must have.
column2: 那的確是個美麗的東西。我真羨慕你,你肯定有滿肚子的故事。

column1: I do not deal much with water, so I have little I can tell you.
column2: 我跟水打的交道不多,能告訴你的也有限。

column1: The nature of air eludes me. I think the keeper of the Magic Shop understands it better than I.
column2: 風的本質我參不透。我想魔法店的店主比我更懂這個。

column1: The Elemental of Earth is a thing of vast destructive capabilities. In my studies, I have found that to master something, you must understand it. What is earth? That is what you need to know.
column2: 土元素精靈是個破壞力驚人的東西。在我的鑽研中我發現,想駕馭一樣東西,你就得先了解它。土是什麼?這才是你該弄明白的。

column1: The elements of earth, air, water, and fire are the essence of the universe. It is said that certain users of magic can give the semblance of life to an element, unleashing the destructive side of its nature.
column2: 土、風、水、火這四種元素是宇宙的本質。據說某些施法者能賦予元素以生命的形貌,放出它本性中具有破壞力的那一面。

column1: Many parts of monsters are usable. I use some for pill components.
column2: 怪物身上有許多部位都派得上用場。我會拿一些來當藥丸的成分。

column1: Did you wish to buy some pills?
column2: 你想買些藥丸嗎?

column1: I have many pills for sale. Which do you want?
column2: 我這兒有許多藥丸出售。你要哪一種?

column1: I have already made this potion for you. I have no more ingredients with which to make another.
column2: 這瓶藥水我已經幫你調好了。我也沒有材料再調一瓶了。

column1: If you will but bring me the ingredients, I should be happy to make the potion for you at no charge.
column2: 只要你把材料帶來給我,我很樂意免費替你調這瓶藥水。

column1: I know only the magic required to do my work. You should ask across the plaza for a better answer.
column2: 我只懂得幹活兒所需的那點魔法。你該去廣場對面問問,興許能得到更好的答案。

column1: Even the Sultan (May Peace Be Always With Him!) has often used my pills.
column2: 就連蘇丹(願平安永遠與他同在!)也常服用我的藥丸呢。

column1: The new Emir, it has been said, uses drugs of the darkest nature -- the ones which poison the mind and soul.
column2: 據說新埃米爾用的是最陰暗的藥物——那種會毒害心智與靈魂的東西。

column1: You can see the Palace of the Sultan over its gates east of here.
column2: 往這兒以東,你能在城門那頭望見蘇丹的宮殿。

column1: The desert is a wonderful source of components for my pills.
column2: 沙漠是我藥丸成分的絕佳來源。

column1: I have a reward notice posted there. I pay for pill components.
column2: 我在那兒貼了一張懸賞告示。我會付錢收購藥丸成分。

column1: The inn, I think, is in a plaza to the south of here.
column2: 客棧嘛,我想是在這兒以南的某座廣場上。

column1: I have no idea. I have been inside all day.
column2: 我哪知道。我一整天都待在店裡。

column1: The fountain is the source of water for the city.
column2: 那座噴泉是這座城的水源。

column1: I have been told that the teeth of a dragon are very useful for components, but I have never been fortunate enough to find any.
column2: 有人跟我說,龍牙拿來當成分非常有用,可惜我從沒那個運氣弄到過。

column1: Her shop is west of us, at the end of Dinar Tarik.
column2: 她的店在我們以西,第納爾街的盡頭。

column1: I consult him occasionally for good days to make pills. He is near the end of Tarik of Stars.
column2: 我偶爾會找他算算製藥的好日子。他就住在繁星街盡頭附近。

column1: The Enchantress Aziza has a house along the west side of Shmali Tarik.
column2: 女術士阿姿莎在希瑪利街西側有一棟房子。

column1: The Katta are very similar to us in physiology.
column2: 卡塔族在生理構造上跟我們十分相似。

column1: I am Harik Attar, which in your language means, "Fire Druggist." I was well named.
column2: 我是哈里克·阿塔爾,用你們的話來說,意思就是「火藥師」。這名字取得真貼切。

column1: The incense will cost you but the price of my ingredients, a mere %d centimes.
column2: 這焚香只算你我成本的價,區區 %d 生丁罷了。

column1: You speak as one born to bargain. It shall be 15 centimes.
column2: 你這口才,簡直是天生來討價還價的。就算 15 生丁吧。

column1: But I have already given the dispel potion to you.
column2: 可是驅散藥水我已經給過你了。

column1: If you can but get me the Griffin's feather and the "Fruit of Compassion," I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能替我弄來獅鷲的羽毛和「慈悲之果」,我就把藥水給你。

column1: If you can but get me the Griffin's feather, I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能替我弄來獅鷲的羽毛,我就把藥水給你。

column1: If you can but get me the "Fruit of Compassion," I will give you the potion.
column2: 只要你能替我弄來「慈悲之果」,我就把藥水給你。

column1: I do not usually make potions unless they are special orders. I make pills that do wonderful things. However, I do have a recipe for a Dispel Potion.
column2: 除非是特別訂製,我平常是不調藥水的。我做的是功效奇妙的藥丸。不過,驅散藥水的配方我倒是有一份。

column1: Mana Pills are very useful for those with magical abilities. They restore the magical energy used in spells.
column2: 法力丸對有魔法能力的人非常有用。它能補充施法所消耗的法力。

column1: Vigor Pills aid iron-poor blood, restore vital minerals used up in strenuous exercise, and act as quick picker-uppers in times of stress.
column2: 活力丸能改善貧血、補充劇烈運動所耗掉的重要礦物質,在你緊繃疲累時還能迅速幫你提神。

column1: Healing Pills accelerate the normal healing of the body. A hero such as yourself will undoubtedly need a large supply.
column2: 療傷丸能加速身體的自然癒合。像你這樣的英雄,無疑會需要大量備著。

column1: poison cure pill
column2: 解毒丸

column1: cure pill
column2: 解毒丸

column1: poison cure
column2: 解毒

column1: The venom of the desert scorpion is deadly unless you take these pills before you are struck by its tail.
column2: 沙漠蠍子的毒液可是致命的——除非你在被牠的尾刺扎到之前,先吃下這些藥丸。

column1: mana pill price
column2: 法力丸價格

column1: mana price
column2: 法力價格

column1: The pills you request are a mere %d dinars.
column2: 閣下要的這些藥丸,區區 %d 第納爾而已。

column1: vigor pill price
column2: 活力丸價格

column1: vigor price
column2: 活力價格

column1: healing pill price
column2: 治療丸價格

column1: healing price
column2: 治療價格

column1: poison pill price
column2: 毒藥丸價格

column1: poison price
column2: 毒藥價格

column1: cure pill price
column2: 解毒丸價格

column1: poison cure price
column2: 解毒價格

column1: cure price
column2: 解毒價格

column1: price vigor pill
column2: 活力丸價格

column1: Vigor pills cost %d dinars.
column2: 活力丸要價 %d 第納爾。

column1: price healing pill
column2: 治療丸價格

column1: Healing pills cost %d dinars.
column2: 治療丸要價 %d 第納爾。

column1: price cure pill
column2: 解毒丸價格

column1: price poison cure pill
column2: 解毒丸價格

column1: Poison cure pills cost %d dinars.
column2: 解毒丸要價 %d 第納爾。

column1: price mana pill
column2: 法力丸價格

column1: Mana pills cost %d dinars.
column2: 法力丸要價 %d 第納爾。

column1: Pills tend to stick in your throat if you don't drink plenty of water.
column2: 藥丸若不配著大量的水吞下,可是很容易卡在喉嚨裡的。

column1: fruit compassion
column2: 慈悲之果

column1: compassion fruit
column2: 慈悲之果

column1: The Fruit of Compassion is extremely rare. It can only be given, never taken or bought. I have heard rumors of but one plant in the entire land where you might find this.
column2: 慈悲之果極為稀有。它只能被贈與,絕不能強取或買賣。我聽聞整片大地之上,僅有一株植株可供你尋覓。

column1: scorpion tail
column2: 蠍尾

column1: scorpion sting
column2: 蠍刺

column1: The sting of the Scorpion's tail is death, unless you take my poison cure pills. Unfortunately, even they will not help if you are struck by the tail more than once.
column2: 蠍尾的一螫即是死亡——除非你服下我的解毒丸。可惜啊,要是你被那尾刺扎中不只一次,連我的藥丸也救不了你。

column1: I do not recommend the use of drugs. They can be very dangerous. MY pills, now... they are not drugs. They are a mixture of all-natural ingredients (no artificial colors, flavors, or preservatives) and magic.
column2: 我可不建議用什麼藥物,那玩意兒危險得很。至於我的藥丸嘛……那可不是藥物。它們是純天然原料(不含人工色素、香料,也無防腐劑)再加上魔法調製而成。

column1: griffin feather
column2: 獅鷲羽毛

column1: The griffin is half-bird and half-beast. It has the head and wings of an eagle and the body of a lion. Since it is neither wholly one nor the other, it can be used as a catalyst for change.
column2: 獅鷲半鳥半獸,生著鷹的頭與翅,獅的身軀。正因牠既不完全是這個,也不完全是那個,所以能用作變化的觸媒。

column1: You need to add the victim's hair to the potion I made for you just before you give it to the victim. It must be freshly collected from the victim.
column2: 在你把藥水交給受害者之前的那一刻,得先把受害者的頭髮加進我替你調的藥水裡。而且那頭髮必須是剛從受害者身上採下的。

column1: The hair is the last thing added to the potion just before you give it to the enchanted victim. As the hair of the enchanted creature dissolves in the potion, so too shall the spell dissolve when the victim drinks the potion.
column2: 頭髮是最後加進藥水的東西,就在你把藥水遞給中咒受害者的前一刻。當中咒生物的頭髮溶解於藥水之中,咒語也將在受害者飲下藥水時隨之消解。

column1: The hair must be freshly pulled. That is important.
column2: 那頭髮必須是剛拔下來的。這點很重要。

column1: I am always in need of certain components for my work. If you are brave enough to bring some of these components to me, I am more than willing to pay you for them.
column2: 我做事總是少不了某些素材。你要是膽子夠大,願意替我弄些來,我也十分樂意付你錢。

column1: ghoul claw
column2: 食屍鬼爪

column1: al ghul claw
column2: 食屍鬼爪

column1: The Ghoul is a creature of the night. The scrape of its claws chills the soul and draws the very life away from you. It takes a very agile adventurer to avoid death at its hands.
column2: 食屍鬼是夜行的生物。牠利爪一刮,寒透人的靈魂,還會把你的生命力一點一點抽乾。得是身手極為敏捷的冒險者,才能逃過喪命於牠爪下的命運。

column1: Terrorsauruses are savage beasts which roam the desert dunes. Their scales are sometimes useful for concocting remedies to certain afflictions.
column2: 恐懼龍是遊蕩於沙丘間的兇猛野獸。牠們的鱗片有時能用來調製某些病症的解藥。

column1: I'm not sure what you wish to capture.
column2: 我不太明白你想捕捉的是什麼。

column1: I think you could capture a Fire Elemental in something designed to hold flames, such as a lamp.
column2: 我想,你可以用某種專門盛裝火焰的東西來捕捉火元素,好比說……一盞燈。

column1: price oil
column2: 油的價格

column1: The oil will cost you %d centimes.
column2: 這油要花你 %d 生丁。

column1: pill seller
column2: 賣藥丸的

column1: I sell pills to fix your ills. Catchy phrase, is it not?
column2: 我賣藥丸,治你百般。這口號朗朗上口,是不是?

column1: This is the Apothecary, where one can buy many wondrous pills. I am Harik. If you need something, just let me know.
column2: 這裡是藥鋪,各式各樣的神奇藥丸應有盡有。我是哈里克。你需要什麼,儘管開口。

column1: You can use my marvelous pills to heal your wounds.
column2: 你可以用我這了不起的藥丸來治療傷口。

column1: Mana is magical energy. My mana pills help restore it.
column2: 法力就是魔法的能量。我的法力丸能幫你補回來。

column1: The prices I charge but reflect the cost of materials.
column2: 我開的價,不過是反映原料的成本罷了。

column1: dispel potion
column2: 驅散藥水

column1: itI am sorry, but I used the last of my ingredients making the last one, so I will be unable to make you another.
column2: 很抱歉,我做上一份時把最後的原料都用光了,所以沒辦法再替你做一份。

column1: dispel ingredient
column2: 驅散素材

column1: potion ingredient
column2: 藥水素材

column1: noI will need a Griffin's feather. You must add the hair of the victim yourself at the last moment. It must be fresh.
column2: 我會需要一根獅鷲羽毛。受害者的頭髮得由你自己在最後一刻加進去,而且必須是新鮮的。

column1: y I am sorry, but I used the last of my ingredients making the last one, so I will be unable to make you another.
column2: 很抱歉,我做上一份時把最後的原料都用光了,所以沒辦法再替你做一份。

column1: It is not in my power to give you the answer.
column2: 這個答案,可不在我能力所及的範圍內。

column1: buy powder rol
column2: 購買燃燒之粉

column1: I only have a little left. I really couldn't part with it for any price.
column2: 我只剩一點點了,無論出多少價,我都實在割捨不下。

column1: Are you really certain you need this?
column2: 你真的確定你需要這個?

column1: My knowledge of such things is scarce.
column2: 我對這類事情所知甚少。

column1: I thought you already dealt with that.
column2: 我還以為你早就把那件事擺平了。

column1: Yes, powder of burning might be of some use against an Earth Elemental.
column2: 沒錯,燃燒之粉對付土元素,或許能派上點用場。

column1: Thank you. I wish I could have seen it, though. The fire must have been lovely.
column2: 謝謝你。不過,真希望我能親眼瞧瞧。那把火想必美極了。

column1: Humph. Nobody likes a braggart. You seem to have forgotten who gave you the powder of burning.
column2: 哼。沒人喜歡愛吹牛的傢伙。你好像忘了是誰給你那燃燒之粉的吧。

column1: Yes, I have heard of these things. Wouldn't it be a wondrous sight!
column2: 是啊,這些東西我聽說過。那該是多麼奇妙的景象啊!

column1: I must sadly admit that it did get out of hand. I suppose you did the right thing in vanquishing it.
column2: 我得遺憾地承認,那場面確實失控了。我想,你把牠收拾掉是做對了。

column1: Its capture is not a big loss. The creature wasn't nearly as impressive to watch as the Fire Elemental.
column2: 捕不到牠也算不上什麼大損失。那生物瞧起來,可遠遠比不上火元素那般壯觀。

column1: Such a thing would not be nearly as grand as a Fire Elemental.
column2: 那種東西,可遠遠比不上火元素那般氣派。

column1: thank harik
column2: 感謝哈里克

column1: thank apothecary
column2: 感謝藥師

column1: You thank Harik for giving one of his own lamps.
column2: 你感謝哈里克送了你一盞他自己的燈。

column1: I hope it will help you.
column2: 希望它能幫上你的忙。

column1: You thank Harik for giving you one of his own lamps.
column2: 你感謝哈里克送了你一盞他自己的燈。

column1: I am glad it was of help to you.
column2: 很高興它幫上了你的忙。

column1: You thank Harik for giving you some of his incense for free.
column2: 你感謝哈里克免費送了你一些他的薰香。

column1: I hope it will be of use to you.
column2: 希望它對你有用。

column1: I suppose it would not be appropriate to think only of profits when the city is in danger.
column2: 我想,當這座城市身陷險境時,還只想著利潤,那就太不應該了。

column1: I am glad you were able to put it to good use.
column2: 很高興你能把它好好派上用場。

column1: sell claw
column2: 賣爪

column1: sell ghoul claw
column2: 賣食屍鬼爪

column1: You don't have any.
column2: 你身上一個也沒有。

column1: sell tail
column2: 賣尾

column1: sell scorpion tail
column2: 賣蠍尾

column1: sell scorpion
column2: 賣蠍子

column1: sell stinger
column2: 賣螫刺

column1: sell scorpion stinger
column2: 賣蠍子螫刺

column1: You tell Harik how you collected the ingredients for a potion that broke the spell upon the Brigand leader.
column2: 你告訴哈里克,你是如何收集到那些素材,調出了破解強盜首領身上咒語的藥水。

column1: I hope to discover a way to create a dispel potion that breaks enchantments upon contact with the subject. My own potions would be ineffective against an enchanted being unwilling or unable to drink them.
column2: 我盼著能找出一種方法,調出一碰到對象就能破除魔咒的驅散藥水。我現在這種藥水,對那些不願喝或喝不下的中咒生物可是毫無作用的。

column1: She seems like a very powerful and dangerous being. Perhaps you did the right thing by not interfering with her.
column2: 她看來是個既強大又危險的存在。或許你不去招惹她,是做對了。

column1: Your brave deeds continue to astound me!
column2: 你那些英勇事蹟,真是教我嘆為觀止啊!

column1: You tell Harik about the WIT initiation.
column2: 你向哈里克說起 WIT 的入會儀式。

column1: So you have the ability to calm flames on command? Do you also know of a way to intensify a fire?
column2: 這麼說,你能隨心所欲地平息火焰?那你可知道有什麼法子能讓火燒得更旺?

column1: You shake your head.
column2: 你搖了搖頭。

column1: That is truly a shame.
column2: 那可真是太可惜了。

column1: Harik would probably not be interested in that. Besides, it's a secret organization, remember?
column2: 哈里克大概對那不會有興趣。再說了,那可是個祕密組織,別忘了。

column1: You tell Harik about the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你向哈里克說起那位中了咒的埃米爾。

column1: I should be able to come up with a way to make a dispel potion effective against hairless creatures, but it might take me about a week to prepare it. I should have it ready when you return.
column2: 我應該能想出辦法,調出一種對無毛生物也有效的驅散藥水,不過大概要花上我一週的時間來準備。等你回來時,它應該就備好了。

column1: She seems like a nice person, although perhaps a little bit obsessed with water.
column2: 她看來是個好人,雖然或許對水有那麼點兒癡迷。

column1: Perhaps someday she will regain her human form again. I think I shall have to start looking for alternative dispel potion recipes.
column2: 或許有朝一日,她能重新變回人形。看來我得開始物色別的驅散藥水配方了。

column1: You tell Harik about Omar's performances at the inn.
column2: 你向哈里克說起奧瑪爾在客棧裡的表演。

column1: I personally prefer to read scientific literature in my spare time.
column2: 我個人嘛,閒暇時更愛讀些科學典籍。

column1: You tell Harik about Shameen, Shema, and their inn.
column2: 你向哈里克說起沙明、舍瑪以及他們的客棧。

column1: Harik nods, but doesn't seem overly interested.
column2: 哈里克點了點頭,卻似乎興趣缺缺。

column1: You tell about how you arrived in Shapeir by means of Abdulla's magic carpet.
column2: 你說起自己是如何搭著阿布杜拉的魔毯抵達夏皮爾的。

column1: It is a shame flying carpets are so rare. My pills against airsickness would sell so much better if they were more common as a means of transportation.
column2: 飛毯這麼稀罕,實在可惜。要是它能成為更普遍的代步工具,我那些防暈機藥丸的銷路肯定好上許多。

column1: You tell about your battle with Sweeping Sir James.
column2: 你說起自己與「橫掃詹姆斯爵士」的那場決鬥。

column1: He was a reliable source of components back in the day. Never took it well when I told him I didn't need any more though.
column2: 想當年,他可是我可靠的素材來源。不過呢,我跟他說不再需要了的時候,他可一直沒能釋懷。

column1: You are better off keeping that to yourself. You doubt Harik would be very appreciative of your story.
column2: 這事兒你還是悶在心裡比較好。你猜哈里克八成不會太領情。

column1: You tell about the acrobat's challenge.
column2: 你說起那位雜技藝人的挑戰。

column1: It's not something I would enjoy doing myself. I'm not too fond of heights.
column2: 那可不是我自己會樂在其中的事。我對高處實在不太感冒。

column1: You tell how you met the paladin Rakeesh.
column2: 你說起自己是如何結識聖騎士拉基什的。

column1: Have you seen his leg? It bears a most curious wound. For some reason, it resists the effects of my healing pills, no matter how potent I make them.
column2: 你看過他的腿嗎?那上頭有道極為古怪的傷。不知怎地,無論我把治療丸做得多有效,那傷就是不見好。

column1: caged beast
column2: 籠中野獸

column1: I'm happy to hear that the dispel potion did its job after what you went through to gather the ingredients.
column2: 聽說那驅散藥水終於發揮了作用,我很高興——畢竟你為了收集那些素材吃了不少苦頭。

column1: That was in exceptionally poor taste.
column2: 那實在是低級得可以。

column1: potion dispel
column2: 驅散藥水

column1: Oh yes. Have you had a chance to use it yet? I would be most anxious to hear how it worked.
column2: 噢,對了。你有機會用上它了嗎?我可急著想知道它效果如何呢。

column1: Most interesting, I'm sure, but I'm a busy man.
column2: 想必非常有趣,不過呢,我是個大忙人。

column1: If you need something, I'd be pleased to sell it to you.
column2: 你要是需要什麼,我很樂意賣給你。

column1: You can't get the black cauldron.
column2: 你拿不動那口黑釜。

column1: For a fleeting moment you feel compelled to put your hand in the oven, but the feeling passes.
column2: 有那麼一瞬間,你竟有股衝動想把手伸進爐子裡,不過這念頭很快就過去了。

column1: That's one way to get the point.
column2: 這倒不失為一種「切中要點」的方法。

column1: Don't touch. They're hot!
column2: 別碰。燙著呢!

column1: Taking the skull would be a bone-headed idea. You'd do best to leave it on the shelf.
column2: 拿走那顆頭骨可是個「沒腦子」的餿主意。你最好還是讓它乖乖待在架上。

column1: Alas, poor Harik! (A job for Horatio?)
column2: 唉,可憐的哈里克啊!(這活兒該交給霍拉旭吧?)

column1: You can't take the bottles.
column2: 你拿不走那些瓶子。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like a doggie dish for a Fire Elemental.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是給火元素用的狗食盆。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like Halloween candy.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是萬聖節糖果。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like Old Grandpaw's high-risk moonshine.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是老阿公那要命的私釀烈酒。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like eye of newt (does Wayne know about this?).
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是蠑螈眼珠(這事韋恩知道嗎?)。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like 10-20-40 weight.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是 10-20-40 號黏度的機油。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like leftover cooking grease.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是煮剩的廚餘油脂。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like the kind of fiery spices that are guaranteed to reduce taste buds to ash.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是那種辣到能把味蕾燒成灰的猛火香料。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like a collection of collectible cologne bottles.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是一整套收藏級的古龍水瓶。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like burnt offerings.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是燒焦的供品。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like great green gobs of greasy grimy gopher guts.
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是一大坨綠油油、黏糊糊、髒兮兮的地鼠內臟。

column1: The shelves are filled with unusual objects. You see something that looks like a small quantity of "Shake 'n' Bake."
column2: 架上擺滿了稀奇古怪的玩意兒。你瞧見一樣東西,活像是一小份「搖搖烤」裹粉。

column1: It's time to shelve those irrelevant mutterings.
column2: 是時候把那些不著邊際的嘮叨「束之高閣」了。

column1: The cart puts up a wheely interesting conversation.
column2: 這手推車可「輪番」上演了一場挺有意思的對話呢。

column1: It's neither dandy nor handy when your mouth is quite sandy.
column2: 嘴裡滿是沙子的滋味,可一點都不妙、不順手。

column1: The hook is often used to hold the red-enameled black cauldron off the fire.
column2: 這鉤子常用來把那口紅釉黑釜從火上吊起來。

column1: The hook holds the black cauldron off the fire. It was a donation from Peter Pan's personal mementos.
column2: 這鉤子把黑釜從火上吊起。它可是出自小飛俠彼得潘私人紀念品的捐贈呢。

column1: Judging by the foul smell emanating from within, you'd rather not!
column2: 光是聞那從裡頭飄出來的惡臭,你就寧可敬而遠之!

column1: You'd prefer not to do anything with the foul-smelling pot.
column2: 你壓根不想對那口臭氣熏天的鍋子動手動腳。

column1: Last week's urnings.
column2: 這是上禮拜的「甕」中之物。

column1: How much does a Grecian urn?
column2: 希臘甕能賺多少?

column1: You'll have to wait your turn if you want to earn a burnt urn. Case adjourned.
column2: 想賺個燒焦的甕,那也得排隊等輪到你。本案到此休庭。

column1: Don't bother talking to the urns.
column2: 別費神跟那些甕說話了。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Creative Accounting for Left Brains and Numerilexics."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《左腦人與數字盲的創意記帳術》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "How to Build Your Own Brazier."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《自己動手打造火盆》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Elementary Elementals."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《元素精怪入門》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Herb's Herbs."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《赫伯的百草集》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "How to Make Pills Pay the Bills."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《靠賣藥丸付帳單》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "The Pizza Elemental: Fact or Fiction?"
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《披薩元素精怪:是真是假?》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "By the Time I Am a Phoenix, I'll be Rising."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《等我變成鳳凰,我就浴火重生》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "To Pill a Mockingbird."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《用藥丸殺死一隻反舌鳥》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Flame and Fortune."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《烈焰與財富》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "How to Get Fired Without Losing Your Job."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《如何被炒火而不丟飯碗》。

column1: There seem to be books in many languages, few of which are comprehensible to you, but you do recognize some old favorites, such as "Stupid Elemental Tricks."
column2: 這裡的書似乎用許多種語言寫成,你看得懂的沒幾本,不過你倒認出幾本老相識,像是《元素精怪的蠢把戲》。

column1: The niche is empty.
column2: 這個壁龕是空的。

column1: The ceiling reminds you of a night you spent in the land of Haight-Ashbury many years ago. As a matter of fact, the whole place is like that.
column2: 這天花板讓你想起多年前在海特-艾許伯里度過的某個夜晚。說真的,整間屋子都是那個調調。

column1: You're getting warm... warmer still... hot... very hot... intensely hot... SIZZLING HOT! Congratulations, you've found the ceiling!
column2: 你越來越熱了……更熱了……燙……很燙……燙到不行……滋滋作響地燙!恭喜,你找到天花板了!

column1: The roof, the roof, the roof is on fire.
