column2: 屋頂啊屋頂,屋頂著火啦。

column1: A beautifully ornate cupboard is built into the shop's wall. It is closed.
column2: 店牆裡嵌著一座雕飾華美的櫥櫃。櫃門關著。

column1: You can't open the cupboard. (Heck, you can't even reach it with all those urns in the way.)
column2: 你打不開這櫥櫃。(該死,擺了這麼一堆甕擋著,你連碰都碰不到。)

column1: You wonder if Harik keeps any skeletons in his cupboard.
column2: 你不禁好奇,哈里克的櫥櫃裡是不是藏著什麼見不得人的秘密。

column1: It is Harik's workbench.
column2: 這是哈里克的工作檯。

column1: The workbench is occupied.
column2: 工作檯有人在用。

column1: The art of bench-pressing involves interrogation of workbenches. You hope to someday master this extraordinary skill.
column2: 所謂「逼供工作檯」這門藝術,講究的是審問檯面。你盼著哪天能練成這身絕技。

column1: The Apothecary Shop is aflame with reds, yellows, and oranges, and smoky incense fills the air. The druggist is busy creating pills and powders.
column2: 藥鋪裡紅黃橙三色熊熊燃燒,空氣中瀰漫著裊裊薰香。藥師正忙著調製藥丸與藥粉。

column1: The humidity in here is sweltering. Combined with the heat outside, you don't know how the druggist is capable of working in such an environment.
column2: 這裡濕悶得叫人喘不過氣,再加上外頭的酷熱,你真不曉得藥師是怎麼在這種環境下幹活的。

column1: Careful, Hero! You'll burn your hand.
column2: 小心,英雄!你會燙傷手的。

column1: You can't touch the lamps.
column2: 你不能碰那些燈。

column1: The smoke from the brazier smells of sweet incense and halibut.
column2: 火盆冒出的煙,聞起來有甜薰香混著比目魚的味道。

column1: I suggest you do not touch what you do not know. The wrong drug could easily be fatal.
column2: 我勸你別碰不熟的東西。下錯藥可是很容易要命的。

column1: It needs a pestle to be useful.
column2: 得配上杵才用得上。

column1: You can't take Harik's mortar. He needs it to make his pills.
column2: 你不能拿走哈里克的研缽。他得靠它製藥丸。

column1: It needs a mortar to be useful.
column2: 得配上研缽才用得上。

column1: You can't take Harik's pestle. He needs it to make his pills.
column2: 你不能拿走哈里克的研杵。他得靠它製藥丸。

column1: You can't earnestly bring yourself to take the funerary urn.
column2: 你實在沒辦法昧著良心拿走這只葬甕。

column1: Deathly silence is the funerary urn's response.
column2: 葬甕的回應,是一片死寂。

column1: You can't get a weigh with that. You'd get pounded.
column2: 這你可矇混不過去(秤不過去)。你會被搗成肉泥的。

column1: That would be a weighty conversation.
column2: 那會是場沉甸甸的對話。

column1: Do not forget that Shema dances this night!
column2: 別忘了今晚舍瑪要跳舞!

column1: The Poet Omar will thrill all who hear with his words of song after the setting of the sun, in our own inn!
column2: 日落之後,詩人奧瑪爾將在我們的客棧裡,以歌詠之詞讓所有聽眾如癡如醉!

column1: Before you leave, %s, I must give you something.
column2: 在你離開之前,%s,我有樣東西非給你不可。

column1: In case you do not make it back to the inn before closing time, take our spare key. We lock the inn shortly after midnight.
column2: 萬一你來不及在打烊前趕回客棧,就拿著我們的備用鑰匙吧。我們過了午夜不久就會鎖門。

column1: Take care not to lose it. A night spent on the stones of the plaza drags on forever.
column2: 可別弄丟了。在廣場的石板上過一夜,那感覺長得像沒有盡頭。

column1: You take the key to the front door from Shameen.
column2: 你從沙明手中接過了前門的鑰匙。

column1: The inn's decor looks and feels purr-fectly purr-sian.
column2: 客棧的擺設,看著、感覺著都是「貓」道地的波斯風(喵極了)。

column1: You run your hand down the wall. It feels solid enough.
column2: 你伸手順著牆面摸下去。摸起來夠結實。

column1: The brass bottle is merely decorative. Even if there was liquid inside, it would be too big to lift up to your mouth.
column2: 這只黃銅瓶純粹是裝飾用的。就算裡頭裝著液體,它也太大了,沒法舉到嘴邊喝。

column1: May your journey be swift, Hero-friend, and may your actions bring good fortune to everyone.
column2: 願你一路順風,英雄朋友,願你的作為為眾人帶來好運。

column1: And above all...may you stay safe.
column2: 但最要緊的是……願你平安。

column1: May we have your attention, please? We regret to inform you that the restaurant will be closing for the night. Shema and I would like to thank you for dining with us and are looking forward to your next visit.
column2: 能否請大家注意一下?很遺憾要通知各位,本店今晚即將打烊。舍瑪和我感謝各位光臨用餐,期待您再度蒞臨。

column1: That is enough for tonight, Sitar. Thank you for brightening this eve with your songs.
column2: 今晚就到這兒吧,西塔。謝謝你用歌聲照亮了這個夜晚。

column1: As always, the food was wonderful, Shema. I must remember to invite the two of you over to my home sometime, so I can treat you to some fine cooking of my own.
column2: 一如往常,菜餚美味極了,舍瑪。我得記著哪天請你們倆到我家坐坐,也好讓我露一手好手藝款待你們。

column1: Are you sure you would not rather stay here tonight, Abdulla? If you go outside now, that fire creature may attack you.
column2: 你確定今晚不留下來嗎,阿布杜拉?你這會兒出門,那火怪說不定會撲上來攻擊你。

column1: I am honored by the offer, though I have already taken advantage enough of your hospitality in the past. If the Elemental of Fire wishes to immolate me, it shall have to catch up with me first.
column2: 承蒙好意,我深感榮幸,不過從前我已叨擾你們夠多了。那火元素精怪若想把我燒成灰,也得先追得上我才行。

column1: Ah... our offer remains in case you change your mind.
column2: 啊……我們的邀請仍然有效,要是你改變主意的話。

column1: It is time for me to go as well. It is good to know that all of us can walk safely outside again.
column2: 我也該動身了。能曉得我們大夥兒又能安心走在外頭,真是太好了。

column1: In exchange for sharing your tea with me, I could visit the oasis in the desert and bring you a few waterskins of fresh water if you wish.
column2: 承你請我喝茶,作為回禮,若你願意,我可以去趟沙漠裡的綠洲,給你帶幾袋新鮮的水回來。

column1: It was our pleasure. Please do not think of endangering yourself for our sake.
column2: 這是我們的榮幸。請千萬別為了我們以身犯險。

column1: Tonight seems like a good night for a stroll to the central plaza before heading home. It will be good to watch the moonlight in our beautiful fountain without that Water Elemental marring the sight.
column2: 今晚回家前,似乎正適合到中央廣場散散步。少了那水元素精怪壞了風景,好好欣賞月光灑在我們那座美麗噴泉上,想必愜意。

column1: I thank you, my friends,[  as the sun's light sinks low,[And wish you good tidings.[  Now I, too, must go.
column2: 謝過各位朋友,[  趁這夕陽西沉,[獻上一聲祝福。[  此刻我亦須啟程。

column1: May each of you rest well this night.
column2: 願你們各位今晚好眠。

column1: Good morning, Hero-friend. We did not expect you to be up so early.
column2: 早安,英雄朋友。沒想到你這麼早就起床了。

column1: If you are hungry, I will go and prepare a delicious breakfast for you right away.
column2: 你若餓了,我這就去為你準備一頓豐盛的早餐。

column1: Hero-friend, Shema and I shall be retiring for the night. May you dream of all the adventures still awaiting you and all the rewards to come from them.
column2: 英雄朋友,舍瑪和我要就寢了。願你夢見一切尚在前方等著你的冒險,以及隨之而來的種種獎賞。

column1: The musically talented Katta comes to this inn every evening to perform. He is very good.
column2: 那位才華洋溢的卡塔族樂手,每晚都會來這客棧表演。他真的很厲害。

column1: The Elementals are some sort of magical things which could destroy us all!
column2: 那些元素精怪是某種魔法造物,足以把我們全給毀了!

column1: The land of Shapeir may be plagued with Elementals and Djinns, but this city will ever stand as long as there are heroes such as yourself.
column2: 夏皮爾這片土地或許飽受元素精怪與精靈之患,但只要有像你這樣的英雄在,這座城就永遠屹立不搖。

column1: Raseir and Shapeir were sister cities, separated by the sands of the desert. Now it is said that the fountain of Raseir runs no more and that the city's soul has shriveled like a grape in the sun.
column2: 拉希爾與夏皮爾本是姊妹城,隔著一片沙漠相望。如今聽說拉希爾的噴泉已不再湧流,那座城的靈魂也像曬在烈日下的葡萄,乾癟枯萎了。

column1: Djinn are magical beings, huge monstrous creatures of power capable of tearing a man limb from limb, although I have never personally experienced this.
column2: 精靈是有魔力的存在,身軀龐大如怪物,力量足以把人撕成四分五裂——儘管我本人從沒親身領教過就是了。

column1: Living flames which seek to consume everything!
column2: 那是有生命的烈焰,一心想把一切吞噬殆盡!

column1: We shall be burned to cinders like a saurus steak left too long on the grill!
column2: 我們會被燒成灰燼,活像一塊在烤架上擱太久的索魯斯排!

column1: Only a true hero could have defeated the dreaded Fire Elemental.
column2: 唯有真正的英雄,才能擊敗那令人聞風喪膽的火元素精怪。

column1: It is said to be a living whirlwind.
column2: 據說那是一陣有生命的旋風。

column1: We will all be blown to bits!
column2: 我們全都會被吹得粉身碎骨!

column1: You certainly are quite a hero for defeating the Air Elemental, %s.
column2: 你打敗了氣元素精怪,確實是個了不起的英雄,%s。

column1: It is rock and stone molded into a living creature with all the destructive power of the earthquake.
column2: 那是由岩石與磐石塑成的活物,擁有不下於地震的毀滅之力。

column1: The city will fall and I will perish unless you prevent this!
column2: 除非你出手阻止,否則這座城會淪陷,我也將喪命!

column1: The Earth Elemental was a most fearsome creature, but even it was no match for a true hero.
column2: 土元素精怪是個可怕至極的怪物,但即便是它,也敵不過一位真正的英雄。

column1: I have fought many monsters in my travels through the desert.
column2: 我在橫越沙漠的旅途中,跟許多怪物交過手。

column1: This is a land of magic and mystery.
column2: 這是一片充滿魔法與神秘的土地。

column1: The other day I was talking to the Sultan (May He Live Forever!). As Harun Al-Rashid thanked me for my gift of the flying carpet and complimented me upon my courage in fighting against those vile brigands of Spielburg, he mentioned that there might be a need of a hero around here soon. The Sultan must have talked to the Astrologer.
column2: 前些日子我跟蘇丹(願他萬壽無疆!)交談。哈倫·阿爾-拉希德為我獻上的飛毯向我道謝,還誇我有膽量,敢去對抗史畢柏格那群卑劣的山賊;他順口提到,這一帶不久後恐怕會需要一位英雄。蘇丹想必是跟占星師談過了。

column1: Arus was the kindest of men, quick to hug you and call you brother, but I must admit that he was not very brave.
column2: 阿魯斯是再仁厚不過的人,動不動就擁你入懷、喚你一聲兄弟,不過我得承認,他不太有膽量。

column1: You hope to come to WIT's end, ha ha! I have heard rumors that the wizard's school is around here, but I, who have lived my life here, have seen nothing of it.
column2: 你想找到 WIT 的盡頭嗎,哈哈!我聽過傳聞,說那所巫師學院就在這一帶,可我在這兒住了一輩子,卻連個影子也沒見著。

column1: As I looked around the Sultan's Palace the other day, I marveled at its wealth and beauty. Perhaps someday you, too, will be important enough to be invited there.
column2: 前些日子我在蘇丹的宮殿四下打量,對它的富麗堂皇驚嘆不已。也許有朝一日,你也能有夠分量,獲邀到那兒去。

column1: There will be a caravan to Raseir leaving later this month. I, for one, am happy not to have to be on it.
column2: 這個月稍晚,會有一支前往拉希爾的商隊出發。至少我本人,很慶幸自己不必跟著去。

column1: The desert is full of perils for the unwary, and only brave men or fools dare go there. I used to travel through the desert often.
column2: 沙漠處處是陷阱,專等粗心大意的人,只有勇者或傻子才敢往那兒闖。我以前可是常常穿越沙漠的。

column1: When it comes to money, I am the person to ask! You will need to find the Moneychanger to trade over your coins before you can buy anything here.
column2: 講到錢哪,問我就對了!你得先找到錢莊兌換你的錢幣,才能在這裡買東西。

column1: It has been a long time since I have visited there, but I know this city like the back of my hand. It is to the east of us, off the Plaza of the Fighters. Or was that to the west?
column2: 我有好一陣子沒去那兒了,不過這座城我可是熟得跟自己的手背一樣。它就在我們東邊,鬥士廣場旁邊。還是說……是在西邊來著?

column1: Thieves' guild? What need have you for thieves? There is no such guild in this fair city!
column2: 盜賊公會?你找盜賊做什麼?咱們這座好城裡可沒這種公會!

column1: A place to relax, to talk to friends, to eat and drink, to watch entertainment! What better place to be?
column2: 一個能放鬆、能跟朋友聊天、能吃喝、能看表演的地方!還有哪兒比這更棒?

column1: In the day, it is hot... at night, it is cold. It is always the way this time of year.
column2: 白天熱……夜裡冷。每年這個時節向來如此。

column1: The fountain is the center of life to our city.
column2: 噴泉是我們這座城的生命中心。

column1: I have heard that in Raseir they now forbid people to speak to one another. How can one live in such a way?
column2: 我聽說在拉希爾,如今竟禁止人們彼此交談。這種日子叫人怎麼過?

column1: She is a very beautiful woman, and very attracted to me, I can tell. You will find her at the end of Dinar Tarik, a street to the... hum... I think to the west of here.
column2: 她是個極美的女子,而且我看得出來,她對我很有意思。你可以在第納爾塔里克街的盡頭找到她,那條街在……嗯……我想是在這兒的西邊吧。

column1: The Astrologer is a very important man here. He can see the future in the stars! You should go to him and find out your fortune, for is it not written, 'A man should know his fate?' His house is at the end of the Tarik of Stars.
column2: 占星師在這兒是個舉足輕重的人物。他能從星辰中窺見未來!你該去找他,問問你的命運,難道古話不是這麼寫的嗎——「人當知其命」?他的宅子就在星辰塔里克街的盡頭。

column1: It is said, 'The Poet sees the soul and lets it fly.' I, too, was a poet in my youth.
column2: 古話說:「詩人洞見靈魂,放它自由翱翔。」我年輕時,也曾是個詩人哪。

column1: I was always more trader than merchant, for I loved to travel too much.
column2: 我這人一向是行商多於坐賈,只因我太愛四處奔波了。

column1: The Katta make great neighbors and friends, and Shameen and Shema are the finest of Kattas!
column2: 卡塔族是極好的鄰居與朋友,而沙明和舍瑪,更是卡塔族裡頂頂出色的!

column1: He lives in the desert by the oasis. He is a very wise man.
column2: 他住在沙漠裡的綠洲旁。他是位非常有智慧的人。

column1: I am Abdulla Doo. It is an old family name.
column2: 我是阿布杜拉·杜。這是個古老的家族姓氏。

column1: Ah, the enchanting Shema! Her dance must be seen to be savored.
column2: 啊,迷人的舍瑪!她的舞,非親眼一見不足以細細品味。

column1: Like a cat, Shameen lands upon his paws wherever he goes. This inn is the Oasis of Culture for Shapeir.
column2: 沙明就像隻貓,走到哪兒都能穩穩落地。這間客棧,正是夏皮爾的文化綠洲。

column1: He was a generous and friendly man, and Raseir was a place worth living. Now Arus is lost, his brother sits on his throne, and Raseir stagnates.
column2: 他是個慷慨又和善的人,當年的拉希爾也是個值得安居的地方。如今阿魯斯下落不明,他的弟弟坐上了王位,拉希爾就此一蹶不振。

column1: There is no greater treasure than friendship.
column2: 世上沒有比友誼更珍貴的寶藏了。

column1: If you had not returned our stolen carpet and treasures, I would still be sitting there, wasting away to a mere shadow of myself.
column2: 若不是你找回了我們被偷走的飛毯與財寶,我至今仍會枯坐在那兒,日漸消瘦,只剩一具軀殼罷了。

column1: I have given the carpet to the Sultan, and in return he has made me wealthy beyond even my dreams.
column2: 我把那飛毯獻給了蘇丹,作為回報,他讓我富甲一方,連我做夢都不敢想。

column1: If I had not fought off so many in the battle in Spielburg Pass, perhaps you would not have made it through the brigand lair, my friend.
column2: 若不是當年我在史畢柏格隘口那一戰殺退了那麼多敵人,朋友,你或許就闖不過那山賊巢穴了。

column1: Why, it is the month of the Serpent, a time of ill omen.
column2: 哎呀,如今正值巨蛇之月,是個凶兆當頭的時節。

column1: This is the year of the Djinn. A most inauspicious time. Perhaps I should consult my friend the Astrologer.
column2: 今年是精靈之年,最是不吉的年份。也許我該去請教我那位占星師朋友。

column1: To speak of omens, you should talk to the Astrologer.
column2: 若要談起預兆,你該去找占星師談談。

column1: Shema's cooking is fantastic and her tea is heavenly. Truly the Katta are friends beyond measure.
column2: 舍瑪的廚藝一流,她沏的茶更是有如天賜。卡塔族真真是無可比擬的好朋友。

column1: Why, the very plazas of our city are a thriving marketplace.
column2: 哎呀,我們城裡的座座廣場,本身就是一處繁榮興旺的市集。

column1: You should get out and see the city. There are many people to meet and talk with in Shapeir.
column2: 你該出去走走,看看這座城。夏皮爾裡有許多人值得你結識、攀談。

column1: Magic is very much a part of life in Shapeir.
column2: 在夏皮爾,魔法可是生活中再尋常不過的一部分。

column1: The subject is too hot for me.
column2: 這個話題對我來說太燙手了。

column1: A dreadful Fire Elemental stalks the plazas, burning all it touches!
column2: 一頭可怕的火元素精怪在廣場間遊蕩,凡它所觸,無不焚毀!

column1: I regret that I cannot tell you what you wish to know.
column2: 很抱歉,你想知道的事,我沒法告訴你。

column1: It grieves me very much, but I do not know what to say.
column2: 這令我十分難過,可我實在不知該說什麼好。

column1: His music conveys the atmosphere of this land to all who listen.
column2: 他的樂音,將這片土地的氣息傳遞給每一位聆聽者。

column1: It is good to be here again. You will always be welcome in our inn for the help you have given that allowed us to return here.
column2: 能再回到這裡真好。多虧你出手相助,我們才得以回來,我們的客棧永遠歡迎你。

column1: All Katta in that city were driven out of their homes there. Only my cousin, Sharaf, did not leave. I fear for him.
column2: 那座城裡所有的卡塔族,都被趕出了家門。只有我的堂親沙拉夫沒走。我真為他擔心。

column1: I am not as skilled in the ways of magic as some. I know only the quiet magic of the dance, and of how to make an inn seem a home.
column2: 論魔法之道,我不如有些人那般精通。我只懂得舞蹈那份靜默的魔力,以及如何讓一間客棧有家的感覺。

column1: The Sultan here has always protected and welcomed our people.
column2: 這裡的蘇丹,向來庇護並接納我們族人。

column1: The old Emir of Raseir was a kind and gentle man. I cannot understand how the new Emir can be so cruel to our people.
column2: 拉希爾從前那位埃米爾,是個仁慈溫和的人。我實在不明白,新任的埃米爾怎能對我們族人如此殘酷。

column1: The Palace of the Sultan is east of our inn. You can see it over the gates from the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿在我們客棧的東邊。從宮殿廣場那兒,你能越過城門望見它。

column1: The journey to Raseir is very far and very dangerous. It can only be made by caravan. If you do journey there, please look for my cousin, Sharaf.
column2: 前往拉希爾的路途十分遙遠,也十分凶險,只能隨商隊同行。你若真要去那兒,請替我尋一尋我的堂親沙拉夫。

column1: There are many dangers in the desert. You must be very careful there.
column2: 沙漠裡危機四伏。你在那兒可得萬分小心。

column1: You have no need of money in our home. You will always be our guest.
column2: 在我們家裡,你用不著錢。你永遠是我們的座上賓。

column1: The Adventurer's Guild is northwest, off the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 冒險者公會在西北方,鬥士廣場旁邊。

column1: This is your home as well as ours.
column2: 這裡既是我們的家,也是你的家。

column1: I prefer our warm, dry air to the cold and wet of Spielburg.
column2: 比起史畢柏格那又濕又冷的天氣,我更喜歡咱們這兒溫暖乾爽的空氣。

column1: The fountain is directly north of here. It is the source for all our water.
column2: 噴泉就在這兒的正北方。我們所有的水都靠它供應。

column1: Be wary of her. It is said she uses men.
column2: 對她可得當心。聽說她慣於玩弄男人。

column1: He is a wonderful poet and he knows many things. We are most lucky that he comes here.
column2: 他是位了不起的詩人,而且博學多聞。他願意上這兒來,真是我們天大的福氣。

column1: What better job for a Katta than one where you sit all day in the warm sun and chatter with friends?
column2: 對卡塔族來說,還有什麼工作比這更好——整天坐在暖陽下,跟朋友東家長西家短?

column1: Our people were here long before your kind moved here. This land shall always be our home.
column2: 早在你們這族人遷來之前,我們族人就在這兒了。這片土地永遠是我們的家。

column1: My name, in the language of our people, means 'She of the Dance.'
column2: 我的名字,在我們族人的語言裡,意思是「舞之女」。

column1: His name means 'Honor.' He is very brave. It is said that he refused to leave Raseir so that he could work to bring freedom to his homeland again.
column2: 他的名字意為「榮譽」。他非常勇敢。聽說他拒絕離開拉希爾,只為留下來奔走,好讓自己的故土重獲自由。

column1: I have prepared it exactly to your liking, with only the freshest and most succulent ingredients. May you enjoy every bite.
column2: 我完全照你的口味備好了,用的全是最新鮮、最鮮嫩多汁的食材。願你吃得津津有味,一口都不放過。

column1: It is hoped that you will enjoy what I prepare. You have only to order it.
column2: 但願我做的菜能合你心意。你只管點便是。

column1: The tea is made from fresh herbs and honey and is brewed by the sun.
column2: 這茶是用新鮮香草與蜂蜜製成,靠日光慢慢沏出來的。

column1: We traveled to Spielburg in hopes of finding a hero to restore Raseir to our people.
column2: 我們遠赴史畢柏格,盼著能找到一位英雄,把拉希爾重新交回我們族人手中。

column1: If you had not returned our treasures to us, we would still be in that cold land, away from friends and family.
column2: 若不是你把財寶歸還給我們,我們至今仍會困在那片寒冷的土地上,遠離親朋故舊。

column1: All Katta are related in some manner to one another.
column2: 所有卡塔族,彼此之間多少都沾親帶故。

column1: It is a movement to overthrow the current government of Raseir and restore the old ways.
column2: 那是一場運動,旨在推翻拉希爾現今的政權,恢復昔日的舊制。

column1: He too, is our forever friend.
column2: 他也是我們永遠的朋友。

column1: The dance that I perform was, at one time, to ready our people for battle, and... ah... for fertility. Now it is only for fun.
column2: 我所跳的這支舞,曾有一段時日,是用來讓我們族人備戰之用,還有……啊……求子之用。如今嘛,純粹是為了好玩了。

column1: My nephew Shallah went to Tarna after the new emir took over Raseir's throne. It is similar to Shapeir in some ways, yet also very different. I hope he is doing well.
column2: 我的姪兒沙拉,在新任埃米爾奪下拉希爾王位後,去了塔爾納。那地方在某些方面與夏皮爾相似,可又截然不同。但願他一切安好。

column1: You throw Shema a subtle wink and try out your most playful pick-up line.
column2: 你朝舍瑪悄悄使了個眼色,試出你最俏皮的搭訕台詞。

column1: You are... ah... most kind to say such... ah... sweet things to me, Hero-friend.
column2: 你……呃……真是太體貼了,竟對我說這些……呃……甜言蜜語,英雄朋友。

column1: You try another pick-up line on Shema.
column2: 你又對舍瑪丟出另一句搭訕話。

column1: Shema merely gives you an uncomfortable glance and doesn't respond.
column2: 舍瑪只是不自在地瞥了你一眼,沒有回應。

column1: soul mate
column2: 靈魂伴侶

column1: life mate
column2: 終身伴侶

column1: Shameen is both my husband and my soul mate.
column2: 沙明既是我的丈夫,也是我的靈魂伴侶。

column1: If the new Emir hates Katta so, Sharaf could be in deadly trouble.
column2: 要是新任埃米爾這麼厭惡卡塔族,沙拉夫恐怕會有性命之憂。

column1: We typically eat such things for meals around here.
column2: 在我們這兒,這類東西平常就是拿來當飯吃的。

column1: The wild desert Saurus is quite tasty when properly prepared.
column2: 野生的沙漠索魯斯只要料理得當,味道相當不錯。

column1: I had them specially imported from Spielburg for you.
column2: 這些是我特地為你從史畢柏格進口來的。

column1: Katta are very wary of flames. Please be careful around it.
column2: 卡塔族對火焰非常戒慎,請你在它附近多加小心。

column1: Katta are very wary of flames.
column2: 卡塔族對火焰非常戒慎。

column1: People would come from far away to shop and browse in the bazaars of the Katta in Shapeir and Raseir. Now there is no bazaar at all in Raseir.
column2: 從前人們會大老遠跑來夏皮爾和拉希爾,逛卡塔族的市集、採買貨物。如今拉希爾連一座市集都沒有了。

column1: I worry for you in these dangerous times, Hero.
column2: 在這樣危險的時局裡,我很為你擔心,英雄。

column1: I only know that Elementals are dangerous. Perhaps others could tell you more.
column2: 我只知道元素精靈很危險。也許別人能告訴你更多。

column1: You will have to ask Abdulla about that.
column2: 那件事你得去問阿布杜拉。

column1: Shema looks shyly down and shakes her head.
column2: 舍瑪害羞地垂下頭,搖了搖。

column1: Forgive me, Hero-friend, but I am ignorant of such things.
column2: 請原諒我,英雄朋友,這種事我實在一無所知。

column1: In the daytime, you will find him playing his instrument in the very plaza outside our inn. In the evenings, he entertains our patrons here.
column2: 白天的時候,你會看到他在我們客棧外的廣場上彈奏樂器;到了晚上,他就在這裡為客人們表演。

column1: This city is but an island of beauty amidst the shifting sands and timeless mountains.
column2: 這座城市不過是流沙與亙古群山之間,一座美麗的孤島罷了。

column1: Elementals are dangerous and very destructive. Those wise in the ways of the elements could tell you more.
column2: 元素精靈危險而且極具破壞力。通曉元素之道的智者能告訴你更多。

column1: That city used to be but a mirror to Shapeir. Now it is said that it is a foreshadow of what is to come.
column2: 那座城市從前不過是夏皮爾的一面鏡子。如今有人說,它預示著即將到來的命運。

column1: There are many creatures in the desert who would seek to harm you.
column2: 沙漠裡有許多生物,個個都想加害於你。

column1: Katta magic is subtle and hidden from most people. We let you see our magic only because you are our friend.
column2: 卡塔族的法術細膩而隱秘,對大多數人從不外露。我們讓你見識我們的法術,只因你是我們的朋友。

column1: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid welcomed and sheltered the Katta driven from their homes in Raseir. His name will be honored forever by our people.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德接納並庇護了那些被逐出拉希爾家園的卡塔族。他的名字將永遠受我們族人景仰。

column1: Those driven from Raseir speak highly of the former Emir Arus al-Din. Of his brother, Ali Hasan, they merely spit.
column2: 那些被逐出拉希爾的人,對前任埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁讚譽有加;一提到他弟弟阿里·哈桑,卻只是啐上一口。

column1: The Sultan's Palace lies directly east of the Plaza of the Fountain. It is said that although no one sees the Sultan, the Sultan sees all.
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿就在噴泉廣場的正東方。據說,雖然沒人見得著蘇丹,蘇丹卻洞悉一切。

column1: Abdulla is the one to ask about that.
column2: 那件事該去問阿布杜拉。

column1: Sands that change colors with the time of day, the desert is dear to our people. However, it is best viewed at night. It is hard to survive the heat of the sun.
column2: 沙色隨著一天時辰流轉變幻,沙漠對我們族人而言彌足珍貴。不過,它最美還是在夜裡;白晝的烈日之下實在難以撐住。

column1: You have no need for your money in our inn. You will need to see the Moneychanger, however, before you can buy something in Shapeir.
column2: 在我們客棧裡你不必花錢。不過要在夏皮爾買東西,你得先去找錢莊兌幣的人。

column1: The Guild is west of the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 公會在噴泉廣場的西邊。

column1: This is truly what an inn should be: the center of all. With Shema to dance and the Poet Omar to entertain, there will always be something of interest here.
column2: 這才是客棧真正該有的樣子:萬眾匯聚的中心。有舍瑪起舞、有詩人奧瑪爾助興,這裡永遠都有值得一看的熱鬧。

column1: It is gloriously warm as always.
column2: 還是一如往常地暖洋洋,真教人舒坦。

column1: The pathway to the fountain is north of our plaza.
column2: 通往噴泉的小路就在我們廣場的北邊。

column1: I have heard that the armies the Sultan sent to Raseir were destroyed by a great wind.
column2: 我聽說蘇丹派往拉希爾的軍隊,被一陣狂風給摧毀了。

column1: The Moneychanger will change the money of Spielburg for the coins of Shapeir.
column2: 錢莊那人會把史畢柏格的錢,兌換成夏皮爾通用的錢幣。

column1: The Katta believe one's fate lies within oneself, not in the stars above.
column2: 卡塔族相信,一個人的命運操之在己,而非繫於天上的星辰。

column1: I have heard only that she is very knowing in the ways of magics.
column2: 我只聽說她對各種法術之道極為通曉。

column1: Poetry is revered in this land. The words of the Poet Omar speak both to the heart and to the mind.
column2: 在這片土地上,詩歌備受尊崇。詩人奧瑪爾的字句,既觸動人心,也啟迪心智。

column1: Katta are fine sellers of many things, for they know the true value of wealth.
column2: 卡塔族善於買賣各式各樣的貨物,因為他們深諳財富的真正價值。

column1: We are a little people and few, but like grains of sand, we will always belong to the desert.
column2: 我們是個渺小而稀少的民族,卻像一粒粒沙子,永遠屬於這片沙漠。

column1: Shameen, in the tongue of my kindred, means, 'He of the Lion.'
column2: 「沙明」這名字,在我族人的語言裡,意思是『屬於獅子的人』。

column1: There is none in all Shapeir to compare to my beautiful and wondrous mate.
column2: 放眼整個夏皮爾,沒有人比得上我那美麗又奇妙的伴侶。

column1: Abdulla may seem foolish at times, but there is much in what he says.
column2: 阿布杜拉有時看似憨傻,但他話裡可大有玄機。

column1: The Katta feel you should go where your heart leads you, and there you will find yourself.
column2: 卡塔族認為,你該去往內心指引的方向,在那裡你才會找到真正的自己。

column1: Shema believes he still lives, but from all I have heard of Raseir, I am doubtful.
column2: 舍瑪相信他還活著,但聽過種種關於拉希爾的傳聞後,我心裡是存疑的。

column1: We journeyed to Spielburg to bring back a hero.
column2: 我們遠赴史畢柏格,就是為了帶回一位英雄。

column1: I have heard the bazaar in Tarna is a very interesting place to be. It is almost as warm there as it is here, it is always filled with merchants and people from many different tribes and cultures gather there.
column2: 我聽說塔爾納的市集是個非常有意思的地方。那兒幾乎跟這裡一樣暖和,總是擠滿了商人,還匯聚了來自各個部族與文化的人們。

column1: Shema's nephew is a skilled artist who left Shapeir to try his luck in the land of Tarna to the west. It has been a long time since we have last heard from him.
column2: 舍瑪的外甥是個技藝精湛的畫匠,他離開夏皮爾,到西邊的塔爾納去闖蕩碰運氣。我們已經很久沒有他的消息了。

column1: When Shema dances, fires awaken. At least, for Katta.
column2: 舍瑪起舞時,火焰也隨之甦醒。至少對卡塔族來說是如此。

column1: To be truly wealthy, you must learn what it is to be content.
column2: 要真正富有,你必須先學會什麼叫知足。

column1: We are kin, the Liontaur race and the Katta.
column2: 獅人一族和卡塔族,我們是同源的親戚。

column1: He is my sister's eldest boy, and a fine cook in his own right.
column2: 他是我姊姊的長子,本身也是個出色的廚子。

column1: In the Plaza of the Fountain and other plazas, the Katta merchants set up their shops.
column2: 在噴泉廣場和其他幾座廣場上,卡塔族的商人都擺起了攤子。

column1: Your room awaits you, just off the passage behind me. You will have a place to stay here as long as you wish to be our guest.
column2: 你的房間就在我身後那條走道的旁邊,正等著你。只要你願意當我們的客人,這裡就一直有你落腳的地方。

column1: Whenever you are hungry, you have but to sit at your table and Shema will be honored to serve you. You are our guest.
column2: 無論何時餓了,你只管坐到自己的桌前,舍瑪會很榮幸地為你張羅。你是我們的貴客。

column1: Because you defeated the Brigands in Spielburg and returned our stolen treasure, we shall be forever in your debt. Joining us on our journey to Shapeir and helping us set up this inn has made you our friend, as well.
column2: 因為你在史畢柏格擊敗了強盜,把我們被偷走的寶物找了回來,我們將永遠欠你這份恩情。你一路陪我們到夏皮爾,又幫忙張羅起這間客棧,如今更成了我們的朋友。

column1: It is not good to speak of evil, but there is much of it in Raseir.
column2: 談論邪惡並非好事,但拉希爾如今正充斥著這樣的東西。

column1: There is much of which I have no knowledge. Forgive me.
column2: 有太多事是我不知道的,請你見諒。

column1: Ah, Hero of the North, I am but a Katta. I cannot speak upon what I do not know.
column2: 啊,來自北方的英雄,我不過是個卡塔族罷了。我不知道的事,實在無從說起。

column1: But in the Doer's[    arrogance and pride,[ The light grows ever stronger[    now outside,[ A hero strives to force[    the Darkness back,[ And into morning shall[   be Raseir's guide.
column2: 然而在那行事者的[    傲慢與自負之中,[ 城外的光芒[    正愈發熾亮,[ 一位英雄奮力[    逼退黑暗,[ 終將引領拉希爾[   走向黎明。

column1: The city of Raseir has been overcome by Darkness, yet there shall be a hero to return her to the light.
column2: 拉希爾城已被黑暗吞噬,但終將有一位英雄,把她重新帶回光明之中。

column1: Even as we left Spielburg valley, I heard that Elsa von Spielburg is now seeking a way to lift the curse and drive Baba Yaga from the land.
column2: 就在我們離開史畢柏格谷地時,我聽說艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格正在尋找辦法,要解除詛咒、把芭芭雅嘎逐出那片土地。

column1: I planned to defeat Baba Yaga myself, you know. I would have done so, had you not completed the task before me!
column2: 你知道嗎,我本來打算親手收拾芭芭雅嘎的。要不是你搶在我前頭把事辦成了,我早就動手啦!

column1: You're already seated.
column2: 你已經坐好了。

column1: You are indeed quite the hero, my friend.
column2: 你還真是個了不起的英雄,我的朋友。

column1: Yes, take a seat at the table behind you and Shema will be happy to serve you.
column2: 好的,在你身後那張桌子坐下吧,舍瑪會很樂意為你張羅。

column1: Little did we know that the weary adventurer who wandered into our Spielburg inn would be the very person to bring us back home.
column2: 我們當時萬萬沒想到,那個拖著疲憊身子走進我們史畢柏格客棧的冒險者,竟會是帶我們回家的人。

column1: It is good to be here again. The atmosphere of Shapeir is much friendlier than Spielburg. I can tell Shema is much happier now too. It grieved me to see her so homesick over there.
column2: 能再回到這裡真好。夏皮爾的氛圍比史畢柏格親切多了。看得出來舍瑪現在也開心多了。當初在那邊看她思鄉成那樣,我心裡實在難受。

column1: I cannot help but feel sorrow for the Baron. We departed Spielburg to much fanfare and in high spirits, yet to this day Baba Yaga's curse still hangs over him.
column2: 我實在忍不住為男爵感到難過。當年我們在一片歡送與興高采烈中離開史畢柏格,可直到今天,芭芭雅嘎的詛咒仍籠罩在他身上。

column1: It is a certainty Baron Stefan von Spielburg is grateful you managed to lift the curse of the vile one, Baba Yaga.
column2: 可以肯定的是,史蒂凡·馮·史畢柏格男爵一定很感激你,竟有本事解除那邪物芭芭雅嘎的詛咒。

column1: You tell Shameen about WIT and your decision to reject their proposal.
column2: 你向沙明說起 WIT,以及你拒絕他們提議的決定。

column1: Many things about your story are very hard to conceive, but I am happy you decided not to stay there. I have a strong feeling Shapeir still has need of your skills.
column2: 你故事裡有許多事都教人難以置信,不過我很高興你決定不留在那兒。我有種強烈的預感,夏皮爾仍然需要你的本事。

column1: That would probably result in you being kicked out of the inn.
column2: 那麼做大概只會害你被趕出客棧。

column1: Shema and I would have lost everything, had the thing of flames set our inn ablaze. We are greatly in your debt, Hero-friend.
column2: 要是那團火焰之物把我們的客棧給點著了,我和舍瑪可就一無所有了。我們欠你天大的恩情,英雄朋友。

column1: You tell about the Fire Elemental running rampant in the plaza outside. Shameen nods sadly.
column2: 你說起外頭廣場上那隻四處肆虐的火元素精靈。沙明難過地點了點頭。

column1: The knowledge that our inn is likely its first target is not easy to bear. Shema and I are not sure what to do.
column2: 一想到我們的客棧很可能是它的頭一個目標,這份心情實在不好受。我和舍瑪都不知道該怎麼辦才好。

column1: Though there is really nothing we can do to stop such magic, we do not feel comfortable leaving our home and possessions at the mercy of it.
column2: 雖然面對這種法術我們其實束手無策,可要把家園和家當全交給它擺布,我們心裡終究過不去。

column1: I am glad you managed to do what the Sultan's guards could not.
column2: 我很慶幸,連蘇丹的衛兵都辦不到的事,你竟然做到了。

column1: I do hope the Sultan is all right. Such Elementals do not make for pleasant neighbors.
column2: 我真心盼著蘇丹平安無事。那種元素精靈可不是什麼好相處的鄰居。

column1: The thing of rock and stone is quite fearsome indeed. People are afraid to walk the streets with it attacking everything in sight.
column2: 那團岩石巨物著實嚇人。它見什麼砸什麼,人們都不敢上街了。

column1: You are indeed brave to take on such a hulking mass of stone.
column2: 你竟敢去對付那麼一座龐然巨石,實在勇氣可嘉。

column1: It is hoped that you will forgive us for reducing our beverage servings. The city's water supply is already running low and must be conserved.
column2: 還望你見諒,我們不得不減少供應的飲品份量。城裡的供水已經告急,非得省著用不可。

column1: Every living being in the city now has a life-debt to you, Hero-friend. We are truly honored to have you as our guest.
column2: 如今城裡每一個活著的生靈,都欠你一條活命之恩,英雄朋友。能有你這位貴客,我們真是無上光榮。

column1: You tell Shameen that you enjoyed the journey to Shapeir on Abdulla's carpet.
column2: 你告訴沙明,你很享受坐著阿布杜拉的飛毯前往夏皮爾的那趟旅程。

column1: It was a memorable experience, was it not? Shema and I enjoyed every minute of it.
column2: 那真是段難忘的經歷,不是嗎?那一路上的每一刻,我和舍瑪都樂在其中。

column1: You tell about your battle with the Brigand inside the city.
column2: 你說起你在城裡與那名強盜的一場惡鬥。

column1: Someone who can elude the city guards for such a long time is a dangerous person indeed. It is good he will no longer prowl the streets of Shapeir from now on.
column2: 能躲過城衛兵這麼久的人,確實非常危險。從今往後他再也不能在夏皮爾的街頭出沒,真是太好了。

column1: You tell Shameen that the inn is a very comfortable place to stay and thank him for allowing you to reside here at their expenses.
column2: 你告訴沙明,這間客棧住起來十分舒適,並感謝他讓你白吃白住、落腳於此。

column1: Setting up this inn would have taken us considerably longer without your assistance. Shema and I would not have been able to come back to this land without your help. We are happy to return your favors in some small way.
column2: 要不是有你幫忙,我們張羅這間客棧得多花上好些時日。沒有你相助,我和舍瑪根本回不了這片土地。如今能略盡棉薄報答你的恩情,我們由衷歡喜。

column1: You tell Shameen that you greatly enjoyed watching Shema's dance.
column2: 你告訴沙明,你非常享受觀賞舍瑪的舞蹈。

column1: You should also tell her yourself. She will be very happy to hear it coming from one such as you.
column2: 你也該親口告訴她。從你這樣的人口中聽到這話,她一定會非常開心。

column1: Watching her dance always brings back very fond memories for me. I have been an admirer of her dancing long before I became her lifemate.
column2: 看她跳舞總勾起我許多美好的回憶。早在成為她的終身伴侶之前,我就一直是她舞藝的傾慕者。

column1: You tell Shameen that you enjoyed listening to Omar's poetry.
column2: 你告訴沙明,你很喜歡聆聽奧瑪爾的詩。

column1: As do we, my friend. Performances like his are what Shema and I truly missed in Spielburg.
column2: 我們也一樣,我的朋友。像他這樣的表演,正是我和舍瑪在史畢柏格時最懷念的東西。

column1: You tell Shameen about the decor items that you ordered from the merchants.
column2: 你向沙明說起你跟商人們訂購的那些裝飾擺設。

column1: Many Katta are fantastic artisans and craftsmen. Katta are known the world over for putting much love into their creations.
column2: 許多卡塔族都是了不起的工匠藝師。卡塔族在傾注心血於作品這件事上,可是天下聞名的。

column1: Such actions were very kind of you. I do not know of many people who would be willing to help a tree.
column2: 你這份心意實在仁厚。我可沒見過幾個人,願意去幫一棵樹的忙。

column1: You tell Shameen that the Saurus you used to ride around on is actually the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你告訴沙明,你先前一直騎著到處跑的那隻索魯斯,其實是中了魔法的埃米爾。

column1: Let us hope Aziza can find a way to restore Arus back to his former self. It would surely be horrible for him to spend the rest of his life as a mount.
column2: 但願阿姿莎能找到辦法,讓阿魯斯恢復原本的模樣。要他下半輩子都當一頭坐騎,那也未免太可怕了。

column1: You tell Shameen the food and drinks always taste extremely well.
column2: 你告訴沙明,這裡的飯菜和飲品總是好吃得不得了。

column1: Shema is an excellent cook, is she not? Perhaps you should tell her personally that you enjoy her cooking.
column2: 舍瑪是個出色的廚子,不是嗎?或許你該親口告訴她,你很喜歡她做的菜。

column1: al scurva
column2: 阿爾·斯庫瓦

column1: You tell Shameen about how you saved Al Scurva.
column2: 你向沙明說起你如何搭救了阿爾·斯庫瓦。

column1: Some are simply without manners. His actions almost tempt me to consider your own a waste of a valuable dispel potion.
column2: 有些人就是沒半點教養。他那副德性,差點讓我覺得你那瓶珍貴的驅散藥水根本是白費了。

column1: It is rare to see a Liontaur around these parts. Most of them are too proud to mingle with other species.
column2: 在這一帶能見到獅人可不常見。他們大多自視甚高,不屑與其他種族來往。

column1: You tell Shameen about what you've done in the city so far.
column2: 你向沙明說起你至今在城裡做過的種種事蹟。

column1: There is always much to do around here. I hope your adventures will still afford you some room to relax and enjoy yourself.
column2: 這附近總有忙不完的事。我盼著你的冒險之餘,還能留點空檔讓你放鬆放鬆、好好享受。

column1: She is not seen in public very often, but she is widely known for her wisdom and the Sultan often consults her for advice on matters concerning the arcane.
column2: 她很少在公開場合露面,卻以智慧聞名遐邇,蘇丹遇到玄奧之事,也常向她請教。

column1: You tell Shameen about the rope-walking challenge.
column2: 你向沙明說起那場走繩索的挑戰。

column1: I remember we used to hold such challenges specifically for Katta. Have you ever tried crossing a rope that was partially on fire?
column2: 我記得我們從前還特地為卡塔族辦過這類挑戰呢。你可曾試過走過一條半截著火的繩索?

column1: Ah, that is indeed quite a tale, my friend.
column2: 啊,這還真是段精彩的故事,我的朋友。

column1: thank katta
column2: 感謝卡塔族

column1: thank her
column2: 感謝她

column1: thank shema
column2: 感謝舍瑪

column1: Normally, I would have been excited to see a new and unfamiliar land. But the town of Spielburg was small and we could not venture outside because of the monsters in the forest.
column2: 換作平常,能見識一片陌生的新天地,我本該興奮才對。可史畢柏格鎮太小了,森林裡又有怪物,我們根本不敢往外踏一步。

column1: Shameen and I did not adjust well with Spielburg's citizens. I barely went outside. There was nowhere to venture and I could not grow accustomed to the cold climate. The only entertainment venue was that seedy tavern.
column2: 我和沙明跟史畢柏格的居民實在合不來。我幾乎足不出戶,既沒地方可去,又適應不了那寒冷的天氣。唯一能消遣的地方,就只有那間破破爛爛的小酒館。

column1: I am most happy to be back in Shapeir again. It is you who made that possible.
column2: 能再回到夏皮爾,我打從心底高興。這一切都是你成全的。

column1: It grieves me very much that Baba Yaga remains there to torment the populace. If only we could have done more.
column2: 芭芭雅嘎仍盤踞在那兒折磨百姓,這讓我萬分痛心。要是我們當初能多做點什麼就好了。

column1: You were most brave and wonderful to defeat such an evil user of magic, my Hero.
column2: 你竟能擊敗那麼一個邪惡的施法者,真是勇敢又了不起,我的英雄。

column1: You tell Shema about WIT and your decision to reject their proposal.
column2: 你向舍瑪說起 WIT,以及你拒絕他們提議的決定。

column1: Those wizards could do so much good with their powers and yet they choose to remain isolated. Why?
column2: 那些巫師明明能用他們的力量造福世人,卻偏偏選擇自我封閉。這是為什麼呢?

column1: I am happy you remained down-to-earth.
column2: 我很高興你依然這麼腳踏實地、不擺架子。

column1: Shema would most likely be very disappointed with you if you told her about that.
column2: 要是你把那件事告訴舍瑪,她八成會對你大失所望。

column1: Yes, everyone is talking about it. Thank you very much for saving our beloved inn.
column2: 是啊,大家都在議論這件事。太感謝你救了我們心愛的客棧。

column1: Even the prospect of losing our home, possessions, and source of income worried me greatly.
column2: 光是想到可能會失去家園、家當和生計來源,就已經叫我憂心忡忡了。

column1: I only wish that we could repay your bravery with more than food and shelter.
column2: 我只盼著,我們能用比飯食和住處更多的東西,來報答你的勇氣。

column1: You tell about the Fire Elemental running rampant in the plaza outside. Shema looks on the verge of tears.
column2: 你說起外頭廣場上那隻四處肆虐的火元素精靈。舍瑪聽著,眼眶都快泛淚了。

column1: I hope we are not going to lose our home. Where shall we live?
column2: 我真盼著我們別失去這個家。不然往後該住到哪兒去呢?

column1: You tell Shema that you'll find a way to get rid of the Fire Elemental.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你會想辦法除掉那隻火元素精靈。

column1: Thank you. I really hope you will.
column2: 謝謝你。我真心盼著你能辦到。

column1: So you have the thing of air with you now? Is that safe?
column2: 這麼說,那團風之物現在就在你身上?這樣安全嗎?

column1: You assure Shema that the Air Elemental is now harmless.
column2: 你向舍瑪保證,那隻風元素精靈如今已經無害了。

column1: May you keep close guard of it. If such a thing ended up in the wrong hands, misfortune would surely start all over again.
column2: 願你把它看守得緊緊的。這種東西要是落到壞人手裡,災禍勢必又會從頭再來一遍。

column1: Yes, the wind can be heard and felt throughout the entire city. I would not wish to live near the palace at the moment.
column2: 是啊,整座城裡都聽得見、感受得到那陣風。眼下我可不想住在宮殿附近。

column1: I heard that the mighty Liontaur from the Guild fought it, but he was forced to retreat. I hope he recovers soon, for there has been no other brave enough to contest it.
column2: 我聽說公會那位威猛的獅人跟它交過手,卻被逼得不得不撤退。但願他能早日康復,因為再沒有別人敢去跟它較量了。

column1: I have been hearing all sorts of stories about it already.
column2: 關於這件事,我已經聽到各式各樣的說法了。

column1: Some of them more exaggerated than others.
column2: 有些版本可比別的誇張多了。

column1: Shameen and I have decided to be very careful with the little water we still have left.
column2: 我和沙明已經打定主意,要把剩下的這點水省著用、格外當心。

column1: Once our current supply runs out, we shall have no more.
column2: 眼下這點存量一旦用完,我們就再也沒有水了。

column1: Many people forget how valuable the fountain really is, until we risk losing it.
column2: 很多人都忘了那座噴泉究竟有多寶貴,直到我們快要失去它的那一刻。

column1: You tell Shema that you enjoyed the journey to Shapeir on Abdulla's carpet. Her face brightens up as you mention the journey.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你很享受坐著阿布杜拉的飛毯前往夏皮爾的那趟旅程。你一提起那段旅程,她的臉龐頓時亮了起來。

column1: Was it not exciting? Before we traveled to Spielburg, I always wondered how it would be to fly through the air and watch the land from above.
column2: 那不是很刺激嗎?在我們動身前往史畢柏格之前,我總是好奇,翱翔天際、俯瞰大地會是什麼滋味。

column1: I had hoped that Abdulla would not sell the carpet to the Sultan. I would love to re-experience such a ride once more.
column2: 我原本還盼著阿布杜拉別把飛毯賣給蘇丹呢。我真想再體驗一回那樣的飛行。

column1: I thought the stories about a Brigand preying on people inside the city walls were mere rumors. It is good you managed to defeat such a dangerous man.
column2: 我本以為城牆內有強盜專挑人下手的傳言,不過是空穴來風。你竟能擊敗那麼一個危險的傢伙,真是太好了。

column1: You tell Shema that the inn is a very comfortable place to stay and thank her for allowing you to reside here free of charge. She smiles.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,這間客棧住起來十分舒適,並感謝她讓你免費落腳於此。她微微一笑。

column1: It is the least Shameen and I could do after you helped us return to our homeland and set up the Katta's Tail.
column2: 在你幫我們返回故土、張羅起「卡塔之尾」客棧之後,這已經是我和沙明所能盡的最起碼的心意了。

column1: I am most pleased to hear that you are content with what we have to offer.
column2: 聽到你滿意我們所能提供的一切,我打從心底高興。

column1: You tell Shema that you greatly enjoyed watching her dance.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你非常喜歡看她跳舞。

column1: Thank you. It is nice to hear such praise from people other than Shameen.
column2: 謝謝你。能聽到沙明以外的人這樣誇獎,真開心。

column1: I grew quite nervous during my first performance at our new inn. I was afraid that I had forgotten the correct techniques. I have not danced for such a long time.
column2: 在我們新客棧第一次表演時,我緊張得不得了,深怕自己把正確的舞步都忘光了。我已經好久好久沒跳舞了。

column1: Shameen occasionally asked me to dance for him in Spielburg. But with the cold weather and the brigands forcing us to remain in such a depressing environment, I did not feel like dancing there.
column2: 在史畢柏格時,沙明偶爾會要我跳給他看。但那裡天寒地凍,又有強盜逼得我們困在那種令人沮喪的環境裡,我實在沒心情跳舞。

column1: You apologize to Shema for missing her dance yet again.
column2: 你向舍瑪道歉,說自己又一次錯過了她的舞。

column1: I understand that the life of an adventurer is eventful and hectic, even at the best of times. Do not worry yourself over it, Hero.
column2: 我明白,冒險者的生活就算在最好的時候,也是事多又忙碌。別為這件事放在心上,英雄。

column1: Despite Shema's gracious acceptance, you can tell by the crestfallen look in her eyes that she hides her disappointment well.
column2: 儘管舍瑪大方地接受了,你還是從她眼中那抹失落看得出來——她只是把失望藏得很好罷了。

column1: You apologize to Shema for missing her last dance.
column2: 你向舍瑪道歉,說自己錯過了她上一場的舞。

column1: No matter, Hero. I shall dance again for you another night.
column2: 沒關係的,英雄。改天晚上我會再為你跳一支。

column1: You tell Shema that you would very much like to watch her dance.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你非常想看她跳舞。

column1: Thank you, Hero-friend. Sometimes I dance in the evenings, right here at the Katta's Tail.
column2: 謝謝你,英雄朋友。我有時會在傍晚跳舞,就在這「卡塔之尾」客棧裡。

column1: She seems like a good person, although maybe a little bit intimidating too.
column2: 她看起來是個好人,不過或許也有點讓人不敢造次。

column1: You tell Shema that you enjoyed listening to Omar's poetry.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你很喜歡聽奧瑪爾吟詩。

column1: He is very good. I am very glad that he comes here. It is nice sometimes to sit back and enjoy another's performance.
column2: 他真的很厲害。我很高興他願意來這裡。偶爾能放鬆下來,欣賞別人的表演,也是一種享受。

column1: You tell Shema about the decor items you ordered from the merchants.
column2: 你向舍瑪提起你向商人們訂購的那些擺飾。

column1: I hope the results of the Katta merchants' efforts please you well. I know that much time and care is put into their craftwork.
column2: 希望卡塔商人們的心血成果能讓你滿意。我知道他們的手工活兒可是花了好多時間與心思的。

column1: What a touching story, Hero-friend. I do hope she will regain her human form someday. Such a tale lacks its 'happily ever after' ending.
column2: 多麼動人的故事啊,英雄朋友。我真心希望她有朝一日能恢復人形。這樣的故事,還缺了個「從此幸福快樂」的結局呢。

column1: You tell Shema that the Saurus you used to ride around on is actually the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,你以前騎來騎去的那頭索魯斯,其實是被施了法的埃米爾。

column1: I am so glad to hear he is still alive. But what an embarrassing ordeal for him.
column2: 聽到他還活著,我真是太高興了。不過這對他來說,可真是難堪的一場折磨。

column1: I hope you will be careful in Raseir. I would hate to see you return in a similar form.
column2: 希望你到拉希爾時多加小心。我可不想看到你也變成那副模樣回來。

column1: You notice the hint of a smile on Shema's face as she ponders that last notion.
column2: 你注意到舍瑪一邊細想著最後那句話,嘴角一邊浮起一絲微笑。

column1: You tell Shema the food and drinks always taste extremely well.
column2: 你告訴舍瑪,這裡的菜餚和飲料總是美味得不得了。

column1: Thank you. I am glad you enjoy what I prepare. If something does not taste well, please say so.
column2: 謝謝你。你喜歡我做的東西,我很開心。要是哪樣不合口味,請儘管說。

column1: I would not want you to spend all your money at the stand of Scoree and Sloree, after all.
column2: 畢竟,我可不希望你把錢全花到斯柯里和斯洛里的攤子上去。

column1: You tell Shema about how you saved Al Scurva.
column2: 你向舍瑪講起你如何救了阿爾·斯庫瓦。

column1: What a rude man. I am not surprised at how one with such a temperament ended up in his predicament.
column2: 真是個粗魯的傢伙。脾氣這種的人會落得那種下場,我一點也不意外。

column1: You tell Shema about what you've done in the city so far.
column2: 你向舍瑪說起你至今在城裡做過的種種事蹟。

column1: I am sure that you shall enjoy your stay in Shapeir, despite the difficult times.
column2: 我相信,儘管時局艱難,你還是會喜歡待在夏皮爾的。

column1: Word has it he was once a great king, yet he lives the life of a commoner. How humble he must be.
column2: 聽說他曾是位偉大的國王,如今卻過著平民的日子。他想必是個極謙卑的人。

column1: You tell Shema about the rope-walking challenge.
column2: 你向舍瑪提起走繩索的挑戰。

column1: I would not mind trying this, but that performer does not accept challenges from Katta. We are far too nimble.
column2: 我倒是不介意試試,可那個表演者不接受卡塔族的挑戰。我們實在太靈巧了嘛。

column1: A thousand thanks for that wonderful tale, Hero. I am always impressed by your adventures.
column2: 那精彩的故事,真要謝你千百回,英雄。你的冒險總是讓我嘆服不已。

column1: thank them
column2: 向他們道謝

column1: thank poet
column2: 向詩人道謝

column1: thank omar
column2: 向奧瑪爾道謝

column1: thank jaafar
column2: 向賈法爾道謝

column1: It is hoped you enjoyed your first sights of our beautiful city of Shapeir.
column2: 但願你會喜歡初次見到我們美麗的夏皮爾城的這番景象。

column1: If you will be seated, then you will see my Shema dance.
column2: 只要您入座,便能看我家舍瑪起舞。

column1: The Poet will soon speak, if you will but be seated.
column2: 只要您入座,詩人很快便會開口。

column1: If it would please[the Hero so,[Then there is that[which he should know.[A simple prize[from Sultan wise,[For deeds of air,[the land does owe.
column2: 若英雄[願聞其詳,[有一樁事[該讓他知曉。[一份薄禮[出自睿智蘇丹,[為那馭風之功,[此地深感虧欠。

column1: From the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elemental of Air.
column2: 謹代蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願他萬世治世!),奉上此囊,以酬謝你對抗風之元素時最英勇無畏的功勳。

column1: You receive the reward of 50 dinars.
column2: 你獲得了五十第納爾的賞賜。

column1: The Sultan of Shapeir[has now decreed,[That this hero is a hero[here indeed.[For actions fundamental[in dispelling Elementals,[A reward for he who freed[us with all speed.
column2: 夏皮爾的蘇丹[今已昭告天下,[這位英雄[確是名副其實的英雄。[因其驅散元素[立下根本之功,[特賞這位[飛速解我們於危難之人。

column1: From the Sultan Harun al-Rashid (May He Reign Forever!), we present thee with this purse for thy services most brave and daring against the Elementals of Earth and Water.
column2: 謹代蘇丹哈倫·拉希德(願他萬世治世!),奉上此囊,以酬謝你對抗土與水之元素時最英勇無畏的功勳。

column1: You receive the reward of 100 dinars.
column2: 你獲得了一百第納爾的賞賜。

column1: Hero-friend, a most strange fire has been seen outside the inn. Take care as you leave.
column2: 英雄朋友,客棧外頭出現了一團最詭異的火焰。你出去時可要小心。

column1: The Poet has personally asked that you be present tonight, for he wishes to speak with you after his reading.
column2: 詩人親自交代,希望你今晚務必到場,因為他想在朗誦結束後與你談談。

column1: I fear, Hero-friend of the Katta, that Shapeir again needs your aid. There is talk that an Elemental of the Earth is destroying the city.
column2: 卡塔的英雄朋友,只怕夏皮爾又得仰仗你的相助了。外頭傳言,有個土之元素正在摧毀這座城。

column1: The Enchantress Aziza sends you this message of gratitude and requests that you visit her home tomorrow.
column2: 女術士阿姿莎託我捎來這封致謝的口信,並請你明日到她家中一趟。

column1: It is said that Uhura at the Guild Hall has a message for you as well.
column2: 聽說公會大廳的烏胡拉也有話要傳給你。

column1: It is said that Uhura at the Guild Hall has a message for you.
column2: 聽說公會大廳的烏胡拉有話要傳給你。

column1: Hero, the Enchantress Aziza requests that you visit her home this day.
column2: 英雄,女術士阿姿莎請你今日到她家中一趟。

column1: Welcome, Hero.
column2: 歡迎你,英雄。

column1: Welcome, Hero. It is good to see you here safe and sound.
column2: 歡迎你,英雄。看到你平安無事地回到這裡,真好。

column1: The inn is filled with Katta merchants who wish you luck upon your departure.
column2: 客棧裡擠滿了卡塔商人,都來為即將啟程的你送上祝福。

column1: You know you will always have a place to stay and fine food to eat in Shapeir. Shameen, Shema, and I cannot thank you enough for helping us recover our stolen treasure.
column2: 你知道,在夏皮爾,你永遠有個落腳處,有美食可享用。沙明、舍瑪和我,真不知該如何謝你幫我們尋回被偷走的寶藏。

column1: It is good to be back home, even though I have had troubling news. My old friend Arus al-Din, the Emir of Raseir, is missing. He was a good man, but not as wise as I.
column2: 能回到家鄉真好,儘管我收到了令人憂心的消息。我的老友、拉希爾的埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁失蹤了。他是個好人,只是沒我那麼睿智罷了。

column1: Ah, but you have not even seen our beautiful Shapeir, and already I speak of problems. You need to go out, explore and discover why this is indeed a land of enchantment.
column2: 唉,你都還沒好好看過我們美麗的夏皮爾,我就先談起這些煩心事了。你該出去走走,探一探,親自發掘為何這裡真是一片充滿魔法的土地。

column1: I have something to give you, Hero-friend.
column2: 我有樣東西要交給你,英雄朋友。

column1: Shema and I will be locking the door soon. We do not wish to shut you out, so please take our spare key with you. It will allow you to enter the inn at any time. Please take care not to lose it.
column2: 舍瑪和我很快就要鎖門了。我們可不想把你關在外頭,所以請帶上我們這把備用鑰匙吧。有了它,你隨時都能進客棧來。請務必小心,別弄丟了。

column1: After long and busy days, Shema and I like to share a cup of herbal tea before heading to bed. If you have no other plans for the night, why not join us?
column2: 忙碌漫長的一天過後,舍瑪和我喜歡在就寢前共享一杯花草茶。今晚你若沒別的安排,何不一起來坐坐?

column1: Unfortunately, the kitchen is already closed but if you are thirsty we would be honored to share some of our herbal tea with you.
column2: 很可惜,廚房已經打烊了,不過你若口渴,我們很榮幸能分你一些花草茶。

column1: Good evening, Hero-friend. Unfortunately, the kitchen is already closed but if you are thirsty we would be honored to share some of our herbal tea with you.
column2: 晚安,英雄朋友。很可惜,廚房已經打烊了,不過你若口渴,我們很榮幸能分你一些花草茶。

column1: I fear that you are not likely to receive many patrons today, for the thing of fire that attacked the merchants yesterday has returned and seems to have no intention of leaving the plaza anytime soon.
column2: 只怕你今天招不到幾位客人,因為昨天襲擊商人的那團火,又回來了,而且看樣子一時半刻也沒打算離開廣場。

column1: Abdulla, you should not have placed yourself in danger by coming here today. Had the thing of fire attacked you, you surely would have been killed.
column2: 阿布杜拉,你今天實在不該冒這個險跑來這裡。萬一那團火襲擊了你,你準沒命。

column1: The Fire Elemental was indeed heading toward me, and just as I was preparing to run for the door, the gate guard entered and started yelling and waving at it. It seemed to draw the creature's attention, for it immediately chased after him. I do not think it caught him, but what a reckless thing to do!
column2: 那火之元素確實朝我衝來,就在我正準備往門口逃時,城門衛兵衝了進來,對著它又喊又揮手。這似乎引開了那怪物的注意,它立刻轉頭追了過去。我想它應該沒追上他,可這種事做起來也太魯莽了!

column1: Ah... quite so. But I am happy to hear that he was unharmed.
column2: 啊……正是如此。不過聽到他毫髮無傷,我也就放心了。

column1: Good morning, Abdulla. It is good to see you at our inn once again.
column2: 早安,阿布杜拉。又能在我們客棧見到你,真好。

column1: Good morning. It pleases me to see that this inn was spared the fury of the Fire Elemental.
column2: 早安。看到這間客棧逃過火之元素的肆虐,我真高興。

column1: It will take some time to remove the burn marks from the plaza outside, but we are happy that the extent of the damage was not too great.
column2: 要清掉外頭廣場上的焦痕還得花上一些時間,不過所幸災情不算太嚴重,我們已經很慶幸了。

column1: Shema's tea will be in high demand today, for a creature of water has appeared in the fountain and the guards have told all merchants and citizens to stay away.
column2: 舍瑪的茶今天肯定供不應求,因為噴泉裡冒出了一隻水之怪物,衛兵已經叫所有商人和市民通通走避了。

column1: That is alarming news. We shall have to conserve our water supplies and serve tea sparingly. I hope no one will mind.
column2: 這消息可真叫人心驚。我們得省著用水,供茶也得節制些了。但願大家不會見怪。

column1: Ah, I must admit running all the way over here to tell you the news has made me quite thirsty, but I understand.
column2: 唉,我得承認,一路跑來向你報這消息,讓我渴得不得了,不過我能體諒。

column1: Good morning, Abdulla. It is good to see you at our inn once again. It has been more than a week since we last saw you.
column2: 早安,阿布杜拉。又能在我們客棧見到你,真好。距離上回見你,已經一個多禮拜了。

column1: Things have been busy since I returned to Shapeir. But no business should ever get in the way of opportunities to relax and talk with friends.
column2: 自從我回到夏皮爾,事情就一直忙個不停。不過再忙的生意,也不該耽誤了放鬆、跟朋友聊天的機會。

column1: Everybody's asleep, Hero. Since there's nobody around to prepare meals or even talk back, the best thing you can do is hit the sack as well.
column2: 大家都睡了,英雄。既然沒人能幫你張羅吃的,連個搭話的人都沒有,你最好也趕緊去睡吧。

column1: Soon Shapeir will be awash in flames. Hero, you must save us all again!
column2: 夏皮爾就快被火海吞沒了。英雄,你得再一次拯救我們大家!

column1: I must be sure to consult the Astrologer about the Fire Elemental. It must certainly be a bad omen.
column2: 我一定得去請教占星師關於火之元素的事。這肯定是個不祥之兆。

column1: Alas, again our great city is in peril! The most feared of all Elementals has been unleashed upon us -- the Water Elemental!
column2: 唉,我們的偉大城市又陷入危難了!所有元素中最令人聞風喪膽的那一個,如今降臨在我們頭上——水之元素!

column1: It is a good thing that you are around to save us, %s.
column2: 幸好有你在身邊救我們,%s。

column1: There's nothing left on the tray.
column2: 托盤上什麼都不剩了。

column1: Aside from your water, you have nothing to drink. You would no doubt receive more tea with a meal.
column2: 除了你的水之外,你沒別的可喝了。要是點份餐,肯定就能再添些茶。

column1: This is the table you usually eat your meals at. There is a green pillow on the floor near it, inviting you to sit and relax.
column2: 這是你平常用餐的桌子。旁邊地上擺著一個綠色坐墊,彷彿在招呼你坐下歇歇腳。

column1: You should sit down first before ordering a meal.
column2: 你該先坐下,再點餐。

column1: Stealing lamps may cast you in a bad light.
column2: 偷油燈可會讓你顯得形象很差喔。

column1: No djinni in this one.
column2: 這盞裡頭可沒有精靈。

column1: There's no need to turn the tables on anybody.
column2: 犯不著跟誰翻桌動氣。

column1: You should leave the lamps on the tables for the benefit of other inn patrons.
column2: 你該把油燈留在桌上,方便客棧裡的其他客人。

column1: The Saurus oil in the lamps is not drinkable.
column2: 油燈裡的索魯斯油可不能喝。

column1: Your friends are letting you stay at their inn for free. No need to test the limits of their kindness by starting a pillow fight in here.
column2: 你的朋友讓你免費住在他們的客棧裡。犯不著在這裡掀起一場枕頭大戰,去試探他們善意的底線。

column1: The curtains are just there for ambiance. There's no need to close them.
column2: 這些簾子只是用來營造氣氛的,沒必要把它們拉上。

column1: People generally don't have a high opinion of drunkards talking to their bottle, but they hold sober ones in even lower esteem.
column2: 人們一般都瞧不起對著酒瓶說話的醉漢,但對清醒著還這麼做的人,評價只會更低。

column1: The tea, as Abdulla has told you many times, is wonderful.
column2: 這茶,正如阿布杜拉跟你說過好多次的,棒極了。

column1: Your breakfast is delicious.
column2: 你的早餐很美味。

column1: You have a wonderful meal. There are numerous salads, two main courses, fruit, hot mint tea, dessert, and all the bread you could want. You feel stuffed and content.
column2: 你享用了一頓豐盛的大餐。各式沙拉、兩道主菜、水果、熱騰騰的薄荷茶、甜點,還有想吃多少就有多少的麵包。你只覺得肚子撐得飽飽的,心滿意足。

column1: Fortunately, your strenuous regimen as a hero prevents you from looking as round as Abdulla.
column2: 所幸,身為英雄那一套操勞的鍛鍊菜單,讓你不至於吃得像阿布杜拉那樣圓滾滾。

column1: After a meal like that, you feel it will be a struggle even to make it to bed. But, what the hey, you're a hero, you can do it!
column2: 吃完那樣一頓,你只覺得連挪到床邊都是場硬仗。不過,管它的,你可是英雄,這點事難不倒你!

column1: Fortunately, heroes never get bellyaches.
column2: 所幸,英雄是從來不會鬧肚子痛的。

column1: Unfortunately,  you never really cared for sauerkraut and knockwurst, but you are too polite to tell Shema that. You must admit the honey and lemons on the knockwurst aren't too bad, but the sauerkraut curry is a bit much.
column2: 可惜,你向來不怎麼愛吃酸菜配大香腸,只是你太有禮貌,沒好意思跟舍瑪說。你得承認,大香腸淋上蜂蜜和檸檬還算不賴,但那酸菜咖哩就有點過頭了。

column1: There is an empty teapot and teacup on the tray.
column2: 托盤上擺著一只空茶壺和一只空茶杯。

column1: It makes your mouth water.
column2: 這讓你直流口水。

column1: The tea looks delicious and smells faintly of mint leaves.
column2: 這茶看起來很可口,還隱隱透著薄荷葉的清香。

column1: Tables magically producing food on command only appear in fairytales.
column2: 桌子一聲令下就變出食物,那種事只會出現在童話裡。

column1: You have a wonderful meal. There are numerous salads, two main courses, fruit, dessert, and all the bread you could want. You feel stuffed and content.
column2: 你享用了一頓豐盛的大餐。各式沙拉、兩道主菜、水果、甜點,還有想吃多少就有多少的麵包。你只覺得肚子撐得飽飽的,心滿意足。

column1: A few crumbs on the tray are all that remains of your meal.
column2: 托盤上只剩幾粒碎屑,那就是你這頓飯僅存的痕跡了。

column1: There is no more food left. You'll have to order some more.
column2: 沒剩半點吃的了。你得再點一些才行。

column1: This pillow is reserved for the musician who performs at the inn during the evening hours.
column2: 這個坐墊是留給傍晚時段在客棧表演的樂師的。

column1: The newly refurbished inn has few stories to tell.
column2: 這間剛翻修過的客棧,還沒什麼故事好說。

column1: It is a patron of the inn, drinking and dining, no doubt.
column2: 那是客棧的一位客人,八成正在喝酒用餐。

column1: If you start bothering the paying customers of the establishment, you may end up as one yourself.
column2: 你要是去騷擾店裡那些付錢的客人,搞不好最後自己也得變成其中一個(乖乖付錢)。

column1: The patron is busy dining. Don't bother him.
column2: 那位客人正忙著用餐。別去打擾他。

column1: The tea looks delicious and a sense of relaxation comes over you as its aroma enters your nostrils.
column2: 這茶看起來很可口,當它的香氣飄進鼻間,一股放鬆的感覺也隨之湧上你心頭。

column1: Your friends would probably not mind sharing some tea with you, but you would probably offend them if you drank it without sitting down first.
column2: 你的朋友大概不介意分你一些茶,但你要是連坐都沒坐下就喝,恐怕會冒犯了他們。

column1: You applaud long and hard for your friend Shema's dancing prowess.
column2: 你為好友舍瑪精湛的舞藝,賣力地鼓了好久好久的掌。

column1: In the month of the Serpent,[in the year of the Djinn[A shadow passed over the Katta's Tail Inn[Astrologers foretell that doom shall come to dwell[And Shapeir shall be but sand upon the wind.
column2: 時值巨蛇之月,[正逢精靈之年,[一道暗影掠過「卡塔之尾」客棧。[占星師預言厄運將降臨棲身,[而夏皮爾終將化作隨風飄散的黃沙。

column1: Comes a hero from the north,[riding on the very air,[And this sign is the first to then beware,[For Darkness soon shall fall and shadow cover all,[The city and the ones now living there.
column2: 一位英雄自北方而來,[駕馭著虛空之風,[此乃首要堪憂之兆,[因黑暗即將降臨、暗影籠罩一切——[這座城,以及如今居於其中的人們。

column1: The first doom shall be fire,[which shall burn the very stone.[The next is air, and rocks are overthrown.[Earth shall be the third,[then the final doom is heard,[The water gone, the city parched like bone.
column2: 頭一場厄運是火,[連石頭都將被它燒灼。[接著是風,巨岩為之傾覆。[土為第三場,[而後最終的厄運傳來,[水盡城枯,乾涸如白骨。

column1: Unless the one called Hero is a hero true indeed,[Who comes to help the city in its need,[Then will face the depths of doom[in the darkness of the tomb,[From the Elemental's Master, we are freed.
column2: 除非那名喚英雄之人,[真是名副其實的英雄,[在這城危難之際前來相助,[否則我們將墜入厄運的深淵、[葬身於墓穴的黑暗,[再無人能使我們從元素之主手中解脫。

column1: Came a hero drawing near[to our land of fair Shapeir,[The Katta called and carried that one here.[I shall tell an allegory['bout a quest for love and glory[Which I shall relate if you will lend an ear.
column2: 一位英雄漸漸走近[我們美麗的夏皮爾之地,[卡塔族呼喚並將他帶到了這裡。[我要說一則寓言,[講的是一場追尋愛與榮耀的旅程,[只要您願側耳傾聽,我便道來。

column1: The Hero first faced fire[which was making matters dire,[For the flames he fought were flitting ever higher.[With water first he fought it[and into a lamp he caught it['Ere our city of Shapeir became our pyre.
column2: 英雄首先面對的是火,[把局勢逼到了萬分危急,[因他所戰的烈焰越竄越高。[他先以水迎戰,[再將它收入一盞油燈之中,[趕在夏皮爾城淪為我們的火葬堆之前。

column1: The next he faced was air,[which I'm sure that you're aware,[Was blustering and blowing here and there.[Before the winds became our bane[as a fearsome hurricane,[With a bellows then he caught it fair and square.
column2: 他接著面對的是風,[我想您也清楚,[它正肆虐狂吹、四處橫行。[趕在這風化作可怖颶風、[成為我們的禍根之前,[他用一具風箱,堂堂正正地將它擒住。

column1: So let us raise a cheer for the Hero of Shapeir,[And remember that our praise is quite sincere,[For in the quest for glory as related by this story,[Is a hero who has saved us from our fear,[Is a hero who's a hero without peer!
column2: 那就讓我們為夏皮爾的英雄歡呼喝采,[也請記得,我們的讚頌出自至誠,[因在這故事所述的榮耀追尋之中,[有一位英雄,將我們從恐懼裡拯救出來,[有一位英雄,是無人能及的英雄!

column1: In the month of the Serpent,[in the year of the Spear,[Darkness passed over the town of Raseir,[And the rumor had been said[that the old Emir was dead,[For Arus al-Din had seemed to disappear.
column2: 時值巨蛇之月,[正逢長矛之年,[黑暗掠過了拉希爾城,[而坊間早有傳言,[說老埃米爾已然身故,[因阿魯斯·阿丁似乎就此人間蒸發。

column1: The Emir had a brother,[who quickly took the throne,[but the shadow lurking under soon was known[For the Katta were exiled,[and Raseir was then defiled,[By the rules and regulations that were shown.
column2: 埃米爾有個兄弟,[迅速登上了王位,[但潛伏其下的暗影很快便為人所知。[卡塔族遭到流放,[拉希爾隨之被玷污,[只因那一道道頒布出來的律令與規章。

column1: The Sultan sent his men to restore Raseir to right,[But they did not even have a chance to fight,[For as they crossed the land,[a tremendous storm of sand,[Forced his forces to retreat from their plight.
column2: 蘇丹派出他的人馬,[要把拉希爾重新導回正軌,[但他們連交手的機會都沒撈著,[因在他們橫越大地之際,[一場滔天的沙暴,[逼得他的軍隊只能從困境中撤退。

column1: So now the rumor's told of the city of Raseir,[That the Darkness shadows everything in fear.[And He that now does rule has the Emir as his tool,[Disobedience brings punishment severe.
column2: 如今關於拉希爾城的傳言是這麼說的:[黑暗以恐懼籠罩著一切。[而那如今當權之人,把埃米爾當作他的工具,[但凡違逆,便招來嚴酷的懲處。

column1: The moral of this story as the best of tales must tell[Of the horrors of the orders that befell,[Is the simple corollary that the Hero must be wary,['Ere He journeys to that shadow's citadel,[And we bid him luck and wish him to fare well.
column2: 這故事的寓意,正如最上乘的故事所必須道出的——[講的是那些降臨的律令何其可怖,[其簡明的結論便是:英雄務必步步為營,[趕在他踏上前往那暗影堡壘的旅程之前。[我們祝他好運,願他一路順遂平安。

column1: As you travel to Raseir, %s, know that all of us in Shapeir wish for your safety and the success of your mission.
column2: %s,當你動身前往拉希爾時,請記得,我們夏皮爾的每一個人都祈願你平安,也祝你此行任務圓滿成功。

column1: Not now, Hero... the caravan is about to depart.
column2: 現在不行,英雄……商隊就要出發了。

column1: You store the food rations inside. You remind yourself to take them out of there again before they grow legs and walk out on their own.
column2: 你把口糧收了進去。你提醒自己,得趁它們長出腳、自己走掉之前,記得把它們再拿出來。

column1: You put the x-ray glasses away.
column2: 你把那副透視眼鏡收了起來。

column1: You store the container with oil.
column2: 你把裝著油的容器收了起來。

column1: There's not much point in filling your storage chest with rocks.
column2: 把你的儲物箱塞滿石頭,實在沒什麼意義。

column1: You put the Djinni ring away. Wait a second! You're cheating, aren't you?
column2: 你把那枚精靈戒指收了起來。等等!你在作弊吧,是不是?

column1: You store the unguent in your chest.
column2: 你把那罐藥膏收進了箱子裡。

column1: Hero, it is time for you to awaken. Even now, the caravan to Raseir is preparing to depart.
column2: 英雄,該醒了。此刻,前往拉希爾的商隊正準備啟程呢。

column1: Hero, it is time for you to leave. Even now, the caravan to Raseir is preparing to depart.
column2: 英雄,該動身了。此刻,前往拉希爾的商隊正準備啟程呢。

column1: Once you leave Shapeir, you will be on your own. We can but give you some supplies to make your journey easier.
column2: 一旦你離開夏皮爾,就得靠你自己了。我們頂多只能給你備些物資,讓你的旅途好過一些。

column1: These rations should last you for one more week. I have also prepared an extra set of clothes for you.
column2: 這些口糧應該夠你再撐一個禮拜。我還替你準備了一套換洗的衣服。

column1: I have prepared an extra set of clothes for you.
column2: 我替你準備了一套換洗的衣服。

column1: Even though this is your room, you're still a guest here and your hosts may be displeased if you start throwing magic around in their home.
column2: 就算這是你的房間,你終究還是這裡的客人,你的主人要是看你在他們家裡施起法來,恐怕會不太高興。

column1: This is no time to practice magic. The caravan is about to depart.
column2: 現在可不是練法術的時候。商隊就要出發了。

column1: You put the frame with the dried flowers on the wall in one corner of your room.
column2: 你把那只裝著乾燥花的相框,掛到房間一角的牆上。

column1: You put the brass jug on the display.
column2: 你把那只黃銅水罐擺到展示架上。

column1: You put the brass bottle on top of the closet.
column2: 你把那只黃銅瓶子放到衣櫃頂上。

column1: You carefully hang the weaving on the wall.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把那幅織品掛到牆上。

column1: You put the crafted pot in one corner of the room.
column2: 你把那只手工陶罐擺到房間的一角。

column1: The text of the bumper sticker seems especially suitable for your storage chest, so you stick it to its side.
column2: 那張保險桿貼紙上的字,似乎特別適合貼在你的儲物箱上,於是你把它貼到了箱子側面。

column1: You give the box a place near the storage chest. You wonder if this will ever become a collector's item.
column2: 你替那只盒子在儲物箱旁邊安了個位置。你不禁納悶,這玩意兒哪天會不會變成收藏家的搶手貨。

column1: You put the fan on the window sill.
column2: 你把那把扇子擱到了窗台上。

column1: You place the trash can some distance away from your bed, so that you can toss pebbles into it in the event of boredom.
column2: 你把垃圾桶放在離床有點距離的地方,無聊的時候就可以朝裡頭丟小石子打發時間。

column1: You put the sand dome on top of the closet, so you can look at it while lying on your bed.
column2: 你把那顆沙之圓頂擺在衣櫃上頭,這樣躺在床上就能欣賞它。

column1: It would be prudent to retrieve any items from your storage chest that you may require in Raseir. Things are not so readily available there, so you must leave prepared.
column2: 最好把可能會在拉希爾用到的東西先從儲物箱裡拿出來。那裡可不好補貨,出發前得備齊才行。

column1: You decide to inspect your possessions, just to make certain you didn't leave anything important behind.
column2: 你決定清點一下身上的家當,確認沒漏掉什麼重要的東西。

column1: It is a storage facility with a near-unlimited holding capacity, cleverly disguised as an ordinary wooden chest. This handy piece of furniture will allow you to store all those items that "may come in handy later."
column2: 這是一座容量近乎無限的儲物裝置,巧妙地偽裝成一只普通木箱。有了這件方便的家具,那些「以後說不定用得上」的東西就有地方擺了。

column1: There's nothing you need to get off your chest right now.
column2: 眼下沒有什麼非從箱子裡拿出來不可的東西。

column1: Hanging on the wall is a frame filled with hundreds of dried flowers, forming a picture of the city of Shapeir under a starry sky.
column2: 牆上掛著一只畫框,裡頭用上百朵乾燥花拼出了星空下的夏皮爾城。

column1: Hanging on the wall is a frame filled with hundreds of dried flowers, forming a picture of a blossoming Julanar.
column2: 牆上掛著一只畫框,裡頭用上百朵乾燥花拼出了綻放的茱拉娜爾。

column1: The frame looks fine where it is. There's no point in lugging it around with you.
column2: 畫框掛在那兒挺好的,沒必要拖著它到處跑。

column1: A small but elegant emerald-colored rug adorns the floor.
column2: 地上鋪著一張小巧卻雅致的翠綠色地毯。

column1: A small but elegant sapphire-colored rug adorns the floor.
column2: 地上鋪著一張小巧卻雅致的寶藍色地毯。

column1: A shiny brass jug sits atop the display in your room.
column2: 房裡的陳列架上擺著一只閃亮的黃銅水壺。

column1: A healthy-looking plant adorns one corner of your room.
column2: 房間一角點綴著一盆長得生氣勃勃的植物。

column1: A finely crafted weaving decorates the wall near your bed.
column2: 床邊的牆上掛著一幅做工精細的織錦。

column1: Saba has created a weaving with a picture of you on it.
column2: 薩巴織了一幅有你身影的織錦。

column1: The pot in the corner is the result of Toshur's craftsmanship.
column2: 角落那只陶罐是托舒爾的手藝。

column1: Your storage chest is decorated by the bumper sticker from Sloree and Scoree. It says: "Back off man, I'm a professional hero."
column2: 你的儲物箱上貼著斯洛里與斯科里送的保險桿貼紙,上頭寫著:「閃邊去,老兄,我可是專業英雄。」

column1: While the box is merely a creation of cardboard, you feel like this may someday be a valuable collector's item.
column2: 雖然這只是個硬紙盒,你卻有種預感,它哪天說不定會變成值錢的收藏品。

column1: Keapon's fan would do a good job providing your room with additional fresh air... if only there was a power socket around!
column2: 奇朋的那台風扇若能替房裡送點新鮮空氣倒是不錯……可惜這附近連個插座都沒有!

column1: You have the feeling that this trash can may be worth even less than its potential contents.
column2: 你有種感覺,這個垃圾桶說不定比它裡頭可能裝的東西還不值錢。

column1: Sand is gently whirling around inside the sand dome.
column2: 沙之圓頂裡頭,細沙正輕輕地打著旋兒。

column1: An elegant brass jar is sitting on top of the closet.
column2: 衣櫃上頭擺著一只雅致的黃銅罐子。

column1: This room is significantly bigger than your room at the Hero's Tale Inn in Spielburg. The large bed in the center looks comfortable and the interior of the room itself is surprisingly cool, compared to the heat outside.
column2: 這個房間比你在史畢柏格英雄傳奇旅店住的那間大上許多。正中央那張大床看起來挺舒服,而且跟外頭的炎熱相比,房裡頭竟然出奇地涼爽。

column1: This room is significantly bigger than your room at the Hero's Tale Inn in Spielburg. The large bed in the center looks comfortable and the window lets in enough moonlight to make out your surroundings.
column2: 這個房間比你在史畢柏格英雄傳奇旅店住的那間大上許多。正中央那張大床看起來挺舒服,窗戶透進來的月光足夠你看清四周。

column1: You detect not the slightest hint of dust on the walls and floor. Shema keeps a clean house.
column2: 牆上和地上連一絲灰塵都找不到。舍瑪把家裡打理得一塵不染。

column1: The room is spacious, but not large enough to produce an echo.
column2: 房間挺寬敞,但還沒大到會起回音的地步。

column1: It's not locked, and there's nothing inside anyway.
column2: 沒上鎖,反正裡頭也空空如也。

column1: The skyline of the city is visible through the window.
column2: 透過窗子可以望見城市的天際線。

column1: The window is not large enough to crawl through.
column2: 這扇窗子不夠大,鑽不過去。

column1: Good evening, Shapeir!
column2: 夏皮爾,晚安啦!

column1: Hello, Shapeir!
column2: 哈囉,夏皮爾!

column1: Colorful rugs decorate the area around your bed and the chest.
column2: 床邊和箱子四周鋪著色彩繽紛的地毯。

column1: Don't bother lugging the rugs around.
column2: 別費勁拖著這些地毯到處跑了。

column1: They're not magical.
column2: 它們又沒有魔力。

column1: The perfect place to take a nap after a hard day of adventuring.
column2: 辛苦冒險一整天後,這兒可是打個盹的絕佳去處。

column1: There's no reply.
column2: 沒有任何回應。

column1: A small brass bottle has been placed within a niche in the wall.
column2: 牆上的壁龕裡擺著一只小巧的黃銅瓶。

column1: The bottle is empty and not useful enough to carry around.
column2: 瓶子是空的,也沒什麼用處,犯不著帶在身上。

column1: There's no Djinni inside.
column2: 裡頭可沒有精靈。

column1: The display case near the entrance of the room allows the occupant to store large objects.
column2: 房門口附近的陳列櫃,可讓房客存放大型物品。

column1: The closet is empty. The storage chest contains enough room for all your belongings.
column2: 衣櫃是空的。儲物箱的空間足夠放下你所有的家當。

column1: A closet built into the wall allows the occupant of the room to store additional clothes. It is currently empty.
column2: 牆裡嵌著一座衣櫃,可讓房客多存放些衣物。目前是空的。

column1: Green curtains add to the color of the room.
column2: 綠色的窗簾替房間增添了幾分色彩。

column1: It would be awkward carrying a carpet around with you. It would be best to leave it where it is.
column2: 帶著一張地毯到處跑可不方便,還是讓它留在原處吧。

column1: While the jug looks pretty, it has no practical use to you. There's no reason to remove it from its current spot.
column2: 這水壺雖然漂亮,對你卻沒什麼實際用處,沒道理把它從原位拿走。

column1: While the jar looks pretty, it has no practical use to you. There's no reason to remove it from its current spot.
column2: 這罐子雖然漂亮,對你卻沒什麼實際用處,沒道理把它從原位拿走。

column1: The plant doesn't fit in your backpack very well. Besides, it's fine where it is.
column2: 這盆植物塞不太進你的背包,再說它擺在那兒好好的。

column1: The weaving does a better job at decorating the room than it would decorating the inside of your backpack.
column2: 這幅織錦用來裝飾房間,可比塞進你背包裡好看多了。

column1: It would be a waste using this pot as a common container. You decide to leave it where it is.
column2: 拿這只陶罐當普通容器用實在太可惜了,你決定讓它留在原處。

column1: There's no way to remove the sticker without tearing it. You decide to leave it where it is.
column2: 這貼紙撕下來鐵定會破,你決定讓它留在原處。

column1: Carrying the box inside your backpack for too long might damage the delicate disks inside. You decide to leave the game box where it is.
column2: 把盒子裝在背包裡太久,恐怕會弄壞裡頭脆弱的磁片。你決定讓這只遊戲盒留在原處。

column1: The fan may not be of much use in your room, but it'll be of even less use in your backpack.
column2: 這台風扇擺在房裡或許派不上多大用場,可塞進背包裡只會更沒用。

column1: Unless you get a job as part of the local clean-up crew, there's no point in carrying a trash can with you.
column2: 除非你打算去當本地的清潔隊員,否則帶著個垃圾桶到處跑實在沒道理。

column1: You would only risk breaking it if you were to place it inside your backpack again.
column2: 要是再把它塞回背包裡,只會冒著把它弄破的風險。

column1: Your staying power diminished, Issur takes advantage of the opportunity to slam your hand to the anvil.
column2: 你的耐力一弱,伊蘇爾就趁機把你的手壓向鐵砧。

column1: You can feel Issur weaken from the great effort it has taken to overcome your staying power.[[You take advantage of the opportunity to slam his hand to the anvil.
column2: 你感覺到伊蘇爾為了壓制你的耐力,使盡了力氣而漸漸鬆懈下來。[[你抓住機會,把他的手壓向鐵砧。

column1: You slam Issur's hand to the anvil.
column2: 你把伊蘇爾的手壓向鐵砧。

column1: Issur slams your hand to the anvil.
column2: 伊蘇爾把你的手壓向鐵砧。

column1: The anvil, not being a door, is unaffected by your spell.
column2: 鐵砧又不是門,你的咒語對它起不了作用。

column1: Silly you, the door is already open.
column2: 你真傻,這門早就開著了。

column1: You'll have to get the anvil out of the way first.
column2: 你得先把鐵砧挪開才行。

column1: There's something heavy preventing the trapdoor from opening...
column2: 有個沉甸甸的東西壓著,活板門打不開……

column1: Oh, it's you! Stand somewhere else.
column2: 噢,是你啊!站到別的地方去。

column1: You open the trapdoor to reveal a hole in the floor. You can see Issur's strongbox inside the hole.
column2: 你打開活板門,露出地板上的一個洞。你可以看見伊蘇爾的保險箱就在洞裡。

column1: You got it! The chest is now open.
column2: 搞定!箱子打開了。

column1: You find 100 dinars and 500 centimes, which you happily put away.
column2: 你找到了一百第納爾和五百生丁,樂呵呵地收進了口袋。

column1: Unfortunately, it appears that someone has already stolen all of its contents. Now who would have done such a thing?
column2: 可惜,看來裡頭的東西早就被人偷個精光了。到底是誰幹的好事呢?

column1: That's funny -- your Open spell doesn't seem to work on the already-open strongbox. Oh, yeah, that's right -- it isn't supposed to. You resolve to be more diligent about your studies in the future.
column2: 真好笑——你的「開啟」咒語似乎對已經打開的保險箱不管用。噢對,沒錯——本來就不該管用嘛。你下定決心以後讀書要更用功點。

column1: The anvil is far too heavy to move.
column2: 這鐵砧太重了,搬不動。

column1: The strongbox is much too heavy to move.
column2: 這保險箱太重了,搬不動。

column1: This isn't a good place to practice your Levitate spell.
column2: 這兒可不是練習「漂浮」咒語的好地方。

column1: The guards in Shapeir are nothing if not efficient. You took too much time, and one of Shapeir's finest caught you in the act of this decidedly unheroic pursuit.
column2: 夏皮爾的衛兵辦事可一點都不含糊。你磨蹭得太久,被一名夏皮爾的精銳衛士當場逮個正著——幹這種毫不英雄的勾當。

column1: Caught red-handed
column2: 當場人贓俱獲

column1: You can't move the anvil -- the trapdoor is in your way.
column2: 你搬不動鐵砧——活板門擋著路呢。

column1: You move the anvil aside. Now you can get to the trapdoor.
column2: 你把鐵砧挪到一旁。現在搆得著活板門了。

column1: The anvil is so heavy you can't even budge it. Better work on those muscle-building exercises.
column2: 這鐵砧重得你連動都動不了。看來你得多練練肌肉了。

column1: You've returned the anvil to its original position to hide your illicit activity.
column2: 你把鐵砧搬回原位,好掩蓋你那見不得人的勾當。

column1: It is the only door to the shop.
column2: 這是進這家店的唯一一道門。

column1: You can just see the small wooden trapdoor underneath the anvil.
column2: 你勉強瞥見鐵砧底下有一扇小小的木製活板門。

column1: The small wooden trapdoor is now accessible.
column2: 那扇小小的木製活板門現在搆得著了。

column1: Issur's strongbox is in this hole in the floor.
column2: 伊蘇爾的保險箱就在地板上的這個洞裡。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the trapdoor.
column2: 別費勁跟活板門講話了。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the strongbox.
column2: 別費勁跟保險箱講話了。

column1: You can't open the strongbox. It's locked.
column2: 你打不開這保險箱,它上鎖了。

column1: You close the trapdoor.
column2: 你把活板門關上。

column1: You close and lock the strongbox.
column2: 你把保險箱關上並鎖好。

column1: There's no point in trying that here.
column2: 在這兒這麼做沒什麼意義。

column1: The trapdoor doesn't have a lock to pick.
column2: 這活板門根本沒有鎖可以撬。

column1: Not much point in that! The chest is already open.
column2: 這麼做沒啥意義!箱子早就開著了。

column1: The lock is too tricky for you. You'll have to keep practicing.
column2: 這鎖對你來說太刁鑽了,你還得多練練。

column1: You spread some oil around the base of the anvil.
column2: 你在鐵砧底座四周抹了些油。

column1: The anvil is oiled.
column2: 鐵砧抹好油了。

column1: Open the door if you want out.
column2: 想出去的話就把門打開。

column1: The coat of mail is very heavy and too easily recognizable. You decide to leave it alone.
column2: 這件鎖子甲又重又太顯眼,你決定別碰它。

column1: Issur's weapons and armor are very distinctive. You would not be able to use or sell them without being caught.
column2: 伊蘇爾的武器和盔甲特徵太鮮明。你要是拿去用或拿去賣,鐵定會被逮個正著。

column1: The Tisk tusk is firmly attached to the wall with Tisk tusk tacks. Tsk, tsk!
column2: 提斯克獠牙被提斯克獠牙釘牢牢地釘在牆上。嘖,嘖!

column1: The shield seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這面盾牌似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: The trophy seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這座獎盃似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: There is nothing interesting about the fireplace.
column2: 這座壁爐沒什麼好看的。

column1: The rack seems to be firmly attached to the wall.
column2: 這個架子似乎被牢牢地固定在牆上。

column1: The smaller sword on the rack looks to be of fine workmanship.
column2: 架子上那把較小的劍看起來做工精良。

column1: It contains neither fun nor monkeys.
column2: 裡頭既沒樂子,也沒猴子。

column1: There's no reason to scrape the bottom of the barrel... yet!
column2: 還沒到非得刮桶底搜刮的地步……目前還沒!

column1: It's not a spittoon.
column2: 這又不是痰盂。

column1: A cheery selection of sharp iron tools and instruments rest near the fireplace.
column2: 壁爐邊擺著一排令人眼花的鋒利鐵製工具和器械。

column1: Assorted tools of the ironworker's trade adorn the premises.
column2: 店裡頭擺滿了打鐵這行各式各樣的工具。

column1: None of these items look particularly valuable.
column2: 這些東西看起來都不怎麼值錢。

column1: There are swords, shields, and many other weapons on the wall. This shop seems well-equipped to do business.
column2: 牆上掛著刀劍、盾牌,還有許多別的兵器。這家店看來行頭齊全,生意做得起來。

column1: You have no need for the metalworker's tools.
column2: 你用不著這些金工工具。

column1: The trough contains water used for cooling heated metals.
column2: 這個水槽裡裝的是給燒熱的金屬降溫用的水。

column1: You can't drink this water.
column2: 這水你可不能喝。

column1: Surely you can find a more appropriate place to fill your waterskin.
column2: 你肯定能找到個更合適的地方來裝滿你的水囊。

column1: Don't get that wet.
column2: 別把那弄濕了。

column1: The interior of the weaponer's shop is stuffy and humid, even during the chilly desert nights.
column2: 兵器店的內部又悶又潮,就連在寒涼的沙漠夜裡也是如此。

column1: It is the shop's sign which hangs in the plaza.
column2: 那是這家店的招牌,掛在廣場上。

column1: It seems to be located in the plaza, not in the shop.
column2: 它似乎是擺在廣場上,不在店裡頭。

column1: That would be a heavy conversation.
column2: 那可會是場沉重的對話。

column1: You bellow at the bellows, but it doesn't make them mellow. What a strange fellow!
column2: 你朝著風箱大吼大叫,風箱卻一點兒也沒變得溫順。真是個怪傢伙!

column1: The bellows are not in here.
column2: 風箱不在這裡頭。

column1: It doesn't require voice activation.
column2: 這玩意兒可不需要聲控啟動。

column1: It's the only way out.
column2: 這是唯一的出路。

column1: Yep. They're tools, all right.
column2: 沒錯。它們的確是工具沒錯。

column1: There's no need to pick the lock on the door.
column2: 沒必要撬這道門的鎖。

column1: Thanks, pal. Easy money.
column2: 謝啦,老兄。輕鬆賺。

column1: Ha! Easiest %d dinars I ever made. Let me know if you want to try again.
column2: 哈!這是我賺過最輕鬆的 %d 第納爾。想再來一局就跟我說一聲。

column1: Aw right. Anyone can get lucky. Here's your dough.
column2: 好吧好吧。誰都有走運的時候。錢拿去。

column1: All right, you can have the stupid bellows. See if I care.
column2: 行了行了,那破風箱給你就是了。我才不在乎呢。

column1: All right, you can have the bellows.
column2: 好吧,風箱歸你了。

column1: He gets the bellows down for you and throws them at you.
column2: 他替你把風箱取了下來,然後朝你扔了過來。

column1: So the hero thinks he can restore Raseir to its former glory? Ho, ho. This I've gotta see!
column2: 這麼說,這位英雄以為自己能讓拉希爾重現昔日榮光?嗬,嗬。這我倒非看不可!

column1: So you're gonna restore Raseir to its former glory? Don't give 'em an inch. Show 'em who's boss!
column2: 這麼說,你打算讓拉希爾重現昔日榮光?可別讓他們得寸進尺。讓他們瞧瞧誰才是老大!

column1: Hero of the Katta, huh? Only a Katta would call you a hero.
column2: 卡塔族的英雄,呵?也只有卡塔族才會把你當英雄看。

column1: What's up?
column2: 什麼事啊?

column1: How about another arm wrestling match, buddy?
column2: 再來比一場腕力如何,老兄?

column1: What a stubborn fool. Just can't admit to yourself that I'm so much better than you at skills of strength.
column2: 真是個倔脾氣的蠢蛋。就是不肯承認論力氣這玩意兒,我比你強上一大截。

column1: I could use a work out. Wanna arm wrestle me again?
column2: 我正好想活動活動筋骨。想再跟我比比腕力嗎?

column1: Well, Curly Locks, time for your daily defeat.
column2: 喲,捲毛兄,又到了你每天認輸的時候啦。

column1: The world meets nobody halfway. When you want something, you gotta take it.
column2: 這世道可不會對誰客氣。想要什麼,就得自己去搶。

column1: You know, we could save a lot of time if you just give me your money instead of losing it at arm wrestling.
column2: 我說啊,你乾脆直接把錢給我,省得拿去比腕力輸掉,還能替咱倆省下不少工夫呢。

column1: How's your arm feeling? Hope I wasn't too hard on you last time.
column2: 你那胳膊還好吧?但願我上回沒把你整得太慘。

column1: You again? Come to taste defeat once more, no doubt.
column2: 又是你?八成是來再嘗一次落敗的滋味的吧。

column1: Let's see how well you've been training those biceps today.
column2: 來瞧瞧你今天那二頭肌練得怎麼樣了。

column1: Ah, easy money. So you want to make me rich?
column2: 啊,輕鬆財。這麼說你是想讓我發大財囉?

column1: You win some, you lose some. Maybe one day you'll get lucky.
column2: 有贏有輸嘛。說不定哪天你就走運了。

column1: Well, wimp, it's about time you dare to pit your puny skill against mine at arm wrestling.
column2: 喲,軟腳蝦,你總算敢拿你那點微末本事,來跟我比比腕力了。

column1: Is today your lucky day? Put down your money and let's see.
column2: 今天是你的幸運日嗎?把錢押上,咱們瞧瞧便知。

column1: It's the fool and his money again.
column2: 又是這蠢蛋帶著他的錢來了。

column1: I hoped you would come back to test my mighty wrestling prowess.
column2: 我正盼著你回來,見識見識我這身了得的角力本領呢。

column1: Ho, ho. The beardless wonder has come to be put in his place again, I see.
column2: 呵,呵。看來這位沒長鬍子的小子又自己送上門來討教訓了。

column1: What do you want now?
column2: 你又想幹嘛?

column1: You back again? I'll let you stay if you want to buy something, but don't push it.
column2: 你又來啦?想買東西就讓你待著,別給我得寸進尺。

column1: You still owe us 5 dinars, grunt! Pay up before we decide to toss you out!
column2: 你還欠我們五個第納爾呢,菜鳥!趕快付清,不然我們可要把你扔出去了!

column1: You still owe us 5 dinars! Pay up before we decide to toss you out!
column2: 你還欠我們五個第納爾!趕快付清,不然我們可要把你扔出去了!

column1: You pay Issur 5 dinars for your newly acquired membership in the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 你付給伊蘇爾五個第納爾,正式加入了永恆戰士團。

column1: Issur shakes his head.
column2: 伊蘇爾搖了搖頭。

column1: Issur mumbles something profane.
column2: 伊蘇爾嘟囔了句髒話。

column1: That's not nearly enough. Pay %d for it or forget it.
column2: 差得遠呢。付 %d 才行,不然就免談。

column1: That's the best price I can do.
column2: 這是我能給的最低價了。

column1: That's it! Go and swindle somebody else.
column2: 夠了!想坑人去坑別人吧。
