column1: Wait a minute! What do you think I am, a moneychanger? Your money is no good. You need to get this changed to dinars and centimes before you can buy anything.
column2: 等等!你當我是錢莊嗎?你這錢在這兒不能用。先去把它換成第納爾和生丁,才能買東西。

column1: You don't have enough money!
column2: 你的錢不夠!

column1: Hey! You don't have the money for this. What do you think this is, a charity? Get some money or get lost!
column2: 喂!你根本付不起。你當這裡是慈善機構嗎?有錢再來,不然滾遠點!

column1: There you go.
column2: 拿去吧。

column1: Issur seems to make a well-balanced dagger, even if he does make you pay for the quality.
column2: 伊蘇爾打的匕首手感平衡得很,雖說這品質得讓你掏不少錢。

column1: Having traded in your old sword and paid the money, you now have one of Issur's swords. For all his bragging, he does make a fine weapon.
column2: 你用舊劍折抵又補了錢,現在拿到了一把伊蘇爾打的劍。別看他愛吹噓,武器確實是好貨。

column1: Time to go home. I'm closing up.
column2: 該回家了。我要打烊啦。

column1: Time to go home. I'm closing up. Beat it!
column2: 該回家了。我要打烊啦。快滾!

column1: You already beat me fair and square.
column2: 你早就堂堂正正贏過我了。

column1: Go away. I've got better things to do than waste my time on you.
column2: 走開。我可沒空浪費在你身上。

column1: You want to wrestle, eh? Just to make it worth my while to put you in your place, why don't we make a little wager, say, 1 dinar? Put down your money and we'll play.
column2: 想比腕力是吧?既然要我費神教訓你,不如下個小注,就一個第納爾如何?把錢押上,咱們就開始。

column1: It'll cost you more to bet me this time. You'll have to put down %d dinars.
column2: 這回跟我賭可貴囉。你得押上 %d 個第納爾。

column1: It will cost you %d dinars to wrestle me for the bellows.
column2: 想為了那風箱跟我比腕力,得押 %d 個第納爾。

column1: You tell Issur about the Air Elemental blowing around near the palace and ask if you may borrow his bellows.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起在宮殿附近亂吹的氣元素,問他能不能借用風箱。

column1: Well, since you put it that way, I guess you do need the bellows. I'll get them for you.
column2: 嗯,你都這麼說了,看來你還真用得上那風箱。我去拿給你。

column1: He gets the bellows down for you and you put them away. Good thing your spell lasted long enough.
column2: 他把風箱取下來給你,你收進行囊。還好你的法術撐得夠久。

column1: OK, OK. I'll make it easier on you. You only have to pay me 10 dinars if you lose at arm wrestling.
column2: 好吧好吧。我放你一馬。腕力輸了你只要付我十個第納爾就好。

column1: OK, OK. Since you are such a wimp, you only have to pay me 10 dinars if you lose at arm wrestling.
column2: 好吧好吧。看你這麼孬,腕力輸了你只要付我十個第納爾就好。

column1: You have told me that story already!
column2: 這故事你已經跟我講過啦!

column1: Do tell. Well, if you want to use my bellows, then you've got to prove your worth. You'll have to defeat me at arm wrestling. You win, you get the bellows. You lose, I get 20 dinars.
column2: 說來聽聽。好吧,想用我的風箱,就得拿出真本事。你得在腕力上贏過我。你贏了,風箱歸你;你輸了,二十個第納爾歸我。

column1: You try to tell Issur about the Air Elemental blowing around near the palace.
column2: 你試著跟伊蘇爾說起在宮殿附近亂吹的氣元素。

column1: That's a nice speech impediment you have there, but I don't know what you're saying.
column2: 你這口齒不清的毛病還真特別,可惜我聽不懂你在講啥。

column1: You have shown yourself worthy by defeating me at arm wrestling. Let me get them for you.
column2: 你在腕力上贏了我,證明了自己的本事。我去拿給你。

column1: He gets the bellows down and tosses them to you.
column2: 他把風箱取下來,扔給了你。

column1: You tell Issur about the Air Elemental blowing around near the palace.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起在宮殿附近亂吹的氣元素。

column1: It's a real pity my bellows went missing. I could have loaned them to you to help catch that thing.
column2: 真可惜我那風箱不見了。不然還能借你去抓那玩意兒呢。

column1: You're supposed to be the hero. Go and deal with it!
column2: 你不是英雄嗎。去搞定它啊!

column1: Good to know you put my bellows to good use.
column2: 很高興我那風箱派上了大用場。

column1: So you got to be the big hero, huh? Just don't forget it was all thanks to my bellows.
column2: 所以你成了大英雄啦?可別忘了這全是靠我那風箱。

column1: Issur seems much more mellow now.
column2: 伊蘇爾現在看起來和氣多了。

column1: Issur continues to smile at you.
column2: 伊蘇爾繼續對你笑著。

column1: You'd better not. Issur would get upset.
column2: 你最好別這麼做。伊蘇爾會發火的。

column1: You don't think Issur would appreciate that.
column2: 你看伊蘇爾可不會領情。

column1: This place has the best weapon shop in the world.
column2: 這裡有全世界最棒的武器鋪。

column1: Magic is magic. Go ask the weird guy at the Magic Shop.
column2: 魔法歸魔法。去問魔法鋪那個怪傢伙吧。

column1: The thing is magic. You need a magic user, not a fighter.
column2: 那玩意兒是魔法。你得找法師,不是戰士。

column1: I compete in arm wrestling contests there.
column2: 我在那兒參加腕力比賽。

column1: I compete in arm wrestling contests there. What do you care about it?
column2: 我在那兒參加腕力比賽。關你什麼事?

column1: Now that's one tough sucker.
column2: 那傢伙還真是個硬骨頭。

column1: Nobody can get near the fountain. Without water, I can't make any more weapons. Didn't you take care of those other magical things? Go do something about it!
column2: 沒人能靠近那噴泉。沒有水,我就打不了武器。你不是收拾過其他那些魔法怪物嗎?快去想想辦法!

column1: If you want monsters, go out in the desert at night. That's where real men get weapon practice, not with some wimpy dame at the Guild Hall.
column2: 想找怪物,晚上去沙漠就有。那才是真男人練武的地方,不是跟公會大廳裡那個孬婆娘磨蹭。

column1: The Sultan has some good men working for him. Some of the guards are my best friends.
column2: 蘇丹手下有不少好漢。有些衛兵是我的死黨。

column1: The desert is where you separate the men from the wimps.
column2: 沙漠就是分辨真男人和孬種的地方。

column1: I don't like thieves. The thief who breaks in here is in real trouble.
column2: 我討厭盜賊。膽敢闖進這裡的盜賊,有他好受的。

column1: That place is for sissies. After all, it's run by a woman.
column2: 那地方是給娘娘腔待的。畢竟是個女人在管嘛。

column1: It's hot. It's always hot.
column2: 熱。一直都這麼熱。

column1: Nice dame.
column2: 不錯的妞兒。

column1: I am Issur of the House of Usher.
column2: 我是亞瑟府的伊蘇爾。

column1: I make the finest weapons in all Shapeir!
column2: 我打的是全夏皮爾最頂尖的武器!

column1: You got to work out daily if you want to build muscles like mine.
column2: 想練出我這身肌肉,就得天天操練。

column1: Paladins can fight good, but they're still wimpy goody-goodies and too high-and-mighty for EOF.
column2: 聖騎士是能打,可說到底還是一群裝模作樣的孬種,自命清高,進不了永恆戰士團。

column1: Those are my trophies from arm-wrestling tournaments in Petalumeir. They are not for sale.
column2: 那些是我在佩塔魯梅爾腕力錦標賽贏來的獎盃。非賣品。

column1: That is sold to one of the Sultan's guards. He picks it up today.
column2: 那是賣給蘇丹一個衛兵的。他今天要來取。

column1: That is sold to someone else. They'll pick it up tomorrow.
column2: 那是賣給別人的。他們明天會來取。

column1: It's hot. Don't touch it.
column2: 那很燙。別碰。

column1: I work the metals there for my fine weapons.
column2: 我在那兒打鐵,做我那些好武器。

column1: I don't know what to tell you about it.
column2: 那事我不曉得能跟你說什麼。

column1: Go ask someone else.
column2: 去問別人吧。

column1: I got nothing to say about that.
column2: 那事我沒什麼好說的。

column1: You think I got nothing better to do than answer stupid questions? Go away.
column2: 你當我閒到只能回答蠢問題嗎?走開。

column1: No more questions. Buy something or get out!
column2: 別再問了。要嘛買東西,要嘛出去!

column1: What's a wimp like you talking about EOF for? Only real men join EOF.
column2: 你這種孬種扯什麼永恆戰士團?只有真男人才能加入永恆戰士團。

column1: You tell Issur about the Goblins you fought in Spielburg.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起你在史畢柏格大戰哥布林的事。

column1: Sounds like that land has some pretty wimpy monsters!
column2: 聽起來那地方的怪物還真夠孬的!

column1: You think about it and then decide not to. You can tell Issur is not interested in socializing.
column2: 你想了想,還是算了。看得出伊蘇爾沒興趣閒聊。

column1: You couldn't even rid the land of some evil dame? What kind of hero is that?
column2: 你連個邪惡婆娘都趕不走?這算哪門子英雄?

column1: Wait a minute, everyone's calling you a hero because you made some old hag leave a valley? What am I missing?
column2: 等等,大家叫你英雄,就因為你逼一個老巫婆離開了某個山谷?我是漏聽了什麼嗎?

column1: You tell Issur about WIT and your decision to reject their proposal.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起 W.I.T. 以及你回絕他們邀約的決定。

column1: Magic? What do you want me to say about that?
column2: 魔法?你要我說什麼?

column1: Mentioning illegal activities is a foolish thing in itself. Mentioning it to a short-tempered person in a room full of weapons is beyond foolish.
column2: 提起違法的勾當本身就夠蠢了。對著一個脾氣火爆、又滿屋子武器的人提這個,那更是蠢到家。

column1: Any time.
column2: 不客氣。

column1: Big deal.
column2: 了不起喔。

column1: Good for you.
column2: 算你行。

column1: Must have been a good fight!
column2: 那一仗肯定夠精彩!

column1: So that lion guy's a paladin? Figures.
column2: 所以那獅子傢伙是聖騎士?難怪。

column1: You tell Issur about the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起中了魔法的埃米爾。

column1: Now there's something you don't hear every day!
column2: 這倒是難得一聽的稀奇事!

column1: You tell Issur about the Poet Omar.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起詩人奧瑪爾。

column1: Poetry's for wimps! The sword is far mightier than the pen.
column2: 詩詞是給孬種寫的!刀劍可比筆桿子厲害多了。

column1: You tell Issur about Shameen and Shema's hospitality at the inn.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起沙明和舍瑪在客棧裡的熱情款待。

column1: You tell Issur about your flight into the city on Abdulla's magic carpet.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起你乘著阿卜杜拉的魔毯飛進城裡的事。

column1: Thatta fact?
column2: 真有此事?

column1: You tell Issur about the rope-walking challenge.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起走繩索的挑戰。

column1: Brute force beats agility, hands down. Any decent fighter's gotta take a hit now and again.
column2: 蠻力勝過敏捷,毫無懸念。像樣的戰士哪有不偶爾挨幾下的。

column1: You tell Issur about the ferocious caged beast.
column2: 你跟伊蘇爾說起籠子裡那頭兇猛的野獸。

column1: Beasts make very worthy adversaries in a fight.
column2: 野獸在戰鬥中可是相當夠格的對手。

column1: You don't say?
column2: 真的假的?

column1: Keep your distance!
column2: 離我遠一點!

column1: I am Issur, and this is my Weapon Shop. I make the best swords and daggers. What do you want?
column2: 我是伊蘇爾,這是我的武器鋪。我打的劍和匕首最棒了。你想要什麼?

column1: You ask Issur if he's willing to arm wrestle you again.
column2: 你問伊蘇爾願不願意再跟你比一場腕力。

column1: If you want to arm wrestle with Issur, you should challenge him.
column2: 想跟伊蘇爾比腕力,你得向他下戰帖。

column1: You need to have the money before you go wagering!
column2: 要下注,先得有錢才行!

column1: I don't have any use for foreign coin. Go see the Moneychanger.
column2: 外國錢幣我用不上。去找錢莊吧。

column1: It would be best to keep your hands to yourself.
column2: 你最好別亂動手。

column1: You'd better ask Issur about the weapons.
column2: 武器的事,你最好問伊蘇爾。

column1: You cannot take the armor.
column2: 你拿不走那盔甲。

column1: Issur probably wouldn't appreciate your redecoration efforts.
column2: 伊蘇爾大概不會欣賞你這番重新布置的心意。

column1: If you roll out this barrel, Issur may very well decide to roll YOU out, flat on your face!
column2: 你要是敢滾走這木桶,伊蘇爾八成會把「你」滾出去,讓你臉著地!

column1: Given that they've been sitting next to the fire for so long, grabbing them would probably result in a searing burn to your hand.
column2: 它們在爐火旁擱了這麼久,你要去抓,八成會把手燙傷。

column1: There's no reason to open or close the door.
column2: 沒理由開門或關門。

column1: Leave the door alone!
column2: 別碰那扇門!

column1: You'd only burn your hand.
column2: 你只會燙到手。

column1: Don't touch it!
column2: 別碰它!

column1: Why would you need my bellows? What's the story, bub?
column2: 你要我的風箱幹嘛?到底怎麼回事,老兄?

column1: You surreptitiously spread some oil around the base of the anvil, making sure Issur isn't watching.
column2: 你趁伊蘇爾沒注意,偷偷在鐵砧底座周圍抹上了油。

column1: Not in the middle of the day!
column2: 大白天的可不行!

column1: That will be the place to see just how good a fighter you are. I get tired of not using spear and shield for so long a while. Maybe you and I will go check out skills there.
column2: 那裡正好能看出你身手到底有多好。長矛和盾牌擱著沒用這麼久,我都手癢了。或許咱倆可以去那兒切磋切磋。

column1: No magic, though. I don't do magic. No throwing things either. Only melee fighting.
column2: 不過不准用魔法。我不碰魔法。也不准扔東西。只能近身肉搏。

column1: No magic, though. I don't do magic.
column2: 不過不准用魔法。我不碰魔法。

column1: No throwing things, though. Only melee fighting.
column2: 不過不准扔東西。只能近身肉搏。

column1: I not be seeing who leave it. It come while I feed Simba.
column2: 我沒看到是誰留下的。我餵辛巴的時候它就在那兒了。

column1: You may be wanting to know about some basic combat actions. I be happy to teach you. Go to training room first.
column2: 你或許想了解些基本的戰鬥動作。我很樂意教你。先去訓練室吧。

column1: I be glad to accept your challenge. But we must be going to training room first.
column2: 我很樂意接受你的挑戰。不過咱們得先去訓練室。

column1: Before Simba was born, I be doing plenty of hunting in desert. Fought many monsters. If you be wanting to know more about them, just say so.
column2: 辛巴出生前,我在沙漠裡狩獵過不少。打過許多怪物。你要是想多了解牠們,儘管說。

column1: That be where you sign in to be adventurer in Shapeir.
column2: 那是你在夏皮爾登記成為冒險者的地方。

column1: That be the motto of the EOF. Read it.
column2: 那是永恆戰士團的格言。讀一讀。

column1: I learned to use that weapon when I was only little bigger than Simba.
column2: 我學會用那武器的時候,個頭只比辛巴大一點點。

column1: My people be very far from here. I be Simbani warrior, but Simbani woman cannot be wife and warrior. So I be coming to Shapeir to find father so I can bear child. Now, I have Simba and still remain Simbani warrior.
column2: 我的族人離這兒很遠。我是辛巴尼戰士,可辛巴尼女子不能既當人妻又當戰士。所以我來到夏皮爾尋找孩子的父親,好讓自己生育。如今我有了辛巴,卻仍是辛巴尼戰士。

column1: My home be good place to hunt and roam the great savannas and jungles. The desert of Shapeir not have much shade to hunt by day, and is too cold for hunting by night. I only give combat practice lessons here.
column2: 我的家鄉是狩獵的好地方,能在遼闊的草原和叢林裡馳騁。夏皮爾的沙漠白天沒什麼陰涼可供狩獵,夜裡又太冷。我在這兒只教戰鬥課。

column1: When I return to Tarna, I begin teaching Simba about hunting too.
column2: 等我回到塔爾納,也要開始教辛巴狩獵。

column1: Simba means "Lion" in my language. Rakeesh say he sounds like a lion cub when he cries.
column2: 辛巴在我的語言裡是「獅子」的意思。拉基什說他哭起來像隻小獅崽。

column1: My land and people are many months travel from here.
column2: 我的家鄉和族人離這兒要走上好幾個月。

column1: If you be fighting Scorpion, you must watch the tail. If the tail gets you, then you will be plenty sick, maybe dead, unless you get healing. Scorpion be not very smart fighter though.
column2: 你要是跟蠍子打,一定要盯著牠的尾巴。尾巴刺中你,你就會病得很重,搞不好還會沒命,除非趕快治療。不過蠍子打起來腦袋不太靈光。

column1: Brigands be only moderate fighters, but they can still kill unprepared victims. You be fine as long as you attack and defend better than they do.
column2: 強盜的身手只算中等,可碰上沒準備的人照樣能要命。只要你攻防都比他們強,就沒事。

column1: Ghouls be bad magic things out only at night. They eat the dead, but they will not be picky if they get the chance to eat you. They steal your life and then use it to cast spells against you. Avoid their spells and kill them quickly while they be weak.
column2: 食屍鬼是只在夜裡出沒的邪惡魔物。牠們吃死人,不過要是逮著機會吃你,可不會挑嘴。牠們會偷走你的生命力,再拿來對你施法。閃開牠們的法術,趁牠們虛弱時速戰速決。

column1: One Jackalman not be all that tough, but many Jackalmen be wearing down even good fighters. Keep rest of the pack off your flank at all cost.
column2: 一隻豺狼人不算多難纏,可一大群豺狼人連好戰士都能磨垮。無論如何都要擋住其餘的那群,別讓牠們繞到你側翼。

column1: Dinosaur thing with many claws and teeth. It likes to eat people. Look out when it leaps at you. It can tear you up, if you do not dodge.
column2: 那是種長著一堆爪牙的恐龍似的東西。牠愛吃人。牠撲過來時可要當心。你要是不閃,牠能把你撕個稀爛。

column1: Few weeks ago, one Terrorsaurus approach city and invade pen. Tried to kill riding sauruses. Rakeesh was nearby and helped gate guard slay it. Now head hangs here on wall.
column2: 幾週前,有隻恐懼龍逼近城裡,闖進了畜欄,想殺光那些坐騎索魯斯。拉基什正好在附近,幫城門衛兵宰了牠。如今牠的頭就掛在這牆上。

column1: Strong fighter with claws and beak, it be. Tear you to shreds unless you dodge and fight better.
column2: 牠是個有爪有喙的猛將。你要是閃不開、打不贏,就會被撕成碎片。

column1: That be the name of adventurer who donated the stuffed Jackalman head to the Guild before making career change. I hear he be Brigand now.
column2: 那是把豺狼人頭標本捐給公會、後來轉行的那個冒險者的名字。聽說他現在成了強盜。

column1: Simba's father be in the Sultan's guards. Strong fighter, he be almost better than me.
column2: 辛巴的爸爸在蘇丹的衛隊裡。他是個強悍的戰士,差一點就比我還厲害了。

column1: There be jobs, plenty rewards for good adventurers. There be many more before I come here, but I be taking care of Simba too much now for jobs.
column2: 這裡有工作,好冒險者能拿到不少賞金。我來這裡之前還更多呢,只是現在我顧辛巴顧得緊,沒空接活了。

column1: Rakeesh be very fine warrior. He knows to be gentle and kind, too. Very wise, very strong, but his leg be plenty bad, hurt him all the time.
column2: 拉基什是個了不起的戰士。他懂得溫柔、也懂得仁慈。又有智慧又強壯,可惜那條腿傷得很重,一天到晚折磨著他。

column1: One very special, a paladin be. Talk to Rakeesh, he be knowing very much about paladins.
column2: 有一種特別的,叫聖騎士。去找拉基什聊聊,他對聖騎士這檔事懂得可多了。

column1: Rakeesh leg be broken badly in fight long time. It did not heal right.
column2: 拉基什那條腿很久以前在一場打鬥裡傷得很重,後來就沒好過。

column1: Men join to show off muscles and talk big.
column2: 男人們加進來,不過是想秀秀肌肉、吹吹牛罷了。

column1: Someone must be sending the bad magic that comes here. That wizard most likely be in Raseir, since the bad things happen there first.
column2: 一定有人在把這些邪惡的魔法送過來。那個巫師八成在拉希爾,因為壞事都是先從那兒開始的。

column1: That thing be one of the people of the Djinn.
column2: 那東西是精靈一族的。

column1: In the Plaza of the Fountain be the man who makes things to help people. He always needs things to be making what he does.
column2: 噴泉廣場上有個專做東西幫人的人。他要做東西,總是缺這缺那。

column1: That be a very strange man, I think. His Shop of Magic be east of here in the Plaza of the Fountain.
column2: 我看哪,那是個很古怪的人。他的魔法店在這兒往東,噴泉廣場那邊。

column1: That man has more muscles than he has brains. He makes good weapons, though. His swords be very fine.
column2: 那人肌肉比腦子多。不過他打的武器是真好,劍尤其精良。

column1: The Sultan be very wise and generous. Sometimes he has Rakeesh give rewards to those who have done things for him.
column2: 蘇丹既英明又大方。有時候他會叫拉基什替他賞賜那些為他出過力的人。

column1: Rakeesh won his sword in mighty battle. He call it Soulforge.
column2: 拉基什這把劍是在一場惡戰裡贏來的,他管它叫「鑄魂」。

column1: To be real hero here, you need to become paladin.
column2: 想在這兒當個真英雄,你得成為聖騎士才行。

column1: Never turn your back to moose. Especially near mooseberry bushes.
column2: 千萬別把背對著駝鹿,尤其是在駝鹿莓叢旁邊。

column1: Maybe we practice combat skills, you and I.
column2: 也許咱倆可以練練身手,你和我。

column1: This be a good place to stay while Simba be so small. Soon he be big enough to travel. Then we be going back to my home.
column2: 辛巴還這麼小,這裡是個落腳的好地方。等他大到能上路了,我們就回我的家鄉去。

column1: Very bad things happened there, maybe they start to happen here. I be hoping not. I be strong fighter, but such things come of bad magic. Hard to fight magic with a spear.
column2: 那邊出過很可怕的事,搞不好這兒也要開始了。但願不會。我是個強悍的戰士,可這種事是邪惡魔法搞出來的。光憑一桿矛,很難打得過魔法。

column1: Djinn know much magic, some good, some bad. Best you be leaving them alone.
column2: 精靈懂很多魔法,有好的也有壞的。你最好別去招惹他們。

column1: Fire Elemental be thing of bad magic. Even if it be stopped, more bad magic will come down until bad magic wizard be stopped.
column2: 火元素是邪惡魔法的產物。就算你擋下了它,只要那個邪惡巫師沒倒,還會有更多邪法接二連三降下來。

column1: Water be very much needed here. If Water Elemental in the Fountain Plaza not stopped, there be no water. Soon there be no people.
column2: 這裡很需要水。要是不把噴泉廣場那個水元素擋下來,就沒水喝。再過不久,人也都沒了。

column1: Wind be strong, be blowing the city to pieces unless someone stop it.
column2: 那風好強,要是沒人阻止,整座城都要被它吹散了。

column1: You should be asking Rakeesh. He knows much about earth.
column2: 這你該去問拉基什。他對土的事懂得多。

column1: Thing of very powerful magic. Only magic and skill will stop it.
column2: 那是非常強大的魔法產物。只有魔法加上本事才擋得住它。

column1: Many monsters live in the desert. You be going there, you need watch for Jackalmen and Ghouls at night. During day, you find Brigands. Scorpions and Terrorsauruses always a problem.
column2: 沙漠裡住著很多怪物。你要去那兒,晚上得當心豺狼人和食屍鬼。白天嘛,會碰上強盜。蠍子和恐爪龍呢,什麼時候都是麻煩。

column1: If you need to know magic, ask a magic person. I be warrior, not wizard.
column2: 你要打聽魔法的事,去問懂魔法的人。我是戰士,不是巫師。

column1: Good leader. When trouble start to happen in Raseir, he be going with his men there. Magic wind storm blew up and many men died. My man say the Sultan be plenty brave, but could not stop magic.
column2: 好首領。拉希爾一出亂子,他就帶著手下趕過去。結果颳起一場魔法風暴,死了好多人。我男人說蘇丹勇敢得很,可就是擋不住魔法。

column1: Something magic happen to the Emir of Raseir. No one knows for sure what but the one who did it.
column2: 拉希爾的埃米爾出了魔法的事。到底怎麼回事,除了下手的那個,誰也說不準。

column1: My man works at the Sultan's Palace. It be to the east of here.
column2: 我男人在蘇丹的宮殿做事。從這兒往東走就到了。

column1: To go to Raseir you need be going by caravan. It leaves in a week or so. Ask the guard at front gate of the city.
column2: 要去拉希爾,你得跟著商隊走。商隊大概一個禮拜後出發。去城門口問守衛吧。

column1: No place for fools. Too hot in the day, too many monsters at night. Very easy to get lost and never come back from there.
column2: 那不是傻子待的地方。白天太曬,晚上怪物又多。一不小心就迷了路,再也回不來。

column1: For money, you could do a job. Read the quest board.
column2: 想賺錢的話,可以去接個活。看看任務板吧。

column1: This place I be working while Simba be small. I be good fighter, good adventurer. You need help fighting, I be good teacher, too.
column2: 辛巴還小,我就在這兒幹活。我是個好戰士、好冒險者。你要人教打鬥,我也是個好師傅。

column1: Hear many good things about your friends at the inn. They be heroes to the other Katta. Say that you be hero too.
column2: 聽說了不少關於你那些客棧朋友的好話。在其他卡塔族眼裡,他們可是英雄。他們還說你也是個英雄呢。

column1: Go out at noon, you will find out what hot can be. No water, and you find out what dead can be.
column2: 正午跑出去,你就會曉得什麼叫熱。再沒水喝,你就會曉得什麼叫死。

column1: You will be finding fountain if you go east of this plaza.
column2: 從這座廣場往東走,你就會找到噴泉。

column1: Rakeesh be saying that bad things happen here soon. Bad magic, he come down, somebody has got to stop him. Maybe you.
column2: 拉基什說這兒很快要出壞事了。邪惡魔法,它要降下來,總得有人去阻止它。也許就是你。

column1: Many people here try to sell many things.
column2: 這裡好多人在叫賣各式各樣的東西。

column1: Katta, they be good hunters if you get them away from the city.
column2: 卡塔族要是離開城裡,可都是好獵手。

column1: You would probably anger her if you tried, and you don't want to see her mad.
column2: 你要真那麼做,八成會惹火她,而你可不會想看她發飆的樣子。

column1: Funny old man in the desert oasis. To find him, you need to be going from the gates to the city lookout. Then go south until you come to the rock shaped like a Griffin's head. Head east until you come to oasis. He will be there.
column2: 沙漠綠洲裡有個古怪的老頭。想找他,你得從城門走到城外的瞭望點,再往南走,走到一塊長得像獅鷲頭的岩石那兒,然後往東走,一直走到綠洲。他就在那裡。

column1: My name be Uhura. It means "Freedom" in my people's voice.
column2: 我叫烏胡拉。在我族人的語言裡,這名字是「自由」的意思。

column1: Maybe they be needing new Guildmaster here soon, I be thinking. When problems be in Shapeir, I be here. When problems be going, I be going.
column2: 我在想哪,這裡很快就要找新的公會會長了。夏皮爾有麻煩,我就留在這兒;麻煩一走,我也就走了。

column1: I do not know what you mean.
column2: 我不懂你的意思。

column1: Maybe we be not speaking the same voice. I be not knowing.
column2: 也許咱倆說的不是同一種話。我聽不明白。

column1: You be forgetting already, Hero?
column2: 你這麼快就忘啦,英雄?

column1: This guy must be one hell of a philosopher.
column2: 這傢伙肯定是個了不得的哲學家。

column1: You have already returned the reward money.
column2: 你已經把賞金退回去了。

column1: You are better off keeping that to yourself. You can already tell beforehand that Rakeesh would not at all approve of your actions.
column2: 這事你最好爛在肚子裡。你心裡早有數,拉基什絕不會贊同你這種做法。

column1: Just walk into the training room behind me. Uhura will be happy to train with you. I hope you do not mind if I watch. I might be able to give you some pointers on how to improve your skills.
column2: 直接走進我身後那間訓練室就行。烏胡拉很樂意陪你練。希望你不介意我在旁邊看,說不定我還能給你指點幾招,讓你練得更精。

column1: Mzuri, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 姆祖里,史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: Mzuri, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 姆祖里,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: Baron be punished very harshly by curse. I be very sad too if Simba be taken away.
column2: 男爵被那詛咒懲罰得好慘。要是辛巴被奪走,我也會傷心得不得了。

column1: Ugly lady-wizard still remains there, but you have saved baron's daughter. I think maybe she will be doing something about curse now.
column2: 那個醜女巫師還賴在那兒,不過你救了男爵的女兒。我想她現在也許會著手解那道詛咒了。

column1: She sound plenty bad. Good thing you think smarter than ugly lady-wizard or maybe you be a frog right now.
column2: 聽起來她壞透了。還好你比那醜女巫師機靈,要不你這會兒搞不好成了隻青蛙。

column1: Good thing you didn't stay there. City may need you here soon.
column2: 還好你沒留在那兒。這座城很快可能就需要你了。

column1: You doubt Uhura would approve of your illicit activities. You're better off keeping that tale to yourself.
column2: 你看烏胡拉八成不會贊同你那些見不得光的勾當。這段往事還是爛在肚子裡比較好。

column1: We have warrior initiation in my village too, but it test more than just how hard you can hit opponent.
column2: 我們村子裡也有戰士的入會試煉,不過考的可不只是你下手有多重。

column1: You did good job saving the city.
column2: 你把城救下來了,幹得漂亮。

column1: Open plaza give it plenty of room to dodge water. But it won't go into narrow alleyway on its own.
column2: 開闊的廣場讓它有大把空間躲水。可它自己是不會鑽進窄巷子裡的。

column1: Good thing for Shapeir you be around. Guards already gave up trying to make it leave.
column2: 夏皮爾有你在真是萬幸。守衛們早就放棄趕它走了。

column1: I be not knowing how to take on such a creature. You cannot just stick weapon into it.
column2: 我可不知道該怎麼對付這種東西。你又不能拿武器直接往它身上戳。

column1: You be very brave. Nobody else dared to go near it anymore.
column2: 你膽子真大。再沒別人敢靠近它了。

column1: Yes, I saw how it fought with Rakeesh. Hope you have more luck.
column2: 是啊,我親眼看它跟拉基什交手的。但願你運氣好一些。

column1: You be real hero. Water be more important than anything else in this land.
column2: 你是真英雄。在這片土地上,水比什麼都金貴。

column1: Hope you be able to beat Water Elemental too. There be no other place nearby to get enough water for entire city.
column2: 但願你也能打贏水元素。附近再沒別的地方能弄到夠全城人喝的水了。

column1: So steed be ruler of Raseir? I be hoping you treated your saurus well.
column2: 這麼說,你那坐騎竟是拉希爾的統治者?但願你沒虧待了你的索魯斯。

column1: With impolite attitude like that, I be not surprised he was turned into beast.
column2: 態度那麼無禮,他被變成野獸我一點也不意外。

column1: I only know that be no natural creature around here. Maybe it be magical.
column2: 我只曉得這附近沒有那種天生的生物。八成是魔法變出來的。

column1: That not be familiar name to me.
column2: 這名字我聽著陌生。

column1: Rakeesh visit her sometimes too. I be not very comfortable around enchanters.
column2: 拉基什偶爾也會去拜訪她。我待在術士身邊,總覺得不太自在。

column1: That will make great bedtime story for Simba.
column2: 這故事拿來哄辛巴睡覺再好不過了。

column1: We have people like that in my village too. They don't make poems, but simply collect stories. Still very much fun to listen to.
column2: 我們村子裡也有那樣的人。他們不作詩,只是把故事收集起來。可聽起來照樣很有意思。

column1: You better train some more here before going into desert. Only your pride can get hurt in here.
column2: 進沙漠之前,你最好在這兒多練練。在這裡,頂多傷到你的自尊罷了。

column1: You be getting better already. It be very important not to lose patience. Battle skills take time to improve.
column2: 你已經越來越強了。最要緊的是別失了耐性。戰鬥的本事是要花時間磨的。

column1: You be doing well, but you still lose. In real battle, it be not important if monster barely beats you or not.
column2: 你打得不錯,可還是輸了。真打起來,怪物是險勝你還是怎樣,根本不重要。

column1: Maybe I just got lucky. We may be needing more matches to find out.
column2: 也許只是我運氣好。咱們多打幾場才看得出真章。

column1: Maybe you be better or maybe I be having bad day. I be curious how next training will go.
column2: 也許是你更強,也許是我今天狀態不好。我倒好奇下回練起來會怎樣。

column1: You be good enough to beat me, but in real battle, it be also important to avoid as much damage as possible.
column2: 你已經強到能贏我了,不過真打起來,盡量少挨點傷也很要緊。

column1: Maybe I should start thanking you for training from now on.
column2: 也許從今往後,該換我謝你陪我練了。

column1: There be not much more I can teach you. Maybe you can continue training in desert.
column2: 我能教你的也沒剩多少了。也許你可以到沙漠裡接著練。

column1: I always be up for friendly training session. Just go inside training room and we will fight.
column2: 我隨時樂意切磋切磋。進訓練室來,咱們就開打。

column1: It reminded me of first time I challenged him.
column2: 這讓我想起頭一回向他挑戰的時候。

column1: He beat you, but training with him be honor itself already.
column2: 他贏了你,可光是能跟他練上一場,本身就是莫大的榮耀了。

column1: You put up good fight. You held out longer against him than I could.
column2: 你打得很頑強。你撐在他手下的時間,比我當年還久。

column1: That be very close. Maybe you should try again later. You may win next time.
column2: 那場好險。也許你過陣子再試試,下回說不定就贏了。

column1: That be very impressive to watch. You certainly kept the suspense.
column2: 看著真過癮。你可把懸念吊到底了。

column1: You be great warrior. Many Liontaurs would be envious of you.
column2: 你是個了不起的戰士。許多獅人都會羨慕你的。

column1: You beat Rakeesh well. But in real battle, he be harder to defeat.
column2: 你把拉基什打得漂亮。可真上了戰場,他要難對付得多。

column1: There is no need to defend me, Uhura. Our friend earned his victory.
column2: 不必替我說話,烏胡拉。我們這位朋友是憑本事贏的。

column1: Maybe Rakeesh and I should both take instruction from you.
column2: 也許我跟拉基什都該反過來向你討教討教了。

column1: Rakeesh have decades of experience with sword. Train hard first and challenge him when you feel confident enough.
column2: 拉基什練劍可有幾十年的功夫。你先好好練,等夠有把握了,再去向他挑戰。

column1: Rakeesh wouldn't be comfortable fighting with his sword against a dagger. He might cut your arm off when trying to knock your weapon away.
column2: 拿他那把劍對你的匕首,拉基什會放不開手。他想把你的武器打飛時,搞不好一下就削掉你一條胳膊。

column1: I can understand they be eager to get home. I know how it be to be far away from home and family.
column2: 我能體會他們歸心似箭。遠離家鄉、遠離親人是什麼滋味,我懂。

column1: Carpet would be very useful for me to have. Journey from my homeland took very long on foot.
column2: 要是我也有一張那樣的毯子就好用了。從我家鄉一路走來,可費了老長的工夫。

column1: Other guildmaster was in charge when he came in here and offered Jackalman heads as trophies. I hear he be upset when they didn't want to accept more than one.
column2: 他來這兒、拿豺狼人的頭當戰利品那會兒,管事的還是前一任會長。聽說人家只肯收一顆,他還氣呼呼的。

column1: In my village, I be doing balance training very often. Maybe I be trying the man's challenge myself some day.
column2: 在我們村子裡,我常做平衡的訓練。說不定哪天我自己也去試試那個人的挑戰。

column1: That be a very interesting story.
column2: 這故事真有意思。

column1: Ah, the hero of the Kattas! You have some reputation already, to hear merchants talk. If you are as good as they say you are, then you must be plenty good.
column2: 啊,卡塔族的英雄!聽商人們那麼一說,你已經小有名氣了。要是你真有他們講的那麼厲害,那你肯定是相當了得。

column1: I be Uhura. I be honored you visit here.
column2: 我是烏胡拉。承蒙你來造訪,我很榮幸。

column1: Ah, this be the new hero, Rakeesh.
column2: 啊,這位就是那位新英雄吧,拉基什。

column1: You are most welcome in Shapeir. The Sultan has been seeking a hero since the forces of dark magic overwhelmed Raseir.
column2: 夏皮爾誠摯歡迎你。自從黑暗魔法的勢力吞沒了拉希爾,蘇丹便一直在尋訪英雄。

column1: Ah, the new hero be back again, Rakeesh.
column2: 啊,那位新英雄又回來了,拉基什。

column1: It's good sometimes to have Simba with Rakeesh. Hard to feel like strong warrior when feeding baby. Maybe you and I do some practice with weapons today.
column2: 有時候把辛巴交給拉基什帶帶也不錯。一邊餵娃娃,可很難覺得自己像個強悍的戰士。也許你跟我今天可以練練武器。

column1: The Fire Elemental be plenty hard to fight. Maybe the city burn down unless someone get it stopped.
column2: 火元素難打得很。要是沒人把它擋下來,這城搞不好就要燒個精光。

column1: You have saved the city with your courage and your resourcefulness. The land of Shapeir is indebted to you. By order of the Sultan, Harun al-Rashid, I have been asked to give you this reward for your actions.
column2: 你以勇氣和機智救了這座城。夏皮爾這片土地虧欠了你。奉蘇丹哈倫·拉希德之命,我受託將這份賞賜交給你,以表彰你的義舉。

column1: Rakeesh gives you the 50 dinar reward.
column2: 拉基什把五十第納爾的賞金交給了你。

column1: You are given the 50 dinar reward.
column2: 你獲得了五十第納爾的賞金。

column1: We are all truly fortunate that you came to this land.
column2: 你能來到這片土地,實在是我們所有人的福氣。

column1: My spear not do much to Air Elemental near Palace. Maybe you can do something to stop it.
column2: 我那桿矛對宮殿旁那個風元素起不了什麼作用。也許你有辦法阻止它。

column1: Rakeesh, he say that you stop the Air Elemental. Now you are even bigger hero in Shapeir.
column2: 拉基什說是你擋下了那個風元素。如今你在夏皮爾是更了不起的英雄了。

column1: Rakeesh not be here today, Hero. He be speaking with Sultan. They talk about more Elementals coming down soon.
column2: 拉基什今天不在這兒,英雄。他正跟蘇丹談話呢。他們說很快還會有更多元素降下來。

column1: It seems that Shapeir again needs your skills. No one else in this city can handle such a magical being.
column2: 看來夏皮爾又得仰仗你的本事了。這城裡再沒別人對付得了這種魔法生物。

column1: If your leg had not failed you this morning, Rakeesh, the Elemental would be gone.
column2: 要不是你今天早上那條腿不爭氣,拉基什,那元素早就被收拾掉了。

column1: Hero, you should have seen how Rakeesh fought it! His sword burned brightly as he fought that monster. Rakeesh be a great warrior still!
column2: 英雄,你真該看看拉基什是怎麼跟它拚的!他跟那怪物廝殺時,劍身燒得耀眼通紅。拉基什到現在都還是個了不起的戰士!

column1: I am afraid I have not the power to deal with the Elemental now. It will require your skills and magic to overcome the thing, oh Defeater of Elementals.
column2: 恐怕我如今已沒有力量去對付那元素了。要降伏那東西,得靠你的本事與魔法才行,哦,元素的剋星啊。

column1: You tell about your EOF initiation.
column2: 你說起自己在永恆戰士團的入會儀式。

column1: You tell about the threat of the Fire Elemental.
column2: 你說起火元素帶來的威脅。

column1: You tell about the menace of the Air Elemental.
column2: 你說起風元素造成的禍患。

column1: You relate the tale of how you made the Earth Elemental bite the dust.
column2: 你講起自己是如何把土元素打得灰飛煙滅的。

column1: You tell about the menace of the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你說起土元素造成的禍患。

column1: You tell Uhura about how Aziza revealed the true identity of your riding Saurus.
column2: 你向烏胡拉說起阿姿莎如何揭穿你那頭索魯斯坐騎的真實身分。

column1: You tell Uhura about the caged beast.
column2: 你向烏胡拉說起那頭被關在籠裡的野獸。

column1: You tell Uhura about the beast you found in the desert.
column2: 你向烏胡拉說起你在沙漠裡發現的那頭野獸。

column1: You tell Uhura the name Al Scurva mentioned to you.
column2: 你把阿爾·斯庫爾瓦對你提過的那個名字告訴烏胡拉。

column1: You tell Uhura about Omar's performances at the inn.
column2: 你向烏胡拉說起奧瑪爾在客棧裡的表演。

column1: You thank Uhura for the training.
column2: 你向烏胡拉道謝,感激她的指導。

column1: You tell Uhura you'd like to spar with her.
column2: 你告訴烏胡拉,你想跟她切磋一場。

column1: You tell about the last training session with Rakeesh.
column2: 你說起上回跟拉基什的那場訓練。

column1: You tell Uhura you'd like an opportunity to spar with Rakeesh some time.
column2: 你告訴烏胡拉,你希望哪天能有機會跟拉基什切磋切磋。

column1: You tell Uhura about how you met Shameen and Shema in Spielburg and how they convinced you to accompany them to Shapeir after the reward ceremony at Spielburg castle.
column2: 你向烏胡拉說起你在史畢柏格遇見沙明和舍瑪的經過,以及他們如何在史畢柏格城堡的頒獎典禮之後,說動你陪他們一同前往夏皮爾。

column1: You tell about how you returned Abdulla's carpet to him after the Brigands stole it and how he used it to transport the four of you to Shapeir.
column2: 你說起強盜偷走阿卜杜拉的地毯後,你又是如何把它物歸原主,而他又是如何用這張毯子把你們四個一路載到夏皮爾。

column1: You tell about Sweeping Sir James.
column2: 你說起那位「打掃騎士」詹姆斯爵士。

column1: If you do seek to defeat the Earth Elemental which now walks our streets, you must first understand its nature.
column2: 你若真想擊敗如今橫行街頭的土元素,就得先摸清牠的本性。

column1: If you not stop that Earth Elemental today, I think maybe it get too tough to stop.
column2: 你今天再不阻止那土元素,我看牠就要硬到誰也擋不住啦。

column1: That Earth Elemental be plenty tough, but you sure be tougher.
column2: 那土元素是夠硬的,可你肯定更硬。

column1: This note was left here for you.
column2: 這張字條是留給你的。

column1: You read the crumpled paper with the nearly illegible scrawl.
column2: 你讀著這張皺巴巴的紙,上頭潦草得幾乎認不出字。

column1: 'If you know what's good for you, you will obey orders. We will leave them here. If you don't follow them, you'll be sorry. We'll be watching you.'
column2: 「識相的話,就乖乖聽令。我們會把命令留在這兒。不照辦,你會後悔的。我們盯著你呢。」

column1: This be stuck to the door with knife. I think maybe you should find better place to pass notes.
column2: 這是用刀插在門上的。我看你該找個更好的地方傳紙條才對。

column1: You read the crumpled paper with the nearly illegible scrawl and small torn spot.
column2: 你讀著這張皺巴巴的紙,字跡潦草難辨,還破了個小洞。

column1: 'Tomorrow night is your last night. You'll get your final orders then. Be seeing you.'
column2: 「明晚是你最後一晚。到時你會收到最後的命令。回頭見。」

column1: This note be stuck to the door with an old sword. Tell your friend I will show him where to stick it the next time.
column2: 這字條是用一把舊劍釘在門上的。轉告你那位朋友,下次我會讓他知道這玩意該插哪兒。

column1: This time the note be stuck to the door with an old sword. Tell your friend I will show him where to stick it the next time.
column2: 這回字條是用一把舊劍釘在門上的。轉告你那位朋友,下次我會讓他知道這玩意該插哪兒。

column1: You read the crumpled paper with the nearly illegible scrawl and large torn spot.
column2: 你讀著這張皺巴巴的紙,字跡潦草難辨,還破了個大洞。

column1: 'At nightfall tonight, go the door at the end of 'Askeri Darb,' off 'Saif Darb.' Obey, or you'll regret it.'
column2: 「今晚天黑時,前往『賽夫巷』岔出的『阿斯凱里巷』盡頭那扇門。照辦,否則你會後悔。」

column1: 'At nightfall tonight, go the door at the end of 'Saif Darb.' Obey, or you'll regret it.'
column2: 「今晚天黑時,前往『賽夫巷』盡頭那扇門。照辦,否則你會後悔。」

column1: So, Hero, not being content with having merely saved the city, I see that you are about to attempt to save the land of Shapeir as well.
column2: 看來啊,英雄,光是救了這座城你還不滿足,你還打算連整片夏皮爾的土地一併救下來。

column1: You have proven yourself both brave and resourceful by your actions.
column2: 你的所作所為已證明了你既勇敢又機智。

column1: I have seldom met one with such power and so much wisdom and courage as you have shown. Few users of magic dare face what you have faced to protect this city.
column2: 我極少遇見像你這般兼具力量、智慧與勇氣的人。鮮少有施法者敢為了守護這座城,去面對你所面對過的一切。

column1: When first we met, I could feel there was dark within you. Now I can see that the light is stronger, if you will only let it be so. It is a narrow path between darkness and light, and you will always walk that tightrope in your soul.
column2: 我們初次相遇時,我便感覺到你心中有黑暗。如今我看見光明更為強大——只要你願意讓它如此。黑暗與光明之間是一條窄路,你的靈魂將永遠走在那條鋼索上。

column1: The journey you are about to make is to a very dark and dangerous place. The foe you will face is very powerful in the ways of magic.
column2: 你即將踏上的旅程,通往一個極其黑暗而危險的地方。你要面對的敵人,在魔法之道上極為強大。

column1: Be wary and alert, for you will be surrounded by enemies. Yet trust your instincts, my friend, for you may still make allies by your actions.
column2: 務必警醒戒備,因為你將四面皆敵。但相信你的直覺,我的朋友,你的作為仍可能為你贏得盟友。

column1: All of us in this city will be hoping for your safety, for all know of the seriousness of your mission. May fortune smile upon you, %s.
column2: 這座城裡的每一個人都會盼著你平安,因為人人都明白你此行任務的重大。願好運對你微笑,%s。

column1: Keep your weapon handy, Hero, and don't let your enemies get you. You and I maybe practice some more when you get back.
column2: 武器隨身帶好,英雄,別讓敵人逮著你。等你回來,咱倆或許還能再練幾手。

column1: If you will permit it, my friend, perhaps you and I could practice with weapons before you go. I happen to know some skills with weapons that may help you on your mission.
column2: 若你願意,我的朋友,動身前咱倆或許能切磋一番。我恰好懂得幾招兵器技巧,興許能助你完成任務。

column1: The tusks could only have come from a rather large specimen of "Hephalumpis Poohrobinus."
column2: 這對象牙只可能來自某隻體型相當龐大的「噗噗象 Hephalumpis Poohrobinus」。

column1: s You cannot remove the pair of hephalump tusks.
column2: 你拔不下這對噗噗象牙。

column1: 'Tis a tricky task talking to taxidermal tusks.
column2: 跟標本象牙搭話,可是樁棘手活兒。

column1: The standard Guild Hall quest board, one of the less unusual things in this room, has an assortment of notices posted.
column2: 這是公會大廳標準配備的任務告示板,算是這房裡比較尋常的東西,上頭貼滿了五花八門的公告。

column1: A native Fricanian shield and two crossed spears adorn the part of the wall above the doorway.
column2: 一面富利卡尼亞土著盾牌與兩柄交叉長矛,裝飾著門口上方那片牆面。

column1: The shield has been in Uhura's family for many generations.
column2: 這面盾牌在烏胡拉家族裡已傳了好幾代。

column1: You have no use for these weapons. You prefer your sword to a spear and it would take a lot of practice to handle that large shield correctly.
column2: 這些兵器你用不上。比起長矛你更愛你的劍,而要正確使喚那面大盾,還得下不少苦功。

column1: These weapons are of no use to you. You never learned how to wield shields or spears.
column2: 這些兵器對你沒用。你從沒學過怎麼使盾牌或長矛。

column1: There are people in the world who prefer to let their weapons do the talking for them, but you're sure Uhura isn't one of them.
column2: 這世上有些人寧願讓兵器替自己說話,但你確信烏胡拉不是那種人。

column1: Some carpets on the floor make up for the lack of chairs in this room. You wonder in what manner ol' Wolfgang Abenteuer would spend the time here.
column2: 地上鋪的幾張地毯,彌補了這房裡沒椅子的不便。你不禁好奇,老沃夫岡·阿本陶爾會用什麼方式在這兒打發時光。

column1: This place might get a little uncomfortable if you decided to steal the rugs.
column2: 要是你打算偷走這些毯子,這地方恐怕會變得有點不好待。

column1: You're pretty sure Uhura doesn't keep flying carpets in here, and even if she did, you wouldn't know the words to command them.
column2: 你相當確定烏胡拉不會在這兒擺飛毯,就算她真擺了,你也不曉得驅使它們的咒語。

column1: The doorway on the left is blocked off by a curtain. You assume it leads to Uhura's private quarters.
column2: 左邊的門被一道簾子擋住。你猜那後頭通往烏胡拉的私人居室。

column1: No need to call out. There be nobody in there at the moment.
column2: 不用喊啦,這會兒裡頭沒人。

column1: The doorway on the right seems to lead to the practice area.
column2: 右邊那道門似乎通往練武場。

column1: Trying to open a door that isn't there would just make you look silly.
column2: 想去開一扇根本不存在的門,只會讓你看起來傻裡傻氣。

column1: As you noticed, everyone who used to be in the training area is now here, staring at you while you're calling out to nobody in particular.
column2: 你也注意到了,原先在練武場的人全擠到這兒來,直盯著你對著空氣大呼小叫。

column1: There be nobody in the training area at the moment. It used to be busier here in the past.
column2: 這會兒練武場裡沒人。從前這兒可熱鬧多了。

column1: Hujambo, Uhura. Greetings, %s.
column2: 呼將波,烏胡拉。你好啊,%s。

column1: Hujambo, Uhura. Greetings, young adventurer.
column2: 呼將波,烏胡拉。你好啊,年輕的冒險者。

column1: Sijambo, Rakeesh.
column2: 西將波,拉基什。

column1: Word around town says that an Elemental of Fire ravages the southern plaza. I hope for the safety of your friends.
column2: 城裡傳言說有個火元素正在南廣場肆虐。我但願你的朋友們都平安。

column1: If it is within your capabilities to do something about the Fire Elemental, I urge you to take action before it is too late, my friend. If not, then I suggest that you and your friends vacate the inn as soon as possible.
column2: 倘若你有能耐對付那火元素,我勸你趁還來得及趕緊動手,我的朋友。若是不能,那我建議你和朋友們盡快撤離客棧。

column1: I am certain you have heard about the Elemental of Air by now. The strong winds it produces are noticeable throughout the entire city. It may not seem as destructive as the Fire Elemental at first, but that will change soon enough.
column2: 我敢說你這會兒已經聽說那風元素了。牠掀起的狂風,全城都感受得到。乍看之下牠或許不如火元素那般具有破壞力,但很快就不是這麼回事了。

column1: The wind grows stronger and stronger. Defeating something that is not tangible may seem impossible, my friend. Even though it has a well-known weakness, exploiting this weakness is a difficult thing to do.
column2: 風愈刮愈猛。要擊敗一個摸不著的東西,聽來或許像是天方夜譚,我的朋友。儘管牠有個眾所周知的弱點,可要利用這弱點卻是樁難事。

column1: I fear, my friend, that the city will not stand for very long if the Air Elemental is not stopped. Soon, the Elemental will grow strong enough to level the Shapeirian Palace.
column2: 我擔心啊,我的朋友,風元素若不加以阻止,這座城撐不了多久。要不了多久,那元素就會強到足以把夏皮爾王宮夷為平地。

column1: For now, the Earth Elemental is still vulnerable to a conventional force. But time is running short. Soon, it will have drawn enough strength from Shapeir's stones to crush the entire city in one blow.
column2: 眼下那土元素還抵擋不住尋常武力。但時間不多了。再過不久,牠就會從夏皮爾的磚石中汲取夠多力量,一擊便能把整座城碾碎。

column1: I asked the Sultan to order the populace not to approach the fountain at any cost. The Water Elemental's very touch will drain the last drop of fluid from the body of anyone who approaches.
column2: 我已請蘇丹下令,要百姓無論如何都別靠近那座噴泉。誰要是靠近,水元素只消一碰,就會把那人體內最後一滴水分都榨乾。

column1: However, it will not be long before people become parched and desperate. It is a matter of time before the citizens of Shapeir begin falling prey to the Elemental. Water is life itself in this land.
column2: 然而要不了多久,人們便會口乾舌燥、走投無路。夏皮爾的市民淪為那元素的犧牲品,只是遲早的事。在這片土地上,水即是生命本身。

column1: Time is running out, I am afraid, and so is the city's water supply. Either the lack of water will destroy Shapeir, or the flood that follows it. I am afraid the fate of the city is, once again, in your hands, Hero.
column2: 恐怕時間快用完了,城裡的水源也是。不是缺水毀了夏皮爾,就是隨之而來的大水。恐怕這座城的命運,又一次落到了你手上,英雄。

column1: Word of the Fire Elemental's defeat is already spreading. You have done a very good job, Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 火元素被擊敗的消息已經傳開了。你幹得真漂亮,史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: Your victory over the Air Elemental has reached far and wide. The citizens of Shapeir are very relieved that the creature has been captured.
column2: 你戰勝風元素的事蹟已傳遍四方。夏皮爾的市民都鬆了一大口氣,慶幸那怪物終於被擒。

column1: I have heard that things have already returned to normal at the Gate Plaza. It is most fortunate that you were able to defeat the Elemental before it caused too much damage.
column2: 我聽說城門廣場那邊已經恢復正常了。所幸你趕在那元素造成太大破壞之前就把牠擊敗了。

column1: I am certain you now have many admirers in town. However, be sure not to let pride get the better of you.
column2: 我敢說你如今在城裡有不少仰慕者了。不過,千萬別讓驕傲沖昏了頭。

column1: The Sultan is well aware of the selfless actions you have taken on behalf of the city. In time, you may be granted an audience with him.
column2: 蘇丹很清楚你為這座城所做的無私之舉。假以時日,你或許能獲得晉見他的機會。

column1: So, how was your meeting with Sultan man today?
column2: 那麼,你今天跟蘇丹老兄會面得怎麼樣啊?

column1: It would be foolish to assume that the Air Elemental was the final disaster to be unleashed upon this city. The guards shall do their best to keep the population safe and out of harm's way should another Elemental arrive.
column2: 若以為風元素就是降臨這座城的最後一場災難,那可就太天真了。萬一又有元素出現,衛兵會盡全力保護百姓、讓他們遠離危險。

column1: However, when it comes to defeating those horrid creatures, %s, the city must depend on resourceful individuals such as yourself.
column2: 然而,真要說擊敗那些可怕的怪物,%s,這座城還得仰仗像你這般足智多謀的人。

column1: I am glad you managed to succeed where I could not. You have slain a mighty foe today.
column2: 我很高興,我辦不到的事你卻辦成了。你今天斬殺了一個強敵。

column1: It is a good thing that people can safely walk the streets again, thanks to you.
column2: 多虧了你,人們又能安心走在街上了,這真是件好事。

column1: People all over the city are talking about your triumph over the Elemental of Water. You have saved the very heart of Shapeir.
column2: 全城的人都在談論你戰勝水元素的事蹟。你拯救了夏皮爾的命脈所繫。

column1: I see you are still carrying a container of the Water Elemental's essence. Hold on to it, my friend, as I foresee it will serve you well in the future.
column2: 我看你還帶著一罐水元素的精華。好好留著,我的朋友,我預感它將來會對你大有用處。

column1: I see it be getting late. I think I better be closing the Guild now. Please come back tomorrow.
column2: 我看天色不早了。這會兒我該把公會關門啦。請明天再來吧。

column1: I see it be getting late. It be bedtime for Simba. Kwa heri, friend.
column2: 我看天色不早囉。辛巴該睡覺啦。誇赫里,朋友。

column1: Take care and come back soon, young adventurer.
column2: 保重,早點再來,年輕的冒險者。

column1: Farewell and may we meet again soon, %s.
column2: 珍重,願我們不久後再相見,%s。

column1: May good fortune watch over you while you are in Raseir, %s. This city will be looking forward to your return.
column2: 願好運在你身處拉希爾期間護佑著你,%s。這座城會盼著你歸來。

column1: Take care and come back soon, %s.
column2: 保重,早點再來,%s。

column1: You say farewell and leave the Guild Hall.
column2: 你道別後,離開了公會大廳。

column1: You say farewell to Uhura and Rakeesh and leave the Guild Hall.
column2: 你向烏胡拉與拉基什道別,離開了公會大廳。

column1: You sign your name in the book with a flourish.
column2: 你龍飛鳳舞地在簿子上簽下自己的名字。

column1: You have already signed it. Perhaps you'll be able to update your entry at some point in the near future.
column2: 你已經簽過了。也許不久後你會有機會更新自己的條目。

column1: This Guild Hall is more colorful and primitive than others that you have seen. As is the custom, artifacts are displayed on the walls, notices are posted on the quest board, and the emblem of the Eternal Order of Fighters is prominent.
column2: 這座公會大廳比你見過的其他幾座更繽紛、也更原始。按照慣例,牆上陳列著各式文物,任務告示板貼著公告,而永恆戰士團的徽記則格外醒目。

column1: You get a feel for the general area.
column2: 你大致感受了一下這片地方的氛圍。

column1: The Hall isn't big enough to produce a good echo.
column2: 這座大廳不夠大,激不起像樣的回音。

column1: The last entry is "%s, Hero of Spielburg."
column2: 最後一條條目寫著「%s,史畢柏格的英雄」。

column1: The log is for the most part written in a language you can't read.
column2: 這本記事簿絕大部分是用一種你看不懂的文字寫成的。

column1: The book may have tales to tell, but this isn't the way to find out.
column2: 這本簿子或許藏著不少故事,但這可不是把它們挖出來的法子。

column1: One does not just join the Eternal Order of Fighters, nor is this the place if you could.
column2: 永恆戰士團可不是你說加入就能加入的,就算能,這兒也不是入團的地方。

column1: If you got real guts[and don't know the meaning of fear;[If you got real muscles[and don't take guff from no one;[If you got a real weapon[and know how to use it;[then maybe you are good enough[to join the[Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 你若有真膽量[且不知恐懼為何物;[你若有真肌肉[且不受任何人鳥氣;[你若有真兵器[且懂得如何使喚它;[那你或許就夠格[加入[永恆戰士團。

column1: We pick only the best to join and we'll be watching you, so don't just be good -- be great! Be a member of EOF![[Eternal Order of Fighters[The Guts, the Glory, the Greatest!
column2: 我們只挑最頂尖的入團,而且我們會盯著你,所以別只是好——要好到頂天!來當永恆戰士團的一員吧![[永恆戰士團[膽量、榮耀、最強悍!

column1: This is a logo of the Eternal Order of Fighters. There is some text written on the plaque.
column2: 這是永恆戰士團的標誌。牌匾上寫著一些文字。

column1: Some Fricanian drums sit in one corner of the room. You wonder if Uhura ever uses them.
column2: 房間一角擺著幾面富利卡尼亞鼓。你好奇烏胡拉到底用不用得上它們。

column1: You find out that your drumming skills aren't nearly good enough to play more than a few beats.
column2: 你發現自己這點打鼓本事,頂多敲個幾下,差得遠呢。

column1: Beat it!
column2: 滾開!

column1: The pillows look comfortable enough.
column2: 這些枕頭看起來挺舒服的。

column1: The pillows are very soft to the touch.
column2: 這些枕頭摸起來非常柔軟。

column1: You could try to smother yourself with one of these, but you wouldn't look very heroic doing so.
column2: 你倒是可以拿其中一個把自己悶死,只不過那副模樣可一點兒也不英雄。

column1: Uhura prefers very heavy spears for their greater impact.
column2: 烏胡拉偏愛很重的長矛,為的是更大的衝擊力道。

column1: These spears don't look very suitable for combat. They're probably just here to decorate the place.
column2: 這些長矛看起來不太適合實戰。它們八成只是擺在這兒當裝飾的。

column1: That would only result in Uhura getting upset.
column2: 那麼做只會惹烏胡拉不高興。

column1: The table looks solid enough.
column2: 這張桌子看起來挺結實的。

column1: Uhura wouldn't approve of that.
column2: 烏胡拉可不會贊成那麼做。

column1: I wooden't do that.
column2: 我「木」勸你別那麼做。

column1: A few primitive axes are leaning against the wall.
column2: 幾把原始的斧頭靠在牆邊。

column1: You're not very skilled in the use of these things.
column2: 你可不太擅長使這玩意兒。

column1: Don't axe. Don't tell.
column2: 別「斧」問,我也別「斧」答。

column1: The pot holds Uhura's water supply.
column2: 這口鍋裝著烏胡拉的存水。

column1: It's cumbersome carrying a pot of water around.
column2: 拎著一鍋水到處跑,可真不方便。

column1: You have your own water supply. Don't live off somebody else's.
column2: 你自己有水。別占別人的便宜。

column1: You're better off paying the fountain a little visit after leaving here.
column2: 你離開這兒以後,還是去噴泉那兒走一趟比較好。

column1: You're better off refilling your waterskins at the fountain, Hero.
column2: 你還是到噴泉那兒把水袋裝滿比較好,英雄。

column1: An old shield is gathering dust against the wall.
column2: 一面舊盾牌靠在牆邊積著灰。

column1: Uhura doesn't approve of theft. Better leave it where it is.
column2: 烏胡拉可不贊成偷竊。還是把它擱在原處吧。

column1: You blow some dust off the shield.
column2: 你吹掉了盾牌上的一些灰塵。

column1: The bow and quiver on the wall are for times of war.
column2: 牆上的弓與箭袋是為戰時備著的。

column1: Your archery skills need some serious improvement before this bow would be of any use to you.
column2: 你那點射箭本事得好好精進一番,這把弓對你才派得上用場。

column1: It's not Laura.
column2: 這可不是蘿拉。

column1: This creature's head looks like that of an overgrown canine, but if the plaque is to be believed it must have been separated from a humanoid torso. The plaque reads: "Jackalman slain by Sweeping Sir James."
column2: 這頭顱看起來像隻過分巨大的犬科動物,但若牌匾所言不假,牠原本必是接在一具人形軀幹上的。牌匾上寫著:「豺人,由橫掃詹姆斯爵士所斬殺。」

column1: XThe Guild Hall would lack a certain charm if you were to take the stuffed heads away.
column2: 你若把這些標本頭顱搬走,公會大廳怕是要少了幾分韻味。

column1: You doubt the creature was ever capable of speaking to begin with, but if it was that ability is now gone.
column2: 你懷疑這怪物打從一開始就不會說話,就算牠會,如今這本事也沒了。

column1: The stuffed head brings back memories of your encounters with those lovable Saurus Rexes in Spielburg. This relative doesn't look as bulky, but seems every bit as mean. The plaque reads: "Terrorsaurus slain by Rakeesh Sah Tarna."
column2: 這顆標本頭顱勾起了你在史畢柏格遭遇那些可愛索魯斯雷克斯的回憶。這位親戚看起來沒那麼壯碩,可凶狠勁兒卻是一點不差。牌匾上寫著:「恐魯斯,由拉基什·薩·塔爾納所斬殺。」

column1: The Guild Hall would lack a certain charm if you were to take the stuffed heads away.
column2: 你若把這些標本頭顱搬走,公會大廳怕是要少了幾分韻味。

column1: Sauruses were never known for their extensive vocabulary. Dead Sauruses are no exception to this rule.
column2: 索魯斯從來就不以詞彙豐富著稱。死掉的索魯斯也不例外。

column1: You really don't want to start carrying that head around again.
column2: 你實在不想再把那顆頭顱帶在身上到處跑了。

column1: You'd rather not. What if it started snarling back?
column2: 你寧可不要。萬一牠又齜牙咆哮起來怎麼辦?

column1: The head on the wall resembles a human skull with a thin layer of dead skin covering it. The mouth of the creature is filled with rotting teeth. Its mere appearance is enough to ruin your appetite. The plaque below it reads: "Ghoul slain by %s."
column2: 牆上那顆頭顱像是一具人類頭骨,蒙著薄薄一層死皮。怪物的嘴裡盡是腐爛的牙齒。光是這副模樣就足以叫你倒盡胃口。底下的牌匾寫著:「食屍鬼,由%s所斬殺。」

column1: It's the head of the rare southern moose. You can tell by the hat.
column2: 這是稀有的南方駝鹿的頭。看那頂帽子就知道。

column1: Uhura would have your head off before you got ten feet. Besides, there is no baby market in Shapeir.
column2: 你還沒跑出十呎,烏胡拉就會把你的腦袋砍下來。再說,夏皮爾可沒有寶寶市場。

column1: If the moose comes loose due to excessive use, your very own head might replace this one's use.
column2: 要是這駝鹿因為被你頻頻把玩而鬆脫,搞不好你自己的腦袋就得頂上來補這個缺。

column1: Where's Rocky?
column2: 洛基跑哪兒去了?

column1: That be my place to stay. I think you shall not go there.
column2: 那是我住的地方。我看你還是別進去的好。

column1: I have trouble remembering names, but I never forget a fez.
column2: 我記名字老是記不牢,可一頂土耳其氈帽我可從來不會忘。

column1: There will be given to the one who restores the Emir Arus al-Din to his rightful place in the Palace at Raseir the sum of 1000 dinars. By order of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid.
column2: 凡能使埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁重返拉希爾王宮、回到其應有之位者,將獲賞一千第納爾。蘇丹哈倫·拉希德諭令。

column1: He who is willing to brave the desert should seek Harik Attar in his shop at the sign of the mortar and pestle by the fountain. That one shall find rewards for his courage.
column2: 凡願冒險穿越沙漠者,當往噴泉旁掛著研缽與杵招牌的店鋪,尋訪哈里克·阿塔爾。此人將因其勇氣而獲得酬賞。

column1: The Way of the Paladin:[To seek.[To learn.[To do.
column2: 聖騎士之道:[求索。[修習。[實踐。

column1: Those who do service to the city and land of Shapeir shall know the rewards of goodness. By order of the Sultan Harun al-Rashid.
column2: 凡為夏皮爾之城與土地效力者,終將領略善行之回報。蘇丹哈倫·拉希德諭令。

column1: There is needed for a spell of great power the whirling part of a Dervish. Inquire at the Magic Shop. Keapon Laffin, prop.
column2: 欲施一道大威力法術,需取苦行僧旋轉之部分。詳情請洽魔法店。店主:奇彭·拉芬。

column1: Despite the heaviness of your backpack, you feel your load magically lighten and you are able to walk unencumbered once again. However, you expect this to only be the case for the duration of your stay in WIT's halls.
column2: 儘管你的背包沉甸甸的,你卻感到肩上的重擔奇異地輕了下來,又能毫無負累地走動了。不過你料想,這只會在你逗留於 WIT 殿堂期間管用。

column1: You may go no further.
column2: 你不得再往前一步。

column1: You wonder: wouldn't it have been better to have endured your trial by fire?
column2: 你不禁尋思:當初忍受那場烈火試煉,豈不更好?

column1: That was the Wiz, that was
column2: 那就是巫師,沒錯啦。

column1: If you are truly so eager to practice the forbidden arts, go ahead and seek out this individual on your own. You are no longer welcome here. Go!
column2: 你若當真這般急著修習禁術,那就自個兒去尋訪此人吧。這裡再也不歡迎你了。走!

column1: Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, Archmage Extraordinaire, your would-be initiate has returned.
column2: 紹貝爾格的伊拉斯謨大師,非凡的大法師,您那位準入門弟子回來了。

column1: Very good. Now, young Hero of Spielburg, remember: To try and fail there is no shame...
column2: 很好。如今,年輕的史畢柏格英雄,記住:嘗試而失敗,並不可恥……

column1: As long as you can pass the blame?
column2: 只要你能把過錯推給別人?

column1: As long as you improve your aim.
column2: 只要你能改進你的準頭。

column1: Just don't make your excuse lame! (Heh, heh)
column2: 可別把藉口找得太爛就行!(嘿,嘿)

column1: However, we see that you do not yet have knowledge of all the spells required. Go then, and master the spells available to you. Return when you think yourself ready.
column2: 然而,我們看出你尚未習得所有必要的法術。那就去吧,把你已能取得的法術都練到精通。等你自認準備妥當,再回來。

column1: Walk the path of wizardry.
column2: 踏上巫術之道。

column1: Earth Wizard
column2: 土系巫師

column1: Air Wizard
column2: 風系巫師

column1: Water Wizard
column2: 水系巫師

column1: Fire Wizard
column2: 火系巫師

column1: Who are you that enters the lofty portals of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 踏入巫師技術學院巍峨門戶的你,究竟是何人?

column1: Who seeks to enter?
column2: 何人求入?

column1: It is the ivory path.
column2: 這是象牙之道。

column1: The door at the end of the path leads somewhere strange, no doubt.
column2: 這條路盡頭的那扇門,八成通往某個怪地方。

column1: Low Voice
column2: 低沉的嗓音

column1: How rude.
column2: 真沒禮貌。

column1: Fluid Voice
column2: 流暢的嗓音

column1: He doesn't seem to be house trained.
column2: 他似乎還沒學會規矩。

column1: Breathy Voice
column2: 氣音般的嗓音

column1: I say we scatter him to the four winds.
column2: 我說,把他打散到四面八方去吧。

column1: Ardent Voice
column2: 熱切的嗓音

column1: No, he's merely young and impetuous.
column2: 不,他只是年輕氣盛罷了。

column1: Begone! You may return when you have learned to behave.
column2: 退下!等你學會規矩,再回來吧。

column1: The path beneath you is so smooth, it feels like your feet simply glide across it when you walk.
column2: 腳下的路平滑無比,走起來雙腳簡直像是滑行而過。

column1: There are two intricately carved creatures -- probably dragons -- placed above either side of the path.
column2: 路的兩側上方各擺著一隻雕工精細的生物——大概是龍吧。

column1: You can't reach them.
column2: 你搆不到牠們。

column1: While they do remind you of the Gargoyle who sat atop Erasmus' house in Spielburg, it's obvious that these particular dragons are nothing more than statues. Consequently, they won't speak to you.
column2: 牠們確實讓你想起史畢柏格伊拉斯謨家屋頂上的那隻石像鬼,不過很明顯,這兩條龍不過是雕像罷了,自然不會跟你說話。

column1: It is the ornate column.
column2: 這是根華麗的石柱。

column1: You can't reach it from here and thus, can't touch it.
column2: 你從這裡搆不著它,因此也碰不到。

column1: This is it... the WIT.
column2: 就是這裡了……巫師技術學院 WIT。

column1: You've heard jokes where the wit passed well over your head, but this takes the cake.
column2: 你聽過不少笑話,玄機(wit)從你頭頂飛過你卻沒聽懂,但這個可真是登峰造極。

column1: This is the decorative frieze.
column2: 這是裝飾用的雕帶。

column1: This is the ornamental frieze.
column2: 這是花飾雕帶。

column1: You can't reach anything over there from where you stand.
column2: 站在這裡,你那邊什麼都搆不著。

column1: The portrait shows Harry Houdini in a typical state of intense concentration.
column2: 這幅肖像畫的是哈利·胡迪尼,一副他招牌式的全神貫注模樣。

column1: The portrait shows a wizard with a severe nature. He appears totally humorless.
column2: 這幅肖像畫的是位神情嚴峻的巫師,看起來毫無幽默感。

column1: The portrait of Erasmus appears to be a duplicate of the one you remember hanging in his house on Zauberberg. You think that perhaps he has a few more crinkles around his eyes.
column2: 這幅伊拉斯謨的肖像,看來和你記憶中掛在他札烏柏格家中的那幅一模一樣。你覺得他眼角的皺紋大概又多了幾條。

column1: That's funny! He doesn't look anything like the way Peter Ledger drew him in Conquests of Camelot.
column2: 真有意思!他長得跟彼得·萊傑在《卡美洛的征服》裡畫的完全不一樣。

column1: That's funny! He doesn't look anything like Sam Neill.
column2: 真有意思!他長得一點也不像山姆·尼爾。

column1: The portrait is politely formal, befitting Aziza the Enchantress.
column2: 這幅肖像端莊正式,正符合女術士阿姿莎的氣質。

column1: Like most of the wizards, Zara's expression is serious and intent. She shows little propensity towards humor.
column2: 和多數巫師一樣,薩拉的神情嚴肅而專注,幾乎看不出半點幽默感。

column1: You always wondered what Erana looked like. She reminds you of Genesta, a Faerie you once knew.
column2: 你一直好奇伊瑞娜長什麼模樣。她讓你想起珍奈絲塔,一位你曾認識的精靈。

column1: The picture is entirely black.
column2: 這幅畫一片漆黑。

column1: You peer into the limitless void and see nothing but infinite blackness.
column2: 你凝視著無垠的虛空,除了無盡的黑暗,什麼也看不到。

column1: A void of caution: hands off!
column2: 虛空提醒一句:別亂碰!

column1: The void eats up any words as soon as you utter them.
column2: 話一出口,就被虛空吞得一乾二淨。

column1: Torch sconces line the walls. Each one bears a magical green flame.
column2: 牆上排著一列火炬燭台,每一座都燃著魔法的綠色火焰。

column1: Blowing the magical flames out would not be an ideal way to impress your judges.
column2: 把這些魔法火焰吹熄,可不是博取評審好感的好辦法。

column1: The back wall stretches up into the black void. On it, you see a door. You wonder where it leads.
column2: 後方的牆一路向上延伸,沒入漆黑的虛空。牆上有一扇門,你不禁猜想它通往何處。

column1: You think it best to keep your hands to yourself in this place.
column2: 在這種地方,你覺得還是別亂動手為妙。

column1: Aziza, Enchantress of Shapeir, Wizard of Water: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 夏皮爾的女術士、水之巫師阿姿莎:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: I cannot pass judgment upon the candidate until I have worked with him for some months. I am unwilling to have someone I do not know well as my initiate.
column2: 在與這位候選人共事數月之前,我無法對他下定論。我不願收一個我不甚了解的人當入門弟子。

column1: You must choose another sponsor.
column2: 你必須另選一位舉薦人。

column1: Houdini, Prestidigitator Paramount of far-away lands, Master of Escape: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 胡迪尼,遠方異域的至尊幻術師、脫逃大師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear a man's distant voice, apparently under great stress.
column2: 你聽見一個男人遙遠的聲音,顯然正承受著極大的壓力。

column1: Mmmmph! I'm (gasp) a bit tied up (m-m-mmmmph!) at the moment, uh... uh... (ack!) The name escapes me. Now, you really MUST excuse... (choke)... me.
column2: 唔唔!我(喘)現在有點……脫不開身(嗚嗚嗚!),呃……呃……(啊!)名字一時想不起來。現在,你真的得讓我先……(嗆)……失陪了。

column1: Ad Avis, Wizard of the Southern Sands, Enchanter of the Elements: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 南方沙漠之巫師、元素的施法者阿德·阿維斯:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear a man's voice, deep and sonorous.
column2: 你聽見一個男人低沉而洪亮的嗓音。

column1: So, a want-to-be wizard seeks me for a master? I am not interested in half-trained novices.
column2: 哼,一個想當巫師的小子來求我當師父?我對半吊子的新手沒興趣。

column1: However, should you actually become a wizard, you might be of use to me. There is much I can teach you.
column2: 不過,倘若你真成了巫師,或許對我還有些用處。我能教你的東西可多著呢。

column1: Until that time, find someone else to paper-train you.
column2: 在那之前,另找個人來幫你打基礎吧。

column1: Zara Shashina, Wizard of the Eastern Woods, Emissary of the Faerie Folk: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocracy?
column2: 東方森林之巫師、精靈族使者薩拉·夏希娜:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: Outside of the few spells I gave him, I have no knowledge of his skills or abilities. I will not accept responsibility for him.
column2: 除了我教他的那幾道法術之外,我對他的技能和能耐一無所知。我不會為他擔負這份責任。

column1: There is a moment of silence.
column2: 一陣沉默。

column1: Do you actually request such a...[whimsical wizard to be your sponsor?
column2: 你當真要請這麼一位……[古靈精怪的巫師當你的舉薦人?

column1: It was the choice he made.
column2: 這是他自己的選擇。

column1: But you know what Erasmus is like. Do we have to endure his sense of levity?
column2: 可你也知道伊拉斯謨那德性。我們非得忍受他那一套輕浮的玩笑不可嗎?

column1: It was the initiate's decision. Let him abide with it.
column2: 這是入門弟子自己的決定。就讓他承擔吧。

column1: Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, Archmage Extraordinaire: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 札烏柏格的伊拉斯謨大師、非凡的大法師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: You hear the familiar voice of Erasmus.
column2: 你聽見伊拉斯謨熟悉的嗓音。

column1: Let me see...[%s...[Ah, yes. The young Hero who was so bright.
column2: 我瞧瞧……[%s……[啊,沒錯。就是那位聰明伶俐的年輕英雄。

column1: You hear the squeaky voice of Fenrus.
column2: 你聽見芬魯斯尖細的嗓音。

column1: You think anyone who laughs at one of your jokes to be intelligent.
column2: 凡是會被你的笑話逗笑的人,你都覺得他聰明。

column1: A good sense of humor is an excellent indicator of superior intellect.
column2: 良好的幽默感,正是過人智慧的絕佳指標。

column1: Yes, but the keyword is 'good.' Heh, heh.
column2: 沒錯,可關鍵字是「良好」。嘿嘿。

column1: Erasmus! Do you or do you not accept %s?
column2: 伊拉斯謨!你到底接不接受 %s?

column1: I, Magister Erasmus of Zauberberg, do hereby accept %s as my initiate for WIT.
column2: 我,札烏柏格的伊拉斯謨大師,在此正式接受 %s 成為我在 WIT 的入門弟子。

column1: Wizard's Institute of Technocery.
column2: 巫師技術學院。

column1: Of course. Now, Hero of Spielburg, before you can be initiated into WIT...
column2: 當然。好了,史畢柏格的英雄,在你正式入門 WIT 之前……

column1: ...Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you must first prove you are magically and mentally worthy. You will be presented with a choice of three objects.
column2: ……也就是巫師技術學院,你得先證明自己在法術與心智上都夠格。你面前會擺出三件物品供你選擇。

column1: Using the spells at your command, you must choose the correct object, move it to the stand, and cause it to ring using only three spells. The choice of the first two spells should be self-evident, and the third is also...
column2: 運用你所掌握的法術,你必須選對正確的物品,把它移到架上,並讓它響起來——而且只能用三道法術。前兩道法術的選擇應該不言自明,第三道也同樣……

column1: Oh, all right. Well, good luck, young Hero, and remember the joke about the man who grew more mellow as he grew balder...
column2: 噢,好吧。那麼,祝你好運,年輕的英雄,還記得那個關於某人越禿頭脾氣越好的笑話嗎……

column1: He no longer had hair-brained schemes?
column2: 因為他不再有那些毛躁的餿主意(hair-brained)了?

column1: No, he no longer had such a hair-triggered temper.
column2: 不,是因為他不再那麼一觸即發(hair-triggered)了。

column1: Very well then, young Hero, please step into the dark room. Purely a formality. Don't worry about a thing.
column2: 那麼,年輕的英雄,請走進那間暗室吧。純粹只是個形式,什麼都不必擔心。

column1: Do you wish to take the tests?
column2: 你想接受測驗嗎?

column1: Ouch, my forehead!
column2: 哎喲,我的額頭!

column1: To our regret, she has not answered our summons for many years. You must choose another sponsor.
column2: 很遺憾,她已多年未回應我們的召喚。你必須另選一位舉薦人。

column1: Trismagister Merlyn Ambrosius, Wizard of the Isle of Gramarye: do you accept %s to be your initiate into the Wizard's Institute of Technocery?
column2: 三重大師梅林·安布羅修斯,格拉瑪萊島之巫師:你是否接受 %s 成為巫師技術學院的入門弟子?

column1: Have we met before? No, I don't believe so. Sorry.
column2: 我們以前見過嗎?不,我想沒有。抱歉。

column1: Is it ignorance or folly behind that decision?
column2: 這決定背後,是無知,還是愚蠢?

column1: Are you aware of who you are requesting to train you?
column2: 你可知道,你請來訓練你的是何方人物?

column1: The one you are referring to has been exiled from the Wizard's Institute of Technocery and will not be able to train you HERE.
column2: 你所指的那位,早已被逐出巫師技術學院,在「這裡」是無法訓練你的。

column1: Do you abide with your decision?
column2: 你要堅持這個決定嗎?

column1: You find yourself in a strange place. For some reason, you cannot move your feet, although you see nothing constraining them. A strange feeling passes through you, as though someone were examining you closely.
column2: 你發覺自己置身於一個怪異的所在。不知怎地,你的雙腳動彈不得,卻又看不見有什麼束縛著它們。一股奇異的感覺穿過你的全身,彷彿有人正在仔細端詳著你。

column1: As you begin to cast the spell...
column2: 就在你開始施法之際……

column1: You hear the voice of Erasmus echoing in this chamber.
column2: 你聽見伊拉斯謨的嗓音在這間石室裡迴盪。

column1: The spells to cast and their proper order should be obvious. They are to me.
column2: 該施哪些法術、按什麼順序施,應該很明顯。至少對我來說是。

column1: That's because you made up this test.
column2: 那是因為這測驗本來就是你出的。

column1: At any rate, Hero, you should take some time and think about the situation next time. Adieu.
column2: 無論如何,英雄,下次你該花點時間好好想想眼前的狀況。再會了。

column1: Very good, %s. You have shown yourself very capable in figuring out the spells needed for the pretest.
column2: 很好,%s。你已充分展現出本事,順利想通了預試所需的法術。

column1: However, before you can be considered a true wizard, you must pass the Trial of Initiation which the wizards of WIT will give you.
column2: 然而,在被認定為真正的巫師之前,你還必須通過 WIT 諸位巫師為你準備的入門試煉。

column1: I warn you, this test will take all of your wits and magical skill in order to succeed. If you succeed, you will then face... but I can say no more.
column2: 我警告你,這場測驗得用上你全部的機智與法術才有可能過關。倘若你成功了,接下來你將面對……不過我不能再多說了。

column1: Say no more.
column2: 別再說了。

column1: So, Hero, good luck!
column2: 那麼,英雄,祝你好運!

column1: Promises, promises. You said you would say no more! (heh, heh)
column2: 說好的呢、說好的呢。你說過不再多說的!(嘿嘿)

column1: Oh, Fenrus.
column2: 唉,芬魯斯。

column1: Good luck, %s.
column2: 祝你好運,%s。

column1: It's not real obvious at first what this does, but it's intricately carved.
column2: 乍看之下,看不太出來這東西有什麼用,不過雕工倒是十分精細。

column1: If it looks like a bell and it sounds like a bell, it's a bell.
column2: 看起來像鈴,聽起來也像鈴,那它就是個鈴。

column1: You can't get any of the bells.
column2: 這些鈴你一個也拿不到。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to any of the bells.
column2: 別費勁去跟這些鈴說話了。

column1: Ask not for whom the bell tolls... it tolls for YOU.
column2: 別問喪鐘為誰而鳴……它就是為「你」而鳴。

column1: She was the belle of the ball 'til someone tolled on her.
column2: 她本是舞會上的紅顏(belle),直到有人去告了她一狀(tolled)。

column1: Darkness surrounds you in all directions.
column2: 黑暗從四面八方將你包圍。

column1: You must now stand into the wind.
column2: 現在,你必須迎風而立。

column1: The wizard seems to have left behind his staff.
column2: 那位巫師似乎把他的法杖留下了。

column1: The wizard's staff spins faster and faster.
column2: 巫師的法杖越轉越快。

column1: We see that you haven't enough magical power remaining to finish our tests. We dismiss you from our presence.
column2: 我們看得出,你剩下的法力已不足以完成我們的測驗。我們准你退下了。

column1: The ends of the staff are spinning much too rapidly.
column2: 法杖兩端轉得實在太快了。

column1: That didn't seem to do anything.
column2: 那似乎沒起什麼作用。

column1: That didn't work.
column2: 沒用。

column1: You can't get the wizard's staff.
column2: 你拿不到那位巫師的法杖。

column1: The pathway appears suspended in this timeless, dimensionless void. It leads off into infinity.
column2: 這條小徑彷彿懸浮在這片無時無刻、無維無度的虛空之中,一路通向無垠。

column1: The scenery is awe-inspiring, but unchanging.
column2: 這景象令人嘆為觀止,卻一成不變。

column1: You've just managed to click on Roger Wilco, who is still floating around lost in space, time, sequels, and every other dimension, waiting for you to purchase his latest adventure and help him out of his latest bind.
column2: 你剛好點到了羅傑·威爾科——他依然在太空、時間、續作以及其他各種維度裡漂泊迷失,就等著你掏錢買他最新一集的冒險,好幫他脫離最新一次的困境。

column1: You've clicked on a speck of dimensionless void dust.
column2: 你點到了一粒無維無度的虛空塵埃。

column1: The last time you were in a dimensionless void, it was vast. But this one's only half vast.
column2: 上回你身處無維虛空時,它浩瀚無垠(vast)。可這一片頂多只能算半吊子(half vast)罷了。

column1: A dimensionless, endless void, trapped between space, time, and the restroom of a cheap diner.
column2: 一片無維無盡的虛空,被困在太空、時間,以及某家廉價小餐館的廁所之間。

column1: You've never seen a dimensionless void this big before. Not dimension -- this other void over here.
column2: 你從沒見過這麼大的無維虛空。不是說維度——是這邊這另一片虛空啦。

column1: That's neither here nor there.
column2: 這既不在這兒,也不在那兒(無關緊要)。

column1: That's almost halfway to Cleveland.
column2: 那都快走到克利夫蘭的半路上了。

column1: There used to be nothing here. But not anymore.
column2: 這裡本來什麼都沒有。但現在不一樣了。

column1: There's not much to do there.
column2: 那兒沒什麼好做的。

column1: Don't say another void.
column2: 別再提虛空(void)了。

column1: You get no response
column2: 你沒得到任何回應

column1: Picture this -- a wizFramed wizard.
column2: 想像一下——一個被裱進畫框(wizFramed)的巫師。

column1: It's an ancient-looking man in a wizard suit.
column2: 那是個一身巫師裝束、看上去蒼老的男人。

column1: You must now face raging earth and stone.
column2: 現在,你必須面對狂暴的土石。

column1: The wall is made of earth and stone.
column2: 這道牆是由泥土與石塊砌成的。

column1: The wall looks back at you with a menacing look in its eyes.
column2: 那道牆回望著你,眼神中透著一股威嚇之意。

column1: The wall appears docile for the time being.
column2: 這道牆暫時看來還算溫馴。

column1: It's difficult to look behind you and maintain your footing at the same time, but it seems the wall has recovered from your spell.
column2: 要一邊回頭、一邊穩住腳步可不容易,不過那道牆似乎已經從你的法術中恢復過來了。

column1: That was about as useful as bouncing a Flame Dart off of a brick wall.
column2: 那大概就跟拿火鏢往磚牆上彈一樣管用。

column1: You really shouldn't get him angry. You wouldn't like him when he's angry.
column2: 你真的不該惹他生氣。他發起火來,你可不會喜歡。

column1: It seems to have absorbed your spell's energy.
column2: 它似乎把你法術的能量給吸收掉了。

column1: That was about as useful as bouncing a Force Bolt off of a brick wall.
column2: 那大概就跟拿力矢往磚牆上彈一樣管用。

column1: You don't have the ability to Fetch such a huge object.
column2: 你還沒本事用「取物」術搬動這麼龐大的東西。

column1: It sure LOOKED easy.
column2: 看「起來」是挺容易的。

column1: It's too slippery to climb.
column2: 太滑了,爬不上去。

column1: You've got to be kidding.
column2: 你在開玩笑吧。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the earthen wall.
column2: 別費勁去跟這道土牆說話了。

column1: You perform a quick equipment check. Everything seems to be in place.
column2: 你迅速檢查了一遍裝備,看來樣樣都還在。

column1: You must now overcome the subtle strengths of water and ice.
column2: 現在,你必須克服水與冰那難以察覺的力量。

column1: It is a gigantic piece of ice blocking the path.
column2: 一塊巨大的冰擋住了去路。

column1: The fissure in the ice seems to be its weakest spot.
column2: 冰上的那道裂縫,似乎正是它最脆弱的地方。

column1: The Force Bolt has no effect against such a solid mass of ice.
column2: 面對這麼一塊堅實的冰,力矢術毫無作用。

column1: You need to target your spell better to use the iceberg's weakness against it.
column2: 你得把法術瞄得更準,才能利用這座冰山的弱點來對付它。

column1: You seem to have melted the hoarfrost. Now you can see that the iceberg is fractured.
column2: 你似乎把那層白霜給融化了。現在你看得出,這座冰山已經裂開。

column1: You seem to just be melting some of the massive iceberg.
column2: 你似乎只是把這座龐大冰山的一小部分融化掉而已。

column1: You don't have the ability to move such a huge object.
column2: 你還沒本事搬動這麼龐大的東西。

column1: The ice is too solid. You will need to break the fissure first.
column2: 這冰太堅實了。你得先把那道裂縫弄開才行。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the iceberg.
column2: 別費勁跟那座冰山講話了。

column1: You must now withstand the fury of my flames.
column2: 現在,給我承受烈焰之怒吧!

column1: There is an open door in the middle of the pathway.
column2: 通道中央有一扇敞開的門。

column1: There is a door blocking the pathway.
column2: 一扇門擋住了通道。

column1: With the door open, the fire's heat is more intense.
column2: 門一開,火焰的熱氣更猛烈了。

column1: The door resists your flame.
column2: 那扇門抵擋住了你的火焰。

column1: The flames seem to burn more intensely.
column2: 火焰似乎燒得更旺了。

column1: The door seems to have absorbed your spell.
column2: 那扇門似乎把你的法術吸收掉了。

column1: The flames have died down, only to reveal a hole burned into the pathway.
column2: 火焰漸漸熄滅,卻在通道上燒出了一個洞。

column1: The flames are as calm as they're going to get.
column2: 火焰已經平息到不能再平息了。

column1: That turned down the heat, but the flames are burning through the door again.
column2: 那壓低了熱度,可火焰又開始燒穿那扇門了。

column1: Say, have you figured this out, or are you just playing it by ear?
column2: 喂,你到底想通了沒?還是純粹在亂試碰運氣?

column1: It seems to have no effect on the closed door.
column2: 對那扇緊閉的門似乎毫無作用。

column1: This doesn't look good. The fire is just getting hotter.
column2: 情況不妙,火越燒越燙了。

column1: You did it! You're at WIT's end.
column2: 你辦到了!你已經走到 WIT 的盡頭。

column1: Don't bother talking to the door.
column2: 別白費口舌跟門講話了。

column1: Don't bother talking to the flames.
column2: 別白費口舌跟火焰講話了。

column1: There are some flames behind the door.
column2: 門後有一些火焰。

column1: Don't play with fire, Hero.
column2: 別玩火,英雄。

column1: Knock knock, who's there?
column2: 叩叩叩,是哪位?

column1: Heat who?
column2: 「熱」哪位?

column1: Heat who hesitates is lost!
column2: 猶豫不決的人就要被「熱」掉啦!

column1: Now stop wasting time.
column2: 好了,別再浪費時間了。

column1: You hear the voices.
column2: 你聽見了那些聲音。

column1: Our time is much too valuable to be wasted upon one with so little magic. Do not bother returning until you have acquired more power.
column2: 我們的時間太寶貴,可不能浪費在魔力如此低微之人身上。等你修得更強的力量,再回來吧。

column1: You will certainly have to be more clever and more skilled if you hope to be initiated. Perhaps you should practice your spells more.
column2: 你若想通過入門考驗,勢必得更機靈、更有本事才行。或許你該多練練你的法術。

column1: A door appears.
column2: 一扇門出現了。

column1: That didn't have any effect on the door.
column2: 那對那扇門毫無作用。

column1: The wall seemed to absorb your spell's energies.
column2: 那面牆似乎把你法術的能量吸收掉了。

column1: Having already passed the trials of the Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you discover that your Open spell is no longer effective on the door.
column2: 你既已通過巫師技術學院的試煉,便發覺你的「開啟」術對這扇門已不再管用。

column1: This door doesn't have a lock.
column2: 這扇門沒有鎖。

column1: The prince of Shapeir he's not.
column2: 他可稱不上是夏皮爾的王子。

column1: You can't reach him.
column2: 你搆不著他。

column1: He doesn't look like he has time to stop and chat.
column2: 他看起來沒空停下來閒聊。

column1: He's not interested in your possessions.
column2: 他對你身上的東西沒興趣。

column1: Apparently, you're now in the land where this mysterious magician resides. It seems the wizards of WIT didn't really go out of their way to pick a pleasant place for you to appear.
column2: 看來你現在身處這名神祕巫師居住的國度。WIT 那群巫師顯然沒費什麼心思,替你挑個舒服點的落腳處。

column1: In fact, they may have very well chosen the worst landing spot...
column2: 事實上,他們挑的搞不好是最糟糕的降落點……

column1: With your current skills, there's no way you can survive in a hostile environment like this. Now you know why Quest for Glory III didn't take place here.
column2: 憑你目前的本事,在這種險惡的環境裡是絕對活不下來的。現在你總算明白,《英雄傳奇III》為什麼不選在這裡上演了吧。

column1: Vuja de?
column2: 既視感反過來?

column1: This be the training area. Sometimes Rakeesh and I spar here when his leg be not hurting.
column2: 這裡就是訓練場。拉基什的腿不疼的時候,有時候我倆會在這兒過招。

column1: If you have some spare time, we can practice a bit together.
column2: 你要是有空,咱倆可以一起練練。

column1: You may be warrior, but there still be plenty of things to learn about combat.
column2: 你或許是戰士,但搏鬥這門學問,還多得是東西要學。

column1: You may not be warrior, but basic combat knowledge still be useful to keep you alive.
column2: 你或許不是戰士,但懂點基本的搏鬥門道,還是能保你一命。

column1: Swinging your sword, then moving back into neutral position often leaves openings in your enemy's defense unexploited.
column2: 揮劍之後就退回中立架式,往往會白白放過敵人防禦上露出的破綻。

column1: Next time you fight, try following up a normal swing with a secondary swing from left to right. You will be able to do a lot more damage that way.
column2: 下回交手,試著在一記普通揮砍之後,緊接一記由左往右的補刀。這樣能打出多得多的傷害。

column1: Double swings can wear down an enemy's defense, but still leave your opponent standing and thus in a position to mount an offense.
column2: 連環雙砍能磨掉敵人的防禦,但對手仍站得住腳,還能伺機反撲。

column1: Next time you fight, try following up an inversed swing with an overhead slash. This will knock most opponents off their feet when it connects.
column2: 下回交手,試著在一記反向揮砍之後,接一記由上往下的劈斬。一旦命中,多數對手都會被掀翻在地。

column1: An opponent whose attack you just ducked will be off-balance for a split-second. By reacting quickly, you can catch him off-guard with a quick retaliatory attack.
column2: 你剛閃過攻擊的對手,會有那麼一瞬間重心不穩。只要反應夠快,就能趁他沒防備,快速回敬一擊。

column1: Next time you fight, try swinging your weapon in an upward arc after ducking a high attack. Your opponent will need quick reflexes to avoid it.
column2: 下回交手,蹲身閃過高位攻擊後,試著朝上劃出一道弧形揮擊。對手得有極快的反應才躲得掉。

column1: An opponent whose attack is blocked by your shield will recoil for a moment. A keen swordsman can use that opportunity to launch a quick counterattack.
column2: 攻擊被你的盾牌擋下的對手,會有那麼一下子往後一頓。眼明手快的劍客就能抓住這空檔,迅速還以一擊。

column1: Next time you fight, try launching a quick swing after the enemy's attack bounces off your shield.
column2: 下回交手,在敵人的攻擊被你盾牌彈開之後,試著快速揮出一刀。

column1: When you catch your opponent's blade with your own weapon, you will be in an opportune position to quickly retaliate, as long as you keep your wits about you.
column2: 當你用自己的兵器架住對手的刀刃時,只要你保持冷靜,就佔到了迅速反擊的有利位置。

column1: Next time you fight, try launching a quick swing after the enemy's attack bounces off your blade.
column2: 下回交手,在敵人的攻擊被你的刀刃彈開之後,試著快速揮出一刀。

column1: Dodging attacks be good, but against multiple enemies, you may run out of room quickly.
column2: 閃避攻擊是好事,可一旦面對多名敵人,你很快就會沒地方閃了。

column1: If you get into fight with a pack of Jackalmen, protect your flanks by kicking approaching foes in the chest while focusing on the main attacker.
column2: 你要是跟一群豺狼人打起來,就一邊盯緊主攻的那個,一邊踢逼近敵人的胸口,護住自己的側翼。

column1: Jumping back is often the most inefficient way to defend yourself, as you give up terrain and you will be left in a position where a quick counterattack is not an option.
column2: 向後跳開往往是最沒效率的防守方式,因為你讓出了地盤,還會陷入無法迅速反擊的處境。

column1: Yet, by using your instincts, you will be able to launch the occasional counterattack against your foe, doing large amounts of damage.
column2: 不過,只要靠著直覺,你偶爾也能對敵人發動反擊,打出大量傷害。

column1: When you avoid attacks by jumping backward, the leaping lunge may be triggered at random.
column2: 當你以向後跳的方式閃避攻擊時,有機率隨機觸發騰躍突刺。

column1: Jumping over a low attack is a good way to avoid damage, but a quick secondary attack by the enemy may still catch you off guard when you land.
column2: 跳過低位攻擊是躲傷害的好辦法,但你落地時,敵人快速的第二擊仍可能殺你個措手不及。

column1: Next time you fight, try executing an overhead slash while coming down from a jump.
column2: 下回交手,試著在跳躍下落的同時,使出一記由上往下的劈斬。

column1: Being on feet be better than being on ground. You cannot attack while on ground. When being hit, use agility to land on feet and keep guard up.
column2: 站著總比躺地上強。倒在地上你就沒法攻擊。挨打時,靠你的敏捷穩穩落地,把架式撐住。

column1: Daggers are incapable of inflicting large wounds, but their light weight makes it easy to string attacks together.
column2: 匕首沒法造成大傷口,但它輕巧,容易把一連串攻擊串起來。

column1: Next time you fight, try following up a swing with a plunging attack. This will compensate for the limited damage your weapon can do.
column2: 下回交手,試著在一記揮砍後接一記下捅。這能彌補你兵器傷害有限的不足。

column1: You be plenty handy with daggers. If you be carrying additional daggers, throwing them at enemy be good way to weaken him.
column2: 你使匕首挺順手嘛。你要是多帶幾把,朝敵人擲過去也是削弱他的好法子。

column1: My land and people be many months travel from here.
column2: 我的故土和族人,離這兒有好幾個月的路程。

column1: Scorpion be not very smart fighter, so keep eye on what attacks they use. Learn to predict what comes next.
column2: 蠍子不是什麼聰明的鬥士,所以盯緊牠用的招數,學著預判牠下一步。

column1: Brigands be only moderate fighters, but they can still kill unprepared victims. They sometimes carry throwing daggers, so keep guard up at distance too. It usually be best to stay close to them.
column2: 強盜的身手只算普通,可照樣能宰了沒準備的倒霉鬼。他們有時候帶飛刀,所以隔著距離也得把架式撐住。對付他們,通常還是貼近了打最妥當。

column1: Ghouls be powerful, but their power has limits. Defend until their energy runs dry and don't let them steal more from you or recover power. Attack while they be weak.
column2: 食屍鬼很強,但牠的力量有極限。一路防守到牠能量耗光,別讓牠再從你身上奪取力量、也別讓牠回血。趁牠虛弱時下手。

column1: If you fight Jackalmen pack, you must keep moving at all times. Trading hits be very bad idea. Hit and run to slowly wear the pack down while keeping yourself alive.
column2: 跟一群豺狼人打,你就得時刻不停地移動。跟牠們對拚硬挨是極糟的主意。打了就跑,一邊保命,一邊慢慢把這群傢伙磨垮。

column1: Terrorsaurus cannot defend, but can take lots of hits. Quick stabs halt its attacks, but you still need good dodging skills so you do not die while wearing it down. Wearing it down takes a long time.
column2: 恐爪龍不會防守,可牠超耐打。快速戳刺能打斷牠的攻擊,但你還是得有過硬的閃避本事,才不會在磨牠的過程中先掛了。把牠磨垮可得花上好一陣子。

column1: If you need to know magic, ask a magic person. I am warrior, not wizard.
