column2: 你要是想懂魔法,去問懂魔法的人。我是戰士,不是巫師。

column1: My man works at the Sultan's palace. It be to the east of here.
column2: 我男人在蘇丹的王宮做事。王宮就在這兒的東邊。

column1: I tell you that before, Hero. You be forgetting already?
column2: 這話我早跟你說過了,英雄。你這就忘啦?

column1: I am sorry, but I know little of the art of fighting with a dagger and thus would not be an appropriate sparring partner for you.
column2: 不好意思,我對用匕首搏鬥的門道所知不多,當不了你合適的陪練對手。

column1: My friend, you should first recover some of your energy or take a stamina pill.
column2: 我的朋友,你該先恢復點體力,或者吃顆耐力丸。

column1: A Ghoul is a very dangerous thing to fight. Its very touch draws your life from you. It can use that life to strengthen the magical essence that is animating its body.
column2: 食屍鬼是極危險的對手。光是被牠碰一下,你的生命力就會被抽走。牠還能拿那股生命力,去強化驅動牠軀體的魔法本源。

column1: Ghouls can cast spells by drawing upon that essence and their magical powers recover over time. The best way to defeat a Ghoul is to let it exhaust its energy reserves and then kill it before it recovers its power.
column2: 食屍鬼能汲取那股本源來施法,而且魔力會隨時間慢慢恢復。要打贏食屍鬼,最好的辦法就是逼牠把儲備的能量耗盡,趁牠尚未回復力量之前一舉了結牠。

column1: The Terrorsaurus is very swift and aggressive. You will only survive an encounter with one if you manage to avoid its relentless attacks long enough to kill it. Try to wear it down with quick stabs when it moves in to attack.
column2: 恐爪龍既迅捷又凶猛。唯有你能避開牠那連綿不絕的攻擊,撐得夠久把牠耗死,才有可能在跟牠交手後活著走出來。趁牠逼近攻擊時,試著用快速戳刺把牠磨垮。

column1: Brigands search the desert for unwary travelers. They will kill gladly and steal whatever they can. Their damage potential is very limited though. As long as you keep your guard up, they should pose a minimal threat.
column2: 強盜在沙漠裡四處搜尋掉以輕心的旅人。他們殺人毫不手軟,能搶的一概不放過。不過他們造成傷害的能耐相當有限。只要你把架式撐好,他們頂多算個小威脅。

column1: When a pack of Jackalmen attack, it is the weakest which strikes first. Focus on the main attacker until that foe is defeated, or you will find yourself overwhelmed by their numbers.
column2: 一群豺狼人來襲時,最弱的那隻會先出手。盯緊主攻的那個,直到把牠解決掉,否則你會被牠們的數量淹沒。

column1: Make sure to remain on the move all the time to avoid being ganged up on. Focus on avoiding damage at first, so do not end up getting hit from multiple sides at once.
column2: 務必時刻保持移動,免得被圍攻。一開始先以閃傷為重,才不會落到同時被好幾面夾擊的下場。

column1: If escape is not an option, observe the Scorpion carefully. Scorpions are not very creative attackers and by watching their movements, you will soon be able to predict their next action. Use that to your advantage.
column2: 要是逃不了,就仔細觀察那隻蠍子。蠍子的攻擊套路了無新意,只要盯著牠的動作,你很快就能預判牠的下一步。好好利用這一點。

column1: I personally do not prefer their use.
column2: 我個人並不愛用那玩意兒。

column1: Spells may save your life in combat, provided you know how to use them effectively.
column2: 只要你懂得有效運用,法術在戰鬥中或許能保你一命。

column1: The doorway leads back to the main Guild Hall.
column2: 這道門通回公會大廳。

column1: If you wish to leave through the doorway, your feet will be more useful than your hands.
column2: 你要是想從這道門離開,你的腳會比你的手管用得多。

column1: You'd look more than a little silly calling out to the doorway while everyone is here in the room with you.
column2: 大夥兒都跟你待在這屋裡,你還對著門口大喊大叫,看起來可不只是有點傻而已。

column1: There's a niche with a bench in the back wall. It's probably where the spectators are seated during competitions.
column2: 後牆有個帶長凳的壁龕。比武的時候,觀眾大概就坐在那兒。

column1: You didn't come here to take a break. There won't be any action in here unless you participate in it.
column2: 你來這兒可不是為了歇腳的。除非你親自下場,否則這裡不會有什麼好戲。

column1: You say a few words to your non-existent audience.
column2: 你對著根本不存在的觀眾說了幾句話。

column1: You're standing in an impressive-looking training room. Racks with weapons line the walls and a large mosaic pattern marks the combat area. The place seems well-kept and frequently used.
column2: 你正站在一間氣派的訓練室裡。兵器架沿牆排開,一大片馬賽克圖案標出了搏鬥區。這地方看來保養得宜,使用頻繁。

column1: It be getting dark outside already. No more training for today. Kwa heri, friend.
column2: 外頭天都黑啦,今天不練了。Kwa heri(再會),朋友。

column1: Farewell and take care, my friend.
column2: 珍重,保重啊,我的朋友。

column1: You say goodnight to Uhura and leave the Guild Hall.
column2: 你向烏胡拉道了晚安,離開公會大廳。

column1: You remove the Zap charge from your weapon.
column2: 你把附在兵器上的電擊充能解除了。

column1: You want me to explain basic combat actions before we start for real?
column2: 要我在咱們真正開打之前,先講講基本的搏鬥動作嗎?

column1: This spear and Fricanian shield appear to be Uhura's combat equipment.
column2: 這把長矛和弗里坎尼亞盾,看來是烏胡拉的戰鬥裝備。

column1: Since you're not trained in their use, there's little point in trying to fight with them.
column2: 你既沒受過使用它們的訓練,硬拿來打也沒多大意義。

column1: That's odd. No response.
column2: 真奇怪,沒反應。

column1: A large tile pattern on the floor indicates the battle area. It's large enough to allow multiple duels at the same time.
column2: 地板上一大片磚紋標出了戰鬥區。它大得足以同時容納好幾場對決。

column1: The pattern on the ground feels slightly smoother than the rest of the floor.
column2: 地上那片圖案,摸起來比地板其餘部分稍微滑一些。

column1: En garde.
column2: 預備——出招!

column1: Simba looks excited. You wonder if his mother always allows him to attend combat training.
column2: 辛巴看起來興奮得很。你不禁納悶,他母親是不是總准他來看搏鬥訓練。

column1: They are fine satin drapes.
column2: 那是幾幅上好的緞面帷幔。

column1: Twin brass sconces are affixed to the back wall.
column2: 後牆上釘著一對黃銅壁燈座。

column1: There's no multi-headed Hydra in Shapeir, so you do not need a torch.
column2: 夏皮爾可沒有多頭海德拉,所以你用不著火把。

column1: Talking to the unlit torch wouldn't be a heated debate.
column2: 跟一支沒點燃的火把講話,可激不起什麼「火熱」的爭論。

column1: The growth of this indoor plant appears to be stunted from lack of sunlight.
column2: 這株室內植物,似乎因為缺乏日照而長得發育不良。

column1: Watch where you plant your hands!
column2: 你的手是要往哪「栽」啊,看著點!

column1: You decide to stick with Shema's cooking.
column2: 你還是決定乖乖吃舍瑪做的飯就好。

column1: Several tribal spears are sitting in the rack.
column2: 架上擺著好幾支部落長矛。

column1: Nah, you'll get to do that in Quest for Glory III.
column2: 算了吧,這檔事你留到《英雄傳奇III》再說。

column1: Spear me baby, one more time.
column2: 長矛刺我吧寶貝,再來一次。

column1: It's either a huge Nigerian flag, a ceremonial banner, or some kind of stretcher for carrying injured combatants out of the arena.
column2: 這要嘛是一面巨大的奈及利亞旗,要嘛是面儀式用的旗幟,要嘛是某種把受傷的鬥士抬出競技場的擔架。

column1: Leave that where it is.
column2: 那東西就擱原地別動。

column1: This looks like an executioner's axe, as it seems too large for practical combat.
column2: 這看起來像把劊子手的斧頭,因為它大得不像是實戰用的。

column1: Nah, you'll get to do that in Quest for Glory V.
column2: 算了吧,這檔事你留到《英雄傳奇V》再說。

column1: The woven basket contains a few sharp-tipped close-combat daggers.
column2: 那只編織籃裡裝著幾把尖頭的近戰匕首。

column1: These daggers are not well-balanced for throwing, and they are not yours to take anyway.
column2: 這些匕首重心不適合投擲,何況也輪不到你拿走。

column1: This sword probably belongs to one of the Shapeirian guards.
column2: 這把劍多半是某個夏皮爾衛兵的。

column1: You do not know how to wield a sword.
column2: 你不會使劍。

column1: You prefer your own sword.
column2: 你還是偏愛自己那把劍。

column1: You initiate some sharp conversation with the sword.
column2: 你跟那把劍展開了一番「鋒利」的對話。

column1: Wooden racks surround the combat area. Many weapons occupy them.
column2: 木製兵器架環繞著搏鬥區,上頭擺滿了各式兵器。

column1: You hear a soft groan coming from the room in the back. Maybe you should be quieter before you wake someone up.
column2: 你聽見後屋傳來一聲輕輕的呻吟。或許你該小聲點,別把人吵醒了。

column1: You hear someone stirring in the adjacent room.
column2: 你聽見隔壁房間有人翻身動了動。

column1: What was that?
column2: 那是什麼聲音?

column1: When deciding whether to stay or leave, it is a good thing to remember whether you were welcome to begin with.
column2: 在決定該留還是該走的時候,先想想自己當初到底受不受歡迎,總是件好事。

column1: Loitering is Lethal
column2: 逗留要命

column1: There's not much hope for a thief who can't even break the laws of gravity without getting himself hurt.
column2: 一個連違抗地心引力都會把自己摔傷的盜賊,實在沒什麼指望。

column1: And for my next trick...
column2: 接下來,看我變下一個戲法……

column1: The fall didn't harm you half as much as Rahat and his brother did after rushing in to see what the loud THUD was.
column2: 這一摔對你的傷害,還不及拉哈特和他兄弟衝進來查看那聲「砰」是怎麼回事之後,給你的傷害的一半。

column1: The fall didn't harm you half as much as Quadir and his brothers did after rushing in to see what the loud THUD was.
column2: 這一摔對你的傷害,還不及夸迪爾和他幾個兄弟衝進來查看那聲「砰」是怎麼回事之後,給你的傷害的一半。

column1: An experienced thief knows when not to press his luck.
column2: 老練的盜賊懂得什麼時候別太得寸進尺。

column1: Can't Take a Hint?
column2: 聽不懂暗示?

column1: Mother's jewelry box has been burgled. Qadir, come quickly!
column2: 娘的首飾盒被偷了。卡迪爾,快來!

column1: Mother's jewelry box has been burgled. Brother, come quickly!
column2: 娘的首飾盒被偷了。兄弟,快來!

column1: Smelling a rat, the guard and his brother conduct an extensive search of the house. You are quickly found and dragged off to the Sultan's dungeon.
column2: 那衛兵和他兄弟覺得事有蹊蹺,把屋子徹底搜了一遍。你很快就被揪了出來,拖進蘇丹的地牢。

column1: The small things
column2: 細微之處

column1: Qadir, you must hurry! Father's heirloom swords are missing!
column2: 卡迪爾,你得快點!爹的傳家寶劍不見了!

column1: Brother, you must hurry! Father's heirloom swords are missing!
column2: 兄弟,你得快點!爹的傳家寶劍不見了!

column1: The little details...
column2: 那些小細節……

column1: You suddenly hear voices outside.
column2: 你忽然聽見外頭傳來說話聲。

column1: Hey, Qadir! How was your guard duty?
column2: 嘿,卡迪爾!你站崗站得怎麼樣?

column1: Uneventful. What are you two doing here?
column2: 平安無事。你們倆來這兒幹嘛?

column1: We've come to pick up your brother. He'll be patrolling the western section with us tonight.
column2: 我們來接你弟弟。他今晚要跟我們一起巡西區。

column1: I'll go and see if he's ready to leave.
column2: 我去看看他準備好出門沒。

column1: Just a second. I'll talk to you guys after I wake up my brother.
column2: 等一下,我把弟弟叫醒再跟你們聊。

column1: You hear the sound of a key turning in the lock.
column2: 你聽見鑰匙在鎖孔裡轉動的聲音。

column1: Hey! What are you still doing in bed? Your buddies are already waiting for you outside!
column2: 喂!你怎麼還賴在床上?你的哥兒們都在外頭等你了!

column1: You hear someone groaning.
column2: 你聽見有人在哼哼唧唧。

column1: The slacker is still in the process of getting up. Why don't you two come inside for a while?
column2: 這懶骨頭還在慢吞吞地爬起來。你們倆要不要先進來坐坐?

column1: No thanks. We'll just do a quick patrol around the block and we'll be back in a few minutes.
column2: 不用了。我們先繞這街區巡一圈,幾分鐘後就回來。

column1: Suit yourself.
column2: 隨你便。

column1: Someday they're gonna cut your wages over this. Now get ready and don't make too much noise, huh?
column2: 哪天你會為了這種事被扣薪水的。快準備好,別弄出太大聲響,聽見沒?

column1: You suddenly hear a knock on the door.
column2: 你突然聽見敲門聲。

column1: They're already back. Get moving.
column2: 他們已經回來了。快動身。

column1: Yeah, yeah. Just give me a few more seconds.
column2: 好啦好啦,再給我幾秒鐘。

column1: Hey guys! Sorry to keep you waiting. I'm ready to go.
column2: 嘿各位!抱歉讓你們久等了。我準備好出發了。

column1: Hey, Rahat, we're going to take Nadim for a drink when tonight's shift is over. It was his birthday yesterday. You coming along?
column2: 嘿,拉哈特,今晚當完班我們要帶納丁去喝一杯。昨天是他生日。你要一起來嗎?

column1: Sure, sounds like a great plan!
column2: 當然,聽起來棒極了!

column1: Oh, drat. I forgot I'm flat broke. Can you guys lend me some change?
column2: 唉,糟糕。我忘了自己窮得叮噹響。你們能借我點零錢嗎?

column1: Not until you pay me back the four dinars you still owe me.
column2: 先把你欠我的那四個第納爾還來再說。

column1: Okay, okay. I'll just borrow some change from dad's stash and pay him back when I get my next salary.
column2: 好啦好啦,我去爸的私房錢裡借點,下次領薪水再還他。

column1: Are you really going to wake your parents up over this?
column2: 你真要為這點事把你爸媽吵醒?

column1: Of course not.
column2: 當然不會。

column1: I'll be back in a second.
column2: 我馬上回來。

column1: (Now where did he hide his savings again?)
column2: (他到底把私房錢藏哪兒了?)

column1: (Not here I guess...)
column2: (這裡大概沒有……)

column1: (Hmmm, not here either. Maybe I didn't look hard enough before.)
column2: (唔,這裡也沒有。也許我之前找得不夠仔細。)

column1: What are you doing out there milling about like that?
column2: 你在外頭晃來晃去的幹什麼?

column1: Father's stash has disappeared.
column2: 爸的私房錢不見了。

column1: Smelling a rat, Rahat and his brother conduct an extensive search of the house. You are quickly found and dragged off to the Sultan's dungeon.
column2: 拉哈特和他哥嗅出不對勁,把整間屋子翻了個底朝天。你很快就被揪出來,拖進蘇丹的地牢。

column1: Found out!
column2: 被逮個正著!

column1: Ah, there it is.
column2: 啊,在這兒呢。

column1: Rahat takes some coins from the bag and puts it back.
column2: 拉哈特從袋子裡拿了幾枚硬幣,又把袋子放回去。

column1: Let's go, guys.
column2: 走吧,各位。

column1: Rahat's scream alerts his brother and buddies who violently overwhelm you. By the time they're done with you, you're in an even worse condition than Rahat.
column2: 拉哈特的尖叫驚動了他哥和那群哥兒們,他們一擁而上把你打趴。等他們收拾完你,你的慘狀比拉哈特還慘。

column1: Violence begets violence.
column2: 暴力只會招來暴力。

column1: The cabinet is already open.
column2: 櫥櫃已經開著了。

column1: The hinges of the cabinet let out a slight squeaking noise.
column2: 櫥櫃的鉸鏈發出輕微的吱嘎聲。

column1: As your spell wears off, the cabinet's door falls back into place again with a thud.
column2: 法術效果一退,櫥櫃門「砰」地一聲又落回原位。

column1: Your spell has no effect on the drawer.
column2: 你的法術對抽屜毫無作用。

column1: Rahat yawns and takes a moment to get his bearings back.
column2: 拉哈特打了個呵欠,愣了一會兒才回過神來。

column1: As the pieces of crockery hurtle towards you, you realize that you can't possibly catch them all. Several fall to the floor, crashing loudly and alerting the off-duty guards in the next room.
column2: 瓷器朝你飛撲過來,你發現自己根本接不住全部。好幾件摔落地面,「哐啷」一聲巨響,驚動了隔壁房間下班的衛兵。

column1: The crockery was worthless, so your sentence was less severe than it might have been. (They cut only one of your hands off.)
column2: 那些瓷器一文不值,所以你的刑罰沒那麼重。(他們只砍了你一隻手。)

column1: Give me a hand
column2: 搭把手吧

column1: As it strikes the drapes, Rahat turns around and spots you. He calls for his brother and buddies who are angry. VERY angry. And violent. VERY, VERY violent.
column2: 東西打在帷幔上,拉哈特轉過身,一眼就看見你。他叫來他哥和那群哥兒們,他們火大了。非常火大。而且很暴力。非常非常暴力。

column1: As it strikes the drapes, Rahat rushes back into the room. He calls for his brother and buddies who are angry. VERY angry. And violent. VERY, VERY violent.
column2: 東西打在帷幔上,拉哈特衝回房裡。他叫來他哥和那群哥兒們,他們火大了。非常火大。而且很暴力。非常非常暴力。

column1: As it strikes the drapes, Rahat jumps from his bed. He calls for his brother and buddies who are angry. VERY angry. And violent. VERY, VERY violent.
column2: 東西打在帷幔上,拉哈特從床上一躍而起。他叫來他哥和那群哥兒們,他們火大了。非常火大。而且很暴力。非常非常暴力。

column1: Painful, VERY painful!
column2: 好痛,非常痛!

column1: Using the blade, you easily slice through the tangle of necklaces. As the loose chains fall back into the jewelry box, you are left holding an ornate jade bracelet in your hands. You quietly pocket it.
column2: 你用刀刃輕鬆割開纏成一團的項鍊。鬆開的鏈子紛紛落回珠寶盒,你手裡只剩下一只雕工精緻的玉鐲。你悄悄把它收進口袋。

column1: Your Open spell cannot unlatch the lock on this jewelry box, due to its intricate multi-tumbler-based mechanism.
column2: 你的開鎖術解不開這珠寶盒上的鎖,因為它用的是繁複的多重彈子機構。

column1: The jewelry box is already open.
column2: 珠寶盒已經開著了。

column1: This is not a good time to do that.
column2: 現在不是做那件事的好時機。

column1: You can't do that from up here.
column2: 你在上頭做不到那件事。

column1: That's impossible to do without announcing your presence.
column2: 那件事不可能在不暴露行蹤的情況下完成。

column1: You find several throwing daggers and a healing pill, which you take.
column2: 你找到幾把飛刀和一顆治療藥丸,順手收下。

column1: There are a lot of trinkets in the cabinet, but not a single one of them seems worth taking.
column2: 櫥櫃裡有一堆小玩意兒,但沒一樣值得拿走。

column1: You find a bag filled with coins in one corner of the cabinet, which you quickly take.
column2: 你在櫥櫃一角發現一袋裝滿硬幣的錢袋,迅速收下。

column1: You find a bag filled with coins in one corner of the cabinet, but it looks too heavy for you to take.
column2: 你在櫥櫃一角發現一袋裝滿硬幣的錢袋,但它看起來太重,你拿不動。

column1: There's a lot of uninteresting junk inside the cabinet.
column2: 櫥櫃裡盡是些索然無味的破爛。

column1: You take the bag of coins from the cabinet.
column2: 你從櫥櫃裡取走那袋硬幣。

column1: You take the bag of coins from the cabinet and add 75 dinars and 500 centimes to your purse.
column2: 你從櫥櫃裡取走那袋硬幣,錢包多了 75 第納爾和 500 生丁。

column1: You can't do that while you're hiding.
column2: 你躲著的時候沒辦法做那件事。

column1: There are a lot of trinkets in the drawers, but not a single one of them seems worth taking.
column2: 抽屜裡有一堆小玩意兒,但沒一樣值得拿走。

column1: You find a bag filled with coins in one corner of the drawer, which you quickly take.
column2: 你在抽屜一角發現一袋裝滿硬幣的錢袋,迅速收下。

column1: You find a bag filled with coins in one corner of the drawer, but it looks too heavy for you to take.
column2: 你在抽屜一角發現一袋裝滿硬幣的錢袋,但它看起來太重,你拿不動。

column1: There's a lot of uninteresting junk inside the drawers.
column2: 抽屜裡盡是些索然無味的破爛。

column1: You take the bag of coins from the drawer.
column2: 你從抽屜裡取走那袋硬幣。

column1: You take the bag of coins from the drawer and add 75 dinars and 500 centimes to your purse.
column2: 你從抽屜裡取走那袋硬幣,錢包多了 75 第納爾和 500 生丁。

column1: That's not necessary. The cabinet isn't locked.
column2: 沒這個必要,櫥櫃又沒上鎖。

column1: The hinges are already oiled.
column2: 鉸鏈已經上過油了。

column1: You apply some oil to the hinges of the cabinet.
column2: 你在櫥櫃的鉸鏈上抹了點油。

column1: You quietly put the bag of money back.
column2: 你悄悄把錢袋放了回去。

column1: You didn't go to all the trouble of breaking in and stealing this small fortune only to give it back again.
column2: 你費了這麼大勁破門而入、偷了這筆小財,可不是為了再原封不動還回去。

column1: A cabinet is built into the wall.
column2: 牆裡嵌著一個櫥櫃。

column1: Rahat's buddies were still waiting outside the house and you walked right into them. Time your escape a little better in the future.
column2: 拉哈特的哥兒們還在屋外等著,你正好一頭撞上他們。下次脫身得算準時機才行。

column1: Leaving so soon?
column2: 這麼快就走啦?

column1: It's the door that leads to the alley outside.
column2: 這扇門通往外頭的巷子。

column1: Two cheap pots adorn the dresser. Neither of them look very valuable.
column2: 梳妝台上擺著兩只廉價的罐子,看起來都不怎麼值錢。

column1: They're not valuable enough to take.
column2: 它們不夠值錢,不值得拿。

column1: A large curtain obscures one corner of the room.
column2: 一幅大帷幔遮住了房間的一角。

column1: A modest dresser adorns one corner of the room.
column2: 房間一角擺著一張樸素的梳妝台。

column1: The drapes cover a doorway that presumably leads to a bedroom.
column2: 帷幔遮著一道門口,大概通往臥房。

column1: Better stay away from there. You might wake up one of the occupants.
column2: 最好離那兒遠一點,你可能會吵醒裡頭的住戶。

column1: There's little of interest on the shelf.
column2: 架子上沒什麼值得留意的東西。

column1: Don't waste your time with the contents of the shelf.
column2: 別把時間浪費在架子上那些東西。

column1: A worn rug lies near the front door.
column2: 前門附近鋪著一張破舊的地毯。

column1: The rug looks too old and worn to bother taking, and there is nothing underneath it.
column2: 這地毯又老又破,不值得費事拿走,底下也沒藏什麼。

column1: A round rug and a few cushions lie in this part of the room.
column2: 房間這一處鋪著一張圓地毯,還擺了幾個坐墊。

column1: The cushions aren't worth pilfering, and taking the rug would involve moving the table, waking up the occupants of the house in the process.
column2: 坐墊不值得順手牽羊,而要拿地毯就得搬桌子,這一搬準會吵醒屋裡的住戶。

column1: The layout of the room reminds you a bit of the metalworker's house. You wonder if all residences in Shapeir look like this, or just the ones you're able to access.
column2: 這房間的格局讓你想起金匠的家。你不禁納悶,夏皮爾所有的住宅都長這樣,還是只有你進得去的這幾間?

column1: You are in the front room of a wealthy-looking house. It is nicely furnished.
column2: 你身處一間看似富裕人家的前廳,布置得相當講究。

column1: Keep your voice down. You're not supposed to be here, remember?
column2: 小聲點。別忘了,你本來就不該出現在這裡。

column1: There's no need to pick the lock on the front door.
column2: 沒必要去撬前門的鎖。

column1: The tabletop prosperities perched in proximity appear pricey to the precipitative eye. However, a perceptive purloiner's primary peek can pinpoint phony products.
column2: 桌面珍品近在咫尺,在毛躁的眼裡個個價值不菲。然而,精明的雅賊只消瞥上一眼,便能識破贗品。

column1: It's too early for a curtain call.
column2: 現在謝幕還太早了。

column1: The crockery is too worthless to consider taking.
column2: 這些瓷器太不值錢,根本不必考慮拿走。

column1: Some cheap crockery sits upon the upper shelves.
column2: 上層架子上擺著一些廉價瓷器。

column1: There's a small blue box on the table.
column2: 桌上有個藍色的小盒子。

column1: As expected, the jewelry box is locked.
column2: 不出所料,珠寶盒上了鎖。

column1: You open the jewelry box.
column2: 你打開珠寶盒。

column1: The solid silver scimitars high above the doorway are obviously ornamental.
column2: 門口上方高掛著兩柄純銀彎刀,顯然只是裝飾用的。

column1: You can't do anything with your items while your feet are off the ground.
column2: 你雙腳離地的時候,什麼道具都用不了。

column1: You have already dealt with that.
column2: 那件事你已經處理過了。

column1: The jewelry box is already unlocked.
column2: 珠寶盒已經開鎖了。

column1: This is a difficult lock to pick, with many small and intricate tumblers. You will need more practice.
column2: 這把鎖很難撬,裡頭有許多細小繁複的彈子。你還得多練練才行。

column1: Success! The lock snaps open.
column2: 成功!鎖「喀」地一聲彈開了。

column1: That won't help.
column2: 那沒用的。

column1: They're up there. You're down here.
column2: 它們在上頭,你在下頭。

column1: You carefully lift the twin ornamental swords from their mounting on the wall and put them away.
column2: 你小心翼翼地把那對裝飾雙劍從牆上的架座取下,收了起來。

column1: You need to get closer.
column2: 你得再靠近一點。

column1: Your arms aren't that long. You can't replace them from down here.
column2: 你的手臂沒那麼長,在下頭沒法把它們放回去。

column1: Exercising great caution, you quietly replace the twin ornamental swords back onto their mounting on the wall.
column2: 你萬分謹慎,悄悄把那對裝飾雙劍放回牆上的架座。

column1: The front door leads back to the alley outside.
column2: 前門通回外頭的巷子。

column1: Sifting through the box, you only find more cheap imitations.
column2: 你在盒子裡翻來翻去,找到的盡是些廉價的仿冒貨。

column1: You look again, but find nothing worth taking.
column2: 你又看了一遍,卻沒找到任何值得拿走的東西。

column1: You could not even pay anybody to take such worthless jewelry off your hands.
column2: 這種一文不值的珠寶,你就算倒貼錢也沒人肯收。

column1: The jewelry is junk.
column2: 這些首飾都是破爛。

column1: It has no worth.
column2: 毫無價值。

column1: You rifle through the box once again. There are many necklaces which have been twisted into a tangled jumble.
column2: 你又把盒子翻了一遍。裡頭有許多項鍊纏成了亂糟糟的一團。

column1: Wait! You spy something that looks valuable within the mass of tangled necklaces. However, you cannot get it free with your bare hands.
column2: 慢著!你在那團纏結的項鍊裡瞥見一樣看似值錢的東西。可是光憑赤手空拳,你沒法把它弄出來。

column1: You are only tangling the necklaces more with your bare hands. If you wish to liberate the item, you will need to think of something else.
column2: 你赤手空拳只會讓項鍊纏得更死。要想把那樣東西取出來,你得另想辦法。

column1: Upon close inspection the jewelry items in the box appear to be valueless replicas.
column2: 湊近一瞧,盒子裡的首飾全是不值錢的複製品。

column1: You close the jewelry box to hide the evidence of your crime.
column2: 你關上珠寶盒,湮滅犯罪的證據。

column1: I do not welcome those who are rude in my house.
column2: 我不歡迎在我家裡無禮的人。

column1: That was exhausting. You don't think you'd survive going through that again.
column2: 真是累壞了。你覺得自己再來一次恐怕撐不住。

column1: This alley has several well-tended plants lining the walls.
column2: 這條巷子的牆邊種著幾株照料得很好的植物。

column1: Someone has given these potted plants a lot of care, and they seem well-nourished.
column2: 有人對這些盆栽悉心照料,它們看起來生機盎然。

column1: The plants look good where they are.
column2: 這些植物擺在原處挺好的。

column1: Don't plant your lips there.
column2: 別把嘴湊上去親那兒。

column1: You find that you cannot move.
column2: 你發現自己動彈不得。

column1: I do not welcome those who try to force their entrance into my house.
column2: 我不歡迎硬要闖進我家的人。

column1: Persistence pays off, but this certainly isn't the payoff you had in mind!
column2: 堅持是有回報的,只不過這回報八成不是你想要的那種!

column1: Next time use your head... or maybe your knuckles!
column2: 下次動動腦子吧……或者,動動指關節敲門也行!

column1: It's taps for a zapped sap...
column2: 這蠢蛋被電翻,正好給他奏個喪曲送行……

column1: There is no response.
column2: 毫無回應。

column1: Welcome, %s.
column2: 歡迎,%s。

column1: Who is it that seeks to enter?
column2: 是誰想要進來?

column1: Perhaps you have learned some manners since the last time you came calling. That would be better. Who are you that now seeks entrance to my house?
column2: 也許你上回造訪之後,學會了些禮數。那樣就好多了。如今想進我家的,究竟是何人?

column1: There is no response. It seems even powerful enchantresses need to sleep sometimes.
column2: 毫無回應。看來再強大的女術士,偶爾也是要睡覺的。

column1: It is more polite to ask your leave by saying "farewell" before you leave the table.
column2: 離席之前先說聲「告辭」再走,這樣比較有禮貌。

column1: It is more polite to ask your leave by saying "farewell" before you depart from my presence.
column2: 要離開我面前之前先說聲「告辭」,這樣比較有禮貌。

column1: Since the beast is protected from my true sight, then it is probably not what it seems. You will need a dispel potion to find its true form.
column2: 既然那頭怪物連我的真知之眼都看不透,那它八成不是表面看起來那樣。你需要一瓶驅散藥水,才能看出它的真面目。

column1: You could not have forgotten already?
column2: 你不會這麼快就忘了吧?

column1: Perhaps it would be well to show you as well as to tell you about such things.
column2: 這種事,也許不只說給你聽,還該演示給你看才好。

column1: Should an Elemental of Water take up residence in Shapeir's fountain, the situation would be extremely perilous. Many would die of thirst.
column2: 倘若有一隻水元素盤踞在夏皮爾的噴泉裡,情勢就會危急萬分。許多人會因乾渴而死。

column1: catch fish
column2: 抓魚

column1: No bait. Sorry.
column2: 沒有魚餌,抱歉。

column1: eat fish
column2: 吃魚

column1: Hmm. If you only had some chips...
column2: 唔,要是你再有些薯條就好了……

column1: dark nature
column2: 黑暗本性

column1: Magic can be misused for ill purposes. A foe can seem to be a friend, and the victim is helpless against the foe. The more powerful the magic, the more likely that it can be used for evil purposes.
column2: 魔法可能被拿來作惡。敵人能裝成朋友,受害者卻對他毫無防備。魔法越強大,就越可能被用在邪惡的目的上。

column1: It is always hot outdoors, even in the winter months. My house is well protected against the heat.
column2: 外頭總是炎熱,連冬天也一樣。我這屋子隔熱做得很好。

column1: The Djinn are very powerful magical beings who roam this land. There are five types of Djinn: Djann, Djinn, Shaitan, Ifreet, and Marid.
column2: 精靈(Djinn)是漫遊在這片土地上、法力強大的存在。精靈共分五類:迪安(Djann)、精靈(Djinn)、夏伊坦(Shaitan)、伊夫利特(Ifreet)、與瑪利德(Marid)。

column1: Djinn are more powerful than Djann and rule the air, living in the clouds.
column2: 精靈比迪安更強大,主宰天空,棲身於雲層之中。

column1: Djann are seldom seen; they take the shape of animals and hide in the mountains to avoid outsiders.
column2: 迪安極少現身;牠們化作野獸的模樣,藏身山中以躲避外人。

column1: The Shaitan prefer streams and waterways.
column2: 夏伊坦偏好溪流與水道。

column1: The Ifreet are associated with fire and, as such, prefer the desert.
column2: 伊夫利特與火相連,因此偏愛沙漠。

column1: Most powerful and magical of all are the Marid, and the master of the Marid is Iblis.
column2: 其中法力最強的便是瑪利德,而瑪利德之主正是伊布利斯。

column1: Raseir was like a twin to Shapeir until a year ago this month. The Emir Arus was overthrown and disappeared. His brother now sits upon the throne. There is a darkness over the city through which I cannot see with all my magic.
column2: 直到一年前的這個月,拉希爾還像是夏皮爾的雙生姊妹。埃米爾阿魯斯被推翻、不知去向,如今坐上王位的是他的弟弟。那座城被一層黑暗籠罩,連我傾盡全部法力也看不透。

column1: This city was built around the magic fountain which flows freely. It is protected by the mountains and the desert and has known only peace and prosperity until now.
column2: 這座城是繞著那座自由流淌的魔法泉建起來的。它有群山與沙漠護衛,直到此刻都只嘗過和平與繁榮的滋味。

column1: Many things in this world are called monsters. To my mind, the worst monster is a man who calls himself a friend in order to betray.
column2: 這世上有許多東西被叫作怪物。在我看來,最可怕的怪物,是那種自稱朋友、卻只為背叛的人。

column1: The magic I work with most is water magic, that of knowledge and true seeing. As water can reflect, I too can reflect spells back upon the caster. There is much magic in this land, some of a very dark nature.
column2: 我最常使用的是水之魔法,那是知識與真視之術。正如水能映照,我也能將法術反彈回施法者身上。這片土地上魔法繁多,其中有些本質極為黑暗。

column1: The Sultan Harun al-Rashid is a most wise ruler. He often consults me for information about things. He has a good deal of understanding about people, and he knows how best to use them.
column2: 蘇丹哈倫·拉希德是位極有智慧的統治者。他常向我請教各種事務。他十分懂得看人,也深知如何適才適用。

column1: He is doing everything in his power to prevent the ill omens from coming to pass.
column2: 他正竭盡所能,設法阻止那些凶兆成真。

column1: The Emir Arus was a foolish man who ruled more with his heart than his head. There was magic involved in his disappearance. Since he has been gone, the city of Raseir has fallen into dark times, and many have fled the new tyranny.
column2: 埃米爾阿魯斯是個愚昧的人,治國憑的是心而非腦。他的失蹤牽涉到魔法。自他離開後,拉希爾城便陷入黑暗時局,許多人為逃離新的暴政而出走。

column1: Magical games are an entertaining way to keep one's magical skills and mind in shape.
column2: 魔法遊戲是個有趣的法子,能讓人的法術技藝與頭腦保持靈光。

column1: If you seek the Wizard's Institute of Technocery, you will need to use the very thing for which you seek it.
column2: 你若想找到巫師技術學院,就得用上你尋找它所要用到的那樣東西。

column1: The caravan is currently the only way most people can travel to Raseir or the lands beyond.
column2: 眼下,商隊是多數人前往拉希爾、或更遠之地的唯一途徑。

column1: The Ifreet make the hot desert sands their home. They are seldom seen.
column2: 伊夫利特以炎熱的沙漠為家,極少現身。

column1: I am no friend to thieves.
column2: 我可不是盜賊的朋友。

column1: My magic is very practical. I know many things that go on in this land. Unfortunately, Raseir has been closed to my far-seeing since the Emir vanished. Soon, I will try a different type of spell to see if I can gain more information.
column2: 我的魔法很實用。這片土地上發生的許多事,我都知道。可惜自從埃米爾消失後,拉希爾就對我的遠視之術封閉了。再過不久,我會試試另一種法術,看能不能探得更多消息。

column1: There have been many dark predictions concerning this land lately. You should consult with the Astrologer.
column2: 近來關於這片土地,出現了許多不祥的預言。你該去找占星師問問。

column1: Iblis once sought to rule the world and created a great city in the middle of the desert, but Suleiman ben Daoud summoned all the Djinn to rise against Iblis. Iblis was bound in a statue, and his mighty city was destroyed to become his tomb.
column2: 伊布利斯曾想統治世界,在沙漠正中央造了一座宏偉之城,但蘇萊曼·本·達烏德召集了所有精靈群起反抗他。伊布利斯被封印在一尊雕像裡,他那座雄城也被摧毀,成了埋葬他的墳塚。

column1: Suleiman ben Daoud was the mightiest and wisest of the magician-kings of ancient times.
column2: 蘇萊曼·本·達烏德是上古魔法君王中最強大、也最有智慧的一位。

column1: An Elemental trying to escape will be drawn to a container of an appropriate nature. If there is no container, then it will either flee or dissipate and reappear elsewhere.
column2: 想逃脫的元素精靈,會被一個性質相合的容器所吸引。若沒有容器,牠便會逃走,或是消散後在別處重新現身。

column1: A container can be created by choosing an appropriate receptacle for the element (such as a waterskin for water). Once the Elemental has entered the container, its magical essence will be retained.
column2: 你只要替那元素挑個合適的盛器(例如用水袋裝水),就能造出一個容器。元素精靈一旦進入容器,牠的魔法精華便會被牢牢留住。

column1: One who thus captures an Elemental will have power over it to use as he pleases.
column2: 如此擒住元素精靈的人,便能任意支配牠的力量。

column1: The contrary element or weakness of fire is water; the weakness of water is air; the weakness of air is earth; and the weakness of earth is fire.
column2: 火的剋星、也就是它的弱點,是水;水的弱點是風;風的弱點是土;而土的弱點是火。

column1: Weakening an Elemental will force it to seek escape and thus be captured in a suitable container.
column2: 削弱一隻元素精靈,會逼牠設法逃脫,如此便能將牠收進合適的容器裡。

column1: Miss Manners is a personal friend of mine. We often have tea together.
column2: 禮儀小姐是我的私交好友,我們時常一起喝茶。

column1: The creatures of water are my friends, but whatever you do, don't mention this subject to the owner of the Magic Shop.
column2: 水中的生靈都是我的朋友,但無論如何,千萬別在魔法商店老闆面前提這檔事。

column1: Oh, just the kinds of knickknacks and things we enchantresses are liable to pick up at garage sales and professional seminars.
column2: 喔,就是些小玩意兒罷了,我們這些女術士在二手拍賣和專業研討會上總愛順手撿些回來。

column1: I am not familiar with idle gossip.
column2: 我對那些無聊的閒話可不熟。

column1: The Astrologer is fond of making predictions of things to come. Some of the things of which he speaks actually come to pass.
column2: 占星師喜歡預言將要發生的事。他所說的某些事,確實應驗了。

column1: His poetry speaks more than mere words.
column2: 他的詩,道出的遠不只是字句而已。

column1: The Katta are a clever people. They work only at things they enjoy.
column2: 卡塔族是個聰明的民族。他們只做自己樂在其中的事。

column1: The Dervish is a very odd man. At times, I think he is just a fool, but every once in a while, he says something very wise.
column2: 那位苦行僧是個極古怪的人。有時我覺得他不過是個傻子,但偶爾,他又會說出極有智慧的話來。

column1: My name means 'Highly Prized.' It was the name of my mother.
column2: 我的名字意為「珍貴無比」。這也是我母親的名字。

column1: He is a most worthy and honorable paladin.
column2: 他是位極為高尚、極有榮譽的聖騎士。

column1: A paladin is one who lives with honor and courage throughout life.
column2: 聖騎士,是一生都以榮譽與勇氣立身行事的人。

column1: Keapon's magical abilities are great, but his style is much too childish. Still, if you must purchase his sort of toy magic, his shop is the place to visit.
column2: 奇朋的魔法本事是不小,只是他的路數太過孩子氣。話雖如此,你若非得買他那種玩具魔法,他的店倒是該去的地方。

column1: Water is the element of healing and life. I use it in all my magical workings.
column2: 水是治癒與生命的元素。我所有的魔法施作,都離不開它。

column1: My spells allow me to see things at a distance and to know their true nature.
column2: 我的法術能讓我遠觀萬物,並看清它們的真實本質。

column1: In the desert lands, sharing drinks is a ritual of faith and trust.
column2: 在沙漠之地,共飲一杯是一種信任與誠意的禮節。

column1: Ad Avis cares only for power. It would not greatly surprise me if he were behind the Darkness in Raseir.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯只在乎力量。若拉希爾那片黑暗背後是他在主使,我也不會太意外。

column1: The Apothecary should be able to create such a potion to restore the true form to an enchanted creature.
column2: 那位藥師應該能調配出這樣一帖藥水,讓中了法術的生靈恢復真身。

column1: There are two types of enchantment: that of the body and that of the mind. The one can change a person's form; the other makes a person believe what the caster wishes that person to believe.
column2: 附魔之術分兩種:作用於身的,與作用於心的。前者能改變一個人的形體;後者則讓人相信施法者要他相信的事。

column1: I know of a wizard who once lived in these lands, although he has been gone for many years. He used people as tools to gain power. His name was Ad Avis.
column2: 我曉得有個巫師曾住在這片土地上,雖然他已離去多年。他把人當作奪取力量的工具。他的名字,叫阿德·阿維斯。

column1: The magical fountain is a symbol for our city.
column2: 那座魔法泉,是我們這座城的象徵。

column1: You would know more about that than I.
column2: 那件事,你可比我清楚多了。

column1: When I try to use Far-see upon the city of Raseir, it is as though the city is wrapped in a web of blackness, which I cannot see through.
column2: 每當我試著對拉希爾城施展遠視,那城就彷彿被一張黑暗之網裹住,我怎麼也看不穿。

column1: That is something of which I have little knowledge.
column2: 那件事,我所知甚少。

column1: If you please, I would rather talk of something else.
column2: 若你不介意,我寧可聊些別的。

column1: There's no need for an apology.
column2: 不必道歉。

column1: You are most brash, but I will assume that you mean only to flatter me.
column2: 你還真是莽撞,不過我就當你只是想恭維我罷了。

column1: You already are.
column2: 你已經是了。

column1: Yes, come sit at my table.
column2: 好,過來,坐到我的桌旁。

column1: You challenge Aziza to a friendly magical duel.
column2: 你向阿姿莎提出一場友好的魔法切磋。

column1: Both the Baron and his son paid a high price for being so foolish as to provoke a powerful spellcaster. It is good that you were there to bring their suffering to an end.
column2: 男爵與他兒子愚蠢到去招惹一位強大的施法者,為此付出了慘重的代價。幸虧有你在場,終結了他們的苦難。

column1: It is unfortunate that you could not do more to make the ogress leave the valley and lift her curse.
column2: 可惜你未能再多做些什麼,逼那女食人魔離開山谷、解除她的詛咒。

column1: The Baron is very fortunate that you visited Spielburg, for without your valiant actions, he and his children would still be under that ogress's curse.
column2: 男爵真是萬幸,你當時造訪了史畢柏格;若沒有你那番英勇之舉,他和他的孩子們至今仍困在那女食人魔的詛咒之下。

column1: You tell about WIT and how you decided not to take up a full-time study as wizard after all.
column2: 你說起 W.I.T.(巫師技術學院)的事,以及自己最終為何決定不去當個全職進修的巫師。

column1: Congratulations on passing the tests, wizard. The council was very impressed with you. Given the circumstances, it is good you chose not to stay there. Perhaps you'll be able to pick up studying again if you ever tire of adventuring.
column2: 恭喜你通過了試煉,巫師。議會對你印象極為深刻。就當前情勢而言,你選擇不留下是明智之舉。倘若哪天你厭倦了冒險,或許還能重拾學業。

column1: Hmmm. If you only had some chips...
column2: 嗯……你要是手上有些籌碼就好了……

column1: The creature lost its destructive power when it was imprisoned, but it may still be of use to you somehow.
column2: 那生物被囚禁時便失去了破壞之力,但牠對你或許仍有某種用處。

column1: You may be the city's last hope. If you wish to defeat the creature, you would do well to keep my advice in mind.
column2: 你或許是這座城最後的希望。你若想擊敗那生物,最好把我的忠告記在心上。

column1: You handled the creature well. It won't be much of a threat anymore inside those bellows.
column2: 那生物你處理得很好。被關進那風箱裡後,牠再也構不成什麼威脅了。

column1: I am not very familiar with the element of air. I can tell you what I know, but it may not be enough.
column2: 我對風元素並不太熟悉。我能把我知道的告訴你,但這些恐怕還不夠。

column1: You tell about the destruction of the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你講起自己摧毀土元素精靈的經過。

column1: Only a hero such as yourself could have defeated the Earth Elemental.
column2: 也唯有你這樣的英雄,才能擊敗土元素精靈。

column1: Keep the magical earth you collected with you, for it will probably come in handy later.
column2: 把你採集到的那把魔法之土帶在身上,日後多半派得上用場。

column1: The creature continuously draws strength from the ground upon which it stands. You will have to hunt it down until it has been weakened enough to be captured.
column2: 那生物會不斷從牠腳下的土地汲取力量。你得一路追擊牠,直到牠被削弱到足以收服為止。

column1: The magical essence of the Water Elemental is extremely potent. Keep it safe, for it may be of great value to you later on.
column2: 水元素精靈的魔法精華極為強大。把它好好收著,往後它對你或許大有價值。

column1: This city will only last a few days without relying on the fountain. If you wish to defeat it, heed my words well, because the Water Elemental is the most dangerous Elemental by far.
column2: 這座城一旦失去那座泉的依靠,只撐得了幾天。你若想擊敗它,務必把我的話聽進去,因為水元素精靈是迄今最危險的一隻元素精靈。

column1: Rakeesh is very wise and experienced. He is still a great hero, despite his age. There is much an adventurer such as yourself can learn from him.
column2: 拉基什極有智慧,經驗老到。儘管上了年紀,他依舊是位偉大的英雄。像你這樣的冒險者,能從他身上學到許多。

column1: You tell Aziza you wish to discuss the enchanted Emir with her.
column2: 你告訴阿姿莎,你想跟她談談那位中了法術的埃米爾。

column1: The enchantment he is under is extremely potent, and I feel it is somehow linked with the magic user who placed it upon him. Defeating the one who transformed him should make the spell easy to break.
column2: 他所中的法術極為強大,而我感覺它與那位對他施法的法師之間有某種牽連。只要擊敗那個把他變了形的人,這法術應該就不難破解。

column1: You tell Aziza about Omar's performances at the inn.
column2: 你向阿姿莎說起奧瑪爾在客棧裡的表演。

column1: I agree he is a most talented poet. He even performs at the Palace sometimes.
column2: 我同意,他是位極有才華的詩人。他有時甚至會在王宮裡演出。

column1: You tell Aziza about how you met Shameen and Shema in Spielburg and how they convinced you to accompany them to Shapeir after the reward ceremony at Spielburg castle.
column2: 你向阿姿莎說起,你如何在史畢柏格結識了沙明與舍瑪,又是如何在史畢柏格城堡的授獎典禮後,被他們說動隨他們同往夏皮爾。

column1: It is admirable how far your friends have traveled to help their people.
column2: 你那些朋友為了幫助自己的同胞,不辭千里遠行,實在令人敬佩。

column1: You tell about how you restored Julanar's spirit.
column2: 你講起自己如何讓茱拉娜爾的靈魂重獲安寧。

column1: You did very well. There is nothing more you can do for her, but I foresee that at some point in her future her soulmate will come and break the spell completely.
column2: 你做得很好。你已無法再為她做什麼,但我預見,在她未來的某個時刻,她命中註定的伴侶終將前來,徹底破除那道法術。

column1: That sounds like an enjoyable means of traveling, although I prefer the use of teleport spells myself.
column2: 那聽來是種愉快的旅行方式,不過我自己更偏好用傳送術。

column1: You tell about your battle with Sir James.
column2: 你講起你與詹姆斯爵士那場交手。

column1: I know he was an adventurer who came here seeking glory several years ago. I have never met him though.
column2: 我知道他是個冒險者,幾年前為追尋榮耀來到此地。不過,我從沒見過他本人。

column1: I prefer less rigorous forms of entertainment myself.
column2: 我自己更偏好不那麼費勁的消遣。

column1: It is wise of you to have told me that. The more information I have, the better I can judge things.
column2: 你把那件事告訴我,是明智之舉。我握有的消息越多,就越能把事情看得準。

column1: Come in, sit down, and we shall speak.
column2: 進來吧,坐下,我們好好談談。

column1: I am disturbed that you are not yet a wizard. You very much need to pass the tests at the Wizard's Institute of Technocery if you wish to be more than just a magic user.
column2: 你至今還不是巫師,這讓我有些不安。你若想超越區區法師的身分,就非得通過巫師技術學院的試煉不可。

column1: Come in, sit down, and be welcome.
column2: 進來吧,坐下,歡迎你。

column1: I wish to speak with you tomorrow about Raseir. I intend to work some magic tonight to gather information about the things you will face. I will let you know at our meeting what I find out.
column2: 我想明天跟你談談拉希爾的事。今晚我打算施些法術,蒐集你即將面對的種種訊息。屆時相見,我會把探得的消息告訴你。

column1: But forgive me, I have forsaken my manners. Come in, sit down, and we shall share tea and talk, Hero of Shapeir.
column2: 不過,請原諒我失了禮數。進來吧,坐下,我們一同品茶敘話,夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: You have saved the city through your resourcefulness and bravery.
column2: 你憑著機智與勇氣,拯救了這座城。

column1: You are very much a hero. Welcome to my home, %s. May your deeds be remembered forever.
column2: 你確實是位英雄。歡迎來到我家,%s。願你的事蹟永垂不朽。

column1: I worked some magic last night to uncover information about your trip to Raseir. I need to discuss this with you.
column2: 昨夜我施了些法術,探出了關於你拉希爾此行的消息。我得跟你談談這件事。

column1: Despite your previous behavior in my presence, you have proven yourself a hero. I wish to discuss some information I have learned about Raseir.
column2: 儘管你先前在我面前的舉止有些不堪,你終究證明了自己是位英雄。我想跟你談談我打探到的、關於拉希爾的一些消息。

column1: Welcome, stranger. I shall be honored to have you for a guest.
column2: 歡迎你,陌生人。能請你作客,是我的榮幸。

column1: (Perhaps he has a Saurus throatus?)
column2: (說不定他害了索魯斯喉炎?)

column1: Come in. We shall all sit and see what we shall see.
column2: 進來吧。我們都坐下,看看會看見些什麼。

column1: You must take the caravan to Raseir. I will keep the Emir with me for safekeeping while you find the means to restore him to his rightful shape.
column2: 你必須搭商隊前往拉希爾。在你尋得讓埃米爾恢復本來面目的法子之前,我會把他留在身邊,妥善看顧。

column1: We all are hoping for your success, Hero, and eagerly await the pleasure of your return. Farewell, %s.
column2: 我們都盼著你能成功,英雄,並殷切期待著你歸來的喜悅。再會了,%s。

column1: It was most pleasant talking to you, %s, but I'm afraid it is getting dark. Perhaps we can continue our conversation some other time.
column2: 和你交談十分愉快,%s,只是天色恐怕要暗了。或許我們改天再續這番話吧。

column1: You bid Aziza farewell and take your leave.
column2: 你向阿姿莎道別,起身告辭。

column1: It is very dishonorable of you to try to cast a spell in my home. It would be wise for you to leave at this time.
column2: 你竟想在我家裡施法,實在毫無榮譽可言。此刻你還是離開為妙。

column1: I will not tolerate that sort of language in my house. Please take that behavior where it belongs.
column2: 我不容許這種言語出現在我屋裡。請把那種舉止帶到它該去的地方。

column1: How dare you!
column2: 你好大的膽子!

column1: I do not wish to be confronted with that sort of behavior in my own house. Please take it where it belongs.
column2: 我不願在自己家裡見到那種舉止。請把它帶到它該去的地方。

column1: The tea is cool and refreshing. You find yourself relaxing as you drink and listen to the sound of flowing water.
column2: 這茶清涼爽口。你一邊啜飲,一邊聽著潺潺水聲,不知不覺便鬆弛了下來。

column1: Now that we have shared tea, what is it for which you have come?
column2: 茶也喝過了,那麼,你此行所為何來?

column1: I will not tolerate such rudeness!
column2: 我絕不容許這般無禮!

column1: The table feels cool to the touch. Standing in its vicinity provides a refreshing relief from the harsh climate outside.
column2: 這桌子摸上去沁涼。站在它附近,能讓人從外頭那嚴酷的氣候中得到一絲清爽的舒緩。

column1: Shall we share tea?
column2: 要不要一同喝杯茶?

column1: You should take care not to disrupt anything, lest you upset Aziza.
column2: 你最好留神,別碰亂了什麼,免得惹阿姿莎不快。

column1: It is not polite of you to go where you are not invited. If you wish to speak with me, then come sit down. Otherwise, you are free to leave.
column2: 未受邀就擅闖,可不太禮貌。你若想跟我說話,就過來坐下;不然的話,你大可離去。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the front door.
column2: 別費勁去跟大門講話了。

column1: Your cape-wearing days are over.
column2: 你披風加身的日子,到此結束了。

column1: Talking to tapestries trivializes their truly triumphant tales.
column2: 跟壁毯攀談,可是糟蹋了它們那真真切切、輝煌動人的故事。

column1: It has to be the biggest bunch of carp you've ever seen.
column2: 這準是你這輩子見過最大的一群鯉魚了。

column1: Aziza had many more fish tanks during the Age of Aquariums.
column2: 在那「水族紀元」裡,阿姿莎養的魚缸可比這多得多了。

column1: PLEASE DON'T TAP ON THE GLASS!
column2: 請別敲打玻璃!

column1: Shouting out would be considered rude.
column2: 大聲嚷嚷可會被當成失禮之舉。

column1: While splashing the cool water on your face would be refreshing, you're not sure that it would be the polite thing to do.
column2: 往臉上潑些清涼的水固然爽快,但你不太確定這麼做合不合禮數。

column1: Stick to your drinking from your waterskins.
column2: 還是乖乖喝你水袋裡的水吧。

column1: Don't throw things into the pond. Aziza would not appreciate it.
column2: 別往池子裡丟東西。阿姿莎可不會領情。

column1: There's no door to open.
column2: 這兒沒有門可開。

column1: It's a most polite tea set.
column2: 這是套極有禮數的茶具。

column1: You see some moisturizing shampoo.
column2: 你看到一些保濕洗髮精。

column1: You see a purposeless porpoise.
column2: 你看到一隻無所事事的海豚。

column1: You see a gratuitous goblet.
column2: 你看到一只多此一舉的高腳杯。

column1: You see a Deluxe Donut Dunker.
column2: 你看到一台豪華甜甜圈沾醬器。

column1: You see a squirt bottle.
column2: 你看到一個擠壓噴瓶。

column1: You see a note in a bottle. Fan mail from some flounder?
column2: 你看到一個瓶中字條。是哪條比目魚寄來的粉絲信嗎?

column1: You see a bottle of butter-flavored syrup.
column2: 你看到一瓶奶油風味糖漿。

column1: You see a 10-speed Oysterizer.
column2: 你看到一台十段變速的「牡蠣果汁機」。

column1: You see a bug in a bottle.
column2: 你看到一隻瓶中的蟲子。

column1: You see a Klein bottle.
column2: 你看到一個克萊因瓶。

column1: Amazing! Somebody actually built a computer in a bottle. How do they do that?
column2: 太神奇了!竟然真有人在瓶子裡造了一台電腦。這是怎麼辦到的?

column1: You see a jar of freeze-dried water.
column2: 你看到一罐冷凍乾燥水。

column1: You see a vial of saline solution.
column2: 你看到一小瓶生理食鹽水。

column1: You see a flask of rehydration solution.
column2: 你看到一瓶補水溶液。

column1: You see a can of wetting solution.
column2: 你看到一罐潤濕液。

column1: You see a bottle of Eau de Anchovy.
column2: 你看到一瓶「鯷魚香水」。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns Volume 1 of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series: "20,000 Leaks Under the Sink."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《潮間自助海帶叢書》第一冊:《水槽下的兩萬處漏水》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns Volume 2 of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series: "Mussel-Building with Barnacle Jones."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《潮間自助海帶叢書》第二冊:《跟藤壺瓊斯一起練蚌肌》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns Volume 3 of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series: "Learn to Program in Sea++."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《潮間自助海帶叢書》第三冊:《學會用 Sea++ 寫程式》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns Volume 4 of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series: "Learn to Play 'Go Fish' like a Card Shark."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《潮間自助海帶叢書》第四冊:《像鯊魚老千一樣玩「釣魚」紙牌》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns Volume 5 of the Tide-Life Self-Kelp Series: "How to Teach Generosity to Shellfish People."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《潮間自助海帶叢書》第五冊:《如何教自私的貝殼人慷慨待人》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "New Clear Fishin'."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《清澈新釣法》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "Fear of Swimming."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《游泳恐懼症》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "Lure of the Flies."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《蒼蠅毛鉤的誘惑》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "The Tides of Marsh."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《沼澤潮汐》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "The Poseidon Adventure."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《海神號歷險記》。

column1: Through the mildew, you are able to recognize some of the book titles. For instance, Aziza owns "Clam of the Baskervilles."
column2: 透過霉斑,你還勉強認得出幾本書名。比方說,阿姿莎收藏了《巴斯克維爾的巨蚌》。

column1: You don't have time for reading books.
column2: 你沒空看書。

column1: Don't bother talking to the books.
column2: 別跟書講話了。

column1: You can't take any items from the shelves. They belong to Aziza.
column2: 架上的東西你不能拿,那都是阿姿莎的。

column1: Better shelve that idea for now.
column2: 這個念頭還是先擱著吧。

column1: You can't get the lily pads.
column2: 你拿不到那些睡蓮葉。

column1: That's not the tastiest way of getting more greens in your diet.
column2: 靠這個來多吃點蔬菜,可不怎麼美味。

column1: The tea is cool and refreshing.
column2: 這茶清涼又提神。

column1: It is actually quite a drop. You would do better to use the steps.
column2: 這落差其實還挺大的,你最好還是走階梯。

column1: The Siddhis are the tantric powers of mastery over the elements of air, earth, fire, and water.
column2: 悉地(Siddhi)是駕馭風、土、火、水四大元素的密法之力。

column1: My version of the Wizard's Whirl, which I call "Sim-Siddhi," assists those of wizard-kind in developing these powers.
column2: 我自創的「巫師渦旋」改良版,我把它叫做「模擬悉地」,能幫助巫師一族培養這些力量。

column1: As a token of my respect for beating me, please accept this humble gift.
column2: 你贏了我,為表敬意,請收下這份小小的禮物。

column1: You feel your understanding of the Flame Dart spell grow.
column2: 你感覺自己對火鏢咒語的領悟更深了。

column1: Next time you fight, try adding additional heat to your spell by holding down the spell's hotkey.
column2: 下次戰鬥時,試著按住咒語的熱鍵,替你的法術再加把火。

column1: As a token of my respect for beating me, please let me restore your magical powers.
column2: 你贏了我,為表敬意,讓我替你恢復法力吧。

column1: You are obviously enchanted, so we need now to see what is your true form.
column2: 你顯然中了魔法,所以我們得看看你的真面目究竟是什麼。

column1: Water of wisdom, liquid of life,[I call upon thee three times three,[Mirror without what lies within,[So we shall have the true to see.
column2: 智慧之水,生命之液,[我以三乘三之數召喚你,[映照外相所藏之內裡,[讓我們得見真實之形。

column1: Look into the water, and we shall see your true form.
column2: 凝視這水面,我們便能看見你的真形。

column1: Ah, the Emir Arus al-Din. There was good reason why my spell could not locate you in Raseir.
column2: 啊,原來是埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁。難怪我的咒語在拉希爾找不到你,原來是有原因的。

column1: You are under a most powerful enchantment. The caster must be a very skilled wizard to craft such a spell.
column2: 你身上罩著一道極強的魔咒。能編織出這種法術的施術者,必定是位身手高明的巫師。

column1: Water of wisdom, liquid of life,[Knowledge now is what I seek,[Reveal the caster of the spell,[upon the one which I bespeak.
column2: 智慧之水,生命之液,[我此刻所求乃是知識,[揭示這道咒語的施術者,[在我所指之人身上現形。

column1: I will tell you of a time long ago when women wore veils and were but wives and chattel to men.
column2: 我要跟你說一段很久以前的往事,那時候女人都蒙著面紗,不過是男人的妻子與財物罷了。

column1: There was a woman -- a beautiful woman, a loving woman -- who wished with her very soul to become a healer, and thus aid others.
column2: 有一個女子——美麗的女子,溫柔的女子——她打從靈魂深處渴望成為一名醫者,好去幫助別人。

column1: Just the facts, ma'am.
column2: 夫人,只說重點就好。

column1: Her father tried to dissuade her, saying that this was no task for a woman of her gentleness and sensitivity. She would go about her duties quietly, but each evening she would tell him again that she wished to become a healer.
column2: 她父親想勸退她,說以她這般溫柔細膩的女子,不適合做這種事。她白天默默操持家務,可每到傍晚,她又會再次向父親訴說,她多麼想成為一名醫者。

column1: At last her father gave in, and she was trained in the art of healing, despite the jeers and condescension of the male students and teachers. She became a healer and removed her veil forever.
column2: 父親終於讓步,她開始學習醫術,儘管男學生與男老師對她嘲笑輕視。她成了一名醫者,並從此摘下了面紗。

column1: She soon discovered that life was not easy for a woman healer. Many men distrusted her skills and refused to call upon her. Some men called upon her only to try to take advantage of her beauty.
column2: 她很快就發現,當一個女醫者的日子並不好過。許多男人不信任她的醫術,不肯找她看診;有些男人找上她,卻只是想佔她美貌的便宜。

column1: One day, a man came to her and told the tale of his brother dying in the desert. She did not quite trust his manner of looking at her, but she could not refuse aid to one who might need it.
column2: 有一天,一個男人來找她,說他兄弟正命喪沙漠之中。她並不太信任他看她的那種眼神,可面對一個或許真有難處的人,她又無法拒絕伸出援手。

column1: She followed him out of the city and into the desert. She was led to an encampment of ragged tents. She soon learned to her sorrow that the tale of the brother was but a lie. These were Brigands, and what they did with her, I shall not say.
column2: 她跟著他出了城,進了沙漠。他把她帶到一處破帳篷搭成的營地。她很快就痛苦地明白,那段兄弟的故事不過是個謊言。那些人是強盜,至於他們對她做了什麼,我就不說了。

column1: She tried to escape them the next day, and ran to the mountains to hide. They were swift to follow. They had nearly recaptured her when she called aloud for help. A Djinni heard her cry and transformed her into a tree.
column2: 第二天她試圖逃走,跑進山裡躲藏。那些人緊追不捨,眼看就要再次抓住她,她放聲呼救。一位神靈聽見了她的哭喊,把她化成了一棵樹。

column1: You could be the one to give her back her soul and free her heart from wood. You will try this, Hero, will you not?
column2: 你或許就是那個能還她靈魂、把她的心從樹木中解放出來的人。你會去試試的,對吧,英雄?

column1: There are three things you must give her to save her soul: a gift of kindness, a gift of magic, and a gift of love.
column2: 要救她的靈魂,你必須給她三樣東西:一份善意的禮物、一份魔法的禮物,還有一份愛的禮物。

column1: The gift of kindness is simple. It is only what you would give to someone who has been in the desert far too long.
column2: 善意的禮物很簡單,就是你會給一個在沙漠裡待得太久的人的東西。

column1: You must then tell her about yourself, remind her what it is to be human. Your words are as important as the gift, for they give meaning to the act.
column2: 接著你必須向她講述你自己,提醒她身為一個人是什麼滋味。你的話語和禮物同等重要,因為是它們賦予了這個舉動意義。

column1: The gift of magic is the gift of sacrifice. The gift must be something you gained through hardship and from aiding others. It must also be something from which a plant can gain strength.
column2: 魔法的禮物是犧牲的禮物。這份禮物必須是你歷盡艱辛、又透過幫助他人才換來的東西,而且還得是能讓一株植物從中汲取力量的東西。

column1: Tell her then how you got the gift and why. You will be reminding her of why she became a healer.
column2: 然後告訴她你是怎麼得到這份禮物的、又是為了什麼。你會藉此提醒她,當初她為何要成為一名醫者。

column1: The last gift again is simple, a mere gesture of affection. Remind her that there are still people in the world who care about others.
column2: 最後一份禮物同樣簡單,不過是一個表達情意的舉動。提醒她,這世上仍有人懂得關心別人。

column1: There is only one thing you need to say to her now, to remind her who she is. Her name is Julanar, "Flower of the Pomegranate."
column2: 現在你只需要對她說一句話,提醒她自己是誰。她的名字是茱拉娜爾,意思是「石榴之花」。

column1: Finally, there is something you need to say to her, to remind her who she is. Her name is Julanar, "Flower of the Pomegranate."
column2: 最後,你還有一句話必須對她說,提醒她自己是誰。她的名字是茱拉娜爾,意思是「石榴之花」。

column1: When you have done this, your part in her tale will be complete. Julanar will not be able to walk this land as a human woman until another frees her with the power of true love, but you can at least free her spirit by giving her these gifts.
column2: 做完這些,你在她故事裡的角色就算圓滿了。茱拉娜爾要重新以人類女子的身分行走這片土地,還得等另一個人用真愛之力解開她的束縛;不過,只要把這些禮物送給她,你至少能先解放她的靈魂。

column1: What's a pomegranate?
column2: 石榴是什麼?

column1: You will find her within a day's ride to the east, at the base of the mountains. May you heal the healer, %s.
column2: 往東騎一天的路,在群山腳下你就能找到她。願你治癒那位醫者,%s。

column1: I know you can achieve the gift of kindness, but you do not yet have a suitable gift of sacrifice. Perhaps you will be able to do this another time.
column2: 我知道善意的禮物你做得到,可是你還沒有一份合適的犧牲禮物。或許改天你才辦得成這件事。

column1: There are four basic elements which make up the universe: water, earth, air, and fire.
column2: 構成這個宇宙的有四種基本元素:水、土、風、火。

column1: An Elemental is formed when magic shapes an element into a semblance of a living creature.
column2: 當魔法把一種元素塑造成近似活物的模樣,元素生物便就此誕生。

column1: Elementals are very powerful and destructive. They can be weakened by the contrary element, but they cannot be destroyed.
column2: 元素生物威力強大,極具破壞性。你能用相剋的元素削弱它,卻沒辦法消滅它。

column1: Once weakened, the Elemental can be captured. Once captured, the magical nature of the Elemental can be used for helpful rather than harmful purposes.
column2: 一旦被削弱,元素生物就能被捕獲。一旦被捕獲,它那股魔法本質便能化害為利,為人所用。

column1: The essence of fire is hunger.
column2: 火的本質是飢餓。

column1: As long as there is something nearby to burn, it will be able to move freely. It can consume the very bricks which make up the floor of the plazas.
column2: 只要附近有東西可燒,它就能自由移動。連鋪在廣場地上的磚塊,它都能一口吞噬。

column1: Until it is contained, it cannot be controlled.
column2: 在它被封住之前,沒人能控制它。

column1: The weakness of fire is water, but the Elemental of Fire will avoid water by moving away from its source.
column2: 火的弱點是水,但火元素會主動遠離水源,藉此避開水。

column1: Unless you find some way to drive or lure the Elemental away from the open plazas, where there is much it can burn, to a more confined area, you will not be able to use water against it to capture it.
column2: 開闊的廣場上有太多東西可供它燃燒。除非你想辦法把元素生物趕出去、或誘到一處更封閉的地方,否則你就沒辦法用水來對付並捕獲它。

column1: Once it is captured and contained, it behaves as a magical flame.
column2: 一旦被捕獲封住,它就會變成一團魔法之火,乖乖聽話。

column1: The essence of air is freedom of movement.
column2: 風的本質是行動的自由。

column1: As long as there is a place for air to go, it will continue to grow in destructive power.
column2: 只要還有地方讓風流動,它的破壞力就會不斷壯大。

column1: Only when it's bound to earth can it be contained.
column2: 唯有把它束縛於土,才能將它封住。

column1: You should ask someone better versed than I in the nature of air how to go about this.
column2: 至於該怎麼做,你該去請教一位比我更懂風之本質的人。

column1: The essence of earth is strength.
column2: 土的本質是力量。

column1: It must be worn away before it can be captured, and it is only vulnerable to flame.
column2: 它必須先被磨耗削弱才能捕獲,而它唯一的弱點就是火焰。

column1: It might be best contained in the type of cloth sack used to haul sand in this city.
column2: 要封住它,最好是用這城裡那種搬運沙子的布袋。

column1: The essence of water is attraction.
column2: 水的本質是吸引。

column1: The Elemental will draw all water into itself. It can draw water from the very body of a human that approaches it.
column2: 這元素生物會把所有的水都吸進自己體內。連走近它的人,身上的水分都會被它抽乾。

column1: In the fountain, the Water Elemental will draw upon the boundless magical stream which is the lifeblood of the city. People will be dying of thirst as it grows in power enough to engulf the entire city in a flood.
column2: 待在噴泉裡,水元素就能汲取那條無窮無盡的魔法水脈——那可是整座城市的命脈。等它壯大到足以用洪水淹沒全城,人們就要渴死了。

column1: As long as the Water Elemental is in the fountain, the Elemental cannot be harmed or contained.
column2: 只要水元素還在噴泉裡,你就傷不了它,也封不住它。

column1: It is only vulnerable to air. Once away from the fountain, it will be drawn to the nearest source of water.
column2: 它唯一的弱點是風。一旦離開噴泉,它就會被最近的水源吸引過去。

column1: You might then contain it.
column2: 到那時,你或許就能把它封住了。

column1: Access to second tier granted.
column2: 已開放進入第二層。

column1: Access to third tier granted.
column2: 已開放進入第三層。

column1: Access to fourth tier granted.
column2: 已開放進入第四層。

column1: Access to goal granted.
column2: 已開放進入終點。

column1: Select spell to cast.
column2: 選擇要施放的咒語。

column1: You're a promising player. Congratulations on your victory.
column2: 你是個很有前途的棋手。恭喜你獲勝。

column1: It seems victory is mine. I hope you enjoyed the game nevertheless.
column2: 看來這局是我贏了。不過還是希望你玩得盡興。

column1: Select obstacle to move.
column2: 選擇要移動的障礙。

column1: You don't have enough mana to cast that spell.
column2: 你的法力不足,施不了那道咒語。

column1: Each player controls a small, Elemental-like creature as it tries to reach the center of the game tapestry.
column2: 每位玩家各操控一隻形似元素生物的小傢伙,設法讓牠抵達棋盤織錦的正中央。

column1: The tapestry itself contains areas representing the primal elements, and you must help your creature traverse these areas. You play the game by casting spells.
column2: 織錦本身分成幾塊區域,各自代表一種原始元素,你得幫你的小傢伙穿越這些區域。這遊戲靠施放咒語來進行。

column1: Each turn, the player is required to either cast one spell on the board or move his creature to another tier of the board, if possible. Afterward, the creature will attempt to travel around the board.
column2: 每一回合,玩家必須選擇在棋盤上施放一道咒語,或是(在可行的情況下)把自己的小傢伙移到棋盤的另一層。之後,小傢伙就會試著繞著棋盤移動。

column1: Casting Trigger will change the form of your creature according to the elemental cycle -- Air to Earth, Earth to Fire, Fire to Water, or Water to Air.
column2: 施放「觸發」會讓你的小傢伙依循元素循環變換形態——風變土、土變火、火變水,或水變風。

column1: Casting Flame Dart will change an earth zone on the tapestry to fire. Casting Calm will turn a fire zone to air. Neither spell can be cast directly on a zone occupied by one or more players.
column2: 施放「火鏢」會把織錦上的一塊土區變成火區;施放「平靜」則會把一塊火區變成風區。這兩道咒語都不能直接施放在已有一名以上玩家佔據的區域上。

column1: Casting Open will remove one of the obstacles on the tapestry. This will allow your creature to pass through, and it may cause one section of the tapestry to affect another. Moving an obstacle will always result in it appearing somewhere else on the same tier.
column2: 施放「開啟」會移除織錦上的一個障礙,讓你的小傢伙得以通過,還可能使織錦的一處區域影響到另一處。每次移動障礙,它總會出現在同一層的別處。

column1: Of course, the tapestry has a life cycle of its own. Zones of the tapestry will be affected by their neighbors and by the Elementals occupying that zone at that moment.
column2: 當然,這片織錦自有它的生命循環。織錦上的各區域會受相鄰區域影響,也會受當下佔據該區的元素生物影響。

column1: An earth area that is exposed to more fire than water will ignite, becoming a fire zone. An air area that is exposed to more earth than fire will solidify to become an earth zone.
column2: 一塊土區若接觸的火多於水,就會被點燃,變成火區;一塊風區若接觸的土多於火,就會凝固,變成土區。

column1: A fire area that is exposed to more water than fire will be doused to become an air zone.
column2: 一塊火區若接觸的水多於火,就會被澆熄,變成風區。

column1: Your creature will generally try to walk around the board in a clockwise fashion, but it is restricted by its own nature and its surroundings.
column2: 你的小傢伙一般會試著順時針繞著棋盤走,但牠會受到自身本性與周遭環境的限制。

column1: A Fire Elemental moves quickly and will burn earth, but cannot pass through water or air.[An Earth Elemental moves slowly, but is stopped only by fire. Earth will take over an air zone if possible.
column2: 火元素移動迅速,會焚燒土地,卻無法穿越水與風。[土元素移動緩慢,但只有火才攔得住牠。一有機會,土就會接管一塊風區。

column1: The Water Elemental moves quickly, and will change fire into air. It cannot pass through earth and is slowed down by air.[The Air Elemental is very quick, but is stopped by earth and fire. It is not in air's nature for it to change even its nemesis, water.
column2: 水元素移動迅速,會把火變成風。牠無法穿越土,且會被風拖慢速度。[風元素極為敏捷,卻會被土與火攔住。即便面對牠的剋星水,風的本性也不會去改變它。

column1: If a creature is occupying a zone whose nature changes to an element incompatible with the creature's nature, the creature will be taken back to its starting position.
column2: 若某隻小傢伙所在的區域,本質變成了與牠本性不相容的元素,牠就會被送回起點。

column1: Only after traversing each of the four zones on a tier will a creature be allowed to travel down to the next tier. The goal of the game is to reach the center of the game tapestry before your opponent does.
column2: 只有走遍某一層上的四塊區域之後,小傢伙才能往下走到下一層。這遊戲的目標,就是搶在對手之前抵達棋盤織錦的正中央。

column1: Select zone to extinguish.
column2: 選擇要熄滅的區域。

column1: Select zone to burn.
column2: 選擇要點燃的區域。

column1: You don't have any mana pills.
column2: 你身上沒有法力丸。

column1: You can't use that spell there.
column2: 那道咒語不能用在那裡。

column1: If you are the right sort of person for the right sort of work, then when the time comes, arrangements might be made.
column2: 如果你是適合做這種工作的那種人,那麼時機一到,事情或許就能安排妥當。

column1: There is a Weapon Shop in this city which has a trapdoor beneath its anvil. If you can but move the anvil and open the trapdoor, you shall be richly rewarded. I shall also be rewarded, knowing that I have contributed to the fall of the Weaponer's house.
column2: 這城裡有家武器鋪,鐵砧底下藏著一道暗門。只要你能搬開鐵砧、打開暗門,就會獲得豐厚的報酬。而我也算有所回報——畢竟我出了一份力,促成那武器匠一家的垮台。

column1: You and I could make a very fine couple.
column2: 你跟我,可以湊成很登對的一對呢。

column1: I am always pleased to exchange money for strangers.
column2: 我向來樂意替外鄉人兌換錢幣。

column1: There are many merchants around here.
column2: 這一帶商人多得很。

column1: My name is Dinarzad, which means 'Child of Gold Coins.'
column2: 我叫迪娜札德,意思是「金幣之女」。

column1: Oil is to loosen locks, relieve squeaky hinges, and make other things slide smoothly.
column2: 油是用來鬆動鎖頭、潤滑吱嘎作響的鉸鏈,還有讓其他東西滑順起來的。

column1: Fool! You have only to bring me what you find from where I send you, and I shall pay you what those things are worth.
column2: 蠢蛋!你只管把我派你去的地方找到的東西帶回來給我,我自會按它們的價值付你錢。

column1: I love a good thieves' tool!
column2: 我最愛一套好用的賊匠工具了!

column1: There is a rumor to the effect that I am very fond of strangers.
column2: 有個傳聞是這麼說的:我這人很喜歡外鄉客。

column1: The Fountain Plaza is to the east of here. Follow the street eastward whenever you can.
column2: 噴泉廣場就在這裡的東邊。能往東走的時候就盡量沿街往東走。

column1: The only weather I care about is whether you and I can exchange something besides glances.
column2: 我唯一在意的「天氣」,就是你我之間除了眉來眼去,還能不能交換點別的。

column1: The inn is to the southeast of here, but you will have to take the roundabout route to get there.
column2: 旅店在這裡的東南邊,不過你得繞一段路才到得了。

column1: The Guild Hall is to the west of here. You have only to follow the alleyway until it again turns west, and then follow it to the Plaza of the Fighters.
column2: 公會大廳在這裡的西邊。你只要順著巷子走,直到它再次往西轉,然後一路走到戰士廣場就是了。

column1: There is no thieves' guild around here. Certainly I can satisfy your needs better than any mere guild. Only in Raseir do you need such union scum.
column2: 這一帶可沒有什麼盜賊公會。我當然比那種公會更能滿足你的需求啦。只有在拉希爾,你才會需要那種組工會的人渣。

column1: I deal in money. I will be only too happy to exchange your old, worn-out foreign money for money which is a bit more practical around here.
column2: 我做的是錢的生意。我非常樂意把你那些又舊又破的外幣,換成在這一帶比較好用的錢。

column1: There is a caravan traveling to Raseir nearly every month, I believe.
column2: 我記得幾乎每個月都有一支商隊前往拉希爾。

column1: The Palace is a place I would very much like to visit someday.
column2: 王宮是個我很想哪天去逛逛的地方。

column1: The old Emir of Raseir was a fool, and fools are easy to deal with. But from all I hear, whoever controls Raseir now is far from a fool.
column2: 拉希爾的老埃米爾是個蠢蛋,而蠢蛋好對付得很。可就我聽到的種種消息來看,如今掌控拉希爾的那個人,可一點都不蠢。

column1: The Sultan (May He Live Forever!) is very wise and watches over everything. It is best not to try to break the laws around here unless one is very, very cunning.
column2: 蘇丹(願他萬壽無疆!)極為睿智,凡事都看在眼裡。在這一帶,除非你非常、非常狡猾,否則最好別想著去犯法。

column1: I am far better than any old Djinni in a lamp, provided you rub me the right way.
column2: 只要你會「摸」得對,我可比那種困在燈裡的老神靈強多了。

column1: Raseir was once a place where anyone with some brains and skills could make some quick cash. Now that things have changed under the new Emir, only those who toady to the authorities can get ahead.
column2: 拉希爾從前是個只要有點頭腦和本事的人,就能快快撈一筆的地方。如今新埃米爾上任、世道一變,只剩下那些對當局阿諛奉承的人才出得了頭。

column1: Ferrari is an excellent example of that type of person -- only too willing to kiss and tell.
column2: 費拉里就是那種人的絕佳範例——巴不得拍完馬屁再去打小報告。

column1: Shapeir is a very safe and orderly place under the watch of our Sultan. It can be very dangerous for those who break the law -- and get caught.
column2: 在我們蘇丹的看顧下,夏皮爾是個十分安全又井然有序的地方。對那些犯了法——又被逮到的人來說,這裡可凶險得很。

column1: The Guard, Franc, is my own and does not speak.
column2: 那個守衛法蘭克是我的人,他不會說話。

column1: If you have to ask about it, then you certainly don't deserve it.
column2: 這種事你要是還得開口問,那你肯定就不配擁有它。

column1: You seem to be a very dangerous man. A girl cannot be too careful. Perhaps when we know each other better, though...
column2: 你看起來是個很危險的男人。女孩子家可不能不小心。不過嘛,等我們彼此更熟一點,或許……

column1: Do not trust him in Raseir unless you are a thief. Even then, watch your backside.
column2: 在拉希爾,除非你自己也是個賊,否則別信他。就算你是賊,也得當心點,別讓人從背後捅你一刀。

column1: The anvil is made of iron and is very heavy. You will need either a great deal of strength or proper preparation to move it.
column2: 那鐵砧是鐵打的,非常沉重。你得要嘛有一身蠻力,要嘛事先做好妥當的準備,才搬得動它。

column1: I prefer jackals. They're so much better for preying around.
column2: 我比較中意豺狼。牠們可會「祈」食得多了。

column1: Don't be so sheepish. I love to kid around.
column2: 別這麼畏畏縮縮像隻綿羊嘛。我最愛跟人「逗」著玩了。

column1: You need me, and perhaps some day I'll knead you.
column2: 你需要我,而說不定哪天,我也會好好「揉捏」你一番。

column1: I know of a place where you can, ah, practice your skills. You have only to bring to me what you find there for both of us to benefit.
column2: 我知道有個地方,你可以,呃,練練你的身手。你只要把在那兒找到的東西帶來給我,你我就都能得利。

column1: Centimes are the brass coins that are used everywhere around here.
column2: 生丁是這一帶到處通用的黃銅幣。

column1: Dinars are the gold coins of Shapeir.
column2: 第納爾是夏皮爾的金幣。

column1: Crowns and kopeks are the currency of the land of Mordavia. I seldom exchange either type of coin, for travelers from Mordavia stopped coming to Shapeir some time ago.
column2: 克朗與戈比是莫達維亞那片土地的貨幣。這兩種錢我都很少兌換,因為來自莫達維亞的旅人,早在一段時日前就不再來夏皮爾了。

column1: Royals and commons are the currency of the Kingdom of Tarna. I have many such coins in stock, for there are two travelers from Tarna in this city as we speak.
column2: 皇幣與平幣是塔爾納王國的貨幣。這種錢我手上存了不少,因為此刻城裡正好有兩名來自塔爾納的旅人。

column1: gold coin
column2: 金幣

column1: silver coin
column2: 銀幣

column1: Golds and silvers are the currency of Spielburg Valley and its surrounding regions. They are fairly common coins.
column2: 金幣與銀幣是史畢柏格谷地及其周邊地區的貨幣,算是相當常見的錢幣。

column1: Drachmas are the currency of the kingdom of Marete. During the winter months, numerous merchants from Silmaria journey here to trade and import goods. Many drachmas pass through my hands.
column2: 德拉克馬是馬瑞特王國的貨幣。每到冬季,就有許多來自席爾瑪利亞的商人遠道而來通商、進貨,經我手的德拉克馬可不少。

column1: exchange rate
column2: 匯率

column1: I will gladly trade your foreign money for our dinars and centimes. I always enjoy helping others, and I give the best rate around.
column2: 我很樂意把你的外幣換成我們的第納爾和生丁。我向來樂於助人,而且給的匯率是這一帶最好的。

column1: Early evening is best for talk. It leaves the night for the fun part.
column2: 傍晚最適合聊天,這樣才能把夜晚留給好玩的部分。

column1: Bring things of value for me to evaluate. I'm sure you'll find I make more cents for you that way.
column2: 帶些值錢的東西來讓我估價吧。你會發現,這樣我能替你多賺幾個「分」。

column1: Sorry, my dear, but you'll need a bigger tool.
column2: 抱歉,親愛的,你得換把更大的傢伙才行。

column1: You say, "Why yes, I have it right here."
column2: 你說:「當然啦,東西就在我這兒。」

column1: )Dinarzad smiles and looks at you expectantly.
column2: 蒂娜薩德微微一笑,滿懷期待地看著你。

column1: Men! All talk and no action.
column2: 男人哪!光出一張嘴,從不來真的。

column1: Dinarzad just smiles at you.
column2: 蒂娜薩德只是對你淺淺一笑。

column1: Let us talk about things more important to us.
column2: 我們來聊點對彼此更要緊的事吧。

column1: I am sorry, but I have no information of value to you on that subject.
column2: 抱歉,關於那件事,我沒有什麼對你有用的消息。

column1: You could use more practice at this! I'll reduce my asking price by two dinars, but only because I happen to be very fond of you.
column2: 你這方面還得多練練!我把開價砍掉兩第納爾,不過純粹是因為我剛好挺中意你。

column1: Very well, I will tell you what you wish to know for only 15 dinars.
column2: 好吧,只要十五第納爾,我就告訴你想知道的事。

column1: That's as far as I'll go, Hero.
column2: 就只能到這兒了,英雄。

column1: That's a real cute sign, my little Hero, but these are business hours.
column2: 這手勢還真可愛,我的小英雄,可現在是做生意的時間。

column1: Is that a thieves' sign, or are you just happy to see me?
column2: 那是盜賊的暗號,還是你只是見到我太高興了?

column1: She looks like a tasty morsel. 'Maybe later,' you think.
column2: 她看起來像塊誘人的小點心。「也許等等吧。」你心想。

column1: She coyly evades your kiss.
column2: 她嬌羞地避開了你的吻。

column1: You tell how you joined the Thieves' Guild in Spielburg.
column2: 你說起自己在史畢柏格加入盜賊公會的經過。

column1: There is no Thieves' Guild around here. The Sultan and his men keep a close watch over this city. Had there been an official gathering place for thieves, they certainly would have found it by now.
column2: 這附近可沒有什麼盜賊公會。蘇丹和他的人把這座城盯得緊緊的。要真有盜賊正式聚會的地方,他們早就查出來了。

column1: Of course, there are still plenty of opportunities for the skilled purloiner around here, and you will find the games I like to play a lot more gratifying than those dull old dagger-tossing contests that you mentioned in Spielburg.
column2: 當然啦,這一帶對身手俐落的小偷來說機會多得很,而且你會發現,我喜歡玩的把戲,可比你說的那些史畢柏格無聊的擲匕首比賽過癮多了。

column1: You tell about how you became the hero of Spielburg.
column2: 你說起自己如何成了史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: I'm sure you are dying to spend that reward money. I will be happy to change your foreign currency to the dinars of the realm.
column2: 我猜你早就等不及要花那筆獎金了。我很樂意把你的外幣換成本國通用的第納爾。

column1: You mean Baba Yaga is still in Spielburg valley? I would have thought that a handsome hero such as yourself has what it takes to move a woman.
column2: 你是說芭芭雅嘎還在史畢柏格谷地?我還以為像你這樣英俊的英雄,自有本事打動一個女人呢。

column1: Turned her into a frog, you say? And I thought Prince Charming was supposed to do the opposite.
column2: 把她變成青蛙?你說真的?我還以為白馬王子該做的是反過來才對。

column1: You tell about WIT and your decision to stay in Shapeir.
column2: 你說起巫師技術學院,以及自己決定留在夏皮爾的事。

column1: You are a smart man. Everyone knows the true pleasures in life are not found in an ivory tower.
column2: 你是個聰明人。誰都知道,人生真正的樂趣,可不在那象牙塔裡頭。

column1: You tell Dinarzad about the burglary in the house on Sahir Tarik.
column2: 你向蒂娜薩德說起薩希爾塔里克那棟房子裡的竊案。

column1: I will be sorry to see you leave for Raseir. I haven't had this rewarding a relationship with anyone for a long time. I hope that someday you will be able to start up a business of your own, %s. You would certainly be entitled to it.
column2: 看你動身前往拉希爾,我會捨不得的。我已經好久沒和誰有過這麼有賺頭的交情了。我希望有朝一日你能自己開店,%s。你絕對配得上。

column1: You tell Dinarzad a few things about your break-in at Issur's shop.
column2: 你向蒂娜薩德透露了幾件你闖進伊舒爾店裡的事。

column1: It is such a shame that his ego can not be as easily deflated as his strongbox, wouldn't you agree?
column2: 可惜啊,他的自尊心可不像他的保險箱那麼好洩氣,你說是不是?

column1: You tell Dinarzad a few things about your adventure in the metalworker's house.
column2: 你向蒂娜薩德說了幾件你在金匠家裡冒險的事。

column1: I admire your skills and courage. You would make such a suitable business partner.
column2: 我佩服你的本事和膽量。你會是個再合適不過的生意夥伴。

column1: You tell a little bit about EOF.
column2: 你稍微提了一下永恆戰士團的事。

column1: I am not one of those cheap hussies who fawns at the sight of muscles. You need a lot more than mere brute strength to impress me.
column2: 我可不是那種一見肌肉就獻媚的廉價貨色。光憑一身蠻力,可打動不了我。

column1: You are a very clever person to overcome such a deadly creature.
column2: 你能制伏那樣致命的怪物,真是個聰明人。

column1: You tell about the Fire Elemental in the Gate Plaza.
column2: 你說起城門廣場上的火元素。

column1: I hope you can do something to stop that thing. Think of all the damage it could cause if it started setting people's houses on fire.
column2: 我希望你能設法阻止那東西。想想看,要是它開始放火燒人家的房子,會造成多大的損失。

column1: You obviously have something between your ears that is more reliable than air.
column2: 看來你那兩耳之間裝的東西,可比空氣靠譜多了。

column1: You tell about the Air Elemental blowing around in the Plaza of the Palace.
column2: 你說起在皇宮廣場呼嘯亂竄的風元素。

column1: I am used to dealing with windbags and blowhards, but in this case I am at a loss for advice.
column2: 我見慣了滿口空話、愛吹牛的傢伙,可這一回,我還真給不出什麼建議。

column1: You tell about the Earth Elemental stomping around on the streets.
column2: 你說起在街上橫衝直撞的土元素。

column1: I know. It is getting hard to count money. The coins keep bouncing around on my desk.
column2: 我知道。現在數錢都難了,銅板在我桌上跳個不停。

column1: You tell about how you pulverized the Earth Elemental.
column2: 你說起自己如何把土元素轟成碎片。

column1: That goes to show that size does not necessarily matter.
column2: 這正說明了,塊頭大不見得就管用。

column1: You tell about the Water Elemental in the fountain.
column2: 你說起噴泉裡的水元素。

column1: Water may be the one thing more valuable in this land than money.
column2: 在這片土地上,水也許是唯一比錢還值錢的東西。

column1: Are you equally skilled at sweeping real women off their feet?
column2: 那你把真正的女人迷得神魂顛倒,也一樣在行嗎?

column1: He and I used to do business from time to time. That was when he was still an adventurer looking for wealth and fame. I suppose he decided he enjoyed wealth and infamy more. I guess he got what was coming to him.
column2: 他和我以前偶爾做做生意。那時候他還是個追逐財富與名聲的冒險者。我想後來他覺得財富配上臭名更對他胃口吧。我看他是自作自受。

column1: You tell about the trip on Abdulla's magic carpet.
column2: 你說起搭乘阿布杜拉魔毯的那趟旅程。

column1: Such a shame that the carpet was not yours to keep. Nothing like a magic carpet ride to capture a girl's heart.
column2: 可惜那毯子留不住啊。要擄獲女孩的芳心,沒什麼比得上一趟魔毯之旅了。

column1: You tell about Shema's dancing performance.
column2: 你說起舍瑪的那場舞蹈表演。

column1: If a Katta woman is already capable of moving you, I would love to see how you react to my techniques.
column2: 要是一個卡塔女子就能打動你,那我倒真想看看,我的手段會讓你有什麼反應。

column1: You tell about your stay at the inn.
column2: 你說起自己在客棧落腳的事。

column1: I'm sure you are enjoying your stay in our fair city, but I can tell you there are more exciting places in Shapeir to spend the night.
column2: 我相信你在我們這座美麗的城市住得挺愉快,不過我可以告訴你,夏皮爾還有更刺激的地方可以過夜。

column1: You tell about Omar's poetry reading.
column2: 你說起奧瑪爾的那場吟詩。

column1: Surely there are more exciting ways to spend the evening.
column2: 夜晚要過得精采,肯定還有更刺激的法子。

column1: You tell about how you helped out the enchanted tree in the desert.
column2: 你說起自己如何幫了沙漠裡那棵被施了魔法的樹。

column1: Perhaps I should let you tend to me sometime too. I would not need watering in order to warm up to you.
column2: 也許哪天我也該讓你來照料照料我。要對你動心,我可用不著澆水。

column1: You tell Dinarzad about the enchanted Emir.
column2: 你向蒂娜薩德說起那位中了魔法的埃米爾。

column1: Do be careful when you go to Raseir. I would hate to see your good looks ruined in a similar manner.
column2: 你去拉希爾的時候可得當心。我可不想看到你這張俊臉也落得同樣的下場。

column1: You tell about the caged beast.
column2: 你說起那頭被關在籠裡的野獸。

column1: This is why I often prefer to ask for my payment in advance.
column2: 這正是為什麼我常常寧可要人先付錢。

column1: You tell about Rakeesh.
column2: 你說起拉基什的事。

column1: Rumor has it he is royalty. I could certainly not tell by the amount of money he carried with him.
column2: 有傳言說他是皇室出身。光看他身上帶的那點錢,我可一點兒也看不出來。

column1: She does not seem like a very exciting person to be around.
column2: 她這人待在身邊,似乎沒什麼意思。

column1: You tell about the rope-walking challenge.
column2: 你說起那場走繩索的挑戰。

column1: Many people I have known are skilled at balancing and walking on ropes. An agile body is useful for so many things.
column2: 我認識的不少人都精於平衡、走繩索。身手靈活,用處可多著呢。

column1: She coyly evades your advances.
column2: 她嬌羞地避開了你的挑逗。

column1: My, you certainly have a way with words. You might say you have a golden tongue.
column2: 哎呀,你嘴可真甜。簡直可以說你有一條金舌頭呢。

column1: Dinarzad gives you a disapproving look.
column2: 蒂娜薩德對你投來不以為然的一瞥。

column1: You're not very good at this, are you? But I'll give you an additional percentage just because I happen to like dealing with you. You're such a card.
column2: 你這方面不太行,對吧?不過我還是多算你幾成,純粹因為我剛好喜歡跟你打交道。你這人真有趣。

column1: The teapot is worth 20 dinars. I would normally give you 10 dinars for it, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 這茶壺值二十第納爾。照理我只給你十第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: The bowl is worth 40 dinars. I would normally give you 20 dinars for it, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 這碗值四十第納爾。照理我只給你二十第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: The silver dagger is worth 30 dinars. I would normally give you 15 dinars for it, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 這把銀匕首值三十第納爾。照理我只給你十五第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: The silver scimitars are worth 90 dinars. I would normally give you 45 dinars for them, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 這對銀彎刀值九十第納爾。照理我只給你四十五第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: The jade bracelet is worth 50 dinars. I would normally give you 25 dinars for it, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 這只玉鐲值五十第納爾。照理我只給你二十五第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: I expect that a keen jackal would uncover about 60 dinars worth of loot there. I would normally give you 30 dinars for it, but I will pay you %d dinars because I like you.
column2: 我估摸,精明的胡狼能在那兒挖出約莫六十第納爾的贓物。照理我只給你三十第納爾,不過因為我中意你,就付你 %d 第納爾。

column1: You drive a hard bargain. I can pay you an additional %d dinar, but no more.
column2: 你這價砍得真狠。我頂多再加你 %d 第納爾,不能再多了。

column1: You drive a hard bargain. I can pay you an additional %d dinars, but no more.
column2: 你這價砍得真狠。我頂多再加你 %d 第納爾,不能再多了。

column1: Dinarzad takes the teapot and hands you the money.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下茶壺,把錢遞給你。

column1: Dinarzad takes the bowl and hands you the money.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下碗,把錢遞給你。

column1: Dinarzad takes the silver dagger and hands you the money.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下銀匕首,把錢遞給你。

column1: Dinarzad takes the silver scimitars and hands you the money.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下那對銀彎刀,把錢遞給你。

column1: Dinarzad takes the jade bracelet and hands you the money.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下玉鐲,把錢遞給你。

column1: Dinarzad takes the key and hands you the money, but not before giving you a long appraising look.
column2: 蒂娜薩德收下鑰匙,把錢遞給你,不過遞之前還意味深長地打量了你好一會兒。

column1: Here is your money.
column2: 這是你的錢。

column1: Here you are. I just love intimate changes like this.
column2: 拿去吧。我就愛這種親密的「兌換」。

column1: As much as I enjoy your company, it is time to close up. Perhaps we can spend another night together soon.
column2: 雖然我很享受有你作伴,不過該打烊了。也許我們很快又能共度一夜。

column1: You hear the shutter's lock being released. It must be time for Dinarzad to open up shop.
column2: 你聽見窗板的鎖被打開的聲音。一定是蒂娜薩德要開門做生意了。

column1: The door's lock is too complex for your magic.
column2: 這道門的鎖太精巧,你的魔法奈何不了它。

column1: The shutters are firmly locked and barred from the inside.
column2: 窗板從裡頭牢牢上了鎖、閂得死死的。

column1: The shutter's locks are too strong for your magic.
column2: 窗板的鎖太堅固,你的魔法奈何不了。

column1: I usually don't care much for men who come early, but in your case, I'll make an exception.
column2: 我通常不太喜歡太早來的男人,不過對你嘛,我就破個例。

column1: So, we have a stranger to our fair land. Welcome, traveler from afar. Come closer, that you might find all that you seek here.
column2: 瞧,我們這片美麗的土地來了位生面孔。歡迎你,遠道而來的旅人。靠近些,也許你尋覓的一切,都能在這兒找著。

column1: I am Dinarzad, Changer of Money for Shapeir. Would you like to exchange something with me?
column2: 我是蒂娜薩德,夏皮爾的錢幣兌換人。你想跟我換點什麼嗎?

column1: The word on the street is that you seek to quash the oppression of Raseir and bring freedom there. Let us hope that you are as skilled as I have heard, for you will certainly not get by on looks alone.
column2: 街頭巷尾都在傳,說你打算終結拉希爾的壓迫、把自由帶到那兒。但願你真如傳聞中那般有本事,因為光靠這張臉,你可絕對混不過去。
