column1: If you are interested, and I think you will be, I do have a job for you.
column2: 要是你有興趣——我想你會有的——我這兒倒有件差事要交給你。

column1: If you find yourself walking the streets at around noon of this night, perhaps you should stroll down a certain street I could mention and check out the lock on a certain door.
column2: 今晚午夜時分,要是你正巧在街上閒逛,也許該到我說的某條街上走一遭,順道瞧瞧某道門上的鎖。

column1: Most of the family will be staying out very late tonight, and at that house is a silver tea service for which I have a great fondness.
column2: 那家人今晚大多會在外頭待到很晚,而那棟屋子裡,有一套我十分鍾愛的銀茶具。

column1: If you happened to be in the neighborhood, you might pick it up for me. Are you interested in this little job?
column2: 要是你剛好經過那一帶,不妨替我把它取回來。對這件小差事有興趣嗎?

column1: You appear to have become quite proficient in your craft. Would you like another opportunity to show me how well you handle your tool? I may have some more work for you.
column2: 看來你這門手藝已經相當純熟了。想不想再給我一次機會,讓我瞧瞧你那傢伙使得多順手?我這兒也許還有些活兒給你。

column1: Rumor has it that a thief broke into the metalworker's house this night. There will be extra guards patrolling the streets. Tomorrow will be a good time to sleep in.
column2: 有傳言說,今晚有個小偷闖進了金匠家。街上會多派衛兵巡邏。明天正好可以睡個懶覺。

column1: Rumor has it that a thief broke into the metalworker's house the other night. There will be extra guards patrolling the streets tonight. It will be a good night to sleep in.
column2: 有傳言說,前幾晚有個小偷闖進了金匠家。今晚街上會多派衛兵巡邏。正好可以睡個好覺。

column1: By the way, did you happen to pick up the little item I mentioned before?
column2: 對了,我先前提的那件小東西,你可有順手帶來?

column1: I've seen many men in my time, and you're certainly one of them.
column2: 這輩子我見過的男人多了去,你嘛,當然也算其中一個。

column1: You might want to know where a certain someone (who shall be nameless, yet for whom I have little love) conceals his safe.
column2: 你也許會想知道,某個人——名字就不提了,反正我對他也沒什麼好感——把他的保險箱藏在哪兒。

column1: Ah, the sunshine returns to our corner of the alley. Would you like me to be of service to you?
column2: 啊,陽光又照進我們這條巷子的角落了。要我為你效勞嗎?

column1: Happy to see me, or is that just the money in your pocket?
column2: 見到我太高興了,還是只是你口袋裡的錢在作怪?

column1: A pleasure to see you again. I like a man who just keeps on coming.
column2: 很高興又見到你。我就喜歡這種一來再來的男人。

column1: Try as you might, you cannot bend the iron grillwork with your bare hands.
column2: 任你怎麼使勁,也沒法用赤手扳彎那鐵柵欄。

column1: Don't bother talking to the iron grillwork.
column2: 別費勁跟鐵柵欄說話了。

column1: The shutter covers the window. It looks like the business is not in business at the moment.
column2: 窗板遮住了窗子。看樣子這生意眼下沒在做。

column1: The oil sconce is not lit.
column2: 那盞油壁燈沒點著。

column1: The alley through which you entered looks lighter, even when compared to this well-lit alcove.
column2: 你進來的那條巷子看起來更亮,連這燈火通明的小凹間都比不上。

column1: The alley through which you entered looks lighter than the dark alcove in which you now find yourself.
column2: 你進來的那條巷子,看起來比你現在置身的這個昏暗小凹間還要亮。

column1: You can't take the oil sconce.
column2: 你拿不走那盞油壁燈。

column1: Don't lick the torches.
column2: 別去舔那些火把。

column1: That would be a good way to burn your tongue.
column2: 那可是燙傷舌頭的好辦法。

column1: This small alcove at the end of the alley has a small window with an iron grate above it. Two oil sconces illuminate the guard standing nearby and the woman behind the window.
column2: 巷子盡頭這個小凹間,有一扇小窗,窗上方裝著鐵柵。兩盞油壁燈照亮了站在一旁的衛兵,以及窗後的女子。

column1: This small alcove at the end of the alley has a small window with an iron grate above it. The two oil sconces used for illumination are doused and there is no one about.
column2: 巷子盡頭這個小凹間,有一扇小窗,窗上方裝著鐵柵。原本用來照明的兩盞油壁燈都熄了,四下無人。

column1: This small alcove at the end of the alley has a small window with an iron grate above it. The window is closed off with wooden shutters and there is no one about.
column2: 巷子盡頭這個小凹間,有一扇小窗,窗上方裝著鐵柵。窗子用木窗板封住了,四下無人。

column1: Be careful what you touch around here. Everything from the walls to the tiles are covered in dirt and grime.
column2: 在這一帶碰東西可得當心。從牆面到地磚,全都沾滿了塵土污垢。

column1: Be careful what you touch around here. Everything from the walls to the tiles are covered in dirt and gunk and grime.
column2: 在這一帶碰東西可得當心。從牆面到地磚,全都沾滿了塵土、黏垢和污泥。

column1: There is no reply.
column2: 沒有任何回應。

column1: He's a man of few words. Actually, he's a man of NO words.
column2: 他是個沉默寡言的人。其實該說,他是個一個字都不吐的人。

column1: Franc is not interested in that.
column2: 法蘭克對那不感興趣。

column1: You cannot open the shutters. They are locked and firmly bolted from the inside.
column2: 你打不開那些窗板。它們從裡頭上了鎖、閂得牢牢的。

column1: No amount of talking will make the shutters open up for you.
column2: 任你說破嘴,那些窗板也不會為你打開。

column1: That would have no effect on the barred shutters.
column2: 那對閂死的窗板起不了作用。

column1: It leads to Franc's quarters.
column2: 這通往法蘭克的住處。

column1: That would be tough with Franc standing right in front of it.
column2: 法蘭克就杵在那前面,這可不好辦。

column1: The door appears to be barred from the inside. What a disappointment.
column2: 這道門似乎從裡頭閂上了。真叫人失望。

column1: Your lock-picking skill isn't high enough to open the lock on this door.
column2: 你的開鎖技巧還不夠高,打不開這道門上的鎖。

column1: You got the tumbler sequence correct. However, just as you can tackle the final tumbler, you hear a noise behind the door. You quickly take your lock pick out of the lock.
column2: 你撥對了鎖簧的順序。然而就在你正要對付最後一根鎖簧時,卻聽見門後傳來一陣聲響。你連忙把開鎖工具從鎖孔裡抽了出來。

column1: As you take your lock pick out of the lock, you hear a soft noise behind the door.
column2: 你把開鎖工具從鎖孔抽出時,聽見門後傳來一陣輕微的聲響。

column1: You hear another noise behind the door, this time slightly louder. Maybe you disturbed the occupant.
column2: 你又聽見門後傳來一陣聲響,這回稍微大了些。也許你驚動了裡頭的人。

column1: As you have another go at the lock, the door suddenly opens and Franc makes it clear to you how much he hates people trying to enter his quarters.
column2: 你正要再撥一次鎖,門卻猛地打開,法蘭克明白無誤地讓你領教到,他有多痛恨有人想闖進他的住處。

column1: Persistence didn't pay off
column2: 這回鍥而不捨可沒得到好下場

column1: It seems the door is locked. You find no way to open it.
column2: 這道門看來是鎖上了。你找不到任何辦法打開它。

column1: No magic words will open this door.
column2: 什麼咒語也打不開這道門。

column1: I have not yet had time to find your answers.
column2: 我還沒來得及替你找到答案。

column1: You must return another day.
column2: 你得改天再來一趟。

column1: I have told you all I presently know of your future. However, I predict that I will know far more in the future.
column2: 關於你的未來,我目前所知的都已經告訴你了。不過我預言,將來我會知道得更多。

column1: eye shadow
column2: 眼影

column1: The planet known as the Eye has crossed the sign of the Shadow. The Shadow is watching us, and who knows what evil lurks? I sure don't.
column2: 那顆叫做「眼」的行星已經越過了「影」的星座。「影」正盯著我們瞧,誰曉得有什麼邪惡潛伏其中?反正我是不曉得。

column1: The Eye of the Shadow is over the city. The Dark Hand is moving toward us. There will be misfortune here shortly.
column2: 「影之眼」高懸城上。「黑手」正朝我們逼近。不久之後,此地將有災厄降臨。

column1: The Dark Hand is over Raseir now. Its ruling planet has been missing since it passed across the Dragon.
column2: 「黑手」如今籠罩著拉希爾。自從它掠過「龍」之後,主宰它的那顆行星便消失無蹤了。

column1: The Djinn are ruled by the moon. In a short while, the moon will pass between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth. The moon will be weakened, and the Djinn made strong.
column2: 精靈受月亮主宰。再過不久,月亮將穿行於「蠍」的鉗爪與「龍」的利齒之間。月亮會被削弱,而精靈將因此強盛。

column1: The planet Salim is a sign of safety. Amal is the ruling planet of hope. With those missing from the sky, we are indeed in trouble. Make sure you keep in touch with your insurance agent.
column2: 薩林行星是平安的徵兆,阿瑪爾則是主宰希望的行星。如今這兩顆都從天空消失了,我們可真是麻煩大了。我勸你最好跟你的保險業務員保持聯絡。

column1: The planet Lahib is currently ascending. Who knows what danger this could bring?
column2: 拉希卜行星目前正在上升。誰曉得這會帶來什麼樣的危險?

column1: The stars have very little to say about that.
column2: 關於那件事,群星沒什麼好說的。

column1: The planet Lahib is ascending. A Fire Elemental will gain more strength as it rises.
column2: 拉希卜行星正在上升。隨著它升起,火元素將獲得更強的力量。

column1: I've told you about that once already, haven't I?
column2: 那件事我不是已經跟你說過一遍了嗎?

column1: You know, I have never seen the stars in that particular configuration.
column2: 你知道嗎,我從沒見過群星排成那種奇特的格局。

column1: A Water Elemental at this time would be deadly. The Dragon is high, and all things magical in nature are more powerful.
column2: 此刻若出現水元素,那可是致命的。「龍」高懸天際,凡帶有魔法本質的事物都會更加強大。

column1: The Wind sign approaches the city. You would do well to avoid tornadoes today.
column2: 「風」之星座正逼近這座城市。今天你最好離龍捲風遠一點。

column1: Watch for falling rocks.
column2: 小心落石。

column1: Elementals are things of magic and thus are ruled by the Dragon.
column2: 元素生物本是魔法之物,因此受「龍」所主宰。

column1: Magic ebbs and flows with the positions of the stars.
column2: 魔法隨著群星的位置而消長起落。

column1: I never was very good at those things.
column2: 那些東西我向來不太在行。

column1: I can only tell you what the stars say. You are the one who has to figure out what they mean.
column2: 我只能告訴你群星所言。至於那是什麼意思,得靠你自己去參透。

column1: Personally, I don't subscribe to that particular theory.
column2: 就我個人而言,我並不認同那套說法。

column1: The Sultan (May His Name Be Printed on the Stars!) consults me regularly.
column2: 蘇丹(願其名永鐫於星辰之上!)常來向我請教。

column1: The sign of the Emir Arus al-Din got crossed with that of the Saurus. He has been missing ever since.
column2: 埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁的星象與索魯斯的星象交錯在一起,從那之後他就失蹤了。

column1: I visit the Palace each morning to tell of the night's events.
column2: 我每天清晨都會進宮,稟報昨夜星象所示之事。

column1: I will inform the Caravan Master when the omens are auspicious for the caravan.
column2: 等徵兆顯示商隊出行吉利時,我自會通知商隊隊長。

column1: I depend upon the generosity of my clients to maintain my lifestyle. If you wish to pay me for the knowledge I have shared with you, I would be most grateful.
column2: 我這日子全仰賴主顧們的慷慨打賞。倘若你願意為我分享的這些學問付點酬勞,我可是感激不盡。

column1: The prediction for this night is mostly darkness with occasional light toward morning.
column2: 今夜的預報是:大致漆黑一片,近天明時偶有微光。

column1: I talk to the stars, and the things they tell are more than mere rumor.
column2: 我與群星對話,它們所告知的可不只是道聽塗說。

column1: I am Abu al-Njun.
column2: 我是阿布·納瓊。

column1: I have been stargazing since I was little. There is so much to learn from astrology.
column2: 我從小就開始觀星。占星之術,可有學不完的學問哪。

column1: The sign of the Katta is visible just after sunset.
column2: 卡塔族的星座,在日落之後便清晰可見。

column1: The casting of darts is the astrologer's version of the scientific method.
column2: 擲飛鏢,正是占星師版本的科學方法。

column1: The orrery works on magnetic principles. It was told to me that it describes an obscure system, containing such planets as Xenon, Ortega, Pestulon, Phleebhut, and Uranus. I don't know what to think about Uranus.
column2: 這台太陽系儀靠磁力原理運轉。有人告訴我,它描繪的是一個冷僻的星系,裡頭有像氙星、歐特加、佩斯圖隆、弗利布特,還有天王星這些行星。天王星嘛……我實在不知該怎麼看待它。

column1: What a beauty, eh? I love being up high, nearer the stars.
column2: 美吧?我就愛待在高處,離星辰更近些。

column1: star chart
column2: 星圖

column1: Actually, my stockbroker gave me the chart you see on the wall.
column2: 說實話,你看牆上那張圖,是我的股票經紀人送的。

column1: Hasan is my lens washer. Wonderful boy, but a bit convexing at times.
column2: 哈山是我的鏡片清潔工。是個好孩子,只是有時候腦筋有點「凸」槌。

column1: The Dragon is the sign of vast magical power.
column2: 「龍」是浩瀚魔力的徵兆。

column1: The Scorpion portends poison and deceit.
column2: 「蠍」預示著毒害與欺詐。

column1: Avoid it whenever possible.
column2: 能避就避,千萬別碰。

column1: The stars predict that only a great hero may aid us through the upcoming darkness.
column2: 群星預示:唯有一位偉大的英雄,方能助我們渡過即將到來的黑暗。

column1: They are small, burning objects which move constantly in the night sky.
column2: 它們是一些細小燃燒的物體,在夜空中不停移動。

column1: The relationships of the stars influence our lives constantly.
column2: 群星之間的關係,無時無刻不影響著我們的人生。

column1: Constellations are wonderful cars, but I personally prefer Novas and Galaxies.
column2: 「星座」牌的車子是不錯啦,不過我個人還是偏愛「新星」跟「銀河」這兩款。

column1: dark hand
column2: 黑手

column1: The stars which shape the Hand signify action. When the Hand is dark, it means oppression.
column2: 構成「手」這個星象的群星,象徵著行動。而當「手」轉暗,那便意味著壓迫。

column1: Astrology is based on the principle of 'as above, so below.' The heavens reflect the earth, and vice versa.
column2: 占星術奠基於「上行下效、天地相應」的原則。天界映照人間,人間亦映照天界。

column1: The planet Salim has been missing from the sky for several days now. Last night, the planet Amal was gone. Instead, a large, fiery star blazed from the eye of the Dragon. Even now, the Dragon and the Scorpion are rising.
column2: 薩林行星已經從天空消失好幾天了。昨夜,連阿瑪爾行星也不見了蹤影,取而代之的,是一顆碩大如火的星辰,從「龍」之眼處熊熊燃燒。即便此刻,「龍」與「蠍」也正冉冉升起。

column1: These guides to future actions are everywhere in the sky. The secret is to understand their meaning.
column2: 這些指引未來行動的徵兆,佈滿了整片天空。訣竅就在於——讀懂它們的含意。

column1: My job is to reveal the future to those who seek it. Perhaps you wish to know your own fortune.
column2: 我的本行,就是為求問者揭示未來。也許你也想知道自己的命運如何?

column1: I have seen signs of great destruction over our city. These are most disturbing. It might be a good time to take a vacation.
column2: 我看見了預示本城將遭大難的星象。實在令人不安。眼下,或許正是出門度個假的好時機。

column1: As I have said, you have lived a most interesting life.
column2: 正如我所說的,你這一生,過得可真是精采絕倫。

column1: Thank you. The more you tell me of your past the better I can judge your future.
column2: 謝謝你。你把過去的事告訴我得越多,我就越能斷準你的未來。

column1: Ah, %s. It is indeed a name for heroes.
column2: 啊,%s。這的確是個屬於英雄的名字。

column1: It is well that you have come, then. The signs show that we will soon need a hero here.
column2: 那麼,你的到來正是時候。星象顯示,此地不久便需要一位英雄。

column1: What an interesting name, %s. It certainly isn't one I would have expected you to bear.
column2: 真是個有趣的名字,%s。這可不是我料想得到你會頂著的名號。

column1: Come in, come in, Hasan, come in.
column2: 進來,進來,哈山,快進來。

column1: Oh, that is not you, is it? It is hard for me to tell at such a distance.
column2: 喔,原來不是你呀?隔這麼遠,我實在看不太清楚。

column1: Come in anyway, and welcome. I am Abu al-Njun and I am the 'Astrologer of the Stars and Omens.'
column2: 不管是誰,進來吧,歡迎歡迎。我是阿布·納瓊,人稱「星辰與徵兆的占星師」。

column1: Who are you that I might know you?
column2: 請問尊姓大名,讓我也好認識認識?

column1: It is good that you returned; there is much I need to tell you.
column2: 你回來得正好,我有許多話要告訴你。

column1: Alas, never before have I read such troubling signs as those which guide you. Your future is very dark.
column2: 唉,我從未讀到過像指引你命運這般令人憂心的星象。你的未來,一片黑暗哪。

column1: I have heard that you shall travel to Raseir. Good luck, and may the stars watch over and guide you.
column2: 我聽說你將動身前往拉希爾。祝你好運,願群星照看並指引你。

column1: There are omens of fire throughout the sky, and many portents of destruction. Now, what is it that you wish to know this bright day?
column2: 滿天都是烈火的徵兆,以及種種毀滅的預示。那麼,在這晴朗的一天,你想知道些什麼呢?

column1: A sign of wind rises in the east and the city is like a leaf before it. How is it that I may help you?
column2: 東方升起了「風」之星象,這座城就像它面前的一片落葉。我能幫你什麼忙呢?

column1: I have seen signs of heavy, impending doom. Now how else can I make your day?
column2: 我看見了沉重而迫近的厄運之兆。那麼,我還能為你做點什麼,讓你今天開心些呢?

column1: Water rules this day -- both lack of it and the surfeit of it. You should either invest heavily in wine or waterwings.
column2: 今日由「水」主宰——既鬧水荒,又會水患成災。我勸你,要嘛重金囤酒,要嘛多買幾副游泳臂圈。

column1: Just a moment. I'm doing a very important derivation right now.
column2: 稍等一下。我現在正在做一道非常重要的推算。

column1: Let's see now, if the moon is in the seventh house (mumble, mumble)...
column2: 讓我想想,假如月亮落在第七宮(喃喃,喃喃)……

column1: ... hmm... and Jupiter aligns with Mars (mumble, mumble)...
column2: ……嗯……再加上木星與火星連成一線(喃喃,喃喃)……

column1: ...then peace shall guide our planet and love...?!
column2: ……那麼,和平與愛將會指引我們的星球……?!

column1: Naw, it'll never happen.
column2: 算了吧,這種事絕不可能發生。

column1: Now what was it you needed to know?
column2: 好,你剛剛是想知道什麼來著?

column1: There will be a great rise in celebrations today, followed by a rapid decline tomorrow. What else can I do for you?
column2: 今天慶典之事將大為興盛,而明天則會急轉直下。我還能為你做點什麼呢?

column1: It was nice of you to drop by, but nightfall is upon us and I need to get to my observatory. Please come back tomorrow.
column2: 承蒙你光臨,只是夜幕已然低垂,我得上我的天文台去了。請你明天再來吧。

column1: There is a dart stuck in the wall.
column2: 牆上插著一支飛鏢。

column1: The dart does not belong to you.
column2: 這支飛鏢不是你的。

column1: You can't get the model of the unknown planetary system.
column2: 你拿不走那具未知星系的模型。

column1: You can't get the map of the heavens.
column2: 你拿不走那張天界圖。

column1: You can't get the psychic dartboard.
column2: 你拿不走那塊通靈飛鏢靶。

column1: You can't get the cryptic chart of mystical origins.
column2: 你拿不走那張來歷神祕的奧義圖。

column1: Please, I would prefer if you do not go up there. That leads to my observatory on the roof where I keep very delicate instruments.
column2: 拜託,我希望你別往上頭去。那兒通往屋頂的天文台,我放了好些極為精密的儀器。

column1: Abu already has everything he needs.
column2: 阿布需要的東西,早就一應俱全了。

column1: You do not need to take the bed's canopy.
column2: 你用不著把床上的帷幔拿走。

column2: 你聽見了另一個聲音。

column1: No, Father.
column2: 不是,父親。

column1: No, Fathher.
column2: 不是,爹啊。

column1: Old Man
column2: 老人

column1: Huh. I'm hearing things again, I guess.
column2: 嗯。我看哪,我又出現幻聽了。

column1: Yes, Father.
column2: 是的,父親。

column1: Huh. Must've been my imagination again.
column2: 嗯。準是我又在胡思亂想了。

column1: Huh, Kamar, your father is becoming senile.
column2: 唉,卡瑪爾啊,你老爹是越來越老糊塗囉。

column1: Father, that was not me. There is someone else in the house!
column2: 父親,剛剛那不是我。屋裡還有別人!

column1: My Sons! Kamar! Kareem! Abdul! Jabbar! The house is being robbed!
column2: 我的兒子們!卡瑪爾!卡林姆!阿布杜!賈巴爾!家裡進了賊啦!

column1: But Faddher, that wasn' me who jusht said that.
column2: 可是爹啊,剛剛說話的不是我啦。

column1: Oh, I thought I was in here. I guess I must be in the other room.
column2: 喔,我還以為我人在這間呢。看來我準是在另一個房間。

column1: Kamar! There is someone in the house!
column2: 卡瑪爾!屋裡有人!

column1: Is that you, Kareem?
column2: 是你嗎,卡林姆?

column1: Is that you, Abdul?
column2: 是你嗎,阿布杜?

column1: Is that you, Jabbar?
column2: 是你嗎,賈巴爾?

column1: You hear the voice of an old man.
column2: 你聽見一位老人的聲音。

column1: Is that you, Kamar?
column2: 是你嗎,卡瑪爾?

column1: You hide.
column2: 你躲了起來。

column1: While running for home. Looks like a squeeze play! You'll never get to first base if you don't remember the basics. Thieves' Handbook rule number one: When in doubt, don't.
column2: 眼看就要跑回本壘了,卻碰上一記強迫取分!連基本功都記不牢,你連一壘都上不了。《盜賊手冊》第一條鐵則:拿不定主意時,就別動手。

column1: Caught stealing...
column2: 行竊被逮……

column1: Having discovered your presence, it takes but a short search to discover your hiding place.
column2: 既然已經察覺到你的存在,只消稍一搜尋,你的藏身之處便無所遁形了。

column1: Clean up after yourself
column2: 善後要做好

column1: You'll have to think faster than that.
column2: 你的腦筋得轉得再快一點才行。

column1: Hiding is a Lost Art
column2: 藏匿,可是一門失傳的藝術

column1: As the pieces of pottery hurtle towards you, you realize that you can't possibly catch them all. Several fall to the floor, crashing loudly and alerting the old man.
column2: 那些陶器碎片朝你飛來,你這才驚覺自己根本接不完。好幾片摔落在地,匡啷一聲響,驚動了那位老人。

column1: As it strikes the drapes, the sound frightens the old man. He calls for his sons who are angry. VERY angry. And violent. VERY, VERY violent.
column2: 它撞上了窗簾,那聲響嚇了老人一跳。他喚來了兒子們,個個火冒三丈。非常火大。而且動起手來。非常、非常凶狠。

column1: As it strikes the wardrobe, the sound alerts the old man. He calls out for his sons. They are less than gentle with you.
column2: 它撞上了衣櫥,那聲響驚動了老人。他喚來了兒子們。他們對你可一點都不客氣。

column1: No stealing aloud!
column2: 不准出聲行竊!

column1: As it strikes, the sound alerts the old man. He calls out for his sons. They are less than gentle with you.
column2: 它撞了上去,那聲響驚動了老人。他喚來了兒子們。他們對你可一點都不客氣。

column1: What a blitz! It looks like you lost yardage on that play. Brothers: 6, %s: 0
column2: 好一記猛攻!這一回合你看來是倒退了不少碼。兄弟隊:6 分,%s:0 分。

column1: Quarterback sack...
column2: 四分衛擒殺……

column1: You take the tea service and stash it away.
column2: 你拿走了那套茶具,順手藏進懷裡。

column1: You oil the rusty hinges. Hopefully they won't creak now.
column2: 你給生鏽的鉸鏈上了油。但願這下它們不會再嘎吱作響了。

column1: You oil the rusty hinges. Hopefully they won't creak anymore.
column2: 你給生鏽的鉸鏈上了油。但願這下它們再也不會嘎吱作響了。

column1: You find nothing of value inside the wardrobe.
column2: 你在衣櫥裡沒找到什麼值錢的東西。

column1: There is something hidden in the bottom corner of the wardrobe.
column2: 衣櫥底部的角落裡藏著什麼東西。

column1: The doors open slowly. The hinges make a loud squeaking sound.
column2: 櫥門緩緩開啟,鉸鏈發出一陣刺耳的嘎吱聲。

column1: Fortunately, nobody seems to have heard it.
column2: 所幸,似乎沒有人聽見那聲響。

column1: You open the wardrobe doors.
column2: 你打開了衣櫥門。

column1: You find an emerald-studded silver bowl and stash it away.
column2: 你找到一只鑲滿翡翠的銀碗,順手藏了起來。

column1: You close the wardrobe doors.
column2: 你關上了衣櫥門。

column1: Someone should really oil those hinges.
column2: 真該有人去給那些鉸鏈上點油了。

column1: You find a trapdoor concealed below this carpet.
column2: 你發現這塊地毯底下藏著一道暗門。

column1: You hear the sound of someone singing something about a "Whirling dervisssh" and the sound of a key trying to find a lock.
column2: 你聽見有人哼著什麼「旋轉的苦行僧~」之類的小調,還夾雜著一把鑰匙東戳西戳、找不著鎖孔的聲音。

column1: Is that you Kareem?
column2: 是你嗎,卡林姆?

column1: Yesh, Fatther.
column2: 是滴,爹啊。

column1: Lucky for you, Kareem was a little too inebriated to see you. Better not press your luck next time. Find a good place to hide.
column2: 算你走運,卡林姆醉得太厲害,沒瞧見你。下回可別再這麼僥倖了。快找個好地方躲起來。

column1: You unroll the rug back to its original position.
column2: 你把地毯重新攤平,鋪回原來的位置。

column1: You open the trapdoor and discover a heavy, locked chest concealed beneath the floorboards.
column2: 你打開暗門,發現地板下藏著一口沉重的、上了鎖的箱子。

column1: You open the trapdoor.
column2: 你打開了暗門。

column1: You hear a voice outside.
column2: 你聽見外頭傳來一個聲音。

column1: Now, is it this door?
column2: 那麼,是這扇門嗎?

column1: I think I just passed the alleyway. But was I coming or going?
column2: 我好像剛走過那條巷子。可我這是要回家,還是要出門來著?

column1: What was it Father told me? If the key around my neck opens the lock... Ah! This must be my house.
column2: 父親是怎麼交代我的來著?要是我脖子上掛的這把鑰匙能開這道鎖……啊!那這準是我家沒錯了。

column1: You hear a key turning in the lock.
column2: 你聽見鑰匙在鎖孔裡轉動的聲音。

column1: I think so, Father.
column2: 我想是吧,父親。

column1: Have we met before?
column2: 咱們以前見過嗎?

column1: I'm Abdul, the youngest son of Abu bin Ma'amar.
column2: 我是阿布杜,阿布·賓·馬瑪爾家最小的兒子。

column1: Why are you fooling with Father's secret hiding place which I'm not supposed to ever mention?
column2: 你幹嘛去動父親那個祕密藏寶處?那地方我可是連提都不准提的呀。

column1: Kamar! See what foolishness your brother is speaking of.
column2: 卡瑪爾!快來瞧瞧你弟弟在胡說八道些什麼。

column1: Grab him!
column2: 抓住他!

column1: Grab whom? Oh, him!
column2: 抓住誰?喔,他呀!

column1: Father, why is your secret hiding place open?
column2: 父親,你的祕密藏寶處怎麼是開著的?

column1: (I wonder why father would have left the carpet rolled up? Oh well...)
column2: (真奇怪,父親怎麼會把地毯捲起來不放呢?算了,管它的……)

column1: It's a good thing you are lucky, because you sure pick lousy places to hide.
column2: 幸虧你運氣好,因為你挑的藏身地點實在是爛透了。

column1: You were lucky that Abdul wasn't smart enough to suspect a burglary. Next time, you'd best replace the rug.
column2: 算你走運,阿布杜還沒精明到會懷疑有人入室行竊。下回,你最好把地毯鋪回原位。

column1: Your senses tell you it might be safe to come out now.
column2: 你的直覺告訴你,現在出來,或許是安全的了。

column1: Wasn't that fun today? It isn't often we get to torture a thief. If you ask me, criminals have too many rights these days.
column2: 今天玩得夠爽吧?難得有個小偷可以拷打。要我說啊,這年頭罪犯的權利還真是太多了。

column1: Baddhi Slama
column2: 巴迪‧斯拉瑪

column1: Boy, yeah! Can't wait 'til tomorrow when we get to cut off his OTHER hand! Har Har.
column2: 可不是嘛!等不及明天把他另一隻手也剁了!哈哈。

column1: Yeah, then he'll have no RIGHTS LEFT. Ho ho ho.
column2: 對啊,到時候他就「右」也沒、「左」也沒囉。呵呵呵。

column1: See ya tomorrow, Jabbie, old buddy!
column2: 明天見啦,老兄賈比!

column1: Right! Ho ho ho.
column2: 沒錯!呵呵呵。

column1: Oh, rat-dung! You made me drop my keys. Where'd they go?
column2: 哎呀,該死!被你害得鑰匙掉了。跑哪去了?

column1: Here they are. See you tomorrow, Baddhi.
column2: 在這兒。明天見,巴迪。

column1: Kamar! Come here! There is a thief in the house!
column2: 卡馬爾!過來!家裡有小偷!

column1: Yes, Father. But why is the carpet not over the secret trapdoor?
column2: 是,父親。可是地毯怎麼沒蓋在那道暗門上?

column1: I have returned from the late watch, Father.
column2: 我守完夜班回來了,父親。

column1: You've already done that.
column2: 你已經做過了。

column1: The trapdoor swings open. You see a heavy, locked chest concealed beneath the floorboards.
column2: 暗門應聲掀開。你看見地板下藏著一只沉重、上了鎖的箱子。

column1: The chest's lock springs open, the lid swings wide, and you see some dinars within.
column2: 箱鎖彈開,蓋子大張,你看見裡頭有些第納爾。

column1: The chest is already open.
column2: 箱子已經開了。

column1: You can't Fetch the trapdoor.
column2: 你沒辦法把暗門取過來。

column1: You can't Fetch the chest. It's much too heavy.
column2: 你沒辦法把箱子取過來。它實在太重了。

column1: Your spell latches onto the 60 dinars.
column2: 你的咒語牢牢扣住了那 60 枚第納爾。

column1: You hear two men talking outside the door.
column2: 你聽見門外有兩個人在交談。

column1: You've already taken the dinars from the chest.
column2: 你已經把第納爾從箱子裡拿走了。

column1: Keep your feet on the ground, Hero.
column2: 腳踏實地一點,英雄。

column1: Did you hear that noise, Kamar? See what it might be!
column2: 卡馬爾,你聽見那聲響沒?去看看是什麼!

column1: There's nothing underneath the tablecloth.
column2: 桌布底下什麼也沒有。

column1: You're already hiding in a good place.
column2: 你已經躲在一個好地方了。

column1: The tablecloth is almost the same color as your clothes.
column2: 那桌布的顏色跟你的衣服幾乎一模一樣。

column1: You can't seem to find a lock to pick on the trapdoor.
column2: 這道暗門上你似乎找不到鎖可撬。

column1: You have already opened the locked chest.
column2: 你已經把上鎖的箱子打開了。

column1: The trapdoor is closed.
column2: 暗門關著。

column1: Upon looking closer, you notice something strange about the chest.
column2: 湊近一瞧,你發覺這箱子有點古怪。

column1: You see a large chest. It is locked.
column2: 你看見一只大箱子。它上了鎖。

column1: You see a large, open chest. There is something inside.
column2: 你看見一只敞開的大箱子。裡頭有東西。

column1: You see a large, open chest. It is empty.
column2: 你看見一只敞開的大箱子。裡頭是空的。

column1: You are down to your last dagger. Better hold on to it.
column2: 你只剩最後一把匕首了。還是留著吧。

column1: It's not safe to go that way right now.
column2: 現在往那邊走並不安全。

column1: The pots on this shelf are pretty, but pretty worthless.
column2: 架上這些壺挺漂亮,可惜漂亮得一文不值。

column1: There is, however, a silver tea service on the top shelf which looks valuable.
column2: 不過最上層架子上有一套銀製茶具,看起來挺值錢。

column1: You can't that there.
column2: 你沒辦法在那兒那樣做。

column1: You can't do that there.
column2: 你沒辦法在那兒那樣做。

column1: The chest is locked.
column2: 箱子上了鎖。

column1: Searching further, you find a false bottom in the chest. Beneath it is a silver dagger.
column2: 再翻找一下,你在箱子裡發現了夾層。底下是一把銀匕首。

column1: You find nothing else of value.
column2: 你沒再找到別的值錢東西。

column1: There's little to say about the chest, other than the fact that it's locked.
column2: 關於這箱子沒什麼好說的,就是上了鎖罷了。

column1: You see a large, open chest. There is a stash of coins inside.
column2: 你看見一只敞開的大箱子。裡頭藏著一堆錢幣。

column1: You find 60 dinars, which you quickly put away.
column2: 你找到 60 枚第納爾,趕緊收進口袋。

column1: Searching further, you find a false bottom in the chest. Beneath it you find a silver dagger, which you take and stash away.
column2: 再翻找一下,你在箱子裡發現了夾層。底下找到一把銀匕首,你拿起來收好。

column1: You'll need to open the chest first.
column2: 你得先把箱子打開。

column1: You see nothing else to take.
column2: 你沒看到別的可拿了。

column1: Together you will save the Emir. You and Ad Avis will get the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 你們將一同拯救埃米爾。你和阿德‧阿維斯會取得伊布利斯之像。

column1: You there, try walking through the door now!
column2: 喂,你!現在試著穿過那道門!

column1: No, no, fool! Pass through it![[Oh, get out of the way.
column2: 不不,蠢貨!是「穿」過去![[唉,讓開。

column1: You have no idea how or why you got here, but it is nice to be able to help such a nice man.
column2: 你完全搞不清楚自己怎麼來的、又為什麼會在這裡,不過能幫上這麼好的人,感覺真不賴。

column1: I cannot be mistaken. My translation of the prophecy must be correct. It must be you. Of all the stupid heroes in the world, I had to be cursed with you!
column2: 我不可能弄錯。我對預言的解讀必定無誤。一定是你。全天下那麼多蠢英雄,我偏偏被你給纏上!

column1: What is wrong? How does it go?
column2: 出了什麼岔子?那段是怎麼說的?

column1: For one thousand years and a year,[Iblis will be bound...[[I know that it has been one thousand and one years to this very day, so that MUST be correct.
column2: 「歷經一千年又一年,[伊布利斯將受縛……」[[我清楚今天正好滿一千零一年,所以這段「絕對」沒錯。

column1: Beneath the tombstones of the city that he once did found.[[This is the Forbidden City, there is no doubt of that.
column2: 「就在他昔日所建之城的墓碑之下。」[[這裡就是禁城,這點毫無疑問。

column1: se"Then comes a hero from the northland, led unto despair..."[[You are the only hero that I have found recently; it must be you! And I have no doubt you will despair before I am through with you.
column2: 「屆時北地來一英雄,被引向絕望……」[[近來我找到的英雄就只有你一個;非你莫屬!而且我敢保證,等我收拾完你,你絕對會嘗到絕望的滋味。

column1: Passing through the door unopened, that he will find there.[[The door! Accursed door. Why can you not pass through it?
column2: 「他將在那裡,穿越一道不曾開啟之門。」[[那道門!該死的門。你怎麼就穿不過去?

column1: ("When the moonlight shines between the Dragon's jaws...[[And is caught and held there by the Scorpion's claws."
column2: 「當月光灑落於巨龍之顎間……[[並為天蠍之爪所擒所握。」

column1: The moon! It is not due to rise between the two constellations until tomorrow! How can I be wrong?
column2: 月亮!它要明天才會升到那兩個星座之間!我怎麼可能算錯?

column1: I WILL NOT BE DENIED!
column2: 我絕不容許失敗!

column1: I cannot be wrong. It did not say one thousand and one years and a day. No!
column2: 我不可能錯。預言上沒寫「一千零一年又一天」。沒有!

column1: But how can the moon be where it cannot be? You! You're the hero. Answer me how the moon can be between the Dragon and the Scorpion?
column2: 可月亮怎能出現在它不該在的地方?你!你是那個英雄。回答我,月亮怎能落在巨龍與天蠍之間?

column1: You whip the mirror out of your pocket and carefully direct the reflected moonlight between the constellations.
column2: 你迅速從口袋裡抽出鏡子,小心翼翼地把反射的月光導引到兩個星座之間。

column1: At last! Now how does the rest of the prophecy go?[["And at last the hero 'He Who Waits Behind' shall see,[While Iblis rises by the Dark One and the light shall flee. By the name of Suleiman, so shall this be!"
column2: 總算成了!那預言剩下的怎麼說來著?[[「終有一日,『候於其後者』那位英雄將親眼目睹,[伊布利斯隨黑暗之主而起,光明則四散奔逃。以蘇萊曼之名,此事必成!」

column1: 1)Now you know all you need to know. Go in there and bring me the Statue of Iblis!
column2: 1)現在該知道的你都知道了。進去,把伊布利斯之像給我帶出來!

column1: Bah! What use are you? Be silent.
column2: 呸!你有什麼用?閉嘴。

column1: You find that you have no desire to speak.
column2: 你發覺自己完全提不起說話的念頭。

column1: Passing through the door... what is it about that door? Door with stars, constellations! The Scorpion and the Dragon are on the door! It must be when the moon shines upon the door that you may pass through it.
column2: 穿越那道門……那門到底有什麼玄機?門上有星星,是星座!天蠍和巨龍都刻在門上!想必得等月光灑在門上,才能穿得過去。

column1: Let me see, where is the moon now? The moon is to the southeast. But the door is facing north! The moonlight will not pass over us enough to cast light upon the door.
column2: 讓我想想,月亮現在在哪兒?月亮在東南方。可那道門朝北!月光照不過來,落不到門上。

column1: Why is this happening to me? After all these years of planning, how can so many things go wrong?
column2: 為什麼偏偏是我遇上這種事?籌劃了這麼多年,怎能出這麼多差錯?

column1: Well, Hero from the northland, are you so powerful a magician that you can move the moon from its orbit and force it to shine on the door? Or are you so strong that you can bend light? What can you say or do that will let you pass the door?
column2: 好啊,北地來的英雄,難道你法力高強到能把月亮拖出軌道、逼它照在門上?還是你力大無窮,能把光線都掰彎?你倒是說說、做做看,有什麼法子能讓你穿過那道門?

column1: You find you can speak and move your arms again, but you can't seem to move your legs.
column2: 你發覺自己又能開口、能動手臂了,可雙腿卻怎麼也動不了。

column1: Bah! You have no way of getting through that door. And if your usefulness has ended... So be it!
column2: 呸!你根本沒法穿過那道門。既然你已經沒了用處……那就這樣吧!

column1: By the powers of all Darkness[By the power of my name[Shall your soul be mirrored outward,[and your body be the same!
column2: 「以一切黑暗之力[以吾名之力[令爾之魂如鏡般翻轉於外,[爾之軀亦復如是!」

column1: ar[Oooo... I hate it when this happens.[[[[
column2: ar[嗚噢……我最討厭碰上這種事了。[[[[

column1: Going the way of the Dinosaur?
column2: 要步上索魯斯的後塵嗎?

column1: There is a strong aura of magic emanating from the door ahead of you. Ad Avis seems to be surrounded by magic, and for some reason, so do you.
column2: 前方那道門散發出一股強烈的魔法氣息。阿德‧阿維斯似乎被魔法環繞,而不知為何,你也是。

column1: The door is sealed by magic stronger than any you may ever command.
column2: 這道門被一股遠勝你所能駕馭的魔法封住了。

column1: You find that you are unable to concentrate well enough to cast that spell at the moment.
column2: 你發覺此刻心神難以集中,施不出那道咒語。

column1: This fallen tower once stood guard over the ancient city.
column2: 這座傾頹的高塔,昔日曾守衛著這座古城。

column1: The pale moon provides the only illumination for this eerie tableau.
column2: 慘白的月亮,是這幅詭譎景象唯一的光源。

column1: You can't open the entrance to the Forbidden City.
column2: 你打不開禁城的入口。

column1: The sand and rocks at your feet were once part of the cliffs above you.
column2: 你腳邊的沙石,曾是頭頂那片峭壁的一部分。

column1: Don't bother me with silly quest... Mirror? Do you have a mirror? USE it, you fool!
column2: 別拿這種蠢問題煩我……鏡子?你有鏡子嗎?「用」它啊,你這蠢貨!

column1: What about it? The door is clearly there, emblazoned with stars. Wait, they are constellations! There is the Scorpion, below is the Dragon. Perhaps you are not totally worthless after all. We need but wait for the moon to shine upon the door.
column2: 那門怎麼了?門明明就在那兒,上頭飾著星星。等等,那是星座!那兒是天蠍,底下是巨龍。看來你也不是全然一無是處嘛。咱們只需等月光灑上那道門便是。

column1: Silence, fool! I am pondering the significance of that at this very instant.
column2: 閉嘴,蠢貨!此刻我正在細想那玄機所在。

column1: Your friend ignores you. He is concentrating intensely on the door.
column2: 你那「朋友」沒理你。他正全神貫注地盯著那道門。

column1: You can say goodbye all you want, but putting your intention into practice proves impossible without the ability to move your legs.
column2: 你愛說多少聲再見都行,可雙腿動彈不得,這念頭終究沒法付諸行動。

column1: As your eyes adjust, you find yourself in a bedroom. Someone is asleep nearby, surrounded by thin curtains. The occupant of this bedchamber must collect weapons, for there are many attached to the walls.
column2: 待眼睛適應後,你發覺自己身在一間臥房。不遠處有人睡著,四周圍著薄薄的帷幔。這間臥房的主人想必愛收藏兵器,牆上掛了好多。

column1: As you enter Khaveen's room again, and as your eyes once again become accustomed to the darkness, you perceive a dramatic difference.
column2: 當你再度踏進哈維因的房間,眼睛再一次適應黑暗後,你察覺到一個天差地別的變化。

column1: Khaveen and his bodyguards are waiting for you with swords drawn and smiles on their faces.
column2: 哈維因和他的護衛們正等著你,刀劍出鞘,臉上掛著笑。

column1: A man who enjoys his work
column2: 一個樂在工作的人

column1: This room's thin carpet does little to muffle the noise made by the loose floor boards beneath it.
column2: 這房間薄薄的地毯,壓根蓋不住底下鬆動地板發出的聲響。

column1: The sleeper appears to be stirring. Perhaps you should not move until after he has gone back to sleep.
column2: 睡著的人似乎動了動。或許你該等他重新睡熟了再行動。

column1: As you and the man look at each other, you realize just whose house this is.
column2: 你和那人四目相對,這才驚覺自己闖進了誰的家。

column1: What was that noise?
column2: 那是什麼聲音?

column1: The guard quickly calls in other guards and you are overwhelmed.
column2: 衛兵迅速喚來其他人,你寡不敵眾,當場被制伏。

column1: What noise, Sir?
column2: 什麼聲音,大人?

column1: Never mind, go back to your post.
column2: 算了,回你崗位上去。

column1: Khaveen takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again. The last thing you see is the smile on Khaveen's face.
column2: 哈維因抄起一把劍,親自確保你這輩子再也偷不了東西。你眼前最後映入的,是哈維因臉上的笑。

column1: Sneaking up on a Raseirian guard is never a good idea. Especially when he has been hand-picked by Khaveen.
column2: 想偷襲拉希爾的衛兵,從來就不是好主意。尤其這名衛兵還是哈維因親手挑出來的。

column1: Fatal Distractions
column2: 致命的分心

column1: Your attack awakens Khaveen, who alerts the guards in the other room. You are quickly overwhelmed. It would be best if you don't announce your presence when attempting to be a burglar.
column2: 你的攻擊驚醒了哈維因,他立刻喚來隔壁房的衛兵。你轉眼便被制伏。想當竊賊,最好別大聲昭告自己來了。

column1: Aggression was the death of you
column2: 逞兇害了你的命

column1: Khaveen cries out in pain, and the guard summons other guards who quickly subdue you. Khaveen makes certain that you will never bother him again.
column2: 哈維因痛呼出聲,衛兵喚來同伴,三兩下便把你制伏。哈維因確保你再也沒法來騷擾他。

column1: Khaveen calls for his guards, and you are quickly overwhelmed. Khaveen then takes a sword and personally makes certain you'll never steal again.
column2: 哈維因高聲喚來衛兵,你轉眼寡不敵眾。哈維因接著抄起一把劍,親自確保你這輩子再也偷不了東西。

column1: The guard was not asleep, and he didn't get his job by being slow to anger. Your movements triggered his highly-honed killer instinct. At least your end was a quick one.
column2: 那衛兵並沒睡著,他能坐這位子,可不是靠脾氣好混來的。你的動作觸動了他磨得鋒利的殺手本能。至少你死得乾脆。

column1: The Direct Approach
column2: 直來直往

column1: Khaveen is not kind to those who break into his house and use magic against him, but you do not live to tell about it.
column2: 對闖進他家、還對他施法的人,哈維因從不手軟,而你也沒能活著把這事說出口。

column1: The End
column2: 劇終

column1: Your spell awakens Khaveen, who alerts the guards in the other room. You are quickly overwhelmed. It would be best if you don't announce your presence when attempting to be a burglar.
column2: 你的咒語驚醒了哈維因,他立刻喚來隔壁房的衛兵。你轉眼便被制伏。想當竊賊,最好別大聲昭告自己來了。

column1: Spelling it out...
column2: 把話「咒」明白……

column1: The cabinet door's hinges make a loud noise.
column2: 櫥櫃門的鉸鏈發出好大的聲響。

column1: The guards from the other room calmly arrest you and place you in prison for a short but unpleasant time.
column2: 隔壁房的衛兵不慌不忙地逮住你,把你關進牢裡,雖不算久,卻也不好受。

column1: Your Calm spell causes Khaveen to fall back to sleep.
column2: 你的「鎮定」咒讓哈維因重新睡了過去。

column1: The guard, hidden by the shadows, appears alert despite the late hour.
column2: 那衛兵藏身於陰影中,雖已夜深,卻依舊警覺。

column1: It would be wise not to announce your presence to the guard.
column2: 最好別讓那衛兵發覺你來了。

column1: The man asleep within the bed looks vaguely familiar.
column2: 床上睡著的那人,看著有幾分眼熟。

column1: The statue of a bird is intricately carved from some sort of dark crystal.
column2: 這尊鳥形雕像,用某種深色水晶精雕細琢而成。

column1: It would be wise not to announce your presence to the occupant.
column2: 最好別讓房裡的人發覺你來了。

column1: There is nothing special about the cabinet door.
column2: 這櫥櫃門沒什麼特別的。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the antique cabinet.
column2: 別費勁去跟這只古董櫥櫃搭話了。

column1: The cabinet is old, but well built.
column2: 這櫥櫃雖老,做工卻紮實。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the bird statue.
column2: 別費勁去跟那鳥形雕像搭話了。

column1: The door to the cabinet is not open.
column2: 櫥櫃的門沒開。

column1: As you hold the bird in your hand, for a moment you wonder, "Is this the stuff dreams are made of?" You decide "nah" and put it away.
column2: 把那鳥握在手裡的一瞬,你不禁尋思:「這就是夢想的材料嗎?」你想了想,「算了吧」,便收了起來。

column1: The cabinet door is locked.
column2: 櫥櫃門上了鎖。

column1: The lock yields to your skill.
column2: 那鎖敵不過你的身手,應聲而開。

column1: The hinges have been oiled.
column2: 鉸鏈已經上過油了。

column1: You just stole it. You have no wish to return the falcon to the cabinet.
column2: 你才剛把它偷到手。你可沒打算把那隻獵鷹放回櫥櫃裡。

column1: You brace yourself to break the cabinet door open.
column2: 你穩住身子,準備硬把櫥櫃門撞開。

column1: The noise of the breaking glass did not go unnoticed. Khaveen called his guards who quickly overcame you. Your end was not a happy one.
column2: 玻璃碎裂的聲響並沒能瞞過誰。哈維因喚來衛兵,三兩下便把你制伏。你的下場可不怎麼好看。

column1: The Unsubtle Approach.
column2: 粗暴蠻幹法。

column1: The window you came through looks out onto the plaza.
column2: 你爬進來的那扇窗,正對著廣場。

column1: There's nobody on the plaza or in this room you want to announce your presence to.
column2: 廣場上也好、這房裡也罷,沒有哪個是你想讓他知道你來了的。

column1: You don't need to hide at the moment.
column2: 此刻你用不著躲藏。

column1: You're already hiding.
column2: 你已經躲起來了。

column1: The divan looks very stylish and uncomfortable.
column2: 這張長榻看著很時髦,坐起來卻很不舒服。

column1: There's nothing you can do with the divan.
column2: 這張長榻你拿它沒辦法。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the divan.
column2: 別費勁去跟那張長榻搭話了。

column1: The floor is made of loosely joined hardwood boards.
column2: 地板是用一塊塊接得鬆鬆垮垮的硬木板鋪成的。

column1: Someone is asleep nearby, surrounded by thin curtains. The occupant of this bedchamber must collect weapons, for there are many attached to the walls.
column2: 不遠處有人睡著,四周圍著薄薄的帷幔。這間臥房的主人想必愛收藏兵器,牆上掛了好多。

column1: Well-worn weapons line the walls, suggesting that their owner enjoys their use.
column2: 牆上排著一件件用得發舊的兵器,看得出主人挺愛使它們。

column1: They're too clunky to take with you.
column2: 它們太笨重了,帶不走。

column1: Keep your voice down or one of these may end up embedded in your torso.
column2: 把聲音壓低,不然這裡頭某一件,可能會插進你的身子。

column1: The bed is covered with pillows of various colors.
column2: 床上鋪滿了五顏六色的枕頭。

column1: There's a thin rug in front of the bed.
column2: 床前鋪著一張薄薄的小地毯。

column1: This is the last place you want to lie down.
column2: 這是你最不該躺下的地方。

column1: Keep your voice down or you may end up taking a permanent nap.
column2: 把聲音壓低,不然你恐怕會睡上一覺、再也醒不來。

column1: It's not valuable enough to take with you.
column2: 它不夠值錢,不值得帶走。

column1: You doubt that this is a magical rug.
column2: 你很懷疑這是塊魔毯。

column1: The bed curtains make it difficult to see the sleeping figure.
column2: 床帳擋著,讓你很難看清楚床上熟睡的身影。

column1: The curtains make it harder for you to see the man in the bed, but they also make it harder for him to notice you if he should awaken. Leave them in place.
column2: 床帳一方面讓你看不清床上那人,但反過來想,萬一他醒了,也比較不會發現你。就讓它留著吧。

column1: The thin curtains wave slightly as you blow onto them.
column2: 你朝那薄薄的床帳吹了口氣,它輕輕飄動了一下。

column1: Keep your voice down if you value your life.
column2: 要是你還想活命,就把聲音壓低點。

column1: Leave your surroundings alone and focus on getting out of here alive.
column2: 別管周遭那些東西了,專心想辦法活著離開這裡。

column1: As you move toward Khaveen, a board squeaks and Khaveen turns to look at you. Your weapon can't stop him from starting to shout.
column2: 你正朝哈維因靠近,一塊地板突然吱呀作響,哈維因轉過頭來看你。你的武器再快,也來不及阻止他放聲大喊。

column1: You are quickly overwhelmed and dragged off to the dungeon, but at least you have some satisfaction knowing Khaveen will no longer trouble Raseir.
column2: 你很快就被壓制住,被拖進了地牢。不過至少有一點讓你聊以自慰——哈維因再也不會禍害拉希爾了。

column1: Killing can be fatal.
column2: 殺人,可是會出人命的。

column1: The occupant of the house doesn't know you're here. Try to keep it that way.
column2: 這屋子的主人還不知道你在這兒。最好讓他一直蒙在鼓裡。

column1: You would be better off quietly climbing back out the window.
column2: 你最好還是安安靜靜地從窗戶爬回去。

column1: You have 10 seconds to prepare for battle.
column2: 你有十秒鐘準備迎戰。

column1: The large sword is doing you no good on the far side of the room.
column2: 那把大劍擱在房間另一頭,對你一點用都沒有。

column1: The room is in total darkness as you enter. You can't see your hand in front of your eyes.
column2: 你一踏進房間,裡頭漆黑一片,簡直伸手不見五指。

column1: You do, however, hear some movement around you, although it's hard to locate the source of the sound.
column2: 不過你確實聽見周圍有動靜,只是很難判斷聲音是從哪兒傳來的。

column1: Stand where you are.
column2: 站在原地別動。

column1: Same Voice
column2: 同一個聲音

column1: You now face the most important decision of your life...
column2: 此刻,你面臨這輩子最重要的抉擇……

column1: Are you a man, or just a craven coward?
column2: 你到底是條漢子,還是個貪生怕死的懦夫?

column1: Still the Same Voice
column2: 還是那個聲音

column1: Step forward and obey, or now run away!
column2: 上前聽命,還是現在就逃跑!

column1: Use the dodge keys in order to avoid getting hit. Use the escape key to chicken out.
column2: 用閃避鍵躲開攻擊。要是想當縮頭烏龜,就按 Esc 鍵。

column1: While struggling to escape, you become aware that the hilt of the masked swordsman's scimitar is heading for your jaw at an unhealthy velocity...
column2: 你正掙扎著想脫身,卻發現那蒙面劍客的彎刀刀柄,正以一種對健康極為不利的速度往你下巴招呼過來……

column1: You suddenly notice the hilt of the masked swordsman's scimitar approaching your jaw at unhealthy velocity, but you're unable to do anything about it ...
column2: 你突然察覺那蒙面劍客的彎刀刀柄正以驚人的速度逼近你的下巴,卻怎麼也來不及反應……

column1: Some hero YOU are!
column2: 你還真是個「英雄」啊!

column1: You are grabbed and roughly tossed through the door back into the alley.
column2: 你被人一把抓住,粗魯地從門口扔了出去,摔回巷子裡。

column1: You dash across the room, grab the only available weapon in sight, and turn to face your adversary.
column2: 你衝過房間,一把抄起眼前唯一能用的武器,轉身面對你的對手。

column1: You speed to the door, throw it open, and head for the alley. You trip over the door's threshold and land sprawling on your face.
column2: 你飛奔到門邊,一把推開門,朝巷子衝去。結果被門檻一絆,整個人臉朝下摔了個狗吃屎。

column1: As you prepare to cast the spell...
column2: 你正準備施法……

column1: Choir of Voices
column2: 眾聲齊唱

column1: CHEAT! CHEAT!
column2: 作弊!作弊!

column1: That last hit was more than you could take.
column2: 最後那一擊,你終究是扛不住了。

column1: ...in a very unpleasant predicament.
column2: ……陷入了極為不妙的處境。

column1: You're trying! You're trying!
column2: 你正在努力!你正在努力!

column1: Big little guy, isn't he?
column2: 別看他個子小,塊頭可不小,對吧?

column1: You could allow it to do the talking for you, if you could only reach it.
column2: 你大可以讓它替你說話——只要你搆得著它的話。

column1: Good idea. But it's not exactly within reach.
column2: 好主意。可惜它不太搆得著。

column1: The floor is paved with large tiles.
column2: 地板是用大塊的石磚鋪成的。

column1: You can't see a thing.
column2: 你什麼也看不見。

column1: There's nothing you can do with the tiles. Concern yourself with more pressing matters.
column2: 這些石磚你什麼也做不了。還是操心點更要緊的事吧。

column1: The floor feels rather smooth.
column2: 地板摸起來相當光滑。

column1: You might get plenty of opportunity to kiss the floor if you keep dallying for long.
column2: 你要是再這麼磨蹭下去,可有大把機會跟地板來個親密接觸。

column1: You call out, but there's no response.
column2: 你出聲呼喚,卻沒人應答。

column1: It looks like a cross between your average fraternal organization meeting hall and a dungeon. The shackles give it a special flavor.
column2: 這地方看起來像是兄弟會集會廳跟地牢的綜合體。牆上那些鐐銬更替它添了幾分「特殊風味」。

column1: The walls probably feel ordinary enough.
column2: 這幾面牆摸起來大概也就那樣,沒什麼特別。

column1: You don't feel anything in front of you.
column2: 你伸手往前摸,什麼也沒摸著。

column1: Crying out for help isn't gonna be of any use.
column2: 在這兒大喊救命,一點用也沒有。

column1: It's either another exit, or the entrance to another room. It's too dark in there to determine it from here though.
column2: 那不是另一個出口,就是通往另一個房間的入口。不過裡頭太黑了,從這兒實在看不出究竟。

column1: Doorways aren't operated. You just walk through them.
column2: 門口沒什麼好操作的,你直接走過去就是了。

column1: After being dragged to the dungeon of the palace, you were roughly searched, beaten, and questioned by the guards. They warned you that Khaveen will be coming soon to personally question you...
column2: 你被拖進王宮地牢後,衛兵粗暴地搜身、毒打,還逼問了你一頓。他們警告你,哈維因很快就會親自來審問你……

column1: ...and that you won't enjoy it.
column2: ……而那場面,你肯定不會好受。

column1: The Katta is thin and looks as if he has been recently beaten.
column2: 這名卡塔族人骨瘦如柴,看樣子最近才剛挨過打。

column1: I would tell you all you wish to know, but now it is time to depart... in great haste!
column2: 閣下想知道的,小的都願意告訴您,只是眼下該動身了……而且得快!

column1: Truly, you have many questions I might answer, but the guards will soon return. We must leave this most dangerous place.
column2: 閣下確實有許多問題,小的都能解答,只是衛兵很快就要回來了。我們得離開這個最最危險的地方。

column1: I will answer all later. But now that you have so skillfully opened the door, we should leave.
column2: 日後小的定當一一稟告。不過您方才這麼俐落地把門打開了,咱們還是趕緊走吧。

column1: It is straw.
column2: 這是稻草。

column1: You don't need any straw.
column2: 你用不著稻草。

column1: Your equipment has been carefully searched and neatly laid out on a table, awaiting Khaveen's inspection.
column2: 你的裝備被仔細搜查過,整整齊齊擺在一張桌上,就等哈維因來驗看。

column1: You recover all of your equipment.
column2: 你取回了所有的裝備。

column1: That is difficult to accomplish from inside the cell.
column2: 待在牢房裡,這事可不好辦。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to your equipment.
column2: 別費勁跟你的裝備講話了。

column1: The heavy wooden door leads to the hallway. You can hear the sound of many boots marching and several guards talking.
column2: 這扇厚重的木門通往走廊。你聽得見許多靴子踏步行進的聲音,還有幾名衛兵在交談。

column1: You can't open the heavy wooden door from inside the cell.
column2: 在牢房裡頭,你打不開這扇厚重的木門。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the wooden door.
column2: 別費勁跟木門講話了。

column1: The bars on the window are heavy iron and are set in stone.
column2: 窗上的欄杆是厚重的鐵條,牢牢嵌在石頭裡。

column1: You can't open the barred window.
column2: 你打不開這扇裝了鐵欄的窗戶。

column1: Don't bother talking to the barred window.
column2: 別費勁跟鐵欄窗講話了。

column1: The ventilation ducts are heavily reinforced.
column2: 那些通風管道加固得十分牢靠。

column1: You can't open the ventilation ducts.
column2: 你打不開那些通風管道。

column1: Don't bother talking to the ventilation ducts.
column2: 別費勁跟通風管道講話了。

column1: You may be able to turn the tables on this situation... just not in that way.
column2: 眼下這局面你或許還有機會扭轉……只是,不是用那種法子。

column1: Don't bother talking to the wooden table.
column2: 別費勁跟木桌講話了。

column1: You follow the guards through the dark streets, past the gates of the palace, and into the dungeon of Raseir.
column2: 你跟著衛兵穿過漆黑的街道,經過王宮的大門,被押進了拉希爾的地牢。

column1: Why? You're trying to escape, remember?
column2: 幹嘛?你可是想越獄的,別忘了。

column1: Don't bother talking to the gate.
column2: 別費勁跟大門講話了。

column1: The bars are made of heavy iron and it would take far too long to bend or break them, even with your great strength.
column2: 這些欄杆是厚重的鐵條打的,就算憑你那身蠻力,要把它們扳彎或弄斷,也得耗上老半天。

column1: The heavy iron bars don't even budge.
column2: 那些厚重的鐵欄杆紋風不動。

column1: Don't bother talking to the iron bars.
column2: 別費勁跟鐵欄杆講話了。

column1: Don't bother talking to the stone wall.
column2: 別費勁跟石牆講話了。

column1: You've already shown him your pin.
column2: 你已經把胸針給他看過了。

column1: A pin like that can only be seen by a friend, such is its magic. You must be very trusted by my people to gain such a thing.
column2: 像這樣的胸針,唯有朋友才看得見,這正是它的魔力所在。您能得到這東西,想必是深受我族信任之人。

column1: Well, Katta-friend, let us escape this very dangerous place.
column2: 那麼,卡塔之友,咱們快逃離這個極其危險的地方吧。

column1: Thank you, Effendi. It has been weeks since I have eaten food that was not spoiled. I shall not forget this.
column2: 謝謝您,閣下。小的已經好幾個禮拜沒吃過沒餿掉的東西了。這份恩情,小的不會忘記。

column1: Thank you greatly, Effendi. Maybe my judgement has been affected by hunger, but I do not think I have ever tasted anything better in my entire life.
column2: 萬分感激,閣下。或許是餓昏頭了,影響了小的判斷,可這輩子,小的還真沒嚐過比這更美味的東西。

column1: Thank you, Effendi. They spend very little water on prisoners these days. I shall not forget this act of kindness.
column2: 謝謝您,閣下。這年頭他們捨不得給囚犯多少水喝。您這份善意,小的銘記在心。

column1: You have no reason to show that to the Katta.
column2: 你沒理由把那東西拿給這位卡塔族人看。

column1: The Katta watches you calmly with a mixture of interest and suspicion.
column2: 這名卡塔族人神色平靜地打量著你,眼神裡既有幾分好奇,又帶著幾分戒備。

column1: I have heard from others in the Underground that there is a concealed passage in the room outside this cell.
column2: 小的聽地下組織的其他人說過,這間牢房外頭的房間裡,藏著一條暗道。

column1: The entrance to the passageway should be here...
column2: 暗道的入口應該就在這兒……

column1: Yes, it is here. Lend your strong shoulder to the task.
column2: 沒錯,就在這兒。借您那壯實的肩膀使把勁吧。

column1: Thanks to your great strength, we can leave immediately.
column2: 多虧您這身蠻力,咱們這就能走了。

column1: I have helped others escape through the secret passageways.
column2: 小的曾幫過其他人從這些暗道逃出去。

column1: Do not fear. I will lead you to safety through the passageways, Katta-friend.
column2: 別怕。小的會領您穿過暗道,平安脫身,卡塔之友。

column1: Perhaps, before you leave, you should pick up your equipment over there near the cell.
column2: 或許,在動身之前,您該先把擱在牢房那邊的裝備拿上。

column1: But do not dally too long, or the guards will surely come.
column2: 不過可別磨蹭太久,否則衛兵準會趕來。

column1: You hear the sound of guards talking just outside the door.
column2: 你聽見就在門外,有衛兵在交談。

column1: As you struggle with the astonishingly tough Katta, the guards enter and separate you both, taking you to a cell from which you cannot escape.
column2: 你跟這名出奇硬朗的卡塔族人扭打成一團,衛兵衝了進來把你倆架開,把你押進了一間插翅難逃的牢房。

column1: Next time, try Dale Carnegie
column2: 下次,還是去翻翻卡內基的《人際關係》吧

column1: The Katta cries out in pain, causing the guards to rush in. They take you to a cell from which you cannot escape.
column2: 卡塔族人痛得叫出聲來,引得衛兵衝了進來。他們把你押進一間插翅難逃的牢房。

column1: What were you thinking?
column2: 你到底在想什麼啊?

column1: Sports are Healthy?
column2: 運動有益健康?

column1: You and Sharaf wander the passages beneath the palace. Without your equipment, you both perish in the darkness.
column2: 你和沙拉夫在王宮底下的通道裡四處摸索。少了裝備,你倆雙雙葬身在這片黑暗之中。

column1: Forget Something?
column2: 忘了什麼吧?

column1: You wander the passages beneath the Palace. Without your equipment, you perish in the darkness.
column2: 你在王宮底下的通道裡四處摸索。少了裝備,你葬身在這片黑暗之中。

column1: The guards come in and take you to a cell you can't escape from.
column2: 衛兵走了進來,把你押進一間插翅難逃的牢房。

column1: In Guarded Condition
column2: 看守森嚴

column1: The straw below the Katta catches on fire and fills the cell with smoke. You die of asphyxiation. Worse, you lose the game.
column2: 卡塔族人身下的稻草燒了起來,整間牢房煙霧瀰漫。你窒息而死。更慘的是,你輸掉了這場遊戲。

column1: The straw catches on fire and the smoke quickly draws the attention of the guards patrolling outside the window. Maybe you should have opted for a more subtle escape.
column2: 稻草燒了起來,濃煙很快引來窗外巡邏衛兵的注意。或許,你該選個更不動聲色的逃法才對。

column1: The Final Straw
column2: 壓垮駱駝的最後一根稻草

column1: The guards come in and drag you away for your interrogation with Khaveen. It is less than pleasant.
column2: 衛兵走進來,把你拖去接受哈維因的審問。那滋味,可不怎麼好受。

column1: Looks as though you could have used more body-building practice. You'll just have to try again.
column2: 看來你平日的健身練得還不夠。只能再試一次了。

column1: You don't seem to be strong enough to force the door. You'll have to try something else.
column2: 你的力氣似乎還不足以把門硬撞開。得換個別的法子才行。

column1: Looks like you should have eaten your Wheeties this morning. You did feel the door give a little when you hit it this time.
column2: 看來你今早該多吃幾碗飯補補力氣。不過這回你撞上去,倒是真感覺到門鬆動了一點點。

column1: The cell door is already unlocked.
column2: 牢門早就沒鎖了。

column1: You don't know how to pick locks.
column2: 你不會撬鎖。

column1: You don't have any lock-picking equipment.
column2: 你沒有任何撬鎖的工具。

column1: You find that the guards took all your tools when they searched you. Your equipment is beyond reach. You'll need to find something else.
column2: 你發現衛兵搜身時把你所有的工具都拿走了。你的裝備根本搆不著。你得另想辦法。

column1: There's no reason to. The door isn't locked to begin with.
column2: 沒這個必要。這門打從一開始就沒鎖。

column1: That's a very good idea. But how?
column2: 這主意挺不錯。可是,要怎麼弄呢?

column1: The cell's door is already closed.
column2: 牢門早就關上了。

column1: Through the dim light filtering past the keyhole, you can make out the barracks. A guard stands directly on the other side of the door.
column2: 藉著從鑰匙孔透進來的微光,你勉強看清外頭的營房。一名衛兵就站在門的正對面。

column1: The cell door is already open.
column2: 牢門早就開著了。

column1: The rock is much too heavy for your spell to effect it.
column2: 這塊石頭太重了,你的法術奈何不了它。

column1: The secret passageway is already open.
column2: 那條暗道早就開著了。

column1: The barred window doesn't open.
column2: 那扇裝了鐵欄的窗戶打不開。

column1: The ventilation ducts do not open.
column2: 那些通風管道打不開。

column1: Your spell has attracted the attention of the guards.
column2: 你的法術引來了衛兵的注意。

column1: Your dagger has attracted the attention of the guards.
column2: 你的匕首引來了衛兵的注意。

column1: Your rock has attracted the attention of the guards.
column2: 你那塊石頭引來了衛兵的注意。

column1: Levitating here will only leave you with a bump on your head.
column2: 在這兒施展飄浮術,只會讓你撞得頭上腫一個大包。

column1: That has no effect on the cell door.
column2: 那對牢門起不了任何作用。

column1: Remembering your Advanced Adventuring lessons, you set to work on the cell door lock, using the pointed end of your sapphire pin as a lock pick.
column2: 你想起進階冒險課上學的本事,拿藍寶石胸針的尖端當撬鎖工具,開始對付牢門上的鎖。

column1: Remembering your Advanced Adventuring lessons, you set to work on the cell door lock, using the nail you found as a lock pick.
column2: 你想起進階冒險課上學的本事,拿撿來的那根釘子當撬鎖工具,開始對付牢門上的鎖。

column1: Which stone are you trying to push? There are many here, but none of them appear to be moveable.
column2: 你想推哪塊石頭?這兒石頭多得是,可沒一塊看起來推得動。

column1: You find a secret panel on the back wall.
column2: 你在後牆上發現了一塊暗板。

column1: The stone slides back to reveal a hidden passageway.
column2: 石塊向後滑開,露出一條隱密的通道。

column1: Before you escape, perhaps you should get your equipment.
column2: 在逃走之前,或許你該先把裝備拿上。

column1: The Katta leads you through dark passages under the Palace to a secret exit into the Raseir streets.
column2: 卡塔族人領著你穿過王宮底下的黑暗通道,來到一處通往拉希爾街頭的隱密出口。

column1: You find yourself crawling through a twisting passage for what seems like forever. When it comes to a dead-end, you force open another panel and find yourself in the streets of Raseir.
column2: 你在一條曲折的通道裡爬行,彷彿沒個盡頭。爬到死路時,你硬推開另一塊暗板,赫然發現自己已經身在拉希爾的街頭。

column1: You see some graffiti scratched into the wall. It says "M. Scofield was here."
column2: 你看見牆上刻著幾行塗鴉,上頭寫著「M. 史考菲爾到此一遊」。

column1: The stone wall is just that.
column2: 這道石牆,也就那樣,沒什麼名堂。

column1: The walls in the cell are solid, mortared stone.
column2: 牢房的牆是用灰漿砌得結結實實的石頭。

column1: You have discovered a secret passageway leading underneath the Palace.
column2: 你發現了一條通往王宮底下的暗道。

column1: You look around and see a large stone, whose mortar has been loosened.
column2: 你環顧四周,看見一塊大石頭,它周圍的灰漿已經鬆動了。

column1: Nobody can stare down a Katta. After several seconds, you become bleary-eyed and turn away.
column2: 沒人瞪得贏卡塔族人。才幾秒鐘,你就看得兩眼發花,只好把頭撇開。

column1: I would love to hear more, Hero, but I think we should first escape this cell.
column2: 小的很樂意多聽您說些,英雄,只是小的以為,咱們還是先逃出這間牢房要緊。

column1: Shapeir is a fine city, but it is not the home that Raseir was to the Katta.
column2: 夏皮爾是座好城市,但對卡塔族來說,它終究不是拉希爾那樣的家園。

column1: The grandmothers of our grandmothers' grandmothers were born in this place. Katta have lived here since before there was even a city. No one can keep the Katta from their home. The Katta will come back.
column2: 我們祖母的祖母的祖母,就是在這片土地上出生的。早在這裡還沒有城的時候,卡塔族就住在這兒了。沒有誰能把卡塔族擋在家門外。卡塔族一定會回來。

column1: The magic of the Katta is subtle -- an oil lamp that never burns out, a door that always opens to your touch, a pin that can only be seen by friends.
column2: 卡塔族的魔法細膩低調——一盞永不熄滅的油燈、一扇一碰就開的門、一枚只有朋友才看得見的別針。

column1: The Sultan of Shapeir is very powerful, but too far away to aid Raseir.
column2: 夏皮爾的蘇丹勢力強大,可惜離得太遠,救不了拉希爾。

column1: The new Emir is but a fool, and the Underground will soon depose him.
column2: 新任埃米爾不過是個蠢蛋,地下組織很快就會把他拉下台。

column1: The resistance consists of many of the guards and townsfolk who rebel against the tyranny of the new Emir.
column2: 反抗軍裡有不少是不堪新埃米爾暴政、起來造反的衛兵和鎮民。

column1: There are many passages under the Palace. Many times I have traveled through them to help release prisoners.
column2: 王宮底下有許多密道。我多次穿行其中,幫忙救出囚犯。

column1: The Blue Parrot Inn is a meeting place for dogs who lick Khaveen's boots and then growl at him behind his back.
column2: 藍鸚鵡客棧是一群走狗碰頭的地方——當面舔哈維因的靴子,背地裡又對他齜牙咧嘴。

column1: The fountain of Raseir was once as beautiful and ever-flowing as Shapeir's. However, since the darkness came, it has ceased to flow.
column2: 拉希爾的噴泉曾經和夏皮爾的一樣美麗、終年不竭。可是自從黑暗降臨,它就再也不流了。

column1: It is rumored that Khaveen is but a tool for someone more cruel and powerful. Someone who uses magic to rule Raseir.
column2: 傳言哈維因不過是某個更殘忍、更強大之人的傀儡——那人用魔法統治著拉希爾。

column1: We are little folk, yes. Still, we can bring down the state.
column2: 沒錯,我們是小人物。可就算是小人物,照樣能掀翻一個政權。

column1: My name, Sharaf, means 'honor.'
column2: 我叫沙拉夫,這名字的意思是「榮譽」。

column1: There is no mistaking the art of my people. That is a very valuable gift you have. The guards did not take it from you because they could not see it while you are wearing it. Only a friend or one who would be your friend can see such a pin.
column2: 我族的手藝絕對假不了。你手上那件可是無價之寶。衛兵沒從你身上拿走它,是因為只要你戴著,他們就看不見。這種別針,只有朋友、或是願意與你為友的人才看得見。

column1: There is a concealed passageway somewhere outside this cell. I have helped others escape by passageways before, but this is the first I have been trapped in a cell. I am not easily captured.
column2: 這牢房外某處藏著一條密道。我以前靠密道幫不少人逃過,可這還是頭一回換我被關進牢裡。要抓住我可沒那麼容易。

column1: He is very brave and honorable. He swore as the Katta were driven from Raseir that he would find a Hero to restore our homeland. It seems he must have found one.
column2: 他既勇敢又重榮譽。卡塔族被趕出拉希爾時,他發誓要找到一位英雄,重建我們的家園。看來他真的找到了。

column1: Shema is my cousin. She is very bright and knows how to run an inn. She is also a good judge of character and would know a true hero when she saw you.
column2: 舍瑪是我的表親。她非常聰明,懂得怎麼經營客棧,看人也很準,一眼就能認出誰是真英雄——比如你。

column1: She is my cousin. When you spoke of her, I thought Khaveen was playing a trick.
column2: 她是我的表親。你提起她的時候,我還以為是哈維因在耍什麼花招。

column1: Khaveen is the most feared man in all Raseir, although rumor has it he is not the most powerful.
column2: 哈維因是全拉希爾最令人聞風喪膽的人,不過傳言說,他並不是權勢最大的那一個。

column1: The guards are just outside the door over there, and will be returning soon.
column2: 衛兵就在那扇門外,馬上就要回來了。

column1: Katta understand honor in ways most humans do not appreciate. Honor cannot be given or won by contest. It comes to you only through diligently placing your own needs below concern for others.
column2: 卡塔族對榮譽的體會,是多數人類所無法領會的。榮譽既不能憑空給予,也不能靠比試贏來。唯有時時把自己的需求擺在他人之後,榮譽才會降臨在你身上。

column1: I was captured when a former member of the Underground betrayed us, and the guards found where I stayed.
column2: 地下組織裡一個叛徒出賣了我們,衛兵查出我藏身的地方,我就這麼被抓了。

column1: Of such things, I do not know what to say.
column2: 這種事,我實在不知該說什麼。

column1: I am afraid I can add but little to your knowledge.
column2: 恐怕這方面我幫不上你什麼忙。

column1: Let's hope that you fare better than this cell's previous occupant.
column2: 但願你的下場能比這牢房上一位住客好一些。

column1: Alas, poor Yorick...
column2: 唉,可憐的尤利克……

column1: You'll need to use your own head if you want to escape from here alive.
column2: 想活著逃出這裡,你得好好動動自己的腦袋。

column1: You search the cell and find a rusty nail in a crack between the stones of the floor.
column2: 你搜遍牢房,在地板石縫間找到一根生鏽的釘子。

column1: Despite the heat outside, the floors and walls in here are stone cold.
column2: 外頭熱得要命,這裡的地板和牆壁卻冰冷如石。

column1: As you start, you see fear in the slave woman's eyes.
column2: 你才一動手,就看見那名女奴眼裡滿是恐懼。

column1: Guards! Guards! Come quickly!
column2: 衛兵!衛兵!快來啊!

column1: Guards! Guards! Come quickly! My mistress is in danger.
column2: 衛兵!衛兵!快來啊!我的女主人有危險!

column1: Please, Hero, we must hurry. The guards will be searching for me soon. I need your clothes.
column2: 拜託,英雄,我們得快點。衛兵很快就會來搜我。我需要你的衣服。

column1: The guards will be here any minute.
column2: 衛兵隨時都會到。

column1: What goes on here?
column2: 這裡出什麼事了?

column1: Guards! To me!
column2: 衛兵!到我這兒來!

column1: Suddenly the guards break in and drag you off to the Harem. You marry Khaveen, and both live happily ever after.
column2: 衛兵突然破門而入,把你拖進後宮。你嫁給了哈維因,從此兩人過著幸福快樂的日子。

column1: Sheep in Sheik's clothing
column2: 披著酋長外袍的綿羊

column1: Suddenly, the guards walk in and grab all of you, just as Zayishah had warned. Zayishah is returned to the harem. You are taken to the dungeon where Khaveen is waiting for you.
column2: 衛兵突然走進來,把你們全抓了起來——正如札伊夏所警告的。札伊夏被送回後宮,你則被帶到地牢,哈維因正在那裡等著你。

column1: Unwanted Visitors
column2: 不速之客

column1: She Sears you with her gaze, then beats you to a pulp with a Macy and takes your money. You are unable to Ward off the blows and are soon J.C. Penniless.
column2: 她用眼神把你燒成焦炭,再拿大鎚把你揍成肉泥,搶走你的錢。你連招架之力都沒有,轉眼就被打得一文不名。

column1: Giving Credit where Credit's Due
column2: 功過分明,該記的賬一筆不漏

column1: You are met outside by several guards, who cheerfully drag you away to the dungeons of Raseir.[[[
column2: 你在外頭撞見好幾名衛兵,他們樂呵呵地把你拖進拉希爾的地牢。[[[

column1: You are dragged away to the dungeons of Raseir.[[[[[
column2: 你被拖進了拉希爾的地牢。[[[[[

column1: Not a Vacation
column2: 這可不是來度假的

column1: Guards quickly burst into the room. They drag you off to prison and Khaveen personally oversees your stay. It is not long.[[
column2: 衛兵迅速衝進房間,把你拖去坐牢,還由哈維因親自監看你的牢獄生活。日子不會太長。[[

column1: Discretion is the Better Part of Safety
column2: 謹慎方為保命之道

column1: How do I look? Do you think this will fool anybody? It's so nice not to have to wear a stupid veil.
column2: 我看起來怎麼樣?你覺得這身能騙過人嗎?不用再戴那條蠢面紗,感覺真好。

column1: Please, in order to leave the city I need one more thing, although it will be dangerous for you. I must have your visa.
column2: 拜託,我要離開這座城還缺一樣東西,雖然這會讓你陷入危險。我非得拿到你的通行證不可。

column1: The guards will be coming soon in search of Zayishah. You must not be seen talking with me, Effendi. It is forbidden.
column2: 衛兵很快就會來找札伊夏。閣下,絕不能讓人看見你跟我說話,這是違禁的。

column1: You are dragged away to the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: 你被拖進了拉希爾的地牢。

column1: Is it you or is it Memorex?
column2: 這到底是真人,還是放錄音帶?

column1: Whoo! You haven't seen a figure like that since you visited your accountant!
column2: 哇喔!自從上次去找你的會計師算帳,你就再也沒見過這麼好看的「數字」了!

column1: Yup, she's a girl all right.
column2: 嗯,沒錯,她確實是個姑娘。

column1: The clothes first, please.
column2: 麻煩先把衣服給我。

column1: Thank you ever so much. In return for your favors, I can only give you a token.
column2: 太感謝你了。為了報答你的恩情,我能給的只有這一點小小心意。

column1: Here is my mirror. You have but to show it to any woman in the Palace and she will aid you.
column2: 這是我的鏡子。你只要拿給王宮裡任何一位女子看,她就會幫助你。

column1: Again, my thanks and may fortune favor you. Farewell.
column2: 再次謝謝你,願好運與你同在。再會了。

column1: Wait until she clears the alleyway before you leave, Effendi.
column2: 閣下,等她走出巷子,你再離開。

column1: My thanks for your kind thoughts, but now is not an ideal time for such things.
column2: 謝謝你的好意,可眼下實在不是談這些的時候。

column1: I have all the clothes I need, thank you.
column2: 我需要的衣服都齊了,謝謝你。

column1: You start to take off your vest, and then you realize that Shema packed you a change of clothes. You hand her your spare outfit.
column2: 你正要脫下背心,忽然想起舍瑪幫你準備了一套換洗衣物。於是你把那套備用衣服遞給了她。

column1: You hand her the spare change of clothes that Shema packed for you.
column2: 你把舍瑪幫你打包的那套備用衣物遞給了她。

column1: Thank you so very much.
column2: 真是太謝謝你了。

column1: She doesn't need it.
column2: 她用不著那個。

column1: Your spare outfit lies on the floor.
column2: 你那套備用衣服攤在地上。

column1: It's Zayishah's discarded harem attire.
column2: 這是札伊夏脫下不要的後宮服飾。

column1: You won't be needing them.
column2: 這些你用不上了。

column1: Don't bother talking to the veil.
column2: 別費事去跟一條面紗講話了。

column1: If you need a place of safety in the Palace sometime, the harem will welcome you for the aid you gave my mistress. I shall tie a veil from the battlements at night to guide you.
column2: 日後你若需要在王宮裡找個安身之處,後宮會看在你幫過我女主人的份上接納你。夜裡我會在城垛上繫一條面紗,替你指路。

column1: Thank you greatly, Effendi Hero.
column2: 萬分感謝,英雄閣下。

column1: I must hurry before the eunuchs discover I am gone. Khaveen will have the city searched. Please, may I have your visa?
column2: 我得趁太監們還沒發現我不見之前快走。哈維因會下令搜城。拜託,能把你的通行證給我嗎?

column1: Mayzun is Zayishah's servant.
column2: 梅珍是札伊夏的侍女。

column1: Zayishah's servant pays no attention to you.
column2: 札伊夏的侍女對你不理不睬。

column1: She looks pretty busy right now.
column2: 她現在看起來忙得很。

column1: He is a brutal man. I do not like the way he looks at me. One day, he took my father and me down to the dungeons to watch the torturing of prisoners. He had the same kind of look as he watched their pain. I will not marry him.
column2: 他是個殘暴的人。我討厭他看我的那種眼神。有一天,他帶著我父親和我下到地牢,看囚犯受刑。他望著那些人痛苦時,眼裡也是同樣的神情。我絕不嫁給他。

column1: This city is a model of how Ad Avis thinks the world should be run. Ad Avis will be the ruler, of course. He has my father under a spell and my father will do anything Ad Avis says.
column2: 這座城就是阿德·阿維斯心目中世界該有的樣板。當然,統治者非他莫屬。他對我父親下了咒,父親如今對他言聽計從。

column1: I have no time to speak of trifles, I must escape quickly.
column2: 我沒空閒聊瑣事,得趕緊逃走。

column1: My father has given in to Khaveen and all his lies. I am to be married in two weeks when Khaveen takes over the city and Ad Avis leaves.
column2: 我父親已向哈維因和他那一套謊言屈服了。再過兩週,等哈維因接管這座城、阿德·阿維斯離開,我就要被嫁出去。

column1: My father is Ali al-Din Hasan. He is the Emir of this city, at least in name, but he has no control over anything, not even in the Palace. He is but a puppet for Khaveen and Ad Avis.
column2: 我父親是阿里·阿丁·哈桑。他名義上是這座城的埃米爾,實則什麼都管不了,連王宮裡的事也作不了主。他不過是哈維因和阿德·阿維斯的傀儡。

column1: In your clothes, I can leave the city. I have special letters of envoy signed by Khaveen. I need only your visa to be free.
column2: 換上你的衣服,我就能離開這座城。我有哈維因簽署的特使文書,只差你的通行證,我就自由了。

column1: servant woman
column2: 侍女

column1: slave woman
column2: 女奴

column1: Mayzun is my servant woman. She is also my friend. I trust her with my life.
column2: 梅珍是我的侍女,也是我的朋友。我把性命都託付給她。

column1: I fear and hate him more than Khaveen. It is he who destroyed my Uncle Arus and made Raseir the terrible place it now is. Ad Avis was my uncle's vizier.
column2: 比起哈維因,我更怕他、更恨他。就是他害死了我的叔父阿魯斯,把拉希爾變成如今這副可怕的模樣。阿德·阿維斯曾是我叔父的宰相。

column1: There are only two passions to Ad Avis: complete power and complete control. He will stop at nothing to gain those.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯只有兩樣渴望:絕對的權力,和絕對的掌控。為了得到這兩樣,他什麼事都做得出來。

column1: I do not know what magic Ad Avis has, but it is clear he is a wizard. You must not get near him unless you are protected against spells.
column2: 我不曉得阿德·阿維斯使的是什麼魔法,但他顯然是個巫師。除非你有防咒的護身手段,否則千萬別靠近他。

column1: My name is Zayishah, daughter of Emir Ali al-Din Hasan.
column2: 我叫札伊夏,是埃米爾阿里·阿丁·哈桑的女兒。

column1: I am the Emir's daughter, Zayishah.
column2: 我是埃米爾的女兒,札伊夏。

column1: The Katta used to fill this city, but they were driven out. I think Khaveen is allergic to them.
column2: 這座城從前到處都是卡塔族,後來卻被趕了出去。我猜哈維因大概對他們過敏。

column1: I do not know the truth of this rumor, but I have heard that Ad Avis is very much afraid of fire.
column2: 這傳言是真是假我不清楚,但我聽說阿德·阿維斯非常怕火。

column1: The fountain of the city dried up just after my Uncle Arus disappeared.
column2: 就在我叔父阿魯斯失蹤之後,這座城的噴泉便乾涸了。

column1: They are the guards for the women in the Harem.
column2: 他們是看守後宮女眷的衛兵。

column1: All the women must stay there now, hiding their faces behind veils. Did you know it is forbidden for a man to speak to a woman unless she is his mother, daughter, or wife? It is all Ad Avis' doing. He must really hate women.
column2: 如今所有女子都得待在那裡,以面紗遮臉。你知道嗎?男人不准和女子說話,除非對方是他的母親、女兒或妻子。這全是阿德·阿維斯搞出來的。他八成是真的恨透了女人。

column1: This was a beautiful place to live a year ago. All of these changes took place because of Ad Avis.
column2: 一年前,這裡還是個適合安居的好地方。這一切的改變,全是因為阿德·阿維斯。

column1: The Sultan is like a father to me. I will be glad to see him again and listen to his poetry.
column2: 蘇丹待我如同父親一般。能再見到他、聽他吟詩,我會很高興。

column1: The Caravan Master is my friend. He taught me how to ride when I was little.
column2: 商隊隊長是我的朋友。我小時候,是他教會我騎乘的。

column1: I know how to survive in the desert; it is my home. Before Ad Avis took over the city, I roamed it freely at will. The caravan is but a day's ride ahead of me. I will catch it easily.
column2: 我懂得在沙漠裡求生,那裡就是我的家。阿德·阿維斯接管這座城之前,我可以隨心所欲地在沙漠中遊走。商隊只比我早一天的路程,我輕輕鬆鬆就能追上。

column1: blue parrot
column2: 藍鸚鵡

column1: blue parrot inn
column2: 藍鸚鵡客棧

column1: I have heard that the inn is but a den of thieves. It certainly pays a fortune in bribes to Khaveen.
column2: 我聽說那間客棧根本是個賊窩。它向哈維因進貢的賄款肯定是一筆天文數字。

column1: I have some money and some jewels, but I may need them to bribe some guards and survive outside. I know my father will reward you greatly, if things ever return to normal around here.
column2: 我手上有些錢和珠寶,不過這些可能得拿去賄賂衛兵、撐過在外的日子。要是這裡哪天恢復正常,我父親一定會重重報答你。

column1: The Palace is filled with guards, and all women must stay in the Harem.
column2: 王宮裡到處是衛兵,所有女子都得待在後宮。

column1: My father would never overthrow his brother, no matter what foul rumors you have heard. My father is just a puppet for Ad Avis.
column2: 不管你聽到什麼難聽的謠言,我父親絕不會推翻自己的兄長。他只是阿德·阿維斯的傀儡罷了。

column1: There are but two monsters in this city. One is Khaveen, the other Ad Avis.
column2: 這座城裡只有兩頭惡魔。一頭是哈維因,另一頭是阿德·阿維斯。

column1: I will travel with the caravan back to Shapeir. I know the Sultan Harun al Rashid. He will let me stay at his Palace.
column2: 我會跟著商隊回到夏皮爾。我認識蘇丹哈倫·拉希德,他會讓我住進他的王宮。

column1: Ad Avis is a master of magic and exults in showing off how powerful he is. He has boasted that soon he will have the power to rule the world. I can only hope he is mistaken.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯是個魔法大師,最愛炫耀自己有多強大。他誇口說,用不了多久他就會擁有統治世界的力量。我只能祈禱他是想錯了。

column1: Please, I have so little time.
column2: 拜託,我的時間真的不多了。

column1: There is nothing I can say about that.
column2: 那件事我無可奉告。

column1: He has a snake's eyes, and hates all women.
column2: 他長著一雙蛇眼,憎恨天下所有女人。

column1: I have been a servant to Zayishah since she was little, and her slave since Ad Avis made slaves. I will do anything for her.
column2: 打從札伊夏還小,我就一直是她的侍女;自阿德·阿維斯立下奴隸制後,我又成了她的奴婢。為了她,我什麼都願意做。

column1: I have been a servant to her since she was little, and her slave since Ad Avis made slaves. I will do anything for her.
column2: 打從她還小,我就一直是她的侍女;自阿德·阿維斯立下奴隸制後,我又成了她的奴婢。為了她,我什麼都願意做。

column1: Khaveen will not marry Zayishah. I will kill him myself before I will let him have her.
column2: 哈維因休想娶到札伊夏。在他得逞之前,我會親手了結他。

column1: The eunuchs watch the women to make sure they cannot leave the harem.
column2: 太監們看守著這些女子,確保她們無法離開後宮。

column1: Do you not know better than I?
column2: 難道你知道的不比我多嗎?

column1: It is forbidden for a man to speak to a woman unless she is his mother, wife, or slave.
column2: 男人不准和女子說話,除非對方是他的母親、妻子或奴婢。

column1: No man is allowed in the harem. The women there know how to keep secrets.
column2: 後宮不准任何男人進入。那裡的女子個個都懂得守口如瓶。

column1: Zayishah will be safe there.
column2: 札伊夏在那裡會很安全。

column1: The city has been destroyed by Khaveen and Ad Avis.
column2: 這座城已被哈維因和阿德·阿維斯毀了。

column1: The Sultan (May He Reign Forever!) will care for Zayishah in Shapeir, if she can but get there.
column2: 蘇丹(願他萬世統御!)會在夏皮爾照料札伊夏,只要她能順利抵達那裡。

column1: The Emir is a weak fool who thinks he rules this city.
column2: 埃米爾是個軟弱的蠢蛋,還以為自己當真統治著這座城。

column1: There are many secret ways through the Palace.
column2: 穿越王宮的密道多得很。

column1: Zayishah knows the Caravan Master. He will take her to Shapeir.
column2: 札伊夏認識商隊隊長,他會帶她去夏皮爾。

column1: The desert is a safer place for Zayishah than this city.
column2: 對札伊夏來說,沙漠都比這座城安全。

column1: If the fountain was still running, Khaveen would not have such power as he has now. He controls who gets water.
column2: 要是噴泉還在流,哈維因就不會有今天這般權勢。是他掌控著誰能取得水源。

column1: I am called Mayzun.
column2: 人家都叫我梅珍。

column1: There is one Katta left in the city. He is with the Underground.
column2: 城裡還剩一個卡塔族人,他跟地下組織在一起。

column1: Khaveen finds great pleasure in the pain of others.
column2: 哈維因以他人的痛苦為樂。

column1: There are many who resent Ad Avis and Khaveen and wish to restore the city.
column2: 有許多人對阿德·阿維斯和哈維因恨之入骨,一心想要復興這座城。

column1: I know nothing.
column2: 我什麼都不知道。

column1: I can tell you but little.
column2: 我能告訴你的也不多。

column1: I hope with all my heart that you can restore Raseir.
column2: 我打從心底盼著你能重建拉希爾。

column1: The slave woman glares at you.
column2: 那名女奴狠狠瞪了你一眼。

column1: You wouldn't want to take advantage of me, would you?
column2: 你不會想佔我便宜吧,嗯?

column1: You, sir, are a cad. Get lost!
column2: 先生,你真是個下流胚子。滾開!

column1: Well, you can't. So there.
column2: 哼,你辦不到。就是這樣。

column1: Please, Hero. You must help me.
column2: 拜託,英雄,你一定要幫我。

column1: You Discover you have no Visa, so you offer her your Master's Card.
column2: 你發現自己沒有簽證,於是把你的大師卡遞給她。

column1: Good. I would have had to refuse.
column2: 很好,不然我只能拒收了。

column1: Please, Hero, your visa. There isn't much time.
column2: 拜託,英雄,你的簽證,我們沒多少時間了。

column1: You tell about the Elementals that attacked Shapeir.
column2: 你說起那些襲擊夏皮爾的元素生物。

column1: I would not be surprised if Ad Avis was responsible for what happened there. Why would he stop at simply destroying Raseir?
column2: 就算這一切是阿德·阿維斯搞的鬼,我也不會意外。他怎麼可能只滿足於毀掉拉希爾?

column1: I would not be surprised if Ad Avis was somehow responsible for what happened there. Why would he stop at simply destroying Raseir?
column2: 就算這背後多少有阿德·阿維斯的影子,我也不會意外。他怎麼可能只滿足於毀掉拉希爾?

column1: You tell about your stay in the Blue Parrot Inn.
column2: 你說起自己在藍鸚鵡客棧落腳的事。

column1: Is it not sad that a seedy den of thieves is now the best thing this city has to offer a traveller looking for a place to spend the night?
column2: 一座藏污納垢的賊窩,竟成了這城裡能給旅人過夜的最好去處,豈不令人心酸?

column1: You tell about your meetings with Signor Ferrari.
column2: 你說起自己和費拉里先生打過交道的事。

column1: He seems like a very untrustworthy person. There are too many people like him around here these days.
column2: 他看起來很不可靠。這年頭,這城裡像他這樣的人實在太多了。

column1: You tell about Ugarte's arrest.
column2: 你說起烏加特被逮捕的事。

column1: Thanks to the commotion in the Fountain Plaza, I was able to make it here without being noticed.
column2: 多虧噴泉廣場鬧出那場騷動,我才能神不知鬼不覺地溜到這裡。

column1: You tell about Al Scurva who was supposedly Ad Avis' apprentice.
column2: 你說起據說是阿德·阿維斯徒弟的阿爾·史庫瓦。

column1: I think I remember seeing him a few times in the presence of Ad Avis. I do not think he was very succesful as an apprentice, for Ad Avis was always yelling at him.
column2: 我好像見過他幾次跟在阿德·阿維斯身邊。這徒弟當得不怎麼樣吧,阿德·阿維斯老是對他大吼大叫。

column1: You tell about your encounters with Khaveen.
column2: 你說起自己和哈維因交手的經過。

column1: If Khaveen were to marry me, he would become my father's lawful heir. That thought alone makes me shudder.
column2: 要是哈維因娶了我,他就成了我父親的合法繼承人。光是想到這點,我就渾身發抖。

column1: You have already told her that story.
column2: 這個故事你已經跟她說過了。

column1: That is most interesting, but I must hurry.
column2: 真是有意思,可是我得趕緊走了。

column1: Zayishah seems a bit annoyed by your gesture. Perhaps this isn't the best time for that.
column2: 查伊夏似乎對你的舉動有點不悅,也許現在不是做這種事的時候。

column1: I'd love to hear more, Hero, but I haven't the time right now.
column2: 英雄,我很想多聽一些,可惜現在實在沒空。

column1: You tell about how you defeated the Elementals that attacked Shapeir.
column2: 你說起自己是怎麼擊敗那些襲擊夏皮爾的元素生物。

column1: Raseir is in great need of a hero such as you. It is hoped your actions will return this city to its former state somehow.
column2: 拉希爾正迫切需要像你這樣的英雄。但願你的作為能讓這座城,設法回到從前的模樣。

column1: You tell the slave woman you hope her mistress will be safe.
column2: 你對那名女奴說,希望她的女主人能平安無事。

column1: If she can but get past the guards at the gates, she will be fine. The true monsters preying on her reside inside this city's walls.
column2: 只要她能闖過城門口的衛兵,就沒事了。真正盯著她不放的惡魔,就住在這座城牆之內。

column1: Raseirian hospitality has known better days.
column2: 拉希爾人的待客之道,早已不復當年。

column1: People who profit off the sorry state of our city are the vultures of this society.
column2: 那些靠著我們這座城的悲慘處境牟利的人,就是這個社會的禿鷹。

column1: I do not know how I would have located you if it had not been for Khaveen arranging that arrest for you to witness.
column2: 要不是哈維因安排了那場逮捕讓你親眼看見,我真不知道該怎麼找到你。

column1: What kind of person would willingly accept Ad Avis as his mentor?
column2: 什麼樣的人會心甘情願拜阿德·阿維斯為師?

column1: If Khaveen were to marry to Zayishah, he would become heir to the throne of Raseir. At that point, he would have no further use for the Emir and only one other use for poor Zayishah.
column2: 要是哈維因娶了查伊夏,他就成了拉希爾王位的繼承人。到那時候,埃米爾對他就再無用處,而可憐的查伊夏,也只剩下一種用途了。

column1: You tell about how the missing Emir.
column2: 你說起失蹤的埃米爾。

column1: He was a kind man and it is good to hear he is still alive, but I do not know whether he should be reinstated as Emir once he regains his human form. His lack of judgement is partially responsible for getting Ad Avis in power to begin with.
column2: 他是個善心人,聽說他還活著我很高興。可是他恢復人形之後,該不該再讓他當回埃米爾,我可說不準。當初阿德·阿維斯能上位,有一部分正是怪他識人不明。

column1: Most interesting.
column2: 真是太有意思了。

column1: A beautiful hand-woven rug decorates the center of the floor.
column2: 地板中央鋪著一張漂亮的手織地毯。

column1: A very ornate vase with a beautiful plant. Certainly an un-Raseirian sight.
column2: 一只極盡華麗的花瓶,插著一株美麗的植物。在拉希爾,這實在是難得一見的景象。

column1: The veil is made of light gauze, just opaque enough to prevent any would-be peepers from seeing anything fun.
column2: 這面紗是用輕薄的紗料做的,剛好不透到讓那些想偷瞄的傢伙看不到任何好料。

column1: Wearing the Ali Fakir Genuine X-Ray Glasses, you can see right through the light gauze of this veil.
column2: 戴上阿里·法基爾正牌透視眼鏡,你就能一眼看穿這面紗的輕薄紗料。

column1: This veil is much like the other.
column2: 這面紗和另一面差不多。

column1: The ceiling is inlaid with many colorful woods.
column2: 天花板嵌著許多色彩繽紛的木料。

column1: The room is lavishly appointed in the manner of an Emir's palace.
column2: 這房間布置得富麗堂皇,儼然是埃米爾宮殿的氣派。

column1: The pillars are made of white marble.
column2: 那些柱子是用白色大理石砌成的。

column1: Please, you must help me. Take off your clothes.
column2: 求求你,你一定要幫我。把你的衣服脫下來。

column1: I must leave the city before I am forced to marry Khaveen. I have heard you are a hero. With your help, I can be out of the city before nightfall.
column2: 我必須趁著還沒被逼嫁給哈維因之前離開這座城。聽說你是個英雄,有你相助,我天黑前就能出城。

column1: I wish to disguise myself as you and leave with your visa, so please, let me exchange clothes with you.
column2: 我想喬裝成你的模樣,拿著你的簽證離開,所以拜託,讓我和你換一下衣服吧。

column1: You extinguish your lamp.
column2: 你熄滅了你的油燈。

column1: The Same Voice
column2: 同一個聲音

column1: That's good. You obeyed at least one order. Now walk through the door.
column2: 很好,你至少服從了一道命令。現在,穿過那道門。

column1: The Same Voice As Before
column2: 還是剛才那個聲音

column1: The Eternal Order of Fighters has no need for fools who can't obey a simple order. Go away! You'll never be a real hero!
column2: 永恆戰士團不需要連一道簡單命令都聽不懂的蠢蛋。滾吧!你永遠成不了真正的英雄!

column1: Couldn't even break out of the chains. Whatta wimp!
column2: 連鎖鏈都掙不開,真是個窩囊廢!

column1: There is a message scrawled on the stone alley floor. It says: "The Eternal Order of Fighters does not want Wimps and Losers."
column2: 石板巷地上潦草地寫著一行字:「永恆戰士團不收窩囊廢和魯蛇。」

column1: d The Same Voice As Before
column2: 還是剛才那個聲音

column1: The Eternal Order of Fighters has no need for cheaters who try to pull dirty tricks in our combat test.
column2: 永恆戰士團不需要在我們的戰鬥試煉裡耍陰招的騙子。

column1: Enter the darkness.
column2: 步入黑暗之中。

column1: This is not a good place to practice your Levitate spell..
column2: 這可不是練習漂浮咒的好地方。

column1: Still The Same Voice
column2: 仍然是那個聲音

column1: You were given an order. Obey!
column2: 已經給你下令了。服從!

column1: That Voice Again
column2: 又是那個聲音
