column1: SEYou faintly hear "To the left... up... ah, right there!" The lamp glows faintly with pleasure, then decides it isn't dark enough to really put on a show, and goes out again.
column2: 你隱約聽見:「往左……往上……啊,就是那裡!」油燈愜意地微微亮了一下,接著又覺得這裡還不夠暗、施展不開,於是再次熄滅了。

column1: e That Voice Again
column2: 又是那個聲音

column1: You hear a voice from inside...
column2: 你聽見裡頭傳來一個聲音……

column1: A Voice From Within
column2: 裡頭傳來的聲音

column1: You do not need the light. Put it away.
column2: 你用不著燈火,把它收起來。

column1: That would not be a good idea right now.
column2: 現在這麼做可不是個好主意。

column1: You're in a blind alley. There's a door at the end. The street sign reads 'Saif Darb.'
column2: 你身在一條死巷裡,盡頭有一道門。路牌上寫著「賽夫·達布」。

column1: You've been locked out.
column2: 你被鎖在外頭了。

column1: The door is open. It is very, very dark on the other side.
column2: 門開著,門那頭一片漆黑,黑得不見五指。

column1: You feel victorious!
column2: 你感到一陣勝利的暢快!

column1: The voices call to you.
column2: 那些聲音呼喚著你。

column1: Well done. Now finish him off!
column2: 幹得好,現在,給他最後一擊!

column1: Kill him!!
column2: 殺了他!!

column1: The man left standing.
column2: 那人還站在原地。

column1: You don't feel like telling stories now.
column2: 你現在沒心情說故事。

column1: You sure showed him who's boss! He's quite dead. Or is he? Something felt a little funny when you slashed him.
column2: 你可讓他見識了誰才是老大!他死得透透的了。還是說……沒死透?你砍下去那一刀,總覺得哪裡怪怪的。

column1: He's at your mercy. The voices urge you to show none.
column2: 他已任你處置,那些聲音慫恿你別留半點情面。

column1: It's still dark in the other room, but it's obvious you're being watched from there.
column2: 另一個房間仍舊一片漆黑,但顯然有人正從那裡盯著你看。

column1: That door leads to another room, you think.
column2: 你猜,那道門通往另一個房間。

column1: Nobody is in there to answer your call.
column2: 裡頭沒有人回應你的呼喊。

column1: You guess this is the EOF hall.
column2: 你猜,這裡就是永恆戰士團的大廳。

column1: Now that's a REAL hero. Show no mercy to losers!
column2: 這才叫真正的英雄,對魯蛇就該毫不留情!

column1: Isn't that right, Walid?
column2: 對吧,瓦立德?

column1: Dat's right, Boss!
column2: 冇錯,老大!

column1: Your real equipment is in the other room. You have been tested to prove your worthiness to join the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 你真正的裝備在另一個房間。方才那一切,都是為了考驗你,證明你夠格加入永恆戰士團。

column1: You have shown through your obedience, guts, and skill that you should now be one of the highest ranks of EOF. You are now a Brother Scorpion. Welcome to the club!
column2: 你以服從、膽識和身手證明了自己,如今你該躋身永恆戰士團的最高階。從現在起,你就是「蠍子兄弟」了。歡迎入夥!

column1: What a turkey! Even when you win, you don't have the guts to finish the task. Never show mercy to an enemy.
column2: 真是個沒用的傢伙!都贏了還沒膽量把事情做絕。對敵人,絕不留情。

column1: You can fight, and you've got some guts, but you just can't obey a simple order. We figured we'd let you join EOF despite the fact that you failed. You are admitted to the beginning rank of our order. You are now a Brother Saurus. Welcome to EOF.
column2: 你能打,膽子也還算有,可就是聽不懂一道簡單的命令。雖然你沒過關,我們還是決定收你進永恆戰士團。你被編入我們最底層的階級,從現在起,你就是「索魯斯兄弟」了。歡迎加入永恆戰士團。

column1: You can't go in there.
column2: 你進不去那裡。

column1: The shackles that bound you hang limply from the wall.
column2: 先前鎖住你的鐐銬,如今軟趴趴地垂掛在牆上。

column1: The door leads to the alley, you think.
column2: 你猜,那道門通往巷子。

column1: Your sword lies discarded on the floor.
column2: 你的劍被丟在地上。

column1: You don't need it.
column2: 你用不著它。

column1: It's a flickering flame.
column2: 那是一團搖曳的火光。

column1: Watch it, Mac. Customers aren't allowed in the service area.
column2: 看著點,老兄。客人不准進維修區。

column1: Sorry, Mac, but yer Saurus won't be ready 'til Tuesday.
column2: 抱歉啦老兄,你的索魯斯禮拜二才修得好。

column1: Just park 'er outside in the alley, Mac, and we'll get to it as soon as we can.
column2: 先把牠停在外頭巷子裡吧老兄,我們一有空就幫你弄。

column1: You the Brougham model? Won't be finished today. Check with us after noon tomorrow.
column2: 你是那台布魯厄姆型號的車主吧?今天弄不完,明天過了中午再來問問。

column1: Can't get to it until next week, Mac. We're all filled up with orders from that caravan.
column2: 下禮拜才輪得到你那台啦老兄,那支商隊的訂單把我們塞爆了。

column1: New exhaust system!
column2: 全新排氣系統!

column1: This is what happens when Brigands drag race in the alleys.
column2: 這就是強盜在巷子裡飆車的下場。

column1: This model's a real dinosaur!
column2: 這型號簡直老掉牙了!

column1: Grind off the rust... fresh coat of paint... just like new!
column2: 磨掉鏽斑……刷上新漆……跟新的一樣!

column1: This is in case someone needs his Saurus lifted a foot taller.
column2: 這是給那些想把自家索魯斯墊高一呎的人準備的。

column1: Yessir, you can get a head at Salt's Repair Shop!
column2: 沒錯,先生,在索特修理店,你能換到一顆「頭」!

column1: You can even get some tail at Salt's Repair Shop!
column2: 在索特修理店,你甚至還能弄到「尾巴」呢!

column1: Someone left a left-ended end wrench.
column2: 有人留下了一把左端的扳手。

column1: The wrench on the right is the right right-ended end wrench.
column2: 右邊那把,才是對的右端扳手。

column1: It's closing time, Mac. Clear outta here.
column2: 打烊時間到了老兄,給我清場。

column1: This state-of-the-art spare engine part will make your Saurus stop and start.
column2: 這個最頂尖的引擎備用零件,能讓你的索魯斯說停就停、說走就走。

column1: Hey! Don't do that!
column2: 喂!別亂動!

column1: Need a lift?
column2: 要不要墊高一下?

column1: It doesn't say jack.
column2: 它一個字也不吐。

column1: Leave the manual labor up to Salt.
column2: 粗活就交給索特來吧。

column1: You can't talk to the spare part.
column2: 你沒辦法跟一個備用零件講話。

column1: The conveyer belt delivers spare parts into the mechanic's garage.
column2: 輸送帶把備用零件一路送進技師的修理廠裡。

column1: You cannot operate it by hand.
column2: 你沒辦法用手操作它。

column1: That's not the best place to stick your tongue.
column2: 那可不是把舌頭伸過去的好地方。

column1: It is a sign advertising the mechanic's business.
column2: 那是一面替技師生意打廣告的招牌。

column1: The sign is sitting at just the right angle. There's no need to nudge it any further.
column2: 招牌的角度擺得剛剛好,沒必要再去動它。

column1: Read it and it will tell you all it has to say.
column2: 讀一讀,它要說的全在上頭了。

column1: Fill 'er up?
column2: 加滿嗎?

column1: Don't touch that!
column2: 別碰那個!

column1: Somehow drinking from your waterskin seems more appealing.
column2: 不知怎地,還是喝你水袋裡的水更順口些。

column1: It's oil, slick.
column2: 那是機油,滑溜得很。

column1: Toil with oil and your hands you shall soil.
column2: 跟機油較勁,雙手就準髒兮兮。

column1: It won't raise your Communication stat, but it might help you to talk smooth.
column2: 這玩意兒提不了你的溝通屬性,倒是能讓你講起話來油嘴滑舌。

column1: Various tools and implements of the mechanic's trade litter the premises.
column2: 各式各樣的技師工具和器械,散落了一地。

column1: You don't want any of that stuff. You don't need it either.
column2: 那些東西你不會想要,也用不著。

column1: This sheet depicts some of the early model dinosaurs. It shows progressively newer models as your eyes scan down the page. Evolution is an extraordinary thing!
column2: 這張圖畫的是幾種早期型號的恐龍,你的視線往下掃,型號就一台比一台新。演化真是樁了不起的事!

column1: Leave that up for the edutainment of the customers.
column2: 就讓它掛在那兒,給客人寓教於樂吧。

column1: It is the very model of a modern Saurus status sheet.
column2: 這可是一張不折不扣的現代索魯斯狀態表。

column1: Wooden you know it? They're crate.
column2: 你「木」會曉得吧?它們可「箱」當不賴呢。

column1: The crates are stacked exactly where Salt needs them. He surely would not appreciate you moving them around on him.
column2: 這些木箱正好堆在索特要的位置,你要是擅自幫他挪來挪去,他肯定不會領情。

column1: You'd need to use a more forceful means of getting them to open up.
column2: 想撬開它們,你得用上更猛一點的手段。

column1: Those are the sorriest bunch of Saurii you've ever seen.
column2: 那是你見過最慘不忍睹的一群索魯斯。

column1: These models have seen better days.
column2: 這幾台型號,風光的日子早就過去了。

column1: Not only are they broken, they can't talk either.
column2: 它們不只壞了,還連話都不會講。

column1: This place looks like a cross between an abattoir and a garage. Mechanical body parts and tools are scattered untidily across the floor. A gruff mechanic goes about his work paying little regard to your presence.
column2: 這地方看起來像是屠宰場和修車廠的綜合體。機械軀體零件和工具凌亂地散了一地。一個粗聲粗氣的技師埋頭幹著活,對你的到來幾乎不理不睬。

column1: You're not exactly handy when it comes to things of a mechanical nature.
column2: 一碰上機械方面的東西,你就不怎麼在行了。

column1: This place gives new definition to the term 'chop shop.'
column2: 這地方替「拆車廠」這個詞下了全新的定義。

column1: You no gonna regret this, boss.
column2: 你絕對唔會後悔嘅,老大。

column1: Guaranteed the best in town or you no getta you money back.
column2: 包你全城最讚,唔係嘅話,錢一文都唔退你。

column1: How's about a nice... Wait!
column2: 不如來件漂亮的……等等!

column1: What is-a that up there?
column2: 上頭那個係乜嘢?

column1: No, no. That costume just will not do.
column2: 唔得唔得,呢套行頭實在唔行。

column1: Now you look like a hero, Shapeir style.
column2: 呢下你睇起嚟先似個英雄,夏皮爾風格嘅!

column1: You find you cannot move. The man before you has a low, pleasant voice and eyes like a snake.
column2: 你發現自己動彈不得。眼前這個男人嗓音低沉悅耳,一雙眼睛卻像蛇一樣。

column1: Remember your friend, Ad Avis.
column2: 記住你的朋友,阿德·阿維斯。

column1: Remember my voice. Remember my face.
column2: 記住我的聲音,記住我的臉。

column1: Trust me, I am your friend.
column2: 相信我,我是你的朋友。

column1: You try to struggle, but your body will not obey.
column2: 你試圖掙扎,身體卻不聽使喚。

column1: You cannot even close your eyes, but you try to avoid his gaze. There is something about his eyes, though...
column2: 你連眼睛都閉不上,只能拚命想避開他的目光。可是他那雙眼睛,總有種說不出的魔力……

column1: I am your friend, and you trust me.
column2: 我是你的朋友,你信任我。

column1: Trust in my words. Trust in my eyes.
column2: 信任我的話語,信任我的雙眼。

column1: Look in my eyes, and trust me.
column2: 看著我的眼睛,信任我。

column1: You look at his eyes, and cannot look away. They seem to glow. His words seem to whirl around your head.
column2: 你望進他的眼睛,移不開目光。那雙眼彷彿在發光,他的話語在你腦中盤旋打轉。

column1: Obey now your friend, Ad Avis.
column2: 現在,聽從你的朋友——阿德·阿維斯。

column1: Obey now my words. Obey now my voice.
column2: 聽從我的話語,聽從我的聲音。

column1: Trust in your friend, and obey me.
column2: 信任你的朋友,服從我。

column1: You find yourself relaxing. You are with a friend. His eyes are gentle, and his smile warms you.
column2: 你感到全身放鬆下來。你與一位朋友同在。他的眼神溫和,笑容暖入你心。

column1: Together we seek to aid our friend, Emir Arus. I was his trusted vizier, remember?
column2: 我們要同心協力,幫助我們的朋友——埃米爾阿魯斯。我曾是他最信任的宰相,記得嗎?

column1: You find yourself nodding at this humble, gentle man.
column2: 你不自覺地對這位謙遜溫和的人點了點頭。

column1: To end the evil reign of Khaveen and restore our friend the Emir Arus, together we must go out into the desert.
column2: 要終結哈維因的邪惡統治,讓我們的朋友埃米爾阿魯斯重掌大權,我們就得一同踏進沙漠。

column1: We must journey to the Forbidden City and recover the magic item that will break the spell upon the Emir.
column2: 我們必須遠行到禁城,取回那件能解除埃米爾身上咒語的魔法器物。

column1: We NEED to get the Statue of Iblis!
column2: 我們一定要拿到伊布利斯的雕像!

column1: Yes, you know this is something you've got to do, but you frown as you try to remember something you once heard about Iblis.
column2: 沒錯,你知道這是你非做不可的事——可是你皺起眉頭,努力回想自己曾聽過關於伊布利斯的某些傳聞。

column1: You remember the Statue of Iblis, don't you? It has remarkable powers and can break any spell it touches.
column2: 你還記得伊布利斯的雕像吧?它擁有非凡的力量,碰到的任何咒語都能破解。

column1: We need to take the statue to Arus, our friend. Together, let us save the city of Raseir!
column2: 我們得把雕像帶去交給我們的朋友阿魯斯。讓我們攜手拯救拉希爾城!

column1: He puts his arm on your shoulders and you smile. Here is a man of great wisdom that you have trusted and respected, and he needs your help.
column2: 他把手搭上你的肩,你露出微笑。眼前這位充滿智慧的人,是你一向信任又敬重的對象,而此刻他需要你的幫助。

column1: Gee, you didn't even reach the end of your rope.
column2: 唉呀,你連繩子的另一頭都還沒走到呢。

column1: In circuses (and beach movies) they use a net
column2: 在馬戲團裡(還有海灘電影裡)他們可是會張一張安全網的喔

column1: Walk across the rope and correct any tilting by tapping the appropriate key to lean in the opposite direction. Use the Hold Position option in emergencies, but avoid using it too often.
column2: 走過繩索,身子一歪就按對應的鍵往反方向傾斜把它調回來。情況危急時可以用「穩住不動」這招,但別太常用。

column1: This must be another room of Keapon's shop. You can hardly see a thing, but it's strangely cool in here and you can feel a draught of some sort.
column2: 這想必是奇朋店裡的另一個房間。你幾乎什麼都看不清,但這裡涼得出奇,還能感覺到一股穿堂的氣流。

column1: Woah! That's deep! As the "room" lights up, you realize you're standing on top of a pillar very high above the ground. You think you can vaguely see Shapeir far below.
column2: 哇!好深哪!「房間」一亮起來,你才發現自己正站在一根高聳的石柱頂端,離地老遠。你隱約覺得能看見遠在下方的夏皮爾。

column1: After overcoming the initial surprise, common sense kicks in and you realize there weren't any unrealistically tall pillars protruding from Shapeir when you got here, so what you're seeing must be an illusion of some kind.
column2: 等最初的驚嚇過去,常識回到腦中——你想起來,當初抵達夏皮爾時,城裡根本沒有這種高得離譜的石柱聳立著,所以眼前這幅景象八成是某種幻象。

column1: You don't have time for that!
column2: 你可沒空管那個!

column1: Good. We start with some footwork. First, move backward.
column2: 很好。咱們先從步法練起。先,往後退。

column1: Like this...
column2: 像這樣……

column1: Now you try.
column2: 現在換你試。

column1: Move back one more time.
column2: 再往後退一次。

column1: We now both be standing at edge of combat area. None of us be capable of moving farther backward.
column2: 咱倆現在都站在戰鬥區的邊緣啦,誰也沒法再往後退了。

column1: Now, walk forward again to advance on opponent.
column2: 好,現在再往前走,向對手逼近。

column1: If enemy be right in front of you, you cannot advance further.
column2: 敵人就在你面前,你就沒法再往前進啦。

column1: Footwork also be important when getting out of harm's way. There be various ways to dodge incoming attacks.
column2: 閃避傷害時,步法一樣要緊。躲開來襲的攻擊,法子可多著呢。

column1: Most common way of dodging be moving sideways. Be careful if enemy swings with his weapon. If you be dodging in the wrong direction, you be walking right into his blow.
column2: 最常見的閃避法,就是往側邊移。敵人揮武器時可得當心,要是你閃錯方向,那可是自己撞上他那一擊。

column1: Ready? Go!
column2: 準備好沒?上!

column1: Watch my movements carefully. In real combat, it be difference between life and death.
column2: 仔細看我的動作。真打起來,這可是生死之別。

column1: Try again.
column2: 再試一次。

column1: Good. In real combat, opponent may be faster. I be going easy on you now. Reflexes and blade must be equally sharp, otherwise you lose.
column2: 很好。真打時,對手可能更快。我現在還手下留情哪。反應跟刀都得一樣利,不然你就輸定了。

column1: There be more ways to evade blows in combat. All of them based on principle of footwork.
column2: 戰鬥中還有更多躲開攻擊的法子,全都建立在步法的道理上。

column1: Easiest way is jumping backward and moving out of my reach. Let's try.
column2: 最簡單的就是往後跳,跳出我搆得到的範圍。來試試。

column1: Problem with jumping backward be that you can get yourself cornered this way.
column2: 往後跳的毛病在於,這樣容易把自己逼到死角。

column1: If an attack is aimed high, you can quickly duck under it.
column2: 要是攻擊往高處招呼,你可以迅速低身從底下鑽過。

column1: This not be very tiring, and you remain within range for a quick counterattack, but will only work if act at last moment.
column2: 這招不太費力,還能讓你留在範圍內好快速反擊——但只有在最後一刻出手才管用。

column1: Need good timing. Let's try.
column2: 時機得抓得準。來試試。

column1: Well timed. Now we try something similar. If enemy uses attack aimed at kneecaps or feet, you can jump up to avoid it.
column2: 時機抓得好。現在咱們試個類似的。敵人朝你膝蓋或腳下招呼時,你可以往上一跳躲開。

column1: I do a low stab and you dodge by jumping.
column2: 我來個低刺,你就跳起來閃。

column1: You be quick learner. Last defensive action be parry skill. Footwork be very important, but it be not the only way to save your life in battle.
column2: 你學得真快。最後一個防禦動作是格擋。步法很要緊,但它不是戰場上保命的唯一法子。

column1: You can block attacks with weapon. Swordsmen often favor blade to stop attacks from other swordsmen instead of dodging.
column2: 你可以用武器擋下攻擊。對上別的劍士時,劍士往往愛用刀身擋招,而不是閃躲。

column1: Ready? Now!
column2: 準備好沒?就現在!

column1: Good timing be required for blocking. Let's try again.
column2: 格擋得抓準時機才行。再試一次。

column1: Good anticipation be good addition to reflexes. Try to anticipate when I will strike.
column2: 預判得好,反應就更如虎添翼。試著預判我什麼時候會出手。

column1: Make sure to defend like that in actual combat, too.
column2: 真打的時候,也要記得這樣防。

column1: Now, we do some attacking. Combat can't be won unless you can damage enemy.
column2: 好,咱們來練練進攻。傷不到敵人,這仗就贏不了。

column1: The safest and fastest way to attack be the stab or thrust. Stab does not do much damage, but enemy won't have much time to defend against it or counter it. Try stabbing.
column2: 最安全也最快的攻擊就是刺擊。刺擊傷害不大,但敵人沒多少時間防或反擊。來,試著刺一下。

column1: Finally, let's practice the swing. This attack be slower than the stab, but inflicts more damage.
column2: 最後,咱們來練揮砍。這一招比刺擊慢,但傷害更大。

column1: Attack me once more.
column2: 再攻我一次。

column1: As you can see, opponent will mind defense, and you better be doing, too.
column2: 看到沒,對手會顧著防守,你最好也跟著防。

column1: That be all for now. Next training session we fight for real.
column2: 今天就到這兒。下次訓練咱們就真刀真槍地打。

column1: One more thing. If you think you be losing, run away. Survival be more important than pride. You may die gloriously out in desert, but nobody here will ever know.
column2: 還有件事。要是你覺得快輸了,就跑。活命比面子重要。你或許能在沙漠裡死得轟轟烈烈,可這兒沒人會曉得。

column1: This tutorial is intended to introduce the player to the basics of the game's battle system.
column2: 本教學的用意,是帶玩家認識遊戲戰鬥系統的基本操作。

column1: Time action better. Do not react too soon. You be giving up valuable terrain that way.
column2: 時機再抓好點。別太早反應,那樣你會白白讓出寶貴的地盤。

column1: You be acting too early. Let's try again.
column2: 你出手太早了。再試一次。

column1: Take too many hits and you fall backward. Without good defense, you be cornered quickly.
column2: 挨太多下,你就會往後倒。防守不好,很快就被逼到死角。

column1: Certain attacks will always knock you back in one hit. Be careful of aggressive opponents.
column2: 有些攻擊一下就能把你打退。碰上兇猛的對手可得當心。

column1: Tutor Info:
column2: 教官情報:

column1: You can block attacks with either shield or weapon. Shield be best for stopping attacks of any kind, but swordsmen often favor blade to stop attacks from other swordsmen. Sword arm often be more nimble than shield arm.
column2: 你可以用盾牌或武器擋攻擊。盾牌擋什麼攻擊都最管用,不過對上劍士時,劍士往往愛用刀身擋招。畢竟持劍的手通常比持盾的手靈活。

column1: You be reacting too early. Let's try again.
column2: 你反應太早了。再試一次。

column1: Clay Doll
column2: 泥娃娃

column1: Ant Hunter
column2: 獵蟻者

column1: Croc Hunter
column2: 獵鱷者

column1: Horn Collector
column2: 集角者

column1: Liontaur Warrior
column2: 獅人戰士

column1: Demon Slayer
column2: 屠魔者

column1: Only the fighter can challenge Rakeesh.
column2: 只有戰士才能向拉基什挑戰。

column1: Creepy Crawler
column2: 爬行怪客

column1: Brother Saurus
column2: 索魯斯兄弟

column1: Brother Scorpion
column2: 蠍子兄弟

column1: Brother Griffin
column2: 獅鷲兄弟

column1: Only the fighter can face Walid.
column2: 只有戰士才能面對瓦立德。

column1: Palace Guards
column2: 宮廷衛兵

column1: Sword Master
column2: 劍術大師

column1: Dark Paladin
column2: 黑暗聖騎士

column1: The showdown with Khaveen is only for the fighter.
column2: 與哈維因的最終對決,只屬於戰士。

column1: Terrorsaurus Rex
column2: 恐爆龍王

column1: Bird of Prey
column2: 猛禽

column1: Winged Legend
column2: 羽翼傳奇

column1: Only the fighter will face Walid.
column2: 只有戰士才會面對瓦立德。

column1: No saurus version available.
column2: 沒有索魯斯坐騎的版本。

column1: You wisely demonstrate your inborn self-preservation skills.
column2: 你明智地展現了與生俱來的保命本領。

column1: You don't have room for that!
column2: 你沒地方放那個!

column1: It might have been a glorious battle, but ones in which the hero dies rarely make the front page.
column2: 這或許是場光榮的戰鬥,可惜英雄陣亡的那種,很少能登上頭版。

column1: No Glory in this Story
column2: 這故事沒榮耀可言

column1: It was a tough battle, and you lost. Never fear! All you have to do is to restore your game, and... What do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
column2: 這是場硬仗,而你輸了。別怕!你只要讀取遊戲存檔就行了,還有……你說什麼,「讀取『什麼』存檔」?

column1: <iWhat a monster
column2: <i好一個怪物

column1: If you want to save the city, you'll have to go for something better than a draw.
column2: 想救這座城,你得拿出比平手更好的成績才行。

column1: Hero: 1, Rocky: 1
column2: 英雄:1,洛基:1

column1: If you're going to use explosives in close quarters, at least be healthy enough to survive the blast yourself.
column2: 要是你打算在近距離使用炸藥,至少先確保自己夠健康,撐得過那場爆炸。

column1: Negative Frag Count
column2: 負殺敵數

column1: You made the Earth Elemental hit the dirt.
column2: 你把土元素打趴在地。

column1: Rocky Info:
column2: 洛基情報:

column1: Brigands in the desert better watch out for you. They be in big trouble.
column2: 沙漠裡的強盜可得提防你了。他們麻煩大囉。

column1: You can take on a pack of Jackalmen now.
column2: 你現在能對付一整群豺狼人啦。

column1: The Terrorsaurus who tries to eat you will be dead meat first.
column2: 想吃掉你的恐爆龍,自己會先變成一坨死肉。

column1: The Ghoulyman who tries to get you now will be sorry.
column2: 現在敢來找你麻煩的食屍鬼,可有得後悔了。

column1: That be enough for now. Soon you be giving me lessons.
column2: 今天就到這兒。要不了多久,就換你來教我啦。

column1: That be enough for today. Soon you be giving me lessons.
column2: 今天就練到這兒。要不了多久,就換你來教我啦。

column1: Either I be out of shape from Simba or you be getting too good for me plenty soon.
column2: 要嘛是我被辛巴搞得身手生疏了,要嘛就是你很快就要強到我招架不住啦。

column1: You very fast learner. I think maybe you be plenty tough enough now.
column2: 你學得真快。我想你現在大概夠硬了。

column1: You got a good swing. My shield arm be sore.
column2: 你這揮砍夠勁。我持盾的手都酸了。

column1: That time, we both got workout. Pretty soon, I be needing rest.
column2: 這回咱倆都練得夠嗆。再過不久,我可得歇口氣了。

column1: You be getting past my shield many times now. You be getting good.
column2: 你現在好幾次都突破我的盾了。你越來越行啦。

column1: Enough for now. You look pretty winded. We go back to hall.
column2: 今天就到這兒。你看來氣喘吁吁的。咱們回大廳吧。

column1: You need to defend better. You hit pretty often, but you get hit too often.
column2: 你的防守得加強。你出招打中是挺多,可挨打也太多了。

column1: You getting much better, but you still be not defending enough.
column2: 你進步很多,可防守還是不夠。

column1: Block my spear or get out of the way.
column2: 擋下我的矛,要不就閃開。

column1: Keep your shield held higher. Easier to block spears and swords with it there.
column2: 盾舉高點。擺那兒擋矛擋劍都更輕鬆。

column1: You let me get you with spear way too often. If I be Ghoul, you be plenty weak right now and I be plenty strong.
column2: 你太常被我的矛戳中了。我要是食屍鬼,你現在早就虛得不行,我可壯著呢。

column1: Do not let me touch you with my spear the next time. I will be plenty mad if some monster tears you to shreds because you do not dodge enough.
column2: 下次別再讓我的矛碰到你。要是哪天有怪物因為你閃得不夠把你撕成碎片,我可要氣壞了。

column1: Don't get into combat with Scorpion until you dodge better.
column2: 閃避練得更好之前,別去跟蠍子硬碰硬。

column1: Dodge! Don't take damage -- get out of the way.
column2: 閃啊!別挨打——快讓開。

column1: Move out of the way more. You got feet -- use them.
column2: 多挪挪位置。你有腳——拿來用啊。

column1: Don't rely on your weapon to save you. Use your body to avoid damage.
column2: 別指望武器救你。用你的身子來閃傷害。

column1: Never stand still. Moving target harder to hit.
column2: 千萬別站著不動。會動的目標才難打中。

column1: Terrorsaurus is strong but stupid. Takes a while before it knows it is dead.
column2: 恐爆龍力大但腦笨。要過好一會兒,牠才曉得自己死了。

column1: Watch my actions. Move before I do.
column2: 看我的動作。趕在我之前先動。

column1: Keep enemy guessing what your next action will be.
column2: 讓敵人猜不透你下一步要幹嘛。

column1: Don't watch my weapon -- watch my eyes. Let them show you where I will hit and when.
column2: 別盯我的武器——盯我的眼睛。讓那雙眼告訴你我會打哪兒、什麼時候打。

column1: Put your body into your thrusts.
column2: 刺擊時把整個身子的勁都使上。

column1: Brigands can't take a lot of damage. Keep up your attacks.
column2: 強盜挨不了多少傷害。攻勢別停。

column1: Scorpion always wiggles tail before it strikes. Be ready to back away.
column2: 蠍子出手前總會擺尾。隨時準備往後退。

column1: Jackalmen may be cowards, but they do fight well in large numbers. Keep weapon on one at a time. Take it out as fast as possible.
column2: 豺狼人或許是膽小鬼,但牠們群起圍攻可真會打。一次盯死一隻,儘快解決掉。

column1: You lower your weapon and yield the combat.
column2: 你放下武器,認輸了。

column1: You not be remembering rules of practice combat. Come back when you ready to fight fair.
column2: 你忘了練習對戰的規矩。等你準備好公平打的時候再回來。

column1: You taught Uhura a lesson.
column2: 你給烏胡拉上了一課。

column1: Uhura Info:
column2: 烏胡拉情報:

column1: You are good, very good. Soon you will be a master with your sword.
column2: 你很行,非常行。要不了多久,你就會成為劍術大師。

column1: However, the real battle is not always won by a swing or a thrust. To fight with honor is not always easy.
column2: 然而,真正的戰鬥未必靠一揮一刺取勝。帶著榮譽去戰鬥,從來不是件容易的事。

column1: Sometimes the way of honor will seem foolish to those that have none. Nevertheless, without honor, there is no victory. With honor, there is no real defeat.
column2: 有時,榮譽之道在那些沒有榮譽的人眼中顯得愚蠢。儘管如此,沒有榮譽,就沒有勝利;有了榮譽,便沒有真正的失敗。

column1: When fighting against a sword, do not let a swing catch you off guard. You can be disarmed that way.
column2: 對上持劍的對手時,別讓一記揮砍打你個措手不及。那樣會被繳了械。

column1: Do not let yourself be distracted by thoughts. Let yourself be one with your weapon.
column2: 別讓雜念分了你的心。讓自己與武器合而為一。

column1: Let your motion flow smoothly as you dodge. Always remain in balance.
column2: 閃避時,讓動作流暢自然。永遠保持平衡。

column1: Stop trying to hit me and hit me!
column2: 別光想著打中我,直接打過來啊!

column1: A combat fought without honor brings no honor onto the combatant. Perhaps we shall practice again when you have learned the meaning of honor.
column2: 不以榮譽而戰,便不能為戰士帶來榮譽。等你領悟了榮譽的真諦,我們或許可以再切磋一次。

column1: You force Rakeesh to yield the combat.
column2: 你逼得拉基什認輸收手。

column1: What a monster
column2: 好個怪物

column1: You just became Scorpion shish-ka-bob.
column2: 你瞬間成了蠍子肉串。

column1: Get the point?
column2: 嚐到刺的滋味了吧?

column1: You have dealt the Scorpion your own deadly sting.
column2: 你以致命的一擊回敬了這隻蠍子。

column1: You have defeated the mighty Terrorsaurus.
column2: 你擊敗了強悍的恐懼龍。

column1: You have triumphed gloriously over the Brigand.
column2: 你光榮地戰勝了這名強盜。

column1: You make a run for the darkened doorway, hoping for it to be another way out.
column2: 你朝那扇昏暗的門奔去,指望它是另一條出路。

column1: Unfortunately, the darkened room appears to be filled with an unknown number of spectators. You are quickly overwhelmed, grabbed, and thrown out.
column2: 可惜那昏暗的房間裡似乎擠滿了不知多少看客。你轉眼就被制伏、抓住,丟了出來。

column1: It seems that whoever took your equipment also confiscated your dagger supply. You'll have to rely on your melee combat skills here.
column2: 看來搶走你裝備的人也順手沒收了你的匕首。在這裡你只能靠近身格鬥的本事了。

column1: You managed to beat the warrior at his own game.
column2: 你以其人之道,還治其人之身,打敗了這名戰士。

column1: It appears the soles of your protective boots have been damaged severely by the Ghoul's acidic bile.
column2: 你護靴的鞋底似乎已被食屍鬼的酸性膽汁嚴重腐蝕。

column1: The Ghoul's poisonous claws have made you too weak to continue. Drained of energy, you fall, and the Ghoul quickly finishes killing you.
column2: 食屍鬼的毒爪讓你虛弱得撐不下去。氣力耗盡,你倒了下去,食屍鬼迅速結束了你的性命。

column1: Too Tired To Stand
column2: 累得站不起來

column1: What good is killing monsters if you don't live to gloat over it?
column2: 要是活不到自鳴得意的那一刻,殺再多怪物又有什麼用?

column1: The Ghoul just discovered the meaning of splash damage.
column2: 食屍鬼剛剛體會到什麼叫濺射傷害。

column1: The Ghoul shatters to pieces.
column2: 食屍鬼碎成了一塊塊。

column1: The Ghoul's body clatters to the sand.
column2: 食屍鬼的屍體嘩啦啦地散落在沙地上。

column1: Ew! It seems the creature's bile has strong corrosive properties. Thank goodness the soles of your boots are as thick as a Saurus skull.
column2: 噁!這生物的膽汁似乎有強烈的腐蝕性。還好你靴底厚得跟索魯斯的頭骨一樣。

column1: You have bested the Jackalman.
column2: 你制伏了這名豺狼人。

column1: You have bested the Jackalmen.
column2: 你制伏了這群豺狼人。

column1: No! Iblis is loose!
column2: 「不!伊布利斯掙脫了!」

column1: Alas, Poor Hero
column2: 唉,可憐的英雄

column1: As you fall in defeat, you dimly hear noise in the background as Iblis rises above the Palace.
column2: 你戰敗倒地時,隱約聽見遠處傳來騷動——伊布利斯正自宮殿之上騰起。

column1: Too tired to win
column2: 累得贏不了

column1: No, Master, we must get through.
column2: 不行,主人,我們非得闖過去不可。

column1: You just taught the palace guards that numbers don't always compensate for skill.
column2: 你剛剛教會了宮廷衛兵一個道理:人多不一定彌補得了技不如人。

column1: Only one man will leave this room, and that's the man left standing. Better make sure it's you.
column2: 這房裡只有一個人能活著走出去,那就是最後站著的那位。最好確保那是你。

column1: After a tough battle, Khaveen finally receives his mortal wound at your hands.
column2: 一番苦戰之後,哈維因終於在你手中受了致命一擊。

column1: For all the fighting you have done before, you have finally met your match. You collapse to the ground.
column2: 打了這麼多場仗,你終於遇上了棋逢敵手。你癱倒在地。

column1: As you see Khaveen readying his sword, you realize he means business and quickly kneel before him.
column2: 你見哈維因架起劍來,曉得他是來真的,連忙跪倒在他面前。

column1: As you grovel upon the floor in front of Khaveen, he takes the opportunity to kick you.
column2: 你在哈維因面前趴伏求饒,他趁機踹了你一腳。

column1: Fool! I will enjoy dealing with you later. Guards, drag him away to the dungeon.
column2: 蠢貨!容後再好好收拾你。衛兵,把他拖去地牢。

column1: You shake your head in defiance.
column2: 你不屈地搖了搖頭。

column1: When I say yield, I mean yield!
column2: 我叫你認輸,你就給我認輸!

column1: You tell Khaveen you won't yield to him, no matter what.
column2: 你告訴哈維因,無論如何你都不會向他低頭。

column1: I said YIELD!!
column2: 我說了——認輸!!

column1: You dare Khaveen to kill you in cold blood.
column2: 你激哈維因,有種就冷血地殺了你。

column1: In response, Khaveen raises his sword to strike.
column2: 哈維因應聲舉劍,作勢要砍。

column1: No need to provoke Khaveen even more. He's already preparing to finish you off.
column2: 犯不著再去激哈維因了。他已經準備要了結你了。

column1: As your eyes dart to your sword, Khaveen moves his blade closer to your chest. Trying to retrieve your blade now would be suicide.
column2: 你的目光瞥向自己的劍,哈維因隨即把劍鋒逼近你的胸口。這時候想去撿劍,無異於自尋死路。

column1: You feel Khaveen's gaze following yours as you look at your weapon again. He's still watching your every move.
column2: 你再次望向自己的武器時,感覺到哈維因的目光也跟著移了過去。他仍盯著你的一舉一動。

column1: You order Khaveen to yield and grovel before you.
column2: 你命令哈維因認輸,並趴伏在你面前求饒。

column1: You tell Khaveen to yield or perish.
column2: 你警告哈維因:要嘛認輸,要嘛受死。

column1: I shall not yield!
column2: 我絕不認輸!

column1: You order Khaveen to grovel once more.
column2: 你再次命令哈維因趴下求饒。

column1: Taking his chances, Khaveen bends down and grabs his sword before you can react.
column2: 哈維因賭上一搏,趁你來不及反應,彎腰一把抓起了他的劍。

column1: You tell Khaveen you will not kill in cold blood and order him to retrieve his weapon.
column2: 你告訴哈維因你不會冷血殺人,並命他撿回武器。

column1: Khaveen seems genuinely surprised, but follows your order.
column2: 哈維因看來是真的吃了一驚,但還是照你的話做了。

column1: Noticing your indecision, Khaveen bends down and grabs his sword before you can react.
column2: 哈維因察覺你的猶豫,趁你來不及反應,彎腰一把抓起了他的劍。

column1: Ask not what you can do for your sword... Ask what your sword can do for you!
column2: 別問你能為你的劍做什麼……問問你的劍能為你做什麼!

column1: Khaveen slashed you.
column2: 哈維因砍中了你。

column1: Right idea, but horrible timing.
column2: 主意不錯,可惜時機糟透了。

column1: Make Plan B harder to anticipate.
column2: 讓B計畫別那麼容易被料中。

column1: Next time you challenge a high-ranking Raseirian officer, make sure you're in condition to win.
column2: 下次要挑戰拉希爾的高階軍官,先確定自己有贏的本錢。

column1: Out of shape?
column2: 狀態不行啊?

column1: You try to dodge and run past Khaveen, but his skill prevents you from doing so. Besides, he'd be on top of you again before you could get that door open.
column2: 你想閃過哈維因衝過去,可他的身手讓你過不了關。何況你還沒來得及打開那扇門,他就又撲到你身上了。

column1: That's it. Beat him over the head with your shield.
column2: 就是這樣。拿盾牌往他腦袋上招呼。

column1: Khaveen receives his mortal wound at your hands.
column2: 哈維因在你手中受了致命一擊。

column1: Hmph, so you think you're skilled enough for a rematch? Don't expect me to hold back on you.
column2: 哼,你以為自己有本事再戰一場?別指望我會手下留情。

column1: You've had your time to gloat! Now it's time to bleed.
column2: 得意夠了吧!現在該流血了。

column1: Taking advantage of your indecision, Khaveen performs a surprisingly accurate slash to your neck, cutting your jugular.
column2: 哈維因趁著你猶豫,出人意料地精準一劃,割中你的脖子,劃斷了頸動脈。

column1: Hah! That was easy. Too easy. And Ad Avis called you a hero! I'm disappointed.
column2: 哈!太輕鬆了,輕鬆過頭了。阿德·阿維斯竟還稱你為英雄!真讓我失望。

column1: Now yield to me and grovel on the floor. I might consider sparing your life. Yield!
column2: 現在向我認輸,趴在地上求饒。我或許會考慮饒你一命。認輸吧!

column1: In an act of desperation, you bend down and reach for your sword. Unfortunately, Khaveen anticipated your action and brings his sword down upon your skull with all his might.
column2: 你狗急跳牆,彎腰去抓自己的劍。可惜哈維因早料到了這一手,使盡全力一劍劈向你的腦門。

column1: Good for you, Master!
column2: 幹得好,主人!

column1: Taking advantage of Khaveen's indecision, you end the fight with a vicious slash to Khaveen's throat.
column2: 你趁著哈維因猶豫,凶狠地一劍劃過他的咽喉,結束了這場戰鬥。

column1: Since you're dead, you can't see anything.
column2: 你都死了,什麼也看不見啦。

column1: Khaveen is closely watching you with a mocking smile on his face.
column2: 哈維因臉上掛著譏諷的笑,緊盯著你。

column1: Khaveen's confident grin has given way to a worried expression.
column2: 哈維因那自信的笑容已換成了憂慮的神情。

column1: You're not in a very enviable situation. Better think of something, and soon!
column2: 你的處境可不怎麼妙。最好快點想個辦法!

column1: Thanks to your quick learning skills, you're once again in control of the situation.
column2: 多虧你學得快,局面又重新回到了你的掌控之中。

column1: Quit sightseeing and watch what you're doing!
column2: 別光顧著看風景,專心一點!

column1: Your sword lies on the ground at your feet.
column2: 你的劍就躺在你腳邊的地上。

column1: Khaveen's sword lies on the ground at his feet.
column2: 哈維因的劍就躺在他腳邊的地上。

column1: You don't need to pick up Khaveen's weapon. If you want to finish him off, you should just use your own blade.
column2: 你犯不著去撿哈維因的武器。想了結他,用自己的劍就行了。

column1: There's no place for you to hide, and the Griffin's mobility is vastly superior to your own. Better save your breath for combat.
column2: 你無處可躲,而獅鷲的機動力遠遠勝過你。還是把力氣留著戰鬥吧。

column1: You showed the Griffin who's boss.
column2: 你讓這頭獅鷲見識了誰才是老大。

column1: You find out you don't compress nearly as neatly as the thing that just ate you.
column2: 你發現自己遠不如剛把你吞下肚的那玩意兒那麼好壓縮。

column1: You managed to beat the sauce out of the creature.
column2: 你硬是把這生物打得稀巴爛。

column1: The co-pilot is unavailable for this battle.
column2: 這場戰鬥無法使用副手。

column1: The city is destroyed. Great job, Hero!
column2: 整座城都毀了。幹得好啊,英雄!

column1: Environmental crisis
column2: 環境浩劫

column1: You did your best, but it just wasn't good enough.
column2: 你已經盡了全力,可惜還是不夠看。

column1: The end of the world!
column2: 世界末日!

column1: The Saurus is an amazing beast. This steed must have remained at the gate, awaiting your future need, while you carried on your business in Raseir.
column2: 索魯斯真是頭了不起的猛獸。你在拉希爾辦事的這段時間,這頭坐騎想必一直守在城門邊,等著你日後差遣。

column1: It appears your Saurus found its way back on its own.
column2: 看來你的索魯斯自己找路回來了。

column1: You can't reach your Saurus from this distance.
column2: 這距離你搆不著你的索魯斯。

column1: Kuwait a minute. Visa please!
column2: 等等(科威特)一下嘛。簽證請出示!

column1: You realize that you have left your visa in the desert, so you improvise.
column2: 你發覺自己把簽證落在沙漠裡了,只好臨機應變。

column1: You realize that you have no visa, so you improvise.
column2: 你發覺自己根本沒有簽證,只好臨機應變。

column1: What's this? We don't take Dinar's Club here.
column2: 這是什麼?我們這兒可不收「第納爾俱樂部卡」。

column1: You are turned back into the desert.
column2: 你被趕回了沙漠。

column1: Your papers are in order. By order of Khaveen, you must have a pleasant stay.
column2: 你的證件沒問題。奉哈維因之命,祝你住得愉快。

column1: Stay where you are, stranger, or you'll discover how sharp a weapon can be.
column2: 別動,陌生人,否則你就要見識見識刀劍有多鋒利了。

column1: So you are the one they call the Hero of Shapeir. Well, understand this: we don't need no stinking heroes around here. Do I make myself clear?
column2: 原來你就是他們口中那位夏皮爾的英雄。好,給我聽清楚:這裡可不需要什麼臭英雄。我說得夠明白了嗎?

column1: You are under arrest for breaking the law of Raseir on this day, the 29th of the Serpent, Year of the Djinn. Come quietly, or you will be taken by force.
column2: 你因觸犯拉希爾律法被捕——本日為精靈之年蛇月二十九日。乖乖就範,否則就動粗硬帶。

column1: You are under arrest for breaking the law of Raseir on this day of the Serpent, Year of the Djinn. Come quietly, or you will be taken by force.
column2: 你因觸犯拉希爾律法被捕——本日為精靈之年蛇月。乖乖就範,否則就動粗硬帶。

column1: You briefly consider struggling with the guards, but realize that there are just too many of them.
column2: 你一度想跟衛兵們搏一搏,卻發覺他們人數實在太多了。

column1: You there! Halt! It is forbidden for you to be out at night. You are guilty of breaking the law of Raseir.
column2: 那邊那個!站住!夜間外出是禁止的。你觸犯了拉希爾律法。

column1: You make the thief sign to the guards. They look down, then turn aside and ignore you.
column2: 你向衛兵們打出盜賊暗號。他們垂下目光,隨即轉過身去,不再理會你。

column1: Do you not know it is forbidden to be out after dark in Raseir? Show me your visa.
column2: 你不曉得在拉希爾天黑後外出是禁止的嗎?把你的簽證拿出來。

column1: You show the guard your visa.
column2: 你向衛兵出示簽證。

column1: You must go immediately to the Blue Parrot Inn. If you are found again outside at night, you will be taken to prison.
column2: 你必須立刻前往藍鸚鵡客棧。要是再被發現夜間在外遊蕩,就把你關進大牢。

column1: You make an excuse to the guard about why you don't have your visa with you.
column2: 你向衛兵找了個藉口,解釋為何沒帶著簽證。

column1: The law states you must carry your visa with you at all times. You are hereby under arrest.
column2: 律法規定你必須隨身攜帶簽證。你就此被捕。

column1: You picked the wrong time to annoy the local authorities. Failure to show your visa provided the guards with a nice excuse to throw you in jail.
column2: 你挑錯了時機去惹當地官府。拿不出簽證,正好給了衛兵把你扔進牢裡的好藉口。

column1: Bad ID
column2: 證件不符

column1: Caught without your visa, you are hauled off to prison for a long, long stay.
column2: 你因沒帶簽證被逮個正著,被拖去大牢,要關上好長好長一段日子。

column1: Get a life -- Get a Visa
column2: 做人要長進——先去辦張簽證吧

column1: The guards start to yell for reinforcements, and before you know it, you are surrounded and overwhelmed. They drag you to the dungeons of Raseir for a most unpleasant stay.
column2: 衛兵們開始大喊著叫援兵,你還沒回過神來,就已經被團團圍住、徹底制伏。他們把你拖進拉希爾的地牢,讓你嚐嚐最難受的滋味。

column1: No one is permitted to leave the city today without letters of envoy, by order of Khaveen.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,今日無使節文書者,一律不准出城。

column1: No one without papers is allowed to pass!
column2: 沒有證件的一律不准通過!

column1: By order of Khaveen, you are not allowed to leave the city without showing your visa, yellow-haired dog.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,黃毛狗,不出示簽證就不准出城。

column1: By order of Khaveen, foreign dog, you are not allowed to leave. Let me see your visa.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,外鄉狗,不准你離開。把簽證給我看看。

column1: By order of Khaveen, Infidel of Shapeir, you may not pass through these gates unless I see your visa.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,夏皮爾來的異教徒,不讓我看到簽證,你休想通過這道城門。

column1: By order of Khaveen, you who come to this city unbidden may not leave as easily as you arrived. Show me your visa.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,你這不請自來之徒,可沒那麼容易說走就走。把簽證拿出來。

column1: By order of Khaveen, you dog, you may not pass without showing your visa.
column2: 奉哈維因之命,你這條狗,不出示簽證就別想通過。

column1: You have no visa? Then you may not pass!
column2: 你沒有簽證?那就別想通過!

column1: Your papers are in order. You may pass.
column2: 你的證件沒問題,可以通過。

column1: Do I make myself clear?
column2: 我說得夠明白了嗎?

column1: Stubborn, aren't you? Well, perhaps some time spent in prison would teach you some manners. To avoid prison in Raseir, you must learn to obey orders.
column2: 挺嘴硬的嘛?好,也許在牢裡關上一陣子能教教你規矩。在拉希爾想不進大牢,就得學會服從命令。

column1: See that you obey all the rules posted around town. Rule number one: strangers must always carry their visa. Do you have your visa?
column2: 城裡張貼的規矩你都得乖乖遵守。第一條:外來者必須隨身攜帶簽證。你的簽證帶了嗎?

column1: Already breaking the rules, are you?
column2: 這就開始壞規矩了是吧?

column1: Here is your visa. Don't leave town without it.
column2: 這是你的簽證。出城別忘了帶上。

column1: It remains a dismal sight.
column2: 那景象依舊一片淒涼。

column1: Guards! There is an intruder in Fountain One. To me!
column2: 衛兵!一號噴泉這裡有人闖入。都到我這兒來!

column1: You see several people gathered in the south part of the plaza.
column2: 你看見廣場南側聚集了幾個人。

column1: After all the trouble you took to get your hands on that raven, it's really a shame you couldn't take any more damage.
column2: 費了那麼大的勁才弄到那隻渡鴉,結果你卻撐不住再多挨一下傷,實在可惜。

column1: After all the effort you took to get up there, it's really a shame you couldn't take any more damage coming down.
column2: 費了那麼大的力氣才爬上去,結果下來時你卻撐不住再多挨一下傷,實在可惜。

column1: You should have tended to your healing, but now it's too late.
column2: 你早該顧著療傷的,可惜現在已經太遲了。

column1: You fell for it!
column2: 你上當啦!

column1: Looks like they were waiting for you. Maybe you should have made them wait a bit longer before you returned.
column2: 看來他們早就在等你了。也許你該讓他們再多等一會兒,再回來才對。

column1: Many unhappy returns
column2: 又是一場不愉快的歸來

column1: You quickly learn that Raseirian guards do not have a particularly high tolerance threshold. However, since you're dead, this lesson is of little benefit to you.
column2: 你很快就學到,拉希爾的衛兵忍耐度可不怎麼高。不過嘛,既然你已經死了,這堂課對你也沒什麼用了。

column1: You WERE warned!
column2: 早就警告過你了!

column1: All things come to those who wait. Looks like you waited too long, and look what came to you!
column2: 好東西總會留給懂得等待的人。可惜你等得太久了,瞧瞧降臨到你頭上的是什麼!

column1: Don't just stand there...
column2: 別光站在那兒發呆……

column1: You took too long to do your deed.
column2: 你拖太久,該做的事沒做成。

column1: This is none of your business. Go away.
column2: 這不關你的事。走開。

column1: No one may pass until Khaveen gives the order.
column2: 哈維因沒下令之前,誰都不准通過。

column1: You were told that you may not pass. Turn now or die!
column2: 已經跟你說過不准通過了。立刻掉頭,否則受死!

column1: Not right now.
column2: 現在不行。

column1: You there, stop!
column2: 喂,你那個,站住!

column1: It is forbidden to be out at night. You are in violation of the laws of Raseir.
column2: 夜間外出乃是禁令。你已經觸犯了拉希爾的律法。

column1: That would only bring more guards.
column2: 那只會招來更多衛兵。

column1: The fountain is as dry as a bone. You can't fill your waterskins here.
column2: 噴泉乾得像枯骨。你沒法在這裡裝滿水袋。

column1: The fountain is as dry as a bone. You can't fill your waterskin here.
column2: 噴泉乾得像枯骨。你沒法在這裡裝滿水袋。

column1: Something in the back of your mind tells you to hold on to the Water Elemental.
column2: 你心底深處有個聲音提醒你,先別放掉那隻水元素。

column1: The plaza seems to be busy today.
column2: 今天廣場似乎挺熱鬧。

column1: The fountain in Raseir's Plaza of the Fountain is cracked and dry.
column2: 拉希爾噴泉廣場上的這座噴泉,早已龜裂乾涸。

column1: You cannot reach the barred window.
column2: 你搆不到那扇裝了鐵柵的窗。

column1: The window is out of reach.
column2: 那扇窗搆不著。

column1: Do not interfere!
column2: 別來礙事!

column1: Hero, you must help me! Do something!
column2: 英雄,你一定要救救我!想想辦法呀!

column1: The swords of the guards press deeper into your skin.
column2: 衛兵的刀刃更深地壓進你的皮肉。

column1: There are no heroes in this town. You of all men should know that, Ugarte.
column2: 這座城裡沒有什麼英雄。烏加特,你最該清楚這一點才對。

column1: I know very little. What is going on? I am innocent of any wrongdoing.
column2: 我什麼都不知道。到底怎麼回事?我可是清白的,什麼壞事都沒做。

column1: If you are so innocent, then what is this?
column2: 你既然這麼清白,那這是什麼東西?

column1: I don't know how that water bag could have gotten there. Someone must have planted it on me.
column2: 我不知道那個水袋怎麼會在那裡。一定是有人栽贓給我的。

column1: Waterskins under your tunic, Ugarte? You know that smuggling water is a crime against the state. You also know what we do to criminals like you.
column2: 烏加特,你長袍底下藏著水袋?你明知走私水是叛國重罪。你也很清楚,我們是怎麼處置你這種罪犯的。

column1: No, no, please. I am innocent! I do not know how it got there. No!
column2: 不,不,求求你們。我是清白的!我真不知道那東西怎麼會在那裡。不要啊!

column1: Hero... Help!
column2: 英雄……救我!

column1: This is the fate of anyone who breaks the rules of the city. Remember this, and see that you do not suffer the same fate.
column2: 這就是膽敢違反本城律法者的下場。給我記住,別讓自己落得同樣的結局。

column1: I hope you are smart enough to learn this lesson. You do not want to see the dungeons of Raseir.
column2: 我希望你夠聰明,能把這堂課學進去。你可不會想見識拉希爾的地牢。

column1: The water spilled by Ugarte.
column2: 烏加特灑出來的水。

column1: You can't get the spilled water.
column2: 那灘灑出來的水你拿不起來。

column1: The floor of the plaza is covered with dirt and filth. Who knows what you might catch if you tried drinking that water.
column2: 廣場的地面盡是塵土與汙穢。誰知道你要是喝了那水,會染上什麼病。

column1: This looks like a nice, quiet spot.
column2: 這地方看起來挺不錯,又安靜。

column1: Hey, where do you think you are going, Master? Iblis is over in the Palace. We haven't got much time. He's getting more powerful.
column2: 欸,主人,你以為你要往哪兒去?伊布利斯就在宮殿那邊。我們時間不多了。他的力量越來越強。

column1: You can't stop now! Iblis is starting to awaken!
column2: 你現在不能停下來!伊布利斯就要甦醒了!

column1: No, no! This way. This way!
column2: 不對,不對!這邊。走這邊!

column1: Wow, What do you know? It worked! My magic really worked!
column2: 哇,你看看!成功了!我的魔法真的奏效了!

column1: We are now in what you call Raseir. This is as close to Iblis as I could get.
column2: 我們現在就在你們所說的拉希爾。這已經是我能把你帶到離伊布利斯最近的地方了。

column1: There are some very powerful spells coming from that Palace over there. We don't have much time!
column2: 那邊的宮殿裡傳出某種非常強大的法術。我們時間不多了!

column1: Well, what are you waiting for? Let's go!
column2: 好啦,你還在等什麼?走吧!

column1: There is a soft whisper from the alleyway.
column2: 巷子裡傳來一陣輕聲的低語。

column1: Hero-friend! What magic you have at your command!
column2: 英雄朋友!你手上掌握的可是好厲害的魔法啊!

column1: The Underground is prepared to attack the Palace. If you need to enter there, we will provide some distraction for you. However, we are almost ready, so you must hurry before you are caught in our battle.
column2: 地下反抗軍已準備好攻打宮殿。你若需要進去,我們會替你製造一些聲東擊西的混亂。不過,我們也快動手了,你得趁早行動,免得被捲進我們的戰鬥裡。

column1: Sharaf silently sneaks off.
column2: 夏拉夫悄無聲息地溜走了。

column1: Halt, in the name of the law!
column2: 站住,以律法之名!

column1: Interfering with a Palace guard on duty is an offense. Move away or we'll take action.
column2: 妨礙宮殿衛兵執勤可是罪行。退開,否則我們就要動手了。

column1: You need to do something about the guards first.
column2: 你得先想辦法對付那些衛兵。

column1: The door is barred from the inside. You'll have to find another way into the Palace.
column2: 這扇門從裡頭閂上了。你得另找路進宮。

column1: You find the gate has been secured on the other side.
column2: 你發現這道門已從另一側鎖死。

column1: By golly, you discovered the tower guard house. Guess you should have tried the side with the scarf.
column2: 我的天,你居然摸到了塔樓的衛兵哨所。看來你早該走有圍巾的那一側才對。

column1: By golly, you discovered the tower guard house.
column2: 我的天,你居然摸到了塔樓的衛兵哨所。

column1: You don't have the strength to break down the door.
column2: 你的力氣還不夠把這扇門撞開。

column1: You there! Halt!
column2: 喂,你那個!站住!

column1: Guards, to me!
column2: 衛兵,到我這兒來!

column1: Timing is everything, and you chose the wrong time to get caught. You are being hauled back to the dungeon as time runs out.
column2: 時機就是一切,而你偏偏挑了最糟的時候被逮。時間一分一秒流逝,你被拖回了地牢。

column1: Guards! To the gates!
column2: 衛兵!守住大門!

column1: I got him! I got him!
column2: 我抓到他了!我抓到他了!

column1: Suddenly, you turn as you hear a rumbling and crashing from inside the Palace.
column2: 突然間,宮殿內部傳來一陣隆隆轟響與崩塌之聲,你猛地回過頭去。

column1: No! Iblis is loose!
column2: 不!伊布利斯掙脫了!

column1: A swarm of five guards comes storming out of the guard tower after you. You fight valiantly and desperately against overwhelming odds.
column2: 五名衛兵成群結隊地從衛兵塔衝出來追殺你。你寡不敵眾,卻仍奮勇拚死搏鬥。

column1: Suddenly, you and the guards turn as you hear a rumbling and crashing from inside the Palace.
column2: 突然間,宮殿內部傳來一陣隆隆轟響與崩塌之聲,你和衛兵們全都猛地回過頭去。

column1: A swarm of five guards comes storming out of the Palace after you. You fight valiantly and desperately against overwhelming odds.
column2: 五名衛兵成群結隊地從宮殿衝出來追殺你。你寡不敵眾,卻仍奮勇拚死搏鬥。

column1: Master, just hurry and get inside the Palace. We don't have much time.
column2: 主人,快點進宮去吧。我們時間不多了。

column1: He's coming after you!
column2: 他追上來了!

column1: It's a dead guard lying on the ground, bleeding all over the place while you just stare.
column2: 地上躺著一名衛兵的屍體,血流了一地,而你只是愣愣地盯著看。

column1: Yep, you killed him!
column2: 沒錯,他是你殺的!

column1: The guards look more relaxed.
column2: 衛兵們看起來放鬆了些。

column1: He doesn't want to shake your hand. He wants to kill you.
column2: 他可不想跟你握手。他想要你的命。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the dead guard.
column2: 別費勁去跟死掉的衛兵搭話了。

column1: My eyes! My eyes! I can't see!
column2: 我的眼睛!我的眼睛!我看不見了!

column1: Other Guard
column2: 另一名衛兵

column1: No, MY eyes! MY eyes are blinded!
column2: 不對,是「我」的眼睛!瞎掉的是「我」的眼睛!

column1: First Guard
column2: 第一名衛兵

column1: Oh, no you don't. MY eyes!
column2: 喔,你少來。是「我」的眼睛!

column1: I should think I would know if I were blind!
column2: 要是我瞎了,我自己怎麼會不曉得!

column1: I'm tellin' ya, I can't see!
column2: 我跟你說啦,我看不見了!

column1: If I put your eyes out, you'll think blind!
column2: 等我把你的眼珠子戳出來,你就知道什麼叫瞎了!

column1: I'm not gonna say it again. I'm blinded!
column2: 我不想再講第二遍。瞎的是我!

column1: Why, I oughta...
column2: 哼,我真該……

column1: I would if I could see ya!
column2: 要是我看得見你,我早就動手了!

column1: Both guards in unison
column2: 兩名衛兵異口同聲

column1: I can see!
column2: 我看得見了!

column1: Hey, what are the gates doing open?
column2: 欸,大門怎麼開著?

column1: The other guard can suddenly see again.
column2: 另一名衛兵突然又看得見了。

column1: What is going on? Guards! The gate!
column2: 出什麼事了?衛兵!守門!

column1: Come out and fight me like a man!
column2: 有種出來,像個男子漢跟我打一場!

column1: You have no choice but to fight.
column2: 你別無選擇,只能應戰。

column1: That wouldn't be useful while the guards are active.
column2: 趁衛兵還活蹦亂跳時用這招,沒什麼用。

column1: Now! Guards, get that intruder!
column2: 就是現在!衛兵,給我拿下那個闖入者!

column1: The gate is being attacked!
column2: 大門遭到攻擊了!

column1: Somebody HELP!
column2: 誰來救命啊!

column1: Defend the gate!
column2: 守住大門!

column1: All guards, to the gate!
column2: 全體衛兵,集合到大門!

column1: Guards! Guards! Guards! Guards!
column2: 衛兵!衛兵!衛兵!衛兵!

column1: Guards, to the gate!
column2: 衛兵,守住大門!

column1: Hey, this guy knows magic.
column2: 欸,這傢伙會魔法。

column1: No, Master! You don't have to fight them. We must stop Iblis!
column2: 不,主人!你不必跟他們纏鬥。我們得阻止伊布利斯才行!

column1: There are no guards to fight.
column2: 沒有衛兵可以打了。

column1: This guard's fighting days are over.
column2: 這名衛兵的征戰生涯到此為止了。

column1: Let's get him, Elija-One! We'll make short work of this hero.
column2: 上啊,伊萊賈一號!三兩下就能解決掉這個英雄。

column1: He's too dead to fight now.
column2: 他都死透了,還打什麼。

column1: As you cast the spell, the guards are at your side with swords drawn. Next time, be more selective where you cast your magic.
column2: 你才一施法,衛兵就拔刀逼到了你身旁。下次施展魔法,挑地方挑仔細點。

column1: Before you can even begin, the guards are at your side with swords drawn. Next time, be more selective where you cast your spells.
column2: 你連起手都還沒來得及,衛兵就拔刀逼到了你身旁。下次施展法術,挑地方挑仔細點。

column1: Hey, what are you doing there? Don't you know it's against the law?
column2: 欸,你在那邊幹嘛?難道你不知道那是違法的嗎?

column1: Well, since they know we're here, what do you do now, Master?
column2: 好吧,既然他們已經發現我們了,主人,你現在打算怎麼辦?

column1: You're not near the gate.
column2: 你離大門還遠著呢。

column1: You can't close the gate.
column2: 你關不上這道門。

column1: It's a scarf left by the harem girl to show the way.
column2: 這是後宮女子留下來指路的圍巾。

column1: The pillars are too slick to climb.
column2: 那些石柱太滑了,爬不上去。

column1: Master, don't go back down there. We must hurry.
column2: 主人,別再下去那邊了。我們得趕緊。

column1: You move deeper into the shadows to avoid the guards.
column2: 你往陰影深處退去,以躲開衛兵的視線。

column1: The plaza in front of the Palace gates is kept much cleaner than all the others.
column2: 宮殿大門前的這座廣場,維護得比其他廣場都要乾淨許多。

column1: This plaza faces the front of the Emir's Palace.
column2: 這座廣場正對著埃米爾宮殿的正門。

column1: Try using the magic rope near the pillars.
column2: 試試在那些石柱附近用魔法繩索。

column1: You have no need to pick locks up here, but you better get moving.
column2: 上了這兒,你用不著撬什麼鎖,不過你最好快點行動。

column1: The Palace doors are wide open.
column2: 宮殿的大門敞開著。

column1: It's a pile of dead guards, oozing blood all over the place while you just stare.
column2: 那是一堆衛兵的屍體,血淌了一地,而你只是愣愣地盯著看。

column1: There are two dead guards lying on the ground, bleeding all over the place while you just stare.
column2: 地上躺著兩具衛兵的屍體,血流了一地,而你只是愣愣地盯著看。

column1: You quickly retrieve all your daggers.
column2: 你迅速把所有飛刀都收了回來。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the dead guards.
column2: 別費勁去跟死掉的衛兵們搭話了。

column1: Interference with a guard in his duties is a punishable offense.
column2: 妨礙衛兵執行職務乃是要受罰的罪行。

column1: Loitering is forbidden. Move on!
column2: 禁止逗留。走開!

column1: Questioning a guard constitutes interference. Leave before it becomes an offense.
column2: 盤問衛兵就算妨礙公務。趁還沒構成罪行,快走。

column1: The guards, tiring of your chatter, grab you and call other guards to their assistance. You are dragged into prison and remain there for a long, long, long time.
column2: 衛兵被你嘮叨個沒完弄煩了,一把抓住你,還喊來其他衛兵幫忙。你被拖進牢裡,在裡頭關了好久、好久、好久。

column1: Curiosity killed the hero
column2: 好奇心害死英雄

column1: You cannot take the scarf.
column2: 你拿不走那條圍巾。

column1: You ride out to join in this new adventure, pausing only to watch the sunrise.
column2: 你騎馬出發,迎向這場嶄新的冒險,只在途中稍稍駐足,看一眼日出。

column1: Centaurs are best at finding their way across the desert.
column2: 半人馬最擅長在沙漠中辨明方向。

column1: The best way to get goods across the desert is by pack Saurus.
column2: 要把貨物運過沙漠,最好的辦法就是靠馱運的索魯斯。

column1: The Bedouin of the desert are good people, for the most part.
column2: 沙漠裡的貝都因人,大致上都是好人。

column1: Mighty muscular.
column2: 肌肉真結實。

column1: A "ship of the desert?"
column2: 所謂的「沙漠之舟」?

column1: Check out those biceps!
column2: 瞧瞧那二頭肌!

column1: What a buffed-out dude you are!
column2: 你這身肌肉練得真壯啊!

column1: What a dashing foreign devil you are!
column2: 你這個外邦人,還真是英姿煥發呢!

column1: The Palace of the Sultan
column2: 蘇丹的宮殿

column1: Will anyone speak for the Stranger of the North?
column2: 可有人願為這位來自北方的陌生人作保發言?

column1: We shall speak for %s.
column2: 我們願為 %s 作保發言。

column1: Who will speak for this Hero of Spielburg?
column2: 誰願為這位史畢柏格的英雄作保發言?

column1: Who will speak for this man of mercy?
column2: 誰願為這位慈悲之人作保發言?

column1: Who will speak for this man of kindness?
column2: 誰願為這位仁善之人作保發言?

column1: Who will speak for this man of courage?
column2: 誰願為這位勇敢之人作保發言?

column1: Who will speak for this man of honor?
column2: 誰願為這位榮譽之士作保發言?

column1: The Sultan shall speak for %s.
column2: 蘇丹願為 %s 作保發言。

column1: Is there anyone now who will speak for this son of the Sultan?
column2: 此刻可有人願為這位蘇丹之子作保發言?

column1: I shall speak for him.
column2: 我願為他作保發言。

column1: I shall speak for %s.
column2: 我願為 %s 作證。

column1: Then let him have a voice that he may speak.
column2: 那就讓他開口,讓他為自己發聲。

column1: You offer a dispel potion which was prepared by Harik for this occasion.
column2: 你拿出哈里克專為此刻調製的驅散藥水。

column1: You offer one of your dispel potions to the Saurus.
column2: 你拿出一瓶驅散藥水,遞向那頭索魯斯。

column1: We are Shameen and Shema, keepers of the Katta's Tail Inn. When the Katta were driven from Raseir, we journeyed to the northland in search of a hero.
column2: 我們是沙明與舍瑪,經營「貓尾客棧」。當年卡塔族被趕出拉希爾,我們便北上,踏上尋找英雄的旅程。

column1: We met %s.
column2: 我們遇見了 %s。

column1: Through his courage and skills, he restored the Baron's son and daughter and defeated the Brigand band terrorizing the valley.
column2: 他憑著勇氣與本事,救回了男爵的兒女,擊潰了肆虐山谷的盜匪。

column1: Through his courage and skills, he restored the Baron's son and daughter and banished the wicked Baba Yaga.
column2: 他憑著勇氣與本事,救回了男爵的兒女,驅逐了邪惡的芭芭雅嘎。

column1: He became Hero of Spielburg.
column2: 他成了史畢柏格的英雄。

column1: He journeyed with us back to Shapeir. Here he defeated the Elementals of Fire, Air, Earth, and Water to save our city.
column2: 他隨我們回到夏皮爾。在這裡,他擊敗了火、風、土、水四大元素精靈,拯救了我們的城市。

column1: He is a true hero.
column2: 他是個真正的英雄。

column1: I am Walid, a guard of the Palace and a member of the Eternal Order of Fighters.
column2: 我是瓦利德,宮廷衛兵,也是永恆戰士團的一員。

column1: This man vanquished me in a fair fight and demonstrated great skills in combat.
column2: 這位先生在堂堂正正的較量中擊敗了我,展現了高超的戰技。

column1: When I lay helpless upon the ground, he was given orders by the members of EOF to kill me.
column2: 當我倒地無力還手時,戰士團的人下令要他殺了我。

column1: Instead, he showed me mercy. This man is a true hero.
column2: 可他卻饒了我一命。這位先生是個真正的英雄。

column1: I am the Enchantress Aziza.
column2: 我是女術士阿姿莎。

column1: This man of his own free will went into the desert and sought the plant-woman Julanar.
column2: 這位先生出於自願,深入沙漠,去尋找化為樹的女子茱拉娜爾。

column1: Through his kindness and compassion, he allowed Julanar to forget the pain and suffering of her past and to again face life with joy.
column2: 他以仁慈與悲憫,讓茱拉娜爾放下過往的痛苦與磨難,重新帶著喜悅面對人生。

column1: This man also found a caged beast in the desert.
column2: 這位先生還在沙漠裡發現了一頭被囚的野獸。

column1: Though it was wicked and most foul, %s turned aside its wickedness and restored its humanity.
column2: 那野獸邪惡又汙穢,%s 卻化解了牠的惡念,使牠重拾人性。

column1: We shall hope it may learn from the kindness of the hero.
column2: 但願牠能從英雄的仁慈中有所領悟。

column1: This man is a true hero.
column2: 這位先生是個真正的英雄。

column1: I am Zayishah, daughter of the Emir Ali al-Din Hasan.
column2: 我是札伊莎,埃米爾阿里·阿丁·哈桑之女。

column1: In order to escape the repression of my homeland, Raseir, I asked this stranger for aid.
column2: 為了逃離故鄉拉希爾的暴政,我向這位異鄉人求助。

column1: At the risk to his own life, he helped me escape from the city.
column2: 他冒著性命危險,助我逃出了城。

column1: Emir Arus al-Din
column2: 埃米爾阿魯斯·阿丁

column1: When Ad Avis transformed me into a Saurus, I lost all hope of returning to my own form.
column2: 當阿德·阿維斯把我變成一頭索魯斯時,我已不抱任何變回原形的指望。

column1: Yet this man showed me his courage, kindness, and honor, and I knew that would restore me.
column2: 然而這位先生以他的勇氣、仁慈與榮譽,讓我明白:正是這些能還我本來面目。

column1: I am Harun al-Rashid, Sultan of the land of Shapeir.
column2: 我是哈倫·拉希德,夏皮爾國度的蘇丹。

column1: I am the Poet Omar, teller of tales for all to hear.
column2: 我是詩人奧瑪爾,為眾人說書講古的人。

column1: By the words which have been spoken,
column2: 憑著方才道出的這番話,

column1: By the deeds that have been done,
column2: 憑著已然成就的這些事,

column1: I proclaim this man a hero,
column2: 我宣告:這位先生是英雄,

column1: I now call this man my son.
column2: 我自今日起,稱他為吾子。

column1: This man has shown courage in the face of great danger,
column2: 這位先生臨大難而不懼,

column1: Kindness to those in need, generosity to the poor,
column2: 對困頓者仁慈,對窮苦人慷慨,

column1: Honesty in dealing with others, and mercy to his enemies.
column2: 待人以誠,待敵以恕。

column1: He is a man of great honor.
column2: 他是個極有榮譽的人。

column1: You tell Rakeesh that you are honored to accept the title.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,你深感榮幸,願意接受這個封號。

column1: Then you are now a paladin.
column2: 那麼,你如今便是聖騎士了。

column1: You tell Rakeesh that the honor is great, but that you feel the way of the paladin is not yours to pursue.
column2: 你告訴拉基什,這份榮耀固然崇高,但你覺得聖騎士之道並非你該追尋的路。

column1: Then I wish you good luck on the road you choose to follow.
column2: 那麼,願你在自己選擇的路上一帆風順。

column1: Welcome, Hero who aided Zayishah.
column2: 歡迎你,助過札伊莎的英雄。

column1: Quickly, you must hide before the eunuch passes. There, go behind the curtain.
column2: 快,趁太監還沒過來,你得趕緊躲起來。那邊,躲到簾子後面去。

column1: The Dark One, Ad Avis, can be seen from the balcony off the next room. All are forbidden to watch what he is doing.
column2: 從隔壁房間的陽台上,可以看見黑暗者阿德·阿維斯。任何人都不准偷看他在做什麼。

column1: If you wish to watch him, you will need to sneak through the next room. We will try to aid you by distracting one of the eunuchs.
column2: 若你想偷看他,就得溜過隔壁房間。我們會設法引開其中一個太監,助你一臂之力。

column1: Quickly, hide again.
column2: 快,再躲起來。

column1: This is the one who gave his clothes to Zayishah.
column2: 就是他,把衣裳讓給了札伊莎。

column1: How handsome he is.
column2: 他生得真俊。

column1: How strong his muscles are.
column2: 他的肌肉真結實。

column1: Lucky Zayishah.
column2: 札伊莎好福氣。

column1: If I had been her, clothes would not be the only thing we exchanged in that room.
column2: 要是換成我,那房裡可不只會交換衣裳而已。

column1: If you had been her, you never would have left that room.
column2: 要是換成你,那房門你怕是出不來了。

column1: How his hair shines like sunlight! His eyes, like starlight.
column2: 他的髮絲亮得像陽光!他的眼眸,宛如星輝。

column1: Already, you moon for him.
column2: 瞧,你已經為他害相思了。

column1: You must go now, before Abu the Eunuch comes back.
column2: 你得趕緊走,趁太監阿布還沒回來。

column1: Wait! A guard comes! Hide quickly!
column2: 等等!有衛兵來了!快躲起來!

column1: There is an uprising in the city and the people are trying to get into the palace!
column2: 城裡發生暴動,百姓正想衝進王宮!

column1: There is an uprising in the city and the people are trying to break down the gate!
column2: 城裡發生暴動,百姓正想撞破城門!

column1: You must watch out for strangers.
column2: 你得提防外人。

column1: A stranger?
column2: 外人?

column1: In here?
column2: 在這裡頭?

column1: Whatever would we do?
column2: 那我們可怎麼辦才好?

column1: (I can think of several things.)
column2: (我倒是想得出好幾樣。)

column1: But you, Barli, you will protect us from strangers.
column2: 不過有你在,巴利,你會保護我們不受外人侵擾的。

column1: No stranger could possibly get past you.
column2: 任誰也別想從你眼皮底下溜過去。

column1: This is, of course, true. No one can get past me.
column2: 那是自然。沒人能從我這兒過得去。

column1: Then perhaps you should go back to your watch.
column2: 那你或許該回去站崗了。

column1: We feel so safe.
column2: 我們覺得好安心。

column1: With you out there.
column2: 有你在外頭守著。

column1: No stranger would dare enter.
column2: 沒有哪個外人膽敢闖進來。

column1: (Enter what?)
column2: (闖進什麼?)

column1: Thank you for warning us.
column2: 謝謝你提醒我們。

column1: We will be very, very careful.
column2: 我們會非常、非常小心的。

column1: You know I will protect you.
column2: 你們知道我會護著你們的。

column1: Go, before some stranger tries to sneak upon the battlements.
column2: 去吧,免得哪個外人想偷溜上城牆。

column1: Who could do that? There are guards below!
column2: 誰能辦到啊?下頭都是衛兵呢!

column1: Go, before Khaveen catches you away from your post.
column2: 去吧,免得哈維因逮到你擅離崗位。

column1: Khaveen? Oh, my.
column2: 哈維因?哎喲,我的天。

column1: Hero, go behind the curtain! A guard comes.
column2: 英雄,躲到簾子後面去!有衛兵來了。

column1: What a pretty smile.
column2: 好甜的笑容。

column1: With teeth like pearl.
column2: 牙齒像珍珠一樣。

column1: And lips made for kissing.
column2: 還有那雙生來就該吻的唇。

column1: And a tongue made for...
column2: 還有那條舌頭,生來就該……

column1: Quickly, go to the doorway and sneak into the other room.
column2: 快,到門口去,溜進另一個房間。

column1: Couldn't he stay longer?
column2: 他就不能多待一會兒嗎?

column1: He just got here.
column2: 他才剛來呢。

column1: Quiet. Go now, Hero, before we all get caught.
column2: 安靜。快走,英雄,免得我們全都被逮著。

column1: It's a harem.
column2: 這是後宮閨房。

column1: Purple drapes frame the windows.
column2: 紫色的帷幔框著一扇扇窗。

column1: Several arched windows look out onto the surrounding parapet.
column2: 幾扇拱形窗戶望出去,正對著四周的女牆。

column1: You don't need to climb out the window.
column2: 你不必爬窗出去。

column1: This door leads into what appears to be the eunuch break room.
column2: 這道門通往的,看樣子是太監們的休息室。

column1: Many exotic pillows adorn the harem.
column2: 閨房裡點綴著許多異國風情的軟枕。

column1: It feels just like a harem!
column2: 這氣氛活脫脫就是後宮嘛!

column1: The drapes aren't meant to close. They're purely for decorative purposes.
column2: 這帷幔本就不是用來拉上的,純粹是擺好看的。

column1: It's an open archway. There's no door to open or close.
column2: 這是個敞開的拱門,沒有門可開可關。

column1: There's no time for lying around!
column2: 可沒空在這兒躺著閒晃!

column1: A bevy of scantily clad young maidens occupy the harem.
column2: 閨房裡聚著一群衣衫單薄的妙齡少女。

column1: You've heard of menage a trois, but this is ridiculous!
column2: 你聽過「三人行」,可這也太誇張了吧!

column1: Not now; you're pressed for time, Hero.
column2: 現在不行,英雄,你正趕著時間呢。

column1: More power to yellow potted flowers.
column2: 祝那盆黃花長得更旺吧。

column1: You can't get the flowers.
column2: 你拿不走那些花。

column1: It's a table with a decorative urn.
column2: 這是張桌子,上頭擺著個裝飾用的甕。

column1: You can't get the urn. (You didn't urn it.)
column2: 你拿不走那個甕。(這可不是你掙來的。)

column1: A low table, typical of Arabian decor.
column2: 一張矮桌,典型的阿拉伯擺設。

column1: Save your party tricks for another time, Hero.
column2: 把你的把戲留到別的時候耍吧,英雄。

column1: Good luck.
column2: 祝你好運。

column1: Come back soon.
column2: 早點回來啊。

column1: Perhaps later tonight?
column2: 或許今晚晚些時候?

column1: And the next night, and the next?
column2: 還有明晚,還有後晚?

column1: Such stories we could tell you.
column2: 我們有好多故事能說給你聽。

column1: Such tales we could make.
column2: 我們能編出好多風流韻事。

column1: Speaking of making tails...
column2: 說到「編尾巴」嘛……

column1: Your mind is always on that end.
column2: 你的心思老往那頭轉。

column1: If the means justify it.
column2: 只要那手段值當。

column1: Be careful. It is very dangerous out there. May fortune favor you, and may you go before you get caught!
column2: 小心點。外頭兇險得很。願好運眷顧你,願你在被逮著之前就脫身!

column1: Hey! You're not a harem girl; you're too ugly!
column2: 喂!你才不是後宮女子,你長得太醜啦!

column1: But then again, you are busted!
column2: 不過話說回來,你露餡啦!

column1: You were pressed for time, but only politicians can press the flesh and get away with it.
column2: 你忙得焦頭爛額,可只有那些政客才能上下打點還全身而退。

column1: Harem today, goon tomorrow
column2: 今日進後宮,明朝當囚徒

column1: Hurry, Hero!
column2: 快點,英雄!

column1: No man is allowed in the harem. The women here know how to keep secrets.
column2: 後宮不准任何男子進入。這裡的女子個個都懂得守口如瓶。

column1: You may keep the mirror, Effendi.
column2: 這面鏡子你留著吧,閣下。

column1: Mayzun does not want any of your possessions.
column2: 梅珠恩不要你的任何東西。

column1: No, Master! Not that way! You will be seen!
column2: 不,大人!別走那邊!你會被人看見的!

column1: Go quickly. Hide in the room and I will distract Hajib at the table.
column2: 快走。躲進房裡,我去把哈吉布引到桌邊。

column1: Hajib, would you please come here a minute? I wish to share some words with you.
column2: 哈吉布,能請你過來一下嗎?我有幾句話想跟你說。

column1: What is it, young and restless one?
column2: 什麼事啊,你這毛躁的小姑娘?

column1: Brothers! A stranger! Help!
column2: 弟兄們!有外人!快來人啊!

column1: You had better find a different hiding place, Master! A man is walking back this way.
column2: 大人,你最好換個地方躲!有人正往這邊走回來了。

column1: Now, hurry, Master. The guard may return any moment!
column2: 快點,大人,衛兵隨時都可能回來!

column1: No, Master! Choose your direction carefully, or you will be caught!
column2: 不,大人!走哪個方向得當心,不然你會被逮著!

column1: Please wait until it's safe, Master! You must not be caught!
column2: 大人,請等安全了再動!你千萬不能被逮著!

column1: You call that sneaking?
column2: 你管這叫潛行?

column1: You quickly discover that, despite the fact they are overweight, the eunuch guards know how to fight. You are quickly overwhelmed and captured.
column2: 你很快就發現,那些太監衛兵雖然個個肥胖,打起來卻有兩下子。你轉眼就被壓制、擒住了。

column1: It's all over but the crying!
column2: 大勢已去,只剩哭的份了!

column1: As they start to drag you away, you hear the Djinni scream, "He's loose! Iblis is free!" As the walls collapse around you, you think to yourself that maybe you should have been sneakier.
column2: 就在他們開始把你拖走時,你聽見那精靈尖叫:「他掙脫了!伊布利斯自由了!」四周的牆壁轟然倒塌,你心想:或許自己當時該再小心隱密一點才對。

column1: The world-famous spitting fish is the pride of Raseir.
column2: 這舉世聞名的噴水魚,可是拉希爾的驕傲。

column1: You can't do anything with the fountain.
column2: 這噴泉你什麼也做不了。

column1: The large baskets are probably empty.
column2: 那幾個大籃子大概是空的。

column1: There's nothing you can do with the baskets.
column2: 這些籃子你什麼也做不了。

column1: You have no desire to wake the sleeping eunuch.
column2: 你可沒興趣吵醒那個睡著的太監。

column1: You have no desire to announce your presence to the eunuch.
column2: 你可沒興趣讓那太監察覺你在這兒。

column1: Watch it! Don't rubber the wrong way.
column2: 當心!別亂揉,揉錯方向可不妙。

column1: There's nothing to do at the table.
column2: 這桌子旁沒什麼好做的。

column1: You have no need of a big, fat pillow... nor of the big, fat eunuchs who occupy them.
column2: 你既不需要又大又胖的軟枕……也不需要那些睡在枕上、又肥又大的太監。

column1: The table is made of hand-rubbed Iblia wood from the hills of the Forbidden City of Iblis.
column2: 這張桌子是用手工打磨的伊布利亞木製成的,木料採自伊布利斯禁城的山丘。

column1: The big, fat pillows are for big, fat eunuchs.
column2: 那些又大又胖的軟枕,是給又肥又大的太監用的。

column1: Don't bother talking to the table.
column2: 別費勁跟桌子說話了。

column1: Don't bother talking to the pillows.
column2: 跟枕頭講話是不會有回應的。

column1: Hey, no drinking on the job!
column2: 喂,上班時間不准喝酒!

column1: The carpet is finely woven of the best Shaperian wool. It looks like Tiram's work.
column2: 這張地毯是用最上等的夏皮爾羊毛細密織成的,看樣子是提拉姆的手藝。

column1: The inky black skies of Raseir are ablaze with stars.
column2: 拉希爾墨黑的夜空,繁星燦爛如火。

column1: You should try to reach your more immediate and urgent goals before attempting to reach for the stars.
column2: 先把眼前最緊急的目標一一達成,再去摘那天上的星星吧。

column1: From this vantage point you can see a balcony outside the door.
column2: 從這個位置望去,可以看見門外有一座露台。

column1: The doorway leads back into the harem.
column2: 這道門通回後宮。

column1: This room is just as grimy and gritty as streets of Raseir. Evidently, no luxuries are afforded for the eunuchs.
column2: 這房間和拉希爾的街道一樣髒兮兮、灰撲撲。顯然,宦官們是享受不到什麼好待遇的。

column1: Don't bother talking to the baskets.
column2: 跟籃子講話是不會有回應的。

column1: Don't bother talking to the fountain.
column2: 跟噴泉講話是不會有回應的。

column1: Don't bother talking to the port.
column2: 跟港口講話是不會有回應的。

column1: Don't bother talking to the torch.
column2: 跟火把講話是不會有回應的。

column1: Be careful what you touch in here.
column2: 在這裡碰東西可得小心一點。

column1: Master, keep your voice down! You must not be discovered!
column2: 主人,小聲點!可千萬不能被發現啊!

column1: You'll need to get there first, Hero.
column2: 你得先到那裡才行,英雄。

column1: You can't take the torch.
column2: 這火把你拿不走。

column1: But this is as far as you'll get!
column2: 不過你也只能走到這裡了!

column1: Good. I have been waiting for this moment.
column2: 很好。這一刻我已經等很久了。

column1: Let's see how much of a hero you really are.
column2: 就讓我瞧瞧你到底算是個多了不起的英雄。

column1: So! The would-be hero who thinks he can fight. I will not fight your petty magic!
column2: 哼!自以為能打的冒牌英雄。我才不屑跟你那點微不足道的法術過招!

column1: So! The would-be hero who thinks he can fight. Ready to take down Raseir's finest swordsman with a mere dagger? How pathetic!
column2: 哼!自以為能打的冒牌英雄。憑一把小匕首就想撂倒拉希爾第一劍客?真是可悲!

column1: You're not worth my time and effort.
column2: 你根本不值得我浪費時間和力氣。

column1: Guards! To me! Guards!
column2: 衛兵!到我這來!衛兵!

column1: Hurry, Master! You must follow me.
column2: 快,主人!你得跟著我走。

column1: You race through the palace passageways, pausing now and again to evade scurrying guards. The Djinni of the Ring, for all of his forgetful nature, seems to have a clear idea of where he's taking you.
column2: 你在宮殿的通道間飛奔,不時停下腳步,躲避四處奔走的衛兵。指環之精靈雖然生性健忘,這回卻對該把你帶往何處,似乎有著清晰的概念。

column1: Through here, Master! Iblis is close, and time is short.
column2: 從這邊走,主人!伊布利斯就在附近,時間不多了。

column1: There, Master. Through that door over there is the ritual chamber where Iblis is being summoned. Let's go!
column2: 就在那裡,主人。穿過那邊那道門,就是召喚伊布利斯的儀式廳。走吧!

column1: The people of the city are revolting, and Ad Avis has to choose this time of all times to do some sort of magical ceremony.
column2: 城裡的百姓正在暴動,而阿德·阿維斯偏偏挑這個節骨眼搞什麼魔法儀式。

column1: I am ordered to guard this door when I should be out leading the troops. A pox upon the head of all magicians. You will have to give the orders.
column2: 我本該在外頭領兵,卻被命令守著這道門。願瘟疫降在所有巫師的腦袋上。命令就由你來下吧。

column1: Let no one into the Palace. If a single person gets past you, I will have your head on a platter.
column2: 不准放任何人進宮。只要有一個人從你眼皮底下溜過去,我就把你的腦袋擱在盤子上端走。

column1: To hear is to obey, sir.
column2: 遵命,小的領命照辦,大人。

column1: Well, what are you waiting for? We need to get to that door.
column2: 喂,你還愣著幹嘛?我們得趕到那道門那兒去。

column1: Now, through the door at the top of the stairs is Iblis. There is some sort of spell on the door, but I don't know what it is. There is an awful lot of magic around here.
column2: 好了,穿過樓梯頂端那道門,就是伊布利斯了。那道門上有某種法術,可我也不曉得是什麼。這附近的魔法多得嚇人。

column1: Master, you've GOT to go through the door at the top of the stairs. Any other way will take too long. Please, it's almost too late.
column2: 主人,你「非」從樓梯頂端那道門進去不可。走別條路都太花時間了。拜託,快來不及了。

column1: So, you have managed to escape. Ad Avis does not know everything, it seems.
column2: 這麼說,你竟然逃出來了。看來阿德·阿維斯也不是無所不知嘛。

column1: Now, through the door at the top of the stairs is Iblis.
column2: 好了,穿過樓梯頂端那道門,就是伊布利斯了。

column1: You don't need the rope for anything now.
column2: 現在這條繩子你已經用不上了。

column1: Please, we've got to do something. Hurry!
column2: 拜託,我們得想想辦法。快點!

column1: Master! Iblis is about to be summoned! Don't just float there -- DO something!
column2: 主人!伊布利斯就要被召喚出來了!別只顧著漂在那兒——快「做」點什麼啊!

column1: Master! Iblis is about to be summoned! Don't just stand there -- DO something!
column2: 主人!伊布利斯就要被召喚出來了!別只顧著傻站在那兒——快「做」點什麼啊!

column1: Suddenly, the Djinni of the Ring starts to scream:
column2: 突然,指環之精靈尖叫起來:

column1: No! He's loose! Iblis is free!
column2: 不!他掙脫了!伊布利斯自由了!

column1: Whatever you did, it didn't work!
column2: 不管你剛才做了什麼,反正沒用!

column1: The walls around you start to shake and crumble. You have a funny feeling that you should have done something differently.
column2: 四周的牆壁開始搖晃、崩塌。你心裡隱隱有種感覺,剛才的做法八成哪裡不對。

column1: Your head still spinning from the fall, Khaveen's mocking laughter tells you that you made a very bad miscalculation.
column2: 墜落讓你腦袋還暈著,哈維因那嘲弄的笑聲告訴你:這回你可是大大地算錯了。

column1: As the guards haul you off for torture and questioning, the Djinni of the Ring starts to scream:
column2: 衛兵把你拖去拷問逼供,這時指環之精靈尖叫起來:

column1: Guards! The balcony! Guards!
column2: 衛兵!露台那邊!衛兵!

column1: That's blown it! We'd better run for it.
column2: 這下全砸了!我們最好快逃。

column1: Khaveen visibly relaxes and appears to lose track of his train of thought. For a moment, he seems not to know what to do.
column2: 哈維因肉眼可見地鬆懈下來,思緒彷彿一時斷了線。有那麼一瞬間,他似乎不知該如何是好。

column1: You did it! Now you must go on.
column2: 你成功了!現在快繼續前進吧。

column1: The only purpose that served was to get Khaveen's undivided attention.
column2: 這麼做唯一的效果,就是讓哈維因把全副注意力都放到你身上。

column1: But Master, the door to the ritual chamber is already open!
column2: 可是主人,儀式廳的門已經開了呀!

column1: The trap on the door is now disarmed, but you just alerted Khaveen to your presence.
column2: 門上的陷阱解除了,可你也因此驚動了哈維因,讓他發現你的行蹤。

column1: Why, there's a perfectly good rope, right here next to the balcony.
column2: 瞧,這兒就有一條好端端的繩子,就在露台旁邊。

column1: No, Master, you can't! We have to get to Iblis!
column2: 不行,主人,不能這樣!我們得趕去找伊布利斯啊!

column1: Hurry! Master! Iblis is about to be summoned!
column2: 快點!主人!伊布利斯就要被召喚出來了!

column1: You search Khaveen's body and find the golden key to his private treasury. He must have millions and millions of dinars. You're rich! Rich beyond your wildest dreams!
column2: 你搜了哈維因的屍體,找到一把通往他私人金庫的金鑰匙。他手上肯定有數不清的第納爾。你發了!發到連做夢都想不到的地步!

column1: Khaveen lies dead on the floor. He'll never harm anyone again.
column2: 哈維因倒斃在地。他再也害不了任何人了。

column1: Khaveen died before he realized what hit him. Not completely fair perhaps, but a lynch mob wouldn't have made it nearly as quick.
column2: 哈維因還沒搞清楚是怎麼回事就死了。這麼做或許不算光明正大,可換成暴民私刑,絕不會死得這麼痛快。

column1: Raseir's mightiest swordsman lies dead at your feet. You commend yourself for defeating such a skilled adversary.
column2: 拉希爾最強的劍客倒斃在你腳下。能擊敗如此高超的對手,你不禁為自己喝采。

column1: You congratulate yourself on getting rid of Khaveen.
column2: 你為自己除掉哈維因而暗自慶賀。

column1: You decide not to waste any more time on Khaveen.
column2: 你決定不再為哈維因浪費半點時間。

column1: A worthy opponent, you say to yourself.
column2: 「真是個值得一戰的對手。」你對自己這麼說。

column1: I fear you cannot reach the door handles from where you are standing.
column2: 恐怕從你站的地方,是搆不著那門把的。

column1: The Djinni tried to tell you about the door, but would you listen?
column2: 精靈早就想提醒你那道門的事,可你哪聽得進去?

column1: Oh, no, Mr. Big-Shot Hero. You had to go messing around with a magical fire trap, and you weren't even healthy enough to withstand a little bitty blast. Shame on you!
column2: 噢,不,大英雄先生。你偏要去招惹什麼魔法火焰陷阱,可你連那一丁點兒爆風都扛不住。真丟臉!

column1: What a blast
column2: 好一場爆炸

column1: You find that the door is locked. It won't open that easily.
column2: 你發現門鎖上了,沒那麼容易打開。

column1: With all the damage you took to get here, it's really a shame you couldn't take any more.
column2: 一路走來你已經傷得不輕,偏偏這回再也撐不住,實在可惜。

column1: What a smack. You might have survived that fall if it wasn't for all the extra weight you were carrying.
column2: 好一記重摔。要不是你身上背了一堆額外的重物,這一跤說不定還摔不死呢。

column1: You Fell for It
column2: 你上當了

column1: That hurt, but it's good that you're tough, agile, and know how to take a fall.
column2: 這一下是挺痛的,不過好在你夠結實、夠靈活,又懂得怎麼摔。

column1: Using the element of surprise to your advantage, you nimbly leap on top of Khaveen and deliver a fatal stab to his heart.
column2: 你趁其不備,靈巧地一躍而起,撲到哈維因身上,朝他心臟狠狠刺下致命一擊。

column1: Is it...?[It is! It's a simulacrum of the Pizza Elemental!
column2: 那是…?[沒錯!那是披薩元素精靈的化身!

column1: The floor appears to be made of elaborate marble.
column2: 地板看來是用精雕細琢的大理石鋪成的。

column1: It is the rope that ties the curtains together.
column2: 這是用來綁束簾幕的繩子。

column1: The Djinni tried to tell you about the door, but would you listen? Oh, no, Mr. Big-Shot Hero. You had to go messing around with a magical fire trap, and you weren't even healthy enough to withstand a little bitty blast. Shame on you!
column2: 精靈早就想提醒你那道門的事,可你哪聽得進去?噢,不,大英雄先生。你偏要去招惹什麼魔法火焰陷阱,可你連那一丁點兒爆風都扛不住。真丟臉!

column1: The balcony overlooks the antechamber to Ad Avis' ritual chamber.
column2: 從這座露台俯瞰下去,正是阿德·阿維斯儀式廳的前廳。

column1: This door leads out to the passageways of the Palace.
column2: 這道門通往宮殿的各條通道。

column1: These red drapes match the ones on the other side of the room.
column2: 這些紅色帷幔和房間另一頭的那些是成套的。

column1: The stairs are not as important as the chamber beyond the top of the stairs.
column2: 樓梯本身並不重要,要緊的是樓梯頂端後面那間廳室。

column1: The decorations and the balcony attest to the fact that this room is used for ceremony and special occasions. For you now, it is simply one more step along the path to the defeat of Ad Avis.
column2: 這些裝飾和那座露台都說明了,這房間是用來舉行典禮與特殊場合的。但對此刻的你而言,它不過是擊敗阿德·阿維斯這條路上的又一道關卡罷了。

column1: Twin axes flank the upper door.
column2: 一對巨斧分列在上方那道門的兩側。

column1: You do not have the skill required to wield one battleaxe, let alone two.
column2: 你連揮舞一把戰斧的本事都不夠,更別說兩把了。

column1: You're really axing for it.
column2: 你這可真是「斧」上門來找揍啊。

column1: So, you have managed to escape. You must either have a lot of skill or a lot of luck.
column2: 這麼說,你竟然逃出來了。看來你不是身手了得,就是運氣好得出奇。

column1: But neither will be of any use to you now. I will collect your head...
column2: 可這兩樣現在都救不了你了。我這就來取你的人頭…

column1: ...after scraping it off the flagstones!
column2: …等我先把它從石板地上刮下來再說!

column1: To your great relief, the enchanted rope resists the blow of Khaveen's sword.
column2: 讓你大大鬆了一口氣的是,那條附了魔的繩子擋下了哈維因揮來的一劍。

column1: Curses! Very well then. I'll just have to deal with you up close!
column2: 可惡!好得很。那我就只好近身來收拾你了!

column1: You glance down. You shiver for a moment, knowing that it's a long way down and there is no net.
column2: 你往下瞥了一眼,不禁打了個寒顫——這底下深得很,而且可沒張安全網接著你。

column1: Its magical power completely spent, the rope retracts again, dropping Khaveen down to the ground far below you.
column2: 魔力徹底耗盡後,繩子再度收了回去,把哈維因摔向你下方遠處的地面。

column1: There! Over there. Iblis is in the center of the ritual chamber. When the last candle is lit, Iblis will be loose. Let's go get him!
column2: 那裡!就在那邊。伊布利斯就在儀式廳的正中央。最後一根蠟燭一點燃,伊布利斯就會掙脫出來。我們去逮住他!

column1: Hear me, O Dark Powers! Light the darkness and bind Iblis to me forever!
column2: 聽我號令,黑暗之力啊!點亮這片幽冥,將伊布利斯永遠束縛於我!

column1: Ad Avis ended your meddling with one accurate shot. Next time, work on those reflexes, Hero.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯一記精準的攻擊,就終結了你的多管閒事。下回練練你的反應吧,英雄。

column1: Too slow to finish the job...
column2: 動作太慢,沒能把事辦成…

column1: When dealing with a barrier that stops any living being, killing yourself is a creative approach, but not necessarily a wise one.
column2: 面對一道能擋下任何活物的屏障,把自己弄死固然是個有創意的法子,卻未必是個聰明的法子。

column1: Know your boundaries...
column2: 認清自己的極限吧…

column1: A magical force makes it impossible to cast spells here.
column2: 有一股魔法力量,讓人在這裡施不出任何法術。

column1: Please, Master, we've got to get over there fast.
column2: 拜託,主人,我們得趕緊過去那邊才行。

column1: Please, Master, we are almost out of time!
column2: 拜託,主人,我們快沒時間了!

column1: Don't you have something you can use? We have to stop Iblis from getting free!
column2: 你身上難道沒什麼能用的東西嗎?我們非得阻止伊布利斯掙脫不可!

column1: Master, you've got to do something!
column2: 主人,你得想想辦法呀!

column1: Do something to knock down that last candle!
column2: 想個辦法把最後那根蠟燭弄倒!

column1: A candle! Iblis will get free!
column2: 一根蠟燭!伊布利斯就要掙脫了!

column1: Another candle! Iblis will be loose!
column2: 又一根蠟燭!伊布利斯就要自由了!

column1: There's only one candle left! Do something!
column2: 只剩最後一根蠟燭了!快想想辦法!

column1: We're too late! Look!
column2: 我們來遲了!你看!

column1: Shake, Rattle, and Roll!
column2: 又搖、又晃、又翻滾!

column1: The buildings start to collapse as Iblis rises up. You frantically hold on to the edge, but it crumbles in your hands and you start to fall. Your only consolation is the look of surprise on Ad Avis' face as the roof collapses on him.
column2: 伊布利斯升起的同時,周圍的建築開始崩塌。你死命攀著邊緣,可它卻在你手中碎裂,害你直直墜了下去。唯一讓你聊以自慰的,是屋頂砸向阿德·阿維斯時,他臉上那副錯愕的表情。

column1: The distance is too far for an accurate throw.
column2: 距離太遠,扔不準。

column1: It's already lit! You can't seem to knock it over!
column2: 它已經點燃了!你似乎沒辦法把它弄倒!

column1: The spell is already broken. You don't need to knock over any more candles.
column2: 法術已經破了。你不必再去弄倒任何蠟燭了。

column1: Ha! You're out of rocks. Now what will you do?
column2: 哈!你的石頭丟光了。現在你還能怎麼辦?

column1: Is that the best you can do, little man?
column2: 你就這點能耐嗎,小子?

column1: Ouch! I'll get you for that!
column2: 哎喲!這筆帳我跟你沒完!

column1: No! No! Master, help me!
column2: 不!不要!主人,救救我!

column1: Yeah! We did it!
column2: 太好了!我們成功了!

column1: The spell on the room is broken now, Master. We can go in.
column2: 房間裡的法術現在破了,主人。我們可以進去了。

column1: Thank You, Master. Now I can take Iblis back to his tomb where he will be safe for another thousand years and a year.
column2: 謝謝你,主人。現在我可以把伊布利斯帶回他的墓塚,讓他再被妥妥地封印一千零一年。

column1: I knew I would remember it. The real prophecy is...
column2: 我就知道我會想起來的。真正的預言是…

column1: Bye bye, Master. It's been fun!
column2: 再見囉,主人。跟你在一起真開心!

column1: NO! After seventy years, no!
column2: 不!熬了七十年,竟然功虧一簣!

column1: Idiot! You have destroyed my spell. Now you shall be destroyed!
column2: 蠢貨!你毀了我的法術。現在輪到你被毀滅了!

column1: I think you made him angry, Master. Better duck!
column2: 我看你把他惹火了,主人。快蹲下躲一躲!

column1: The Djinni did his best to warn you. You are about to become road nachos. Let's hope you saved your game recently.
column2: 精靈已經盡力警告過你了。眼看你就要被輾成一灘肉醬。但願你最近有存過檔。

column1: Missed us! Let's get him, Master! Go over to the other pillar where you can get back at that wicked old human.
column2: 沒打中我們!上吧,主人!到另一根柱子那邊去,你就能反擊那個邪惡的老頭子了。

column1: Better steer clear, boss. This one is big.
column2: 老大,最好閃遠一點。這一招來頭可不小。

column1: I shall destroy you!
column2: 我要毀滅你!

column1: I warned yooooouuuu!
column2: 我可警告過你囉~~~!

column1: Master, hurry up! He's coming after you!
column2: 主人,快點!他追上來了!
