column1: Careful, Master. There is some sort of spell between the pillars. It won't let any magic or anything alive get through from outside. Even so, somehow we've got to stop the last candle from being lit.
column2: 小心,主人。柱子之間有某種法術,不讓任何魔法或活物從外頭穿過去。即便如此,我們還是得設法阻止最後那根蠟燭被點燃。

column1: Excruciating pain pierces through your leg as Khaveen's sword slices right through the muscles of your calf. It's impossible to remain standing, much less keeping your balance.
column2: 哈維因的劍直直劃開你小腿的肌肉,劇痛貫穿整條腿。你根本站不住,更別提保持平衡了。

column1: Your life passes before your eyes, or is it just the other buildings? The magical rope runs out of power as soon as you hit the ground, sending Khaveen right after you.
column2: 你這一生在眼前飛快閃過——還是說,那只是旁邊的建築物?你一落地,那條魔法繩子的力量便耗盡了,緊接著把哈維因也一併摔了下來。

column1: Sadly, there's no time for Raseir to rejoice over Khaveen's demise, as Iblis rises up and destroys the city.
column2: 可惜,拉希爾根本來不及為哈維因之死歡慶,因為伊布利斯已然升起,將整座城市摧毀殆盡。

column1: Your life passes before your eyes, or is it just the other buildings? As you are about to become street pizza, let's hope you saved your game recently.
column2: 你這一生在眼前飛快閃過——還是說,那只是旁邊的建築物?眼看你就要被拍成一張街頭披薩,但願你最近有存過檔。

column1: It's the statue that has held Iblis for a thousand years and a year.
column2: 就是這尊雕像,封印了伊布利斯整整一千零一年。

column1: The only thing you can get right now is killed.
column2: 你現在唯一能「得到」的,就是把自己給害死。

column1: You don't have any water left.
column2: 你的水已經一滴不剩了。

column1: You wet the candle, but that doesn't seem to stop its magic.
column2: 你把蠟燭弄濕了,可這似乎止不住它的魔力。

column1: There's not enough air left to blow down the candles. The Elemental is gone.
column2: 剩下的氣已經不夠把蠟燭吹倒了。那元素精靈已經消失了。

column1: That can't be used on the ritual candle.
column2: 那東西沒辦法用在儀式蠟燭上。

column1: No, no, Master. Iblis is the OTHER way. Let's go!
column2: 不對,不對,主人。伊布利斯在「另一」邊。走吧!

column1: It is a flickering flame.
column2: 那是一簇搖曳的火焰。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 別費勁去跟伊布利斯的雕像講話了。

column1: Master, you need to concentrate on what you're doing!
column2: 主人,你得專心一點,看好自己在幹嘛!

column1: Don't fall! It's a long way down.
column2: 可別掉下去!這底下深得很。

column1: No, Master. You'd only get yourself killed.
column2: 不行,主人。你這麼做只會把自己害死。

column1: Khaveen is following you across the rope, and he's closing in fast.
column2: 哈維因正沿著繩子追過來,而且越逼越近。

column1: Save that for when you have solid ground beneath your feet again.
column2: 等你腳踏實地了再說吧。

column1: He's out of your reach. Better keep it that way, too.
column2: 他在你構不到的地方。最好就讓他待在那兒。

column1: Further conversation is useless. You already know what he's intending to do.
column2: 再說下去也沒用。你已經知道他打算做什麼了。

column1: It's your magic rope... you know, the one you've carried with you for days and days.
column2: 這是你的魔法繩……你帶在身上好幾天的那條。

column1: The brazier is a crucial part of the Iblis ritual.
column2: 那只火盆是伊布利斯儀式的關鍵。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the brazier.
column2: 別費心跟一只火盆說話了。

column1: You cannot reach the brazier.
column2: 你構不到那只火盆。

column1: You're too close to the flames. Ducking here would be dangerous.
column2: 你離火焰太近了,在這兒蹲下去可危險。

column1: There's no reason to duck at the moment.
column2: 現在沒理由蹲下。

column1: The statue has been destroyed.
column2: 雕像已經被摧毀了。

column1: You're already fighting the statue.
column2: 你已經在跟那座雕像纏鬥了。

column1: You're too far away from the statue.
column2: 你離雕像太遠了。

column1: The spell is about to go off! It'll get you for sure!
column2: 法術就要發動了!這下你肯定逃不掉!

column1: [Next time, take a tip from Ad Avis himself. If your spell rebounds from its target, then find another target.[
column2: [下次學學阿德·阿維斯本人的招數。要是你的法術從目標彈了回來,那就換個目標。[

column1: Indirectly Dead
column2: 拐個彎送命

column1: You! You will pay!
column2: 你!你會付出代價的!

column1: By the powers of all Darkness, by the power of my name,[Shall your soul be mirrored outward, and your body be the same!
column2: 以一切黑暗之力,以吾名之力,[願你的靈魂向外映照,願你的肉身亦復如是!

column1: So! The hero has a Reversal spell. Well, there's a way around that!
column2: 哼!原來英雄有反轉法術。也罷,這難不倒我!

column1: Stop him, Master! Don't let him cast that spell!
column2: 攔住他,主人!別讓他施那道法術!

column1: Khaveen will enjoy having you for his very own, but I doubt that you will live very long. You will pay for preventing me from releasing Iblis.
column2: 哈維因會樂於把你佔為己有,不過我看你也活不了多久。阻止我釋放伊布利斯,你會付出代價的。

column1: Ha! Your new looks suit you, but I doubt that you will live very long. You will pay for preventing me from releasing Iblis.
column2: 哈!你這新模樣挺襯你的,不過我看你也活不了多久。阻止我釋放伊布利斯,你會付出代價的。

column1: You will pay!
column2: 你會付出代價的!

column1: Your transmogrification by incantation presages your expiration, but your instigation prevented devastation by Iblis's domination.
column2: 咒語令你改頭換面,預示你大限將至,然你之挑釁,阻了伊布利斯君臨天下的浩劫。

column1: That is, you saved the world, but all you got for it was Saurus breath. Next time, take a moment to reflect before you enter this situation.
column2: 也就是說,你救了世界,結果到手的卻只有一口索魯斯口臭。下次踏進這種場面前,先想清楚一點。

column1: See? We Know Some Big Words!
column2: 瞧見沒?我們可會用大字眼!

column1: You! My spell is broken! You will die!
column2: 你!我的法術被破了!你死定了!

column1: You sure got a charge from that lightning. Perhaps you should have been galvanized into action instead.
column2: 那道閃電可真讓你「來電」了。也許你該被刺激得趕快動起來才對。

column1: Standing Aground?
column2: 原地擱淺?

column1: We did it! Hurray!
column2: 我們成功啦!萬歲!

column1: DO something, Master! He's about to light the final candle, and then Iblis will be FREE!
column2: 快做點什麼啊,主人!他就要點燃最後一根蠟燭了,然後伊布利斯就要被放出來了!

column1: Another candle! Master, hurry!
column2: 又一根蠟燭!主人,快!

column1: The last candle! Iblis is free!
column2: 最後一根蠟燭!伊布利斯自由了!

column1: You must move an unlit candle to break the spell, Master. Please stop that human!
column2: 你得移開一根還沒點著的蠟燭才能破解法術,主人。快攔住那個人類!

column1: That's got it, Boss! Now stop those candles!
column2: 就是這樣,老大!現在快阻止那些蠟燭!

column1: You've already broken the spell, Master. Stop that human!
column2: 你已經破解法術了,主人。攔住那個人類!

column1: You must hit an unlit candle to break the spell, Master. Please stop that man!
column2: 你得打中一根還沒點著的蠟燭才能破解法術,主人。快攔住那傢伙!

column1: Aim a little higher, Master. Maybe you can tip it over onto him.
column2: 瞄高一點,主人。也許你能把它砸翻、壓到他身上。

column1: The Statue of Iblis is protected from harm by the power of Suleiman.
column2: 伊布利斯雕像受蘇萊曼之力庇護,不受傷害。

column1: You are wasting your energies, Master. The Statue of Iblis was created by Suleiman to absorb and contain magic more powerful than yours.
column2: 你在白費力氣,主人。伊布利斯雕像是蘇萊曼所造,能吸收並封鎖比你強大得多的魔法。

column1: Everything just bounces off that human, Boss. You better try something else, quick!
column2: 什麼都從那個人類身上彈開了,老大。你最好快換個招數!

column1: Ad Avis seems to have turned the tables (and your own spells) on you.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯似乎反將了你一軍(連你自己的法術都用上了)。

column1: Role Reversal
column2: 角色反轉

column1: Ad Avis seems to have turned the tables (and your own projectiles) on you.
column2: 阿德·阿維斯似乎反將了你一軍(連你自己的飛射物都用上了)。

column1: Down and out
column2: 倒地出局

column1: You must Fetch an unlit candle to break the spell, Master. Please stop that man!
column2: 你得用取物術抓一根還沒點著的蠟燭才能破解法術,主人。快攔住那傢伙!

column1: The brazier is too heavy to Fetch.
column2: 那只火盆太重,取物術搬不動。

column1: You've got to be kidding! That statue must weigh a thousand pounds!
column2: 你開什麼玩笑!那座雕像少說也有上千磅重!

column1: You don't have time for that now.
column2: 你現在沒空做那個。

column1: The brazier is already lit. Your spell has no effect.
column2: 火盆已經點著了,你的法術不起作用。

column1: Flame Darts don't bounce, but Force Bolts do.
column2: 火鏢不會彈回,但力箭會。

column1: Your tiny flame spell can't ignite these floors. You need fire from another source to stop Ad Avis.
column2: 你那點小火苗法術點不著這些地板。你得從別的火源取火,才能阻止阿德·阿維斯。

column1: Your tiny flame spell can't ignite these floors. You need fire from another source to stop Ad Avis. (Of course, if you had stayed at WIT, they would have taught you a floor-burning spell.)
column2: 你那點小火苗法術點不著這些地板。你得從別的火源取火,才能阻止阿德·阿維斯。(當然啦,你要是當初留在 WIT,他們早就教你燒地板的法術了。)

column1: Good idea! He does look top-heavy with all those arms, doesn't he? Unfortunately, his mass is far too great for that spell to have any effect.
column2: 好主意!他長那麼多隻手臂,看起來頭重腳輕,對吧?可惜他份量實在太重,那道法術根本撼動不了他。

column1: Your Force Bolt lost its energy by the time it struck the wall, Master. Perhaps you'd better get closer.
column2: 你的力箭還沒打到牆就耗盡了力道,主人。你最好靠近一點。

column1: Fool, you can not hurt me now, and I shall send thee to thy death!
column2: 蠢貨,你現在傷不了我,我這就送你上西天!

column1: You seem to have triggered a magical spell on that statue, Master.
column2: 你似乎觸發了那座雕像上的某道魔法,主人。

column1: Some wizard you are. How do you spell relief? M-A-G-I-C!
column2: 你算哪門子的法師。解圍兩個字怎麼寫?念—咒—語!

column1: What a beating. You might have lasted longer, had you been healthier when you came in.
column2: 好一頓痛揍。你進來時要是身子骨硬朗些,說不定還能多撐一會兒。

column1: What a bust. That chiseler gave you no relief. Next time, remember not to stand your ground so long with a stone statue.
column2: 真是慘敗。那塊鑿出來的東西可半點沒手下留情。下次記住,別跟一座石像死守原地耗那麼久。

column1: Statue of Limitations
column2: 石像追訴期

column1: That wouldn't do anything useful.
column2: 那麼做沒什麼用。

column1: You don't have time for that.
column2: 你沒空做那個。

column1: You must get an unlit candle to break the spell, Master. Please stop that man!
column2: 你得拿到一根還沒點著的蠟燭才能破解法術,主人。快攔住那傢伙!

column1: You can't get the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 你拿不走伊布利斯的雕像。

column1: Master, I am sorry, but I cannot allow you to leave without Iblis!
column2: 主人,很抱歉,但我不能讓你丟下伊布利斯就走!

column1: It is too hot to touch.
column2: 太燙了,碰不得。

column1: The heavy statue, a demon of some type, has an enchantment placed upon it.
column2: 這座沉重的雕像是某種惡魔的形貌,身上施了一道魔咒。

column1: The stone statue has crumbled to pieces.
column2: 那座石像已經碎成一地。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the stone statue.
column2: 別費心跟一座石像說話了。

column1: Good idea, but he's too far away.
column2: 好主意,可惜他離得太遠了。

column1: Standing in that ring of flames for too long got you all fired up.
column2: 在那圈火焰裡站太久,可把你「燒」起來了。

column1: Someone turn up the heat in here?
column2: 是誰把這裡的火力開大了?

column1: You wet the candles, but that doesn't seem to stop their magic.
column2: 你把蠟燭弄濕了,可這似乎擋不住它們的魔力。

column1: The Air Elemental's magic is weakened by the presence of the Statue of Iblis. You cannot use the bellows to blow down the candle.
column2: 伊布利斯雕像在場,削弱了風元素的法力。你沒辦法用風箱把蠟燭吹熄。

column1: You stand beside the cracked and dry fountain in Raseir's Plaza of the Fountain. You hold in your hands the waterskin containing the Water Elemental that you captured so long ago in Shapeir.
column2: 你站在拉希爾噴泉廣場那座龜裂乾涸的噴泉旁。手裡握著那只水袋,裡頭裝著你很久以前在夏皮爾捕獲的水元素。

column1: The Elemental, under your command, is released into the fountain.
column2: 那元素聽從你的號令,被釋放進噴泉裡。

column1: You stare in wonder at the powerful transformation of the plaza by the magic of the Water Elemental.
column2: 你驚嘆地望著水元素的魔法,將整座廣場徹底改頭換面。

column1: The Sultan personally sent me to return you to his Palace, Hero of all Shapeir.
column2: 蘇丹親自派我來請您回宮,全夏皮爾的英雄。

column1: The caravan comes under siege from hundreds of nomadic Brigands. Your small group is vastly outnumbered. The situation looks grim.
column2: 商隊遭到數百名游牧強盜圍攻。你們這一小撮人寡不敵眾。情勢看來不妙。

column1: Enter/Return to continue
column2: 按 Enter/Return 鍵繼續

column1: I love the smell of victory in the[morning. It smells like... sweat... [no, no... oregano... nooo... like victory![Yeah, victory! Yeah! Yeah, that's the ticket.
column2: 我就愛聞清晨[勝利的氣味。聞起來像……汗水……[不不……奧勒岡草……不對……像勝利![沒錯,勝利!對啦!對,就是這味兒。

column1: After a week of traveling, you leave the caravan when it comes within sight of the city of Raseir.
column2: 旅行了一週後,等商隊望見拉希爾城時,你便與它分道揚鑣。

column1: The Caravan Master warns you that Raseir is a dangerous place to be and bids you be wary of all you meet there.
column2: 商隊首領警告你,拉希爾是個危險的地方,囑咐你提防在那兒遇見的每一個人。

column1: He rears up and salutes you, and you watch the caravan move onward into the desert.
column2: 他高高揚起前蹄向你致意,你目送著商隊繼續邁向沙漠深處。

column1: You ride alone to the entrance to Raseir, and pass through the barred gates which barely open for you.
column2: 你獨自騎行到拉希爾的入口,穿過那道勉強為你開啟的鐵閘城門。

column1: Of course the Centaur survived the battle.
column2: 那名半人馬當然在這場戰鬥中活了下來。

column1: At least part of the caravan survived the battle.
column2: 至少有一部分的商隊在這場戰鬥中倖存了下來。

column1: You just got RickRoll'd!
column2: 你這下被耍了一道!

column1: For a few moments, your mind is overwhelmed by the squalling, whirling wind.
column2: 有那麼一瞬間,呼嘯打轉的狂風淹沒了你的心神。

column1: Then the euphoric feeling starts to fade and you stand alone in the darkness.
column2: 接著那股飄飄然的快感漸漸消退,你獨自一人站在黑暗之中。

column1: The past few hours are hazy in your memory. You remember only one thing clearly: you are here to find the Statue of Iblis.
column2: 過去這幾個鐘頭在你記憶裡一片模糊。你只清楚記得一件事:你來這裡,是為了尋找伊布利斯的雕像。

column1: On the other side of the stalactites and stalagmites, you see the darkness of a very deep chasm. Good thing you have this lamp to light your way.
column2: 在鐘乳石與石筍的另一頭,你看見一道極深裂谷的漆黑。幸好你有這盞燈替你照路。

column1: It is difficult to judge where you are going in this darkness. It is also very dangerous.
column2: 在這片黑暗裡很難判斷自己往哪兒走。而且這很危險。

column1: You feel what might be a wall of this cave. It feels cold and damp, despite the desert outside. The sound of the wind is mostly above you.
column2: 你摸到一面像是洞穴牆壁的東西。儘管外頭是沙漠,它摸起來卻又冷又濕。風聲多半從你頭頂上方傳來。

column1: You bump your head rather hard into a stalagmite. Or is it a stalactite?
column2: 你的頭重重撞上一根石筍。還是說那是鐘乳石?

column1: As you step forward, you find nothing to step onto. Your hands grab for a stalagmite (or is it a stalactite?), but slip off the slick surface. You find yourself falling...
column2: 你往前一踏,卻發現腳下空無一物。雙手胡亂去抓一根石筍(還是鐘乳石?),卻從那滑溜的表面滑開。你發覺自己正往下墜……

column1: Uh-oh, you don't seem to have a lamp.
column2: 糟糕,你身上似乎沒有燈。

column1: Might as well die now, just to save yourself a little frustration.
column2: 那乾脆現在就死一死,還能替自己省點折騰。

column1: Your Choice of Poison
column2: 任君選毒

column1: OK. Kill me now
column2: 好吧,現在就了結我

column1: Later (but not much)
column2: 晚點(但也撐不久)

column1: Perhaps there's a reason why people are scared of the dark.
column2: 人們怕黑,也許是有道理的。

column1: Whaddaya think?
column2: 你說呢?

column1: Afraid of the dark? You should have been. Perhaps you would have lasted longer if you hadn't taken the darkness so lightly.
column2: 怕黑嗎?你本來就該怕的。要是你別把黑暗看得那麼輕,說不定還能多撐一會兒。

column1: Can't you see?
column2: 你是看不見嗎?

column1: Dancing in the dark is romantic, but dangerous.
column2: 在黑暗中起舞固然浪漫,卻也危險。

column1: You Blew That One
column2: 這回你搞砸了

column1: You can't see to do that. The lamp isn't lit.
column2: 你看不見,沒法那麼做。燈還沒點著。

column1: If you extinguish the lamp, you would be lost in the dark.
column2: 要是你把燈熄了,就會迷失在黑暗裡。

column1: The wind in here is far too strong for you even to attempt to use your rope.
column2: 這裡的風太猛,你連用繩子的念頭都別想。

column1: You have no need of the magic rope here, Hero.
column2: 你在這兒用不著魔法繩,英雄。

column1: There doesn't appear to be a good place to use it here.
column2: 這裡似乎沒有適合用它的地方。

column1: This really isn't the best place to do that.
column2: 這實在不是做那件事的好地方。

column1: The air rushing through the passage is so strong that it threatens to blow you off your feet. You find it impossible to concentrate on anything else.
column2: 穿過通道的氣流強得幾乎要把你掀翻在地。你根本沒法專心去想別的事。

column1: There is a mild feeling of residual magic in the room.
column2: 房間裡殘留著一絲淡淡的魔法氣息。

column1: The rocks around the passageway resist your Flame Dart.
column2: 通道四周的岩石抵擋住了你的火鏢。

column1: Your eyes are blinded by the flash in the dark, and you still can't see.
column2: 黑暗中那道閃光晃花了你的眼,你還是什麼都看不見。

column1: The rock around the passageway is too strong to be affected by your Force Bolt.
column2: 通道四周的岩石太堅硬,你的力箭撼動不了它。

column1: The door resists all attempts to open it.
column2: 這扇門擋住了一切想打開它的嘗試。

column1: Such a weak spell has no effect upon the fury of the wind.
column2: 這麼弱的法術,對這狂暴的風絲毫不起作用。

column1: The wind in here is far too strong for you even to attempt the Levitate spell.
column2: 這裡的風太猛,你連施浮空術的念頭都別想。

column1: There doesn't appear to be a good place to Levitate here.
column2: 這裡似乎沒有適合施浮空術的地方。

column1: You can't reach the door from up here.
column2: 你在這上頭構不到那扇門。

column1: You're already using the lamp.
column2: 你已經在用燈了。

column1: The rock glistens with moisture.
column2: 岩石上閃著濕潤的水光。

column1: Even with the lamp, the darkness can be treacherous.
column2: 就算有燈,這黑暗也可能暗藏殺機。

column1: The wind is at its worst as it comes from the passage above. There must be something you can do to stop its fury.
column2: 風從上方的通道灌下來時最是猛烈。一定有什麼辦法能止住它的狂暴。

column1: The wind blew you off like a leaf from a tree. It doesn't seem as if you can get near enough to cut the wind off at its source.
column2: 風把你像片落葉似的吹得老遠。看來你沒法靠得夠近,從源頭把風截斷。

column1: The wind again caused you to fall like a rock.
column2: 風再一次把你像塊石頭般吹倒在地。

column1: You can't take much more of this. You must stop the wind from blowing before it stops you.
column2: 你撐不了多久了。你得在風撂倒你之前,先讓它停下來。

column1: This is getting you nowhere. Maybe you are looking at the problem from the wrong end.
column2: 這樣下去毫無進展。也許你打從一開始就看錯了問題的方向。

column1: You just aren't strong enough to dislodge the pile of rocks.
column2: 你的力氣就是不夠大,撼動不了那堆石頭。

column1: The swift current overwhelms you with emotions as it sweeps you off your feet and carries you away.
column2: 湍急的水流捲走你的雙腳,將你沖向遠方,百感交集一齊湧上心頭。

column1: Swept away
column2: 捲走

column1: No, dude! It's down. Far down. WIPE OUT!
column2: 不對啦,老兄!是往下。往下深著呢。摔個狗吃屎!

column1: Surf's up?
column2: 浪來了?

column1: You have just fallen off-screen to the jagged rocks below.
column2: 你剛剛墜出了畫面,摔到下方那片嶙峋的亂石上。

column1: Well, would you believe good idea, but bad timing?
column2: 這個嘛,你信不信叫做主意不錯,可惜時機不對?

column1: Missed it by that much
column2: 就差這麼一點點

column1: You should keep your lamp lit here.
column2: 你在這兒該讓燈一直亮著。

column1: OK, but you'd better keep it handy. There may be other dark caves to explore.
column2: 好吧,不過你最好把它擺在順手的地方。說不定還有別的黑暗洞窟等著你去探。

column1: Not now. You're busy.
column2: 現在不行,你正忙著呢。

column1: OK, but there's light enough from the phosphorescent fungi on the walls.
column2: 好吧,不過牆上那些會發光的菌類照得也夠亮了。

column1: There is an aura of magic throughout this room.
column2: 整間房間瀰漫著一股魔法的氣息。

column1: Your spell opens up some of the smaller fissures in the stones.
column2: 你的法術撐開了石頭上幾道較小的裂縫。

column1: The log is too large and has too much momentum for your Fetch spell to have much effect.
column2: 那根木頭又大又衝勁十足,你的取物術起不了多大作用。

column1: This is not a good place to Levitate.
column2: 這裡不是適合施浮空術的地方。

column1: That's a great idea...
column2: 這主意真不錯……

column1: ...but how?
column2: ……可是要怎麼做?

column1: Pushing the rocks proves futile. You'll have to try something else.
column2: 推那些石頭證明是白搭。你得換個法子試試。

column1: The rocks will not burn.
column2: 這些石頭燒不起來。

column1: Either your imagination just got the better of you, or some unknown (yet not wholly unfamiliar) young man was just teleported into the middle of the raging river, a few seconds before falling to his death.
column2: 要嘛是你的想像力作祟,要嘛就是某個你不認識(卻又不完全陌生)的年輕人剛被傳送到了奔騰的河流正中央,幾秒後便墜河身亡。

column1: Wait, maybe it's a cruel Easter egg of some sort. The lengths some programmers go through to get some cheap laugh out of the audience...
column2: 等等,搞不好這是某種惡趣味的彩蛋。有些程式設計師為了讓玩家笑一下,還真是無所不用其極……

column1: Wouldn't it be nicer to have a diving board?
column2: 要是有塊跳水板不就好多了?

column1: There's no need to do that -- you're already over the other side.
column2: 沒這必要——你已經到對岸了。

column1: Try jumping from the mossy bank by the falls.
column2: 試試從瀑布旁那片長滿青苔的岸邊跳過去。

column1: The bank feels slick with water and moss.
column2: 岸邊又是水又是青苔,滑溜溜的。

column1: You'd better get closer to dry land first.
column2: 你最好先靠近乾燥的陸地。

column1: Your foot slips due to the force of the air rushing into that small opening. There is tremendous pressure from all the wind trying to surge into the hole, and you struggle to overcome it.
column2: 氣流灌進那個小開口的力道讓你腳下一滑。所有的風都想擠進那個洞,形成驚人的壓力,你拼了命才頂住。

column1: Whew! That was close.
column2: 呼!好險。

column1: There appears to be an opening in the rocks, but it's hard to see from this side of the river.
column2: 岩石間似乎有個開口,但從河這一側很難看清楚。

column1: Careful examination of the air hole and the rocks above reveals that there are some loose stones that hold the structure together. There are small fissures running between its base and the rocks above.
column2: 仔細查看那個氣孔和上方的岩石,你發現有幾塊鬆動的石頭撐著整個結構。底部和上方岩石之間還有些細小的裂縫。

column1: The rocks lie in a heap, blocking the hole.
column2: 石頭堆成一堆,把洞口堵住了。

column1: A pile of stones now blocks the air hole.
column2: 一堆石頭現在把氣孔堵住了。

column1: You have to cross the river first.
column2: 你得先渡過河才行。

column1: The rushing river surges from above to form a raging waterfall.
column2: 湍急的河水從上方傾瀉而下,形成一道狂暴的瀑布。

column1: The rushing river boils furiously.
column2: 湍急的河水翻騰得厲害。

column1: You can feel the wind rushing through this chasm at an incredible rate.
column2: 你能感覺到風以驚人的速度穿過這道裂谷。

column1: Near the waterfall, the stone has formed into a natural stairway.
column2: 瀑布旁的岩石天然形成了一道階梯。

column1: Great volumes of air are being sucked into this small hole.
column2: 大量的空氣正被吸進這個小洞裡。

column1: You can't climb the wall from your present location.
column2: 從你現在站的位置爬不上這面牆。

column1: The rocks in this room are much too slippery to climb.
column2: 這房裡的岩石太滑了,根本爬不上去。

column1: You lack the skill to even climb a tree, let alone a slippery rock wall.
column2: 你連爬棵樹的本事都沒有,更別說滑溜溜的岩壁了。

column1: The speed of the rushing water would rip the waterskin out of your hand, and you might slip in on the slimy bank.
column2: 湍急的水流會把水袋從你手裡扯走,你還可能在滑膩的岸邊失足落水。

column1: Many's the time you've regretted your lack of a formal swimming education.
column2: 好多次你都後悔自己沒正經學過游泳。

column1: Not now -- you're busy!
column2: 現在不行——你正忙著呢!

column1: You refill your waterskin.
column2: 你把水袋重新裝滿。

column1: The cavern feels slick with water and moss.
column2: 洞穴裡又是水又是青苔,滑溜溜的。

column1: You think that the log is too far away, and you wait for another one.
column2: 你覺得那根木頭太遠了,於是等下一根。

column1: Most of the water you poured over yourself has evaporated. You are starting to get very hot.
column2: 你淋在身上的水大半已經蒸發,開始覺得熱得受不了。

column1: OK, you extinguish the lamp, but you'd better keep it handy. There may be other dark caves to explore.
column2: 好,你把燈熄了,不過最好隨身帶著。說不定還有別的暗洞要探。

column1: This isn't a good place to use your magic rope.
column2: 這裡不適合用你的魔法繩。

column1: There is magic throughout this room.
column2: 這整個房間都充滿了魔法。

column1: As your Calm spell takes effect, the flames from the geysers cease. The room still remains unbearably hot though.
column2: 隨著你的「鎮靜術」生效,噴泉冒出的火焰停了下來。不過房裡還是熱得叫人受不了。

column1: Too hot? Wear your thermal underwear next time.
column2: 太熱了?下次記得穿保暖內衣。

column1: Hot Shot Got Caught for Naught
column2: 逞能英雄白白送命

column1: You stepped on something that wasn't quite solid rock and felt the heat through your boot.
column2: 你踩到一塊不太像實心岩石的東西,熱氣透過靴子傳了上來。

column1: You rekindle your lamp.
column2: 你重新點亮了燈。

column1: Nothing but the voice's demand seems important right now.
column2: 此刻除了那道聲音的命令,別的都顯得無關緊要。

column1: You can see that you are on a ledge overlooking darkness. You can see something that appears deliberately fashioned across the brink, but you cannot tell what it is from where you are standing.
column2: 你看得出自己站在一道俯瞰黑暗的岩架上。崖邊橫著一個像是刻意打造的東西,但從你站的位置看不出那是什麼。

column1: All you can see is the pocket of cavern in which you stand, solid rock on all sides, and a massive doorway.
column2: 你只看得到自己所在的這個洞穴凹處、四面的實心岩壁,還有一道巨大的門。

column1: You can see something that appears deliberately fashioned across the brink, but you cannot tell what it is from where you are standing.
column2: 崖邊橫著一個像是刻意打造的東西,但從你站的位置看不出那是什麼。

column1: The great, ancient doors stand guard, as they have for one thousand and one years.
column2: 那扇巨大的古老門扉守在那裡,一守便是一千零一年。

column1: You're not in a good position to climb.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合攀爬。

column1: Looking down at the sheerness of the sides and the slickness of the rock, you know you would merely wind up falling.
column2: 往下望著陡峭的崖壁和滑溜的岩石,你曉得自己只會落得摔下去的下場。

column1: Looking up at the sheerness of the sides and the slickness of the rock, you know that you wouldn't get very far climbing.
column2: 往上望著陡峭的崖壁和滑溜的岩石,你曉得自己爬不了多遠。

column1: You find yourself unable to move. The voice's demand still rings within your ears.
column2: 你發現自己動彈不得。那道聲音的命令仍在你耳邊迴盪。

column1: You're not in a good position to use the rope.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合用繩子。

column1: You hear a deep, echoing voice:
column2: 你聽見一道低沉、迴盪的聲音:

column1: None shall enter[None shall pass[but he who speaks the Name of Power.
column2: 無人能入[無人能過[唯有道出「力量之名」者方可。

column1: SPEAK NOW THE NAME OF POWER!
column2: 此刻,道出力量之名!

column1: With all the damage you took to get here, it's really a shame you couldn't take any more. You should've tended to your healing, but now it's too late.
column2: 一路走來受了這麼多傷,你竟撐不住了,實在可惜。你早該好好療傷的,可惜現在為時已晚。

column1: The conclusion to this little cliff-hanger was in abbreviated form.
column2: 這段懸崖驚魂的結局,以精簡版收場。

column1: Cliff Notes
column2: 懸崖小記

column1: Your last thoughts as the cavern collapses around you are, "Who thought up this crummy puzzle, anyway?"
column2: 洞穴在你四周崩塌時,你最後的念頭是:「到底是誰想出這種爛謎題的啊?」

column1: You find yourself unable to throw anything. The voice's demand still rings within your ears.
column2: 你發現自己什麼也丟不出去。那道聲音的命令仍在你耳邊迴盪。

column1: There is a subtle residue of magic in this room. Once great and powerful, it is now dormant.
column2: 這房裡殘留著一絲微弱的魔法。它曾經強大無比,如今卻已沉睡。

column1: The great, ancient door is not damaged by your puny effort.
column2: 你那點微不足道的力氣,傷不了這扇巨大的古老門扉。

column1: The great, ancient door easily resists your tiny spell.
column2: 你那點微弱的法術,被這扇巨大的古老門扉輕鬆擋下。

column1: You haven't the power to move such a large object.
column2: 你沒有力氣搬動這麼大的東西。

column1: You're not in a good position to Levitate off of the cliff.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合用「漂浮術」離開崖邊。

column1: You do not need to Levitate back up the cliff.
column2: 你不需要用「漂浮術」飄回崖上。

column1: You can't reach from up here.
column2: 從上面這裡你搆不著。

column1: Stony silence is your answer.
column2: 回應你的是一片死寂。

column1: You're not in a good position to make a jump.
column2: 你現在的位置不適合起跳。

column1: That hurt, but it's a good thing you're tough, agile, and know how to take a fall.
column2: 好痛,不過幸好你夠堅韌、夠敏捷,又懂得怎麼摔。

column1: You really aren't up to this kind of physical abuse.
column2: 你還真承受不了這種折騰。

column1: Considering your IMPRESSIVE skill at climbing, that wasn't exactly a brilliant maneuver. You are beaten, battered, and bruised... but you are down.
column2: 憑你那「了不起」的攀爬技術,這一手實在算不上高明。你被打得鼻青臉腫、遍體鱗傷……但好歹是下來了。

column1: Well, that was one way down. But it hurt... a lot!
column2: 嗯,這也算下來的一種辦法。只不過……痛得要命!

column1: You can't reach the doors from up here.
column2: 從上面這裡你搆不到那些門。

column1: You are in a roughly hewn cave or old mine.
column2: 你身處一座粗鑿而成的洞穴,或是一座舊礦坑。

column1: Beyond this door awaits your fortune.
column2: 門的另一頭,等著你的是財富。

column1: It looks like armor of gold.
column2: 看起來像是黃金打造的盔甲。

column1: Something is piled up in the dark.
column2: 黑暗中堆著什麼東西。

column1: It looks like treasure of jewels and gold.
column2: 看起來像是珠寶和黃金堆成的寶藏。

column1: All that glitters is not gold.
column2: 閃閃發光的未必都是金子。

column1: You cannot return the way you came.
column2: 你沒辦法循原路回去。

column1: The floor of this chamber is smooth stone.
column2: 這間石室的地面是光滑的石板。

column1: Light the lamp!
column2: 把燈點亮!

column1: You die wandering in the darkness.
column2: 你在黑暗中迷失,就此喪命。

column1: Not too bright!
column2: 不太聰明喔!

column1: The stalactite (or is it a stalagmite?) has shattered your dreams of becoming a hero. You feel crushed.
column2: 那根鐘乳石(還是石筍?)粉碎了你當英雄的美夢。你被砸得稀爛。

column1: Knocked by a Rock
column2: 石落英雄殞

column1: You really fell for it. As the ground closes up around you, your last thoughts are: "This is really the pits!"
column2: 你還真栽進去了。地面在你四周合攏時,你最後的念頭是:「這下真是掉進坑裡了!」

column1: buTripped by a Trap
column2: bu中了陷阱栽跟頭

column1: Your animal magnetism obviously sparked this flash of brilliance. Next time you proceed, pay heed to the creed: "To succeed you need to be freed from greed."
column2: 顯然是你那股野獸般的魅力激出了這道靈光。下次行動前,記住這句箴言:「想成功,先戒貪。」

column1: pA Zap for a Permanent Nap
column2: p一道閃光長眠去

column1: Okay, but you'd better keep it handy. There may be other dark caves to explore.
column2: 好吧,不過最好隨身帶著。說不定還有別的暗洞要探。

column1: It is far too dark to be without your lamp. Maybe you should light the lamp again.
column2: 這裡太暗了,沒燈可不行。或許你該把燈再點上。

column1: A strong aura of magic is around the treasure in this room. An even stronger sense of magic radiates from the room beyond the far door.
column2: 這房裡的寶藏周圍縈繞著一股強烈的魔法氣息。而遠處那扇門後的房間,散發出更為強烈的魔法。

column1: The ancient doors magically sealed when they closed. You can't open them.
column2: 那扇古老的門關上時便以魔法封死了。你打不開它。

column1: Speaking the Name of Power only works from the other side of the door.
column2: 道出「力量之名」,只有從門的另一側才有用。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the door.
column2: 別費心跟一扇門講話了。

column1: Bye bye!
column2: 拜拜啦!

column1: The lamp barely illuminates the dull rock above.
column2: 燈光勉強照亮了上方那片黯淡的岩石。

column1: Your feet seem to move with a will of their own.
column2: 你的雙腳像是有了自己的意志,自顧自地走著。

column1: As you draw near, you see that the object is a statue made of crystal. A dark, shapeless form stirs within it.
column2: 你走近一看,才發現那東西是一尊水晶雕像。一團漆黑、沒有形狀的影子在裡頭蠕動。

column1: The crystal's cool glow is numbing. You feel compelled to pick it up, but as you reach out...
column2: 水晶散發的清冷光芒令人麻木。你忍不住想把它拿起來,可就在你伸出手的瞬間……

column1: You are suddenly unable to move.
column2: 你突然動彈不得。

column1: Fool! Some hero you are! A pawn of the very man you sought to destroy.
column2: 蠢貨!虧你還是個英雄!你不過是你一心想剷除之人手中的一枚棋子罷了。

column1: Thanks to your help, I now have that through which I can summon the greatest destructive power ever known -- Iblis himself!
column2: 多虧你幫忙,我如今已握有那件能召喚出史上最強毀滅之力的東西——伊布利斯本尊!

column1: Do you know how long I have awaited this moment? Seventy years! Seventy years I have served the Dark Master while watching for the stars to align.
column2: 你可知道我等這一刻等了多久?七十年!整整七十年,我侍奉黑暗主宰,只為等待群星歸位。

column1: Seventy years I have spent learning the magic that controls men's minds and waiting for a fool to fulfill the prophecy for me.
column2: 七十年來,我潛心鑽研操控人心的魔法,只等一個蠢貨替我完成預言。

column1: Tonight, as the moon arises between the Scorpion's claws and the Dragon's teeth, I shall begin the ritual that shall make me the Master! I will control Iblis, and thus control the world.
column2: 今夜,當明月升起於天蠍之鉗與巨龍之牙之間,我便要開始那場讓我成為主宰的儀式!我將操控伊布利斯,進而操控整個世界。

column1: While you, pawn of prophecy, shall remain behind in this tomb. You shall be "He Who Waits Behind."
column2: 至於你,這預言中的棋子,就留在這座墳墓裡吧。你將成為「滯留於後之人」。

column1: c*Farewell, Hero!
column2: c*永別了,英雄!

column1: A glow illuminates this chamber. It flows from an object high atop the strange structure before you.
column2: 一道光芒照亮了這間石室。它源自你面前那座奇異結構頂端的某件東西。

column1: At the sight of the glowing object you feel irresistibly drawn forward.
column2: 一看見那件發光的東西,你便感到一股無法抗拒的力量牽引你向前。

column1: You've wished away your three wishes, Oh Master of the Ring. Enjoy your stay at Hotel Iblis. It will be a long one, no doubt.
column2: 噢,戒指的主人哪,你三個願望都許完了。好好享受你在「伊布利斯大飯店」的時光吧。毫無疑問,這會是一段很長很長的住宿。

column1: C'mon. Just One More Little Wish...
column2: 拜託嘛,就再許一個小小的願望……

column1: The Djinni is composed entirely of magic, but all other sources of magic in this room are now gone.
column2: 這精靈完全由魔法構成,但房裡其他的魔法來源如今都消失了。

column1: There is still a source of magic in this room.
column2: 這房裡還有一個魔法來源。

column1: Oh, ho ho, Master! That tickles.
column2: 噢,呵呵呵,主人!好癢喔。

column1: Um... Master? I know I'm pretty useless and all that, but maybe I could help you escape somehow.
column2: 呃……主人?我知道我挺沒用的,不過或許我能想個辦法幫你逃出去。

column1: Your spell doesn't even begin to move the rocks blocking the doorway. They are too large.
column2: 你的法術連堵在門口的石頭都撼不動分毫。它們實在太大了。

column1: No, thank you, Master. I've been sleeping for one thousand and one years already.
column2: 不了,謝謝你,主人。我已經睡了一千零一年了。

column1: Master, I am a Djinni. If you want to play fetch, maybe you should get a dog.
column2: 主人,我可是個精靈。你要是想玩你丟我撿,還是去養條狗吧。

column1: The small object seems to resist your Fetch spell.
column2: 那件小東西似乎抗拒著你的「取物術」。

column1: The door is blocked by tons of rocks -- apparently for good.
column2: 門被成噸的石頭堵住了——看來是徹底沒救了。

column1: The door is very old. And ancient, very ancient.
column2: 這扇門非常古老。古老又久遠,久遠得很。

column1: Even with your great strength, it would be impossible to move all of those rocks. You had better find another way to escape.
column2: 就算你力大無窮,也不可能把那些石頭全搬開。你最好另尋出路逃出去。

column1: It would take years to clear away all of those rocks from the door.
column2: 要把門前那些石頭全清掉,得花上好幾年。

column1: The structure in the center of the room is now dark, and a huge pile of boulders block the only door to the cavern.
column2: 房間中央的那座結構如今一片黑暗,一大堆巨石把通往洞穴的唯一一扇門堵死了。

column1: The Statue of Iblis is no longer here. It has been taken by Ad Avis.
column2: 伊布利斯的雕像已經不在這裡了。它被阿德·阿維斯帶走了。

column1: Something flashes in the darkness.
column2: 黑暗中有什麼東西閃了一下。

column1: You see a small, golden ring around the tip of a stalagmite (or is it a stalactite?).
column2: 你看見一枚小巧的金戒指,套在一根石筍(還是鐘乳石?)的尖端上。

column1: You need to get closer to do that.
column2: 你得再靠近一點才行。

column1: You pick up the ring and read the inscription: "He Who Waits Behind." As you put it on your finger, you twist it a bit to see its design.
column2: 你撿起戒指,讀著上頭的銘文:「滯留於後之人」。你把它套上手指,順手轉了一下,想看清它的紋路。

column1: I may grant you three wishes, Oh Master of the Ring. What is your first wish?
column2: 噢,戒指的主人哪,我可以實現你三個願望。你的第一個願望是什麼?

column1: Let's see, that was a small town somewhere around here, isn't it?
column2: 我想想,那是這附近的一座小鎮,對吧?

column1: That's where all the Katta live, I think.
column2: 我記得卡塔族都住在那兒。

column1: We Djinn are very great and powerful. Well, some of us are, anyway.
column2: 我們精靈可是偉大又強大。嗯,反正有些是啦。

column1: pizza elemental
column2: 披薩元素精靈

column1: It's made out of dough with cheese and tomato on top.
column2: 它是用麵糰做的,上頭鋪著起司和番茄。

column1: That's some sort of magical fire, maybe.
column2: 那大概是某種魔法之火吧。

column1: It's an Elemental made out of water.
column2: 那是一隻由水構成的元素精靈。

column1: I think that looks something like a lot of wind.
column2: 我覺得那看起來有點像一大團風。

column1: It's big, and it looks like it is made from rocks.
column2: 它好大,看起來像是岩石做的。

column1: All Djinn know a lot about magic. I'm just not very good at it.
column2: 所有精靈都很懂魔法。只是我這方面不太行而已。

column1: I think Iblis has been taken to some sort of Palace.
column2: 我想伊布利斯被帶到了某座宮殿之類的地方。

column1: The Ifreet like to live in the desert. An Ifreet is a kind of Djinn.
column2: 伊夫利特喜歡住在沙漠裡。伊夫利特是精靈的一種。

column1: There's plenty of money next door, but I think it is trapped.
column2: 隔壁有的是錢,不過我想那是有陷阱的。

column1: It was rumored that the Sultan of Isfahan was in love with a cow, but I guess that was a long time ago.
column2: 傳聞伊斯法罕的蘇丹愛上了一頭母牛,不過我想那是很久以前的事了。

column1: The Katta are some cat people that live in the desert.
column2: 卡塔族是住在沙漠裡的一種貓人。

column1: I've been in the Ring so long, I think I forgot. You can call me Djinni, though.
column2: 我在戒指裡待太久了,大概都忘了。不過你可以叫我精靈。

column1: Well, I was supposed to make sure Iblis doesn't get loose, so now I guess I got to help you bring him back, as the prophecy says.
column2: 這個嘛,我本來該負責看著伊布利斯別跑出來,所以我想現在我得照預言說的,幫你把他帶回來。

column1: Some Djinn can make powerful Elementals, but not me. I did make a pepperoni pizza once, though.
column2: 有些精靈能造出強大的元素精靈,可我不行。不過我倒是做過一次義式臘腸披薩。

column1: Iblis was the most powerful of all Djinn. A thousand years and a year ago he created a huge city here and tried to make all men bow before him.
column2: 伊布利斯是所有精靈中最強大的。一千零一年前,他在這裡造了一座巨大的城市,妄想讓所有人都向他俯首稱臣。

column1: Suleiman summoned all the Djinn to his side, and with their power turned the city into Iblis's tomb. Then Iblis himself was caught and bound, and became the statue you saw here.
column2: 蘇萊曼召集了所有精靈到他身邊,藉著他們的力量,把這座城市變成了伊布利斯的墳墓。接著伊布利斯本人也被捉住、被束縛,變成了你在這裡看到的那尊雕像。

column1: I can give you the ability of ten men at any skill you want to improve upon. You have but to wish for Strength, or Magic, or Intelligence, or Sneaking, or, well, you get the idea.
column2: 不論你想精進哪項本事,我都能賜你十人之力。你只消許願要力量、魔法、智力、潛行,或者,嗯,你懂我意思的。

column1: I can heal your wounds and injuries and make you all better.
column2: 我能治好你的傷口和病痛,讓你恢復得好好的。

column1: I can grant you three wishes by the power of the Ring. I can get you anything you want, as long as it is health, prowess, or teleporting you to Iblis.
column2: 憑著魔戒的力量,我能實現你三個願望。只要是健康、武藝,或是把你傳送到伊布利斯那兒,你想要什麼我都能給。

column1: Suleiman ben Daoud was the wisest of the wise, strongest of the strong, most magicallest of the magicians. He was also very kind and made me 'He Who Waits Behind' and put me in the Ring.
column2: 蘇萊曼·本·達伍德是智者中的智者、強者中的強者、法師中最會魔法的法師。他人也很好,封我為「殿後守候者」,把我封進這枚戒指裡。

column1: I'm the Djinni of the Ring. Suleiman asked for someone to stay here and guard to make sure Iblis didn't escape. I volunteered. All the other Djinn laughed at me, 'cause I'm not very good at magic.
column2: 我是這枚戒指的精靈。蘇萊曼要找個人留在這兒看守,確保伊布利斯逃不掉。我自願留下。其他精靈都笑我,因為我魔法不太行。

column1: But not Suleiman. He said I'd do fine and let me be 'He Who Waits Behind.' I guess he was wrong, huh? Iblis is gone, and you and I are kinda stuck here, aren't we, Master?
column2: 可蘇萊曼沒笑我。他說我做得來,讓我當「殿後守候者」。我猜他是看走眼了吧?伊布利斯不見了,你跟我都算是困在這兒了,對吧,主人?

column1: You are the lord of the Ring, since you put it on and twisted it. Your wish is my command, as long as it helps return Iblis here.
column2: 你戴上戒指又轉了它,你就是這枚戒指的主人。你的願望就是我的命令——只要能幫忙把伊布利斯帶回來就行。

column1: This used to be the city that Iblis created, but after Suleiman and I and all the other Djinn beat Iblis, this became kinda his tomb, I suppose.
column2: 這兒原本是伊布利斯造的城,可自從蘇萊曼、我,還有其他精靈打敗他之後,我想這兒就算成了他的墳墓吧。

column1: I am supposed to make sure Iblis is returned to the Forbidden City. I do have enough magic to take you with me to where Iblis has been taken, I think.
column2: 我的職責是確保伊布利斯被送回禁城。我想,我的魔法應該夠帶你一起去伊布利斯被帶去的地方。

column1: I just don't know too much about that, Master.
column2: 那件事我實在不太懂,主人。

column1: I've been in the ring so long, I can't remember.
column2: 我在戒指裡待太久了,記不得了。

column1: Was that supposed to be a wish, Master?
column2: 那算是一個願望嗎,主人?

column1: No thanks are necessary, Master. It is my purpose to aid you.
column2: 不必道謝,主人。協助你正是我存在的意義。

column1: Very interesting, Master. I have missed much from being in the ring so long.
column2: 真有意思,主人。在戒指裡待太久,我錯過了好多事呢。

column1: By my power, may you have the skill of ten men!
column2: 憑我的力量,願你擁有十人之能!

column1: You suddenly feel much more skilled.
column2: 你頓時覺得身手俐落了許多。

column1: Gee, it's so nice when my magic actually works.
column2: 哎呀,我的魔法真的靈了,感覺真好。

column1: You also feel your combat skills being honed.
column2: 你也感覺到自己的戰鬥技巧更加精進了。

column1: Next time you fight, try following up an overhead slash with an upward jumping slash.
column2: 下回打鬥時,試試在頭頂劈砍之後接一記向上跳斬。

column1: To execute the quadruple slash, press the special hotkey (%s) right after doing an inverse swing.
column2: 要使出四連斬,在反手揮擊之後立刻按下特殊快捷鍵(%s)。

column1: But, Master, I have already granted you that wish.
column2: 可是主人,那個願望我已經幫你實現過了。

column1: Next time you fight, try evading attacks by leaping away and then retaliating with a flying slash.
column2: 下回打鬥時,試試跳開閃避攻擊,再用一記飛斬反擊。

column1: To execute the cannonball strike, press the special hotkey (%s) while the opponent is performing a standing or flying overhead slash.
column2: 要使出砲彈衝擊,在對手施展站立或飛身頭頂劈砍時按下特殊快捷鍵(%s)。

column1: Next time you fight, try using the power of the enemy's swing to execute a spinning slash.
column2: 下回打鬥時,試試借敵人揮擊的力道使出一記旋身斬。

column1: To execute the sword dance, press the special hotkey (%s) after parrying an enemy's swing.
column2: 要使出劍舞,在格擋敵人的揮擊之後按下特殊快捷鍵(%s)。

column1: By my will, may you be well in all ways!
column2: 憑我的意志,願你諸事康健!

column1: You feel a warm glow spreading throughout your body.
column2: 你感到一股暖流在全身上下蔓延開來。

column1: I did it! My spell worked!
column2: 我成功啦!我的咒語靈了!

column1: Well, I can kind of grant you that wish, but it has two problems. First, I can only teleport you to where Iblis is now, and second, it's the last wish I can grant. You'll have to make all of your other wishes first.
column2: 嗯,那個願望我多少能幫你實現,不過有兩個問題。第一,我只能把你傳送到伊布利斯如今所在之處;第二,這是我能實現的最後一個願望。你得先把其他願望全許完才行。

column1: So, if you still want me to do it, repeat your wish and it shall be granted -- I hope!
column2: 所以呢,要是你還想讓我這麼做,就把願望再說一遍,它便會實現——但願如此!

column1: There will be no safety for anyone until Iblis is returned, Master. We must hurry and bring him back!
column2: 在伊布利斯被送回去之前,誰都不會安全,主人。我們得趕緊把他帶回來!

column1: In the name of Suleiman bin Daoud the Binder and by my Power as He Who Waits Behind: Take us to Iblis!
column2: 以縛魔者蘇萊曼·本·達伍德之名,憑我身為殿後守候者之力:帶我們去找伊布利斯!

column1: I am sorry, Master, but honor is the one thing I cannot grant you. You must earn that for yourself.
column2: 抱歉,主人,可榮譽偏偏是我無法賜予你的東西。那得靠你自己去掙得。

column1: Oh, I'm sorry Master, but I can't do that. My magic has no effect on Iblis.
column2: 噢,抱歉主人,那我辦不到。我的魔法對伊布利斯不起作用。

column1: What is your next wish, Oh Master of the Ring?
column2: 您的下一個願望是什麼呢,噢,魔戒之主?

column1: You have but one wish left. Choose carefully, Master.
column2: 您只剩最後一個願望了。慎重選擇,主人。

column1: Well, I'll try, Master of the Ring. Here goes. By my magic, may it be done!
column2: 好,我試試,魔戒之主。來囉。憑我的魔法,願它成真!

column1: There is a quiet pause while nothing happens.
column2: 一陣靜默過去,什麼也沒發生。

column1: I guess I can't grant you that wish. Sorry.
column2: 我看那個願望我實現不了。抱歉。

column1: Very well. By my word, so be it!
column2: 很好。憑我之言,就這麼定了!

column1: There is another moment of silence.
column2: 又是一陣沉默。

column1: I'm afraid I don't know that wish either, Master. What else would you like?
column2: 那個願望我恐怕也不懂,主人。您還想要什麼呢?

column1: I think I know that one. By the ring, do your thing!
column2: 這個我想我會。憑這枚戒指,使出你的本事吧!

column1: The only sound is your own groan.
column2: 唯一的聲響,是你自己的一聲呻吟。

column1: Well, Master, you can always try again. I'll get it right sometime.
column2: 嗯,主人,您隨時可以再試一次。我總有一天會弄對的。

column1: By the way, make my day!
column2: 對了,讓我露一手吧!

column1: This time, the Djinni sighs.
column2: 這一回,精靈嘆了口氣。

column1: Master, I'm sorry. I guess I can only grant you health, prowess, and then teleport you to Iblis.
column2: 主人,抱歉。我看我大概只能賜您健康、武藝,然後把您傳送到伊布利斯那兒。

column1: That didn't sound like a wish to me, Master.
column2: 那聽起來可不像是個願望啊,主人。

column1: Sand, sand, and more sand.
column2: 沙子、沙子,還是沙子。

column1: Ah, Raseir.
column2: 啊,拉希爾。

column1: Different picture, same sky.
column2: 景色不同,天空依舊。

column1: The hills are stark barren rock.
column2: 那些山丘是光禿禿的荒岩。

column1: As you reach the front door, you suddenly realize the strain of the day's events. You are in no condition to go anywhere other than bed.
column2: 走到大門口,你才猛然驚覺這一天折騰下來有多累。除了上床睡覺,你哪兒也去不了了。

column1: In case you were not aware of the matter, my dear sir, it is against the law in Raseir to be abroad at night.
column2: 親愛的先生,若您還不知道的話:在拉希爾,夜裡在外遊蕩可是違法的。

column1: You wander out of the inn and are promptly arrested for vagrancy. Had you not angered Signor Ferrari, you might have received a friendly warning first.
column2: 你晃出旅店,立刻就因遊蕩罪被捕。要不是你惹惱了費拉里閣下,說不定還能先得到一句善意的警告呢。

column1: Persona non Grata
column2: 不受歡迎的人物

column1: Ferrari is fat, but his patience is thin. Your delay has cost you his hospitality.
column2: 費拉里人胖,耐性卻薄。你的拖拖拉拉,讓你失去了他的款待。

column1: Without a place to spend the night, you are arrested for vagrancy and thrown into Khaveen's dungeons.[[[
column2: 夜裡無處棲身,你因遊蕩罪被捕,給扔進了哈維因的地牢。[[[

column1: You took too long
column2: 你拖得太久了

column1: You may call me Ugarte.
column2: 你可以叫我烏加特。

column1: We are old friends, are we not, Ferrari?
column2: 我們是老朋友了,不是嗎,費拉里?

column1: That is all that I have for you now, but I will be keeping my ears open, now that I know you are such a valuable customer.
column2: 眼下我能告訴你的就這些了。不過既然我曉得你是這麼貴重的一位顧客,我會豎起耳朵替你留意的。

column1: I make my living off of rumors.
column2: 我這個人,是靠流言過活的。

column1: You may fill your waterskins with water from my supply for merely 15 dinars. It is a bargain compared to what the Emir's men charge for it.
column2: 你只要花十五第納爾,就能用我這兒的水灌滿你的水囊。跟埃米爾的手下開的價比起來,這可是便宜得很。

column1: I will refill your waterskins for only 20 dinars. It is a bargain compared to what the Emir's men charge for it.
column2: 我替你把水囊重新灌滿,只收二十第納爾。跟埃米爾的手下開的價比起來,這可划算多了。

column1: There is much profit to be made here, if a man keeps his eyes open for opportunity.
column2: 只要一個人睜大眼睛盯著機會,這地方可有不少油水好撈。

column1: I think at times there is too much magic around here.
column2: 我有時候覺得,這一帶的魔法實在太多了。

column1: The new Emir is as big a fool as his brother was. I have heard that he hides in the Palace and will not leave his room.
column2: 新任埃米爾跟他兄長一樣是個大蠢蛋。我聽說他躲在宮裡,連房門都不肯踏出。

column1: The Palace of Raseir is very lovely and dangerous. All the wealth of Raseir remains there, and all the dungeons as well.
column2: 拉希爾的王宮既華美又危險。拉希爾所有的財富都在那兒,所有的地牢也都在那兒。

column1: It is said that money is the root of all evil. It just shows you how spiteful men can be who do not have any money.
column2: 人家說,金錢是萬惡之源。這話正好說明了,沒錢的人能有多刻薄。

column1: Khaveen is a very dangerous man to trifle with, yet there are ways to get around him.
column2: 哈維因是個招惹不得的危險人物,不過要繞過他,還是有法子的。

column1: Since the fountain does not give water, it stands to reason that someone has to. And you would not believe how much the Emir's men charge for water. It is a crime. Fortunately, my prices are much more affordable.
column2: 既然噴泉不出水,照理就得有人來供水。你絕對想不到埃米爾的手下賣水要價多高,簡直是搶劫。所幸,我的價錢親民多了。

column1: There was one Katta that remained behind when the others left. I have heard that he went into hiding, but no one has seen him since.
column2: 別的卡塔族都離開了,卻有一個留了下來。我聽說他躲了起來,從此再沒人見過他。

column1: It would be hard for someone such as myself to make a living there, so I have heard. There are so few laws to work around.
column2: 聽說像我這號人物,在那兒可難討生活了。那地方能鑽空子的法律太少啦。

column1: Khaveen is not the person in charge in Raseir, although he would like to believe otherwise.
column2: 哈維因並不是拉希爾真正當家作主的人,儘管他自個兒挺想這麼以為的。

column1: This 'Someone' told Khaveen to watch for you. It is interesting to see Khaveen play the lackey around here.
column2: 這位「某人」吩咐哈維因留意你。看哈維因在這兒當條走狗,還挺有意思的。

column1: How else is an honest businessman to make a living around here?
column2: 不然,一個老實本分的生意人在這一帶還能怎麼討生活呢?

column1: About that one, I can say no more. It is said that the whisper of his name will draw his attention. I have no wish for him to notice me.
column2: 關於那一位,我不能再多說了。據說只要低聲提起他的名字,就會引來他的注意。我可不希望他盯上我。

column1: No! Nothing! I know nothing about him. It is best such a name be forgotten.
column2: 不!沒什麼!關於他我什麼都不知道。那種名字,最好還是忘了乾淨。

column1: I do what I can to help those poor unfortunates who live around here. Water is my specialty.
column2: 我盡我所能,幫助住在這一帶的可憐人。賣水是我的本行。

column1: I am always fond of those who are willing to pay for my services.
column2: 凡是肯為我的服務掏錢的人,我向來都挺喜歡的。

column1: Khaveen likes to be the one to give the orders around here. He is not happy when he is forced to follow them.
column2: 哈維因喜歡在這兒當那個發號施令的人。一旦被迫聽人差遣,他可就不痛快了。

column1: It is said that, ah, Someone, is waiting for someone to open a door to something. I have had no desire to learn more about it. Things dealing with magic are best left alone.
column2: 據說,呃,某人,正等著有人去打開一扇通往某物的門。我可沒興趣多打聽。沾上魔法的事,最好還是別去碰。

column1: Ah, yes. I have heard it is a haven for the extremely wealthy. Certainly there is much opportunity to be had there.
column2: 啊,沒錯。我聽說那兒是巨富之人的天堂。那地方肯定有不少機會可撈。

column1: It is said that the only way to leave Raseir's dungeons is to die.
column2: 人家說,要離開拉希爾的地牢,唯一的法子就是一死。

column1: Please, sir, my dinars?
column2: 先生,勞駕,我的第納爾呢?

column1: I'm afraid there is little I can tell you that would be of any use.
column2: 恐怕我能告訴你的,沒幾句派得上用場。

column1: I am very sorry, but such matters should not be your concern.
column2: 非常抱歉,可這種事情,不該由您來操心。

column1: You will know what I speak of when you see it.
column2: 等你親眼見著了,自然就明白我說的是什麼了。

column1: Wilmer is like a son to me.
column2: 威爾默對我來說,就像親兒子一樣。

column1: You go past the bar through the door over there. It will be ready for you at nighttime. You need not worry about how much it will cost. We will just put it on your tab.
column2: 你穿過酒吧,走那邊那道門。到了夜裡,房間就會替你備好。費用您不必操心,我們記在您的帳上便是。

column1: When it gets dark outside, the guards patrol the city in search of fools to feed the dungeons.
column2: 天一黑,衛兵就會在城裡巡邏,專找些傻瓜去填地牢。

column1: Despite Marete's charm, I have never visited Silmaria before. My life's work has brought me to Raseir, and here I shall remain until that goal is fulfilled.
column2: 儘管瑪雷特風光迷人,我卻從未去過席爾瑪利亞。我畢生的事業把我帶到了拉希爾,在那目標達成之前,我會一直留在這兒。

column1: That is not relevant to matters at hand.
column2: 那跟眼前的事無關。

column1: I am Ferrari. You may call me Signor Ferrari.
column2: 我就是費拉里。你可以稱我費拉里閣下。

column1: There are no secrets in Raseir. At least, none I am willing to spread.
column2: 拉希爾沒有什麼秘密。至少,沒有什麼是我願意往外傳的。

column1: The fountain of Raseir dried up last year around the time that the Emir first was missed.
column2: 拉希爾的噴泉是去年乾涸的,差不多就在埃米爾頭一回失蹤的那陣子。

column1: The Blue Parrot is the very center for life in Raseir. Everybody goes to the Blue Parrot.
column2: 藍鸚鵡正是拉希爾生活的中心。人人都往藍鸚鵡跑。

column1: This is the Thieves' Guild, my dear sir, and I am the man who controls it.
column2: 這裡是盜賊公會,親愛的先生,而我正是掌控它的人。

column1: Money is not truly what matters in this world. It is what one gains from having money that is important.
column2: 金錢本身並不是這世上真正要緊的東西。要緊的,是有了錢之後能換來什麼。

column1: The current Emir is but a stepping stone for the one behind the throne.
column2: 現任埃米爾,不過是寶座背後那位的一塊墊腳石罷了。

column1: I do not believe in such superstition as Ugarte. Still, the man who caused the overthrow of the Emir is not a person whose name to use in vain.
column2: 我可不像烏加特那樣迷信。不過,那個一手扳倒埃米爾的人,他的名字確實不該隨意掛在嘴邊。

column1: Information is an important commodity in Raseir.
column2: 在拉希爾,情報是一種重要的商品。

column1: You have to respect a man like Khaveen. He does his job so very well.
column2: 像哈維因這樣的人,你不得不佩服。他把份內的事做得太好了。

column1: I will warn you, that name is best not mentioned around here.
column2: 我警告你,那個名字最好別在這一帶提起。

column1: There is nothing about Shapeir which interests me, except for what you have told me.
column2: 夏皮爾沒有什麼能引起我興趣的,除了你方才告訴我的那些。

column1: It is said that all the wealth of Raseir remains in the palace. That is not entirely true.
column2: 人家說,拉希爾所有的財富都藏在王宮裡。那話可不全對。

column1: I came to Raseir a little over a year and a half ago now. A man of my stature has no problem making a profit out of any situation. It is not profit that brought me here, though. I came seeking... ah, but I see that I am boring you. Enough of that subject.
column2: 我來拉希爾,如今算來已一年半多了。像我這般身分地位的人,從任何境況裡撈油水都不成問題。不過,把我引來這兒的並不是利潤。我來,是為了尋找……啊,可我看得出我把你給說悶了。這話題就到此為止吧。

column1: The situation in Raseir has at its center a good deal of magic. I, being a prudent man, do not let such things concern me.
column2: 拉希爾如今的局面,核心就纏著一大堆魔法。我這人謹慎,這種事我才不去沾。

column1: Ugarte would still be here if he had not tried to find more information out for you. They made an example of him for those that are too curious.
column2: 要不是烏加特替你多打聽了些消息,他這會兒還好端端在這呢。他們拿他開刀,給那些太愛多管閒事的人殺雞儆猴。

column1: Come now, we both know what you are good at. The question is, just how good are you?
column2: 行了,你擅長什麼咱倆心知肚明。問題只在於——你到底有多行?

column1: The new regime as ostensibly led by Khaveen certainly altered Raseir.
column2: 表面上由哈維因掌權的新政權,的確把拉希爾整個變了個樣。

column1: If you wish to buy a drink, you must go talk to Wilmer.
column2: 想買酒喝,就去找威爾默。

column1: Life is a game, my friend. It is all a matter of what cards you are dealt and how you play them.
column2: 朋友,人生就是一場牌局。全看你拿到什麼牌,又怎麼打。

column1: Do not let such matters bother you.
column2: 這種事就別放在心上了。

column1: You should ask Ugarte what services he provides.
column2: 你該去問問烏加特,他能幫人辦什麼事。

column1: I have little interest in such matters.
column2: 這種事我可沒什麼興趣。

column1: djinn sling
column2: 精靈一甩

column1: You will not forget one, once you have tried one.
column2: 喝過一杯,你這輩子都忘不了。

column1: rasierish coffee
column2: 拉希爾咖啡

column1: This city is known for its coffee.
column2: 這座城就以咖啡聞名。

column1: Where else can you get water in this city? The Waterman only comes once a week.
column2: 這城裡還能上哪兒弄到水?送水人一週才來一趟。

column1: water man
column2: 送水人

column1: The Emir's man sells a week's worth of water for 10 dinars.
column2: 埃米爾的手下,賣一週份的水要十第納爾。

column1: We sell water for 50 centimes, Raseirish Coffee for 75 centimes, and Djinn Slings for 1 dinar.
column2: 我們這兒水五十生丁,拉希爾咖啡七十五生丁,精靈一甩一第納爾。

column1: That is Signor Ferrari to you, punk.
column2: 給我叫費拉里先生,小子。

column1: Signor Ferrari is to be respected and obeyed.
column2: 費拉里先生這人,得敬著、順著。

column1: If you wish to order a drink, order one. Otherwise, go away.
column2: 想點酒就點,不然就給我走開。

column1: What do you want?
column2: 你想幹嘛?

column1: order raseirish coffee
column2: 點拉希爾咖啡

column1: order coffee
column2: 點咖啡

column1: buy raseirish coffee
column2: 買拉希爾咖啡

column1: buy coffee
column2: 買咖啡

column1: raseirish coffee
column2: 拉希爾咖啡

column1: raseir coffee
column2: 拉希爾咖啡

column1: order djinn sling
column2: 點精靈一甩

column1: order djinn
column2: 點精靈

column1: order sling
column2: 點一甩

column1: buy djinn sling
column2: 買精靈一甩

column1: buy djinn
column2: 買精靈

column1: buy sling
column2: 買一甩

column1: order water
column2: 點水

column1: order rol
column2: 點酒

column1: I only serve the good stuff, and that ain't it.
column2: 我只供應好貨,那玩意兒可不算。

column1: You think that is so important? That is nothing to me.
column2: 你以為那很要緊?對我來說啥也不是。

column1: Ferrari avoids your greeting as he studiously swats flies.
column2: 費拉里只顧著一本正經地拍蒼蠅,根本不理你的招呼。

column1: You're already seated, remember?
column2: 你不是已經坐下了嗎?

column1: If you wish to sit, just walk over to the table and do it.
column2: 想坐就走到桌邊坐下啊。

column1: You speak quite openly of these things. That is a very unhealthy habit around here.
column2: 這些事你倒是說得挺敞亮。在這地方,這可是個要命的壞習慣。

column1: Do not let things concern you so.
column2: 別把這些事太放在心上。

column1: Ha ha, heh! You seem very sure of yourself, don't you?
column2: 哈哈,呵!你對自己倒是挺有把握,是吧?

column1: Ferrari just stares at the black bird.
column2: 費拉里只是直勾勾盯著那隻黑鳥。

column1: Just tell me if you have the bird!
column2: 你倒是說,那隻鳥到底在不在你手上!

column1: Bring the bird here, quickly. Give it to me!
column2: 快把那隻鳥拿來。交給我!

column1: Ferrari just frowns at you.
column2: 費拉里只是衝你皺眉頭。

column1: That is all very interesting.
column2: 這可真是有意思。

column1: You should hurry; there is little time.
column2: 你得快點,沒多少時間了。

column1: Go and bother someone else.
column2: 去煩別人吧。

column1: If you're going to let someone else in on your illegal activities, you should at least pick a trustworthy person.
column2: 要把見不得光的勾當拉別人下水,起碼也挑個靠得住的人吧。

column1: Get lost, punk.
column2: 滾遠點,小子。

column1: Wilmer glares at you out of the corner of his eye, then returns to wiping the bar.
column2: 威爾默斜眼狠狠瞪了你一下,又回頭去擦吧台。

column1: Ah, I am most pleased to make your acquaintance. It is always so good to meet new associates with which to do business.
column2: 啊,能認識您真是太榮幸了。能結交能一起做生意的新夥伴,向來叫人開心。

column1: Oh, you are welcome, sir, most welcome.
column2: 噢,不客氣,先生,十二萬分地不客氣。

column1: If I shared your eagerness to spread that information, you'd be spending the night in an a place a lot more uncomfortable than this inn.
column2: 要是我跟你一樣急著把那消息往外傳,你今晚過夜的地方,可比這客棧難受多了。

column1: But sir, we have not yet done business. Surely what I have to tell you is worth three paltry dinars?
column2: 可是先生,咱倆生意還沒談成呢。我要告訴您的東西,總值區區三第納爾吧?

column1: bargain information
column2: 講價買消息

column1: bargain news
column2: 講價買消息

column1: Well, seeing as you are a hero and all, perhaps we could settle for say, three dinars?
column2: 好吧,看在您是位英雄的份上,要不就三第納爾,成交?

column1: I am sorry, but I am just a poor businessman. Surely a hero such as yourself can spare a few measly dinars for information which might save his life?
column2: 抱歉啊,我不過是個窮做買賣的。像您這樣的英雄,總擠得出區區幾枚第納爾,換一條也許能保命的消息吧?

column1: A thousand pardons, sir, but what is it you wish to bargain for?
column2: 千萬個對不住,先生,您到底想講價買什麼呢?

column1: Most interesting, sir. I always enjoy hearing things from those outside of Raseir.
column2: 太有意思了,先生。我向來愛聽拉希爾城外來的人說事。

column1: Come join me here, my dear sir. You are still a novelty in Raseir, and I take a vested interest in all things new. It is time we discussed some things together, you and I.
column2: 過來陪我坐坐,我親愛的先生。在拉希爾你還是個新鮮人物,而我對一切新事物都頗有興趣。咱倆,是時候談談一些事了。

column1: If you have no further need of me, sir, there are other thirsty customers in need of my wares.
column2: 先生,若您沒別的事找我,還有別的客人渴著,等我的貨呢。

column1: I said give me the bird!
column2: 我說了,把鳥給我!

column1: Perhaps you did not hear me. I asked if you have the bird.
column2: 也許你沒聽清。我問你,那隻鳥在不在你手上。

column1: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed. He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero. Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning as he presses two thin daggers into your back.
column2: 費拉里盯著你,眼裡閃著陰狠的貪婪。他說,他可不會栽在一個半吊子英雄手裡。你忽然覺得背後有動靜,回頭一看,威爾默正咧著嘴笑,兩把薄薄的匕首已抵住你的後背。

column1: The last words you hear are, 'It seems I must keep searching for the Falcon.'
column2: 你聽見的最後一句話是:「看來,我還得繼續找那隻獵鷹了。」

column1: He still don't got it
column2: 他還是沒搞到手

column1: Even as you start, you feel a dagger strike you and see another gleam in Wilmer's hand before he throws it. Then you see nothing at all.
column2: 你才剛動,就感到一把匕首扎進身體,還瞥見威爾默手裡另一把寒光一閃,接著便被擲了出來。然後,你眼前一片漆黑。

column1: Don't DO that!
column2: 別給我來這套!

column1: You find you do not have the money. Ugarte quickly loses interest in you.
column2: 你發現自己根本沒這筆錢。烏加特立刻對你失了興致。

column1: Well, what kind of hero are you? You come into town acting like you are a wealthy man and make poor honest businessmen such as myself work for nothing.
column2: 嘿,你算哪門子英雄?進城時擺出一副闊佬的派頭,害我這種老實巴交的窮生意人白忙一場。

column1: You may live to regret arriving here with no money. Do you think to live on charity? Wilmer, show our poor hero here to quarters more fitting his purse.
column2: 身上沒帶錢就跑來這兒,你早晚會後悔的。你以為能靠施捨過活?威爾默,給咱們這位窮英雄安排個更配得上他錢包的住處。

column1: You are led out of the Blue Parrot, where you are quickly arrested for vagrancy. You spend a long while in Khaveen's dungeons, after all.
column2: 你被人帶出藍鸚鵡客棧,當即以流浪罪被捕。到頭來,你在哈維因的地牢裡蹲了好長一段日子。

column1: Now you're broke AND busted!
column2: 這下你又破產、又被抓進去了!

column1: It seems you do not drink. Perhaps you do not trust my drinks? Ha ha, heh. You are indeed a dangerous man. I like that. You will do well in Raseir.
column2: 看來你滴酒不沾。莫非是信不過我的酒?哈哈,呵。你果然是個危險人物。我喜歡。你在拉希爾會混得很好。

column1: But now, my friend, it is time we discussed some things together, you and I.
column2: 不過現在嘛,朋友,咱倆是時候談談一些事了。

column1: We will reserve a room here for you tonight. You will need to return here before dark, for it is forbidden to be out at night.
column2: 我們今晚替你在這兒留一間房。你得在天黑前趕回來,夜裡是禁止外出的。

column1: Join me this evening and we will talk some more. I have some information which may prove useful to you. I will see you later. I have some thinking to do now.
column2: 今晚過來陪我,咱們再多聊聊。我手上有些消息,也許對你有用。回頭見。我現在得好好想想。

column1: We only accept Raseirian coin here, foreigner.
column2: 外鄉人,我們這兒只收拉希爾的錢。

column1: Hey! You want a free drink, go find a free world!
column2: 嘿!想喝免費的酒,找個免費的世界去吧!

column1: You give Signor Ferrari the bird. A puzzled look crosses his face, and suddenly you realize that he was referring to the Black Falcon.
column2: 你朝費拉里先生比了根中指。他臉上閃過一絲疑惑,你這才猛然意識到——他說的「鳥」指的是那隻黑獵鷹。

column1: When Signor Ferrari realizes that in fact you do not have the Black Falcon, he frowns and motions quickly to Wilmer.
column2: 費拉里先生發現你手上其實沒有黑獵鷹,便皺起眉頭,飛快地朝威爾默使了個眼色。

column1: Before you can turn, you hear the characteristic 'thuk' of a knife imbedding itself into a back. Your back.
column2: 你還來不及轉身,就聽見一聲刀子扎進後背特有的「噗」聲——扎進的,正是你的後背。

column1: You should have given him the bird
column2: 你當初該把那隻鳥交給他的

column1: Ferrari looks at you, his eyes glinting with malign greed. He says he will not be thwarted by some half-baked hero. Suddenly you feel something behind you and find Wilmer grinning with two thin daggers against your back.
column2: 費拉里盯著你,眼裡閃著陰狠的貪婪。他說,他可不會栽在一個半吊子英雄手裡。你忽然覺得背後有動靜,回頭一看,威爾默正咧著嘴笑,兩把薄薄的匕首已抵在你的後背上。

column1: The last words you hear are "The Falcon... I finally have the Falcon."
column2: 你聽見的最後一句話是:「獵鷹……我終於拿到獵鷹了。」

column1: meYou give him the bird
column2: 你把那隻鳥交給了他

column1: The last words you hear are, "It seems I must keep searching for The Falcon."
column2: 你聽見的最後一句話是:「看來,我還得繼續找那隻獵鷹了。」

column1: obHe still don't got it
column2: 他還是沒搞到手

column1: No? No? What do you mean, 'no'?
column2: 不?不?你說「不」是什麼意思?

column1: You leave Ferrari a sorely disappointed man.
column2: 你走的時候,留下一個失望透頂的費拉里。

column1: Since the fountain has dried up, the only place to get water is from the Emir's men. The only place other than myself, that is. It is surprising how much water is worth to one who is thirsty.
column2: 自從噴泉乾涸,要弄水就只能找埃米爾的手下了。當然,除了我這兒以外。對一個渴壞了的人來說,水能值多少錢,實在叫人吃驚。

column1: If you wish to discuss something to our mutual advantage, come sit down.
column2: 想談點對咱倆都有好處的事,就過來坐下。

column1: Get to your feet first, Hero.
column2: 先給我站起來,英雄。

column1: It is very dull, but it does quench the thirst.
column2: 味道平淡得很,不過倒真能解渴。

column1: It tastes very bitter, yet the flavor is oddly likable.
column2: 喝起來苦得很,可那股味道又莫名地討人喜歡。

column1: Slurred speech... unsteady gait... Yup, you're dead, all right.
column2: 說話含糊不清……走路東倒西歪……沒錯,你這是醉死了,八九不離十。

column1: Been slung once too often
column2: 「甩」得太多回了

column1: Wow! You now know where the drink got its name. You feel as if you've just been slung by a Djinn.
column2: 哇!你這下總算明白這酒名是怎麼來的了。你只覺得自己彷彿被一個精靈狠狠甩了一把。

column1: The glass is crusty and filled with warm, stale water.
column2: 杯子結著一層垢,裡頭裝著溫吞吞、發了餿的水。

column1: Inside the glass is a thick, black, molasses-like substance that can be nothing other than Raseirish Coffee.
column2: 杯裡是濃稠、烏黑、像糖蜜一樣的東西,除了拉希爾咖啡不作他想。

column1: There appears to be steam rising out of the glass. The Djinn Sling, the house specialty at the Blue Parrot, reminds you a bit of the Dragon's Breath they served in Spielburg.
column2: 杯子裡彷彿冒著熱氣。這杯精靈一甩——藍鸚鵡客棧的招牌——讓你不禁想起當年在史畢柏格喝過的「龍息」。

column1: Trying to turn the tables on the patrons of the Blue Parrot Inn is never a good idea!
column2: 想對藍鸚鵡客棧的常客來個反將一軍,從來都不是什麼好主意!

column1: Signor Ferrari is ignoring you.
column2: 費拉里先生正不理你呢。

column1: Please do not disturb me. I need to think.
column2: 請別來打擾我。我得好好想想。

column1: Welcome to the Blue Parrot. I am Ferrari, Signor Ferrari. Sit down, my dear sir, relax. You are among friends here.
column2: 歡迎光臨藍鸚鵡。我是費拉里,費拉里先生。坐下吧,我親愛的先生,放輕鬆。在這兒你身邊都是朋友。

column1: There's no need to sit at this time.
column2: 這會兒沒必要坐下。

column1: Will you look what crawled in from the desert? He has been out in the sun so long, his hair has gone yellow!
column2: 瞧瞧這是從沙漠裡爬進來的什麼玩意兒?在太陽底下曬太久,頭髮都曬黃啦!

column1: Now, now, Wilmer, he is a stranger to our fair city. You wouldn't want to give the wrong opinion about the hospitality of Raseir.
column2: 行了行了,威爾默,他可是初來咱們這座好城的外鄉人。你總不想讓人對拉希爾的待客之道留下壞印象吧。

column1: Come here, stranger. I see you have already made the acquaintance of Khaveen, our illustrious captain of the Raseirian Guard.
column2: 過來,外鄉人。我看你已經見過哈維因了——咱們拉希爾衛隊那位赫赫有名的隊長。

column1: So perhaps you already have an idea of Raseirian hospitality. Come here, sit down, and let us have a little chat, you and I.
column2: 那你大概已經領教過拉希爾的待客之道了。過來,坐下,咱倆好好聊上幾句。

column1: Let us drink to our fortunate meeting!
column2: 來,為咱們這場有緣的相逢乾一杯!

column1: I am very pleased to make your acquaintance. I have heard much about you already.
column2: 能認識你我非常高興。關於你的事,我早就聽說了不少。

column1: Ugarte here provides an invaluable service to this community. He obtains things for others which are otherwise unattainable.
column2: 這位烏加特,替這一帶提供了無價的服務。別人弄不到的東西,他都能替人搞到手。

column1: For a nominal fee, of course.
column2: 當然嘍,得收一筆象徵性的費用。

column1: It seems he has some information for you.
column2: 看樣子,他手上有些消息要給你。

column1: A rumor, perhaps, that may influence your actions. I will be happy to share such information for, say, five dinars?
column2: 也許是個能左右你下一步的傳言。這消息我很樂意分享——要不就五第納爾?

column1: Do you have it? Do you have the bird?
column2: 你拿到了嗎?那隻鳥在你手上嗎?

column1: You slept fitfully, dreaming of rat-like monsters pulling at you. In your dream, you felt cruelly exposed, but you re-covered.
column2: 你睡得很不安穩,夢見一群老鼠似的怪物在拉扯你。夢裡你覺得自己赤裸裸地暴露在外,毫無遮掩,但你又把自己重新蓋好了。

column1: Signor Ferrari sends his regrets, but he had some rather disturbing news. He will talk to you this evening.
column2: 費拉里先生托我向你致歉,他收到了一些頗為棘手的消息。今晚他再跟你談。

column1: It is a pity about our friend Ugarte, is it not? I can see you are as broken up by the affair as I am.
column2: 咱們那位朋友烏加特的事,真是可惜啊,是不是?我看得出,你跟我一樣為這事傷透了心。

column1: However, it seems he asked someone the wrong questions about you. That was just a performance put on for your benefit this morning.
column2: 不過嘛,看樣子他為了打聽你的事,問錯了人。今早那一齣,不過是專演給你看的一場戲罷了。

column1: Khaveen has known about Ugarte's water smuggling for a long time. A most dangerous man, Khaveen. Almost as dangerous as you are.
column2: 哈維因早就知道烏加特走私水的勾當了。哈維因這人,危險得很。差不多跟你一樣危險。

column1: As you enter, the sounds of the bar go still.
column2: 你一進門,酒館裡的喧鬧聲頓時靜了下來。

column1: It has been a pleasure dealing with you, my dear sir.
column2: 跟您打交道真是愉快,我親愛的先生。

column1: The drink is sweet, with a very bitter aftertaste.
column2: 這酒甜甜的,後勁卻苦得很。

column1: You drink the bitter, thick liquid.
column2: 你喝下那濃稠苦澀的液體。

column1: It has an odd, almost chemical, taste.
column2: 喝起來有股怪味,幾乎像化學藥劑似的。

column1: You feel strange for a few seconds, but the feeling passes. Ferrari starts to ask some very probing questions, but you evade most of them.
column2: 你恍惚了幾秒,那感覺隨即過去。費拉里開始旁敲側擊地盤問,但大半問題都被你含糊帶了過去。

column1: You are a very closed-mouth individual. You handle your drink very well. I trust you will handle yourself in Raseir as well.
column2: 你這人嘴可真嚴。酒量也好得很。我信你在拉希爾,也能把自己照看得一樣好。

column1: You feel a bit odd. Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to question you. He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly. You are having a difficult time trying not to say too much.
column2: 你覺得有點不對勁。費拉里先生衝你一笑,開始盤問起來。他對夏皮爾尤其感興趣,問得鉅細靡遺。你費了好大勁,才忍住沒說漏嘴。

column1: Humm, there is much to what you say, and much more to what you don't say, I suspect.
column2: 唔,你說的話裡頭大有文章,而你沒說的,只怕更耐人尋味。

column1: You start to feel a bit dizzy. Signor Ferrari smiles at you and starts to ask questions about yourself. You find yourself answering, telling your friend all about your adventures.
column2: 你開始覺得有點暈。費拉里先生衝你一笑,開始打聽起你的事來。你竟不由自主地一一作答,把自己所有的冒險經歷都告訴了這位「朋友」。

column1: He is particularly interested in Shapeir, and questions you thoroughly. After a while, though, you start to get a headache. Ferrari frowns.
column2: 他對夏皮爾尤其感興趣,問得鉅細靡遺。可過了一陣子,你開始頭疼起來。費拉里皺起了眉。

column1: You have led a most interesting and charmed life, it seems. But it would take a miracle to restore Raseir, and Khaveen has outlawed miracles.
column2: 看樣子,你過的這日子可真是又精彩、又有福氣。可要讓拉希爾恢復原樣,恐怕得靠奇蹟——而哈維因,早就把奇蹟列為禁忌了。

column1: You are very dangerous to me, for your actions will have serious repercussions. Nevertheless, you may have your uses.
column2: 你對我來說非常危險,因為你的一舉一動都會掀起大波瀾。不過話說回來,你或許還有點用處。

column1: There is a large, closed door blocking your way. Apparently, the sleeping rooms are only accessible at night.
column2: 一扇又大又緊閉的門擋住了你的去路。看樣子,客房只有到了夜裡才開放。

column1: I should like to speak with you tomorrow about the use of your skills. As head of all illegal activity in Raseir, I am an influential and respected man, and I have no intention of letting an amateur jeopardize my position, if you understand my meaning.
column2: 明天我想跟你談談你那身本事該怎麼用。身為拉希爾一切非法勾當的頭目,我可是個有頭有臉、有權有勢的人物,絕不打算讓一個外行壞了我的地位——你聽得懂我的意思吧。

column1: Good night for now.
column2: 那就先道聲晚安了。

column1: If you want a drink, come up to the front of the bar.
column2: 想喝酒,就到吧台前頭來。

column1: The bar is too dirty, you don't want to touch it.
column2: 吧台髒得很,你可不想碰它。

column1: Now, now. Pleasure before business. Here come our drinks even as we speak.
column2: 行了行了。先享樂,再談正事。瞧,咱們的酒這不就上來了。

column1: Ferrari avoids your eye and your question as he studiously swats flies.
column2: 費拉里一本正經地拍著蒼蠅,既不看你的眼睛,也不答你的話。

column1: I do not know what Ugarte has to say, and I am most intrigued. Perhaps you should relieve our curiosity and pay the man.
column2: 我也不曉得烏加特要說些什麼,實在好奇得很。要不你就行行好,把錢付給他,也好了卻咱們的好奇心。

column1: I asked you to SIT DOWN! Please.
column2: 我叫你坐下!拜託。

column1: If you have the bird, give it to me. Quickly!
column2: 要是那隻鳥在你手上,就交給我。快點!

column1: Ferrari just gestures you away with his fly-swatter. You find the bartender glaring at you.
column2: 費拉里只用他的蒼蠅拍揮手把你趕開。你發現酒保正狠狠瞪著你。

column1: Ferrari isn't interested in the object you offer.
column2: 費拉里對你遞上的東西沒興趣。

column1: Do as Signor Ferrari orders.
column2: 照費拉里先生的吩咐去做。

column1: Wilmer isn't interested in the object you offer.
column2: 威爾默對你遞上的東西沒興趣。

column1: You don't need the oil lamp.
column2: 你用不著那盞油燈。

column1: You don't see one you could get without being noticed.
column2: 你看不到哪一盞能神不知鬼不覺地弄到手。

column1: If you're going to steal a lamp, you'd better choose one in a less conspicuous corner of the room.
column2: 要偷油燈,你最好挑房裡某個比較不顯眼的角落那盞。

column1: You stealthily nab the oil lamp.
column2: 你悄悄地順走了那盞油燈。

column1: Don't bother trying to talk to the thieves.
column2: 別費勁去跟那些盜賊搭話了。

column1: The thieves carry on with their own conversation, not even stopping to glance at your card.
column2: 盜賊們自顧自地聊著,連停下來瞄一眼你的名片都懶。

column1: They aren't interested in the object you offer.
column2: 他們對你遞上的東西沒興趣。

column1: He's too inebriated to even see it, let alone take it from you.
column2: 他醉得連看都看不清,更別說從你手上接過去了。

column1: He can't hear a word you're saying. He's passed out.
column2: 他一個字都聽不進去——他早就醉倒了。

column1: The oil lamp is burning dimly.
column2: 那盞油燈火光昏暗地燃著。

column1: Trying to snuff out another patron's flame in this place may well end up with you being snuffed.
column2: 在這種地方想吹熄別位客人的燈火,搞不好被「吹熄」的會是你自己。

column1: There's no reason to blow the flame out.
column2: 沒理由把火吹熄。

column1: You think back to Ferrari's warning, and decide that practicing on this lock is a little too dangerous. Better wait until evening when the door will be unlocked.
column2: 你想起費拉里的警告,覺得拿這道鎖來練手有點太冒險了。還是等到傍晚門解鎖再說吧。

column1: It is locked. Apparently, the sleeping rooms are only accessible at night.
column2: 鎖著呢。看來臥房得等到晚上才進得去。

column1: You don't know them, so keep your hands to yourself.
column2: 你又不認識他們,手別亂伸。

column1: Nothing short of a bucket of ice and a good night's sleep is going to wake this guy up!
column2: 除非澆他一桶冰水、再讓他好好睡一覺,否則這傢伙是醒不過來了!

column1: Leave him there. He's the mascot.
column2: 讓他待著吧,他可是這裡的吉祥物。

column1: Polly want a respirator?
column2: 波利想要個呼吸器嗎?

column1: You can't take the perch; it's stuck to the parrot.
column2: 你拿不走那根棲木,它跟鸚鵡黏在一塊兒。

column1: It's a little late to talk him down from there.
column2: 現在才想勸他下來,有點太遲了。

column1: You were never one for interior design, but you wouldn't mind tearing down those drapes in hopes that it would liven this place up a little.
column2: 你向來不擅長室內設計,不過倒不介意把那些窗簾扯下來,指望能讓這地方活絡一點。

column1: Who knows what kind of germs reside in these drapes? Definitely not a good place to be sticking your mouth.
column2: 天曉得這些窗簾上窩著哪些細菌?把嘴湊上去絕對不是好主意。

column1: You can't take anything off the shelves, nor can you reach them.
column2: 你拿不走架上任何東西,也根本搆不著那些架子。

column1: Even the good old 'spit and polish' technique won't sanitize this glassware.
column2: 就算使出老招「吐口水再擦亮」,也消不了這些玻璃杯上的毒。

column1: No one pays any attention to you.
column2: 沒人理會你。

column1: It looks fizzy and dangerous.
column2: 看起來冒著泡,挺危險的。

column1: It looks like brown ooze.
column2: 看起來像一灘褐色的黏液。

column1: I am sorry, sir, but I do not accept foreign coin.
column2: 抱歉,先生,小的不收外地的錢幣。

column1: Khaveen has sent the word out to his men that you are to be watched at all times. It seems that, ah, Someone is very interested in your actions. You should be aware that Khaveen does not usually greet strangers at the gate. You were expected.
column2: 哈維因已經放話給手下,要他們時時刻刻盯著你。看來啊,「某位人物」對你的一舉一動很感興趣。你該明白,哈維因平常可不會親自到城門口迎接陌生人。你的到來,是早在意料之中的。

column1: Why would He take an interest?
column2: 祂為什麼會感興趣?

column1: There is the little matter of a prophecy to be fulfilled, and this one may be the one.
column2: 有那麼一樁預言尚待應驗的小事,而這一位,或許正是預言中的人。

column1: I suspected before that you were a very dangerous man to have around, %s. It seems I am correct.
column2: 我先前就懷疑,留你在身邊是個很危險的人物,%s。看來我沒猜錯。

column1: Still, it will be very interesting to see how this game plays out. You may stay tonight in the first room past the door on the left.
column2: 不過呢,瞧瞧這場好戲怎麼收場,倒也挺有意思。今晚你可以住在左邊那道門進去的第一間房。

column1: It seems before I give you what you want, you should give me what I want. It is only fair, no? My dinars, please.
column2: 看來在我給你想要的東西之前,你得先給我我想要的。這才公平,不是嗎?把第納爾交出來吧。

column1: Ugarte is not interested in the object you offer.
column2: 烏加特對你遞上的東西沒興趣。

column1: The man at the corner table uses his whip as a fly-swatter.
column2: 角落桌邊那名男子,拿他的鞭子當蒼蠅拍使。

column1: You have no waterskins to fill.
column2: 你沒有水袋可裝。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskin is already full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現水袋早就裝滿了。

column1: You check and realize that your waterskins are already full.
column2: 你檢查了一下,發現水袋早就都裝滿了。

column1: Forgive me, %s, but I do not accept foreign coin.
column2: 請見諒,%s,小的不收外地的錢幣。

column1: Surely, sir, you did not intend to buy water with your good looks?
column2: 先生,您該不會打算拿您那副好相貌來換水喝吧?

column1: Normally my water costs 20 dinars, sir, but for you I will only require 15.
column2: 先生,我的水平常要價二十第納爾,不過看在您份上,只收您十五就好。

column1: My water will cost you 20 dinars, sir.
column2: 先生,我的水要收您二十第納爾。

column1: Ugarte takes your money and pours some water from his supply into your waterskins.
column2: 烏加特收下你的錢,從他的存貨裡舀了些水倒進你的水袋。

column1: Surely a man such as yourself can easily afford 20 dinars?
column2: 像您這樣的人物,區區二十第納爾總出得起吧?

column1: So, how do you feel about this as a hole?
column2: 那麼,你覺得這個鬼地方怎麼樣?

column1: If you don't mind me saying so, you look like a man with a mission. I like that in a man.
column2: 恕我直言,你看上去像個身負使命的人。我就欣賞這種人。

column1: But perhaps a word of caution. In a city such as Raseir, there are many depths to the problems.
column2: 不過容我提醒一句。在拉希爾這樣的城市裡,問題往往深不見底。

column1: Do not try to change things here too quickly.
column2: 別想太快改變這裡的一切。

column1: You will find that the system is not so bad, once you get used to it.
column2: 你會發現,這套規矩一旦習慣了,其實也沒那麼糟。

column1: However, it would be very dangerous to try to alter that system. You might make enemies.
column2: 然而,妄想去動搖這套規矩可是極其危險的。你會樹敵。

column1: Enough of such morbid thoughts, though. Perhaps we should share a drink in honor of our new relationship.
column2: 不過,別再想這些晦氣事了。也許我們該共飲一杯,慶祝咱倆的新交情。

column1: Would you prefer Raseirish Coffee or a Djinn Sling?
column2: 你想來杯拉希爾咖啡,還是精靈賓治?

column1: I wish to tell you a story, a fable that has a moral.
column2: 我想說個故事給你聽,一則蘊含寓意的寓言。

column1: How you choose to react to this moral is your own life.
column2: 你要怎麼看待這寓意,那是你自己的人生。

column1: As you well know, the old Emir was deposed last year, and his brother took over the Palace.
column2: 你也很清楚,老埃米爾去年被罷黜了,他的兄弟接掌了王宮。

column1: Raseir used to be a place with very few laws and restrictions; a place where the streets were filled with people and merchants and water flowed free from the fountain.
column2: 拉希爾從前是個律法與管制都極少的地方;街上滿是人潮與商賈,泉水自噴泉裡汩汩流淌,任人取用。

column1: That has changed with the coming of Khaveen and his master.
column2: 隨著哈維因和他主子的到來,這一切都變了。

column1: A new order has been established. The weak have perished or fled.
column2: 新的秩序已然建立。弱者不是喪命,就是逃離。

column1: Only the strong who adapted remain. Those that could not adapt were destroyed.
column2: 只有懂得順應的強者留了下來。無法順應的,都被剷除了。

column1: You are strong, but it remains to be seen how adaptable you are.
column2: 你很強,但你究竟有多懂得順應,還有待瞧瞧。

column1: It would be a great shame for you to wind up in Khaveen's dungeons.
column2: 要是你最後落進哈維因的地牢,那可太可惜了。

column1: Enough of that for now. I have someone I would like you to meet.
column2: 這話題先到這兒。我有個人想介紹你認識。

column1: Ugarte, I would like to introduce you to %s.
column2: 烏加特,容我向你介紹%s。

column1: They tend to call attention to themselves, and this gives the profession a bad name.
column2: 那些人總愛招搖,反倒給這一行敗壞了名聲。

column1: If you are as skilled as I have heard, then I might have use for you.
column2: 要是你真如傳聞中那般身手了得,我或許用得上你。

column1: However, first you must prove yourself. Are you willing to do for me a small favor?
column2: 不過,你得先證明自己。你願不願意替我辦件小事?

column1: Do you know what we have here?
column2: 你知道我手上這是什麼嗎?

column1: The sole reason I came to this desert-driven country.
column2: 這正是我來到這個風沙之國的唯一理由。

column1: For seventeen years, I have sought the black bird.
column2: 整整十七年,我一直在尋找那隻黑鳥。

column1: I alone knew that this bird was worth a sultan's ransom.
column2: 唯有我知道,這隻鳥值得上一位蘇丹的贖金。

column1: Let me look, let me see.
column2: 讓我瞧瞧,讓我看看。

column1: It seems that the joke is upon me.
column2: 看來這玩笑是開在我頭上了。

column1: This is not the real Black Falcon.
column2: 這不是真正的黑隼。

column1: There were four of these made. Only one had any value.
column2: 這玩意兒一共做了四隻,只有一隻是真有價值的。

column1: It seems that I must continue my search.
column2: 看來我得繼續尋找下去了。

column1: If you are interested, I would not be adverse to having such a resourceful and skilled person such as yourself at my side.
column2: 若你有意,我倒不反對讓你這般足智多謀、身手不凡的人留在身邊。

column1: If you could but learn to curb those unfortunate heroic tendencies, you would make an excellent partner.
column2: 只要你能學著收斂那些礙事的英雄習氣,準能成為一名出色的夥伴。

column1: Leave me, now. I have a good deal of planning to do.
column2: 現在,退下吧。我有一大堆事要謀劃。

column1: You should answer my question. Would you prefer our famous Raseirish Coffee or a Djinn Sling?
column2: 你該回答我的問題。你想來杯我們著名的拉希爾咖啡,還是精靈賓治?

column1: You hear cursing and grumbling nearby.
column2: 你聽見附近傳來咒罵和牢騷聲。

column1: This drab and filthy room would make a jail cell feel comfortable.
column2: 這間又灰暗又骯髒的房間,讓人覺得牢房都還算舒服。

column1: Congratulations, Paladin!
column2: 恭喜你,聖騎士!

column1: Hero of Two Lands!
column2: 兩國的英雄!

column1: Blue Frog Mode unlocked. Use F10 to activate in-game.
column2: 藍蛙模式已解鎖。在遊戲中按 F10 啟動。

column1: Battle Mode unlocked. Use F4 to activate in-game.
column2: 戰鬥模式已解鎖。在遊戲中按 F4 啟動。

column1: File already exists. Do you wish to
column2: 檔案已存在。你是否要

column1: Press 'Y' to overwrite. Press 'N' to skip.
column2: 按「Y」覆寫,按「N」略過。

column1: Saving Character
column2: 正在儲存角色

column1: Could not create file.
column2: 無法建立檔案。

column1: The save character file, "glory2.sav", has been created in the game directory. Save this file for use with "Quest for Glory III: Wages of War" from Sierra.
column2: 角色存檔「glory2.sav」已建立於遊戲目錄中。請保留此檔,以便在 Sierra 的《英雄傳奇III:戰禍》中使用。

column1: amesThank you for playing "Quest for Glory II: Trial by Fire," and congratulations again on winning. We'll see you again soon in "Quest for Glory III: Wages of War."
column2: ames感謝你遊玩《英雄傳奇II:烈火神兵》,並再次恭喜你通關。我們很快會在《英雄傳奇III:戰禍》中再會。

column1: Thank you for playing "Quest for Glory II: Trial by Fire," and congratulations again on winning. We'll see you again soon in "Quest for Glory III: Wages of War."
column2: 感謝你遊玩《英雄傳奇II:烈火神兵》,並再次恭喜你通關。我們很快會在《英雄傳奇III:戰禍》中再會。

column1: And so, as the moon moves slowly through the starry sky above the mountains and the desert of Shapeir, our Hero bids a fond "adieu" to his latest set of adventures and miserable puns. Where will the road lead next?
column2: 就這樣,當月亮緩緩劃過夏皮爾群山與沙漠上方那片繁星點點的夜空,我們的英雄滿懷不捨地向這場最新的冒險——以及那些慘不忍睹的雙關笑話——道一聲「珍重再見」。前方的路又將通往何方呢?

column1: veBut wait! There's more to come in the next thrilling saga of "Quest for Glory" by Lori and Corey.
column2: ve且慢!Lori 與 Corey 打造的《英雄傳奇》下一段驚險傳奇,還有更多精彩在後頭。

column1: waIf you have not already done so, we encourage you to play "Quest for Glory II" again with the other two character types; many of the puzzles are different or have alternate solutions.
column2: wa如果你還沒試過,我們鼓勵你換另外兩種角色職業再玩一遍《英雄傳奇II》;許多謎題會不一樣,或是另有破解之道。

column1: AbIn the meantime, you are already a winner! Your Hero will now be saved for use in "Quest for Glory III: Wages of War."
column2: Ab而在那之前,你已經是個贏家了!你的英雄此刻將被存檔,以便在《英雄傳奇III:戰禍》中繼續征戰。
